Appendix pseudo-schemas are checked renderings of this table, not permission to infer a type from an English name: every apparent root, ref, OID, digest, Raft pointer, floor inventory, backup closure, or union element must have an explicit row. A field absent from the table is unencodable. This table is the sole input to generated reachability/GC, checkpoint-vector, backup-closure, construction-DAG, compatibility, size-limit, and fuzz walkers.

Ordered transitions have one further normative registry, `command_contracts.toml`; hand-written lists in this document are generated readability projections and are never a second source of truth. One row exists for every role-valid command arm and has the exact schema `{command_contract_id,role:Local|Meta|Shard,outer_command_union,outer_wire_tag,input_schema_id,input_wire_tag,inner_wire_tag?,body_schema_id,result_schema_id,applied_record_schema_id,handler_symbol,transition_class:Semantic|Maintenance|Scaffolding,sequence_effects,expected_state_schema_id,authority_arm,authority_evidence_target_schema_id?,terminal_audit_freeze_arm,terminal_audit_gate_arm,payload_availability_rule,publication_mode:SinglePlane|AtomicProtocolDetach{cross_plane_conservation_recipe_id},construction_dag_recipe_id,consumed_state_slots[DurableStateSlotRef],written_state_slots[DurableStateSlotRef],checkpoint_floor_classes[],backup_restore_gc_classes[],posture_feature_predicate,format_epoch_range,status}` where `DurableStateSlotRef={plane:SemanticPayload|Protocol|PreparedOwnership|Consensus|Bootstrap,role,slot_tag}`. Rows are per concrete inner tag: an outer `DpTransition`, `RemoteRetention`, configuration, delivery, GC, backup, restore, format, bulk, derived-build, or audit-lifecycle family never hides an open subcommand. The paired `command_type_classification.toml` assigns every independently addressable normative input-looking type—including every type whose stable name ends in `Spec`—exactly one class: `RegisteredCommandInput{command_contract_id}`, `PreOrderArtifact`, `CertificateInput`, `ResultOnly`, `ProjectionInput`, or `RuntimeOnly`. A suffix conveys no semantics. In particular an ordinary schema/index definition is a `SchemaOp` carried by a transaction; it cannot silently acquire a catalog/schema/index control command merely because prose calls it a specification.

Generation and CI enforce a two-way total bijection. Every inhabitable Local, Meta, or Shard command-union arm resolves to exactly one live command-contract row; every live row resolves to exactly one union arm, one registered canonical input, one exact body/result/applied-record triple, and one exhaustive Rust apply handler. Every independently addressable input-like type has one classification row, and every `RegisteredCommandInput` row is reachable from exactly one role/tag contract unless an explicit shared-role row names its finite role set. Duplicate tags, aliases that encode a second command, wildcard bodies, unresolved associated types, prose-only phases, unhandled rows, handlers without rows, or a command input classified as its own future result are G0 failures. Generated exhaustive-match tests compare registry rows, Rust enums, encoders/decoders, state-machine transition tables, Appendix renderings, negative fixtures, and every command family named by a feature contract. Thus adding a phase requires one atomic registry/schema/handler/test change; omission cannot degrade into an unreachable recovery state.

`durable_state_slots.toml` is the total cross-plane registry. Its rows point to exactly one backing field registry and use `{plane,role,slot_tag,backing_registry,stable_name,exact_wire_type,reference_semantics,target_schema_id,activation_predicate,initial_value_recipe,transition_writer_contract_ids[],checkpoint_or_snapshot_class,floor_class,backup_class,restore_reconciliation_class,gc_class,role_transition_class,status}`. `state_payload_fields.toml` backs only canonical semantic payloads; `protocol_state_fields.toml` backs the manifest/Raft-maintenance-owned protocol plane; `prepared_state_fields.toml` and `consensus_state_fields.toml` back prepared ownership and Raft hard/snapshot/configuration state. Bootstrap slots remain fixed-location fields validated through `bootstrap_frames.toml` but still receive cross-index rows. Every consumed/written command slot must resolve through this table, every active slot names every legal writer, and every required checkpoint/snapshot/floor/backup/restore/GC/transition walker is generated from it. A feature with no slot, a slot with no writer, an unclassified protocol mutation, or a semantic-payload checker pretending to cover another plane is a G0 failure.

External irreversible **application/authority actions** have one additional total registry, `irreversible_dispatch_contracts.toml`; ad-hoc “call after commit” code is forbidden. An action belongs here when transport may escape the process and the target can durably register, charge, reserve, publish, revoke, destroy, release, or advance a CAS-linear application authority—even when the operation is logically reversible later. The two publication substrates are not recursively modeled as application actions: ordinary state uses the fixed `RootSlot` protocol, and the sole pre-first-root exception uses the exact `prebootstrap_journal_publication_contracts.toml` protocol below. G0 permits no third substrate.

`DispatchSourceGateKind` is the closed union `PublishedSemanticAuthorization{authorization_record_kind,publication_evidence_kind,current_authorization_and_resource_checker}|PublishedExternalCasAuthorization{operation_record_kind,operation_publication_evidence_kind,authority_head_kind,current_operation_authorization_resource_and_direct_predecessor_checker}|PreBootstrapReservationAuthorization{prebootstrap_root_kind,reservation_body_and_head_kind,current_reserved_observation_plan_and_staging_inode_checker}|PreBootstrapClaimedAuthority{prebootstrap_root_kind,claimed_authority_kind,current_claim_plan_and_staging_inode_checker}|PublishedCancellationFence{fence_kind,publication_evidence_kind,current_fence_and_zero_start_checker}|SemanticQuarantine{quarantine_kind,publication_evidence_kind,current_root_and_no_new_reference_checker}|ActivatedReleaseHold{hold_kind,publication_evidence_kind,current_hold_and_participant_checker}|ReservationDisposition{reservation_kind,publication_evidence_kind,current_claim_or_pending_disposition_checker}`. DP/privacy, audit, continuity, catalog, and time-authority CAS operations use `PublishedExternalCasAuthorization`; a bare current external head without the exact published local operation and its publication evidence can never authorize them. `DispatchTerminalImportKind` is the closed union `SemanticImport{command_input_kind}|ProtocolFinalize{maintenance_input_kind}|ExternalCompletionImport{command_or_maintenance_input_kind}|PreBootstrapJournalImport{journal_transition_kind,canonical_bridge_import_kind}`.

`DispatchOriginPlane` is the closed tag `CanonicalProtocol|RestorePreBootstrap`. The unique registry key is `dispatch_contract_id`, not `action_kind`: one provider action kind may have a finite generated contract family when it is legally invoked from more than one origin plane or source-gate/profile class. Exact uniqueness is `(action_kind,origin_plane,role_and_group_predicate,source_gate_arm)`, and each member has a disjoint operation-ID namespace, attempt state slot, authorize/arm/import contracts, terminal schema IDs, and retention floor. `RestoreArchiveLeaseRenew`, `RestoreArchiveLeaseRelease`, reservation claim/consume/burn where applicable, transient cleanup, and any other dual-plane provider action therefore have distinct prebootstrap and canonical contract IDs even when their provider action-body schema is shared. No terminal evidence/import from one contract member can satisfy another.

`ExternalActionRequestBinding` is the portable, reference-free prefix every actual request, status result, refusal, and terminal receipt byte-matches: `{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,role_and_group,service_visibility_epoch,dispatch_contract_id,origin_plane:DispatchOriginPlane,action_kind,authority_domain,target_identity,operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,canonical_action_body_digest,source_gate_kind,source_gate_publication_commitment,authority_binding_public_commitment,request_schema_major,request_schema_minor}`. The binding is constructed only after the registered source gate is durable. Pre-order action bodies and Semantic authorization records therefore contain an operation/idempotency identity plus a canonical action-body recipe, never a future publication commitment or full request. An external authority must key status and idempotency by at least `(authority_domain,target_identity,operation_id)` and permanently reject the same operation ID with different contract, plane, binding, or body bytes.

`AuthenticatedExternalStatusStep<Contract>` is the generated reference-free `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{dispatch_contract_id=Contract},canonical_full_request_digest,status_query_nonce,status_observed_at:AuthorityInstant,status_time_authority_profile_oid,status_class:NotRegistered{authoritative_absence_counter,provider_operation-index-proof_digest}|RegisteredPending{registered_operation_digest,provider_progress_token_digest,provider_counter}|Applied{typed_receipt_digest,result_head_or_target_state_commitment,provider_counter_or_cas_version}|AlreadyApplied{typed_receipt_digest,result_head_or_target_state_commitment,provider_counter_or_cas_version}|PermanentlyRefused{typed_refusal_tombstone_digest,zero_effect_commitment,provider_counter_or_cas_version}|ConflictNoEffect{typed_conflict_evidence_digest,zero_effect_commitment,provider_counter_or_cas_version},prior_status_step_digest?,provider_status_key_id,provider_status_key_epoch,signature}`. Each contract row fixes the action kind, origin plane, admissible status classes, exact proof carried by `NotRegistered`, permitted monotonic step relation, and whether the provider uses a counter, CAS version, or both. A timeout, transport error, unsigned absence, locally elapsed deadline, or response for a different contract/request is not a status step.

`PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<Contract>` denotes a generated finite family, never an erased runtime generic. Every dispatch-contract row emits one distinct registered schema with `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{dispatch_contract_id=Contract},canonical_full_request_digest,dispatch_attempt_generation,source_gate_publication_commitment,status_lineage:[AuthenticatedExternalStatusStep<Contract>],terminal_disposition:Applied{typed_receipt_digest,result_head_or_target_state_commitment}|AlreadyApplied{typed_receipt_digest,result_head_or_target_state_commitment}|PermanentlyRefused{typed_refusal_tombstone_digest,zero_effect_commitment}|ConflictNoEffect{typed_conflict_evidence_digest,zero_effect_commitment},provider_counter_or_cas_version,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. It contains no local or Protocol object identity. `PortableTimeAuthorityIssuanceCloseTerminalEvidence`, `PortableTimeAuthorityIssuanceFenceTerminalEvidence`, `PortableTimeAuthorityRegistryTransitionTerminalEvidence`, and every other named terminal type are exact generated members keyed by contract ID with action-specific typed payloads; they are not aliases that permit cross-action or cross-plane decoding. Elsewhere, notation of the form `PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<Action>` is permitted only when the action has exactly one live contract; a dual-plane action must name its exact `Contract`.

At least one live contract row exists for every external action, and every legal origin-plane/source-gate specialization has exactly one row. The action inventory includes: DP charge and privacy-continuity freeze/retire/profile transitions; audit ticket issue, resolution-batch CAS, and completeness change; backup publication, transparency append, archive-grant issue/renewal, source-release-hold prepare/activate/completion; atomic restore archive acquisition whose typed terminal payload includes generation-one source-lease activation; separate source-`K_oid`/DEK recovery, target-`K_oid` creation, recovery-incarnation-lease acquire/refresh, later source-lease renew/release, reservation claim/consume/burn, pre-bootstrap continuity abandonment, prior-incarnation lease fencing, catalog reserve/activate/tombstone and ExternalCas continuity pending/finalize/abandon; time-authority issuance close/fence/registry transition/service shutdown; distinct GC member reclaim and cancel-before-start operations plus every deletion/crypto-erasure; and KMS/HSM/storage key destruction. There is no separate source-lease-acquire action: the prebootstrap `RestoreArchiveAcquire` contract alone performs Absent -> Registered -> Active and returns both archive acquisition and lease-acquire typed payloads under one request binding; key recovery/creation occurs only under its later refinement-authorized rows. The nonrecursive prebootstrap journal-head CAS publishes dispatch state but is not itself an application action row. Catalog/continuity/time/privacy/audit registries are not exceptions merely because they are CAS-linear. The only v1 **application/provider** transport permitted before a canonical RootManifest is the registered restore pre-bootstrap machine below, and it may send only after its complete SentUnknown generation is journal-head durable. Reservation-claim contracts use `PreBootstrapReservationAuthorization`; post-claim acquisition/use/consume/burn contracts use their exact `PreBootstrapClaimedAuthority`, `ReservationDisposition`, or registered successor gate. Every request, status, receipt, and terminal-evidence byte records its contract ID, origin plane, selected source-gate kind, and commitment; no unconditional “claimed target authority” field is inferred.

Each row declares `{dispatch_contract_id,action_kind,origin_plane:DispatchOriginPlane,operation_id_namespace,role_and_group_predicate,source_gate:DispatchSourceGateKind,operation_plan_kind,canonical_action_body_kind,target_identity_kind,idempotency_key_recipe,canonical_request_transcript_recipe,attempt_slot,authorize_contract_id,arm_contract_id,mark_sent_unknown_contract_id,abandon_before_send_contract_id,status_query_contract,authoritative_not_registered_evidence_kind,post_eligibility_not_registered_contract:Forbidden|RefusalOnly{provider_expiry_checker,permanent_refusal_tombstone_kind}|ExactCleanupRetry{eligibility_kind}|SeparateCleanupAction{action_kind},permanent_refusal_evidence_kind,terminal_receipt_kind,portable_terminal_evidence_kind,allowed_terminal_outcomes,current_root_or_prebootstrap_inventory_checker,configuration_and_writer_fence_checker,continuity_and_time_checker,dispatch_hold_class,terminal_import:DispatchTerminalImportKind,nonterminal_retention_floor,terminal_compaction_rule,status}`. A live row is required before an action encoder, provider adapter, or handler can issue or status-query the action. DP charge and audit ticket/completeness choose `RefusalOnly` when their admission window can expire; reservation consume and source-lease release choose only their exact registered cleanup eligibility. The generated matrix rejects duplicate contract keys, an action/plane/source-gate hole, missing authenticated idempotency/status semantics, timeout-as-success, changed operation/request bytes, a receipt/refusal not bound to the exact contract lineage, a future-receipt cycle, a Protocol object identity in portable evidence, and any send whose exact published source gate is absent or stale.

The generated canonical Protocol-plane machine is `IrreversibleDispatchAttempt<Contract> {dispatch_contract_id:Contract,action_kind,operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,target_identity,canonical_action_body_digest,canonical_request_transcript_digest,source_gate_kind_and_commitment,source_publication_evidence_commitment,authority_binding_public_commitment,service_visibility_epoch,nonretaining_predecessor_digest,state}` with the closed state `Armed{arm_generation,authorized_operation_plan_commitment,expected_predecessor_protocol_state_commitment,expected_source_gate_and_configuration_commitment}|SentUnknown{attempt_generation,arm_generation,arm_publication_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DispatchArmPublicationEvidence<Contract>>,mark_publication_basis_commitment}|AbandonedBeforeSend{attempt_generation,arm_generation,arm_publication_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DispatchArmPublicationEvidence<Contract>>,abandonment_proof_ref:StrongRef<DispatchNeverSentProof<Contract>>,terminal_reason}|TerminalImported{attempt_generation,terminal_outcome:Applied|AlreadyApplied|PermanentlyRefused|ConflictNoEffect,portable_terminal_evidence_identity,external_receipt_or_refusal_import_commitment,status_lineage_commitment}`. `Contract` must have `origin_plane=CanonicalProtocol`; prebootstrap uses its distinct journal-backed generated attempt type keyed by the prebootstrap contract ID. `Armed` and `AbandonedBeforeSend` contain only values known before their own publication; neither names its future Protocol state, RootManifest, slot, or publication generation. If a later consumer requires evidence that abandonment itself was published, it reconstructs `DispatchAbandonmentPublicationEvidence<Contract>` after the fact from authenticated current/historical Protocol-root membership and `RootPublicationEvidence`; that evidence is never embedded in the abandoning successor.

After the Armed-containing root is durable, recovery or the publisher constructs `DispatchArmPublicationEvidence<Contract> {dispatch_attempt_identity_and_commitment,containing_protocol_state_identity,containing_consensus_state_root_identity,containing_root_manifest_identity,winning_slot_index_generation_and_digest,root_publication_evidence:RootPublicationEvidence,source_gate_commitment,current_configuration_writer_fence_continuity_and_service_commitment,evidence_digest}`. `DispatchNeverSentProof<Contract>` binds that evidence, a current authenticated scan of both slots and the exact attempt lineage, and proves that no `SentUnknown` successor for the arm generation was ever published. Each row names the sole legal Armed-abandonment proof, NotRegistered proof, refusal/conflict evidence, typed receipt import, and portable terminal-evidence schema; absence is a G0 failure. `Armed -> SentUnknown` is durably RootSlot-published **before** request bytes can enter a transport. Recovery therefore treats every `SentUnknown` attempt as possibly delivered, first performs the registered authenticated status query, and never infers absence from a timeout. A definitive signed `NotRegistered` result permits only the byte-identical request under a fresh guard while the row's action-specific eligibility still permits first send; if eligibility has expired, it permits no send unless that contract explicitly registers a terminal-cleanup exception. Applied/AlreadyApplied imports the exact receipt; PermanentRefusal imports a signed zero-effect refusal tombstone; ConflictNoEffect imports signed evidence that the requested transition did not occur and cannot later appear under that operation ID. Any ambiguous result permits status/reconciliation only. `Armed -> AbandonedBeforeSend` is the sole no-send terminal and requires the registered proof; disconnect, cancellation, process death, or provider absence is insufficient. Construction-DAG and crash tests cut before/after authorization publication, Armed publication, arm-evidence construction, SentUnknown publication, transport submission, every status class, typed terminal import, and abandonment publication for every registered contract row.

Immediately before the first send or an allowed exact retry, `IrreversibleDispatchGuard<Contract>` rereads the winning RootSlot and both slot generations, the complete current all-plane root inventory, the still-current registered source gate and its checker, current/prospective configuration, writer-fence and continuity heads, service epoch, authority binding, and profiled time evidence. It also acquires a linear `IrreversibleActionHold<Contract>` that conflicts with invalidation of that authorization/quarantine/hold/reservation/CAS/cancellation gate, target resurrection where applicable, configuration/fence/continuity replacement, and authority widening while allowing unrelated database progress. The nonserializable guard is bound to the exact contract's `SentUnknown` attempt and request bytes, is consumed at transport submission, expires on any checked-observation budget breach, and cannot survive restart. A destructive target independently durably records Started under its own current fence before deleting or destroying its first byte/key; a CAS-style external authority durably registers the exact operation/request lineage before applying a terminal direct successor.

The pre-bootstrap analogue is generated from the exact row whose `origin_plane=RestorePreBootstrap`, not from a singular dual-use row and not from a parallel informal protocol. `PreBootstrapDispatchAttempt<Contract>` has the same request/status laws and `Authorized -> Armed -> SentUnknown -> TerminalImported|AbandonedBeforeSend` semantics, but its publication evidence is `PreBootstrapDispatchPublicationEvidence<Contract>` over the authenticated restore dispatch root, `journal_staging_inode_identity`, both mirror slot generations, whole-inode fence, selected prebootstrap source-gate kind/commitment, external journal-head commitment, and sealed plan/action bijection. `PreBootstrapExternalTerminalImportArtifact<Contract>` is the prebootstrap wrapper `{dispatch_contract_id:Contract,origin_plane:RestorePreBootstrap,action_kind,request_binding,dispatch_attempt_and_publication_commitment,terminal_artifact_bytes_and_digest,portable_terminal_evidence_bytes_and_digest,status_lineage,prebootstrap_time_and_source_gate_commitment,dispatch_root_generation,import_digest}`; it deliberately has no `FenceBoundIrreversibleReceiptHeader` or Protocol identity. `PreBootstrapIrreversibleDispatchGuard<Contract>` rereads all of those values plus current external claim/grant/continuity/time evidence immediately before send. The first canonical bridge copies/verifies each terminal-import artifact through a contract-specific canonical import record and imports a sealed, terminal-or-still-recoverable journal with an exact contract-to-attempt bijection. If a nonterminal prebootstrap attempt must be adopted into canonical Protocol for recovery, the Protocol owner is `AdoptedPreBootstrapDispatchAttempt<Contract>` and permanently retains `origin_plane=RestorePreBootstrap`, the prebootstrap operation-ID namespace, source-gate lineage, and prebootstrap terminal-import type; it never mutates into the canonical contract for the same action. No other subsystem may create a private outbox, retry ledger, or direct provider path. These rules are generated compile-time capability boundaries plus lab/runtime oracles, not review conventions.

The only v1 **cross-plane publication mode** is a registry-declared `AtomicProtocolDetach{cross_plane_conservation_recipe_id}` command-contract row; it is not one special command. For each result-producing Local/Meta command that uses this mode, one same-group Raft entry and one RootManifest slot generation publish (a) the semantic outcome/frontier containing only an opaque owner key and detachment commitment and (b) a Protocol PendingVisibility or legal inline-Active owner plus typed resource escrow. The row supplies a generated cross-plane conservation proof over owner key, byte digest, charge, audit candidate, and authority binding; ObjectId/reference edges across the planes are forbidden. All later activation/resume/ACK/release/expiry/send-completion work is Protocol maintenance and cannot write semantic slots. This narrow composite mode is used by ordinary query results, the **initial** subscription cursor/baseline owner, durable artifact outputs, protected errors, and DP results. Subsequent subscription deltas, coalesced replacements, and reset-required transitions are Protocol-only publications under the already-detached cursor identity. Any other multi-plane writer, any subscription-delta semantic rewrite, or a protocol-to-semantic back-edge is a G0 failure.

`state_payload_fields.toml` therefore includes allocation reservations/dispositions, valid-time slices, constraint reservations, resource/escrow/security authority, transaction semantics and capability lookup, privacy/DP semantic request state, audit admission/freeze state, bulk/derived/schema/format authority, backup/restore/GC semantic decisions, topology, keys, and remote-retention/trust obligations. It does **not** contain the audit visibility pipeline, external-audit release-time outcomes, independent result/cursor/artifact delivery lifecycles, Raft hard state, prepared ownership, or RootSlot state. `protocol_state_fields.toml` includes `AuditVisibilityPipeline`, visible audit-outcome/release index, externally imported audit-continuity head, independent result-retention/delivery index, result activation/ACK/release/resume state, cursor/artifact-output transport lifecycles, and other replica/group protocol ownership whose transition must not advance logical semantics. A Local/Meta result-producing semantic command may atomically write both its semantic detachment commitment and a protocol-plane PendingVisibility owner in the same RootManifest publication; later audit visibility, activation, ACK, release, expiry, and resume are quorum-durable maintenance transitions over the protocol plane. Shard rows remain projections or shard-owned protocol state only; they never create a second Meta semantic authority.

Generated reference unions are exact rather than erased. For each containing schema and role, `durable_fields.toml` emits a finite tagged union such as `LocalCommandInputRef`, `MetaAppliedResultRef`, `ShardProtocolEvidenceRef`, or `MandatoryInventoryRef`; every arm names one top-level target schema and carries the target's canonical typed reference encoding. Bare `StrongRef`, `StrongRef<A|B>`, `StrongRef[]`, `result_refs[StrongRef]`, slash-separated pseudo-types, or a generic `RegisteredStrongRef` without a containing-schema-generated arm set are invalid in normative bytes. Compact renderings that use such shorthand are navigation-only and must expand through an exact field-table row. A generated construction-DAG test creates the concrete graph for every command tag and state slot and rejects self, mutual, or future-result edges.

Security binding is schema-enforced rather than name-enforced. `AuthorityOwningRole` is the closed set `Local|Meta`; Shards consume certified projections but never originate an authority record or permit. `AuthorityBindingRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the independently addressable `{role,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,tenant,principal,non_widenable_authority_ceiling_digest,purpose_class,security_state_binding:SecurityStateBinding,authority_digest,body_digest:BodyDigest<AuthorityBindingRecord<Role>>}`; `AuthorityBindingRef<Role>` is the generated exact role arm that strongly selects it. `AuthorityBoundHeader<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the embedded `{authority_binding_ref:AuthorityBindingRef<Role>,authority_digest,security_policy_epoch,revocation_index}` projection and every repeated value must byte-match the record.

`MetaAuthorityBindingProjection` is the portable certified projection `{source_authority_binding_public_commitment,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,tenant_commitment,principal_commitment,non_widenable_authority_ceiling_commitment,purpose_class,exact_clipped_operation_read_write_disclosure_and_resource_scopes,security_policy_epoch,revocation_index,source_meta_prefix,source_configuration_and_retention_grant_evidence,projection_digest,signatures}`. `ShardAuthorityBindingProjection` is the shard-local exact `{security_state_projection:ShardSecurityStateProjection,certified_source_authority_projection_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<MetaAuthorityBindingProjection>,source_authority_binding_public_commitment,tenant_commitment,principal_commitment,non_widenable_authority_ceiling_commitment,purpose_class,exact_clipped_operation_read_write_disclosure_and_resource_scopes,source_meta_prefix,source_configuration_and_grant_commitment,projection_digest}`. The certified source projection and local security projection must agree on namespace/incarnation/service epoch/policy/revocation and cannot be recombined from different Meta prefixes or principals. `AuthorityBindingFor<Role>` is the generated closed role family `Local{header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>}|Meta{header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>}|Shard{projection:ShardAuthorityBindingProjection}`; exactly one arm is inhabitable for a given containing role. Every durable field set marked `authority_bearing=true` must contain exactly one inline complete binding, one `AuthorityBoundHeader`, or—only for a shard consumer—the exact `ShardAuthorityBindingProjection`; scalar epochs/digests, a permit reference alone, the narrower `ShardSecurityStateProjection`, or an opaque “authority projection digest” alone fail G0. Public wire values cannot carry local refs, so their signed/MACed header contains `security_state_binding_public_commitment`; the durable issuance/replay record strongly retains the complete AuthorityBindingRecord and proves that commitment. The generated matrix covers permits, admissions/releases, audit-requirement decisions, prepared/final owners, intents/batches/effects, constraints/resources/escrow, derived identities/builds, plans/replay, result/cursor/output promises, DP, backup/export, descriptor views, cache keys, dispatch attempts, and every command authorization. Every Local/Meta outer command carries exactly one complete `AuthorityBoundHeader` that byte-matches its authorization object and frozen reservation; a Shard command instead carries the exact `ShardAuthorityBindingProjection` named by its imported authorization. Equal-scalar/different-root/service/tenant/principal/ceiling/role/purpose substitution is a mandatory negative fixture.

The sole authority-retention exception is a terminal, nonrevivable, nonpayload tombstone. `TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>` is the reference-free `{role,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,authority_binding_body_digest,authority_digest,security_state_binding_public_commitment,tenant_principal_ceiling_purpose_commitment,final_disclosure_and_answer_contract_commitment,body_digest}`. It is derived byte-for-byte from the last live `AuthorityBindingFor<Role>` before that strong authority edge is released. A terminal commitment can prove identity, nonreuse, disclosure/answer compatibility, and tombstone equality only; it cannot authorize output, lookup of protected payload, resume, widening, mutation, resurrection, or a new capability. Any later status/lookup request must independently validate the caller against the exact current role binding/capability and compare its public commitments before returning a uniformly nonrevealing terminal projection. Any live/revivable/byte-owning state that substitutes this commitment for a full binding fails G0.

G0 enforces four separate bijections: each top-level logical schema has exactly one logical-object row and `ObjectKind`; each physical record has exactly one physical identity row; each fixed bootstrap frame has exactly one bootstrap row; and each embedded durable type/tag has exactly one wire-type row. It also enforces disjointness across classes, the per-field cross-index, code-range/nonreuse rules, every reference target and containing-schema relation, and that every emitted discriminant is registered. Every class requires the appropriate canonical encoder/decoder, construction-order annotation, version/feature/posture compatibility rule, size/resource limits, and fuzz/golden corpus. A missing, duplicated, cross-class, untyped-field, or orphan row is a build failure. Unknown required W12 or restore/activation tags fail closed; a 1.0 reader never partially interprets a sharded database. The canonical content graph is a DAG: every strong, conditional, and weak-digest value in newly encoded bytes must already be known. A future applied result may be described only by a sequence-neutral spec, body recipe, or assigned scalar order, never by its not-yet-computable ObjectId/digest. Self IDs, mutually strong cycles, and an input command that names its own applied result are invalid.

Stored digest fields are never defined by naming convention. Every `durable_fields.toml` digest row declares exactly one semantic class: `TargetDigest` of separately identified bytes, `TranscriptDigest` with a registered transcript recipe, `WeakIdentityDigest`, or `BodyDigest`. A `BodyDigest<T>` row fixes `{domain_separator,schema_major,included_field_tags,excluded_field_tags}`; its default recipe is `BLAKE3(domain_separator || canonical(schema_major, T excluding exactly the one BodyDigest field))`. Signatures, MACs, or other fields are excluded only when the row explicitly lists their stable tags. Unknown exclusions, two body-digest fields, an implicit zero placeholder, or computing over bytes that include the digest itself are G0 errors. Encoder, decoder, field-table, golden-vector, mutation, and cross-version tests all use the same generated recipe. In particular `RaftSnapshot.body_digest`, `ShardHistoryInventory.body_digest`, and `GlobalKeyEnvelopeManifest.body_digest` use registered body transcripts excluding only their own field; `BackupManifest` instead follows its acyclic Body -> SignatureSet -> Manifest construction and contains no self digest.

To avoid the state-root/command-head cycle, canonical state is layered. `LogicalStatePayload` contains the complete semantic state at `{ applied_logical_command_seq, applied_commit_seq }` but no logical-command-head wrapper. `LocalCommandOrigin` is the closed union `Prior{predecessor:ConditionalCommandRef}|RecoveryBridge{bridge_spec_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryBridgeSpec<Local>>,source_manifest_identity:WeakDigest,transform_and_projection_digest}`. After deterministic apply has constructed every result object and the payload, it creates `LogicalCommandRecord { logical_command_seq, origin:LocalCommandOrigin, command:StrongRef<CommitCommand|ControlCommand>, transaction_marker:StrongMarkerRef?, logical_command_hlc, resulting_state_payload_digest:WeakDigest }`. Normal work requires `Prior`; only the first guarded restore command may use `RecoveryBridge`. It then creates `LogicalStateRoot { payload_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStatePayload>, logical_command_head:StrongCommandRef? }`; fresh genesis alone has no head. The payload digest cross-checks the sibling object; it is not a reachability edge. Thus the final root commits both the complete state and its transition record without either object naming a future ObjectId. Checkpoint cuts are the only normal predecessor-reclamation boundary. Each canonical `CommitMarker` is independently encoded and hashed before packing. `MarkerRef { marker_oid, commit_seq }` is the transaction-history identity used by branch heads, version origins/retirements, checkpoints, MMR leaves, and merges. A `MarkerBatch` merely packs length-delimited marker bytes; the object-location index maps each marker OID to its authenticated `(batch_encoding, offset, length)` subobject locator. Objects are assembled in uniquely named staging files, fsynced, sealed with an immutable trailer, renamed to final segment identity, and made directory-durable before publication. Staging files are unreachable state and are cleaned only by a recovery procedure that proves it.

`manifest.root` is a fixed two-slot bootstrap file; these are the only published durable bytes updated in place. Each 4 KiB `RootSlot` contains `{ magic, format, slot_generation, local_writer_fence_epoch, database_id, database_security_namespace_id, cluster_incarnation, incarnation_continuity_profile_id, cluster_incarnation_continuity_digest, continuity_cas_version, service_visibility_epoch, root_manifest_oid[32], RootBootstrap, tear_checksum }`. `IncarnationContinuityProfile` is `DirectoryBound` (ordinary embedded/local writes rely on the exact inode lease, and same-identity restore to another directory is forbidden—clone only) or `ExternalCas` (the Appendix A continuity record and live member lease are mandatory); W12 requires `ExternalCas`. `RootBootstrap` supplies everything needed **before** the object-location index exists: the complete canonical `CipherDescriptorWithoutDigest` for this root; its complete `EncodingDescriptorWithoutId` (including transfer length, both OTI fields, symbol size/count, and symbol-auth profile); `CiphertextId`, `EncodingId`, and ciphertext digest; a complete specialized `ContiguousSpan` placement descriptor containing placement epoch, failure-domain policy and domain ID, segment/offset/length, and symbol-inventory digest; and a noncircular opener bundle for both the root DEK and `K_oid` (inline wrapped keys or authenticated KMS/HSM locators plus wrap digests). Recovery locates the span, scans and authenticates its exact symbol inventory, verifies the inventory digest and reconstructed `PlacementId`, reconstructs the exact object AAD and encoding transcript, decodes the root, verifies its keyed ObjectId, and only then consults its indexes. No descriptor or identity-key field comes from the object being opened. `ObjectId` is already the full logical digest; there is no redundant `root_full_digest`. Recovery rejects a root whose authenticated namespace/database/incarnation/continuity/profile/visibility tuple disagrees with the slot.

Every slot publication is serialized by one per-open-inode `RootPublicationSequencer`; the whole-inode writer lease excludes other processes, while this sequencer excludes stale same-process tasks. It is an asupersync actor/obligation, not a conventional mutex held across `await`, and it grants at most one linear nonserializable `RootPublicationPermit`. Permit acquisition rereads and structurally authenticates both slots and binds `{inode_identity,filesystem_profile,publication_class,expected_winning_slot,expected_winning_generation,expected_winning_slot_digest,expected_manifest_identity,expected_writer_fence_epoch,expected_continuity_profile_digest_and_cas_version,expected_service_visibility_epoch,target_inactive_slot,next_generation,target_manifest_identity,target_bootstrap_digest,target_physical_generation_lease_descriptor_digest,target_lease_inode_identity_and_creation_nonce_digest,incarnation_lease_action_digest}`. The target lease inode must already be created, authenticated, file-durable, and directory-durable before the permit is issued; the permit reopens and byte-matches it. `next_generation=checked_add(expected_winning_generation,1)`; exhaustion fail-closes all further publication and requires a fenced clone/migration rather than wrap or saturation. Two structurally credible equal-highest generations are accepted for read recovery only when their complete authenticated slot bytes are identical; any difference is ambiguity and fail-closes. Write mode first normalizes an identical-equal pair through a specialized takeover/convergence permit or remains read-only.

Immediately before `pwrite`, the permit re-reads the expected winner and live inode/fence/continuity/lease tuple. Any slot, generation, digest, manifest, epoch, CAS, service, path, mount, or lease drift consumes the permit as `AbortedStale` without writing; the caller rebuilds/rebases from the new winner. A successful permit writes exactly one complete inactive slot, performs the registered file and parent-directory durability actions, and rereads/authenticates the new winner and its mandatory closure.

Publication authority is crash-reconstructible rather than dependent on a post-write object. `RootPublicationEvidence` is the deterministic value `{filesystem_profile_id,inode_identity_digest,publication_class,complete_authenticated_root_slot_bytes_digest,slot_index_and_generation,root_manifest_identity,root_bootstrap_digest,physical_generation_lease_descriptor_digest,verified_lease_inode_identity_and_creation_nonce_digest,writer_fence_epoch,continuity_profile_head_and_cas,service_visibility_epoch,mandatory_closure_digest}` with identity `H("fgdb:root-publication-evidence:v1", canonical(all fields))`. Recovery recomputes it solely from the authenticated open inode, winning slot, manifest, registered filesystem profile, reopened lease inode, and mandatory closure; it contains no signature, future ref, or post-publication receipt. Every later member action binds this evidence identity and must also reread the same current source root under its action guard. The publisher may return a signed diagnostic `RootPublicationReceipt {source_root_publication_evidence:RootPublicationEvidence,prior_slot_and_generation,reread_digest,durability_observation,signer_fields,signature}`, but no correctness, liveness, retention, or later receipt depends on that diagnostic being constructed or rooted. A crash after slot durability but before returning reconstructs identical evidence.

Cancellation after write begins must drive the obligation to durable reread or poison write mode; it cannot free the sequencer early. Ordinary semantic apply, atomic semantic-plus-Protocol detachment, prepared-root/Raft-only publication, Protocol visibility maintenance, checkpoint/cut, compaction, restore, role transition, fence-only takeover, creation, retirement, and slot convergence each have a generated legal `publication_class`/guard/manifest-transition row and all use this boundary. No code path may call the slot writer directly.

Publication alternates slots through that permit and never precomputes a generation outside it. Recovery first discards slots whose fixed record fails structural/tear validation, then selects the highest remaining generation. Once a slot is structurally credible, authentication or mandatory-closure failure triggers symbol/replica repair and then **fails closed**; it never silently rolls back to an older authenticated state that may precede an acknowledged commit. Using an older generation requires the explicit restore/rollback protocol in §16, with fencing, incarnation advancement, and an audit/transparency record.

Replicated semantics and replica-local recovery metadata are distinct. Local, Meta, and Shard payload/root schemas are the exact Appendix A role-tagged contracts, including every security/retention/trust/derived/key/delivery/backup/GC field and the fresh bootstrap plus restore-only `RecoveryReconciling` rules. Semantic sequences/HLC advance on every owning-state mutation; commit sequence advances only on graph commit. Replicas at one role/prefix agree on the canonical semantic root.

`RootManifest` is the exact four-posture role-tagged union rendered authoritatively in Appendix A. Every active arm has one applied Semantic root, one role-valid Protocol root, one PreparedOwnership root, one Consensus/Raft root, and the exact position tuple that those roots jointly prove; Local/Meta visible endpoints are selected only inside `AuditVisibilityProtocolState<Role>`, while Shard visibility is selected by `ShardProtocolState` plus its imported Meta projection. No direct audit-pipeline or visible-root field is encoded in RootManifest. RetiredLocal retains only its compact nonsemantic certified transition/retirement proof closure and rejects every semantic, protocol, prepared, Raft, or service root. Mutation/recovery starts from the applied Semantic root, ordinary/public reads start from the Protocol-selected visible endpoint, and metadata-tree entries remain nonretaining locators resolved only after plane-aware liveness. Snapshots retain the manifest plus the complete Semantic/Protocol/Prepared/Consensus and physical recovery closure; replay pins `ReplayLogicalStateEvidence::Local|Sharded`, never a locator map, and RetiredLocal admits neither snapshots nor replay execution.

The current `RootManifest` is deliberately absent from every metadata-tree generation that it bootstraps: its slot's `RootBootstrap` is its sole opener, and a tree `closure_digest` covers only that already-constructed tree closure, excluding the slot, the current manifest, and the bootstrap bytes. A later generation may index an older manifest only as nonretaining history after that older identity is known. This exclusion prevents a future-root identity from entering the tree whose identity the root must already name.

Exactly one process per replica directory may hold the publication lease, and exactly one task within it may hold the root-publication permit. Durable write mode requires a versioned `FilesystemDurabilityProfile`, not a filesystem-name guess. The initial `FGFS-LINUX-LOCAL-V1` profile admits only regular files on checked-in, kernel/mount-option-pinned local ext4 (`data=ordered|journal`, barriers enabled) or XFS (barriers and log-force flushes enabled), with staging and final paths on the same mounted filesystem. DAX, network/cluster filesystems, FUSE/overlay layers, disabled barriers, an unidentifiable mount/device, and every unregistered combination are read-only. `filesystem_profiles.toml` records the allowed kernel/filesystem/mount/device assumptions and probe-suite version; `fgdb doctor --write-profile` performs a scratch-directory conformance probe, while open repeats the mount, stable-inode, and option checks. A future platform profile must prove the same contract before it can enable writes: a nonblocking process-death-released exclusive whole-inode lease linearizable among FrankenGraphDB writers; one cancel-correct in-process publication sequencer; `pwrite` confined to the permit-selected inactive slot even if that slot tears arbitrarily; no collateral damage to the other slot; durable file-data/metadata after its declared sync; durable create/no-replace same-directory rename after parent-directory sync; and no acknowledged volatile-cache state after a successful flush. The profile is an explicit storage assumption—doctor cannot transmute lying hardware into a proof.

Writer acquisition for an existing database is the normative `WriterTakeoverProtocol`. It (1) opens the database directory and `manifest.root` without symlink traversal, records mount/device/inode identity, and acquires the nonblocking exclusive lease on that exact open inode; (2) creates the sole `RootPublicationSequencer` for that leased inode, re-resolves the path, and rejects replacement or identity drift; (3) under the lease, authenticates the highest structurally credible slot and mandatory closure, recovers Raft/audit-visibility-pipeline/restore state, and reconciles every prepared owner; (4) validates the slot's `IncarnationContinuityProfile`: `DirectoryBound` requires the original directory/inode continuity and forbids a restore bridge, while `ExternalCas` fetches the exact current continuity head, byte-matches namespace/incarnation/CAS/visibility state, and acquires a member-bound lease with `OrdinaryWrite` or narrowly `RecoveryOnly` scope; (5) computes `new_epoch = 1 + max(epoch values in the selected slot and its authenticated reachable RootManifest/seals/prepared records)` with checked overflow—unrooted staging cannot raise the fence; (6) mints a linear, nonexportable `PendingFenceGuard { lease,publication_sequencer,stable_identity,old_epoch,new_epoch,exact_recovered_root,continuity_head_and_incarnation_guard }` whose sole effects are building/sealing this fence-only successor and requesting the exact `FenceTakeover` publication permit; (7) uses that permit to persist a successor `RootManifest` that preserves the same canonical logical root and every receipted/ordered obligation, publishes/syncs the inactive slot at `new_epoch`, then rereads/authenticates that exact slot and revalidates continuity; and only then (8) consumes it into the ordinary nonconstructible combined writer/incarnation token in `CommitCx` (or recovery-only token) and enables only the effects allowed by that scope. The pending guard cannot create user data, issue a receipt, acknowledge/propose ordinary Raft, alter canonical state, or publish any other root. There is no timeout or administrative force path around either lease or sequencer. A stale/later/unavailable external continuity head makes the database read-only; a crashed old owner may leave immutable staging bytes, but its already receipted/ordered ownership remains live and is adopted by recovery, while it may not issue a new receipt or publish after either epoch changes.

Every **database-member-issued** irreversible receipt begins with `FenceBoundIrreversibleReceiptHeader {receipt_schema_id,database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,role_and_group,issuing_member_id,configuration_ref,payload_predicate_digest,local_writer_fence_epoch,source_root_manifest_identity,source_root_slot_generation_and_digest,source_root_publication_evidence:RootPublicationEvidence,prepared_owner_or_operation_identity,continuity_profile_id,continuity_head_digest_and_cas_version,member_lease_generation_and_scope?,action_bound_continuity_observation_digest?,irreversible_action_digest,receipt_nonce,signer_key_id_and_epoch}` and signs the header plus the receipt-specific body. `PayloadReceipt`, prepared durability, Raft acceptance/acknowledgement, and member physical-operation receipts cannot omit or redefine these fields. The deterministic source evidence closes the crash gap without a self or future receipt reference. A receipt issued from unreachable staging is not authoritative merely because its signature verifies.

An external KMS/HSM/archive/transparency/provider cannot attest a local member fence and therefore never fabricates that header. Its request carries the sole `ExternalActionRequestBinding` defined above; the provider signs or MACs that complete binding in every status result and terminal artifact. `ExternalTerminalArtifactRef<Contract>` is the generated closed union `Receipt{typed_receipt_ref}|PermanentRefusal{typed_refusal_tombstone_ref}|ConflictNoEffect{typed_conflict_evidence_ref}` for exactly that dispatch contract. A local `ExternalTerminalImportRecord<Contract,Role> {role_and_group,header:FenceBoundIrreversibleReceiptHeader,dispatch_attempt_commitment,external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{dispatch_contract_id=Contract},terminal_artifact:ExternalTerminalArtifactRef<Contract>,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<Contract>>,provider_status_lineage_commitment,exact_artifact_evidence_request_role_and_current_authority_match_digest,import_body_digest}` proves which current fenced member and exact role imported the external evidence. `Role` is generated from the contract row and cannot be erased or cross-decoded; Shard is inhabitable only for rows whose source gate and authority projection explicitly permit Shard. Final Semantic/Protocol transitions consume the contract-typed, role-typed portable artifact, portable terminal evidence, and this import commitment as required by that same registered dispatch contract. External artifacts, portable terminal evidence, and local import records are distinct schemas and are never substituted; two contracts sharing one action kind have disjoint encodings and cannot satisfy one another.

Takeover validates history explicitly. `TakeoverAdoptableReceiptRef<Role>` and `TakeoverAdoptableObligationRef<Role>` are generated closed unions over every receipt/owner class registered for that role. `HistoricalReceiptValidation<Role>` is `{receipt_ref:TakeoverAdoptableReceiptRef<Role>,rooted_owner_ref:TakeoverAdoptableObligationRef<Role>,selected_prior_manifest_identity,selected_prior_slot_generation_and_digest,prepared_or_consensus_membership_proof,complete_closure_reopen_proof,configuration_and_signing_key_validity_proof,issuance_fence_validation:DirectPriorEpoch|PriorTakeoverAdoptionChain{predecessor_adoption_identity_and_checkpoint_proof},continuity_ancestry_proof?,validation_digest}`.

The prebuilt `TakeoverPreservedObligationInventory<Role>` is `{prior_manifest_identity:WeakDigest,prior_slot_generation_and_digest,exact_prior_semantic_protocol_prepared_consensus_root_commitments,sorted_historical_receipt_validations[HistoricalReceiptValidation<Role>],sorted_current_owner_refs[TakeoverAdoptableObligationRef<Role>],complete_receipted_and_ordered_obligation_bijection_proof,current_continuity_head_and_lease_commitment,body_digest}` and explicitly excludes any takeover record, future RaftStateRoot, manifest, or slot. `TakeoverAdoptionRecord<Role>` is then `{inventory_ref:StrongRef<TakeoverPreservedObligationInventory<Role>>,old_fence_epoch,new_fence_epoch,fence_takeover_operation_id,source_root_publication_evidence:RootPublicationEvidence,body_digest:BodyDigest<TakeoverAdoptionRecord<Role>>}`. It contains only the weak prior manifest identity already authenticated by the inventory and does not retain an unbounded manifest chain or a future “resulting root” commitment.

Consensus state owns `TakeoverAdoptionHead<Role> = None|Detailed{record_ref:StrongRef<TakeoverAdoptionRecord<Role>>}|Compacted{checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<TakeoverAdoptionCheckpoint<Role>>}`. Every rooted receipted or ordered obligation appears exactly once in the selected inventory/checkpoint. A direct prior-epoch receipt proves membership in the selected Prepared/Consensus/certificate closure; an older receipt needs an unbroken adoption identity/checkpoint chain, and ExternalCas evidence must be an authenticated ancestor of the current continuity head. `TakeoverAdoptionCheckpoint<Role>` is `{covered_adoption_record_digest:WeakDigest,covered_fence_epoch_range,latest_continuity_commitment,sorted_still_live_owner_refs[TakeoverAdoptableObligationRef<Role>],sorted_terminal_or_transferred_dispositions,complete_covered_obligation_bijection_proof,predecessor_checkpoint_digest:WeakDigest?,body_digest}`. It may replace Detailed only after every covered receipt/owner is terminal or represented by an exact current owner/ref; an activated cut may then release the prior manifest/inventory/record closure. Invalid, missing, duplicated, or unrooted evidence makes takeover recovery-only/read-only; it is never silently abandoned. Step 7 above constructs inventory -> adoption record -> successor Consensus/Raft root with Detailed head -> RootManifest/RootSlot before the new writer token can exist.

Brand-new creation is the sole separate initialization transition. Under an accepted filesystem profile, `CreateNew` opens the directory without symlinks, creates a uniquely named staging root inode with `O_CREAT|O_EXCL`, acquires its whole-inode lease, and receives a `PendingCreationGuard` limited to constructing the complete Genesis closure and valid epoch-1 root slot. It fully writes and syncs that staging inode, verifies it in place, then atomically promotes it with same-directory `renameat2(RENAME_NOREPLACE)` (or the registered equivalent), syncs the parent directory, re-resolves and verifies that `manifest.root` is the same leased inode, rereads/authenticates the slot, and only then consumes the guard into the ordinary writer token. A racing loser never truncates or adopts the winner's file; its staging inode remains unreachable and is eligible only for generation/name-scoped recovery cleanup. A crash before promotion leaves no canonical `manifest.root`; a crash after the directory sync leaves a complete valid one. There is no empty/partial canonical tomb and no “empty file means ours” state.

Every ordinary segment/object/root seal, immutable-root build, prepared-root publication, `PayloadReceipt`, Raft proposal or follower acknowledgement, checkpoint/certificate publication, and `RootSlot` write compares the local `FenceToken` against the live inode lease and published epoch immediately before its irreversible step. Under `ExternalCas` it also revalidates the exact external continuity head/CAS version and a live member-bound `ClusterIncarnationLease` with the required scope; under `DirectoryBound`, the guard proves the unchanged creation directory/inode and cannot authorize cross-directory same-identity recovery. `CommitCx` contains the nonconstructible combined local/incarnation guard. The only pre-token writes are the type-restricted fence-only/Genesis/role-transition/restore operations of their named pending guards; their encoders reject every other object/root kind and consume the guard on promotion. Losing a lease, seeing a higher epoch/incarnation/CAS version, expired external authority, or observing path/inode/profile/visibility drift poisons the guard and fails closed. Local fence epoch is excluded from canonical capsule/marker/state-root bytes, while cluster incarnation and service visibility are canonical domain fields, so replicas agree. Read-only multi-process opens remain supported.

### 5.2 Commit protocol

Single-node and replicated commits use the same state machine; single-node mode is the quorum-one specialization. The durable unit is **not** the user's unresolved intent log. It is a post-rebase, post-constraint, post-SSI canonical effect capsule.

No caller can submit bare mutation intents. `fgdb-authz-types` defines a nonconstructible `AuthorizedMutationPermit` produced only by the trusted policy verifier. It binds database security namespace/incarnation, tenant/principal/issuer, authority digest and non-widenable operation ceiling, policy-semantics ObjectId, policy and revocation indices, root/snapshot and branch scope, exact read `GraphReferenceBinding[]` and write `CoordinateSchemaBinding[]`, topology epoch, expiry, channel/session binding where applicable, and a unique nonce. The coordinator verifies the permit's provenance and scope, recomputes authorization over the **final** effects and any `CoordinateSchemaTransition[]`, and orders the authorization decision against revocation/policy changes. Warden administers issuance later, but the verifier, permit type, and fail-closed revocation interface are W1/W2 dependencies—not optional server middleware.

Re-evaluable intents bind `IntentSemanticsOid`, the content digest of intent-schema version, `language_contract_oid`, semantic/scalar profiles, binder/normalizer version, and every procedure/UDF/module artifact that influenced intent construction. The merge ladder evaluates canonical typed intents under this object, never reparses source text under a new corrigendum or server default; if an artifact is unavailable, rebase fails closed.

1. **Propose.** A writer submits `TxnProposal { snapshot, expected_write_coordinates[], intent_semantics_oid, intents, predicate_witnesses, authorized_mutation_permit, ephemeral_reservation, requested_durable_charge_effects, txn_token }`. It may precompute physical candidates, but no candidate capsule is publishable truth. `txn_token` and every server handle are scoped as §13/App. D require.
2. **Order and validate.** The WriteCoordinator deterministically orders a group, verifies permit provenance/scope, live writer lease/fence, schema/topology/policy/revocation epochs, branch write/envelope-key epochs, resource reservation, and expected branch heads; then it performs FCW/SSI validation and the merge ladder when necessary. Each accepted transaction is evaluated against the committed basis plus earlier accepted effects in that group. A rebase produces new reads, writes, constraints, effects, and key-epoch bindings; it never blesses an old capsule.
3. **Canonicalize final effects.** Verify and preserve every previously ordered escaping allocation, then resolve only still-confined temporary IDs and generated keys; evaluate CAS/upsert/no-op behavior; expand cascades; compute explicit logical retractions and insertions with `BirthOrdinal`s only for genuinely new identities; preserve source origin and assign sequence-neutral `BranchAdmissionOrdinal {merge_ordinal,element_id}` for imports; recompute final authorization including hidden-state cascades; validate constraints and the authoritative durable-quota ledger; materialize canonical `ResourceChargeEffect`/release effects; and rerun SSI over the final footprint. Rebase may preserve an escaping binding or reject the attempt; it may never remap an identity already returned, referenced by a later statement, or otherwise durably exposed. The result is a sequence-neutral `CommittedEffectSet` plus `LogicalDeltaTemplate` (§9.1), independent of block/run layout. The later canonical `MarkerRef` supplies `CommitSeq`, so apply derives `OriginBirthOrder`, `BranchAdmissionOrder {commit_seq,merge_ordinal,element_id}`, effect order, and ordered delta time without changing the approved capsule digest.
4. **Finalize capsules.** Persist one canonical `LogicalDeltaTemplate`, then construct `CommittedEffectCapsule { authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>, read_snapshot_basis, validated_apply_basis, intent_semantics_oid, read_graph_reference_bindings, validated_write_coordinate_bindings, validated_write_key_epoch_bindings, coordinate_schema_transitions, topology_epoch, policy_epoch, revocation_index, committed_effects:StrongRef<CommittedEffectSet<Local>>, final_read_witnesses, final_write_summary, constraint_evidence, authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Local>>, authorization_decision_digest, resource_transition_refs:StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Local>>[], resource_charge_effects, txn_outcome_preparation_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomePreparationRecord>, logical_delta_template_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaTemplate>, logical_delta_template_digest, optional_physical_accelerators, txn_token, final_effect_digest }`. The header, effect set, decision, resource transitions, outcome preparation, reservation, and command all byte-match one complete Local binding. A repeated digest is an integrity cross-check, never a replacement for the typed strong edge. The template digest must match the referenced bytes and the template must match the final effect set; neither field may appear without the other. Each write-key binding fixes the branch envelope/write epoch under which every referenced new ciphertext DEK is wrapped. Physical patches carry an apply-basis digest and may be ignored; unresolved or conditional commands are forbidden. Capsule builders return bytes and a digest to the coordinator, which verifies them against its approved final effects.
5. **Payload durability barrier D1 and stable prepared ownership.** Seal and make directory-durable the final semantic capsule **and its entire mandatory strong closure**: coordinate effect payloads, `LogicalDeltaTemplate`, schema/constraint/resource transitions, authorization decision, merge record, outcome-preparation record, active final-certification reservation when terminal, optional accelerator bytes actually referenced by the command, and every newly introduced semantics/module/profile object. HLC observation and expected order are intentionally excluded from this stable semantic base and live only in the repeatable order-attempt supplement below. Its immutable base inventory defines `PreparedOwnershipId = H("fgdb:prepared-owner:v1", storage_member, replica_namespace_or_incarnation, prepare_id, configuration_state_oid, payload_predicate_digest, base_closure_inventory_digest)`; a scalar configuration epoch is insufficient because same-epoch candidates/joint states can differ. On every replica or availability donor issuing a receipt, create an immutable replica-local `PreparedCommitRecord { prepared_ownership_id, prepare_id, record_generation, predecessor_ref, database/incarnation/configuration-ref/predicate/fence tuple, txn_token, capsule_ref, command_ref?, base_closure_inventory_digest, current_closure_inventory_digest, strong_logical_refs[], exact_realizations[{ object_or_subobject_id, encoding_id, placement_id, key_opener_ref }], status, receipt_nonce }`; its typed references and predecessor are strong. Every successor repeats the stable ownership ID and identical base closure, while its current closure is identical or a strict authenticated superset—for example after adding the completed command. `PreparedPayloadRoot` maps stable ownership ID to the latest record; no record is mutated in place. Insert the record, publish authenticated nonretaining locator entries, persist the updated `RootManifest`, and durably publish a `RootSlot` **before** D1 is complete or a payload receipt may be emitted. Pre-existing objects are revalidated and promoted into the prepared closure; a digest or locator without reachable authenticated bytes is insufficient. A `PayloadAvailabilityCertificate` covers this exact **pre-command semantic base closure** under the exact configuration ObjectId/predicate, including the quorum-one local predicate. Each receipt binds that config OID, predicate digest, stable ownership ID, and base-closure digest; an observed record OID/root generation is diagnostic freshness, never the sole ownership identity. Bytes not yet admitted to a prepared root remain staging data and are unreachable after a crash.
6. **Authorize, command, order, and publish barrier D2.** For each proposal/reproposal, the coordinator builds and D1-certifies a fresh `LocalOrderAttemptInput` over the stable semantic owner, Active final-certification reservation, expected next order, and `LocalHlcProposalEvidence`. Immediately before ordering, voters verify the supplement/certificate, reservation, prepared ownership/configuration and writer fence, selected terminal, and HLC observation age/source policy. They do **not** revalidate mutable heads, key/policy/revocation/conflict/resource bases outside the reservation: those were atomically validated and held before signing, and later changes cannot reinterpret them. The normative immutable `CommitCommand` is `{command_version,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,txn_token,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReservation>,required_reservation_state:Active,order_attempt_input_ref:StrongRef<LocalOrderAttemptInput>,order_attempt_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate>,capsule_ref:StrongRef<CommittedEffectCapsule>,logical_delta_template_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaTemplate>,authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Local>>,resource_transition_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>],txn_outcome_preparation_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomePreparationRecord>,payload_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PayloadAvailabilityCertificate>,expected_branch_heads,expected_branch_key_epochs,topology_epoch,policy_epoch,revocation_index,final_effect_digest}`. The header, authorization decision, final-certification reservation, and repeated authority fields byte-match one complete binding. The expected basis fields are redundant integrity projections that must byte-match the reservation, never fresh CAS gates. The semantic availability certificate covers the stable base; the order-attempt certificate separately covers only the repeatable order/HLC supplement. The coordinator publishes a semantic prepared-record successor that adds and strongly owns the completed command and marks the supplement Ordered in the same accepted transition. **Every durable database, including embedded and server single-node posture, orders through the same internal Raft log; single node is a one-member/quorum-one configuration.** Only `:memory:` runs the same state machine without a durability claim. After log acceptance, the Raft closure and certificate donors retain the command/base/supplement closures through apply and compaction. A semantic prepared owner may abandon only an unreceipted, provably unordered attempt under a current writer/configuration fence, or a receipted attempt after consensus proves it cannot be ordered and every certificate/configuration retention floor has expired. Once any terminal audit lock exists, neither semantic prepared ownership nor final certification may be abandoned; only a provably unordered stale supplement may be retired/replaced. Timeout or process death alone is never abandonment proof.

   Normatively, the inline `HlcProposalEvidence` union below is only a role-shape shorthand. Its two durable object kinds are `LocalHlcProposalEvidence {database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,role:Local,configured_source_id,source_profile_oid,signed_observation,observation_uncertainty,normalization_policy_oid,observation_body_digest}` and `MetaHlcProposalEvidence {database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,role:Meta,configured_source_set_and_epoch,canonically_sorted_signed_observations,required_quorum,observation_uncertainties,normalization_policy_oid,observation_body_digest}`; each registered transcript covers every field except the repeated body digest. The evidence is prebuilt, included in the separately D1-certified `OrderAttemptInput`, and reached by the ordered Local or Meta command only through that exact supplement. No command carries a parallel scalar or observation list, and apply derives its HLC solely from the previous persisted HLC plus the supplement's referenced object. Signature domain, source eligibility, and role are verified from ordered bytes; shards have no such object or semantic-HLC field.

   Local transaction apply also materializes the exact self-contained `LogicalDeltaBatch<LocalCommitted>` before constructing the candidate payload, verifies it against the committed capsule/template/marker, inserts `(next_commit_seq -> batch_ref)` into `LocalDeltaBatchIndex`, and advances `local_delta_frontier` to that same commit sequence in the **same** state transition. The batch is therefore reachable from the candidate root at the first crash point at which the commit exists; there is no separate Local delta-publish command or unrooted interval. Under required audit, the candidate index/frontier remain applied-but-not-visible in their exact pipeline entry until a gap-free audit-visibility advance releases them, while recovery owns the batch through `RaftStateRoot` and the pipeline. `LocalTerminalCompletionSpec` accepts its delta postcondition only when the visible payload's index contains that exact marker/batch and its frontier is at least the commit sequence. Rematerialization and retry must reproduce identical bytes; a missing, duplicate, gapped, wrong-marker, or wrong-frontier insertion fails apply.

   `HlcProposalEvidence` is the closed role union `Local{configured_source_id,source_profile_oid,signed_observation,observation_uncertainty,normalization_policy_oid}|Meta{configured_source_set_and_epoch,canonically_sorted_signed_observations,required_quorum,observation_uncertainties,normalization_policy_oid}`. The registered policy fixes source eligibility, quorum, interval-to-candidate rule, maximum accepted age, and `maximum_forward_jump` relative to the previous logical HLC; missing, duplicated, untrusted, stale, future-skewed, or arithmetically invalid observations reject the proposal. Shards never propose semantic HLC. Deterministic semantic apply verifies the command, closure, and evidence, assigns the next `LogicalCommandSeq`, computes `checked_increment(previous_logical_hlc)`, derives the one normalized candidate from the evidence, rejects a candidate beyond the checked forward-jump bound, and advances `logical_hlc_state=max(checked_increment,normalized_candidate)`. Any sequence/HLC overflow rejects before mutation and permanently fail-closes further semantic mutation against the persisted maximum; arithmetic never wraps or saturates. Transaction apply additionally assigns `CommitSeq`, builds the marker/results/payload/record/root, and transfers ownership before prepared terminalization. Other **semantic** schema/policy/revocation/accounting/key/audit/configuration/GC decisions use typed `ControlCommand` and advance LogicalCommandSeq/HLC but not CommitSeq. Protocol delivery disposition, audit visibility, prepared recovery, Raft hard-state, and physical-progress maintenance use the registered maintenance unions and advance none of those semantic counters.

   `ControlCommand.typed_payload_ref` always names a registered semantic sequence-neutral pre-order spec—Local transaction lifecycle uses begin/terminal/registration/statement/prepare/read-close/terminal-completion/outcome/cancel specs, and other semantic families include checkpoint install, ID/merge/DP, audit ticket/terminal/completeness, key/GC authorization, and configuration—not an applied result. `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec` and result activation/ACK/release/expiry/resume are maintenance inputs and can never inhabit `ControlCommand`. Every comparison CAS is nonretaining; applied records cannot name their future command record. Every semantic apply appends its candidate to `LocalAuditVisibilityPipeline`; RootManifest owns the applied root, protocol root, and pipeline. A NotRequired/StructurallyInapplicable candidate may be made visible in the same composite publication only if no earlier gap exists; otherwise it remains applied-hidden. A Required candidate remains hidden until the external batch receipt and quorum-durable protocol maintenance advance release the gap-free prefix. The client receives semantic completion only after configured durability, durable outcome, protected-audit release, and final RootSlot publication; later revocation fences subsequent work without rewriting a committed result.

`txn_token` is the opaque durable idempotency identity inside §13's MACed `TxnOutcomeCapability`, scoped to database security namespace/incarnation/service-visibility epoch, tenant/principal, authority ceiling, and an issuance/expiry interval. Local 1.0 has the same durable BEGIN, explicit multi-statement, autocommit, result-replay, COMMIT/ROLLBACK, and status semantics later used by W12, but uses the closed Local object family in Appendix A rather than pretending the W12 Meta/shard family exists. A BEGIN first orders `LocalBeginReservationSpec` and exposes `BEGIN_ACCEPTED` only after `Reserved` plus `TxnOutcomeRecord::BeginStaging` are visible; registration atomically binds that reservation, operation-audit owner, workspace generation zero, and `TxnOutcomeRecord::Registered`. Autocommit uses `LocalAttemptRegistrationSpec::Autocommit` and the same lifecycle without emitting BEGIN frames. Every statement is durably Open before execution and terminally Published, Failed, or Abandoned; successful statements advance one predecessor-linked workspace generation, while result bytes remain spill-backed under a nonexpiring `PendingVisibility` retention hold. The gap-free audit-visibility transition changes that owner only to `ReleasedAwaitingActivation`; a distinct fresh `ProtectedOutputActivationSpec` later creates the first delivery time basis, lease, and capability. Read-write commit first orders `LocalPrepareAdmissionSpec`, then builds the ordinary final capsule and unchanged `CommitCommand`; read-only completion uses `LocalReadCloseSpec`. Cancellation/rollback uses strengthened `TxnAbortSpec`, never an out-of-band coordinator flag.

`TxnOutcomeIndex` is therefore the sole Local token/capability lookup and attempt-lifecycle authority, mapping each scoped token to `Detailed{outcome_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeRecord>}|Compacted{summary_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnOutcomeSummaryRoot>,leaf_key_digest}|ExpiredTombstone{token_digest}`. `TxnOutcomeRecord` is predecessor-linked and moves only through `BeginStaging -> Registered/StatementOpen -> PrepareAdmitted -> CommittedPendingPostconditions -> Committed`, or the typed `Aborted`, `ReadClosed`, and `NeverRegistered` terminals described in Appendix A. An identical retry joins and returns the original durable state; a mismatched request, authority, epoch, token, idempotency key, statement, or expected predecessor fails closed. Before any terminal result is returned, the exact semantic outcome and required durability/delta/result/audit/resource postconditions reach `TerminalReady`. Records, compact summaries, permanent token tombstones, and verifier keys remain through the advertised lookup/retry/retention floor. After expiry a valid-MAC matching-domain capability returns `TokenExpired`; it is never treated as a new transaction. Cancellation after terminal ordering may return `Unknown(txn_token)` only when the caller lacks enough evidence to distinguish the already durable state, and resolves solely through scoped status lookup—it never promises rollback of a possibly committed transaction.

The protocol has two **durability barriers**, not literally two syscalls: each barrier includes every file and parent-directory sync required by the platform. Group commit amortizes both. Recovery starts from the highest valid root slot, reconciles `PreparedPayloadRoot` with the one-member or multi-member Raft log, completes every committed-but-unapplied command, and only then considers fenced abandonment/collection of prepared records. The complete Local/Meta audit pipeline is recovered from RootManifest and the Raft closure; its gap-free candidate chain remains retained and invisible beyond the visible endpoint while recovery reconciles external terminal records/batches and may order the exact visibility-advance command. Recovery verifies both applied and visible endpoints, the authoritative checkpoint, and the contiguous logical-command suffix; for transaction records it additionally verifies the contiguous transaction-marker subsequence, authenticates and decodes every referenced final capsule, and applies canonical effects. RaptorQ may repair damaged compatible symbols within the configured erasure model; it does not replace root redundancy, payload replication, or fail-closed recovery beyond that model.

### 5.3 Snapshots

A read snapshot is the posture-closed runtime `ReadSnapshot<Role> {database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,role_and_group,service_visibility_epoch,selected_slot:{slot_generation,slot_digest,root_manifest_identity},visible_semantic_cut:{state_root_ref,visible_logical_command_seq,visible_commit_high_seq,visible_raft_index,visible_pipeline_order?},protocol_cut:{protocol_state_ref,maintenance_generation,maintenance_applied_index,visible_endpoint_binding_commitment,external_continuity_head_commitment?,security_barrier_state},manifest_visible_endpoint_proof,graph_reference_bindings[],topology_epoch,authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,snapshot_retention_lease:SnapshotRetentionLease}`. `SnapshotRetentionLease` is nonserializable and cannot be placed in canonical bytes. Local/Meta `manifest_visible_endpoint_proof` authenticates `RootSlot -> RootManifest -> AuditVisibilityProtocolState -> AuditVisibilityPipeline.visible endpoint -> exact visible Semantic root/positions`; acquisition rejects `security_barrier_state=AppliedHidden` and never follows the applied tail as a public read basis. The Shard arm instead authenticates its `ShardProtocolState`, exact imported Meta visibility/security projection, and certified visible global prefix. The snapshot authority arm must byte-match the secure-view permit/admission, purpose, tenant/principal/ceiling, and every graph reference; an equal policy root under another authority is not substitutable. `visible_logical_command_seq` fixes the exposed canonical state-machine prefix; transaction-only `visible_commit_high_seq` fixes graph system time. Bindings are unique by `binding_role_id` (not graph/branch), and every selected head is at or below its role's visible high sequence; repeated historical roles acquire distinct ancestry/schema/authorization evidence.
