`DpSeedMaterial` is the exact secret logical plaintext target `{format_version=1,privacy_accounting_domain_id,request_key_digest,mechanism_contract_identity_and_digest,renyi_accounting_profile_identity_and_digest,noise_derivation_profile_oid,seed_bytes:[u8;32],body_digest}`. `seed_commitment = BLAKE3("fgdb.dp-seed-commitment.v1" || canonical(all fields except body_digest))`; every derived draw uses `BLAKE3_keyed(seed_bytes,"fgdb.dp-draw.v1" || request_key_digest || mechanism_contract_digest || draw_domain_tag || checked_draw_ordinal)` and never consumes the runtime/lab replay seed. The object has one secret-classified `logical_object_kinds.toml` row, a maximum encoded size of 512 bytes, a registered BodyDigest/AAD transcript, deterministic public synthetic test vectors, mutation/cross-profile tests, and a fuzz decoder whose corpus contains no production entropy. Production `seed_bytes` may exist only in the nonspillable secret arena or authenticated ciphertext; it is redacted from debug formatting, metrics, audit payloads, errors, crashpacks, core-dump profiles, and decision cards, and is synchronously overwritten on every success/error/cancel/panic ownership path. Any platform-specific locked-memory or compiler-barrier implementation lives in the named unsafe-boundary ledger with a scalar safe fallback; inability to obtain the configured secret-memory profile fails before preparation rather than silently weakening it.

Before preparation, `CryptoCx` creates exactly one `DpSeedEnvelope<Role:AuthorityOwningRole> {authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,request_key_ref:StrongRef<DpRequestKey<Role>>,seed_profile_oid,entropy_source_profile_oid,seed_commitment,seed_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<DpSeedMaterial>,sorted_key_wrap_refs[StrongRef<DpSeedKeyWrap>],noise_derivation_profile_oid,mechanism_contract_ref,body_digest}`. The header byte-matches the request and every opener/wrap scope. The plaintext is production entropy independent of lab/replay seeds; every draw is domain-separated. Public certificates contain only the seed commitment and authority public commitments.

External accounting is the sole irreversible debit authority. `DpDebitDerivationCertificate<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,request_key_ref:StrongRef<DpRequestKey<Role>>,mechanism_contract_ref:StrongRef<DpMechanismContract>,renyi_accounting_profile_ref:StrongRef<RenyiAccountingProfile>,profile_grid_and_fixed_point_digest,principal_ceiling_basis_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyCeilingBasis>,neighboring/contribution/sensitivity commitments,derived_debit:RenyiDebitVector,derivation_checker_version,proof_artifact_ref,body_digest}`. Every referenced basis byte-matches the one header and request. `PrivacyChargeAuthorization` is the portable signed `{privacy_accounting_domain_id,privacy_principal_or_alias_domain_commitment,request_key_digest,canonical_request_digest,mechanism_contract_identity_and_digest,renyi_accounting_profile_identity_and_digest,maximum_authorized_debit,ceiling_basis_digest,authority_binding_public_commitment,security_state_binding_public_commitment,tenant_principal_ceiling_purpose_commitment,allowed_snapshot_graph_disclosure_and_answer_commitment,authorization_audit_commitment,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before:AuthorityInstant,expires_at:AuthorityInstant,signer_epoch,signature}`. It contains no local `StrongRef`, policy root, revocation root, audit ticket, or `SecurityStateBinding`; the local charge operation record owns the exact request/certificate/decision and proves each public commitment. `PrivacyRequestTombstoneLeaf` is the minimal `{nonce_key_digest,request_key_digest,canonical_request_digest,charge_operation_id,debit_digest,seed_commitment}`; it stores no plaintext query/parameters/snapshot. Forensic detail is a separately encrypted, access-controlled, expiring record and is never retained merely by the permanent tombstone.

`PortablePrivacyChargeRequest` is the reference-free pre-effect `{privacy_accounting_domain_id,client_nonce_key:DpClientNonceKey,canonical_request_digest,charge_operation_id,request_key_identity_and_digest,privacy_principal_and_alias_commitment,mechanism_contract_identity_and_digest,renyi_accounting_profile_identity_and_digest,profile_grid_and_ceiling_basis_digest,renyi_debit:RenyiDebitVector,debit_derivation_certificate_identity_and_digest,seed_commitment,request_tombstone_leaf_bytes_and_digest,authorization_bytes_and_digest,request_body_digest}`; `charge_operation_id` depends on the stable nonce/key/contract/profile/debit/seed commitment, not a CAS attempt. Local `PrivacyChargeOperationRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,request_key_ref:StrongRef<DpRequestKey<Role>>,mechanism_contract_ref:StrongRef<DpMechanismContract>,renyi_accounting_profile_ref:StrongRef<RenyiAccountingProfile>,debit_derivation_certificate_ref:StrongRef<DpDebitDerivationCertificate<Role>>,request_tombstone_leaf_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyRequestTombstoneLeaf>,authorization_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyChargeAuthorization>,portable_request_ref:StrongRef<PortablePrivacyChargeRequest>,portable_request_digest,charge_operation_id,external_dispatch_operation_recipe,body_digest}`. Every identity/digest in the portable request must byte-match its local owner. `DpTransitionSpec::ArmCharge` selects ChargeRequested and owns this exact operation record before a linear `ExternalPrivacyChargeSendGuard {armed_request_record_ref,operation_record_ref,current_external_head_observation_ref,portable_request_ref,nonce_binding_commitment,continuity/time/action guards,unconsumed}` can contact the registry. Only unarmed Prepared may abandon. Timeout/crash after arming leaves ChargeRequested; recovery queries/retries the same operation ID and bytes.

The external registry permanently indexes nonce bindings, operation decisions, and request tombstones. A new valid request atomically checks/inserts the nonce binding, adds exactly one debit and tombstone, and constructs the direct successor reference-free `PrivacyContinuityRecord {privacy_accounting_domain_id,privacy_epoch,accounting_profile_identity_and_digest,cumulative_renyi_state_commitment,client_nonce_binding_root,request_tombstone_root,predecessor_digest,external_registry_id,cas_version,status:Active|Frozen|Retired,last_transition:Genesis|Charge{charge_operation_id,nonce_key_digest,request_key_digest,charge_request_digest,debit_digest,seed_commitment,tombstone_leaf_digest}|Administrative{transition_bytes_and_digest},issued_at,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}` and then reference-free `ExternalPrivacyChargeReceipt` binding both head bytes/digests/versions/profile identities, nonce/key/op/debit/seed, inclusion proofs, request binding, and signatures. Retry returns identical bytes without another debit; nonce/key/operation drift fails permanently. Portable `PrivacyContinuityAdministrativeTransition` is exactly `Freeze{reason,authorization_bytes_and_digest,effective_epoch}|Retire{replacement_or_terminal_basis,complete_live_charge_and_lease_disposition,authorization_bytes_and_digest}|AccountingProfileChange{predecessor_profile_identity_and_digest,successor_profile_identity_and_digest,grid_conversion:Forbidden|ExactRegisteredEmbedding,ceiling_carry_proof,authorization_bytes_and_digest}`. It contains no `StrongRef` or local locator. No refund transition exists.

The sole Chronicle import is bounded `PrivacyContinuityImportSpec<Role>` for Local or Meta with exact expected current portable head, local ledger/request-index, current profile refs, and a nonempty direct-successor chain. `PrivacyContinuityImportStep<Role>` is the closed union `Charge{prior_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyContinuityRecord>,returned_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyContinuityRecord>,receipt_ref:StrongRef<ExternalPrivacyChargeReceipt>,operation_record_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyChargeOperationRecord<Role>>,disposition:BindChargeRequested{expected_request_record_ref,expected_seed_envelope_ref}|RestoreBindRetainedSeed{expected_predecessor_record_ref,seed_envelope_ref,reconciliation_evidence_ref}|ImportSameNamespaceMaterialPending{request_absence_or_backup_cut_proof_ref,reconciliation_evidence_ref}|AccountingOnlyForeignNamespace{foreign_namespace_id}}|AdministrativeFreeze{prior_ref,returned_ref,transition_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyContinuityAdministrativeTransition>,freeze_authorization_ref}|AdministrativeRetire{prior_ref,returned_ref,transition_ref,complete_local_nonterminal_disposition_proof_ref}|AccountingProfileChange{prior_ref,returned_ref,transition_ref,successor_profile_ref:StrongRef<RenyiAccountingProfile>,exact_grid/ceiling_carry_proof_ref}`. Steps form one direct-successor chain. Apply recomputes every nonce/Rényi/tombstone/admin transition, validates every portable identity against its local typed owner, creates `DpChargeBinding {role,request_key_ref,nonce_key_digest,charge_operation_record_ref,receipt_ref,prior_and_returned_continuity_refs,accounting_profile_ref,debit_digest,seed_commitment,tombstone_inclusion_proof_ref,source_import_spec_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyContinuityImportSpec<Role>>,assigned_role_order,body_digest}`, advances the exact `PrivacyLedgerState`, and selects the role-valid request/admin states. The preexisting spec and assigned scalar order replace any forbidden reference to the future applied record. A frozen/retired head forbids new Prepare/ArmCharge and profile drift cannot reinterpret an old vector. There is no RecordCharge command.

The exact request machine is `Prepared -> AbandonedBeforeCharge|ChargeRequested`; `ChargeRequested -> ChargeRefused|ChargedIdle|ChargedMaterialPending`; `ChargedMaterialPending -> ChargedIdle|UnavailableAfterCharge`; `ChargedIdle -> Computing(generation)`; and `Computing -> ChargedIdle|Computing(generation+1)|ResultCommitted`. `DpRequestRecord<Role>` remains predecessor-weak and state-shaped. `ResultCommitted` stores `{charge_binding_ref,seed_envelope_ref,committed_output_ref:StrongRef<DpCommittedOutput<Role>>,delivery_id,result_detachment_commitment}`—never a strong protocol-retention ref back from semantic state. The transition union remains Prepare/Abandon/Arm/Refusal/Attach/Unavailable/Start/Yield/Reclaim/Commit/Compact; exact restore/import dispositions are the only way to select ChargedMaterialPending. No state is Delivered/Acknowledged/Released.

`PrivacyExecutionLease<Role>` is the signed `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,request_key_ref,charge_binding_ref,accounting_profile_ref,privacy_epoch,allowed_snapshot_and_graph_bindings,mechanism_debit_and_seed_commitments,compute_generation,writer_or_consensus_fence,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,signer_epoch,signature}`. `DpComputeOwnerLease` adds owner/resource reservation and strongly selects this lease. Reclaim requires exact expiry/fencing evidence and checked generation increment. The nonserializable `DpSensitiveAccessGuard` exists only from the current Computing record plus matching admission/charge/seed/complete authority/execution lease. In W12 Meta alone owns seed/noise; `GlobalDpExecutionGrant` and `DpShardAggregateFragment` have exact registered certificate schemas binding request/charge/generation/profile/mechanism, clipped shape, shard/configuration, security projection, idempotency digest, and no seed/public-output authority. Meta verifies the complete participant set and applies one seed.

DP output uses the ordinary cross-plane result machine. `DpOutputCandidate<Role>` binds current Computing, charge/execution/admission/release evidence, manifest/digest/contracts, and delivery policy. CommitResult atomically constructs the semantic `DpCommittedOutput`/ResultCommitted detachment plus the Protocol PendingVisibility owner and resource escrow in one manifest publication; neither plane strongly points back to its owner. The generated result-owner registry supplies the exact `DpResult` arm everywhere. Required audit creates no lease/capability; visibility maintenance releases it to ReleasedAwaitingActivation, and an independent fresh activation later creates the first lease/capability without blocking the audit prefix. Later delivery maintenance never changes the DP request and cannot redraw/recharge. `DpSpentTombstone` permanently retains only nonce/request/op/debit/receipt/seed/output-or-no-output/result-disposition commitments; plaintext query/parameters and seed ciphertext follow separate profiled forensic/key-retention floors.

`DpRequestIndex<Role>` maps the key to Detailed or a permanent `DpSpentTombstone` retaining key, operation, debit/receipt, seed commitment, output/no-output commitment, and result disposition commitment. Compaction cannot select a charged nonterminal state or release an independently retained result/forensic object. Same-identity restore remains hidden while it imports every post-backup external debit: matching retained seed becomes ChargedIdle, exhaustively missing material becomes UnavailableAfterCharge, and ResultCommitted must reconstruct its ordinary result index or fail as corrupt. Clone shares the source accounting domain unless threshold-authorized `PrivacyDomainSplitAuthorization` proves disjoint populations. Permanent tests cover fixed-point accounting, alias composition, arm-before-send, lost external replies, every seed/CAS/import crash point, no-access-before-guard compile failures, compute fencing, W12 fragment completeness/no-shard-noise, audit visibility and ordinary result races, seed secrecy, and backup-behind restore with retained/missing seeds/results.

### 12.8 Audit

Compliance audit is a separately retained, signed append-only chain—not “whatever commit history remains.” Signatures, sequence continuity, and anchoring prove **append integrity and post-ticket gap/truncation detection**, not that a buggy or compromised ingress could never omit an event before logging. Each same-identity lineage has a stable `AuditDomainId`. An external monotonic authority CAS-publishes reference-free `AuditContinuityRecord {database_security_namespace_id,audit_domain_id,audit_epoch,highest_ticket_sequence,ticket_index_commitment,unresolved_ticket_commitment,audit_chain_head_digest,predecessor_digest,external_registry_id,cas_version,status:Complete{profile_oid}|PermanentGap{latest_transition_bytes_and_digest,current_profile_oid},last_transition:TicketIssued{ticket_digest}|TicketsResolvedBatch{role_and_group,first_pipeline_order,last_pipeline_order,batch_digest,ordered_resolution_commitment,last_candidate_root_digest:WeakDigest}|CompletenessChanged{transition_bytes_and_digest},signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`, while retaining authenticated ticket/event/resolution/transition/CAS-receipt bytes or authenticated resolver references—not commitments alone. A one-ticket resolution is canonically `TicketsResolvedBatch` with a one-entry range; `TicketResolved` is not a second durable encoding. The record contains no `StrongRef`, consumer-local locator, or future receipt identity. The batch arm is one atomic direct CAS successor and is legal only for a nonempty contiguous role-pipeline range whose individually verified entries appear in the same canonical order.

An `AuditCompletenessProfile` names which operations require an externally reserved `AuditTicket` before protected processing. Ticket issuance first CAS-adds a pending ticket externally, then orders its local import through exact `AuditTicketAdmissionSpec { ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>, expected_prior_continuity_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>, expected_prior_cas_version, returned_continuity_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>, external_cas_receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuditExternalCasReceipt> }`. Admission verifies that the returned record and receipt are the direct `TicketIssued` CAS successor, advance the expected ticket sequence by one, add exactly this ticket to the ticket-index and unresolved commitments, and change no unrelated continuity field; orphan-ticket recovery uses the same relation. Protected processing begins only after that imported admission is audit-visible.

Protected construction has four explicit, noninterchangeable orders. **Local admitted transaction:** ticket admission/claim -> authorization/final rebase/outcome preparation -> ordered final-certification reservation plus coordinate/obligation holds -> sequence-neutral audit event/resolution bodies and immutable semantic plan -> replaceable event attempt/locks/shares/event -> replaceable resolution attempt/locks/shares/resolution -> triple-bearing `TerminalAuditGate` -> stable capsule/template/semantic prepared closure -> repeatable `LocalOrderAttemptInput` plus availability certificate -> command/order/candidate. **Local control or pre-effect terminal:** ticket/claim -> authorization -> `AuditTerminalFreezeSpec::Arm` plus generated control-basis hold -> the same body/semantic-plan/replaceable-attempt triple -> stable typed control base -> repeatable Local order supplement -> command/order/candidate. **W12 admitted write:** ticket/registration claim -> union authorization/resources/gate-free outcome preparation -> published admission -> shard fragments/prepares/constraints/serialization -> ordered Global final-certification reservation/holds -> audit semantic plan and replaceable event/resolution attempts -> gate -> stable Meta command base -> repeatable `MetaOrderAttemptInput` -> Global command/candidate. **Read in either posture:** ticket/claim -> read admission -> authorized traversal/buffered result -> release decision -> freeze/hold where state-changing publication requires it -> audit bodies/attempts/gate -> stable publication/read-close base -> role order supplement -> candidate -> external batch receipt -> gap-free `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec` (output becomes ReleasedAwaitingActivation) -> independent fresh `ProtectedOutputActivationSpec` -> output. Thus Local outcome preparation carries its eventual gate, W12 preparation never does, a read-release decision cannot authorize the traversal that produced it, time-authority failure cannot block the audit prefix, and neither HLC nor assigned order appears in signed audit bodies.

The event binds the exact ticket request, namespace/domain/profile/principal, authorization decision, and one tagged outcome commitment: `TransactionMutation { final_effect_digest, transaction_effect_basis_commitment }`, `ControlMutation { control_kind, spec_body_digest, expected_state_conditions_digest, semantic_transition_basis_commitment }`, canonical read result, denial, or stable failure/no-effect proof. `transaction_effect_basis_commitment` hashes only the sequence-neutral final effect, role-free coordinate/head expectations, schema/constraint/resource/authorization inputs, intent semantics, and transaction token; it explicitly excludes capsule/command/certificate identities, `TerminalAuditGate`, assigned sequence/HLC/marker identity, and chain hash. `spec_body_digest` hashes only canonical `SequenceNeutralSpec.spec_body`, excluding its terminal gate; `semantic_transition_basis_commitment` hashes the deterministic pre-order before/after relation expressed by that body and expected conditions, never an applied record/root identity. Event and resolution attestation times are pre-order signer observations, not the apply-assigned `logical_command_hlc`; expected sequence/state conditions make a stale prebuilt terminal fail closed. `AuditTicketResolution` is a tagged terminal in which `Completed`, `Denied`, and `Failed` each strongly name that exact event; `Abandoned` carries a signed typed no-effect reason and proof. There is no optional unbound `event_digest`.

State-changing apply verifies the complete staged signing chain and gate byte-for-byte and proves the later full spec/capsule/command/marker/result is the unique deterministic extension of those sequence-neutral commitments. Visibility uses a bounded ordered pipeline, not a role-global stop-the-world fence. `AuditVisibilityPipelineEntry<Role>` is `{pipeline_order,role_order,raft_index,predecessor_candidate_identity:WeakStateIdentity,candidate_state_root_ref:StrongRef<RoleStateRoot>,proposal_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,post_apply_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,origin_local_writer_fence_epoch,release:RequiredPending{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>,terminal_resolution_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketResolution>,gate_commitment}|NotRequiredReady{nonrequired_evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence}|AuditSystemReady{system_authority_evidence},candidate_publication_commitment,retained_closure_bytes,body_digest}`. `AuditVisibilityPipeline<Role>` is `{role_and_group,generation,visible_pipeline_order,visible_role_order,visible_raft_index,visible_state_root_ref:StrongRef<RoleStateRoot>,applied_tail_pipeline_order,applied_tail_role_order,applied_tail_raft_index,applied_tail_state_root_ref:StrongRef<RoleStateRoot>,ordered_entries[every pipeline order in (visible_pipeline_order,applied_tail_pipeline_order]],total_retained_bytes,entry_limit,byte_limit,external_continuity_head_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>,body_digest}`. `LocalAuditVisibilityPipeline` fixes `Role=Local`/`RoleStateRoot=LogicalStateRoot`; `MetaAuditVisibilityPipeline` fixes `Role=Meta`/`RoleStateRoot=GlobalStateRoot`; Shard is uninhabited. The pipeline is separately addressed and owned by RootManifest/Raft state—not by either state root—so construction is acyclic.

Every Local/Meta **semantic** logical command—Required, NotRequired, or audit-system scaffolding—applies to the pipeline's current applied semantic tail, constructs its complete command result/payload/record/candidate root first, and then constructs one pipeline successor. A NotRequired/AuditSystem-ready candidate may be released in that same composite publication only when no earlier gap exists; otherwise the successor appends exactly one entry while its effects remain unpublished. Entry `i` byte-matches candidate `i-1` as its semantic predecessor. `pipeline_order` and role logical-command order are strictly contiguous; entry Raft indexes are strictly increasing, not contiguous, because registered maintenance entries may intervene. Each entry carries `raft_gap_proof_ref:StrongRef<RaftGapClassificationProof>` covering every index since the previous semantic entry as one exact role-valid zero-semantic/pipeline-effect maintenance command; missing, duplicate, unclassified, or semantic skipped entries fail apply/recovery. Thus recovery still has one linear semantic candidate chain without lying about the consensus log. NotRequired/AuditSystem-ready entries cannot leapfrog an earlier Required entry. The visible root remains at the last released entry; ordinary mutation/recovery may continue from applied semantic state only under the security-prerequisite rule, while reads use visible state. At pipeline limits, user semantic work receives deterministic backpressure while audit/recovery/ownership-preserving maintenance uses reserved capacity. Prepared ownership, snapshots/checkpoints, reconfiguration, GC, backup, restore, and takeover retain and transfer both the queue and every gap proof. No candidate result/error/changefeed/system-graph/metric/ack is observable before release.

Security-affecting candidates are a deliberate v1 visibility barrier. `command_type_classification.toml` marks every policy, revocation, verifier/evaluator, grant, service-epoch, descriptor-visibility, time-authority, and other authorization-semantic mutation `security_barrier=true`. `AuditVisibilityPipeline` records `earliest_unreleased_security_barrier_order?` and its exact security-binding predecessor/successor commitments. Once such a candidate is applied but not visible, no later user/protected admission, graph observation, authorization decision, capability issuance/refresh, query execution, or data-dependent response may be ordered from the applied tail. Only audit advancement, recovery, already-admitted terminalization under its pinned non-widenable authority, and ownership-preserving maintenance may proceed; every output still reauthorizes against the visible current state. The public response to a newly attempted operation is the same bounded, data-independent audit-backpressure class used at pipeline saturation, never a policy-specific denial or existence signal. This conservative barrier means a hidden widening never grants authority, a hidden narrowing never produces a distinguishable early denial, and no later command can depend on a security state that the caller cannot yet observe. A future selective meet/intersection optimization requires a separate proved noninterference feature and is off in v1.

`AuditExternalResolutionBatch` is the pre-CAS portable `{audit_domain_id,role_and_group,expected_prior_continuity_digest,expected_prior_cas_version,first_pipeline_order,last_pipeline_order,ordered_required_entries[{pipeline_order,ticket_digest,event_digest,resolution_digest,candidate_root_digest}],interleaved_ready_entry_commitment,resulting_ticket_and_unresolved_commitments,resulting_audit_chain_head_digest,batch_digest}`. The external authority verifies every required entry and atomically installs the direct `TicketsResolvedBatch` successor, returning reference-free `AuditExternalBatchCasReceipt {authority_id,expected_head_digest,expected_cas_version,resulting_head_bytes_and_digest,resulting_cas_version,batch_digest,cas_before,cas_after,monotonic_counter,signatures}`. A single-ticket resolution is the same schema with a one-entry range. Partial acceptance is impossible: conflict returns no successor, and recovery rereads the authority head before retry. Batching may stop before any not-yet-resolved Required entry, but cannot omit/reorder a Required entry inside its range. Both objects are registered `PreOrderArtifact`/`ExternalCertificateInput`, never logical commands; `AuditTicketResolutionBatchSpec` does not exist.

The release plane is deliberately outside canonical semantic state. `AuditVisibilityProtocolState<Role>` is the manifest/Raft-maintenance-owned `{role_and_group,maintenance_generation,maintenance_applied_index,pipeline_ref:StrongRef<AuditVisibilityPipeline<Role>>,visible_audit_outcome_index_ref:StrongRef<VisibleAuditOutcomeIndex<Role>>,result_independent_retention_index_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionIndex<Role>>,subscription_cursor_delivery_index_ref:StrongRef<SubscriptionCursorDeliveryIndex<Role>>,pending_cursor_output_index_ref:StrongRef<PendingCursorOutputIndex<Role>>,artifact_output_delivery_index_ref:StrongRef<ArtifactOutputDeliveryIndex<Role>>,protected_error_delivery_index_ref:StrongRef<ProtectedErrorDeliveryIndex<Role>>,protocol_resource_escrow_index_ref:StrongRef<ProtocolResourceEscrowIndex<Role>>,external_continuity_head_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>,security_barrier_state:None|AppliedHidden{pipeline_order,predecessor_binding_commitment,successor_binding_commitment},nonretaining_predecessor_digest,body_digest}`. `VisibleAuditOutcomeIndex` maps each released ticket/candidate to its exact `AuditOutcomeRecord`, external batch receipt/member ordinal, visibility-advance maintenance identity, and public release commitment. It contains no unreleased outcome. The delivery indexes own their exact PendingVisibility/ReleasedAwaitingActivation/ActiveDeliverable/terminal records, capability lineage where applicable, typed resource escrow, and byte-retention closure. Canonical payloads own only semantic effect, audit admission/freeze/gate commitments, and an opaque detachment commitment/key; they never claim that a later release-time outcome, lease, capability, or delivery activation is already part of an earlier candidate root.

`AuditVisibilityProtectedOutputReleaseRecipe<Role>` is the closed union `Result{pending_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Role>>,release_subject_commitment}|SubscriptionInitialBaseline{pending_ref:StrongRef<SubscriptionCursorDeliveryRecord<Role>>,release_subject_commitment}|SubscriptionDelta{pending_ref:StrongRef<SubscriptionCursorDeliveryRecord<Role>>,release_subject_commitment}|SubscriptionReset{pending_ref:StrongRef<SubscriptionCursorDeliveryRecord<Role>>,release_subject_commitment}|ArtifactOutput{pending_ref:StrongRef<ArtifactOutputDeliveryRecord<Role>>,release_subject_commitment}|ProtectedError{pending_ref:StrongRef<ProtectedErrorDeliveryRecord<Role>>,release_subject_commitment}`. It contains no clock, time evidence, absolute window, lease, capability nonce, or activation recipe. `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Role>` is `{expected_protocol_state_ref:StrongRef<AuditVisibilityProtocolState<Role>>,expected_pipeline_ref:StrongRef<AuditVisibilityPipeline<Role>>,expected_maintenance_generation,expected_visible_pipeline_order,advance_through_pipeline_order,external_batch_receipt_refs[StrongRef<AuditExternalBatchCasReceipt>],ordered_entry_release_evidence,sorted_audit_outcome_recipes[AuditOutcomeRecipe<Role>],sorted_protected_output_release_recipes[AuditVisibilityProtectedOutputReleaseRecipe<Role>],complete_released_ticket_and_pending_output_bijection_proof_ref:StrongRef<ReleasedVisibilityBijectionProof<Role>>,expected_returned_continuity_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>,resulting_pipeline_body_recipe,resulting_protocol_state_body_recipe,system_authority_evidence:StructuralAuditExemption::AuditSystemAuthority}`. It is the **sole** ordered Local/Meta visibility input, but its command-contract class is `Maintenance` on the Protocol plane: it consumes one same-group Raft maintenance-log position, advances no LogicalCommandSeq/GlobalLogicalCommandSeq, CommitSeq, logical HLC, semantic command head, or semantic state root, and appends no pipeline candidate. `AuditTicketResolutionBatchSpec` and a semantic `AuditVisibilityAdvance` command do not exist.

When a later Local/Meta Semantic command must prove that an earlier candidate is already visible, it consumes a portable certificate rather than a Protocol-plane strong reference or “mirror.” `PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Role>` is inhabitable only for `Role=Local|Meta` and is `{authority_domain_projection,semantic_applied_identity,candidate_state_commitment,pipeline_order,release_kind:InlineAuditSystem|MaintenanceVisibilityAdvance,audit_release_commitment,resulting_visible_endpoint_commitment,resulting_protocol_state_commitment,root_manifest_and_slot_durability_commitment,authority_binding_public_commitment,certificate_attempt_and_signatures}`. The certificate is constructed only after the releasing RootManifest/RootSlot is durable, contains no local ObjectId reference to Protocol state, and is independently verified against the same-group configuration/signing chain. `SemanticVisibilityCertificateRef<Role>` is a typed strong reference only within the same Local/Meta Semantic group; a shard imports a specialized `CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>` or its typed Meta projection. Freeze, terminal completion, restore activation/service preparation, role transition, backup release, and any other later Local/Meta Semantic phase use this certificate. `freeze_mirror_record_ref`, audit visibility mirror records, and a direct Semantic edge to an audit pipeline/protocol record are invalid schema fields.

Shard has no audit-visibility pipeline. Its equivalent publication proof is `ShardStatePublicationCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,source_command_identity,source_applied_shard_raft_index,resulting_shard_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,resulting_protocol_state_commitment,current_imported_meta_authorization_visibility_commitment,configuration_and_topology_identity,writer_fence_and_continuity_commitment,root_manifest_and_slot_durability_commitment,root_publication_evidence:RootPublicationEvidence,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`. `CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>` is the generated closed role union `Local{visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local>>}|Meta{visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>}|Shard{publication_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ShardStatePublicationCertificate>}`. Cross-role users receive the matching certified-remote wrapper. Any subsystem that needs “the authorizing state is durably published” across all roles—including GC/key dispatch—uses this union; it never pretends a Shard candidate passed a nonexistent audit pipeline.

Maintenance apply verifies a gap-free prefix, direct external-head succession, every Required resolution, every NotRequired/system proof, exact candidate/configuration/fence identities, and one release recipe for every and only PendingVisibility protected output created by an entry entering the prefix. It then atomically (a) moves the pipeline visible endpoint to the last released candidate root even when a later unresolved suffix remains, (b) installs the exact release-time audit outcomes, (c) replaces each PendingVisibility owner with `ReleasedAwaitingActivation` carrying the immutable release evidence but still no clock/lease/capability, (d) advances the imported external continuity head, and (e) clears a security barrier exactly when its entry is released. The applied semantic tail and unresolved entries are unchanged. Time-authority unavailability, an expired prospective lease recipe, or missing capability bytes cannot block audit-prefix progress because none is an input. A ticket/output bijection mismatch or RootManifest/maintenance CAS change applies nothing. Matching retry returns the same maintenance result and protocol generation. A stale, overlapping, gapped, wrong-role, substituted candidate, partial batch, noncontiguous configuration chain, merely signed head, output-bijection mismatch, or attempted leapfrog fails.

`ProtectedOutputActivationSpec<Role>` is the separate Protocol-maintenance union `Result{released_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Role>>,time_basis_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryLeaseTimeBasis<Role>>,time_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,prebuilt_initial_resume_replay_ref:StrongRef<ResumeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|SubscriptionInitialBaseline{released_ref:StrongRef<SubscriptionCursorDeliveryRecord<Role>>,time_basis_ref:StrongRef<SubscriptionDeliveryLeaseTimeBasis<Role>>,time_evidence_ref,prebuilt_initial_resume_replay_ref}|SubscriptionDelta{released_ref,time_basis_ref,time_evidence_ref,prebuilt_successor_resume_replay_ref}|SubscriptionResetNotice{released_ref,time_basis_ref,time_evidence_ref,prebuilt_successor_resume_replay_ref}|SubscriptionResetBaseline{reset_ack_ref,time_basis_ref,time_evidence_ref,prebuilt_successor_resume_replay_ref}|ArtifactOutput{released_ref:StrongRef<ArtifactOutputDeliveryRecord<Role>>,time_basis_ref:StrongRef<ArtifactOutputLeaseTimeBasis<Role>>,time_evidence_ref,prebuilt_initial_resume_replay_ref}|ProtectedError{released_ref:StrongRef<ProtectedErrorDeliveryRecord<Role>>,time_basis_ref:StrongRef<ProtectedErrorLeaseTimeBasis<Role>>,time_evidence_ref}`. Each arm reauthorizes the exact released subject against the current complete security binding and current service authority, proves the full minimum delivery window, and CASes only that released record to its Active state. It has structural protocol-system authority and no recursive terminal audit gate. Missing/stale evidence delays only that output; recovery may build a fresh recipe without changing audit visibility or semantic state. Same-publication inline activation remains legal only for an already-visible NotRequired subject; Required release and activation are always distinct v1 maintenance transitions.

`RootManifest` publishes the new protocol root and visible endpoint in one D2 slot update after the maintenance Raft entry is committed/applied under the configured durability rule. Public semantic reads use the pipeline's visible candidate root; audit/status/output guards additionally require the matching current protocol release/activation entry. This two-plane join permits partial-prefix progress without pretending that release-time facts existed inside an older candidate or exposing a later unresolved candidate. Result ACK/release/expiry/resume are likewise Protocol-plane maintenance transitions over the independent index, so they can terminate transport/retention while an unrelated semantic audit suffix remains; their public replies use the same protocol maintenance durability and send guards. Recovery reconstructs the semantic candidate queue and protocol indexes from RootManifest, the maintenance log, prepared ownership, and exact external heads/receipts, then idempotently finishes committed maintenance before serving.

Required-audit throughput and latency are benchmarked as a separate governance profile with pipeline limits, batch size, external sequencer RTT, resolution throughput, visible-prefix lag, and head-of-line saturation reported. Baseline no-audit numbers never imply the same visibility latency. The pipeline amortizes external RTT through contiguous batches but does not claim to exceed the external authority's sustained resolution capacity; backpressure is the honest overload behavior.

Audit lifecycle transitions cannot recurse through their own gate. Semantic `AuditTicketAdmissionSpec` and narrowly scoped `AuditRecoverySpec`/`AuditCompletenessTransitionSpec` have `TerminalAuditGate::StructurallyInapplicable{AuditSystemAuthority}` in their sequence-neutral wrappers; `AuditTerminalSpec { ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>, expected_pending_ticket_digest:WeakDigest, event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>, terminal_resolution_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketResolution> }` consumes exactly that already-pending ticket and may not request another. `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Local|Meta>` is instead a Protocol-plane maintenance input with an explicit unforgeable system-authority arm and no `TerminalAuditGate` or semantic wrapper. `AuditTerminalSpec` is for non-mutating and pre-effect terminal paths, not a second mutation terminal. Every transaction command carries its one terminal gate in its transaction input, while every semantic control obtains its one Required, validated NotRequired, or StructurallyInapplicable gate only through its referenced spec wrapper; no command-level duplicate exists. If required external evidence is unavailable, gated work remains disabled.

Resumption first builds reference-free portable `AuditCompletenessTransitionRequest {audit_domain_id,expected_prior_continuity_digest,expected_prior_cas_version,exact_missing_ticket_or_event_interval,missing_commitment,evidence_or_reason,old_profile_identity_and_digest,new_profile_identity_and_digest,permanent_effective_audit_epoch,authorization_bytes_and_digest,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_request_digest,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. Local `AuditCompletenessTransitionOperationRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the pre-send owner `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,portable_request_ref:StrongRef<AuditCompletenessTransitionRequest>,expected_prior_continuity_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>,old_profile_ref:StrongRef<AuditCompletenessProfile>,new_profile_ref:StrongRef<AuditCompletenessProfile>,authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>>,external_dispatch_operation_recipe,body_digest}` and proves every portable identity/digest against its typed owner. Only after its role-valid publication evidence may universal dispatch send the portable request. The external authority CAS-installs the direct `CompletenessChanged` successor that embeds only the portable transition bytes/digest, then emits a separate reference-free `AuditExternalCasReceipt`.

`AuditCompletenessTransitionSpec<Role>` is `{operation_record_ref:StrongRef<AuditCompletenessTransitionOperationRecord<Role>>,expected_prior_continuity_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>,expected_prior_cas_version,returned_continuity_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>,external_cas_receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuditExternalCasReceipt>,dispatch_terminal_evidence_ref,exact_request_returned_head_receipt_and_profile_match_digest,terminal_audit_gate:StructurallyInapplicable{AuditSystemAuthority}}`; it imports that exact pair locally through the same pipeline rules. This order is acyclic: no portable pre-CAS object owns a local reference and no continuity record points to its later receipt or import. The continuity status and future profile validator permanently exclude the recorded gap; no later operator, restore, or profile change may reassert completeness across it. Semantic payload admission state plus protocol pipelines/outcomes, snapshots, configuration/GC/key floors, and backups retain the complete active chain and authenticated external locators. Same-identity restore reconciles it before ticket-gated operations resume. Only events admitted through this gate receive a source-completeness claim within the trusted-process threat model. Unticketed/sampled read telemetry is labeled non-complete.

---

## 13. Fabric: Server, Protocols, and the Embedded API

1. **Embedded**: `fgdb::Database::open(path | :memory:)` returns an incarnation/service-epoch-guarded handle only from an Operational root backed by the current service-promotion receipt; hidden restore and `RetiredLocal` reject ordinary open. Authorized sessions expose prepared statements and explicit transactions with begin idempotency, server-derived statement classification, pinned error policy, certified workspace generation, ownership renewal, and ordered transaction reattachment. Execution returns only an audit-visible `PublishedResultStream`. Explicit async `finish()` emits ACK after complete consumption; explicit async `release()` authenticates and orders the same `RESULT_RELEASE` transition as FGP; explicit `cancel_pending_send()` only stops not-yet-written transport. Rust `Drop` performs local structured-cancellation cleanup and sender detachment but **cannot authorize durable result release**; independently retained bytes remain resumable until ACK, explicit release, or profiled expiry. `commit()` returns `WriteCommitted|ReadClosed|Aborted|Unknown`, never a marker-shaped read close, and `status(TxnOutcomeCapability)` resolves BeginStaging, detailed/compacted NeverRegistered, in-progress, terminal-pending, and detailed/compacted terminal-ready outcomes identically. Rust-native typed accessors and capability-gated procedures obey the same authorization and epoch boundary. `fgdb-python` consumes the allowed `fnx-python` binding boundary rather than adding PyO3 directly to engine crates; ABI3/NumPy support remains isolated from durable/runtime layers. A claimed NumPy view is zero-copy only for matching contiguous authorized buffers and otherwise returns an explicit copy.
2. **`fgdbd` server**: multi-database, multi-tenant by capability; connection admission via semaphore + rate-limit combinators; graceful drain on shutdown (region close = no dropped in-flight commits, structurally).
3. **FGP (native wire protocol)** uses Appendix D's sole connection machine: protocol-only HELLO, principal authentication, protected database selection, then a posture/incarnation/service-receipt-bound Ready state. Every role-sensitive identity and dispatch is an exact `Local|Sharded` union. BEGIN, transaction reattach/renewal, autocommit, subscription creation, export, replay, result resume/ACK/release, unsubscribe, and E-to-E2 capability migration are durably idempotent. Every protected result, baseline, delta, proof, replay/export byte, cancellation explanation, and data-dependent error is buffered until its exact authorization and audit-visible release evidence exists, then consumes a fresh linear `PublicFrameSendGuard` immediately before each frame write. Transaction-bound statements are serialized in v1; unsupported savepoint, partial-rollback, or concurrent-statement profiles are rejected. Draining admits no new session-owned children and waits only for queued/writing transport obligations to become Sent/Failed, required audit publications, prepublication cancellations, admitted-decision recovery, terminal records, and durable detach/pause of senders. Independently retained result/cursor/export/replay owners survive session/process close and do not block drain; only explicit ACK/release/close or profiled expiry retires them. Appendix D's state, frame, retry, privacy, and posture rules are normative; adapters cannot weaken them.

   Ephemeral `stmt_id`, `stream_id`, delivery cursor, open-query, active-transaction, and transient-reservation handles are server-minted random or MACed capabilities scoped to protocol version, database security namespace, **cluster incarnation, service visibility epoch**, tenant, principal, non-widenable authority digest, session, and creation policy/revocation epochs. Prepared statements bind the `PreparedQueryTemplate` digest, language/catalog/schema compatibility contracts, and authority ceiling; each execution resolves a fresh `BoundQuery` and reauthorizes it. Exactly four durable authority-bearing capabilities deliberately survive connection loss: §9.5's `ResumeCapability`, §5.6's `MergeOutcomeCapability`, §14.3's `ReadYourWritesCapability`, and `TxnOutcomeCapability`.

   All four use the exact wire-safe `DurableCapabilityHeader {capability_format_version,capability_kind,issuer_id,mac_key_id,mac_key_epoch,projection_domain_commitment,security_state_binding_public_commitment,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expiry,capability_nonce:PublicHandle<CapabilityNonce>}`; namespace/incarnation/service/posture/principal/authority are committed by the projection domain and retained in the replay object's complete binding, not repeated as correlation-friendly scalar fields. It contains no local ref or policy/revocation counter. A capability is `{header,kind_specific_body,mac}`, and the domain-separated MAC covers the complete header/body. Every live, replayable, or byte-owning durable issuance/replay object carries `authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>` and proves its complete binding derives both public commitments. Only a nonrevivable, nonpayload terminal tombstone may replace that header with `TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>`. The Txn body is exactly `{request_kind:ExplicitBegin|AutocommitExecute,scoped_request_commitment,txn_handle:PublicTxnHandle,outcome_lookup_handle:PublicHandle<TxnOutcomeLookup>,capability_lineage_token:PublicStateToken<TxnCapabilityLineage>}`. A Txn capability permits lookup plus narrowly scoped Reattach/Renew; neither grants direct statement/commit/rollback. Rotation/expiry respect promised floors. Presentation validates current same-or-narrower complete binding and fresh time evidence; bad MAC/domain/binding/profile returns uniform NotVisibleOrUnknown. Consumed-predecessor recovery reproduces only the installed successor while that successor/result remains live and exposes no outcome.

   `DurableCapabilityTerminalTombstone<Role,Kind>` denotes a generated finite family, not an erased generic, with exact common fields `{role,capability_kind=Kind,capability_commitment,projection_domain_commitment,subject_key_commitment,lineage_token_commitment,last_generation,terminal_disposition:Acknowledged|ExplicitlyReleased|SubjectTerminal|Expired|MigratedConsumed,terminal_subject_or_successor_commitment,expiry_or_terminal_evidence_commitment,issuer_and_verifier_epoch_commitment,terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,minimum_nonreuse_lookup_and_replay_floor,body_digest}`. Named, noninterchangeable members exist for TxnOutcome, Resume, MergeOutcome, and ReadYourWrites. They contain no canonical capability bytes, verifier-key ref, replay object, protected payload, lease, live subject, or predecessor edge and cannot authorize lookup, replay, delivery, migration, or successor issuance. `ResultCapabilityTombstone`, subscription/artifact tombstones, begin/outcome-index expiry leaves, merge expiry leaves, and capability-migration consumed leaves are exact generated members or exact wrappers around one member; an informal `{capability_commitment,expiry_evidence_digest}` tuple is not a complete durable schema.

   `TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord` is the immutable compact replay object `{public_fields:{txn_handle,outcome_lookup_handle,capability_lineage_token},canonical_capability_bytes,capability_commitment,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,canonical_request_digest,request_kind,scoped_request_idempotency_key_digest,internal_txn_token,internal_outcome_capability_key,internal_capability_generation,lineage_nonce,issuer_and_verifier_key_epoch,verifier_key_ref:StrongRef<DurableCapabilityVerifierKey>,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expiry,minimum_begin_retry_floor,body_digest}`; stored bytes decode and byte-match every public projection/MAC/commitment while the server-side index maps opaque handles to exact internal subjects. Internal generation zero precedes reservation and successors precede Reattach/Renew. `TxnCapabilityLineageRoot<Role>` maps each key to `Current{current_replay_ref}|CurrentWithConsumedPredecessor{current_replay_ref,predecessor_replay_ref,consuming_request_digest,recovery_until:SuccessorAcknowledgedOrSuccessorTerminal}|TerminalCurrent{current_replay_ref,terminal_subject_commitment}|ExpiredTombstone{tombstone_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityTerminalTombstone<Role>>}`. TerminalCurrent remains a live replayable capability and therefore retains the full replay object/header; only ExpiredTombstone drops it. There is no independent short recovery deadline: the predecessor remains capability-safely recoverable until the successor is explicitly acknowledged/consumed or its transaction/capability lifetime terminally ends. Evidence-bound expiry then installs the exact permanent tombstone.

   **Durable active-transaction ownership and reattachment.** Session binding is represented by an ordered, generation-fenced lease, never by an in-memory connection pointer. `TxnOwnershipLease<Role>` is the closed Local/Meta specialization `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,registration_ref:StrongRef<RoleAttemptRegistration>,ownership_generation,owner_session_binding_digest,owner_auth_generation,current_outcome_capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,current_capability_commitment,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,open_statement_state:Idle|Open{statement_identity,statement_state_version,detached_sender_state},control_mode:FullControl|RollbackAndStatusOnly,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,weak_prior_lease_identity,body_digest:BodyDigest<TxnOwnershipLease<Role>>}`. It never strongly names a predecessor. `TxnOwnershipDirectory<Role>` maps each registered nonterminal attempt to exactly `Owned{lease_ref:StrongRef<TxnOwnershipLease<Role>>}|PrepareFenced{prepare_admission_ref:StrongRef<RolePrepareAdmission>,last_ownership_generation,capability_lineage_ref:StrongRef<TxnCapabilityLineageRoot>}|Terminal{terminal_lookup_key}`. Registration atomically installs generation zero; every statement registration/publication/abort, commit, rollback, and ownership transition must present an ephemeral handle whose `{registration,ownership_generation,session_binding,authority_binding}` byte-matches the current Owned leaf. A later generation therefore fences every old process/session/handle before it can order another effect.

   `TxnOwnershipTransitionSpec<Role>` is the closed union with no shared ambiguous body. `Reattach {expected_owned_lease_ref,presented_capability_validation_ref,presented_replay_ref,expected_capability_lineage_ref,canonical_request_idempotency_key,new_session_binding_digest,new_auth_generation,current_authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,requested_control_mode,open_statement_disposition:NoOpen|TransferRecoverableExecution{old_compute_owner_fence_ref,new_compute_owner_recipe,complete_task_and_sender_handoff_proof_ref}|CancelAndTerminalize{structured_cancel_completion_ref,statement_terminalization_ref,result_detachment_proofs[]},prebuilt_successor_capability_replay_ref,successor_lease_body_recipe,terminal_audit_gate}` may change session only through one exact open-statement disposition. `Renew {expected_owned_lease_ref,presented_current_capability_validation_ref,expected_capability_lineage_ref,canonical_request_idempotency_key,required_same_session_binding_digest,required_same_auth_generation,current_authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,requested_not_before,requested_expires_at,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,prebuilt_successor_capability_replay_ref,successor_lease_body_recipe,terminal_audit_gate}` cannot transfer session/control mode and requires Usable evidence for the exact successor window/action. Both validate domain/principal/generation and CAS one successor. Reattach and Renew are both authorized narrow actions; an open statement can never be left without a compute owner or terminalization path. PrepareFenced permits status only.

   Lease expiry cannot strand an attempt. Every active lease satisfies `expires_at <= current_outcome_capability.expiry`. `TxnOwnershipExpiryAbortSpec<Role>` references `time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>` and requires its decoded tag/classification be Expired for the exact lease/action; a ref to an inline enum variant is illegal. It also requires complete structured cancellation/open-statement terminalization/result detachment/workspace-resource/conflict evidence and is legal only before PrepareFenced. Renewal lost-response recovery uses the lineage rule above. Model/crash tests cover every boundary and no-stranding condition.

   `resume_capability_arms.toml` is the single source for both `ResumeCapabilityBody` and `ResumeCapabilitySubjectBasis`; generation rejects arm drift. `ResumeCapabilityReplayRecord` is `{fields:{header:DurableCapabilityHeader{capability_kind=Resume},body:ResumeCapabilityBody},canonical_capability_bytes,capability_commitment,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,subject_basis:ResumeCapabilitySubjectBasis,capability_generation,lineage,issuer/key epochs,verifier_key_ref,time profile/window,minimum_resume_or_result_floor,body_digest}`. Subject arms are exactly `SubscriptionCursor{cursor_creation_request_digest,resume_cursor_id,cursor_state_version,public_derived_identity_commitment,baseline_id,public_state_commitment}`, `BufferedResult{posture,owner_basis:GeneratedResultDeliveryOwnerBasis,result_delivery_id,lease_generation,delivery_state_version,canonical_result_digest}`, and `DurableArtifactOutput{posture,request_key_digest,output_kind,output_delivery_id,lease_generation,delivery_state_version,canonical_output_digest,manifest_and_policy_basis_commitment}`. Stored bytes decode/byte-match the corresponding public body. Current/immediate-predecessor retention lasts through successor acknowledgement or subject terminal lifetime; only evidence-bound terminal disposition installs tombstones.

   `MergeOutcomeCapabilityBody` is exactly `{merge_handle:PublicMergeHandle,target:WireGraphReferenceSpec,canonical_request_commitment,capability_lineage_token:PublicStateToken<MergeCapabilityLineage>}`. `MergeOutcomeSubjectBasis<Role>` is `{merge_token,target_internal_binding,target_basis:StrongMarkerRef,source_heads[StrongMarkerRef],merge_bases[StrongMarkerRef],resolution_policy_oid,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,lookup_and_retention_floor,public_body_digest}`. `MergeOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord` owns that basis plus canonical body/bytes/commitment/request/verifier/window/floor fields and repeats the same complete header only where the generated schema row requires a byte-equality cross-check. `MergePrepareSpec` strongly names it; detailed/compacted/tombstone states follow the same retention rules. A public marker selector resolves only through its authenticated retained mapping and exact authority; it is never decoded as an internal marker identity.

   `ReadYourWritesCapabilityReplayRecord` is `{fields:{header:DurableCapabilityHeader{capability_kind=ReadYourWrites},body:{posture,txn_outcome_capability_commitment,registration_handle:PublicRegistrationHandle,floors:PublicRywFloorSet,capability_lineage_token:PublicStateToken<ReadYourWritesLineage>}},canonical_capability_bytes,capability_commitment,subject_basis_ref:StrongRef<ReadYourWritesSubjectBasis<Role>>,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,issuer/key/verifier/time-window/floor fields,body_digest}`. The public body contains no redundant authority ceiling; the header/binding owns it. The subject basis supplies the exact marker/branch/visibility/retention map and byte-matches `floors`. It contains no WeakMarkerIdentity, raw Raft/logical/commit/audit counter, or local ref on wire. It is issued only with TerminalReady and permits waiting/reading at an authorized subset of the opaque committed floor set under current same-or-narrower authority. All capability kinds have exact restart/rotation/lost-response/expiry/substitution byte tests.

   `FrameLimits` bounds encoded and uncompressed bytes, compression ratio, nesting, columns, parameters, FEC symbols, streams, in-flight bytes, result rows/bytes, and idle time. The decoder validates declared sizes and reserves resources before allocating or FEC decoding. Authentication follows protected transport and transcript-bound negotiation. Compression is disabled for mixed attacker-controlled/secret frames unless a reviewed profile permits it. Factorized frames carry canonical authorized values, never run references. Session types enforce order; runtime validators enforce values, epochs, authorization, and resource bounds. Appendix D is normative, not a sketch.
   **Durable artifact outputs and replay execution.** Export, public replay-manifest retrieval, and proof bundles are not socket-owned streams. `DurableArtifactOutputKind=Export|ReplayManifestProjection|ProofBundle`; `DurableArtifactOutputRequestKey` is `{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,posture,tenant,principal,non_widenable_authority_digest,kind,scoped_idempotency_key,canonical_request_digest}`. Reusing the scoped key with any body/domain/authority drift fails before source observation or charge. `DurableArtifactOutputManifest` is `{format_version,kind,public_format_contract_oid,public_scope_projection,disclosure_contract_digest,canonical_encoding_version,byte_count,ordered_chunks[CanonicalResultChunkDescriptor],canonical_output_digest,source_retention_refs[TypedArtifactSourceRetentionRef],resource_charge_ref,body_digest}`; its public digest covers kind/public contracts/encoding/count and exact concatenated bytes, excluding internal identities/refs/charges/placements and itself.

   Semantic `ArtifactOutputRequestRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is state-shaped `Reserved{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,source_pins,resource_reservations}|MaterializedDetached{authority_bound_header,manifest_ref:StrongRef<DurableArtifactOutputManifest>,output_delivery_id,delivery_policy_ref:StrongRef<ArtifactOutputDeliveryPolicy<Role>>,audit_gate_commitment,detachment_commitment}|FailedDetached{terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,protected_error_key,public_error_commitment,audit_gate_commitment}` and never stores a delivery lease, capability, ACK, release, or protocol-record ref. The terminal arm is nonrevivable and payload-free; a failed request that still owns an error manifest uses the separate live ProtectedError lifecycle below. `DurableArtifactOutputRequestRegistry<Role>` owns request idempotency and compacted semantic floors. Materialization atomically transfers the charge to `ArtifactOutputResourceEscrow<Role>` and creates Protocol `ArtifactOutputDeliveryRecord<Role>::PendingVisibility{common,manifest_ref,policy_ref,escrow_ref,audit_gate_commitment}` unless same-apply visibility creates ReleasedAwaitingActivation or a legal inline fresh recipe selects ActiveDeliverable. Pending/released states have no time window/capability and emit neither READY nor bytes.

   `ArtifactOutputActiveCommon<Role>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,request_key_digest,output_delivery_id,delivery_state_version,canonical_output_digest,semantic_detachment_identity,nonretaining_predecessor_digest}`; `ArtifactOutputTerminalCommon<Role>` replaces the header with `terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>` and retains only terminal/public identities. `ArtifactOutputDeliveryRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the exact Protocol state union `PendingVisibility{common:ArtifactOutputActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref,policy_ref,escrow_ref,audit_gate_commitment}|ReleasedAwaitingActivation{common:ArtifactOutputActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref,policy_ref,escrow_ref,audit_release_evidence,release_maintenance_ref}|ActiveDeliverable{common:ArtifactOutputActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref,lease_time_basis_ref,lease_generation,capability_lineage_ref,escrow_ref,sender_generation}|Acknowledged{common:ArtifactOutputTerminalCommon<Role>,ack_commitment,capability_tombstone_ref,escrow_disposition_commitment}|ReleasedUnacked{common:ArtifactOutputTerminalCommon<Role>,release_or_expiry_commitment,capability_tombstone_ref,escrow_disposition_commitment}`; terminal arms retain no strong authority, manifest, lease, payload, capability replay, escrow, or predecessor edge. `ArtifactOutputDeliveryIndex<Role>` maps request and output-delivery keys to the one current record plus a compacted disposition floor. Audit visibility changes PendingVisibility only to ReleasedAwaitingActivation; independent activation supplies a fresh `ArtifactOutputLeaseTimeBasis<Role>`, exact time evidence, and prebuilt generation-zero `ResumeCapabilityReplayRecord`. Resume/ACK/authenticated explicit release/profiled expiry are maintenance transitions with the same full-header/nonwidening receipt, sender fencing, send-completion bridge, capability-lineage, resource-conservation, and lost-response rules as query results. Checkpoint/protocol snapshot, backup, restore, GC, and posture transitions walk both the semantic request registry and Protocol delivery index. Local/Meta payloads root only the semantic registry; `RootManifest`'s protocol state roots only the delivery index.

   **Protected errors are bounded durable outputs, not an exception path.** A data-dependent error is first canonicalized to `ProtectedErrorManifest {error_format_version,public_reason:PublicReasonProjection,padded_public_fields,canonical_error_bytes,error_digest,disclosure_contract_digest,maximum_replay_window,body_digest}`. The canonical bytes contain exactly that frozen projection; `protected_error_classes.toml` maps hidden constraint/existence/authorization details to `PublicReasonProjection::Opaque{OperationDidNotComplete,...}` unless the bundle carries the exact portable receipt for one registered disclosed code. No internal reason class, policy counter, existence fact, or raw digest is serialized. Semantic `ProtectedErrorRequestRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` stores only `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,request_key_digest,error_manifest_ref:StrongRef<ProtectedErrorManifest>,delivery_policy_ref:StrongRef<ProtectedErrorDeliveryPolicy<Role>>,semantic_resource_transition_ref,audit_gate_commitment,detachment_commitment}`. `ProtectedErrorActiveCommon<Role>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,request_key_digest,error_delivery_id,delivery_state_version,error_digest,disclosure_contract_digest,semantic_detachment_identity,nonretaining_predecessor_digest}`; `ProtectedErrorTerminalCommon<Role>` replaces the header with `terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>` and retains only terminal/public identities. The same publication creates `ProtectedErrorResourceEscrow<Role>` and Protocol `ProtectedErrorDeliveryRecord<Role> = PendingVisibility{common:ProtectedErrorActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref,policy_ref,escrow_ref,audit_gate_commitment}|ReleasedAwaitingActivation{common:ProtectedErrorActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref,policy_ref,escrow_ref,audit_release_evidence,release_maintenance_ref}|ActiveDeliverable{common:ProtectedErrorActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref,lease_time_basis_ref,lease_generation,sender_generation,escrow_ref}|SentReplayable{common:ProtectedErrorActiveCommon<Role>,send_completion_ref:StrongRef<ProtectedErrorSendCompletionRecord>,replay_time_basis_ref:StrongRef<ProtectedErrorReplayTimeBasis<Role>>,escrow_ref}|Acknowledged{common:ProtectedErrorTerminalCommon<Role>,ack_commitment,escrow_disposition_commitment}|Expired{common:ProtectedErrorTerminalCommon<Role>,expiry_evidence_commitment,escrow_disposition_commitment}`. Pending/released states have no clock or send authority; SentReplayable owns the exact profiled replay basis rather than a raw `replay_until`. Terminal arms retain no manifest, lease, replay basis, escrow, or live authority edge. `ProtectedErrorDeliveryIndex<Role>` indexes both request key and delivery ID, so exact request retry returns the same padded public error while a conflicting request digest fails uniformly. Audit visibility only releases; independent activation installs a fresh bounded lease. `ProtectedErrorSendCompletionRecord` is the durable maintenance bridge from the exact ephemeral `TransportSendObligation::Sent` transcript to SentReplayable. `ERROR_ACK` or profiled expiry alone disposes escrow; socket loss, cancellation, drain, and Rust Drop do not. Errors have no fifth durable capability: replay requires the original scoped request key under a fresh same-principal Ready binding, exact current disclosure authority, and fresh `TimeValidationEvidence::Usable` for the retained replay basis. A failure in this delivery path closes or returns a fixed protocol-mechanics failure and never recursively creates another protected error.

   Internal `ReplayManifest` ObjectIds are never wire selectors. `ReplayPublicHandleRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is state-shaped: `Active{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,replay_handle:OpaquePublicNonce,manifest_ref:StrongRef<ReplayManifest>,disclosure_contract_digest,handle_generation,created_at_role_command_seq}|Revoked{terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,replay_handle,terminal_generation,revocation_commitment}|Expired{terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,replay_handle,terminal_generation,expiry_evidence_commitment}`. Terminal arms retain no manifest, executable/archive evidence, replay bytes, verifier, or live authority edge. It is indexed by `ReplayRegistry`; every active use reauthorizes the opaque handle and emits only a typed `PublicReplayManifestProjection` through the artifact-output registry. Replay execution is separate: `ReplayExecutionIndex<Role>` uniqueness-serializes its request and `ReplayExecutionRecord` owns execution state under its own full header. Its query output uses the ordinary result owner arm `ReplayExecution{registration_ref,publication_applied_ref}`, result manifest, independent retention, resume, ACK, and release machinery—never an ad hoc stream or the artifact-output registry. Public replay evidence contains commitments only and cannot expose a DP seed.

   **Wire-safe public types.** Incoming graph selection is `WireGraphReferenceSpec {client_binding_ordinal,graph_selector,branch_selector,system_time_selector,valid_time_selector,required_public_schema_contract}`. Every opaque value is built under `PublicProjectionDomain {database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,posture,principal_scope_commitment,non_widenable_authority_commitment,projection_key_epoch}`. `PublicHandle<Kind>` is a pre-minted random 128-bit nonce plus a domain-separated MAC over domain/kind/nonce; it is never derived from an ObjectId, marker, counter, name, or low-entropy hidden fact. `PublicStateToken<Kind>` is a separately pre-minted random CAS nonce plus the same scoped MAC for one externally addressable state transition; it is not a serialized internal generation/version. `OpaquePublicCommitment<Kind>` is `PRF(projection_key, "fgdb:opaque:" || domain || kind || per_subject_random_salt || canonical_internal_subject_commitment)` and therefore cannot be used as an offline dictionary or cross-principal/cross-epoch correlation oracle. Replay retains the exact bytes and verifier-key floor; key rotation never recomputes a live public token.

   The sole portable field-declassification proof is `PortablePublicDisclosureReceipt {receipt_version,projection_domain_commitment,disclosure_profile_oid,stable_projection_subject_commitment,allowed_public_field_bitmap,receipt_free_projection_digest,signer_id,signing_key_id,signer_epoch,signature}`. Its signature covers every preceding field and the digest of the **receipt-free** projection body, never itself. It contains no session/Ready binding, local reference, state root, topology identity, policy/revocation counter, or internal position, so frozen bytes survive reconnect and compaction. The later `PublicFrameSendGuard` separately binds those stable bytes to the current Ready/session/auth generation immediately before write.

   The closed public projections are posture-tagged and noninterchangeable: `PublicMarkerProjection = Local|Sharded{handle:PublicHandle<Marker>,commitment:OpaquePublicCommitment<Marker>,disclosure:Opaque|Disclosed{public_comparison_domain:PublicHandle<CommitComparisonDomain>,public_ordinal,receipt}}`; `PublicLogicalPositionProjection = Local|Sharded{handle:PublicHandle<LogicalPosition>,commitment:OpaquePublicCommitment<LogicalPosition>,disclosure:Opaque|Disclosed{axis:LocalLogical|GlobalLogical,public_comparison_domain:PublicHandle<LogicalComparisonDomain>,public_ordinal,receipt}}`; `PublicSnapshotProjection = Local|Sharded{snapshot_handle:PublicHandle<Snapshot>,snapshot_commitment:OpaquePublicCommitment<Snapshot>,logical_position:PublicLogicalPositionProjection,normalized_valid_time_projection,selector_echo_commitment,receipt:None|Some{PortablePublicDisclosureReceipt}}`; and `PublicResultOwnerProjection = Local|Sharded{owner_handle:PublicHandle<ResultOwner>,owner_commitment:OpaquePublicCommitment<ResultOwner>,disclosure:Opaque|Disclosed{owner_class:TerminalRead|AutocommitWriteTerminal|Statement|DpResult|ReplayExecution,receipt}}`. A disclosed ordinal exists only in a registered public comparison domain/epoch with an authenticated server-side map and retention floor; otherwise v1 emits Opaque.

   Digest projections have distinct wire tags and transcripts: `PublicEffectProjection`, `PublicPublicationProjection`, `PublicResultDigestProjection`, and `PublicOutputDigestProjection` are each `Omitted{commitment:OpaquePublicCommitment<ExactKind>}|Disclosed{digest_of_authorized_public_bytes,receipt}`. A disclosed digest is recomputed from the registered authorized public transcript, never copied from an internal final-effect, publication-chain, result-manifest, or storage digest; cross-kind substitution fails decoding. `PublicReasonProjection` is `Opaque{coarse_class:OperationDidNotComplete,reason_commitment:OpaquePublicCommitment<Reason>}|Disclosed{public_reason_code:RegisteredPublicReasonCode,reason_commitment,receipt}`. `protected_error_classes.toml` maps every hidden constraint/existence/authorization cause to the Opaque arm unless the receipt permits one exact public code, with padded byte/timing classes and differential noninterference tests. `StablePublicReasonClass` and generic `PublicDigestProjection` are invalid types.

   `PublicEffectiveSnapshotSet` is `{set_handle:PublicHandle<SnapshotSet>,sorted_bindings[{client_binding_ordinal,graph_binding_handle,branch_handle,effective_snapshot:PublicSnapshotProjection}],set_commitment}`; `PublicRywFloorSet` is `{set_handle:PublicHandle<RywFloorSet>,visibility_floor:PublicLogicalPositionProjection,sorted_branch_floors[{client_binding_ordinal,graph_handle,branch_handle,head:PublicMarkerProjection}],set_commitment}`. Arrays are unique and sorted by client binding ordinal. Outgoing graph selection is `PublicGraphBindingProjection {public_binding_role_id,graph_handle,branch_handle,effective_snapshot:PublicSnapshotProjection,public_schema_contract_digest,disclosure_decision_digest}`; normalized valid time has exactly one owner inside the snapshot. `PublicMarkerSelector` is a capability-safe handle plus expected marker commitment backed by the server's retained mapping/floor; a display commitment alone cannot select a merge basis.

   Signed/random public artifacts are prebuilt, never invented by deterministic apply. `TerminalPublicProjectionBundle<Role>` is `{role,internal_terminal_descriptor_digest,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,receipt_free_terminal_core,receipt_free_projection_digest,portable_disclosure_receipt,frozen_public_progress_bytes,frozen_terminal_outcome_bytes,prebuilt_result_capability_commitments[],prebuilt_read_your_writes_issuance_ref?,original_response_commitment,bundle_digest}`. It remains a byte-owning, capability-gated live object even though its internal subject is terminal, so a `TerminalAuthorityCommitment` is insufficient until later compaction removes every protected byte and replay promise. The non-self-referential response commitment covers version, frozen terminal core, and listed prebuilt capability commitments, excluding itself and later audit/send evidence. `NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Role>` and `MergePublicProjectionBundle<Role>` use the same shape with their exact internal subject. Construction is internal subject + pre-minted handles/salts -> receipt-free public body -> digest -> signed portable receipt -> frozen bytes -> bundle -> semantic completion/publication input. Apply only byte-matches and installs. Detailed values and compact summaries retain/copy the same frozen bytes and receipt; current authority either permits them exactly or returns uniform `NotVisibleOrUnknown`, never recomputes a redacted variant.

   `ReadYourWritesSubjectBasis<Role>` is `Local{registration_ref,terminal_marker_ref:StrongMarkerRef,exact_visible_logical_floor,sorted_written_branch_heads[StrongMarkerRef],retention_floor_ref,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,public_floor_set_digest}|Meta{registration_ref,terminal_global_marker_ref:StrongMarkerRef,exact_visible_global_floor,sorted_written_branch_heads[StrongMarkerRef],retention_floor_ref,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,public_floor_set_digest}`. `ReadYourWritesIssuanceBundle<Role>` owns that basis, one `PublicRywFloorSet`, the prebuilt capability replay record/canonical bytes, and their byte-match proof. A RYW capability therefore has an exact internal wait/retention subject even when every public position is opaque.

   `wire_types.toml` materializes noninterchangeable aliases including `PublicTxnHandle`, `PublicRegistrationHandle`, `PublicResultDeliveryHandle`, `PublicArtifactOutputHandle`, `PublicCursorHandle`, `PublicBaselineHandle`, `PublicDeliveryLeaseHandle`, `PublicMergeHandle`, `PublicAuditSubjectHandle`, `PublicErrorDeliveryHandle`, and matching `PublicStateToken` kinds for ownership, delivery, cursor, artifact, error, staging, workspace, and capability lineage. Appendix D's short field labels denote these exact aliases; raw internal identities/generations are never implied. Numeric chunk ordinals, delivery sequence within one disclosed cursor, statement request ordinal, and flow-credit counters remain public protocol counters. `ProofBody` is the closed union `RawCommitMembership|RawContiguousRange|FilteredMembership|FilteredCompleteness|ReplayProjectionVerification|OutputDigestVerification`, each with a registered version, bounds, authorization rule, and verifier—there is no generic payload arm. FGP FEC uses `PublicFecTransferDescriptor` and channel-exporter-authenticated `WireFecSymbolRecord`; at-rest `SymbolRecord`, CiphertextId, EncodingId, PlacementDescriptor, object DEK, and placement metadata are forbidden wire types. G0 rejects any protected frame containing ObjectId, any reference tag, WeakMarkerIdentity, GraphReferenceBinding, CoordinateSchemaBinding, DerivedIdentity, raw internal counter/frontier/generation, ReplayManifest, or storage-symbol type. A generated taint checker proves every wire path passes an exact projection constructor and fresh send guard.

4. **HTTP/2 + gRPC + WebSocket** (asupersync http): query endpoint, subscription streams, health/metrics, bulk import/export endpoints (multipart or ATP bonded pull).
5. **Bolt-compat subset** (`fgdb-bolt`): `BoltCompatProfileV1` is an explicit negotiated downgrade: read-only autocommit and read-only explicit transactions only; every RUN is server-classified ProvenReadOnly before graph access; no writes, subscriptions, CDC, export, replay, proof, FEC, factorized frames, capability presentation, or authority refresh. Bolt RECORD bytes use the ordinary read/audit/send guards and independent result retention. Socket success and final SUCCESS are not ACKs. Authenticated DISCARD or RESET creates one exact Bolt-source client-release receipt per addressed published result; transport loss creates none, so otherwise output survives to profiled expiry even though Bolt cannot resume it. Unsupported semantics fail with stable documented divergence. Adapter conformance compares only the common supported surface, and the negotiated profile is published in READY/metadata.
6. **QoS and admission** separates ephemeral runtime reservations from durable quota state. A hierarchical `ResourceVector { cpu_ns, resident_bytes, temp_bytes, durable_bytes, read_iops, write_iops, egress_bytes, output_bytes, output_rows, snapshot_age, branch/index/view/subscription counts, subscription_backlog }` is reserved before transient work and released by obligation. Durable bytes/branches/indexes/views/subscriptions/history are enforced by an authoritative Chronicle-sequenced `ResourceLedgerState`; every create/grow/delete/expiry carries `ResourceReservationId` plus canonical `ResourceChargeEffect`/release in the same final capsule as the durable object change (§5.2). Concurrent sessions therefore serialize quota validation with publication, and crash recovery replays charge and object atomically. Maintenance has protected emergency reserves. Hedging is allowed only within ephemeral reservations and never chooses strict logical order by wall-clock race; DP work forbids independent hedges.
7. **Formats and migration** (`fgdb-formats`) import the declared fnx/CSV/JSONL/Parquet-lite profiles under explicit size, recursion, schema, and decompression limits. A `.fgdb` archive is a signed `BackupManifest` plus exact root/head, object inventory, base dependencies, key wraps, format/profile epochs, and transparency checkpoint. Decode proofs alone do not establish backup completeness or semantic validity (§16).
8. **Deterministic extensibility:** replicated/apply/merge logic is limited to validated Datalog or the fuel/memory/stack-bounded deterministic bytecode VM with mediated authority and pinned arithmetic. Native Rust procedures remain embedded-host extensions and are categorically barred from replicated logical effects, merge/apply, constraints, and replay-guaranteed queries; a conformance test cannot prove arbitrary native code deterministic. Native read procedures receive authorized views and are labeled external nondeterminism unless their outputs are excluded from strict replay.
9. **CDC export** sends canonical committed logical deltas with stable idempotency tokens and at-least-once delivery. Raw global feeds may attach contiguous-range proofs; filtered feeds label membership versus completeness exactly as §5.8 requires. External sinks provide their own transactional/idempotent boundary.

---

## 14. Aegis: Replication, HA, and the Distribution Doctrine
