**Gate G1 — "The Engine Lives":** fresh-creation three-phase bootstrap and embedded authorized path; bounded resources; declared GQL/Cypher feature subset, synchronous Unicode bundle, and matrix; acyclic durable-object construction; final-capsule/command/pre-minted-merge coherence; stable D1 prepared-root ownership and quorum-one ordered-command crash recovery; generated complete projected checkpoint-vector + mandatory genesis/paired-operational checkpoint-floor install + logical-command/transaction-marker suffix recovery with no historical-floor retention chain, including inherited absent bytes under a dominating replacement Provisional cut and proof that it authorizes no new reclamation before separate activation; exact Raft snapshot cut binding; serializable witness families formally and independently validated; accepted-filesystem conformance plus lease-owner-death/fence-root-before-enable/torn-root/marker-tail/stale-writer/bootstrap/recovery matrices green; constraint state authoritative; compaction/label changes preserve logical digests; strict canonical replay closure works; every clause reachable by the compiled/negotiated G1 capability manifest is live, otherwise that capability is absent. Full language breadth is not claimed until its binder/operator rows exist.

G1 additionally requires executable reference/oracle coverage for normalized valid-time slices; first-escape and finalization allocation under forced rebase; resource/constraint/escrow atomicity; complete security-root binding; generation-fenced transaction ownership and prepare handoff; and the Local audit visibility pipeline with multiple unresolved candidates, out-of-order external completions, batch release, restart, and bounded backpressure. The command/state registries must demonstrate that every active Local transition owns an exact rooted result and recovery path. Any pre-rebase capsule, post-filter authorization, ambient allocator counter, implicit result release, or optional audit fence is a gate failure.

**Gate G2 — "One Version Universe":** verified generated-complete checkpoint-install+logical-command suffix recovery and per-graph retention over mixed transaction/control commands; branch fork double rotation, exact envelope DAG/AAD and `StrongCiphertextRef` realization retention, snapshot/advancing handoff, re-encryption/movement under live grants, sibling/post-fork-parent non-decryption, and pre-minted merge-token/imported-range-idempotent explicit-base merges across history cuts; conditional source templates plus self-contained retained Ripple batches; publicly authenticated baseline results and atomic/coalesced delta provenance/output; durable cursor-generation plus tagged baseline/delta lease, baseline-ACK/delta-ACK/resume/lost-response/expiry recovery with no delta-before-baseline or silent gap; table/graph views with explicit read policies; watermark/tail-correct indexes; historical/capability-safe ANN roots with exact fallback; authorized Prism projections; exact Local backup barrier/pin -> copy and independent archive reopen -> closure certificate -> seal -> publication receipt/archive grant -> ordered pin release; RestoreSourceLease -> exhaustive leaves-first incarnation transform/re-encryption -> guarded RecoveryReconciling bridge -> identity/privacy/audit/cluster-incarnation reconciliation -> hidden activation and service preparation -> continuity-then-catalog CAS receipt -> Local service promotion -> independent target reopen -> completion/audit visibility advance -> lease release, with stale incarnation/service epochs and old capabilities rejected; Sharded round-trip remains reserved for W12; empirical/statistical gates reported with assumptions.

G2 also proves the state-shaped independent result, DP-result, and replay-execution owner machines: only Active arms retain bulk bytes, explicit authenticated release/expiry or complete ACK creates compact terminal evidence, and disconnect/Drop/cancel cannot do so. Bulk import's source-row/chunk/logical-effect bijection survives retry/crash and commits only through ordinary transactions. Derived shadow builds catch up to one exact frontier, validate, and become visible only through a transactional SchemaOp. Exact format rewrites are semantic no-ops, while durable-format activation/first-write and decoder floors are recovery-safe.

**Gate G3 — "Verified & Networked":** bounded FGP connection, explicit multi-statement transaction ownership/reattachment, query/state-shaped result-delivery/durable-output/subscription/FEC machines and adapter parity, including durable BeginStaging reservation and lost-BEGIN recovery; statement sequence/key/class and workspace generations; exact SymbolRecord; RESULT_BEGIN/CHUNK/END plus terminal-or-statement ACK/typed release; read-close/write-commit outcomes; and the sole wire-safe `PublicTxnStatusProjection` mapping: unreleased semantic terminal state is `FinalizingOpaque`, released terminal state with incomplete typed postconditions is `TerminalVisiblePendingPostconditions`, and complete state is `TerminalReady`. Internal `TxnCompletionState::TerminalPending` descriptors and bitmaps never serialize. NeverRegistered detailed/compacted and terminal detailed/compacted status are byte-identical; canonical baseline/delta transport and ACK/resume, and cluster-incarnation/service-visibility binding cover every authenticated frame, handle, cursor, capability, and adapter. Uniform NotVisibleOrUnknown is limited to invalid/unauthorized/wrong-epoch lookup. End-to-end Warden gates noninterference, declared constraint declassifications, revocation, externally monotonic irreversible-before-access DP debit/rollback reconciliation, Local two-stage current-root-recomputed key destruction, and exact audit `PendingUnclaimed -> Claimed(owner) -> Resolved` plus per-signer lock/candidate/external-batch-CAS/gap-free visibility recovery; lifecycle gates are nonrecursive and permanent gaps are durable. Local receipt-first restore requires continuity/catalog fencing, hidden direct-endpoint negatives, independent archive and target reopen, completion, and source-lease release. Multi-member Raft proves prepared-root payload ownership/availability before ordering, exact stable/joint/final-retirement-floor semantics, paired checkpoint/floor replacement, exact-cut snapshots, leader loss at every phase, two-prefix fenced reads, Local/multi-member all-typed-root GC with exact current-root recomputation and physical-retirement evidence, and idempotent unknown/abort outcomes; W12 quarantine/aggregate GC remains W12-only. Rollback, witnessed-detection, and witnessed-non-equivocation claims remain distinct, with quorum intersection, durable witnesses, consistency, freshness, cross-log, and joint-rotation matrices green wherever prevention is claimed. Raw versus filtered proof scopes, formal/model bounds, and implementation bridges land before their advertised guarantees.

G3's protocol matrix includes explicit `TXN_REATTACH`/renewal, `QUERY_CANCEL` as compute/sender control only, `RESULT_RELEASE` as the sole client-directed durable release, output ACK/release/resume, exact cumulative flow-credit accounting, public proof/FEC schemas, and Bolt's declared read-only downgrade. Every protected frame is bound to one release/send-guard arm and current complete security binding. DP E2E proves arm-before-send external charging, exact receipt import, Meta-only noise, charged-crash recovery, and no seed/result leakage. Rolling mixed-version tests exercise advertise, joint support, write activation, first target write, refusal to downgrade, rewrite, and decoder retirement.

**Gate G4 — "Leapfrog, Published":** all activated §17 benchmark manifests green/public; 100% of G0's 1.0 `LanguageContract`—ISO minimum closure, `GG01` or `GG02` plus one of `GG20`–`GG23` as applicable, all prerequisites, required scalar profile, validated synchronous Unicode/UTS #10/UAX #15/UAX #31 bundle and UCS_BASIC behavior, and named optional IDs—passes its ISO-ID/TCK corpus, while every out-of-contract feature is rejected/documented; canonical replay/archive/answer requirement/profile/result-contract and security matrices; production-format recovery plus Local receipt-first restore, continuity split-brain, stale service epoch, independent archive/target reopen, and source-lease-release drills; crash-fault multi-writer preparation and globally ordered commitment; evidence reports correctly qualified; papers match proven scope; 1.0. W12 sharding is excluded and cannot reuse G4 evidence as its gate.

G4 additionally publishes the durable-format compatibility matrix and completed upgrade/downgrade drills; allocation/escrow/valid-time/security/transaction-ownership/result-retention/DP/audit-pipeline invariant clause reports; bulk and derived-build conformance; and exact backup/restore/GC inventory coverage for every newly activated state slot. Documentation, robot schemas, and benchmark claims are generated or checked against the frozen registries. A feature whose command/state/checker row is disabled is absent from the 1.0 capability manifest, not described as partial.

**Gate W12 — "One Universe, Many Shards" (post-1.0):** every reserved kind/tag/field has an exact registry row; role-tagged Local/Meta/Shard logs, roots, floors, and exact-cut snapshots reject cross-role commands; fresh Sharded Genesis and audited Local freeze/seal/activation/authority transfer/RetiredLocal finalization/reclamation pass every crash point; and every semantic mutation, including single-shard work, enters Meta Raft for one global logical/transaction/HLC order. BEGIN durably reserves its key/outcome capability before response; registration atomically claims the operation ticket and roots workspace zero before every serializable read. Every explicit statement is Meta-registered against one sequence/key/class/error policy and workspace generation; every routed shard reads registered snapshot plus the exact certified overlay for every previously touched shard; Meta publication atomically advances statement/delivery/workspace/witness/effect state and constructs the successor Registered outcome. No result streams before its distinct statement/read-close audit visibility release and typed delivery lease; terminal/autocommit and statement results share exact ACK/typed-release transitions. Cancellation proves open-statement, result, overlay, and remote-grant discharge. Read close is a Global control. ReadWrite admission names complete statement/delivery/workspace frontiers; effect-only bases then feed one union-wide authorization decision, while terminal audit remains a separately signer-locked event/resolution. Fence-free prepares, constraint/serialization certificates, pre-command availability, Meta applied-hidden candidate/external batch CAS/visibility advance, decision-certificate publication, shard decision, typed visibility projection, post-visibility acknowledgements, complete GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch publication, and TerminalReady status pass exact DAG/crash proofs. Detailed/compacted NeverRegistered and terminal outcomes are byte-identical; attempt compaction's build/attest/publish protocol proves every result/workspace/remote-grant/public-status obligation before release.

W12 additionally proves certified shard allocation reservations with permanent spent commitments; Meta escrow custody with staged/active shard grants and conservation bijection; complete security-state projections; transaction ownership generation transfer; DP clipped fragments with one Meta charge/noise/result owner; format support across Meta plus current/joint shard groups; and exact role payload-root coverage. Meta/Shard GC must pass Requested/Started/Completed-or-all-cancelled state machines before aggregate completion. Local-to-Sharded capability migration is exclusive and service-epoch fenced; outstanding Local results/cursors either follow their certified transition policy or require a new baseline, never reinterpret an old ObjectId.

Every cross-group edge passes two-sided CertifiedRemoteStrongRef trust/retention/GC tests; every participant prepare has durable availability and one terminal decision; no timeout fractures atomicity under crash/partition/leader loss/reconfiguration. The independent global SCC/history oracle finds no committed cycle across local, cross-shard, negative/gap/path, read-only, workspace, and batch interleavings; deterministic constraint owners/reservations agree with the single-threaded reference. Branch/key/ID/security/resource/privacy/audit authority and activation state is Meta-atomic; shard-local key material changes only through certified Staged-to-Active or destruction protocols. Cross-shard reads use one retained registered snapshot plus exact workspace generation and overlays. Remote configuration trust and the six-stage grant release, replacement Provisional cuts with no-new-reclaim before activation, current-root staged key zero/destroy/member-KMS completion, and GC preflight/quarantine/no-resurrection/member/aggregate completion pass models and physical evidence gates. Exact backup at barrier P proves per-shard copy/reopen/closure, portable Global manifest publication, archive grants, ordered shard release acks, and Meta finalize. Restore proves continuity reservation, exhaustive leaves-first readdress/re-encryption, hidden activation/visibility/service preparation, receipt-first continuity/catalog publication, Meta finalize, shard open, independent reopen, operational acknowledgements, completion, and source-lease release. Three-phase topology copy/cutover/retire proves no ambient evidence, straddled epoch, or premature byte/key retirement. Every failure artifact carries §15's structured replay fields. Meta and every shard pass payload/certificate loss, checkpoint+suffix, donor loss, rolling upgrade, and exact status/backup/restore matrices; every compiled-and-negotiated subordinate clause of FG-INV-01–20 is live and published benchmarks disclose the global sequencer ceiling. Otherwise `sharding=true` is absent.

---

## 20. Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Reality check | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Scope: this is a multi-hundred-KLOC system | True — and smaller than frankensqlite, on stronger foundations | Agent-swarm methodology with ledger-driven exit criteria (proven twice in-family at this scale); sim-first testing collapses the debugging tail that normally dominates DB schedules; crate boundaries sized for parallel agents |
| Free-Join + factorization + adaptivity interplay is the hardest novel engineering | Correct — this is the crown-jewel risk | Build order inside W5: binary FreeJoin → run-trie WCOJ → factorized columns → adaptivity; each stage gets independent semantic and benchmark evidence; published artifacts are behavioral references, not inherited certificates. |
| Graph-SSI phantom/path witness design could be unsound | Central correctness risk | SERIALIZABLE rejects unverified witness families; formal concretization/may-affect proofs, optimizer-preservation laws, independent small-model histories, and gap/negative/path mutation tests gate each family. SNAPSHOT is an explicit weaker contract, not containment for a false serializable claim. |
| HNSW under MVCC/capabilities/compaction | Known-hard industry-wide | Pin historical authorized topology roots, compensate complete delta tails, retain exact fallback, and label recall evidence by population. Output filtering and exact rerank cannot repair missed candidates. |
| Adaptive tiering thrashes (representation churn eats its own benefit) | The classic failure mode of every adaptive store | SPRT migration cards with indifference-region hysteresis and model-conditional type-I/type-II evidence (§6.6); benefit must exceed conversion cost + uncertainty; pinned deterministic fallback policy under lab; observed migration churn is a separate gated §17 metric |
| Witness refinement becomes a second query (cost eats the abort savings) | Possible on pathological predicates | Refinement is budgeted with conservative-abort fallback; `WitnessTrace` is a byproduct of normal execution (no re-execution); refined-vs-saved accounting on every decision card; the knob defaults conservative |
| Nightly Rust | Both foundations already pin nightly; MSRV churn is real | Pinned toolchain per repo (house pattern), portable-SIMD isolated behind `fgdb-codec`/kernel traits with scalar fallbacks |
| fnx algorithms not yet trait-generic or external-memory where required | Verified: `GraphView` exists with integer rows; compatible larger-than-memory coverage is partial | W8 upstream/cursor work plus spillable materialization and family-specific external algorithms. Unsupported functions remain unregistered—there is no “always-correct fallback” that secretly requires the whole graph in RAM. |
| Final-capsule encoding and quorum distribution limit commit throughput | Correctness requires them before publication | Pipeline/group finalization after coordinator-approved effects, benchmark the complete D1/D2/Raft path, and optimize only after effect-digest and availability invariants remain green. |
| Root/checkpoint/retention protocol loses recovery closure | Catastrophic | Dual root slots, full closure validation, checkpoint authority transfer, stale-writer fencing, two-root GC accounting, and crash matrices at every file/directory barrier. |
| Kùzu's fate as a cautionary tale (great engine, no oxygen) | The sharpest non-technical risk | FrankenGraphDB is an open-source ecosystem asset; B1/B4/B5/B6 target agent memory, auditability, and governance without making ungrounded market guarantees. |
| The calibration plane becomes ornamental math (monitors nobody gates on) | The classic fate of statistical infrastructure | Every Sextant artifact binds to a named decision card, `evidence.toml` row, or `slo.toml` row—or is removed as dead instrumentation. It can gate an adaptive promotion or empirical claim but never masquerades as an invariant checker. |
| Adaptive policies diverge logical results | Fatal | FG-INV-19 separately pins answer-affecting choices in the bound/replay closure and logical-command-sequences canonical-state-affecting policy epochs; answer-preserving physical classification has equivalence tests and replica logical-digest oracles. Physical encodings need not be identical. |
| Statistical guarantees rest on modeling assumptions | Unavoidable | Every claim names assumptions and scope; monitoring can trigger fallback but does not retroactively restore invalid coverage. Safety remains independent of statistical success. |
| In-house cryptography fails despite vectors | High-impact specialized risk | Prefer audited foundation primitives; constant-time/misuse verification, algorithm agility, independent review, and external release audit are mandatory. |
| Planner-only authorization leaks through another path | Unacceptable | `fgdb-secure-view` is the unforgeable access boundary for storage, indexes, Prism, Ripple, UDFs, backup, errors, and observability; cross-capability differential matrices gate releases. |
| Global meta sequencing preserves semantics but may bottleneck post-1.0 sharding | Deliberate correctness/performance tradeoff | The protocol is fixed in §14.6: no shard-local semantic bypass, exact global certification and one-prefix visibility. Batch/pipeline the meta order and publish the ceiling honestly; reopen scalar time only as a new architecture with new Chronicle/branch/Ripple/replay proofs, never as an optimization. |
| Newer runtime surfaces adopted for Aegis (choreographic codegen, coded fan-out) prove less mature than core asupersync | Adoption risk, inherited not created | Each adoption sits behind a protocol trait with the hand-built degenerate mode preserved (static membership, unicast fan-out, hand-written state machines); the lab's dual-run harness compares adopted vs. degenerate paths before any promotion |

---

## Appendix A — On-Disk Object Formats (normative contract)

`RoleTransitionActivationState` is the closed role union `Meta{migration_nonce,transition_ref:StrongRef<CertifiedRoleTransitionRef>,continuity_reservation_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionContinuityReservationReceipt>,target_service_visibility_epoch,phase:ProjectionPreparedHidden{activation_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}|ServicePrepareApplied{prepare_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}|FinalizedPendingIndependentReopen{finalize_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,seal_state:Unsealed|Sealed{seal_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionPromotionRootSeal<Meta>>}}|MetaReopenedAwaitingShardAcks{reopen_confirm_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,seal_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionPromotionRootSeal<Meta>>,independent_reopen_proof_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionIndependentReopenProof<Meta>>}|Complete{completion_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,seal_release_state}}|Shard{migration_nonce,transition_ref:StrongRef<CertifiedRoleTransitionRef>,shard_id,continuity_reservation_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionContinuityReservationReceipt>,target_service_visibility_epoch,phase:ProjectionPreparedHidden{install_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}|OpenedPendingIndependentReopen{open_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,seal_state:Unsealed|Sealed{seal_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionPromotionRootSeal<Shard>>}}|ShardOperationalAwaitingMetaCompletion{reopen_confirm_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,seal_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionPromotionRootSeal<Shard>>,independent_reopen_proof_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionIndependentReopenProof<Shard>>}|Complete{completion_apply_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,seal_release_state}}`. This exact schema supersedes any earlier compact navigation rendering. The state object, reached by the payload's existing strong field, is the canonical owner of role-transition seals/proofs; `None` is legal only outside a role transition. Post-publication visibility/state certificates live in the certificate ledger and are supplied separately to later pin-release/service consumers as `CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>`; the Complete root cannot embed the certificate that certifies itself. Generated construction-DAG negatives reject that future back-edge. Generated phase/bootstrap/admission rows require `RoleTransitionPreparedHidden` through prepare, `OperationalPendingIndependentReopen` through Unsealed/Sealed, `Operational` plus an awaiting phase after reopen, and ordinary service only in `Complete`.

All durable encodings use a declared format major/minor, little-endian fixed integers, canonical unsigned LEB128 only where the field says varint, length-before-allocation, checked arithmetic, and per-kind maximum sizes. Unknown additive-minor fields are length-delimited and skipped; unknown required flags fail closed. `CanonicalScalarProfile` defines float/decimal/string/time bytes. Readers authenticate/validate headers and resource bounds before allocating or decompressing.

**Reference semantics.** Every ObjectId-bearing edge declares a wire tag: `StrongRef{oid}` (always followed); `StrongMarkerRef{marker_ref}` and `StrongCommandRef{command_ref}` (retain the named history object); `ConditionalCoordinateRef{oid,graph,branch}` (sequence-neutral payload whose retention sequence comes from its enclosing committed marker); `ConditionalMarkerRef{marker_ref,axis:Global|Branch(graph,branch)}` and `ConditionalCommandRef{command_ref,axis:Logical}` (followed until an authenticated matching checkpoint/cut); `WeakMarkerIdentity{marker_oid,commit_seq}` (provenance/identity only); `WeakLocator`/`PhysicalRealizationRef` (nonretaining local lookup metadata); or `WeakDigest{digest}` (comparison only). Bare `MarkerRef{marker_oid:[u8;32],commit_seq:u64}` and `CommandRef{command_record_oid:[u8;32],logical_command_seq:u64}` are identities, **not reachability by themselves**; an enclosing tag supplies that. `StrongCommandRef` always means a previously constructed `LogicalCommandRecord`; an applied result cites its already identified input and assigned order only through the exact closed `AppliedControlRef`, `AppliedAbortRef`, or `AuthorityAppliedRef` tags below, never an ambient/local-only assumption.

`ConsensusDomain {database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role:Local|Meta|Shard{shard_id},group_id}` is the indivisible authority/consumer identity in every signed, MACed, indexed, or retained cross-group transcript. `group_id` is also a random 256-bit database-global unique identifier, but no validator relies on uniqueness without matching the complete domain. Later shorthand `authority_group_id`, `consumer_group_id`, or `group_id` in a cross-group schema means the corresponding full `ConsensusDomain`; wire encoders never serialize the scalar alone. `RemoteObjectIdentity {authority_domain:ConsensusDomain,object_oid,object_kind,canonical_digest}` is comparison-only and never a local reachability edge.

`RootAuthorityTrustBody` is the signature-free immutable `{authority_domain:ConsensusDomain,artifact_kind:FreshShardedGenesis|CertifiedLocalRoleTransition,canonical_genesis_or_transition_bytes,target_configuration_oid,target_configuration_canonical_digest,externally_pinned_root_policy_id,expected_root_verification_key_set_digest,threshold,source_identity_or_transition_continuity_commitment,body_digest:BodyDigest<RootAuthorityTrustBody>}`; its registered body transcript includes every preceding field and excludes exactly `body_digest`. `RootAuthorityTrustArtifact` is `{body_ref:StrongRef<RootAuthorityTrustBody>,body_digest:BodyDigest<RootAuthorityTrustBody>,canonical_root_authority_signature_set,signed_transcript_digest:TranscriptDigest<RootAuthorityTrustArtifact>}`. Each root signature signs the domain-separated tuple `{schema/tag,authority_domain,artifact_kind,body_digest,externally_pinned_root_policy_id}`; the artifact transcript includes the body digest and canonically sorted signatures and excludes exactly `signed_transcript_digest`. Validation first resolves the deployment's externally pinned policy, key set, and threshold and byte-matches their IDs/digest to the body, then verifies signatures and transcript; keys merely embedded in the object can never select their own trust root. The canonical genesis/transition bytes and policy evidence remain strongly retained and independently reverified. `RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence` is the nonrecursive imported leaf `{authority_domain:ConsensusDomain,configuration_oid,configuration_canonical_digest,canonical_configuration_bytes,configuration_adoption_raft_index,adoption_log_prefix_digest,member_verification_key_set,payload_predicate_digest,trust_transition:Genesis{root_authority_trust_artifact_ref:StrongRef<RootAuthorityTrustArtifact>}|Successor{predecessor_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence>,joint_transition_transcript,old_configuration_quorum_signatures,new_configuration_quorum_signatures,final_retirement_floor_digest}|ValidatedCheckpointSuccessor{predecessor_anchor_ref:StrongRef<ValidatedRemoteConfigurationAnchor>,joint_transition_transcript,old_configuration_quorum_signatures,new_configuration_quorum_signatures,final_retirement_floor_digest},minimum_configuration_retention_floor,signer_epoch,configuration_quorum_signatures}`. Current-member signatures alone are never a trust root. Embedded canonical configuration bytes recompute the OID/digest and self-bind the complete domain; the transition proves adoption from an already trusted root artifact, predecessor, or anchor. `RemoteConfigurationRef` means a consumer-local `StrongRef<RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence>`.

Authority-local certificate object `T` and its imported representation are deliberately different schemas. `ExportLeaf<T> {domain:ImportedCertificateDomain,target_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,export_projection_version,object_specific_scalar_projection,local_strong_ref_projection[RemoteObjectIdentity],target_closure_inventory_digest,authority_ledger_floor,quorum_signatures}` replaces every authority-local strong reference with a typed remote identity/closure commitment while preserving every verifier-relevant scalar. Its generated projector is injectively bound to the canonical digest of `T`; a consumer never decodes an authority-local `StrongRef` as local. `RemotePayloadAvailabilityEvidence` is the consumer-local leaf `{authority_configuration_evidence_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,certificate_export_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<PayloadAvailabilityCertificate>>,target_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,target_closure_inventory_digest,payload_predicate_digest,receipt_set_commitment,encoding_placement_coverage,failure_domains,authority_retention_floor,signer_epoch,authority_quorum_signatures}`; the authority ledger separately roots the real certificate/receipts.

W12's one cross-consensus edge is `CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<T> {authority_domain:ConsensusDomain,authority_configuration_evidence_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,consumer_domain:ConsensusDomain,target_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,export_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<T>>,target_closure_inventory_digest,payload_availability_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemotePayloadAvailabilityEvidence>,retention_grant_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>}`. Every `StrongRef` inside it targets a complete consumer-local leaf; none targets `T` in the authority group. The generated `T -> ExportLeaf<T>` verifier and field-level schema registry define exactly which refs become remote identities. `grant_id = H("fgdb:remote-grant:v1", authority_domain, consumer_domain, target_identity, grant_nonce)`; consumer and authority maps are keyed by `(authority_domain,grant_id)`, and any collision with nonidentical bytes fails closed. `RemoteGrantTargetRef` is the containing-schema-generated closed union with one typed strong-reference arm per exportable authority-local target kind; it has no generic target arm. `RemoteRetentionGrantSpec` is authority-group input `{grant_id,authority_domain:ConsensusDomain,authority_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,consumer_domain:ConsensusDomain,target_ref:RemoteGrantTargetRef,target_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,target_closure_inventory_digest,payload_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PayloadAvailabilityCertificate>,minimum_authority_checkpoint_floor,grant_nonce}`. The target ref and identity must be the exact registered pair. Authority apply creates `RemoteRetentionGrantRecord {spec_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantSpec>,authority_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,state:Active}` in `RemoteRetentionObligationRoot`, which roots the exact target closure. Its quorum exports `RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence {authority_configuration_evidence_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,grant_record_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,grant_id,consumer_domain:ConsensusDomain,target_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,target_closure_inventory_digest,payload_availability_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemotePayloadAvailabilityEvidence>,authority_order_index,minimum_authority_checkpoint_floor,grant_nonce,signer_epoch,authority_quorum_signatures}`. The consumer may publish the wrapper only after authenticating all leaves and byte-matching both complete domains; the authority ledger, not the evidence, owns the remote target.

Release is an explicit six-transition handshake, never inferred from time or a missing client. (1) After canonically removing/replacing every reference while retaining the grant leaf, the consumer orders `RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestSpec {grant_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>,consumer_release_state_identity:WeakDigest,complete_consumer_root_digest,consumer_checkpoint_floor,successor_grant_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>?,release_nonce}`; apply rechecks the complete consumer root, constructs `RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestRecord {spec_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestSpec>,consumer_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,consumer_no_reference_floor_digest}`, and installs `ReleaseRequested`. That type state cannot acquire or create another local ref under the old grant; any concurrent/new use must first acquire and publish a distinct successor grant. (2) Only afterward does the consumer quorum create authority-local `RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,request_record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestRecord>,complete_consumer_root_digest,consumer_no_reference_floor_digest,successor_grant_identity?,release_nonce,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}` after rechecking the root. A generated specialized `ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestCertificate>` replaces its local refs with remote identities; the later consumer publish control installs this portable leaf as `ReleaseCertified`. (3) The authority imports that leaf and orders `RemoteRetentionReleaseApplySpec {active_grant_record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantRecord>,release_request_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestCertificate>>,expected_active_grant_digest,verified_consumer_no_reference_floor,successor_transfer_proof?}`. Apply rechecks every stronger authority root, removes the active grant, and creates `RemoteRetentionReleaseTombstone {grant_record_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,grant_spec_digest:WeakDigest,target_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,request_certificate_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,release_nonce,authority_command_input_digest:WeakDigest,authority_order_index}` with no strong edge to the active record, spec, target, or apply command. The release command/log retains those inputs only until its verified cut. (4) Only after that applied root does the authority quorum create local `RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,tombstone_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseTombstone>,grant_id,consumer_domain:ConsensusDomain,target_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,release_nonce,authority_order_index,authority_state_root_digest,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}` and its specialized portable ExportLeaf. (5) A later authority publish control atomically creates `RemoteRetentionAckPublishRecord {ack_certificate_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>,ack_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>>,authority_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,summary_key}` and installs `RemoteReleaseSummaryEntry {authority_domain,consumer_domain,grant_id,target_identity,release_nonce,ack_leaf_identity,ack_digest,authority_order_index,published_at_order_index,permanent_release_proof_floor}` in `RemoteReleaseSummaryRoot`. The summary and nonrecursive archive-backed ack leaf remain available through the database's capability-safe permanent release-proof floor and require no new releasable grant merely to prove the old grant released. (6) The consumer imports the portable leaf without a CRSR, rechecks that its current complete root still contains no old-grant ref, orders `ConsumeReleaseAck`, and creates `RemoteRetentionConsumeAckRecord {ack_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>>,consumer_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef,current_no_old_grant_ref_proof_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionNoOldGrantProof>,summary_key}`; only then may it retire request/grant evidence after its checkpoint floor. `AuthorityAppliedRef = Local{command_ref:StrongRef<ControlCommand>,logical_command_seq}|Meta{command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlCommand>,global_logical_command_seq}|Shard{command_ref:StrongRef<ShardControlCommand>,shard_raft_index}` is used only inside its owning group and prevents role ambiguity. A crash at any point leaves an authority target root, permanent release proof, or replayable pending transition; no imported leaf contains an ordinary strong edge into its source group.

W12 history wrappers are distinct rather than overloading the local `LogicalCommandRecord` tag: `StrongGlobalCommandRef{record_oid,global_logical_command_seq}`, `ConditionalGlobalCommandRef{record_oid,global_logical_command_seq,axis=GlobalLogical}`, and `WeakGlobalCommandIdentity{record_oid,global_logical_command_seq}` target `GlobalTxnRecord|GlobalControlRecord`; `ConditionalGlobalTxnInputRef{command_oid,assigned_global_logical_command_seq,axis=GlobalLogical}` targets the already-built `GlobalTxnCommand` and is valid only when the record at that sequence names the same input; `StrongShardCommandRef{record_oid,shard_id,shard_raft_index}`, `ConditionalShardCommandRef{record_oid,shard_id,shard_raft_index,axis=ShardRaft}`, and `WeakShardCommandIdentity{record_oid,shard_id,shard_raft_index}` target `ShardCommandRecord`. Strong variants retain, conditional variants stop only at a verified matching meta/shard checkpoint cut, and weak variants compare only. Cross-group carriage of any target still requires `CertifiedRemoteStrongRef`; these wrappers are only within the owning consensus group.

Canonical result objects that can be produced in either posture use closed role tags rather than pretending every input is local: `AppliedControlRef = Local{command_ref:StrongRef<ControlCommand>,logical_command_seq} | Meta{command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlCommand>,global_logical_command_seq}`; `AppliedAbortRef = LocalControl{command_ref:StrongRef<ControlCommand>,logical_command_seq} | LocalTxn{command_ref:StrongRef<AdmittedTxnAbortCommand>,logical_command_seq} | MetaControl{command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlCommand>,global_logical_command_seq} | MetaTxn{command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnCommand>,global_logical_command_seq}`; and `AuditCandidateRef = Local{blocked_after_logical_command_seq,candidate_state_root_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStateRoot>} | Meta{blocked_after_global_logical_command_seq,candidate_state_root_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStateRoot>}`. The group-role and lifecycle-stage validator accepts only the matching tag: active/unadmitted Local cancellation uses LocalControl, admitted Local abort uses LocalTxn, active/unadmitted Meta cancellation uses MetaControl, and admitted Meta abort uses MetaTxn. These tags are part of the v1 durable schema and the generated spec/result compatibility matrix; no decoder infers posture from ambient configuration.

Key-envelope openers use the separate retaining physical edge `StrongCiphertextRef<T> { logical_oid, ciphertext_id, dek_id, ciphertext_digest, protected_length, key_opener_ref:KeyOpenerRef<T>, recoverability_profile, selected_realizations[CiphertextRealizationRef<T>],payload_availability_certificate_ref:PayloadAvailabilityCertificateRef<T> }`. `KeyOpenerRef<T>`, `CiphertextRealizationRef<T>`, and `PayloadAvailabilityCertificateRef<T>` are generated exact unions whose arms are legal for that ciphertext class and owning role; no bare `StrongRef` or cross-role certificate decodes. Its target ciphertext, complete authenticated realization inventory, opener, and certificate exist before the referring envelope object, and the certificate must prove that the selected set satisfies the named profile under the exact configuration. It pins that `CiphertextId` and the selected recoverable `EncodingId`/`PlacementId` set; recoding or movement first creates a successor envelope/grant edge with a newly certified set before the old set can retire, while re-encryption cannot substitute a new ciphertext under a live reference. This is intentionally stronger than ordinary logical reachability and is included in GC, reconfiguration, backup, and key-destruction floors.

The schemas below use a flattened prose rendering, but **names and suffixes never imply reference strength**. Every field's exact type, cardinality, identity class, target, role/posture predicate, retention/cut behavior, and `PriorObject` construction annotation come from §5.1's generated `durable_fields.toml`; the rendering checker rejects any brace field that lacks or disagrees with its row. A compact phrase such as “typed mandatory inventory” is a named closed sub-schema, never an open bag or slash-separated set of ambient refs. Bootstrap-slot root identity is an external GC root and is followed as strong. Pending/prepared traversal treats a registered `ConditionalCoordinateRef` as strong because no committed-marker cut context exists. Archive and prior-incarnation identities are separate nonlocal classes and can never enter the current local ObjectId mark set. Every concrete target of a strong or currently followed conditional edge must exist before the parent encoder runs. Recursive persistent nodes are legal only in that child-before-parent direction. Generated schema/reference/DAG checkers compare the field table, Appendix rendering, Rust encoders, and registry rows; they reject unresolved, untyped, role-invalid, self, mutual, or future-result cycles and fail G0 on drift. Unknown tags or context fail closed.

**RootSlot + RootBootstrap.** Each slot is exactly 4096 bytes at offsets 0 and 4096:

```text
RootSlot {
  magic="FGRT", format_major:u16, format_minor:u16, slot_generation:u64,
  local_writer_fence_epoch:u64, database_id:[u8;16],
  database_security_namespace_id:[u8;32], cluster_incarnation:u64,
  incarnation_continuity_profile_id:u16,
  cluster_incarnation_continuity_digest:[u8;32], continuity_cas_version:u64,
  service_visibility_epoch:u64,
  root_manifest_oid:[u8;32],
  bootstrap: RootBootstrap, reserved_zeroes, tear_checksum:[u8;32]
}
RootBootstrap {
  root_encoding_id:[u8;32], root_placement_id:[u8;32],
  root_placement_epoch:u64, failure_domain_policy_id:[u8;32],
  root_failure_domain_id:[u8;32], segment_id:[u8;16], offset:u64,
  encoded_len:u64, root_symbol_inventory_digest:[u8;32],
  object_kind:u16, canonical_plaintext_len:u64, codec_profile:u32,
  compressed_len:u64, data_crypto_profile:u32, dek_id:[u8;32],
  nonce_len:u8, nonce_or_siv:[u8;32], object_tag_len:u8,
  fec_profile:u32, transfer_length:u64, oti_common:u64, oti_scheme:u32,
  symbol_size:u32, source_block_count:u32, symbol_auth_profile:u16,
  ciphertext_id:[u8;32], ciphertext_digest:[u8;32], opener_kind:u16,
  oid_key_id:[u8;32], opener_payload_len:u16, opener_payload:[u8;1024],
  opener_digest:[u8;32]
}
```

Unused nonce/opener bytes are zero. Profile registries fix bounds and tag lengths. Together with `root_manifest_oid`, these fields reproduce the root's exact `CipherDescriptorWithoutDigest`, complete `EncodingDescriptorWithoutId`, and specialized `PlacementDescriptorWithoutId { encoding_id, root_placement_epoch, failure_domain_policy_id, ContiguousSpan { root_failure_domain_id, segment_id, offset, encoded_len, root_symbol_inventory_digest } }` byte-for-byte. Recovery scans the declared span, authenticates canonical SymbolRecords, verifies the sorted inventory commitment, and recomputes `root_placement_id` before decode. `opener_payload` is a versioned bundle of inline wraps or authenticated KMS/HSM locators sufficient to recover both the named root DEK and immutable `K_oid` without consulting the object index; its digest covers kind, key IDs, locators/wraps, and namespace/incarnation. The authenticated root repeats the exact database/security-namespace/incarnation tuple, and recovery rejects disagreement before following its closure. A structurally valid highest-generation slot is never silently rolled back after authentication/closure failure (§5.1).

**Filesystem durability and writer takeover.** `FilesystemDurabilityProfile` is a signed/checked-in artifact `{ profile_id, profile_version, os/kernel_range, allowed_filesystems_and_mount_predicates, forbidden_layers/options, required_lock_primitive, owner_death_rule, stable_inode_rule, slot_write_rule, file_sync_rule, directory_sync_rule, rename_no_replace_rule, cache_flush_assumption, probe_suite_oid }`. `FGFS-LINUX-LOCAL-V1` has exactly §5.1's ext4/XFS allowlist; a profile/mount not matching every predicate is not writable. A local `FilesystemInstanceRecord { profile_id, probe_suite_oid, mount_id, filesystem_type, canonical_mount_options, device_chain_digest, directory_device_inode, manifest_device_inode, checked_at, result_digest }` is diagnostic evidence, never a transferable proof or semantic root.

The takeover checker accepts only `NoGuard -> LeaseHeld(identity) -> ClosureRecovered(old_epoch) -> PendingFenceGuard(old_epoch+1) -> FenceRootDurableAndReread(old_epoch+1) -> WriterEnabled(FenceToken)`. Creation is `NoGuard -> StagingInodeLease -> PendingCreationGuard(epoch=1) -> CompleteStagingRootVerified -> RenameNoReplaceAndDirectorySync -> CanonicalRootReread -> WriterEnabled`. `PendingFenceGuard` is linear and can encode/seal exactly one fence-only `RootManifest` preserving the recovered state plus its inactive `RootSlot`; `PendingCreationGuard` can encode/seal only the registered Genesis closure and epoch-1 slot. Neither type implements user-segment, prepared-owner, receipt, Raft, canonical-transition, or general-root traits. `FenceToken` is nonserializable and nonconstructible outside the VFS/commit boundary; it binds the live lease handle, stable directory/root identity, profile, slot generation, and epoch. The fence-only root/slot must retain the exact prior canonical logical root, Raft/audit candidate, prepared root, and durable/applied/visible positions. Every immutable segment trailer records `{ database_security_namespace_id, cluster_incarnation, local_writer_fence_epoch, segment_id, sealed_len, inventory_digest, trailer_checksum }`; after promotion the local VFS refuses a seal, prepared-root update, receipt signature, Raft append acknowledgement/proposal, or root publication without the matching live token. Already receipted/ordered records from an older epoch remain strong obligations but cannot authorize new old-epoch publication. The crash matrix cuts after every lease/identity/recovery/object-sync/slot-write/slot-sync/staging-rename/directory-sync/reread step and includes owner death, path replacement, mount drift, stale descriptors, epoch overflow, competing creators, orphan staging, and a lying-sync profile that must be rejected.

**Cipher/encoding/placement records and SymbolRecord.** `CipherDescriptorWithoutDigest` is `{ logical_oid, object_kind, canonical_plaintext_len, codec_profile, compressed_len, data_crypto_profile, dek_id, object_nonce_or_siv, object_tag_len }`. Object AAD is the domain string plus logical OID and this canonical descriptor. `CiphertextRecord` is `{ descriptor, ciphertext_id, ciphertext_digest, object_tag_digest, protected_length }`; `CiphertextId` hashes descriptor+ciphertext+object tag. Immutable `EncodingDescriptorWithoutId` is `{ ciphertext_id, fec_profile, transfer_length, oti_common, oti_scheme, symbol_size, source_block_count, symbol_auth_profile }`, and `EncodingId` hashes the domain/version plus its **complete canonical bytes**. Separately, `PlacementDescriptorWithoutId` is `{ encoding_id, placement_epoch, failure_domain_policy, location_form }`, where `location_form = ContiguousSpan{failure_domain_id,segment_id,offset,encoded_len,symbol_inventory_digest} | Explicit{sorted_symbol_inventory_and_locators,failure_domains}`. `PlacementId` hashes the placement domain/version plus exactly that descriptor; immutable `PlacementRecord { placement_id, descriptor }` merely stores and cross-checks it. Moving/adding symbols creates a new placement record without changing encoding identity. KEK/wrap epoch is absent from both ciphertext and encoding identity. The authenticated local physical-realization index maps `CiphertextId`, `EncodingId`, and `PlacementId` to these records with nonretaining locators. An ordinary decoder obtains the complete descriptor/OTI/auth profile from an authenticated `EncodingDescriptorWithoutId` (or FGP's authenticated encoding prelude) before accepting any symbol; a bare `SymbolRecord` is never self-authorizing.

```text
SymbolRecord {
  magic="FGEC", format_version:u16, header_len:u16, record_len:u32,
  logical_oid:[u8;32], ciphertext_id:[u8;32], encoding_id:[u8;32],
  object_kind:u16, source_block:u32, esi:u32, symbol_len:u32,
  transfer_length:u64, oti_common:u64, oti_scheme:u32, flags:u32,
  symbol_mac_profile:u16, symbol_mac_len:u16,
  payload:[u8;symbol_len], symbol_mac:[u8;symbol_mac_len]
}
```

The symbol MAC key/transcript are exactly §5.1's domain-separated construction. All framing/OTI/length fields and payload are MACed. Truncated/noncanonical tails are ignored only in proved-unreferenced staging; a reachable sealed tail triggers authenticated repair or fail-closed recovery.

**LogicalStatePayload / LogicalStateRoot.** The following is a compact Local navigation projection of generated `LogicalStatePayload`, not a field-exhaustive alternate schema: `{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,bootstrap_phase:Genesis|CheckpointInstalled|Operational|RecoveryReconciling|RecoveryAbandoned|OperationalPendingIndependentReopen,applied_logical_command_seq,applied_commit_seq,global_transaction_marker_head:StrongMarkerRef?,branch_head_map_root(entries carry StrongMarkerRef),branch_manifest_root,branch_key_epoch_registry_root,key_envelope/grant_registry_root,branch_merge_ledger_root,graph_partition_state_roots,catalog_root,schema_roots,id_allocator_root,identity_continuity_record_ref,constraint_state_roots,resource_ledger_root,privacy_ledger_root,privacy_continuity_record_ref,dp_request_index_root,txn_outcome_index_root:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeIndex>,local_begin_idempotency_index_root:StrongRef<LocalBeginIdempotencyIndex>,local_statement_index_root:StrongRef<LocalStatementIndex>,local_txn_outcome_summary_root:StrongRef<LocalTxnOutcomeSummaryRoot>,local_delta_batch_index_root:StrongRef<LocalDeltaBatchIndex>,local_delta_frontier,terminal_certification_reservation_root:StrongRef<TerminalCertificationReservationRoot<Local>>,recovery_checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>?,retained_stream_floor,retention_map_root:StrongRef<RetentionMap>,derived_generation_registry_root:StrongRef<DerivedGenerationRegistry>,replay_registry_root,logical_hlc_state,topology_epoch,policy_epoch,revocation_index,replicated_key_wrap/signing/capability-verifier roots,audit_ticket_index_root,audit_root,transparency_root,backup_registry_root,restore_state_ref:StrongRef<RestoreState>?,restore_retention_anchor:RestoreRetentionAnchor<Local>,gc_decision_root,remote_retention_authority_root:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionObligationRoot>,remote_retention_consumer_root:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionConsumerRoot>,remote_configuration_trust_root:StrongRef<RemoteConfigurationTrustRoot>,sharding_migration_state_ref:StrongRef<ShardingMigrationState>?,config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>?}`. Independent result/cursor/artifact delivery, the imported audit-continuity release head, and the audit visibility pipeline are protocol slots, not fields silently omitted from this semantic projection. The exact bounded `LocalDeltaBatchIndex` and cut lifecycle are defined with `LogicalDeltaBatch` below. `ShardingMigrationState` is `FreezePendingAudit{freeze_ref:StrongRef<ShardingFreezeRecord>}|FrozenVisible{freeze_ref,visibility_advance_maintenance_identity:MaintenanceAppliedRef}|RoleTransitionLocked{begin_ref:StrongRef<BeginRoleTransitionRecord>}|Resolved{terminal_identity}`; no variant names a future semantic command. Remote roots are empty reserved values before W12 preparation. Fresh bootstrap permits Genesis -> CheckpointInstalled -> Operational; restore/reopen/abandonment follow the exact later state machine and the retention anchor must match each phase. `LogicalStateRoot` is `{payload_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStatePayload>,logical_command_head:StrongCommandRef?}`; fresh genesis alone has no head.

For Local, the activated **semantic** state-slot closure additionally requires `security_state_binding_root`, `valid_time_slice_registry_root`, `constraint_reservation_index_root`, `escrow_ledger_root`, `allocation_reservation_and_disposition_root`, `txn_ownership_directory_root`, `txn_capability_lineage_root`, `artifact_output_request_registry_root`, `replay_manifest_registry_root`, `format_compatibility_state_ref`, `bulk_load_registry_root`, `derived_build_registry_root`, `audit_terminal_freeze_index_root`, and `restore_registry_root`. `local_result_delivery_index_root`, `result_independent_retention_index_root`, and `delivery_lease_registry_root` are obsolete alternate names for the one canonical Protocol slot `result_independent_retention_index`; none is encoded in `LogicalStatePayload`. Semantic owners store only delivery ID/detachment commitments, while RootManifest's protocol root owns the bytes. The audit pipeline is likewise not a payload slot, avoiding a content cycle. Feature-disabled semantic slots use registered empty/None recipes. `state_payload_fields.toml` supplies only semantic fields; `protocol_state_fields.toml` supplies the result/cursor/output/audit slots and forbids duplicate aliases.

The generated common header of every Local/Meta/Shard payload also contains `{incarnation_continuity_profile_id,cluster_incarnation_continuity_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>?,cluster_incarnation_continuity_digest,continuity_cas_version,service_visibility_epoch,time_authority_binding:Local{registry_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityRegistry<Local>>}|Meta{registry_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityRegistry<Meta>>}|Shard{active_projection_ref:StrongRef<ActiveMetaProjectionRoot>,projected_registry_digest,projected_selected_profile_oid,projection_meta_prefix},restore_registry_root:StrongRef<RestoreRegistry>,terminal_audit_freeze_index_root:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeIndex>}`. `DirectoryBound` uses no external record ref and a fixed local continuity digest; `ExternalCas` requires `Some` and exact byte equality with the live authority. `restore_state_ref` is present only during a live restore, while `restore_registry_root` retains terminal projection/promotion/tombstone evidence. The audit-freeze index is active only in Local/Meta and is the canonical empty reserved value in Shard. Shards may validate only the exact current active/closing/draining profile projection certified in their active Meta projection; they cannot originate profile changes. RootManifest and RootSlot repeat and cross-check the same continuity/profile/digest/version/visibility tuple.

**Local 1.0 durable transaction formats.** Local and W12 deliberately share only embedded value schemas (`TxnCompletionState`, posture-tagged `SemanticTerminalDescriptor`, and `TerminalPublicOutcome`) plus public protocol semantics. They do not share durable registration, workspace, statement, result-owner, outcome, audit-owner, claim, or command objects. A Local decoder rejects every `Global*`, Meta, shard, certified-remote, or global-prefix identity in this family, and a W12 decoder rejects every Local identity. `LocalAuditTicketOwner` is `Operation{txn_token,registration_binding_digest}|Statement{registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,statement_seq,statement_idempotency_key}|Control{control_operation_tag,request_idempotency_key,request_basis_commitment}`. `LocalAuditTicketClaimRecord` is `{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,owner:LocalAuditTicketOwner,claiming_control_ref:StrongRef<ControlCommand>,claim_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,request_basis_commitment}` and is constructed during the registration/claim apply before its owning Local record. `LocalOperationAuditAdmission` is `NotRequired{evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence}|PendingUnclaimed{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,ticket_admission_record_ref:StrongCommandRef,operation_request_basis_commitment}|Claimed{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,claim_record_ref:StrongRef<LocalAuditTicketClaimRecord>,owner:LocalAuditTicketOwner::Operation,operation_request_basis_commitment}`. `LocalStatementAuditAdmission` has the analogous NotRequired/PendingUnclaimed/Claimed forms with `LocalAuditTicketOwner::Statement`; protected Local controls use the Control arm through their exact audit admission. No Local field can encode a global transaction ID, registration generation, GlobalControlRecord, or GlobalControlCommand.

The generated Local payload schema corrects the compact navigation list above by requiring `local_conflict_index_ref:StrongRef<LocalConflictIndex>` immediately after the statement/outcome roots. Genesis installs the canonical empty index. Its checkpoint vector, `ConfigPayloadFloor`, mandatory inventory, backup closure, restore transform/reconciliation, GC root inventory, key-destruction scan, and role-transition projection each have a generated required row; omission disables SERIALIZABLE operation and is a G0 failure, not a recoverable cache miss.

The same normative schema requires `policy_root_ref:StrongRef<SecurityPolicyRoot>`, `revocation_root_ref:StrongRef<RevocationRoot>`, `constraint_state_directory_root:StrongRef<ConstraintStateDirectoryRoot>`, `local_constraint_reservation_root:StrongRef<LocalConstraintReservationRoot>`, `resource_ledger_root:StrongRef<ResourceLedgerState>`, `escrow_ledger_root:StrongRef<EscrowLedgerRoot>`, and the valid-time contract/slice/temporal-constraint roots activated by the schema. Scalar policy/revocation fields are cross-checks only. Genesis installs canonical empty roots or DenyAll security policy as configured. Every generated checkpoint/floor/inventory/backup/restore/GC/key/transition row covers them; a generic `constraint_state_roots` or untyped `resource_ledger_root` rendering does not satisfy the registry.

`LocalConflictAttemptKey` is `{txn_token_digest,registration_identity}`. `LocalConflictEdge` is `{kind:wr|ww|rw,from:LocalConflictAttemptKey,to:LocalConflictAttemptKey,coordinate_or_predicate_domain,proof_ref:LocalConflictProofRef,first_observed_statement_seq,edge_digest}` where `LocalConflictProofRef` is a generated closed union over version, absence/range, path, constraint, and final-write proof objects. `LocalConflictEntry` is `{registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,registered_snapshot,start_logical_command_seq,terminal_logical_command_seq?,state:Active|PrepareAdmitted{admission_generation}|Committed|Aborted|ReadClosed,statement_frontier,workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalCumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,cumulative_write_summary_ref:StrongRef<LocalWriteSummary>?,incoming_edges[LocalConflictEdge],outgoing_edges[LocalConflictEdge],overlap_interval:{start_prefix,closed_prefix?},detail_state:Detailed|Compacted{summary_ref:StrongRef<LocalConflictSummaryLeaf>}}`. Arrays/maps use canonical key/digest order and exact deduplication; an edge must appear byte-identically in both endpoint projections.

`LocalConflictIndex` is `{generation,basis_logical_command_seq,entries[LocalConflictAttemptKey->LocalConflictEntry],safe_snapshot_frontier,overlap_close_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalConflictOverlapFloor>,root_digest:BodyDigest<LocalConflictIndex>}`. `LocalConflictIndexBasis` is the nonretaining typed `{basis_state:WeakStateIdentity,index_generation,index_root_digest,basis_logical_command_seq,safe_snapshot_frontier,overlap_close_floor_digest}` used by serialization/final-certification inputs; a bare `WeakStateIdentity` is insufficient. Registration atomically inserts Active with workspace zero. Every successful statement publication atomically replaces that exact entry with its successor statement/workspace/witness/write summary and all newly proved bidirectional edges. Prepare admission replays `may_affect` in both directions against every overlapping entry, reruns the registered SCC algorithm, and atomically selects PrepareAdmitted; commit, admitted abort, active cancel, or read close installs the terminal prefix and state in the same command as outcome/attempt changes. Terminal completion changes no serialization decision but retains the entry through its postcondition lifetime.

`LocalConflictOverlapFloor` is `{closed_through_start_prefix,safe_snapshot_frontier,oldest_live_overlapping_start,retained_detailed_attempt_key_commitment,retained_edge_proof_commitment,checker_version}`. `LocalConflictCompactionSpec {expected_index_basis:LocalConflictIndexBasis,sorted_selected_terminal_keys,sorted_summary_leaf_refs[StrongRef<LocalConflictSummaryLeaf>],no_future_overlap_edge_proof_ref:StrongRef<LocalNoFutureConflictEdgeProof>,expected_oldest_live_overlapping_start,expected_attempt_outcome_statement_workspace_retention_discharge,terminal_audit_gate}` may replace detail only when every possible future transaction has a start/snapshot beyond the selected entry's closed overlap interval and no live witness can add an edge; it never deletes the authenticated summary or changes public outcomes. Every follower reconstructs `may_affect`, verifies symmetric edges/proofs and workspace transport, and reruns SCC before signing `LocalSerializationCertificate`, whose `snapshot_and_conflict_index_basis` field is exactly `LocalConflictIndexBasis`. Missing/stale/asymmetric evidence, unsupported exact witness semantics, or resource exhaustion conservatively aborts/waits. Write-skew, multi-edge rw cycles, same-I/O-batch writers, read-only dangerous structures, statement-workspace transport, restart, checkpoint, compaction, and detail-retirement races are permanent oracle/model tests.

`LocalBeginIdempotencyIndex` maps `(database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,tenant,principal,scoped_begin_idempotency_key)` to exactly `Reserved{reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalBeginReservationRecord>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|BoundDetailed{registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|BoundCompacted{summary_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnOutcomeSummaryRoot>,leaf_key_digest,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|NeverRegisteredDetailed{terminal_ref:StrongRef<LocalNeverRegisteredRecord>,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Local>>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|NeverRegisteredCompacted{summary_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnOutcomeSummaryRoot>,leaf_key_digest,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Local>>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|ExpiredTombstone{scoped_key_digest,txn_token_digest,capability_commitment,expiry_evidence_digest}`. `LocalBeginReservationSpec` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,canonical_begin_request_digest,operation_class:ReadOnly|ReadWrite,isolation_profile,transaction_error_policy,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,scoped_begin_idempotency_key,expected_index_absence:WeakAbsenceProof,staging_resource_reservation_ref:StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>,operation_audit_admission:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::PendingUnclaimed|NotRequired,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. The header is the sole namespace/incarnation/service/tenant/principal/ceiling/policy/revocation source and byte-matches the replay object, resource reservation, and audit admission. Apply constructs `LocalBeginReservationRecord {authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,source_spec_ref:StrongRef<LocalBeginReservationSpec>,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,canonical_begin_request_digest,txn_token,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,capability_commitment,reserved_at_logical_command_seq,staging_generation}`, installs Reserved with the replay ref, and constructs `TxnOutcomeRecord::BeginStaging`; only that visible state permits BEGIN_ACCEPTED. Byte-identical retry returns the same identities. `LocalBeginTerminalSpec {reservation_ref,internal_reason_class,no_registration_or_effect_proof_ref,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Local>>,terminal_audit_gate}` alone consumes Reserved and creates `LocalNeverRegisteredRecord {reservation_ref,internal_reason_class,no_registration_or_effect_proof_ref,public_projection_bundle_ref,terminal_audit_gate,applied_control_ref}` plus NeverRegisteredDetailed. The bundle's internal subject/reason/request/domain byte-match the spec; deterministic apply invents no reason mapping, receipt, handle, or response bytes. A required ticket resolves directly from PendingUnclaimed with `AuditResolutionOrigin::Unclaimed`. Timeout, disconnect, or memory loss alone is not terminal evidence, and a terminal begin key can never later bind. The spec/record decode the replay object, require request_kind ExplicitBegin and exact request/domain/authority/profile/window equality, and derive txn_token/capability commitment from it; no duplicate inline capability encoding is trusted.

`LocalAttemptRegistrationSpec` has the closed binding `ExplicitBegin{reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalBeginReservationRecord>,expected_index_value:Reserved,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|Autocommit{autocommit_idempotency_key,canonical_request_digest,classification_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalStatementClassification>,expected_absence_proof:WeakAbsenceProof,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}` plus `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,authority_permit_ref:AuthorityPermitRef<Local>,requested_snapshot_bounds,txn_operation_ceiling:TxnOperationCeiling,transaction_error_policy,initial_workspace_recipe,operation_audit_admission:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::PendingUnclaimed|NotRequired,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. Apply verifies the header, permit, replay object, begin reservation, audit admission, snapshot, and ceiling all name one complete Local binding; verifies any autocommit classification is within the ceiling; atomically claims the operation ticket when required; changes explicit Reserved -> BoundDetailed or creates the autocommit BoundDetailed lineage under the scoped client key; and constructs `LocalAttemptRegistration {authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,source_spec_ref,applied_control_ref,reservation_ref?,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,request_kind:ExplicitBegin|AutocommitExecute,txn_token,txn_operation_ceiling,autocommit_classification_ref?,registered_snapshot,authority_permit_ref,transaction_error_policy,operation_audit_admission:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,registered_at_logical_command_seq}` followed by workspace generation zero and the succeeding `TxnOutcomeRecord::Registered`. A Local token has exactly one attempt; there is no Local attempt-family generation. No statement/storage observation may precede visible registration. Both arms decode and byte-match the replay record; ExplicitBegin must match the reservation's same ref, while Autocommit requires request_kind AutocommitExecute and stores the scoped client key and classification before work. Exact lost EXECUTE retry returns the same registration/classification/outcome capability; body or authority drift fails.

`LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,authority_relation_to_registration:Exact|Narrowed{nonwidening_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuthorityNonwideningProof>},workspace_generation,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>?,through_statement_seq,statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalStatementRegistration>?,published_intent_batch_ref:StrongRef<GraphIntentBatch>?,overlay_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalWorkspaceOverlayRoot>,cumulative_effect_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalCumulativeEffectRoot>,cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalCumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,cumulative_mutation_potential,branch_key_epoch_commitment}`. Generation zero contains the registration's exact header, canonical empty overlay, and ProvenReadOnly potential. Every successor carries the same or a proved narrower complete binding; a policy/revocation/service/principal/purpose/ceiling change without the registered nonwidening proof aborts before observation. `LocalStatementIndex` maps `(registration_identity,statement_seq)` and its scoped idempotency key to the same unique entry `Open{registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalStatementRegistration>}|Published{publication_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,output:LocalStatementPublishedOutput}|Failed{abort_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,stable_failure_class}|Abandoned{abort_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,typed_reason}`. `LocalStatementRegistrationSpec` binds `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,registration_ref,exact_next_statement_seq,statement_idempotency_key,classification_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalStatementClassification>,classification_digest,canonical_statement_digest,parameter_digest,requested_result_answer_contract,expected_prior_statement_frontier,expected_workspace_generation_ref,authority_nonwidening_proof_ref?,transaction_error_policy,statement_audit_admission:LocalStatementAuditAdmission::PendingUnclaimed|NotRequired,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. Apply byte-matches the current full binding, classification, bound query/parameters, attempt ceiling, and workspace relation; requires no Open statement; claims the distinct Local statement owner when required; constructs `LocalStatementRegistration {authority_bound_header,source_spec_ref,applied_control_ref,registration_ref,statement_seq,statement_idempotency_key,classification_ref,derived_statement_class,canonical_statement_digest,parameter_digest,result_answer_contract,workspace_generation_ref,transaction_error_policy,statement_audit_admission:LocalStatementAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,registered_snapshot}`, and inserts Open. Exact retry joins; any sequence/key/classification/body/workspace/authority/audit drift fails.

`LocalSerializationCertificate` is a same-group certificate `{SameGroupCertificateHeader,registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,lifecycle_basis:Statement{statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalStatementRegistration>,prior_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,successor_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>}|Prepare{terminal_admission_basis_digest}|ReadClose{read_close_basis_digest},snapshot_and_conflict_index_basis,complete_read_witness_refs[LocalReadWitnessRef],complete_write_summary_refs[LocalWriteSummaryRef],candidate_edges[],retained_overlap_projection_digest,scc_verifier_version,acyclic_result,conservative_fallbacks[],certificate_attempt_plan_ref,quorum_signatures}`. `LocalReadWitnessRef` and `LocalWriteSummaryRef` are generated closed unions over the registered Local witness/summary targets; raw StrongRef bags are forbidden. Every follower reconstructs the Local SSI graph and fixed snapshot/workspace inputs. `LocalStatementPublicationSpec` is `{statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalStatementRegistration>,registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,expected_index_value:Open,statement_audit_admission:LocalStatementAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,read_access_admission_ref:StrongRef<ReadAccessAdmissionRecord>,read_result_release_decision_ref:StrongRef<ReadResultReleaseDecisionRecord>,local_serialization_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalSerializationCertificate>,expected_prior_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,successor_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,successor_cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalCumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,successor_cumulative_effect_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalCumulativeEffectRoot>,published_output:LocalStatementPublishedOutput,expected_statement_and_outcome_frontiers,terminal_audit_gate}`. `LocalStatementPublishedOutput` is `NoResult{no_result_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoResultProof>,terminal_delivery_frontier_digest}|Buffered{manifest_ref:StrongRef<LocalBufferedResultManifest>,canonical_result_digest,delivery_policy_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryPolicy<Local>>,inline_activation_recipe:InlineResultDeliveryActivationRecipe<Local>?}`. The optional recipe is legal only when this same apply is provably the next gap-free visible candidate and the gate is NotRequired; otherwise it must be absent. Tag, statement class, result owner, schema/answer contract/digest, prior/successor workspace, witness/effect roots, security binding, and ticket owner must byte-match. Buffered apply creates the role-valid applied publication reference, resource transfer, and independent-retention record; it creates `ActiveDeliverable` only through a legal inline activation, otherwise `PendingVisibility` with no lease/capability/expiry. It then changes Open to Published, advances the statement/workspace/delivery frontiers, and constructs the immediately succeeding `TxnOutcomeRecord::Registered`. A pending candidate remains invisible until its exact statement pipeline entry is released by `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec`, whose matching activation recipe installs the first lease. `LocalStatementAbortSpec` alone changes Open to `Failed|Abandoned`, resolves that statement ticket, proves no result/workspace effect, releases named resources, advances its no-result terminal frontier, and applies the registration-pinned `ContinueAttempt|AbortAttempt` policy.

`result_owner_arms.toml` is the sole authoritative family for manifest owner, pre-apply owner basis, post-apply semantic owner, delivery owner, retention owner, disposition, resume subject, and public owner commitment. It generates role-exact arms for `TerminalRead`, `AutocommitWriteTerminal`, `Statement`, `DpResult`, and `ReplayExecution`; an arm cannot be added in prose to only one union. `ReplayExecution` binds its replay-execution registration/publication identities, manifest/result contract, and execution request digest, and uses the same protocol delivery lifecycle. `LocalBufferedResultOwner`, `GeneratedResultDeliveryOwnerBasis<Role>`, `ResultSemanticOwnerRef<Role>`, and their Meta forms are generated views of this registry. `LocalBufferedResultManifest` is `{owner:LocalBufferedResultOwner,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,canonical_schema_ref:StrongRef<ResultSchema>,public_schema_digest,canonical_encoding_version,result_answer_contract,row_count,byte_count,ordered_chunks[CanonicalResultChunkDescriptor],canonical_result_digest,resource_charge_ref:StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>}` and obeys the exact public transcripts. `ResultDeliveryServiceAuthority` is `DirectOrdinary{continuity_digest,service_catalog_version}|PromotionReceiptRequired{promotion_manifest_digest,expected_catalog_predecessor_version,promotion_receipt_digest}`. Exhaustive generation/round-trip tests reject missing arms and role substitution.

The nonexpiring previsibility object is `ResultDeliveryPolicy<Role> {role,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,owner_basis:GeneratedResultDeliveryOwnerBasis<Role>,manifest_ref:ResultManifestRef,canonical_result_digest,result_answer_contract,release_subject_digest,pre_minted_delivery_id,initial_lease_generation,service_authority:ResultDeliveryServiceAuthority,permitted_time_authority_profile_set,min_first_delivery_window,nominal_lease_duration,maximum_lease_duration,chunk_replay_profile,maximum_replay_count,initial_capability_generation,initial_capability_nonce,capability_verifier_key_ref:StrongRef<DurableCapabilityVerifierKey>,body_digest}`. It contains policy/duration, not an absolute window, lease, or capability bytes; it cannot authorize output or expiry. The owner/manifest/resource escrow and policy remain durable while audit is pending, so arbitrary head-of-line delay cannot consume a client window.

`ResultDeliveryLeaseTimeBasis<Role>` is built only for an immediately visible inline result or after a pending result has reached `ReleasedAwaitingActivation`: `{policy_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryPolicy<Role>>,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,exact_release_subject,delivery_id,lease_generation,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,minimum_remaining_first_delivery_window,successor_capability_generation,successor_capability_nonce,capability_verifier_key_ref:StrongRef<DurableCapabilityVerifierKey>,body_digest:BodyDigest<ResultDeliveryLeaseTimeBasis<Role>>}`. Its policy projection, current same-or-narrower complete authority binding, service epoch/receipt, owner, manifest, digest, duration bounds, verifier, and immutable release evidence must byte-match; `expires_at-not_before` is checked and the signed validation interval's latest instant plus the minimum window must not exceed `expires_at`. `SubscriptionDeliveryLeaseTimeBasis<Role>`, `ArtifactOutputLeaseTimeBasis<Role>`, and `ProtectedErrorLeaseTimeBasis<Role>` use the same complete header/window/verifier contract with their own noninterchangeable owner and policy fields. `InlineResultDeliveryActivationRecipe<Role>` and `ResultDeliveryActivationRecipe<Role>` both contain `{time_basis_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryLeaseTimeBasis<Role>>,issuance_time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,prebuilt_initial_resume_replay_ref:StrongRef<ResumeCapabilityReplayRecord>,expected_released_retention_ref?}` and differ only in the registered construction site. The latter is consumed by `ProtectedOutputActivationSpec::Result`, never by `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec`. Missing/stale time evidence delays only activation; a fresh recipe may retry against the same released owner. Thus a hidden/released-but-inactive result has no aging lease, and audit visibility never depends on time service health.

Visible activation constructs `LocalResultDeliveryLease {owner:LocalResultDeliveryOwner,manifest_ref,policy_ref,time_basis_ref,delivery_id,lease_generation,canonical_result_digest,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,issuance_time_validation_evidence_ref,chunk_replay_profile,maximum_replay_count,successor_capability_fields,activation_applied_ref:ResultActivationAppliedRef}` where `ResultActivationAppliedRef` is exactly `InlineSemantic{applied_control_ref,logical_command_seq}|ProtocolActivation{maintenance_applied_ref,maintenance_index}`. The lease header must byte-match its time basis and release decision. `LocalResultDeliveryOwner` is `TerminalRead{registration_ref,read_close_applied_ref}|AutocommitWriteTerminal{registration_ref,terminal_completion_applied_ref,terminal_ready_outcome_ref}|Statement{registration_ref,statement_registration_ref,publication_applied_ref}|DpResult{request_key_digest,commit_result_applied_ref}`. `LocalResultDeliveryIndex` is only the stable Local alias for the Protocol-plane `ResultIndependentRetentionIndex<Local>`; no field with that name is encoded in `LogicalStatePayload`. `DeliveryLeaseExpiryEvidence` is the pre-order `{lease:Local{lease_ref:StrongRef<LocalResultDeliveryLease>}|Sharded{lease_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryLease>},expected_active_deliverable_retention_state_digest,deadline,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,stable_expiry_reason,validator_version}`; evidence must be Expired for this exact active lease/deadline/release action, and neither PendingVisibility nor ReleasedAwaitingActivation is an inhabitable subject. `ResultReleaseEvidence<Local>` is exactly `ClientRelease{receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuthenticatedClientResultReleaseReceipt<Local>>}|LeaseExpired{expiry_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DeliveryLeaseExpiryEvidence>}`. Session/region death only closes transport obligations, and attempt terminalization never releases promised result bytes.

`ResultResourceEscrow<Role>` is the Protocol-plane conservation object `{role,semantic_resource_transition_ref:StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>,semantic_detachment_identity,delivery_id,charge_class_and_amount,resource_profile_oid,state:Owned,body_digest}`. Initial semantic result detachment atomically debits/transfers the exact charge out of `ResourceLedgerState` once and creates this escrow plus PendingVisibility, ReleasedAwaitingActivation, or legal inline ActiveDeliverable in the same RootManifest publication.
