Release/activation/resume preserve Owned; ACK/explicit release/profiled expiry consumes it and constructs a compact protocol disposition whose amount exactly equals the escrow. Later maintenance never mutates the semantic ledger. Cursor, artifact, and protected-error delivery use distinct typed escrow arms under the same conservation registry.

`LocalResultDeliveryTransitionSpec` is the Local Maintenance `Acknowledge|ReleaseUnacked` union with exact owner/manifest/lease/ActiveDeliverable/digest/prior-state/escrow/audit fields. Acknowledge requires `AuthenticatedClientResultAckReceipt<Local>`; release requires role-valid release/expiry evidence. Apply creates one ACK/release record, terminal retention successor, and protocol escrow disposition. It never changes a semantic payload or delivery frontier—the semantic command already detached that frontier. Before terminal publication it fences the sender generation, cancels Queued frames, and joins Writing frames. No protected bytes appear before activation. The write-terminal owner arm is uninhabited before `LocalTerminalCompletionSpec` selects TerminalReady; that semantic completion creates the publication hold/escrow, audit visibility releases it, and inline or independent Protocol activation constructs the lease.

`AuthenticatedClientResultReleaseReceipt<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the immutable pre-release proof `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,session_authorization_ref:StrongRef<SessionAuthorizationRecord<Role>>,auth_context_generation,session_binding,public_authority_commitment,posture_and_role:Local|Meta,result_owner_identity,result_delivery_id,expected_active_delivery_state_version,expected_active_retention_digest,request_id,stream_id,strictly_monotone_release_nonce,source:ClientResultReleaseAuthority,canonical_release_request_digest,server_process_incarnation,accepted_child_state_version,server_signing_key_epoch,signature}`. The public commitment is a redundant integrity projection of the full header/session record, never a substitute. It is created only after an explicit FGP `RESULT_RELEASE`, embedded `release()`, or Bolt DISCARD/RESET request passes current-epoch authentication, nonreplay, exact owner/delivery/state, and same-or-narrower authority checks. Duplicate request bytes reproduce the same receipt; nonce, source, body, authority, or state drift fails.

`ResultEndSendCompletionRecord` is the immutable durable bridge from ephemeral transport to result ACK: `{authority_binding_public_commitment,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,session_binding,server_process_incarnation,sender_generation,result_owner_identity,result_delivery_id,delivery_state_version,resume_capability_commitment,result_end_frame_digest,complete_canonical_byte_length,canonical_result_digest,flow_reservation_identity,flow_bytes_and_rows_committed,write_start_authority_and_frame_time_requirement_set_commitment,transport_sent_obligation_commitment,sent_completion_nonce,server_signing_key_epoch,signature}`. It is constructed only after the complete authenticated RESULT_END frame reaches `TransportSendObligation::Sent`, the exact flow reservation is reconciled, and the write-start authority/time requirement-set commitment byte-matches the send obligation; it owns no result bulk bytes and is retained through ACK/reconnect/expiry resolution. `AuthenticatedClientResultAckReceipt<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the distinct immutable proof `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,session_authorization_ref:StrongRef<SessionAuthorizationRecord<Role>>,auth_context_generation,session_binding,public_authority_commitment,posture_and_role:Local|Meta,result_owner_identity,result_delivery_id,expected_active_delivery_state_version,expected_active_retention_digest,presented_resume_capability_commitment,result_end_send_completion_ref:StrongRef<ResultEndSendCompletionRecord>,request_id,stream_id,strictly_monotone_ack_nonce,canonical_ack_request_digest,server_process_incarnation,accepted_child_state_version,server_signing_key_epoch,signature}`. The repeated public commitment and send-completion projection must byte-match the full current header. It exists only after the authenticated peer ACKs that exact send-completion transcript under current same-or-narrower authority. It cannot be constructed from a socket write, local EOF, partial-read inference, sender close, or release request. Duplicate bytes reproduce one receipt; a different frame/result/state/capability/nonce loses the CAS. `ResultAckRecord` strongly owns this receipt and carries only weak authenticated identities of the retired lease/manifest/retention predecessor.

`AuthenticatedClientCancelReceipt` does not exist. `SessionRegionCloseReceipt` is the distinct structured-concurrency proof `{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,session_binding,server_process_incarnation,region_id,region_generation,posture:Local|Sharded,result_owner_identity,result_delivery_id,close_cause_class,region_close_transition_digest,child_obligation_inventory_digest,zero_live_transport_sender_and_chunk_obligation_proof,close_receipt_nonce,server_signing_key_epoch,signature}`. It discharges only in-process sender/chunk obligations after the owning region irreversibly closes; it is not result ACK/release evidence, does not change durable retention, and conveys no authority to discard resumable bytes. QUERY_CANCEL, Rust Drop, process death, socket EOF, timeout, or an unsigned region observation proves neither ACK, durable release, nor expiry.

Published results use an explicit capability-owned transfer, so transaction completion and compaction never wait for a client ACK. `ResultManifestRef` is the exact role union `Local{manifest_ref:StrongRef<LocalBufferedResultManifest>}|Sharded{manifest_ref:StrongRef<BufferedResultManifest>}`; `ResultLeaseRef` and `ResultSemanticOwnerRef` are corresponding generated closed role unions. `ResultResumeCapabilityCommitment` binds the opaque capability's domain, namespace, incarnation, service epoch, tenant/principal, non-widenable authority ceiling, complete security-binding commitment, delivery ID/generation, manifest/result digest, expiry profile/window, verifier-key epoch, and successor nonce.

The common body is deliberately split. `DeliveryTransitionAppliedRef` is `InlineSemanticDetachment{applied_semantic_ref:AppliedControlRef}|ProtocolMaintenance{maintenance_applied_ref:MaintenanceAppliedRef}`; it describes the actual constructor and never fabricates a protocol predecessor for initial detachment. `ResultActiveCommon<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{role,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,semantic_owner_ref:ResultSemanticOwnerRef<Role>,semantic_owner_commitment,semantic_detachment_identity,delivery_id,lease_generation,delivery_state_version,canonical_result_digest,result_answer_contract,weak_prior_retention_identity,latest_transition_ref:DeliveryTransitionAppliedRef,lookup_and_tombstone_floor,body_digest}`. `ResultTerminalCommon<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{role,terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,semantic_owner_commitment,semantic_detachment_identity,delivery_id,terminal_delivery_state_version,canonical_result_digest,result_answer_contract,weak_prior_retention_identity,weak_manifest_identity,weak_lease_identity?,terminal_protocol_transition_ref:MaintenanceAppliedRef,lookup_and_tombstone_floor,body_digest}` and has no strong authority-binding, semantic-owner, manifest, lease, replay, verifier, escrow, or bulk-predecessor edge. The terminal commitment is legal only because these arms are nonrevivable and own no protected result bytes.

`ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Role>` is the Protocol-plane state-shaped union `PendingVisibility{common:ResultActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref:ResultManifestRef,delivery_policy_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryPolicy<Role>>,resource_escrow_ref:StrongRef<ResultResourceEscrow<Role>>,release_subject_commitment}`; `ReleasedAwaitingActivation{common:ResultActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref:ResultManifestRef,delivery_policy_ref,resource_escrow_ref,audit_release_evidence:AuditReleaseEvidence,release_maintenance_ref:MaintenanceAppliedRef}`; `ActiveDeliverable{common:ResultActiveCommon<Role>,manifest_ref:ResultManifestRef,delivery_policy_ref,lease_ref:ResultLeaseRef,resource_escrow_ref,current_resume_replay_ref:StrongRef<ResumeCapabilityReplayRecord>,consumed_immediate_predecessor_replay_ref:StrongRef<ResumeCapabilityReplayRecord>?,resume_capability_commitment,replay_count,release_subject_commitment,audit_release_evidence:AuditReleaseEvidence}`; `Acknowledged{common:ResultTerminalCommon<Role>,ack_ref:StrongRef<ResultAckRecord>,resource_disposition_ref:StrongRef<ResultResourceDispositionRecord>,capability_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<ResultCapabilityTombstone>}`; or `ReleasedUnacked{common:ResultTerminalCommon<Role>,release_ref:StrongRef<ResultReleaseRecord>,resource_disposition_ref:StrongRef<ResultResourceDispositionRecord>,capability_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<ResultCapabilityTombstone>}`. No arm strongly names a predecessor. Pending and ReleasedAwaitingActivation own manifest/policy/escrow but no absolute window or capability; only ActiveDeliverable owns lease/replay. Terminal evidence has weak prior identities and exact escrow disposition. Each maintenance transition CAS-replaces the protocol index leaf; after the covering protocol snapshot/cut, prior bulk becomes unmarked while tombstone evidence remains. Generated plane, reachability, conservation, and construction-DAG oracles prove these properties.

`ResultResumeTransitionSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the exact ordered `RotateResume` input `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,authority_nonwidening_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuthorityNonwideningProof>,active_deliverable_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Role>>,expected_delivery_state_version,expected_lease_generation,presented_resume_validation_ref:StrongRef<DurableCapabilityValidationEvidence>,presented_current_or_consumed_predecessor_replay_ref:StrongRef<ResumeCapabilityReplayRecord>,canonical_resume_boundary:{chunk_ordinal,canonical_byte_offset,row_boundary_digest},canonical_request_idempotency_key,prebuilt_successor_resume_replay_ref:StrongRef<ResumeCapabilityReplayRecord>,expected_replay_count,checked_successor_replay_count,maximum_replay_count,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply verifies active index membership, exact canonical boundary, current or registered consumed-immediate-predecessor lineage, exact or proved narrower complete authority against the active record/capability, same result/delivery/version, checked increment, and `successor_count <= maximum`. It constructs a new ActiveDeliverable record with the current full binding, same active ownership, successor replay ref/capability commitment, incremented version/count, and no predecessor edge; atomically swaps the index; and returns deterministic `RESULT_RESUMED` bytes. An identical retry returns the same successor; a different boundary/generation/authority loses, and exceeding the maximum yields a typed non-mutating `ReplayLimitReached` result without releasing the data. ACK, explicit release, expiry, audit visibility, and resume all CAS the same active version, so exactly one conflicting transition wins. Local and Global command registries include `ResultResumeTransitionSpec`; restart/key-rotation/lost-response and every resume-vs-ACK/release/expiry race are permanent DPOR gates. `ResultReleaseEvidence<Role>` has the same two tag shapes for Local and Meta, but its receipt reference is role-specialized and noninterchangeable; no QUERY_CANCEL, socket close, Drop, or process/session event can construct either arm.

The result-producing Local/Meta semantic command uses a registered `AtomicProtocolDetach` publication: the semantic payload advances its delivery frontier to `DetachedToProtocolRetention{delivery_id,detachment_commitment}` while the same RootManifest slot generation installs the new Protocol owner and typed resource escrow. Neither plane points to the other by ObjectId. It may create `ActiveDeliverable` only when its NotRequired/covered gate and expected pipeline basis prove the resulting candidate visible in the same publication and it carries a valid inline activation recipe. A visible inline subject without valid time evidence creates `ReleasedAwaitingActivation`; every Required gate and every NotRequired candidate behind an earlier gap creates `PendingVisibility`. `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec` carries a sorted exact bijection from every pending protected output in the released prefix to one release recipe and atomically replaces it with ReleasedAwaitingActivation while advancing the manifest-owned visible endpoint. The independent activation transition later builds the fresh lease/capability for one released output without holding up that endpoint. No public data frame can select PendingVisibility or ReleasedAwaitingActivation; opaque management/status replies may report `AwaitingActivation` without revealing payload-dependent facts.

`ResultIndependentRetentionIndex<Local|Meta>` is the sole authenticated Protocol map `delivery_id -> latest StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Role>>` plus generation, pending/deliverable/terminal counts, compacted-disposition floor, and root digest. `LocalResultDeliveryIndex`/`GlobalResultDeliveryIndex` are documentation aliases only and are not durable schema/field names. Protocol snapshots, backup closure, restore reconciliation, GC inventory, service-epoch transition, and configuration transfer walk it through `durable_state_slots.toml`; semantic checkpoint vectors do not. Semantic attempt/statement indexes retain only `{delivery_id,detachment_commitment}` until their own compaction. Acknowledgement or release CASes exactly one ActiveDeliverable predecessor, closes its sender generation, cancels all Queued frame obligations, joins every Writing obligation to Sent or Failed, updates the Protocol index, and disposes the Protocol escrow in the same maintenance transition before returning a terminal response; it never mutates the semantic resource ledger or advances a semantic sequence. Matching retry returns the identical terminal successor; competing ACK/release/expiry/resume produces exactly one winner and a stable already-terminal response.

`ResultDeliveryDispositionProof` is `{role,semantic_owner_commitment,delivery_id,canonical_result_digest,disposition:PendingIndependent{retention_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Role>>}|ReleasedIndependent{retention_ref}|ActiveIndependent{retention_ref}|Acknowledged{retention_ref,ack_ref}|ReleasedUnacked{retention_ref,release_ref},semantic_frontier_detachment_proof,independent_index_membership_proof,resource_owner_consistency_proof,checkpoint_and_retention_floor}`. `ResultDispositionRef` is the compact typed projection of those five arms. Terminal publication, explicit close/cancel, attempt compaction, workspace release, and outcome compaction require one exact proof for every result owner, but every independent preterminal arm is already terminal for the **attempt's** delivery frontier. It is not terminal for the independent delivery obligation. An attempt outcome, socket/session loss, region cancellation, process crash, service restart, compaction, backup/restore, or Local-to-Sharded activation cannot release a pending/released/active record. Explicit authenticated owner-management release may terminalize PendingIndependent or ReleasedIndependent without a Resume capability; explicit authenticated client release or profiled lease expiry may terminalize ActiveIndependent; complete end-to-end ACK may select Acknowledged. Crash/DPOR tests cover publication before/after visibility release, release-before-activation, activation failure/retry, terminalization before ACK, session death, lost RESULT_END/ACK/release responses, ACK-versus-expiry, attempt compaction, service-epoch change, restore, verifier-key rotation, charge-transfer failure, and GC, requiring byte-identical resume or one explicit terminal disposition with no leaked or prematurely reclaimed byte.

`TxnOutcomeRecord` is the Local predecessor-linked semantic lifecycle object `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,txn_token,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeRecord>?,state:BeginStaging|NeverRegistered|Registered|PrepareAdmitted|CommittedPendingPostconditions|Committed|Aborted|ReadClosed with the exact role-valid bodies above,audit_state:NotRequired{evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence}|ClaimedPending{ticket_digest,claim_identity}|TerminalGateCommitted{ticket_digest,event_digest,resolution_digest,gate_commitment,candidate_pipeline_order},completion_state:TxnCompletionState,time_authority_profile_oid,issued_at,expires_at,minimum_lookup_retention_floor}`. Every successor's header must equal or be a registered nonwidening successor of the registration binding; namespace/incarnation/service/tenant/principal/ceiling/policy/revocation are never repeated as independently forgeable scalars. Detailed terminal records keep the full header because they retain semantic/result/public-projection state; only a later payload-free expired tombstone may use `TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Local>`. It never acquires a strong `AuditOutcomeRecord` edge after its candidate bytes were built. Public terminal/audit state is the join of this immutable terminal-gate commitment with the current protocol `VisibleAuditOutcomeIndex`; a missing/mismatched release remains FinalizingOpaque. Each terminal kind equals its Local semantic outcome kind; no Sharded arm is encodable. `TxnOutcomeIndex` has the Detailed/Compacted/ExpiredTombstone arms defined above. `LocalTxnOutcomeSummaryRoot` stores the full authority header with the exact wire-safe public outcome/commitments, time window, result disposition commitment, lookup floor, and protocol release proof identity needed to reproduce the same response; it cannot answer an in-progress state or retain the protocol root through a semantic back-edge.

`TerminalWriteResultPreparation` is the closed posture union `Local{registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,prepare_admission_ref:StrongRef<LocalPrepareAdmissionSpec>,final_effect_directory_ref:StrongRef<EffectDirectoryRoot>,final_effect_digest,returning_evaluation_plan_ref:StrongRef<DeterministicReturningPlan>,returning_evaluation_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ReturningEvaluationCertificate>,manifest_ref:StrongRef<LocalBufferedResultManifest>,canonical_result_digest,result_answer_contract,delivery_policy_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryPolicy<Local>>,prepared_owner_identity:PreparedOwnershipId,prepared_closure_inventory_digest}|Sharded{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,prepare_admission_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,global_final_effect_directory_ref:StrongRef<GlobalEffectDirectoryRoot>,global_final_effect_digest,returning_evaluation_plan_ref:StrongRef<DeterministicReturningPlan>,returning_evaluation_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ReturningEvaluationCertificate>,manifest_ref:StrongRef<BufferedResultManifest>,canonical_result_digest,result_answer_contract,delivery_policy_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryPolicy<Meta>>,meta_prepared_owner_identity:PreparedOwnershipId,prepared_closure_inventory_digest}`. It exists only for autocommit ReadWrite WriteReturning. The registered evaluator reruns after final deterministic rebase against the final effect directory, and its certificate proves the manifest bytes/contract are exactly the RETURNING projection of that effect set. Prepared ownership's typed closure inventory retains preparation, manifest, chunks, nonexpiring delivery policy, resource charge, security binding, and verifier before command order—never an absolute delivery window or already-running client lease. The object names no future command, outcome, applied record, lease, payload, or root; terminal completion consumes it and creates the publication hold, while visible activation later constructs the first lease.

`LocalPrepareAdmissionSpec` is `{registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,expected_registered_outcome_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeRecord>,admission_generation,expected_statement_frontier,expected_delivery_frontier,expected_statement_index_digest,expected_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,expected_cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalCumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,expected_cumulative_effect_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalCumulativeEffectRoot>,local_serialization_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalSerializationCertificate>,global_authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord>,resource_transition_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>],operation_audit_admission:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,transaction_audit_basis_commitment,finalization_basis_digest,expected_branch_heads[WeakMarkerIdentity],expected_branch_key_epochs,expected_policy_epoch,expected_revocation_index,expected_conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. Apply requires ReadWrite, no Open statement, contiguous terminal statement/delivery frontiers, exact workspace/witness/effect/SSI/security bases, and no prior admission. It constructs immutable `LocalTerminalAdmissionFence::Armed {registration_ref,admission_generation,admission_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,frozen_statement_delivery_workspace_witness_effect_commitment,frozen_branch_heads,frozen_branch_key_epochs,frozen_resource_and_constraint_commitment,frozen_policy_epoch,frozen_revocation_index,frozen_conflict_basis,operation_audit_claim_commitment,transaction_audit_basis_commitment}` (with no future sequence/HLC), makes the succeeding `TxnOutcomeRecord::PrepareAdmitted` the unique canonical selector of that fence, and atomically replaces the token's Detailed entry.

Only then are the final rebased effect directory, optional `TerminalWriteResultPreparation`, and `TxnOutcomePreparationRecord` constructed. `TxnOutcomePreparationRecord` is `{txn_token,expected_prior_state:PrepareAdmitted{outcome_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeRecord>,terminal_admission_fence_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalAdmissionFence>,admission_generation,state_digest},proposed_terminal_kind,final_effect_digest,terminal_write_result:None|Prepared{preparation_ref:StrongRef<TerminalWriteResultPreparation>,required_posture:Local},authorization_decision_ref,resource_transition_refs,terminal_audit_gate}`. Before any terminal audit lock, `LocalFinalCertificationReserveSpec` revalidates the final rebased selection and installs the Active reservation/holds. The audit plan binds that reservation; the stable `CommittedEffectCapsule`, `LogicalDeltaTemplate`, and semantic D1 availability closure include the reservation but no HLC/order observation. After the audit triple is ready, each proposal builds a fresh D1-certified `LocalOrderAttemptInput`; the ordinary `CommitCommand` names both closures. Commit apply requires the exact selected Armed fence and Active reservation whose selection is LocalCommit, atomically consumes the reservation, constructs `LocalTerminalAdmissionFence::TerminalOrdered {predecessor_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalAdmissionFence>,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReservation>,commit_command_ref:StrongRef<CommitCommand>,assigned_logical_command_seq,assigned_commit_seq}`, and makes the succeeding pending outcome select the terminal version. Admitted abort analogously requires an Active reservation whose selection is LocalAbort and a fresh terminal order supplement, consumes it, constructs `LocalTerminalAdmissionFence::Released {predecessor_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalAdmissionFence>,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReservation>,applied_abort_ref:AppliedAbortRef::LocalTxn,assigned_logical_command_seq}`, and selects it through the succeeding aborted outcome. These are new immutable objects, never mutation of the Armed or reservation bytes; the outcome/reservation index CAS forbids a second successor.

`LocalTerminalCompletionSpec` is `{pending_outcome_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeRecord>,semantic_outcome:SemanticTerminalDescriptor::Local,terminal_public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<TerminalPublicProjectionBundle<Local>>,prebuilt_read_your_writes_issuance_ref:None|Some{StrongRef<ReadYourWritesIssuanceBundle<Local>>},expected_required_postcondition_bitmap,observed_satisfied_postcondition_bitmap,configured_durability_evidence_ref,local_delta_frontier_evidence_ref,result_disposition:NoTerminalResult|ReadTerminalResult{delivery_disposition_proof_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryDispositionProof>}|AutocommitWriteReturning{preparation_ref:StrongRef<TerminalWriteResultPreparation>,required_posture:Local,inline_activation_recipe:InlineResultDeliveryActivationRecipe<Local>?},resource_release_refs[],audit_visibility_evidence_ref,expected_conflict_overlap_floor,terminal_audit_gate}`. It monotonically adds verified bits, validates rather than manufactures the frozen public/capability artifacts, and creates `TerminalReady` only when the required bitmap is exactly satisfied; it cannot alter the semantic outcome. The autocommit-write arm uses `AtomicProtocolDetach` to create the owner, resource transfer/escrow, Protocol retention leaf, and only `{delivery_id,detachment_commitment}` in the semantic frontier. A legal visible inline recipe creates ActiveDeliverable; a visible candidate without one creates ReleasedAwaitingActivation; a hidden candidate creates PendingVisibility. All three satisfy the attempt's independent-retention bit, but only ActiveDeliverable can emit bytes or expire under a delivery lease.

`LocalReadCloseSpec` is `{registration_ref,operation_audit_admission,mode:AutocommitResult{read_access_admission_ref,read_result_release_decision_ref,buffered_result_manifest_ref,canonical_result_digest,result_answer_contract,delivery_policy_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryPolicy<Local>>,inline_activation_recipe:InlineResultDeliveryActivationRecipe<Local>?}|ExplicitClose{expected_statement_frontier,expected_delivery_detachment_frontier,result_disposition_proof_refs[StrongRef<ResultDeliveryDispositionProof>],complete_publication_chain_digest,workspace_generation_ref,cumulative_read_witness_root_ref,zero_cumulative_write_effect_proof_ref,no_open_statement_proof_ref},local_serialization_certificate_ref,expected_conflict_index_basis,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply advances LogicalCommandSeq/HLC but not CommitSeq, creates ReadClosed and, for autocommit, atomically detaches the result under the same pending/released/inline-active rule; semantic success waits for the later `LocalTerminalCompletionSpec`, whose now-known internal descriptor permits a prebuilt public projection bundle. It never waits for activation or client ACK.

Cancellation before prepare and admitted abort are distinct schemas. `TxnAbortSpec` is only the active/unadmitted lifecycle path `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,expected_prior:Registered{outcome_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeRecord>,state_digest},all_open_statement_abort_refs[],result_disposition_proof_refs[StrongRef<ResultDeliveryDispositionProof<Local>>],workspace_release_evidence_refs[],conflict_close_evidence_ref,terminal_abort_authority_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAbortAuthority<Local>>,required_abort_source:ClientRequested|Administrative,resource_release_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Local>>],operation_audit_admission,terminal_audit_gate}`. Its terminal-abort authority must use the registered `LifecycleOperation::RequestTerminalAbort{stage:Active}`, and it can never consume a PrepareAdmitted fence or final-certification reservation.

`AdmittedTxnAbortCommand` is the Local transaction-order peer of `CommitCommand`, not a generic ControlCommand: `{command_version,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,terminal_admission_fence_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalAdmissionFence>,required_fence_state:Armed,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReservation>,required_reservation_state:Active,required_selection:FinalCertificationSelection::LocalAbort,required_selected_terminal_digest,order_attempt_input_ref:StrongRef<LocalOrderAttemptInput>,required_order_subject:LocalOrderSubject::Terminal{final_certification_reservation_ref,required_state:Active,selected_terminal_digest},order_attempt_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate>,terminal_abort_authority_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAbortAuthority<Local>>,complete_no_effect_and_release_proof_ref:StrongRef<FinalAbortNoEffectAndReleaseProof<Local>>,resource_release_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Local>>],txn_outcome_preparation_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomePreparationRecord>,payload_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PayloadAvailabilityCertificate>,terminal_audit_gate}`. It must byte-match `FinalCertificationSelection::LocalAbort`, including the exact selected-terminal digest; apply atomically marks the supplement Ordered, consumes the Active reservation, selects `LocalTerminalAdmissionFence::Released` with `AppliedAbortRef::LocalTxn` and the assigned logical sequence, terminalizes outcome/conflict state, and releases exactly the proved holds. It cannot race an ordered commit, create a marker/delta/effect, or be synthesized from timeout. The lifecycle matrix adds `RequestTerminalAbort{stage:Active|PrepareAdmitted}` with exact effect classes; the PrepareAdmitted client/admin source feeds `TerminalAbortAuthority`, while correctness-required aborts use their structural reason-specific authority. The later `LocalTerminalCompletionSpec` validates the frozen public projection after either abort outcome exists. The write-result preparation must equal the one retained by `TxnOutcomePreparationRecord`; completion cannot reevaluate or substitute RETURNING bytes.

`LocalOutcomeCompactionSpec` is `NeverRegistered{detailed_outcome_ref,terminal_begin_ref,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Local>>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,expected_outcome_index_entry:Detailed,expected_begin_index_entry:NeverRegisteredDetailed,summary_leaf_body,all_retention_and_audit_discharge_evidence}|TerminalReady{detailed_outcome_ref,terminal_public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<TerminalPublicProjectionBundle<Local>>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,expected_terminal_public_outcome,expected_outcome_index_entry:Detailed,expected_begin_index_entry:BoundDetailed,summary_leaf_body,result_workspace_conflict_replay_retention_discharge_evidence}`. Apply requires the summary leaf copy the exact frozen public bytes/receipt from the referenced detailed bundle, writes the authenticated leaf, and atomically changes `TxnOutcomeIndex Detailed -> Compacted` together with `LocalBeginIdempotencyIndex NeverRegisteredDetailed -> NeverRegisteredCompacted` or `BoundDetailed -> BoundCompacted`; both compact arms retain the same replay/bundle evidence, and status/acceptance retry bytes remain identical. `LocalOutcomeExpirySpec` is the distinct ordered transition `{capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,summary_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnOutcomeSummaryRoot>,leaf_key_digest,expected_outcome_index_entry:Compacted,expected_begin_index_entry:BoundCompacted|NeverRegisteredCompacted,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,expected_verifier_key_and_profile_floors,expected_begin_retry_and_lookup_floors,permanent_tombstone_body,terminal_audit_freeze:AuditFreezeField::Required<LocalControl>,terminal_audit_gate}`. Evidence must classify the replay record's exact window Expired for this action and every advertised retry/lookup floor must be closed. Apply atomically changes both indexes to authenticated `ExpiredTombstone` values containing token/key/capability commitments and evidence digest, drops the replay/bundle edges only there, and permanently prevents key/token reuse; valid old bytes return TokenExpired while invalid/unauthorized input remains NotVisibleOrUnknown. The construction DAG is fixed: registration -> workspace zero -> outcome; statement successor workspace and manifest -> publication spec/command -> applied publication/detachment/outcome -> payload/record/root; prepare-admission outcome/fence -> final effect directory and optional terminal write-result preparation -> outcome preparation/capsule/prepared ownership -> unchanged `CommitCommand` -> marker/delta/pending outcome -> external audit batch and visibility release -> terminal projection bundle/completion -> optional output activation. No input names its future applied record, payload, command record, root, lease, marker, or summary leaf.

`GenerationRootRef` is the posture-closed union `Local{generation_root:StrongRef}` or `Distributed{manifest_ref:StrongRef<DistributedDerivedGenerationManifest>}`. `DerivedGenerationRegistry` maps a full `DerivedIdentity` strong reference to `{generation_root:GenerationRootRef,global_frontier?,answer_contract:AnswerContract,retention_class,status:Building|Active|Retiring}` and never reconstructs a singular graph/head/schema key. `ShardDerivedComponent` binds `{derived_identity_ref,shard_id,topology_epoch,input_global_batch_interval,resulting_global_frontier,partition_and_authority_coverage,answer_contract,component_root:StrongRef}`; `ShardDerivedComponentCertificate` adds `SameGroupCertificateHeader`, availability/retention floors, and quorum signatures. A meta-local `DistributedDerivedGenerationManifest` is `{derived_identity_ref,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,exact_global_frontier,sorted_component_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardDerivedComponentCertificate>],complete_partition_and_authority_commitment,answer_contract,build_policy_epoch}`. Shard build/advance controls root components in `shard_derived_component_registry_root`; a meta activation control changes `Active` only after every required certified component at one topology/frontier exists. Partial, stale-topology, or mixed-frontier generations remain nonqueryable, and reads use the exact fallback path when the active manifest is missing. Checkpoint/configuration floors, remote grants, topology transfer, backup/replay, and GC retain every Building/Active/Retiring generation through its terminal transition.

**Replica-local RootManifest** is a closed four-posture, role-tagged union. Its common header is `{generation,database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,incarnation_continuity_profile_id,cluster_incarnation_continuity_digest,continuity_cas_version,service_visibility_epoch,replica_id,group_role:Local|Meta|Shard{shard_id}|RetiredLocal,group_id,filesystem_profile_id,filesystem_instance_digest,local_writer_fence_epoch,physical_generation_lease:Active{descriptor:PhysicalGenerationLeaseDescriptor}|Retired{descriptor:RetirementLeaseDescriptor},metadata_tree_bootstraps:GeneratedMetadataTreeBootstrapSet,object_location_index_root,local_encoding_root,local_key_opener_root,raft_state_root:StrongRef<RaftStateRoot>?,nonretaining_predecessor_root_digest}` and exactly one payload. Local, Meta, and Shard require the `Active` arm. `RetirementLeaseDescriptor` is a distinct compact descriptor protecting the RetiredLocal proof closure and is created under the same prepublication inode/nonce contract; it cannot be decoded as an active generation lease. `Local` is `{applied_logical_state_root_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStateRoot>,prepared_payload_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalPreparedPayloadRoot>,audit_visibility_protocol_state_ref:StrongRef<AuditVisibilityProtocolState<Local>>,local_applied_logical_command_seq,local_visible_logical_command_seq,local_applied_index,local_visible_index,local_durable_index,maintenance_applied_index}`. `Meta` is the corresponding `{applied_global_state_root_ref,meta_prepared_payload_root_ref,audit_visibility_protocol_state_ref:StrongRef<AuditVisibilityProtocolState<Meta>>,global_applied_logical_command_seq,global_visible_logical_command_seq,meta_applied_index,meta_visible_index,meta_durable_index,maintenance_applied_index}`. `Shard` is `{applied_shard_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,shard_prepared_payload_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardPreparedPayloadRoot>,shard_protocol_state_ref:StrongRef<ShardProtocolState>,shard_applied_index,shard_visible_index,shard_durable_index,maintenance_applied_index,global_applied_logical_command_seq,audit_visible_global_prefix}`. `RetiredLocal` retains only its compact certified transition/retirement/audit proof closure and explicitly forbids semantic, prepared, Raft, protocol, or service roots.

For Local/Meta the protocol state owns the sole pipeline ref, visible audit outcomes/external head, and independent delivery lifecycles. RootManifest does not duplicate a direct pipeline or visible-state-root edge: its applied semantic root equals the protocol pipeline applied tail, while its visible sequence/index values equal the pipeline visible endpoint selected inside that protocol state. For Shard, `ShardProtocolState` contains only role-valid result/cursor/artifact transport, physical progress, and imported Meta visibility projections; it has no Local/Meta audit pipeline or semantic authority. Every active-role tuple byte-matches hard state, snapshot, configuration, prepared records, protocol state, restore state, and semantic state. Genesis, restore bridge, takeover, and Local-to-Sharded activation must all construct the role-valid initial protocol state before the first manifest; no active manifest can omit it. Cross-role/group/incarnation/continuity/visibility/protocol substitution is invalid.

`InitialProtocolStateRecipe<Role>` is the generated pre-order contract that makes that requirement executable: `{role_and_group,target_incarnation,target_service_visibility_epoch,source_kind:FreshGenesis|Restore|RoleTransition|Takeover,source_recovery_or_transition_projection_commitment?,semantic_applied_and_visible_endpoint_recipes,inherited_or_empty_audit_queue_recipe:LocalMetaOnly{pipeline_recipe,visible_outcome_index_recipe,external_continuity_recipe}|ShardInapplicable,independent_result_cursor_artifact_error_delivery_recipes,protocol_resource_escrow_recipes,capability_invalidation_suspension_or_transfer_recipes,physical_recovery_progress_recipe,security_barrier_recipe,complete_protocol_slot_bitmap,cross_plane_conservation_commitment,body_digest}`. Every active `protocol_state_fields.toml` slot appears exactly once; a source-kind/role matrix rejects missing, extra, or semantically impossible recipes. Pending/released output recipes retain bytes, policy, escrow, and release evidence but no target-era lease or send capability. Takeover preserves the exact prior Protocol state through its adoption record rather than reconstructing it from semantic state.

The universal first-manifest construction order is `semantic payload/command record/state root -> role-valid Protocol state from InitialProtocolStateRecipe -> PreparedOwnership root -> Consensus/Raft hard state, log, snapshot, configuration, and state root -> create/authenticate/fsync generation-lease inode and fsync its directory -> construct PhysicalGenerationLeaseDescriptor -> RootManifest -> RootSlot`. Every later publication uses the same lease-inode-before-manifest tail. A first-manifest builder may hold body recipes for later objects but no earlier object contains a future ObjectId. `PendingCreationGuard`, `PendingRestoreCreationGuard`, and `PendingRoleTransitionCreationGuard` each carry the exact role-valid Semantic/Protocol/Prepared/Consensus/bootstrap/physical-lease recipe commitments and complete slot bitmaps; their encoders accept only the registered source-kind closure. Genesis creates an empty Local/Meta audit pipeline with applied=visible and no barrier, or a Shard Protocol state with its genesis Meta projection. Restore reconstructs the projection's applied/visible split and pending queue. Role transition creates Meta `RoleTransitionPreparedHidden` and Shard `RoleTransitionPreparedHidden` Protocol arms; migrated owners are suspended or transferred without E-era send authority. Mandatory closure, backup/restore, checkpoint/snapshot, GC, format, role-transition, and orphan-cleanup walkers include the exact lease descriptor/inode proof class. Negative first-manifest fixtures delete, duplicate, substitute, or cross-role every plane root and lease field and must fail before write mode or service exposure.

A RetiredLocal manifest is authenticated and recoverable only as a compact terminal proof posture: ordinary open, resurrection, Raft participation, receipt issuance, and service advertisement fail before any logical object is exposed. A `MetadataTreeBootstrap` embeds `{tree_kind,tree_generation,tree_key_id,root_oid,cipher_descriptor,ciphertext_id,encoding_descriptor,placement_records,tree_key_opener,closure_digest}`; generated exact role-specific bootstrap unions replace erased lists. Each authenticated internal edge carries the exact child identity/cipher/encoding/placement/wrap/AAD fields defined in `durable_fields.toml`; parent ObjectId is absent because it is not known before parent encoding. Tree-root bootstrap edges are strong, ordinary locator entries are nonretaining, and semantic/protocol/prepared/terminal-proof marking decides liveness before realization resolution. RetiredLocal trees may locate only their compact proof closure. The predecessor is weak, and independently hashed markers use authenticated nonretaining subobject locators.

The preceding role union is the sole authoritative RootManifest schema; the obsolete `logical_state_root_oid`, `global_state_root_oid`, direct `audit_visibility_pipeline_ref`, and duplicate visible-root fields are unregistered. Generated field fixtures require exactly one applied semantic root and one role-valid protocol root, with the latter's authenticated pipeline selecting the visible root.

`MetadataTreeBootstrap.closure_digest` commits only the already-built metadata-tree closure. That same generation MUST NOT contain any entry—strong, weak, or physical-locator—for its current `RootManifest`, `RootSlot`, or bootstrap bytes; the slot bootstrap is the current manifest's sole opener. This is a construction rule in the generated DAG checker, not merely a GC classification.

**ConfigurationState and Raft persistence.** `ConfigurationState` is `{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role:Local|Meta|Shard{shard_id},group_id,configuration_epoch,form:Stable{voters[],storage_members[]}|Joint{old_voters[],new_voters[],old_storage_members[],new_storage_members[]},sorted_member_descriptors[{member_id,replica_namespace,consensus_signing_key_epoch,payload_receipt_key_epoch,roles,failure_domains[]}],raft_quorum_rule,payload_recoverability_predicate,failure_domain_map,gc_ack_policy,predecessor:WeakConfigurationIdentity?,transition_nonce}`; the ObjectId of these bytes is its identity and is not stored inside itself. Stable/joint quorum validation and the one-member specialization are exactly §14.2. Every same-group receipt/certificate carries `configuration_state_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>` plus `payload_predicate_digest`, and its database/security-namespace/incarnation/role/group must match the enclosing log, hard state, snapshot, prepared root, manifest, and command. Another group imports it only through `RemoteConfigurationRef`, whose signed adoption evidence and retention floor are the explicit nonrecursive trust base; identical member lists, epochs, or a bare config OID never authorize cross-group replay.

Every durable posture has the Raft layer; single node is a one-member configuration. `RaftLogSegment` carries the exact domain/index/term/entry fields and a generated closed command union over Local semantic txn/control, Meta semantic txn/control, Shard prepare/decision/control, and `Maintenance{command:RaftMaintenanceCommand}`. `RaftMaintenanceCommand` is the role-tagged outer union `Local{command_ref:StrongRef<LocalMaintenanceCommand>}|Meta{command_ref:StrongRef<MetaMaintenanceCommand>}|Shard{shard_id,command_ref:StrongRef<ShardMaintenanceCommand>}`; role/group validation rejects substitution. `RaftHardState` is `{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role,group_id,current_term,voted_for?,log_first,log_last,commit_index,applied_index,visible_index,configuration_state_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,snapshot_ref?,maintenance_applied_index,certificate_attempt_ledger_root_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptLedger>,prepared_order_attempt_root_ref:StrongRef<PreparedOrderAttemptRoot>,audit_signer_journal_root_ref:StrongRef<AuditSignerJournalRoot>,protocol_state_identity:WeakStateIdentity}`.

`LocalMaintenanceCommand`, `MetaMaintenanceCommand`, and `ShardMaintenanceCommand` each contain the generated role-valid subset of certificate-plan/attempt/publish, order-attempt install/retire, audit signing-attempt staging/supersession, audit visibility (Local/Meta only), protocol result/cursor/artifact/error activation and disposition, prepared-owner recovery, physical progress, and backup/restore protocol operations. The former seven common families are ordinary concrete inner tags, not a second closed union; every inner tag has one command-contract row and one outer route. Maintenance advances Raft/maintenance indexes and its declared nonsemantic planes only; it never advances semantic sequences/HLC/heads or mutates a semantic payload. It uses the same live writer/incarnation/configuration guards and configured quorum durability and is included in snapshots/floors/backup/restore/GC. A self-staleness fixture fails any maintenance handler that changes semantic order.

`RaftConsensusCutProjection<Role>` is the prebuilt snapshot-self-excluding `{database/security namespace/incarnation/role/group,last_included_index:K,term_at_K,vote_and_commit_state_at_K,configuration_at_cut_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,prospective_configuration_refs,log_prefix_digest,retained_suffix_begin_index:K+1,certificate_prepared_remote_obligation_and_configuration_floors,format_first_target_escape_latch,takeover_adoption_head_projection,snapshot_slot:SelfExcluded{InstallThisSnapshot},body_digest}`. It contains no `RaftHardState`, `RaftStateRoot`, current-snapshot ref, or future snapshot identity; the takeover-head projection contains only its current typed ref. The construction-DAG generator rejects any path from this projection back to the snapshot under construction.

`RaftSnapshot` is a role-tagged exact **applied-cut** union. Common fields are `{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role,group_id,last_included_index:K,term_at_K,configuration_at_cut_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,semantic_applied_position_at_K,maintenance_applied_index_at_K,prepared_ownership_state_ref,consensus_cut_projection_ref:StrongRef<RaftConsensusCutProjection<Role>>,retained_suffix_begin_index:K+1,certificate_and_remote_obligation_floors,log_prefix_digest,log_to_all_planes_proof_ref:StrongRef<LogToAllPlanesProof>,body_digest}`. Local/Meta payloads carry the applied semantic state root at K, the complete role-valid `AuditVisibilityProtocolState` after every maintenance entry through K, its possibly earlier visible semantic root/position and unresolved candidate queue, the prepared root, configuration/checkpoint/floor selectors, and state-vector digest. Shard carries its applied semantic root, `ShardProtocolState`, prepared root, imported visible Meta prefix, and matching selectors. The invariant is `last_included_index = K = successor RaftHardState.applied_index_at_cut`; the retained log suffix begins exactly K+1. Construction is cut projection -> snapshot -> successor `RaftHardState{snapshot_ref}` -> successor `RaftStateRoot`, never a cycle. Every semantic/protocol/prepared/consensus object through K is owned by the snapshot closure, and none is also owned as a suffix entry; every object after K is suffix/prepared owned and absent from the cut. An unresolved Local/Meta candidate may cross the visible sub-prefix because the snapshotted protocol state owns its complete queue/closure. This is never described as a visible-only cut.

Configuration transitions bind proposal/post-apply configurations, current configuration equals the applied-tail configuration, and release uses the recorded configuration chain. Takeover preserves an origin-E protocol state under E+1 only through exact `TakeoverAdoptionRecord` validation. `RaftStateRoot<Role>` is `{hard_state_ref:StrongRef<RaftHardState<Role>>,retained_log_segment_refs,configuration_state_refs,matching_applied_cut_snapshot_ref:StrongRef<RaftSnapshot<Role>>?,prepared_and_certificate_ledger_refs,remote_obligation_floor_refs,transition_lock_refs,format_first_target_escape_latch:FormatFirstTargetEscapeLatch<Role>,takeover_adoption_head:TakeoverAdoptionHead<Role>,role_protocol_state_ref,unresolved_candidate_closure_refs,body_digest:BodyDigest<RaftStateRoot<Role>>}`. The hard-state and matching snapshot refs agree; genesis may have no snapshot, while a successor snapshot install follows the projection -> snapshot -> hard state -> root order above. Every reply follows a durable RootManifest publication of the appropriate semantic/protocol/consensus state.

`AuthorityLocalCertificateHeader` is the mandatory prefix of every authority-local quorum-signed receipt, certificate, proof, checkpoint/ack/floor, topology/GC/reconfiguration/activation barrier, and certificate-ledger entry: `{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,authority_group_role,authority_group_id,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,payload_predicate_digest,signer_epoch}`. Object-specific schemas below abbreviate it as `SameGroupCertificateHeader`; that alias is valid only inside the owning group. An imported export leaf instead begins `ImportedCertificateDomain {database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,authority_group_role,authority_group_id,authority_configuration_evidence_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,payload_predicate_digest,signer_epoch}` and contains no raw `StrongRef<ConfigurationState>` across groups. The generated certificate-domain checker requires the authority-local transcript, imported embedded canonical configuration, `RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence`, and outer `CertifiedRemoteStrongRef` to agree exactly; old-incarnation, old-configuration, wrong-role, or wrong-predicate substitution fails before object-specific validation.

Every authority-local quorum artifact also obeys one universal signing protocol. `CertificateUnsignedBodyDigest<T>` is the registered domain-separated transcript recipe over every semantic field of certificate schema `T`, including its full authority/configuration/predicate header and pre-minted scalar `certificate_attempt_id`, but excluding exactly `certificate_attempt_plan_ref`, the canonical lock/share arrays, and aggregate/signature fields. A certificate schema contains no future certificate-ledger publication ObjectId or applied identity: the later ledger entry points to the completed certificate. It excludes no subject, topology, sequence, closure, result, policy, epoch, or verifier field. Before the first signature, a nonsemantic Raft maintenance entry quorum-roots `CertificateAttemptPlan {certificate_attempt_id,certificate_kind,subject_identity,authority_domain,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,payload_predicate_digest,certificate_schema_id,certificate_schema_version,canonical_unsigned_body_digest:CertificateUnsignedBodyDigest<T>,canonical_signer_subset[{signer_id,signing_key_id,signer_epoch}],threshold,signature_algorithm_and_encoding}` in `CertificateAttemptLedger`; this changes no semantic sequence or HLC. Each selected signer durably installs its distinct `CertificateSignerLock {plan_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptPlan>,certificate_attempt_id,subject_identity,certificate_schema_id,canonical_unsigned_body_digest,signer_id,signing_key_id,signer_epoch,state:Locked}` in its fenced signer journal before emitting `CertificateSignatureShare {lock_ref:StrongRef<CertificateSignerLock>,signer_id,signing_key_id,canonical_signature_bytes}`. Signer identity/key/epoch make lock bytes and ObjectIds distinct. The completed certificate carries `certificate_attempt_plan_ref`, the sorted exact lock/share bijection, signer-subset bitmap, and canonical aggregate or canonical signature-set encoding outside its unsigned-body transcript; its validator recomputes `CertificateUnsignedBodyDigest<T>`, verifies every share, exact planned subset/threshold, and the aggregate/ordered-set bytes. A `CertificatePublish` maintenance entry records `CertificateAttemptRecord::Published` after the bytes exist. Thus plan -> per-signer locks -> shares -> certificate -> ledger publication is acyclic while semantic-field substitution still changes the signed digest. Signer order, signature encoding, and exact quorum subset are fixed by the plan, so crash/retry/leader change reproduces one certificate byte string and ObjectId. A signer refuses any other digest for that plan/subject.

An unavailable planned subset may be replaced only through the nonsemantic maintenance command `CertificateAttemptAbandonSpec {plan_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptPlan>,expected_ledger_state:Planned|Collecting,sorted_signer_journal_closure_refs,complete_share_bitmap,quorum_impossibility_proof_ref:StrongRef<CertificateQuorumImpossibilityProof>,no_certificate_or_export_publication_proof_ref:StrongRef<CertificateNonpublicationProof>,successor_signer_epoch_or_configuration,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. Apply creates `CertificateAttemptAbandonment` only when the observed shares cannot meet threshold, every signer capable of completing the planned quorum has durably closed that plan at the named journal generation, and no certificate/export was published; otherwise it rejects. The ledger moves `Absent -> Planned -> Collecting -> Published` or `Planned|Collecting -> AbandonPending -> Abandoned`, never between terminal arms. A successor uses a new `certificate_attempt_id`, the same registered subject and unsigned-body digest unless the semantic owner itself legally changed, and a legal subset under the then-current configuration; it retains old plans, locks, shares, closures, and abandonment proof through the retirement floor. The role-local `CertificateAttemptLedger` recovers partial collections and permits at most one Published certificate identity per `(certificate kind,subject identity,unsigned-body digest)`; conflicting published digests are fatal evidence. The generated field table injects `certificate_attempt_id`, `certificate_attempt_plan_ref`, and the exact lock/share set into every routing, read, prepare, constraint, decision, checkpoint, projection, GC, derived, key, topology, backup/restore, acknowledgement, and `ExportLeaf` certificate even when a compact prose schema omits it. G0 generates include/exclude, signer-substitution, duplicate/missing-share, threshold, aggregate-mutation, abandonment, successor-subset, and maintenance-self-staleness fixtures for every registered certificate schema; only the named construction/signature fields may be excluded. Crash tests stop before/after each plan/lock/share/publication/abandon stage and across leader/configuration change.

**W12 global/shard formats (reserved now, activated only by the W12 feature epoch).** There is one meta consensus group and one consensus group per data shard. A raw `StrongRef` never crosses those group boundaries: every registration, basis, routing proof, prepare/decision/checkpoint certificate, fragment batch, apply acknowledgement, or topology proof used by another group is carried as `CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<T>` and therefore has the two-sided retention/GC obligation in the reference contract. All groups remain inside the declared trusted database-process boundary; object-scoped `ShardCryptoCx` unwrap permits reduce accidental key exposure but are not a TEE or shard-compromise-isolation claim. Meta alone owns semantic time and global authority.

`RemoteGrantTargetRef` is the generated closed tagged union over every role-valid registered grant target, with one stable wire tag and exact role/posture matrix per target; raw `StrongRef` plus a kind sidecar is forbidden. `RemoteRetentionObligationRoot` is the authority-group authenticated map `(authority_domain,grant_id) -> Active{grant_record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantRecord>,target_ref:RemoteGrantTargetRef,consumer_domain,minimum_floor}|TransferredOut{transfer_record_ref:StrongRef<LegacyRetentionAuthorityTransferRecord>,new_authority_domain,target_identity,migration_floor_ref:StrongRef<ShardingMigrationRetentionFloor>}|TransferredInPending{transition_ref:StrongRef<CertifiedRoleTransitionRef>,source_transfer_record_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,new_meta_grant_record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantRecord>,legacy_target_ref:RemoteGrantTargetRef,consumer_domain,minimum_floor}|TransferredIn{transfer_evidence_ref:StrongRef<LegacyRetentionAuthorityTransferEvidence>,legacy_target_ref:RemoteGrantTargetRef,consumer_domain,minimum_floor}|ReleaseApplied{tombstone_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseTombstone>}|AckPublished{tombstone_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseTombstone>,ack_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>>,release_summary_key,retain_ack_through}`. Transfer variants exist only for the one certified Local-to-Meta transition; ordinary reconfiguration uses the successor-grant handshake. Pending retains the already-built new grant and certified legacy target without naming future grant evidence. Release terminal states have no strong path to active target state, while transfer states retain exactly the migration floor/wrapper until adoption.

`RemoteRetentionConsumerRoot` is the consumer-group authenticated map `(authority_domain,grant_id) -> Acquired{grant_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>,local_consumer_set_commitment}|AuthorityTransferPending{transfer_evidence_ref:LegacyTransferEvidenceRef,old_authority_domain,new_authority_domain,local_consumer_set_commitment}|AuthorityTransferAdopted{adoption_record_ref:StrongRef<RetentionAuthorityTransferAdoptionRecord>,new_authority_grant_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>}|ReleaseRequested{request_record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestRecord>,frozen_consumer_set_commitment}|ReleaseCertified{request_record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestRecord>,request_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestCertificate>>,frozen_consumer_set_commitment}|Acknowledged{ack_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>>,release_summary_key,retire_after_floor}`. `ReleaseRequested|ReleaseCertified` encoders reject any added consumer under the old grant. The release-state identity is an already published weak root/record identity strictly before the request spec, never the request's future result.

`RemoteRetentionControlSpec` is the closed role-valid union `AcquireGrant{grant_spec_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantSpec>}|RegisterConsumerGrant{grant_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>,expected_consumer_root_digest}|RequestConsumerRelease{request_spec_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestSpec>}|PublishConsumerReleaseEvidence{request_certificate_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestCertificate>,request_export_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseRequestCertificate>>}|ApplyAuthorityRelease{apply_spec_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseApplySpec>}|PublishAuthorityReleaseAck{ack_certificate_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>,ack_export_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>>}|ConsumeReleaseAck{ack_export_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>>,expected_frozen_consumer_set_commitment}|AdoptLegacyAuthorityTransfer{adoption_spec_ref:StrongRef<RetentionAuthorityTransferAdoptionSpec>}`. There is no generic transfer arm: old-Local transfer records are created only by `BeginRoleTransitionSpec`, Meta transfer evidence only by the certified activation projection, and a consumer uses only the exact adoption arm above. The generated role matrix fixes each arm to authority, consumer, or transition-consumer posture and rejects a record/evidence type in the wrong arm. This union is included in Local/Meta/Shard control registries. Checkpoint/configuration floors classify every authority/consumer/transfer entry plus unpublished certificate-ledger evidence. Crash tests cut before/after all six release transitions and every transfer/adoption/publication transition.

Trust-chain compaction also has an input/result split. `RemoteConfigurationTrustRoot` is the consumer-local authenticated map `authority_domain:ConsensusDomain -> CurrentEvidence{evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence>,trust_generation,minimum_consumer_floor}|ValidatedAnchor{anchor_ref:StrongRef<ValidatedRemoteConfigurationAnchor>,trust_generation,minimum_consumer_floor}`; it is strongly rooted in every Local/Meta/Shard payload, covered by every checkpoint/vector/configuration floor, and is the sole selector of current imported trust. `AdvanceRemoteConfigurationEvidenceSpec` is the ordinary role-valid CAS `{authority_domain,expected_trust_generation,expected_selected_evidence_or_anchor,successor_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence>,direct_joint_transition_proof,minimum_consumer_floor}`; apply verifies the exact predecessor/anchor and atomically selects `CurrentEvidence(successor,generation+1)`. Meta shard adoption and every shard application of a Meta configuration projection that changes authority must embed the same expected selector/generation and successor evidence and perform this CAS atomically; topology/configuration can never advance while trust remains behind.

Anchor compaction is legal only after reissuing/advancing every live wrapper, grant, backup, replay manifest, prepared owner, and certificate-ledger entry to the selected current evidence. `RemoteTrustCompactionPrecondition` is the generated exhaustive `{consumer state/checkpoint/floor basis,current evidence ref,scanned typed root classes,zero_live_reference_commitment_to_every_predecessor_evidence,current-wrapper inventory commitment}`. `ValidateRemoteConfigurationAnchorSpec` is `{authority_domain,current_configuration_evidence_ref,ordered_predecessor_evidence_refs,precondition_ref:StrongRef<RemoteTrustCompactionPrecondition>,expected_consumer_state,expected_trust_root_generation,expected_current_anchor_digest?,validation_policy_oid,transition_chain_digest}`. Apply verifies every transition while predecessors remain rooted and the precondition proves no surviving object still binds one. It constructs `ValidatedRemoteConfigurationAnchor {authority_domain,through_configuration_oid,through_epoch,current_configuration_canonical_bytes,current_configuration_bytes_digest,current_member_verification_key_set,current_payload_predicate_digest,validated_transition_chain_digest,zero_old_evidence_commitment,validation_policy_oid,input_spec_digest:WeakDigest,consumer_applied_identity:WeakAuthorityAppliedIdentity}` and atomically selects its strong anchor at generation+1. Those retained verification keys and the full domain/predicate authorize validation of a `ValidatedCheckpointSuccessor`; a digest alone cannot. `WeakAuthorityAppliedIdentity = Local{command_oid,logical_command_seq}|Meta{command_oid,global_logical_command_seq}|Shard{command_oid,shard_raft_index}` is nonretaining. A later checkpoint successor or ordinary advance replaces the anchor atomically; no trust gap is legal. Only after the anchor command is checkpoint-cut and the zero-live-old proof remains valid may predecessor evidence retire. Both specs are explicit Local/Meta/Shard control variants; no result is prebuilt with its future command.

`TopologyState` is the meta-owned routing authority `{topology_epoch,form:Stable|Joint{old_epoch,new_epoch},partition_scheme:SourceRange{partition_function_oid}|HubVertexCut{vertex_owner_function_oid,edge_owner_function_oid,replicated_hub_profile},meta_group_id,meta_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,sorted_shards[{shard_id,group_id,configuration_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,state:Active|Joining|Draining,failure_domains,key_access_profile}],sorted_range_or_hub_assignments,constraint_owner_function_oid,id_allocation_owner_map,copy_basis_global_prefix?,cutover_global_prefix?,predecessor_topology_identity:WeakDigest?,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef?}`. Assignments are total and nonoverlapping, identify one canonical data/edge owner plus explicit read-only replicas, and use imported configuration evidence rooted in `RemoteConfigurationTrustRoot`. `Joint` retains both maps/configurations until its typed retirement floor. A meta configuration transition constructs a same-routing successor with the phase-appropriate meta config; shard adoption constructs a successor replacing exactly one imported shard config. A scalar epoch cannot authorize routing, prepare, ID allocation, constraints, or GC.

**Fresh W12 genesis and one-way Local-to-W12 activation.** `ShardedGenesisCertificate` is the first object in the construction order: `{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,genesis_nonce,meta_group_id,meta_configuration_canonical_bytes_digest_and_payload_predicate,meta_initial_protocol_recipe_commitment,meta_initial_prepared_and_consensus_recipe_commitment,sorted_shards[{shard_id,group_id,configuration_canonical_bytes_digest_and_payload_predicate,failure_domains,key_access_profile,initial_protocol_recipe_commitment,initial_prepared_and_consensus_recipe_commitment}],topology_and_partition_recipe,generated_initial_meta_projection_slot_recipes[one_per_MetaProjectionSlot],initial_global_prefix,complete_per_group_durable_slot_bitmap_commitment,root_authority_policy_and_key_epoch,configured_root_authority_signatures}`. It commits no future evidence, payload, topology, projection, checkpoint, or root ObjectId/digest. Construction is certificate → per-group `RootAuthorityTrustArtifact` and Genesis configuration evidence → consumer-local `GenesisMetaProjectionPayload` objects → Topology/ActiveMetaProjection roots → Semantic roots → role-valid Protocol roots → Prepared/Consensus roots → manifests/slots. `GenesisMetaProjectionPayload` is `{genesis_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ShardedGenesisCertificate>,consumer_domain:ConsensusDomain,ordered_slot_values[{slot_tag,typed_canonical_value,source_recipe_digest}],exhaustive_slot_bitmap,configuration_evidence_refs[StrongRef<RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence>],availability_floor}`. Its bitmap equals the complete generated `MetaProjectionSlot` registry—not a five-slot subset—and it has no raw remote-group edge. Every Genesis evidence and `InitialProtocolStateRecipe` byte-matches the root-authority-signed role/group entry, and every group verifies the complete first-manifest slot bitmap before emitting its barrier. A self-selected member signature or bare digest is not a trust anchor.

Existing Local databases activate W12 only through one offline, audited, one-way role transition. Before freeze, the coordinator claims the sole exact Appendix A `ServiceVisibilityEpochReservation` under the external reservation-authority protocol; its target epoch is strictly above both the source and external floors, use requires Usable time evidence, and abandonment permanently burns it. No shorter role-transition-specific encoding exists. The coordinator then pre-mints `ShardingRoleTransitionPlan {database/security namespace/incarnation,migration_nonce,service_epoch_reservation_ref:StrongRef<ServiceVisibilityEpochReservation>,source/target service visibility epochs,source Local group/configuration and expected basis prefix,new Meta and sorted shard group/configuration canonical bytes+payload predicates,fresh per-group log-origin nonces,topology/partition body,projection checker/version,local_to_global_projection_ref:StrongRef<LocalToGlobalProjection>,sorted_local_to_shard_projection_refs[StrongRef<LocalToShardProjection>],activation checkpoint/vector/floor body recipes,expected hidden shard barrier predicates,legacy_artifact disposition table,legacy_retention_authority_transfer_plan_ref:StrongRef<LegacyRetentionAuthorityTransferPlan>,cursor transition policy,external continuity/catalog predecessors,rollback_cutoff,completeness_digest}`. Reservation fields byte-match the plan and refer to no future plan identity. Projection objects are pre-order recipes over the existing Local basis plus deterministic freeze result and contain no result/root identity. Every payload field and mandatory inventory class has exactly one disposition `ReencodeForMeta|PartitionAndReencodeForShard{exact shard set}|CertifiedLegacyCarry{promise class,terminal transition}|ArchiveBeforeBegin{archive requirement}|RejectWithBlocker{reason}`. They cover graph/version/constraint/identity/allocator/branch/key/history/derived/replay/backup/cursor/outcome/capability/DP/resource/audit/transparency/prepared/certificate/remote-trust/retention state. Freeze is invalid while any blocker remains.

`ShardingFreezeSpec {plan_ref:StrongRef<ShardingRoleTransitionPlan>,expected_operational_state,expected Local applied/visible state heads and logical/commit/HLC prefixes,drain inventory,expected_audit_pipeline_ref:StrongRef<LocalAuditVisibilityPipeline>,terminal_audit_gate}` rejects new work and drains every active transaction; unadmitted work is explicitly cancelled, every receipted/ordered owner is terminal or covered by the plan's exact transfer, and the pre-freeze audit pipeline is empty (`applied_tail == visible`) after exact advancement. Apply constructs `ShardingFreezeRecord {spec_ref:StrongRef<ShardingFreezeSpec>,applied_control_ref,source_basis_identity:WeakStateIdentity,assigned frozen prefix,branch/schema/policy/revocation/key/audit/continuity commitments,terminal txn/outcome/prepared proof,live promise inventory,plan_digest,deterministic_frozen_result_commitment,state:FreezePendingAudit}`. It does **not** name its future payload/root. The ordinary command apply builds the candidate root and pipeline successor; that pipeline alone owns the candidate until external audit resolution and an exact gap-free `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Local>` make the freeze candidate visible and change migration state to `FrozenVisible`. The freeze transition may begin only from an empty predecessor pipeline and no later command may append behind it except audit advancement or `ShardingUnfreezeSpec`, preventing a hidden post-freeze tail. Only after `FrozenVisible` may transition artifacts be consumed. Unfreeze is legal only from that visible state before Begin, invalidates this plan generation, and requires a newly audited plan/freeze.

Before Begin can be proposed, every new replica directory is prepared under a linear `PendingRoleTransitionCreationGuard {migration_nonce,target_role_and_group,target_manifest_staging_inode_identity_and_filesystem_profile,local_whole_inode_lease,root_publication_sequencer,plan_and_configuration_commitment,initial_protocol_state_recipe_ref:StrongRef<InitialProtocolStateRecipe<Role>>,first_semantic_entry_recipe_commitment,initial_prepared_recipe_commitment,initial_consensus_recipe_commitment,first_manifest_and_slot_recipe_commitment,complete_durable_slot_bitmap,exact_allowed_staging_inventory,role_transition_signature_lock_ref,unconsumed}`. It may create only uniquely named, unreachable transition staging bytes for those exact recipes, the first-entry authorization closure, and its signature lock; it cannot publish `manifest.root`, serve traffic, issue ordinary receipts, or accept another migration nonce. New-group members sign `RoleTransitionJoinAcceptance` only after rereading that complete Semantic/Protocol/Prepared/Consensus/bootstrap staging closure and bitmap. `RoleTransitionReadyCertificate {root-authority domain,plan_ref,service_epoch_reservation_identity,source/target service epochs,sorted new-group acceptances,complete configuration/log-origin/initial-plane-recipe/staging availability commitment,old-Local freeze visibility certificate identity,expiry policy,threshold signatures}` is pre-order evidence that every group can complete. After the dual transition certificate and external continuity reservation receipt exist, the guard may order/apply only the registered first entry under a RoleTransitionOnly lease; it then follows the universal construction order and publishes one complete hidden Meta/shard manifest. Meta begins with an empty AuditSystem-ready pipeline while service-hidden; Shard begins with `ShardProtocolState::RoleTransitionPreparedHidden`. Migrated pending/released output owners enter the recipe as suspended/transferred promises with no E2 lease or send authority. The guard never publishes a headless or plane-incomplete destination manifest and never becomes an ordinary writer token. Creation, restore, and takeover guards cannot substitute for it.

The final old-Local entry is `BeginRoleTransitionSpec {plan_ref,freeze_record_ref:StrongRef<ShardingFreezeRecord>,freeze_visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local>>,role_transition_ready_certificate_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionReadyCertificate>,expected_frozen_visible root/head/prefix,legacy authority-transfer set,migration retention floor recipe,terminal_audit_gate=StructurallyInapplicable{ShardingRoleTransitionAuthority}}`. The portable certificate byte-matches the freeze candidate, exact visible endpoint, releasing RootManifest/slot, and ready-certificate commitment; no Semantic object references the Protocol pipeline. Acceptance is a terminal Raft seal, not merely an apply-time flag: before any voter acknowledges the Begin `AppendEntries` at index `I`, it durably installs `RaftRoleTransitionSeal {plan_digest,begin_command_digest,terminal_local_raft_index=I,log_origin commitments}` in its `RaftStateRoot` and thereafter rejects every entry or snapshot extending beyond `I`. Once a quorum has accepted Begin, Local recovery may only finish this transition or enter an explicitly fenced restore/clone; it can never unseal and resume Local writes. Apply constructs `BeginRoleTransitionRecord`, advances to `(Lf,Cf,Hf)`, installs the canonical `RoleTransitionLocked` state, and applies the precommitted old-Local side of every authority transfer. The certificate requires both quorum-durable seals and quorum apply through Begin, eliminating the committed-but-unapplied append window.

Every old-Local and new-Meta signer first persists `RoleTransitionSignatureLock {migration_nonce,plan_digest,dual_transcript_digest,state=Locked}` in its staging/certificate ledger; quorum intersection plus the one-digest rule prevents two certificates. `RaftRoleTransitionCertificate` is the unique dual-domain transcript `{database/security namespace/incarnation,plan_ref and digest,migration_nonce,service_epoch_reservation_identity,source/target service visibility epochs,frozen-visible source commitment,Begin input/record weak identities plus Local command/marker/MMR/checkpoint chain digests,Lf,Cf,Hf,projection identities/digests,old Local/new Meta domains/configurations/predicates,all pre-minted log origins,sorted shard domains/configurations and expected hidden-ready barrier predicates,topology,external continuity/catalog predecessors,legacy-retention transfer commitment,migration-floor recipe,rollback cutoff,old-Local quorum seal+apply signatures,new-Meta join+signature-lock quorum signatures}`. It names no future state root, barrier, activation record, or result.

Portable `RoleTransitionContinuityReservationManifest` commits that certificate, epoch reservation, exact prior continuity head, terminal Local seal, destination groups/endpoints, and target epoch. External CAS changes `Operational{posture=Local,epoch=E}` to `RoleTransitionReserved{migration_nonce,transition_certificate_digest,source_posture:Local,target_posture:Sharded,source_service_visibility_epoch:E,target_service_visibility_epoch:E2,service_epoch_reservation_id,source_local_group_and_terminal_seal_commitment,target_group_topology_and_endpoint_commitment,transition_writer_authority_digest,lease_policy:RoleTransitionOnly}` and returns reference-free `RoleTransitionContinuityReservationReceipt {continuity_prior_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,continuity_reserved_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,continuity_cas_receipt_bytes_and_digest,service_epoch_reservation_claimed_head_bytes_and_digest,service_epoch_reservation_consumed_head_bytes_and_digest,reservation_consumption_receipt_bytes_and_digest,manifest_digest,target_service_visibility_epoch,authority_signatures}`. `RoleTransitionContinuityReservationImportRecord` is the Meta/bootstrap-local owner `{manifest_ref,portable_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RoleTransitionContinuityReservationReceipt>,claimed_head_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,consumed_head_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,consumption_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ReservationConsumptionReceipt>,continuity_reserved_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>,exact_transcript_match_digest,body_digest}`. The fixed external order is continuity CAS first, then the reservation's direct `ClaimedForUse -> Consumed` CAS whose `applied_use_identity` is that reserved-continuity head. A crash between them leaves a hidden roll-forward state; recovery can only finish the same consumption, and no combined receipt or destination publication exists until both receipts verify. No destination manifest publication is legal before this receipt and its imported consumed-reservation proof. Once it exists, sealed Local cannot serve or obtain OrdinaryWrite authority, and destination groups may use RoleTransitionOnly solely for hidden bootstrap/projection/service preparation. A crash here is unavailable but roll-forward-only; it never creates overlapping Local and Sharded service.

No consumer directly references an old-group object. `CertifiedRoleTransitionRef` is the **bootstrap** consumer-local imported leaf `{certificate_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,embedded_dual_transcript_and_digest,old_local_configuration_evidence_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,new_meta_configuration_evidence_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,old_and_new_signature_sets,consumer_group_role,consumer_group_id,certificate_availability_evidence,source_transition_retention_grant_evidence}`. Its sole retention evidence is the already-existing source/root-authority migration grant established before Begin; it contains no new-Meta grant, activation record, hidden root, or evidence that activation will create. This bootstrap ref is therefore legal in destination first-entry specs.
