Crash tests cover every role and transfer phase for Preparing/Receipted/Ordered/committed-unapplied/applied-visibility-pending states.

Replica-local `MetaPreparedCommandRecord` is `{prepared_ownership_id,record_generation,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<MetaPreparedCommandRecord>?,database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role:Meta,group_id,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,payload_predicate_digest,local_writer_fence_epoch,attempt_or_control_identity,base_closure_inventory_digest,current_closure_inventory_digest,strong_local_refs[StrongRef],certified_remote_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef],exact_realizations[],command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnCommand|GlobalControlCommand>?,status:Preparing|Receipted|Ordered{raft_index}|Applied{global_logical_command_seq}|Abandoned{fence_and_nonorder_proof}}`; ownership IDs, receipts, and successors bind the full domain/config/predicate header, preserve the base, and strengthen current closure. `MetaPreCommandBaseAvailabilityCertificate` begins `SameGroupCertificateHeader` and carries `{prepared_ownership_id,attempt_or_control_identity,base_closure_inventory_digest,sorted_payload_receipts,covered_object_kinds,exclusion_rule:Exactly{ThisCertificate,FutureMetaCommand,OrderAttemptSupplement}}`. It covers only already-built stable semantic evidence/base closure and excludes itself, the future command, and repeatable order/HLC supplements. Each proposal separately builds `MetaOrderAttemptInput` plus `MetaOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate`; voters revalidate its source signatures/set epoch, quorum, age, uncertainty, normalization, expected next sequence, and forward-jump policy against current state. Stale or sequence-losing evidence retires only that provably unordered supplement and may be replaced without changing semantic prepared ownership or audit bytes. After command construction, every proposer root-publishes the stable-owner successor that adds it.

Replica-local `ShardPreparedPayloadRecord` is `{prepared_ownership_id,record_generation,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<ShardPreparedPayloadRecord>?,database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role:Shard{shard_id},group_id,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,payload_predicate_digest,local_writer_fence_epoch,shard_command_identity:ShardCommandIdentity,base_closure_inventory_digest,current_closure_inventory_digest,strong_local_refs[StrongRef],certified_remote_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef],exact_realizations[],command_ref:StrongRef<ShardPrepareCommand|ShardDecisionApplyCommand|ShardControlCommand>?,status:Preparing|Receipted|Ordered{raft_index}|Applied{shard_raft_index}|Abandoned{fence_and_nonorder_proof}}`. Ownership IDs and receipts bind the entire domain/config/predicate header. `ShardPreCommandBaseAvailabilityCertificate` begins `SameGroupCertificateHeader` and carries `{prepared_ownership_id,shard_command_identity,base_closure_inventory_digest,sorted_payload_receipts,covered_object_kinds,exclusion_rule:Exactly{ThisCertificate,FutureShardCommand}}`. For prepare, generated coverage includes exact basis/fragment/admission closure formerly called `ShardPayloadAvailabilityCertificate`; that name is an alias, not a second certificate. Decision-apply and every payload-bearing shard control use the same exact-configuration D1 barrier. A stable-owner successor adds the completed command before proposal. Replica fence epochs never enter canonical semantic objects.

The sole permit schemas are Appendix A's generic `AuthorityPermitRef<Role>`, `MutationPermitReadProjection<Role>`, `ReadCapablePermitRef<Role>`, and `ImportedReadCapablePermitEvidence`; no unparameterized duplicate permit union or digest-only mutation-read projection exists here. `AuditTicketOwner` is the closed tag `Operation{global_txn_id,registration_generation,operation_request_basis_commitment}|Statement{global_txn_id,registration_generation,statement_seq,statement_idempotency_key,statement_request_basis_commitment}|Control{control_request_identity}`. `AuditResolutionOrigin` is `Unclaimed{admission_record_ref:AppliedControlRef,request_basis_commitment,terminal_class:DeniedBeforeClaim|AbandonedBeforeClaim}|Claimed{claim_record_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketClaimRecord>,owner:AuditTicketOwner,request_basis_commitment}`.

The Semantic `AuditTicketIndexValue` is the monotone state machine `PendingUnclaimed{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,admission_record_ref:AppliedControlRef,request_basis_commitment}|Claimed{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,claim_record_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketClaimRecord>,owner:AuditTicketOwner,request_basis_commitment}|TerminalPrepared{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,origin:AuditResolutionOrigin,event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>,resolution_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketResolution>,terminal_gate_commitment,candidate_state_identity:WeakStateIdentity,pipeline_order}`. Only `PendingUnclaimed -> Claimed -> TerminalPrepared(origin=Claimed)` or the no-effect pre-claim `PendingUnclaimed -> TerminalPrepared(origin=Unclaimed)` is legal. The Semantic index never contains an external CAS receipt or visibility result because those facts arise later in the Protocol plane. `VisibleAuditOutcomeIndex` is the sole join from a TerminalPrepared ticket/candidate to its exact external batch receipt/member ordinal, `AuditOutcomeRecord`, and releasing maintenance transition. Byte-identical retry returns the same value; mismatched admission/claim/owner/origin fails, and a ticket can never be claimed by two attempts/statements, prepared for another owner, or released by a nonmatching Protocol outcome.

`OperationAuditAdmission` is `NotRequired{evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence}|PendingUnclaimed{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,ticket_admission_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,operation_request_basis_commitment}|Claimed{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,claim_record_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketClaimRecord>,owner:{global_txn_id,registration_generation},operation_request_basis_commitment}`. `GlobalAttemptRegistrationSpec` accepts only `NotRequired` or `PendingUnclaimed`; registration apply atomically transforms the latter into `Claimed` and creates `AuditTicketClaimRecord {ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,owner:AuditTicketOwner::Operation,request_basis_commitment,claiming_control_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlCommand>,claimed_at_global_logical_command_seq}` before constructing the registration/payload. Every later read, statement publication, admission, cancel, commit, abort, or read-close must carry or derive the exact Claimed value from that registration. The claim remains unresolved until the one terminal attempt read-close/commit/abort event. A pre-registration denial or abandoned BEGIN is one semantic mutation—creation of the capability-visible NeverRegistered outcome—so only `GlobalBeginTerminalSpec` may resolve its exact `PendingUnclaimed` ticket and publish that candidate; `AuditTerminalSpec` remains reserved for terminal paths that create no other durable semantic mutation.

Registration and prepare admission are distinct lifecycle-scaffolding rows. A `GlobalBeginReservationSpec`, `GlobalAttemptRegistrationSpec`, or `AuditTerminalFreezeSpec::Arm` exists before the operation claim it may atomically create, so its wrapper is exactly `NotRequired{evidence:LifecycleScaffolding{decision_ref,registered_scaffolding_spec_tag,scaffolding_body_digest,parent_operation_claim_commitment?,compatibility_row_id}}`; the decision is prebuilt against the pending-ticket/request basis and the registered atomic-claim recipe and never points to the future claim. After registration has produced a claim, `GlobalPrepareAdmissionSpec` may use `NotRequired{evidence:CoveredByParentClaim{claim_ref,coverage_rule_id=LifecycleScaffoldingCoveredByOperationTicket,covered_body_digest}}`. Neither form resolves the operation ticket. The typed evidence byte-matches operation tag, pending admission or claim owner, request/body basis, audit epoch, policy, and revocation state. Arm/claim substitution and any pre-order reference to a future claim are G0-invalid and have negative DAG fixtures. Nonterminal statement results use distinct statement tickets below, never the operation ticket. A completeness profile that requires separate registration/admission events supplies distinct tickets and registered lifecycle tags; negotiation fails rather than overloading a claimed ticket.

BEGIN has its own durable, idempotent lineage before an attempt exists. `TxnOutcomeCapabilityFields` is the shared exact inline value `{header:DurableCapabilityHeader{capability_kind=TxnOutcome},body:{request_kind:ExplicitBegin|AutocommitExecute,scoped_request_idempotency_key_digest,txn_token,outcome_capability_key},mac}`; the body key is pre-minted, every header/body field and MAC byte exists before ordering, and `capability_commitment` is the domain-separated digest of those canonical bytes. `GlobalBeginIdempotencyIndex` maps `(database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,tenant,principal,scoped_begin_idempotency_key)` to exactly `Reserved{reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalBeginReservationRecord>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|BoundDetailed{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalBeginReservationRecord>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|BoundCompacted{summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,leaf_key_digest,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|NeverRegisteredDetailed{terminal_record_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredTerminalRecord>,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Meta>>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,audit_terminal_commitment}|NeverRegisteredCompacted{summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,summary_leaf_key_digest,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Meta>>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>}|ExpiredTombstone{scoped_key_digest,txn_token_digest,capability_commitment,expiry_evidence_digest}`. `GlobalBeginReservationSpec` is the sequence-neutral pre-order body `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,scoped_begin_idempotency_key,canonical_begin_request_digest,operation_class,isolation,transaction_error_policy,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::PendingUnclaimed|NotRequired,expected_begin_index_absence_proof,staging_resource_reservation_ref:StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. The header is the sole namespace/incarnation/service/tenant/principal/ceiling/policy/revocation source. The replay object's capability header/body/request kind/key/domain/authority/profile/window, resource reservation, and audit admission byte-match it; no command trusts a duplicate inline encoding. Apply creates `GlobalBeginReservationRecord {authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,source_spec_ref:StrongRef<GlobalBeginReservationSpec>,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,canonical_begin_request_digest,txn_token,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,capability_commitment,operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::PendingUnclaimed|NotRequired,reserved_at_global_prefix,staging_generation}` and atomically installs `Reserved` plus `GlobalOutcomeDirectoryValue::BeginStaging` with that same replay ref; only then may Fabric emit `BEGIN_ACCEPTED`. A byte-identical retry returns the replay object's identical canonical capability bytes even after key rotation, while a same key with any request, admission, authority, epoch, profile/window, or policy drift fails. Verifier key and accepted time profile remain rooted through the capability lookup/expiry floor.

Registration must consume the exact reservation and atomically change `Reserved -> BoundDetailed`; no later command may return it to staging. `BeginRegionClosedReceipt` is the authenticated runtime record `{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,session_binding,session_region_id,reservation_identity,canonical_request_digest,observed_region_close_generation,no_registration_worker_or_acceptance_response_obligation_proof_digest,issuer_id,key_epoch,signature}` and is distinct from result-delivery `SessionRegionCloseReceipt`. `BeginTerminalEvidence` is the role-checked closed union `Denied{authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord>,stable_denial_class}|AuthenticatedAbandon{request_ref:StrongRef<AuthenticatedBeginAbandonRequest>,request_idempotency_key}|RegionClosed{receipt_ref:StrongRef<BeginRegionClosedReceipt>}|Superseded{request_ref:StrongRef<AuthenticatedBeginSupersedeRequest>,request_idempotency_key,new_request_commitment}`. Its generated compatibility table maps exactly Denied->DeniedBeforeRegistration, RegionClosed->BeginDisconnected, AuthenticatedAbandon->AbandonedStaging, and Superseded->SupersededRetry; every arm binds the same session/principal/authority/reservation/request and includes a typed no-registration/effect/response proof. Time passage, socket suspicion, process death, or a result-delivery receipt inhabits no arm.

`LocalBeginTerminalSpec` is `{reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalBeginReservationRecord>,expected_index_value:Reserved,evidence:BeginTerminalEvidence::Local,internal_reason_class,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Local>>,operation_audit_admission:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::PendingUnclaimed|NotRequired,staging_resource_release_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>],terminal_audit_freeze:AuditFreezeField::Required<LocalBeginTerminal>,terminal_audit_gate}`. `GlobalBeginTerminalSpec` is `{reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalBeginReservationRecord>,expected_index_value:Reserved,evidence:BeginTerminalEvidence::Meta,internal_reason_class,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Meta>>,operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::PendingUnclaimed|NotRequired,staging_resource_release_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>],terminal_audit_freeze:AuditFreezeField::Required<MetaBeginTerminal>,terminal_audit_gate}`. Admission, evidence, internal reason, bundle subject/domain/request, and freeze must byte-match the reservation exactly. Apply resolves that exact ticket when present and creates the role's compactable NeverRegistered record; Meta creates `NeverRegisteredTerminalRecord {reservation_identity:WeakDigest,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,capability_commitment,internal_reason_class,public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle<Meta>>,terminal_global_prefix,audit_terminal_commitment}` plus detailed index/directory entries carrying that same bundle. Deterministic apply never mints a public handle, maps a reason, signs a disclosure receipt, or recomputes response bytes. Neither role creates an attempt, attempt family/key, conflict, prepared, statement, or shard entry. `NeverRegisteredFloorSpec` later rewrites Meta Detailed to Compacted after proving that this reservation never bound or escaped and copies the bundle's exact frozen public bytes into the authenticated summary leaf; both compacted indexes retain the same bundle ref. `GlobalOutcomeExpirySpec` then atomically rewrites both outcome-directory and begin-index leaves to permanent capability/key `ExpiredTombstone` only with matching `TimeValidationEvidence::Expired`, verifier/profile floors, terminal proof, and retention cut; only expiry drops the bundle/replay edges. Neither step requires the index being rewritten to be absent, and neither fabricates an attempt identity.

`AttemptFamilyState` is the authenticated Meta map value `{global_txn_id,latest_generation,latest_state:Active{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>}|PrepareAdmitted{outcome_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord>}|Terminal{outcome_or_summary_ref:StrongRef},active_generation:None|Some{generation},latest_token_digest,successor_eligibility_floor}`. NeverRegistered is exclusively a terminal begin-key/outcome-directory state and never creates or advances an attempt family. `TransactionErrorPolicy` is the closed v1 union `AbortTransactionOnStatementError|ContinueOnDeclaredRecoverable{recoverable_error_class_table_oid}`; unsupported savepoint/partial-rollback semantics are rejected at BEGIN. `GlobalAttemptRegistrationSpec` is sequence-neutral `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,global_txn_id,begin_idempotency_key,begin_binding:BindReserved{reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalBeginReservationRecord>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,expected_index_value:Reserved}|IdempotentBound{reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalBeginReservationRecord>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,existing_registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,expected_index_value:BoundDetailed},txn_token,txn_operation_ceiling:TxnOperationCeiling,autocommit_classification_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalStatementClassification>?,requested_snapshot_selector,minimum_acceptable_snapshot,authority_permit_ref:AuthorityPermitRef<Meta>,transaction_error_policy:TransactionErrorPolicy,initial_workspace_body_recipe,operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::NotRequired|PendingUnclaimed,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,registration_generation,expected_attempt_family_state:First{authenticated_absence_proof}|Successor{prior_generation,prior_terminal_outcome_proof_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptOutcomeProof>,prior_release_and_overlap_floor_ref:StrongRef<AttemptReleaseAndOverlapFloor>}|IdempotentRetry{existing_registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,existing_registration_body_digest},expected_outcome_capability_state:ReservedForThisBegin|IdempotentSameRegistration,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. The full header, permit, replay object, reservation, audit admission, requested snapshot, and topology basis must byte-match one complete Meta binding. The optional classification is required exactly for AutocommitExecute and must be within the ceiling; it is forbidden for ExplicitBegin. Apply decodes the replay object, byte-matches it to the reservation/request/classification, atomically installs BoundDetailed with that same replay ref, and stores it in the first registered outcome lineage. `BoundCompacted` is terminal lookup state, never an idempotent-registration input.

It is applied only through `GlobalControlCommand(kind=RegisterAttempt)`. `First` requires an absent family and generation zero; `Successor` requires checked generation increment, no active generation, an exact terminal predecessor, every required release/overlap floor, and a fresh token. `IdempotentRetry` byte-matches the complete registration. `BindReserved` atomically changes Reserved to BoundDetailed; `IdempotentBound` returns the exact existing registration. Only binding claims the operation ticket, checks the conflict safe-snapshot frontier, inserts Active family/attempt/conflict entries, changes the outcome directory to W12, and constructs `GlobalAttemptRegistration {authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,command_ref,global_txn_id,begin_idempotency_key,capability_replay_ref,request_kind,txn_token,txn_operation_ceiling,autocommit_classification_ref?,registered_snapshot,registered_at_global_logical_command_seq,topology_state_ref,authority_permit_ref,permit_scope_digest,transaction_error_policy,operation_audit_admission,registration_generation}`. Apply then constructs workspace generation zero with the exact same header and ProvenReadOnly cumulative potential plus the Registered outcome. Parallel generations, gaps, token rebinding, classification/ceiling/authority drift, or reuse fail closed. Every transaction registers before its first participant read; denial before registration uses only `GlobalBeginTerminalSpec`.

`ShardStatementWorkspaceDelta` is the shard-local immutable `{authority_projection:ShardAuthorityBindingProjection,execution_scope:ShardStatement,shard_id,workspace_generation,prior_local_workspace_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardTxnWorkspaceRoot>,classification_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<CanonicalStatementClassification>,routed_intent_batch_projection_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GraphIntentBatchProjection>,complete_intent_batch_digest,base_snapshot_read_state_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,ordered_prior_overlay_commitment,statement_read_witness_refs[ShardReadWitnessRef],statement_effect:Write{delta_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaTemplate>}|ReadOnly{no_write_effect_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoWriteEffectProof>},result_fragment_digest,successor_local_workspace_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardTxnWorkspaceRoot>,topology_epoch,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,active_meta_projection_root_ref:StrongRef<ActiveMetaProjectionRoot>,key_epoch_commitment,deterministic_recompute_digest}`. The effect tag must match the derived classification; a ProvenReadOnly statement uses the proof arm. The full authority projection must match the imported registration, batch, active Meta projection, configuration, and clipped scope; a security-only projection is insufficient. `ShardStatementWorkspaceCertificate` strongly names the delta, exact authority/batch projections and digest, prior/successor overlay roots, statement/workspace generation, partition/effect/read commitments, active projection, and signer lock. `GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration` is the Meta object `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,authority_relation_to_registration:Exact|Narrowed{nonwidening_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuthorityNonwideningProof>},workspace_generation,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>?,through_statement_seq,statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStatementRegistration>?,published_intent_batch_ref:StrongRef<GraphIntentBatch>?,sorted_shard_workspace_certificate_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardStatementWorkspaceCertificate>],complete_partition_batch_and_effect_commitment,cumulative_logical_effect_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeTxnEffectRoot>,cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,cumulative_mutation_potential,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,key_epoch_commitment}`. Generation zero carries the registration's exact header and canonical empty overlay with ProvenReadOnly potential; each publication increments once, takes the monotone potential join, and carries the same or a proved narrower complete authority binding. Floors retain every batch/overlay/generation until all typed obligations transfer or close.

`ReadExecutionScope` is the closed posture tag `LocalTerminal{registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>}|LocalStatement{registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalStatementRegistration>,workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>}|MetaTerminal{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>}|MetaStatement{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStatementRegistration>,workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>}|ShardTerminal{registration_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,workspace_generation_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>}|ShardStatement{registration_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,statement_registration_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalStatementRegistration>,workspace_generation_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>}`. Role and Terminal/Statement combinations are disjoint. `ShardReadWitnessBasis` is the shard-owned `{execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::ShardTerminal|ShardStatement,authority_projection:ShardAuthorityBindingProjection,shard_id,shard_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,topology_epoch,registered_snapshot,registration_global_prefix,workspace_generation,local_workspace_overlay_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardTxnWorkspaceRoot>,active_meta_projection_root_ref:StrongRef<ActiveMetaProjectionRoot>,operation_audit_claim_commitment,statement_audit_claim_commitment?,policy_epoch,revocation_index,key_epoch_commitment,audit_visible_prefix,read_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,read_state_checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<ShardCheckpointCertificate>,read_index_or_applied_prefix_proof,queryable_history_inventory_ref:StrongRef<ShardHistoryInventory>,exact_read_graph_bindings,read_access_admission_ref:StrongRef<ReadAccessAdmissionRecord>,read_witness_refs[ShardReadWitnessRef],final_read_summary,result_answer_contract,canonical_result_fragment_digest,read_result_release_decision_ref:StrongRef<ReadResultReleaseDecisionRecord>,recompute_verifier_version,basis_digest}`. The complete projection—not only its digest—must byte-match the imported permit, access admission, active Meta security projection, registration/workspace exports, and release decision; `basis_digest` covers it canonically. `ShardReadWitnessRef` is the generated closed union of role-valid witness targets. The access admission exists before the first cursor/storage/statistics observation; the release decision exists only after the final witness/result digest and cannot retroactively authorize access. Reads evaluate the registered snapshot plus the exact ordered local workspace overlay certified by the imported global generation; a missing prior-touched-shard delta or false empty-overlay proof fails. Before any shard read, operation and applicable statement tickets are imported/claimed through the two certified registrations. The active Meta projection covers their prefix and security slots, and the shard rechecks revocation. No shard decision contains a raw Meta permit, ticket, claim, statement, or workspace reference.

`ReadParticipantRoutingCertificate` is meta-owned `{SameGroupCertificateHeader,execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::MetaTerminal|MetaStatement,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,sorted_read_basis_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardReadWitnessBasis>],sorted_participants,complete_read_footprint,partition_derivation_proof_ref:StrongRef<ParticipantRoutingDerivationProof>,complete_participant_commitment,aggregate_read_state_cut_commitment,aggregate_workspace_overlay_commitment,aggregate_read_witness_commitment,operation_audit_claim_commitment,statement_audit_claim_commitment?,result_answer_contract,canonical_result_digest,one_digest_signer_lock_ref:StrongRef<CertificateSignerLock>,quorum_signatures}`. The proof reaches a fixed point over every routed read/expansion/constraint-disclosure and previously touched workspace shard; it owns the remote state/witness/security/overlay bases through publication and the attempt conflict-retention floor.

Explicit transactions publish each nonterminal statement through Meta order. `StatementAuditAdmission` is `NotRequired{evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence}|PendingUnclaimed{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,ticket_admission_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,statement_request_basis_commitment}|Claimed{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,claim_record_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketClaimRecord>,owner:{global_txn_id,registration_generation,statement_seq,statement_idempotency_key},statement_request_basis_commitment}`. `GlobalStatementRegistrationSpec` is `{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,expected_attempt_state:Active,statement_seq,statement_idempotency_key,classification_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalStatementClassification>,classification_digest,canonical_statement_digest,parameter_digest,requested_result_answer_contract,expected_prior_statement_frontier,expected_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,transaction_error_policy:TransactionErrorPolicy,statement_audit_admission:NotRequired|PendingUnclaimed,expected_conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. Apply requires checked next sequence, current workspace, byte-identical error policy, classification within the attempt ceiling, and classification/bound-query/parameter equality; it claims the distinct ticket, creates `GlobalStatementRegistration {applied_control_ref,registration_ref,statement_seq,statement_idempotency_key,classification_ref,derived_statement_class,canonical_statement_digest,parameter_digest,result_answer_contract,expected_workspace_generation_ref,transaction_error_policy,statement_audit_admission,registered_snapshot,registered_at_global_prefix}`, and inserts Open. Exact retry returns that record; key, classification, sequence, statement, parameter, workspace, security, or scope drift fails. No shard read/effect precedes visible registration.

`ResultSchema` is the immutable public schema `{format_version,columns[{ordinal,name,logical_type,nullability,graph_or_value_annotation}],canonical_public_schema_digest}`, where ordinals are contiguous from zero, names and annotations use the canonical public string/value encodings, and `canonical_public_schema_digest = BLAKE3("fgdb:public-result-schema:v1" || canonical(format_version,columns))`. `CanonicalResultChunkDescriptor` is `{ordinal,canonical_offset,canonical_len,public_chunk_digest,ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<CanonicalResultChunk>}`; descriptors are contiguous and cover exactly `[0,byte_count)` without overlap or gap, and each public digest is over the exact plaintext canonical byte slice under `"fgdb:public-result-chunk:v1"`. `BufferedResultOwner` is the pre-publication closed union `TerminalRead{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>}|AutocommitWriteTerminal{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,prepare_admission_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>}|Statement{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStatementRegistration>}`. `BufferedResultManifest` is the immutable spill-backed closure `{owner:BufferedResultOwner,canonical_schema_ref:StrongRef<ResultSchema>,public_schema_digest,canonical_encoding_version,result_answer_contract,row_count,byte_count,ordered_chunks[CanonicalResultChunkDescriptor],canonical_result_digest,resource_charge_ref:StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>}`. The schema ref must reproduce `public_schema_digest`; descriptors must reproduce `byte_count` and their plaintext digests; and `canonical_result_digest = BLAKE3("fgdb:canonical-result:v1" || public_schema_digest || canonical(result_answer_contract) || canonical_encoding_version || row_count || byte_count || exact_concatenated_canonical_result_bytes)`. That public transcript excludes owner identity, keyed object/schema/chunk identities, ciphertext, resource charge, delivery lease, storage placement, and the digest field itself. The manifest deliberately contains no lease, publication record, read-close record, or delivery state. Chunks use bounded canonical framing, encryption, backpressure-safe iteration, and D1 coverage; prepared ownership retains the manifest before publication, but no schema, row, error detail, or replay reference is client-visible. FGP recomputes the schema, chunk, coverage, and final result digests before emission and on replay; golden and mutation tests flip every included and excluded field to prove the transcript boundary.

`ResultInitialRetentionRecipe<Meta>` is the sequence-neutral `{delivery_policy_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryPolicy<Meta>>,inline_activation_recipe:InlineResultDeliveryActivationRecipe<Meta>?}`; the inline arm is legal only under the same-publication visible-root proof above. `StatementPublishedOutput` is the closed union `NoResult{no_result_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoResultProof>,terminal_delivery_frontier_digest}|Buffered{manifest_ref:StrongRef<BufferedResultManifest>,initial_retention_recipe:ResultInitialRetentionRecipe<Meta>}`; WriteNoResult requires NoResult and every result-bearing class requires Buffered. `ResultDeliveryOwner` is the post-apply closed union `TerminalRead{registration_ref,read_close_applied_ref}|AutocommitWriteTerminal{registration_ref,global_terminal_completion_applied_ref,terminal_ready_outcome_ref}|Statement{registration_ref,statement_registration_ref,publication_applied_ref}|DpResult{request_key_digest,commit_result_applied_ref}`. `ResultDeliveryLease` is constructed only at legal inline-visible activation or by `ProtectedOutputActivationSpec::Result`, as `{owner,manifest_ref,policy_ref,time_basis_ref,delivery_id,lease_generation,canonical_result_digest,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,issuance_time_validation_evidence_ref,chunk_replay_profile,maximum_replay_count,successor_capability_fields,activation_applied_ref:ResultActivationAppliedRef}`. Its header byte-matches the time basis, release decision, owner, and current Meta authority. Construction is manifest/owner/policy/escrow -> PendingVisibility or released owner -> immutable audit release -> fresh evidence -> lease/replay -> ActiveDeliverable successor -> protocol root; none points to a future Global record/root. Semantic `ResultDeliveryState` is `NotApplicableTerminal|DetachedToProtocolRetention{delivery_id,detachment_commitment}`. ACK/release state belongs only to the Protocol record/index, uniformly for terminal and statement results. Sharded write-terminal publication hold is constructed only by `GlobalTerminalCompletionSpec` after TerminalReady and complete commit/delta/durability/audit postconditions; its client lease is constructed only at later activation unless a valid inline-visible recipe exists, and commit never waits for ACK.

`GlobalStatementPublicationSpec` is `{statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStatementRegistration>,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,statement_audit_admission:StatementAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,read_routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ReadParticipantRoutingCertificate>,global_read_authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<GlobalReadAuthorizationDecisionRecord>,sorted_workspace_certificate_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardStatementWorkspaceCertificate>],expected_prior_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,successor_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,final_read_witness_refs[GeneratedCertifiedRemoteReadWitnessRef],statement_serialization_certificate_ref:StrongRef<DistributedSerializationCertificate>,published_output:StatementPublishedOutput,expected_prior_cumulative_read_witness_root:WeakDigest,successor_cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,expected_statement_index_value:Open,expected_attempt_state:Active,expected_conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity,terminal_audit_gate}`. Published-output tag/manifest owner, statement class, exact prior/successor workspace, routed shard set, read witnesses, result, policy/inline recipe, security binding, and audit owner all byte-match. Its statement event/resolution commits the exact result and workspace/effect transition without resolving the operation ticket. When Buffered, its registered `AtomicProtocolDetach` row constructs the role-valid `ResultDeliveryOwner`, semantic resource transfer, Protocol resource escrow, and `ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Meta>`; a legal visible inline activation constructs ActiveDeliverable, a visible candidate without time evidence constructs ReleasedAwaitingActivation, and every hidden candidate constructs PendingVisibility. The semantic statement value is only `Published{publication_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,output:NoResult{delivery_state:NotApplicableTerminal}|BufferedDetached{delivery_id,result_digest,detachment_commitment},workspace_generation_ref}`. The same RootManifest generation updates the Protocol index without a semantic reference to it, constructs the immediately succeeding `GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord::Registered`, advances semantic frontiers, and appends the candidate. Required audit release moves PendingVisibility to ReleasedAwaitingActivation; independent activation later makes it streamable. The semantic attempt can finish or compact while this independent record remains pending, released, or active.

`TerminalOutcomeEvidenceRef` is `Detailed{outcome_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord>}|Compacted{summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,leaf_key_digest}` and is status evidence only, never result-release evidence. `ResultReleaseEvidence<Meta>` is exactly `ClientRelease{receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuthenticatedClientResultReleaseReceipt<Meta>>}|LeaseExpired{expiry_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DeliveryLeaseExpiryEvidence>}`; sender pause, QUERY_CANCEL, session/region/process death, Drop, and attempt terminalization cannot inhabit it. `ResultDeliveryDispositionSpec<Meta>` is the Protocol Maintenance `{active_retention_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Meta>>,service_visibility_epoch,expected_current_promotion_receipt_digest,expected_active_retention_state_digest,transition:Acknowledge{delivery_id,result_digest,ack_receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuthenticatedClientResultAckReceipt<Meta>>}|ReleaseUnacked{public_reason:PublicReasonProjection,evidence:ResultReleaseEvidence},prebuilt_protocol_resource_disposition_ref:StrongRef<ResultResourceDispositionRecord>,prebuilt_capability_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<ResultCapabilityTombstone>}`. It has structural delivery-system authority and no terminal audit gate. Apply decodes ActiveDeliverable to byte-match owner/manifest/lease/replay lineage and current Protocol index membership, then creates exactly `ResultAckRecord|ResultReleaseRecord {owner_digest,delivery_id,result_digest,weak_prior_manifest_identity,weak_prior_lease_identity,prior_retention_state_digest,ack_or_release_evidence_digest,maintenance_applied_ref:MaintenanceAppliedRef,resource_disposition_digest,capability_tombstone_digest}` plus one compact terminal retention successor. Neither terminal evidence record strongly references the manifest, lease, active record, chunks, replay bytes, verifier key, escrow, or bulk charge. It atomically CASes `ResultIndependentRetentionIndex<Meta>` and consumes only the Protocol escrow; the semantic owner/ledger were already detached. Acknowledge requires the exact `RESULT_END` send-completion bridge; release never retracts published result, audit, conflict, workspace, or terminal outcome state. Matching retry returns the same compact record; mismatched nonce, digest, owner, epoch, publication/close, or prior state fails. Attempt compaction accepts PendingIndependent, ReleasedIndependent, ActiveIndependent, Acknowledged, or ReleasedUnacked through `ResultDeliveryDispositionProof` and never waits for client activity.

`GlobalStatementAbortSpec` is `{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,statement_registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStatementRegistration>,expected_statement_index_value:Open,statement_audit_admission:StatementAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,stable_failure_class,no_result_and_no_workspace_effect_proof_ref:StrongRef,resource_release_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>],continuation_action:ContinueAttempt|AbortAttempt,transaction_error_policy:TransactionErrorPolicy,expected_conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity,authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord>,terminal_audit_gate}`. The pinned policy and error-class table uniquely determine `continuation_action`; ambient policy cannot choose after failure. Apply resolves the statement ticket, marks `Failed|Abandoned`, leaves workspace/cumulative witnesses unchanged, and, when required, makes `GlobalAttemptCancelSpec` the only next user transition.

Read-write prepare names the exact contiguous published-statement frontier, delivery-detachment frontier, latest `GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration`, cumulative read-witness root, and cumulative logical-effect root. An Open/unpublished statement, a buffered result not atomically detached into its independent index, a workspace gap, or a mismatched statement/effect frontier makes admission invalid; an Active independently retained result does not. Final `GlobalReadCloseSpec` is the terminal transition for a read-only attempt: autocommit publishes its `BufferedResultOwner::TerminalRead` result directly into independent retention, while an explicit transaction names the complete prior publication/detachment/workspace chain without replaying those bytes. Both rerun serialization over cumulative witnesses, resolve the operation ticket, install `ReadClosed`, and only then permit COMMIT success. Write-then-read executes against the certified workspace overlay, so multi-statement ReadOnly and intermediate ReadWrite results preserve read-your-own-writes without exposing unaudited or SSI-untracked observations; ACK latency is outside transaction correctness.

`GlobalPrepareAdmissionSpec` is `{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,expected_attempt_state:Active,admission_generation,expected_published_statement_frontier,expected_delivery_terminal_frontier,expected_global_statement_index_digest,expected_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,expected_cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,expected_cumulative_logical_effect_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeTxnEffectRoot>,routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ParticipantRoutingCertificate>,sorted_participants,sorted_constraint_owners,sorted_basis_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardEffectBasis>],meta_constraint_reservation_requests[],global_authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAuthorizationDecisionRecord>,resource_transition_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>],global_outcome_preparation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord>,operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,transaction_audit_basis_commitment,global_final_effect_digest,expected_branch_heads[WeakMarkerIdentity],expected_branch_key_epochs,expected_topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,expected_policy_epoch,expected_revocation_index,expected_conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. Its operation-audit value byte-matches registration; a claimed ticket remains unresolved, and the lifecycle-scaffolding proof commits the admission body and transaction decision without consuming it. Apply requires ReadWrite, exact fixed-point/evidence equality, a contiguous statement frontier with every registered statement terminally `Published|Failed|Abandoned`, every Published output either `NoResult/NotApplicableTerminal` or a Buffered result that is delivery-terminal or independently retained, no Open statement, exact workspace/read/effect roots, no prior admission/terminal, and current authorization/resource/outcome/audit state. Every `ShardEffectBasis` must deterministically finalize the corresponding certified workspace overlay rather than re-executing or omitting a prior statement. Apply atomically changes attempt/conflict state to `PrepareAdmitted`, creates Meta reservations, and constructs `MetaTerminalAdmissionFence::Armed {registration_ref,admission_generation,admission_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,frozen_branch_heads,frozen_branch_key_epochs,frozen_write_coordinate_commitment,frozen_constraint_reservation_set_commitment,frozen_resource_transition_set_commitment,frozen_statement_delivery_workspace_witness_effect_commitment,frozen_topology_state_ref,frozen_policy_epoch,frozen_revocation_index,frozen_conflict_basis,operation_audit_claim_commitment,transaction_audit_basis_commitment}`. The fence deliberately contains no assigned future sequence or HLC. It prevents a second admission and roots the preparatory obligations, but the later final-certification reservation is the object that atomically revalidates and holds the exact terminal basis through signing/order. The succeeding `GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord::PrepareAdmitted`, `GlobalAttemptIndex`, and `GlobalConflictIndex` entries are the one canonical selector of that Armed version.

After every required prepare/constraint/serialization certificate and exact commit-or-abort release basis exists, `GlobalFinalCertificationReserveSpec` selects one terminal and installs its Active reservation before the audit plan. A terminal command requires that exact reservation and constructs `MetaTerminalAdmissionFence::TerminalOrdered {predecessor_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalAdmissionFence>,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalFinalCertificationReservation>,global_txn_command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnCommand>,assigned_global_logical_command_seq,assigned_global_commit_seq:None|Some{value}}`; the same atomic lifecycle transition consumes the reservation and selects the fence successor. Admitted abort requires an Active reservation whose selection is MetaAbort and constructs/selects `MetaTerminalAdmissionFence::Released {predecessor_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalAdmissionFence>,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalFinalCertificationReservation>,applied_abort_ref:AppliedAbortRef::MetaTxn,assigned_global_logical_command_seq}`. Old versions remain immutable and retained through terminal compaction; CAS of outcome/attempt/conflict/reservation forbids two successors. No CRSR/prepare is exportable until admission is durable, applied, and visible. A profile requiring a distinct admission-control audit event supplies a separately claimed lifecycle ticket; it never reuses the operation or statement ticket. Routing, participants, bases, owner requests, ticket/basis, statement/delivery frontier, workspace, generation, or any admitted semantic basis cannot change without an admitted abort/new generation; once final certification is Active, its exact held basis cannot drift or be reclaimed until consumption/cancel-before-signature.

Final terminal certification is a distinct ordered reservation **after** final rebase/authorization/serialization and **before** any terminal audit lock or signature. An abort is authorized by a typed terminal-only object rather than a reason string. `AdministrativeAbortAuthorizationRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the exact `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,registration_ref:RoleExactRegistrationRef,operation:LifecycleOperation::RequestTerminalAbort{stage:Active|PrepareAdmitted},administrative_request_ref:RoleExactAdministrativeAbortRequestRef,requested_public_reason_class,declared_disclosure_and_audit_contract,effect_class:LifecycleEffectClass::TerminalAbortNoEffectReleaseOnly,decision,authorization_audit_gate}`; no ReadAccess, mutation, status, or capability-management decision can substitute. `TerminalAbortAuthority<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,registration_ref:RoleExactRegistrationRef,required_lifecycle_stage:Active|PrepareAdmitted,terminal_scope_commitment,source:ClientRequested{lifecycle_decision_ref:StrongRef<LifecycleAuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>>,required_operation:RequestTerminalAbort{stage:required_lifecycle_stage},authenticated_cancel_or_rollback_request_ref:RoleExactTerminalRequestRef,no_new_effect_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoNewEffectProof>}|CorrectnessRequired{original_operation_authorization_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>>,registered_reason:SerializationFailure|RebaseConflict|AuthorizationNarrowedOrRevoked|ConstraintViolation|ResourceFailure|KeyOrTopologyDrift|ParticipantFailure,typed_failure_evidence_ref:RoleExactFinalizationFailureEvidenceRef,complete_no_effect_and_release_proof_ref:StrongRef<FinalAbortNoEffectAndReleaseProof<Role>>,system_abort_authority:StructuralFinalizationAbortAuthority<registered_reason>}|Administrative{administrative_abort_authorization_ref:StrongRef<AdministrativeAbortAuthorizationRecord<Role>>,complete_no_effect_and_release_proof_ref:StrongRef<FinalAbortNoEffectAndReleaseProof<Role>>},body_digest}`. It may only terminate the already registered attempt at the exact stage and release its exact holds; it cannot commit, create an effect, emit result bytes, widen authority, or disclose the internal reason beyond the selected public reason class. Thus serialization/constraint/fencing failures remain fail-closed without inventing a fresh client mutation permit, while an arbitrary component cannot fabricate a reason-only abort.

`FinalCertificationSelection` is the role-checked closed union `LocalCommit{final_effect_directory_ref:StrongRef<EffectDirectoryRoot>,final_effect_digest,serialization_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalSerializationCertificate>,authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Local>>,resource_and_constraint_hold_commitment,outcome_preparation_body_digest,terminal_write_result:None|Prepared{preparation_ref:StrongRef<TerminalWriteResultPreparation>,required_posture:Local}}|LocalAbort{terminal_abort_authority_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAbortAuthority<Local>>,stable_public_reason_class,complete_no_effect_and_release_proof_ref:StrongRef<FinalAbortNoEffectAndReleaseProof<Local>>}|MetaCommit{global_final_effect_directory_ref:StrongRef<GlobalEffectDirectoryRoot>,global_final_effect_digest,distributed_serialization_certificate_ref:StrongRef<DistributedSerializationCertificate>,participant_prepare_and_constraint_certificate_commitment,global_authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAuthorizationDecisionRecord>,resource_and_constraint_hold_commitment,outcome_preparation_body_digest,terminal_write_result:None|Prepared{preparation_ref:StrongRef<TerminalWriteResultPreparation>,required_posture:Sharded}}|MetaAbort{terminal_abort_authority_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAbortAuthority<Meta>>,stable_public_reason_class,complete_no_effect_and_release_proof_ref:StrongRef<FinalAbortNoEffectAndReleaseProof<Meta>>,known_prepare_and_reservation_release_basis_commitment}`.

`LocalFinalCertificationReserveSpec` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,terminal_admission_fence_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalAdmissionFence>,required_fence_state:Armed,certification_generation,selection:FinalCertificationSelection::LocalCommit|LocalAbort,expected_current_branch_heads,expected_branch_key_epochs,expected_policy_epoch,expected_revocation_index,expected_conflict_index_basis,expected_writer_fence_and_configuration,complete_write_coordinate_set,complete_resource_constraint_and_id_hold_set,authorization_ceiling_digest,expected_reservation_index_absence_or_cancelled_predecessor,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. `GlobalFinalCertificationReserveSpec` is the Meta analogue with `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,terminal_admission_fence_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalAdmissionFence>,admission_generation,certification_generation,selection:FinalCertificationSelection::MetaCommit|MetaAbort,expected_current_branch_heads,expected_branch_key_epochs,expected_policy_epoch,expected_revocation_index,expected_conflict_index_basis,expected_topology_and_meta_configuration,complete_participant_write_coordinate_set,complete_resource_constraint_remote_grant_and_id_hold_set,authorization_ceiling_digest,expected_reservation_index_absence_or_cancelled_predecessor,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`.

`LocalFinalCertificationReservation` is exactly `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,source_spec_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReserveSpec>,registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,terminal_admission_fence_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalAdmissionFence>,admission_generation,certification_generation,selection:FinalCertificationSelection::LocalCommit|LocalAbort,validated_branch_head_and_key_epoch_commitment,validated_policy_revocation_and_authority_commitment,validated_conflict_serialization_commitment,validated_local_configuration_commitment,exclusive_coordinate_head_hold_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalCoordinateHoldRoot>,resource_constraint_id_hold_root_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalObligationHoldRoot>,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,state:Active|CancelledBeforeSignature{cancel_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,release_commitment}|Consumed{terminal_input_identity,assigned_logical_command_seq}}`. `GlobalFinalCertificationReservation` is exactly `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,source_spec_ref:StrongRef<GlobalFinalCertificationReserveSpec>,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,terminal_admission_fence_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalAdmissionFence>,admission_generation,certification_generation,selection:FinalCertificationSelection::MetaCommit|MetaAbort,validated_branch_head_and_key_epoch_commitment,validated_policy_revocation_and_authority_commitment,validated_conflict_serialization_commitment,validated_topology_configuration_commitment,exclusive_coordinate_head_hold_root_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalCoordinateHoldRoot>,resource_constraint_id_remote_hold_root_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalObligationHoldRoot>,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,state:Active|CancelledBeforeSignature{cancel_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef,release_commitment}|Consumed{terminal_input_identity,assigned_global_logical_command_seq}}`. Selection, header, registration, terminal fence, authority decision or abort authority, and every held root must byte-match. Apply reruns every named validation against current state and atomically installs the Active record in the role's `TerminalCertificationReservationRoot` with the two hold roots. A conflicting commit/head change, key retirement/destruction, policy/revocation reclamation, topology/configuration retirement, resource release, or GC must wait, transfer the reservation exactly, or reject; later revocation still fences new work and unsent output but cannot reinterpret this already authorized terminal.

`TerminalCertificationReservationRoot<Role>` is the authenticated map `(registration identity,admission generation,certification generation) -> latest StrongRef<RoleFinalCertificationReservation>` plus a unique-active selector per registration, hold-root coverage bitmap, nonretaining predecessor digest, and body digest. Reserve CASes absence/cancelled predecessor to Active; terminal apply or cancel replaces exactly that value with an immutable Consumed or Cancelled successor. Checkpoint/configuration/backup/restore/GC inventories include the root, every Active record, both hold roots, and all audit/order maintenance evidence. A state transition that can affect a held predicate is generated with a mandatory reservation-conflict check; deleting any such check is a G0 negative fixture.

`LocalFinalCertificationCancelSpec` and `GlobalFinalCertificationCancelSpec` each name the exact Active reservation, current index value, complete hold-release recipe, and `NoTerminalPlanLockShareOrOrderProof`. They are legal only before any `AuditTerminalSigningPlan`, event/resolution lock/share, terminal command, or candidate root exists; apply moves the reservation to `CancelledBeforeSignature` and releases exactly its holds. The admission fence remains Armed, so recovery may construct a new higher certification generation or an explicitly certified abort. Once a signing plan or any lock exists, cancellation is permanently illegal and the selected terminal must finish. The audit plan and admitted-write `AuditTerminalFreezeRef` bind the exact Active reservation and selection. The final Local `CommitCommand`/admitted abort or `GlobalTxnCommand` no longer revalidates mutable heads/key/policy/revocation/conflict/resource bases; it verifies the Active reservation, semantic prepared closure, and exact selected terminal, then atomically moves the reservation to `Consumed{terminal_input_identity,assigned_role_order}` and the admission fence to `TerminalOrdered|Released`. Unrelated commands may interleave; commands touching a reserved basis cannot. Crash/reconfiguration/checkpoint floors retain and transfer every Active reservation and hold.

`NoTerminalPlanLockShareOrOrderProof` is the pre-cancellation authority-local certificate `{SameGroupCertificateHeader,role:Local|Meta,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReservation|GlobalFinalCertificationReservation>,required_state:Active,selection_digest,terminal_attempt_index_absence_path,audit_event_and_resolution_signer_journal_absence_or_release_fence_vector,prepared_terminal_command_absence_path,prepared_order_attempt_root_digest,all_matching_order_attempt_dispositions:None|RetiredStale[],semantic_raft_log_nonorder_proof:{term,configuration_ref,read_barrier_index,commit_index,applied_index,closed_semantic_order},candidate_root_and_audit_visibility_pipeline_absence_proof,quiescence_barrier_id,checker_version,certificate_attempt_plan_ref,certificate_signer_lock_refs,quorum_signatures}`. It is built from one linearizable barrier, contains no future cancel command/result, and is invalid if any terminal semantic plan, signing attempt, terminal command, Ordered supplement, candidate, or external audit CAS exists. Generic certificate-plan/lock/share bytes used to sign this proof are a distinct certificate kind and do not count as terminal-audit artifacts.

Order/HLC freshness is deliberately **outside** stable semantic prepared ownership and signed audit bytes. `LocalOrderSubject` is `Terminal{final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReservation>,required_state:Active,selected_terminal_digest}|Control{typed_payload_ref:StrongRef<SequenceNeutralSpec>,spec_body_digest,expected_state_conditions_digest,terminal_audit_freeze_commitment}`; `MetaOrderSubject` is the corresponding `Terminal{final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalFinalCertificationReservation>,...}|Control{typed_payload_ref:StrongRef<GlobalSequenceNeutralSpec>,...}`. After the audit triple is prepared when required—or again after any harmless intervening semantic role command—the proposer builds `LocalOrderAttemptInput {order_attempt_id,subject:LocalOrderSubject,semantic_prepared_ownership_id,hlc_proposal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<LocalHlcProposalEvidence>,expected_next_logical_command_seq,expected_next_commit_seq:NotApplicable|Commit{value},configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,writer_fence_epoch,body_digest}` or `MetaOrderAttemptInput {order_attempt_id,subject:MetaOrderSubject,semantic_prepared_ownership_id,hlc_proposal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<MetaHlcProposalEvidence>,expected_next_global_logical_command_seq,expected_next_global_commit_seq:NotApplicable|Commit{value},configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,body_digest}`. Commit applicability is derived from the immutable selected terminal/control kind; abort/read/control cannot smuggle a commit-sequence assignment. `LocalOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate` and `MetaOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate` use the universal signer protocol, name the exact input/semantic owner/base-closure digest, and cover that already-built supplement closure while excluding themselves/future command. Their plan/lock/share/publication maintenance entries do not change the expected semantic counters. The role-specific `PreparedOrderAttemptRoot<Role>` is `{generation,entries[order_attempt_id -> Available{input_ref,availability_certificate_ref,maintenance_install_index}|Ordered{role_order,commit_order:None|Some,semantic_command_identity,semantic_raft_index}|RetiredStale{retirement_evidence_ref:StrongRef<OrderAttemptRetirementEvidence>,successor_attempt_id}],subject_to_latest_attempt_map,body_digest}`. `OrderAttemptRetirementEvidence` proves at a linearizable read barrier that the attempt was never a semantic log entry/candidate, identifies the semantic counter drift that made it stale, and retains its plan/certificate closure. Only nonsemantic maintenance transitions install or retire entries; semantic apply atomically selects Ordered. Checked counter mismatch, duplicate subject, self-staleness, retirement-after-order, and command/supplement substitution fail closed.

The command strongly names exactly one role-matching order-attempt input and availability certificate. Voters derive the next sequence/HLC only from that input and current persisted HLC and require its subject, semantic owner, spec/reservation, and any audit-freeze commitment to match. If its observation ages out or expected sequence loses a race, the supplement can be retired with a linearizable nonorder proof and a fresh order attempt can be certified without changing semantic ownership or any reservation/audit plan/lock/share/terminal bytes. Every failed supplement remains rooted until the prepared/order-attempt floor proves nonorder. A terminal reservation CAS, or a control spec's exact idempotency/expected-state CAS, admits at most one ordered semantic command across all supplements. Crash tests cover stale evidence before/during signing, intervening commands, leader/configuration change, multiple fresh supplements, and exactly one semantic application.

`ResultDispositionRef` is the exact compact union `PendingIndependent{retention_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Meta>>}|ReleasedIndependent{retention_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Meta>>}|ActiveIndependent{retention_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Meta>>}|Acknowledged{retention_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Meta>>,ack_ref:StrongRef<ResultAckRecord>}|ReleasedUnacked{retention_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord<Meta>>,release_ref:StrongRef<ResultReleaseRecord>}` and must be backed by `ResultDeliveryDispositionProof`. `WorkspaceReleaseEvidenceRef` is `MetaLocal{release_record_ref:StrongRef<WorkspaceOverlayReleaseRecord>}|ShardRemote{release_ack_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardWorkspaceReleaseAck>}`. `GlobalAttemptCancelSpec` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,expected_attempt_state:Active,expected_absent_admission_generation,expected_statement_frontier,expected_delivery_detachment_frontier,expected_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,expected_cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,expected_cumulative_logical_effect_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeTxnEffectRoot>,all_open_statement_abort_refs[StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>],result_disposition_proof_refs[StrongRef<ResultDeliveryDispositionProof>],workspace_release_evidence_refs[WorkspaceReleaseEvidenceRef],terminal_abort_authority_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAbortAuthority<Meta>>,required_lifecycle_operation:RequestTerminalAbort{stage:Active},stable_public_reason_class,no_committed_graph_effect_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoCommittedGraphEffectProof>,resource_release_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Meta>>],operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,terminal_audit_gate:TerminalAuditGate,expected_conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity}`. Its full header, terminal-abort authority, registration, and audit admission byte-match; a Claimed gate is the exact terminal failure/abandonment event and resolution for that operation ticket, while NotRequired carries its validated proof. `GlobalControlCommand(kind=CancelActiveAttempt)` is valid only before admission, after every Open statement has reached `Failed|Abandoned`, and after every published result is detached with one valid disposition proof. It proves there can be no shard prepare under this generation, but does not pretend staged workspace/audit/conflict/resource state never existed and does not release any independent result owner; exact overlay/grant release acknowledgements discharge only their own obligations. Apply installs terminal `Aborted` with `AppliedAbortRef::MetaControl`, closes its conflict vertex, and releases only the named attempt-owned closures. It is the deterministic recovery path for an authenticated client/admin request or separately registered structural recovery authority before admission; client death, clock timeout, suspicion, or a generic authorization decision alone is not a transition.

`MetaReadAuditEvidence` is the closed union `TerminalAttempt{operation:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired}|Statement{operation:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,statement:StatementAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired}`. The exact `GlobalReadAuthorizationDecisionRecord` is the Meta result-release schema defined with the authorization records below; its `ReadAccessAdmissionRecord::MetaTerminal|MetaStatement` exists before routing or observation, while the Global release object is created only after the complete certified shard release-decision set, workspace/witness commitment, realized answer contract, and canonical result digest exist. Scope, workspace generation, permit kind/projection, audit-evidence tag, and every shard access/release pair must agree. The release gate names only the terminal operation ticket for MetaTerminal or the distinct statement ticket for MetaStatement, while both claims remain bound; its complete shard commitment cannot reinterpret one shard decision as Meta-local.

`GlobalReadCloseSpec` is `{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,mode:AutocommitResult{read_routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ReadParticipantRoutingCertificate>,global_read_authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<GlobalReadAuthorizationDecisionRecord>,buffered_result_manifest_ref:StrongRef<BufferedResultManifest>,initial_retention_recipe:ResultInitialRetentionRecipe<Meta>,canonical_result_digest,result_answer_contract}|ExplicitClose{expected_published_statement_frontier,expected_delivery_detachment_frontier,complete_publication_chain_digest,result_disposition_proof_refs[StrongRef<ResultDeliveryDispositionProof>],workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,zero_cumulative_write_effect_proof_ref:StrongRef<ZeroCumulativeWriteEffectProof>,no_open_statement_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoOpenStatementProof>},final_read_witness_refs[GeneratedCertifiedRemoteReadWitnessRef],distributed_serialization_certificate_ref:StrongRef<DistributedSerializationCertificate>,expected_conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity,terminal_audit_gate}`. Autocommit requires `BufferedResultOwner::TerminalRead`; explicit close requires every statement publication audit-visible and every result detached into its independent index. Its event/resolution/gate are the exact terminal extension of the claimed operation ticket. `GlobalControlCommand(kind=ReadClose)` requires `operation_class=ReadOnly`, installs ReadClosed, and advances GlobalLogicalCommandSeq/HLC but not GlobalCommitSeq. Autocommit uses `AtomicProtocolDetach` to create `ResultDeliveryOwner::TerminalRead`, semantic charge transfer/Protocol escrow, one pending/released/legal-inline-active retention record, and only `DetachedToProtocolRetention{delivery_id,detachment_commitment}` semantically. Audit visibility releases a pending record; independent activation creates its fresh lease. The later `GlobalTerminalCompletionSpec` validates the frozen public projection after the internal read-close descriptor is known. Explicit mode returns COMMIT success after completion without waiting for activation or ACK. The general `GlobalControlCommand` is `{command_version,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,kind,typed_payload_ref:StrongRef<GlobalSequenceNeutralSpec>,expected_state_conditions[],order_attempt_input_ref:StrongRef<MetaOrderAttemptInput>,order_attempt_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<MetaOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate>,authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Meta>>,meta_pre_command_base_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<MetaPreCommandBaseAvailabilityCertificate>}`. Its header, authorization decision, typed spec, MetaOrderSubject::Control, expected conditions, final reservation or lifecycle decision where applicable, and audit-freeze commitment must byte-match one complete Meta authority binding. The spec wrapper is the sole terminal audit gate; the command has no duplicate field. Every payload-bearing kind has exact stable pre-command closure coverage, while order/HLC evidence lives only in the replaceable supplement.

`GlobalDerivedGenerationActivationSpec` is `{derived_identity_ref,manifest_ref:StrongRef<DistributedDerivedGenerationManifest>,expected_registry_generation,expected_prior_active_generation?,expected_topology_ref,exact_global_frontier,answer_contract,old_generation_transition:KeepActiveUntilSwap|RetireAfterFloor,terminal_audit_gate}`; apply verifies a complete one-frontier component set, atomically selects it Active, and moves the old generation to Retiring without a query-visible gap. W12 `Fork|Grant|Rotate` are the operation tags inside the exact `GlobalBranchKeyDistributionPlanSpec`; `GlobalBranchKeyManifestActivationSpec` is the only activation tag. There is no `GlobalBranchKeyBundleSpec` alias and no path that lets a shard stage against the future manifest. Meta authority changes only after the complete staged-availability certificate set exists; shards become usable only after the later typed activation projection.

`GlobalSequenceNeutralSpec` is the generated Meta **semantic-input** union described by `command_contracts.toml`; it has no wildcard arm and no independently edited prose allowlist. Its closed v1 family projection contains: guarded recovery/bootstrap; begin reservation and terminalization; attempt, ownership/reattachment/expiry, statement, prepare, read-close, cancellation, outcome, and semantic result-detachment creation; checkpoints, floors, history cuts, configuration and format transition; remote retention and configuration trust; Local-to-Sharded activation, capability migration, legacy-Local transfer and retirement; branch, merge, allocation, valid-time, constraint, resource, and escrow authority; policy, revocation, security binding, time authority, privacy continuity, and DP; audit ticket admission, freeze, signing-plan/attempt, terminal, recovery, and completeness; bulk-load staging; derived-build and schema activation; key distribution/activation/destruction; GC semantic authorization; backup/restore semantic lifecycle; and shard reconfiguration/topology copy-cutover-retire. Every family expands to one row per exact inner tag and one concrete result/applied-record schema. Ordinary catalog, schema, constraint-definition, and index-definition changes appear only as transaction-carried `SchemaOp`s; `CatalogMutationSpec`, `SchemaMutationSpec`, `IndexDefinitionSpec`, and `IndexActivationSpec` are not command types. `AuditExternalResolutionBatch`/receipt are pre-order evidence and `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec` is a Protocol-plane maintenance input, never a `GlobalSequenceNeutralSpec`. DP uses the exact §12.7 semantic transition tags and has no `RecordCharge` or `RecordDelivery` arm.

`MetaMaintenanceCommand` is the generated nonsemantic quorum-durable union over Protocol/PreparedOwnership/Consensus slots. Its exact v1 projection includes `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Meta>`, result activation/ACK/release/expiry/resume, cursor and artifact-output transport lifecycle, prepared-owner/receipt recovery, Raft hard-state/snapshot maintenance, and physical progress/receipt aggregation that changes no semantic authority. Each row declares `sequence_effects=None`, exact maintenance-log ordering and durability, security/audit evidence, protocol slots, snapshot/backup/restore/GC coverage, and whether a public reply is permitted. There is an analogous `LocalMaintenanceCommand`; Shard has only its role-valid nonsemantic subset. Moving an operation between semantic and maintenance planes is a durable-format major change, not a handler implementation choice.

The generated Meta family projections are tested against all feature-state machines. In particular, a compiled W12 build must contain semantic rows for `GlobalBeginReservationSpec`, `GlobalBeginTerminalSpec`, `GlobalAttemptRegistrationSpec`, `TxnOwnershipTransitionSpec<Meta>`, `TxnOwnershipExpiryAbortSpec<Meta>`, `GlobalStatementRegistrationSpec`, `GlobalStatementPublicationSpec`, `GlobalPrepareAdmissionSpec`, `GlobalReadCloseSpec`, `GlobalDecisionCertificatePublishSpec`, `GlobalDeltaPublishSpec`, `GlobalClosedAttemptFloorInstallSpec`, `GlobalCheckpointInstallSpec`, `MetaInitialFloorInstallSpec`, `MetaConfigurationTransitionSpec`, `FormatTransitionSpec<Meta>`, `GlobalEscrowTransferPrepareSpec`, `GlobalEscrowTransferActivateSpec`, `PrivacyContinuityMirrorSpec<Meta>`, the exact Global DP semantic transitions, `AuditTerminalPlanAbandonSpec<Meta>`, `BulkLoadTransitionSpec<Meta>`, `DerivedBuildTransitionSpec<Meta>`, and every exact semantic backup/restore/GC/key/topology transition. Its maintenance projection must separately contain `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Meta>`, every result activation/ACK/release/expiry/resume tag, all protocol-only artifact/cursor/replay delivery tags, and every required prepared/consensus/physical maintenance tag. `AuditTicketResolutionBatchSpec` has no row in either union. Missing, duplicated, wrong-plane, or extra rows fail G0 before the feature can be advertised.

`PublicPostconditionProgressProjection` is `Opaque{progress_commitment:OpaquePublicCommitment<PostconditionProgress>}|Disclosed{required_public_stage_classes[],satisfied_public_stage_classes[],progress_commitment,receipt:PortablePublicDisclosureReceipt}`; its disclosed arrays use stable public classes, not internal bit positions. `PublicTxnStatusProjection` is the sole externally serializable status union and is exactly `BeginStaging{posture,staging_state_token:PublicStateToken<TxnStaging>}|NeverRegistered{posture,public_reason:PublicReasonProjection,original_response_commitment}|InProgress{posture,privacy_safe_phase,progress_state_token:PublicStateToken<TxnProgress>}|FinalizingOpaque{posture,stage_progress_commitment:OpaquePublicCommitment<TerminalStageProgress>}|TerminalVisiblePendingPostconditions{posture,terminal:PublicTerminalProgressProjection,postconditions:PublicPostconditionProgressProjection}|TerminalReady{public_outcome:TerminalPublicOutcome,result_delivery:None|AwaitingActivation{management_handle:PublicHandle<PendingOutputManagement>,state_token:PublicStateToken<ResultDelivery>}|Active{resume_capability},read_your_writes:NotRequested|Issued{capability:ReadYourWritesCapability},audit_release:AuditReleaseEvidence}|TokenNotYetValid|TokenExpired|TimeAuthorityUnavailable|NotVisibleOrUnknown`. Before the exact terminal audit visibility advance, only `FinalizingOpaque` is legal; it reveals no Commit/Abort/ReadClose, marker, sequence, effect, reason, participant, stage cardinality, or result fact. At or after release, the visible-pending arm may expose only the frozen preauthorized projection while semantic postconditions finish. TerminalReady may precede output activation but never exposes bytes or a Resume capability until Active. `SemanticTerminalDescriptor`, required/satisfied internal bitmaps, statement/delivery frontiers, workspace generations, proof/compaction source, and audit/logical positions are forbidden wire fields. Detailed and compacted paths emit byte-identical public projections, disclosure receipts, and prebuilt capabilities; presentation fails uniformly if current authority no longer authorizes every disclosed field rather than widening, silently redacting, or changing the original response.

`PublicMergeStatusProjection` is the sole externally serializable merge-status union: `InFlight{posture,merge_handle:PublicMergeHandle,progress_state_token:PublicStateToken<MergeProgress>,opaque_progress_commitment}|Committed{posture,merge_handle,target:WireGraphReferenceSpec,target_marker:PublicMarkerProjection,final_effect:PublicEffectProjection,publication:PublicPublicationProjection,public_response_commitment}|Rejected{posture,merge_handle,public_reason:PublicReasonProjection,public_response_commitment}|TokenNotYetValid|TokenExpired|TimeAuthorityUnavailable|NotVisibleOrUnknown`. Source heads, merge bases, target basis, conflict cardinality, internal marker/logical/Raft positions, participants, proof storage, and compaction source are never public fields. `MergePublicProjectionBundle<Role>` freezes the Committed or Rejected bytes before semantic apply; detailed and compacted lookup return those exact bytes. A merge is not a transaction attempt and never manufactures or unconditionally returns a transaction-outcome capability; any independently requested transaction that consumes a merge result has its own BEGIN lineage.

`GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord` is the sequence-neutral W12 terminal compare contract `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,attempt_key:{global_txn_id,registration_generation},txn_token,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,expected_registered_outcome_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord>,admission_generation,routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ParticipantRoutingCertificate>,global_authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAuthorizationDecisionRecord>,sorted_resource_transition_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Meta>>],operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,transaction_audit_basis_commitment,constraint_and_participant_basis_commitment,terminal_domain:CommitIfCertifiedElseAbort{global_final_effect_digest,allowed_stable_abort_reason_classes},terminal_write_result:None|Prepared{preparation_ref:StrongRef<TerminalWriteResultPreparation>,required_posture:Sharded},body_digest}`. The header and every typed authority/resource/audit/routing input byte-match one complete Meta binding; no catch-all `authority/resource/audit_basis_commitment` may substitute for the registered refs. It names neither the future admission spec/outcome nor an applied record. Admission verifies the expected `Registered` predecessor and installs `PrepareAdmitted`; the later global transaction command must present the same record and transition only that exact admission generation to one allowed terminal. The Sharded preparation is built only after final effect fixed point/rebase and before this record; admission, fragments, command, and completion all byte-match its final-effect/result/time-basis tuple.

`SemanticTerminalDescriptor` is the **internal-only**, nonretaining inline posture union `Local{terminal:Write{marker_identity:WeakMarkerIdentity,applied_logical_command_seq,applied_commit_seq,final_effect_digest}|Abort{reason_class,terminal_logical_prefix}|ReadClose{applied_logical_command_seq,publication_chain_digest,final_result_digest?}}|Sharded{terminal:Write{marker_identity:WeakMarkerIdentity,applied_global_logical_seq,applied_global_commit_seq,final_effect_digest}|Abort{reason_class,terminal_global_prefix}|ReadClose{applied_global_logical_seq,publication_chain_digest,final_result_digest?}}`. `TxnCompletionState` is the internal lifecycle state machine `InProgress{phase:BeginStaging|Registered|StatementOpen|PrepareAdmitted}|TerminalPending{semantic_outcome:SemanticTerminalDescriptor,required_postcondition_bitmap,satisfied_postcondition_bitmap}|TerminalReady{public_outcome:TerminalPublicOutcome}`; descriptor and outcome posture must match the owning record, but no serializer may substitute the descriptor for a public projection. Local writes require durability, terminal audit visibility, contiguous Local delta publication, result disposition, and resource release bits. Sharded writes additionally require decision publication and every admitted shard's post-visibility acknowledgement; abort/read-close use their exact role-specific bitsets. `GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord` is the canonical registered-attempt value
