`{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,global_txn_id,registration_generation,txn_token,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord>?,state:Registered{registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,published_statement_frontier,delivery_terminal_frontier,latest_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,cumulative_logical_effect_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeTxnEffectRoot>,statement_index_digest}|PrepareAdmitted{admission_spec_ref:StrongRef<GlobalPrepareAdmissionSpec>,terminal_admission_fence_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalAdmissionFence>,admission_generation,published_statement_frontier,delivery_terminal_frontier,workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,cumulative_read_witness_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeReadWitnessRoot>,cumulative_logical_effect_root_ref:StrongRef<CumulativeTxnEffectRoot>,operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,audit_basis_commitment,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef}|CommittedPendingPostconditions{marker_ref:StrongMarkerRef,final_effect_digest,global_txn_command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnCommand>,applied_global_logical_seq,applied_global_commit_seq}|Committed{marker_ref:StrongMarkerRef,final_effect_digest,applied_global_logical_seq,applied_global_commit_seq,global_delta_publish_command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlCommand>,delta_publish_applied_prefix,published_delta_frontier,public_outcome:TerminalPublicOutcome::Sharded}|Aborted{reason_class,applied_abort_ref:AppliedAbortRef,public_outcome:TerminalPublicOutcome::Sharded}|ReadClosed{read_close_spec_ref:StrongRef<GlobalReadCloseSpec>,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,publication_chain_digest,final_result_digest?,public_outcome:TerminalPublicOutcome::Sharded},audit_state:NotRequired{evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence}|ClaimedPending{ticket_digest,claim_identity}|TerminalGateCommitted{ticket_digest,terminal_gate_commitment,candidate_pipeline_order},completion_state:TxnCompletionState,minimum_lookup_retention_floor}`. The header is the sole namespace/incarnation/service/tenant/principal/ceiling/policy/revocation source, and every successor is equal or registered-narrower. Detailed terminal records retain the full header because they own markers, public outcomes, and lifecycle refs; only the final payload-free expiry tombstone may use `TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Meta>`. Its predecessor is the immediately prior attempt-lifecycle value; registration creates the first Registered value and thereby roots workspace generation zero, and every statement publication atomically constructs the immediately succeeding Registered value with all frontiers/roots advanced and makes GlobalOutcomeDirectoryValue::W12 point to it. No semantic state strongly owns a protocol-plane `AuditOutcomeRecord`. A committed write is semantically decided at CommittedPendingPostconditions but ordinary success and ReadYourWritesCapability are unavailable until all typed postconditions yield TerminalReady and the current protocol state proves its terminal gate released; only the matching terminal transition resolves the operation claim.

`GlobalAttemptIndex` maps only an actually registered `(global_txn_id,registration_generation)` to `Active|PrepareAdmitted|Terminalizing{semantic_terminal_kind,missing_postcondition_bitmap}|Terminal`; `AttemptFamilyState` enforces one active registered generation and exact successor rules. A pre-registration `NeverRegistered` outcome has no attempt identity and therefore exists only in the begin-key/outcome-capability directories and their permanent tombstones; no attempt-index row or fabricated attempt burn is legal. `GlobalStatementIndex` maps `(global_txn_id,registration_generation,statement_seq)` and the statement idempotency key to `Open|Published|Failed|Abandoned`, including the immutable result manifest, workspace successor, delivery state, and statement-ticket terminal evidence where applicable. `GlobalTxnOutcomeIndex` maps the scoped outcome token to the latest completion state or the separate begin/never-registered directory value. Family, attempt, statement, begin-key, token, audit-ticket, conflict, workspace, and publication/delivery-frontier entries transition atomically in the command that owns them. Matching retries return the original value; conflicting bodies or owners fail. Capability-safe status resolves the role-tagged directory/summary proof rather than a local-only outcome and reports `TerminalPending` instead of fabricating success while any required postcondition remains incomplete.

`GlobalCheckpointInstallSpec` is `{basis_global_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_global_logical_command_seq,basis_global_commit_seq,basis_payload_digest:WeakDigest,basis_projection_digest,checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<GlobalRecoveryCheckpoint>,checkpoint_state_vector_digest,paired_meta_config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<MetaConfigPayloadFloor>?,retention_cut_body_ref:StrongRef<RetentionCutBody>?,reset_field_tags[],terminal_audit_gate}`. Genesis alone uses no cut body and produces `CheckpointInstalled`; an operational replacement requires the body and paired floor, atomically installs the matching provisional cut, and preserves `Operational`. The single exact `ShardOperationalBootstrapBarrier` schema is the origin-tagged, strongly retaining definition in the activation/genesis contract above; no digest-only alternate encoding exists. `MetaInitialFloorInstallSpec` is `{checkpoint_installed_global_state:WeakStateIdentity,checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<GlobalRecoveryCheckpoint>,checkpoint_state_vector_digest,initial_meta_config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<MetaConfigPayloadFloor>,initial_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,sorted_active_shard_barrier_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardOperationalBootstrapBarrier>],expected_global_logical_command_seq,terminal_audit_gate}` and is the sole fresh `CheckpointInstalled -> Operational` transition. The barrier set equals every and only `Active` topology entry, all at one global prefix; `Joining` shards are unroutable. `GlobalHistoryCutActivationSpec` is `{provisional_cut_ref:StrongRef<ProvisionalRetentionCutSet>,checkpoint_install_record_ref:StrongGlobalCommandRef,expected_retention_map_basis:WeakStateIdentity,verified_contiguous_suffix_start,terminal_audit_gate}` and only strengthens that exact provisional set to `ActivatedRetentionCutSet`.

Meta-owned directories change only through typed CAS controls. `PublishShardCheckpointCertificateSpec` is `{checkpoint_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardCheckpointCertificate>,expected_topology_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,expected_shard_configuration_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,expected_prior_directory_value:WeakDigest?,required_remote_grant_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>,terminal_audit_gate}`; apply byte-matches shard/config/topology/prefix/history interval and installs the exact CRSR. `PublishShardAppliedAckSpec` similarly binds `{ack_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardAppliedAck>,prepare_key,decision_record_identity,decision_publish_record_identity,expected_topology_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,expected_shard_configuration_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,expected_prior_directory_value:WeakDigest?,required_remote_grant_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>,terminal_audit_gate}` and is required for commit and abort acknowledgements.

`GlobalTerminalCompletionSpec` is the sole W12 postcondition transition `{pending_outcome_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord>,semantic_outcome:SemanticTerminalDescriptor::Sharded,terminal_public_projection_bundle_ref:StrongRef<TerminalPublicProjectionBundle<Meta>>,prebuilt_read_your_writes_issuance_ref:None|Some{StrongRef<ReadYourWritesIssuanceBundle<Meta>>},expected_required_postcondition_bitmap,observed_satisfied_postcondition_bitmap,decision_publication_evidence:Committed{decision_publish_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>}|NoDecisionApplicable{terminal_kind:ReadClosed},exact_sorted_post_visibility_shard_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardAppliedAck>],shard_ack_coverage_commitment,delta_completion:Committed{global_delta_batch_ref:StrongRef<GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch>,global_delta_publish_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,published_frontier_proof_ref:StrongRef<GlobalDeltaFrontierProof>}|NoDeltaApplicable{terminal_kind:Aborted|ReadClosed,proof_ref:StrongRef<NoDeltaProof>},configured_durability_evidence_ref:StrongRef<GlobalDurabilityEvidence>,result_disposition:NoTerminalResult|ReadTerminalResult{delivery_disposition_proof_ref:StrongRef<ResultDeliveryDispositionProof>}|AutocommitWriteReturning{preparation_ref:StrongRef<TerminalWriteResultPreparation>,required_posture:Sharded,initial_retention_recipe:ResultInitialRetentionRecipe<Meta>},resource_release_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Meta>>],terminal_visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,expected_conflict_overlap_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConflictOverlapCloseProof>,terminal_audit_freeze:AuditFreezeField::Required<MetaControl>,terminal_audit_gate}`. The portable visibility certificate, not a Protocol-plane `AuditOutcomeRecord` edge, proves that the terminal candidate and audit release are public. Every evidence arm and required bit is generated from terminal kind, participants, result class, and durability policy; arrays are canonical bijections. Apply CASes the exact pending predecessor, monotonically adds only verified bits, validates rather than manufactures the frozen public/capability artifacts, constructs the succeeding outcome, and selects `TerminalReady` only when required equals satisfied.

The autocommit-write arm byte-matches the preparation retained by `GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord` and is legal only for committed WriteReturning. Its `AtomicProtocolDetach` publication creates `ResultDeliveryOwner::AutocommitWriteTerminal`, charge transfer/Protocol escrow, a PendingVisibility, ReleasedAwaitingActivation, or legal inline ActiveDeliverable record/index leaf, and only `{delivery_id,detachment_commitment}` in the semantic frontier. Independent Protocol retention—not activation or client ACK—is the result postcondition. Matching retry returns the same applied record and owner commitments; a different bitmap, participant set, frontier, public bundle, result arm, or predecessor fails. ReadYourWritesCapability and ordinary terminal success remain unavailable until TerminalReady and its exact audit release; output bytes remain unavailable until the result owner is ActiveDeliverable. No prose “mirror” is sufficient evidence.

Attempt-history compaction is cumulative and has three separately ordered phases; deterministic apply never manufactures a quorum signature. `AttemptLookupKey` is the closed tag `ByAttempt{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,global_txn_id,registration_generation}|ByOutcomeCapability{capability_domain_commitment,scoped_token_digest}`. `PublicNeverRegisteredOutcome` is the frozen `{posture,public_reason:PublicReasonProjection,original_response_commitment}` copied from `NeverRegisteredPublicProjectionBundle`. `TerminalAttemptSummaryLeaf` is `NeverRegistered{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,outcome_capability_key:AttemptLookupKey::ByOutcomeCapability,internal_reason_class,public_outcome:PublicNeverRegisteredOutcome,audit_terminal_commitment,internal_staging_generation,internal_terminal_global_prefix}|Terminal{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,attempt_key:AttemptLookupKey::ByAttempt,outcome_capability_key:AttemptLookupKey::ByOutcomeCapability,operation_class,internal_terminal_global_prefix,public_outcome:TerminalPublicOutcome,audit_terminal_commitment}`. The summary retains protected frozen response bytes and therefore must retain the full binding; the later expiry rewrite alone may replace it in lookup indexes with a payload-free `TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Meta>` tombstone. A NeverRegistered leaf has no optional or synthetic attempt identity: absence of registration is part of the tag's meaning. Both public outcomes are copied byte-for-byte from their detailed bundles and include the original response commitment; neither leaf has a retaining edge to audit detail. Internal reason/generation/prefix fields are never serialized by the public status projector. `TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot` is a canonical authenticated persistent map from keyed lookup hashes to leaves plus `{summary_generation,covered_closed_prefix,leaf_count,root_digest}`. Its membership proof is consumed server-side after exact capability validation; the client receives only the frozen public projection plus a blinded proof projection, never the full leaf/path containing attempt keys, prefixes, or authority bindings. A successor root must equal the prior root plus the command's sorted insertions, proven by `PersistentMapExtensionProof`; it may structurally share map nodes but contains no `StrongRef` to a predecessor summary root, audit detail, or heavy attempt evidence, and an existing key can only be an identical idempotent leaf.

`NeverRegisteredEvidence` is `{begin_reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalBeginReservationRecord>,terminal_record_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredTerminalRecord>,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,outcome_capability_key:AttemptLookupKey::ByOutcomeCapability,staging_generation,authenticated_detailed_value_proofs:{global_begin_idempotency_index:NeverRegisteredDetailed,global_outcome_directory:NeverRegisteredDetailed},no_reservation_binding_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoReservationBindingProof>,audit_terminal_commitment,absence_of_exported_registration_or_shard_import_commitment}`. `NoReservationBindingProof` is generated from the current begin-index terminal leaf, the reservation-key uniqueness proof, the Meta prepared/certificate/export roots, and the registered inverse-reference index for reservation identities; it proves that this reservation was never consumed by registration or exported, without inventing an attempt/family key. `NeverRegisteredFloorSpec` is `{basis_global_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_global_prefix,basis_begin_index_digest,basis_outcome_directory_digest,basis_prepared_root_digest,basis_certificate_ledger_digest,basis_reservation_inverse_index_digest,sorted_evidence_refs[StrongRef<NeverRegisteredEvidence>],expected_prior_summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,candidate_summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,extension_proof_ref:StrongRef<PersistentMapExtensionProof>,exact_detailed_to_compacted_begin_and_directory_rewrite_commitment,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply revalidates every detailed value and nonbinding proof against current roots, inserts the exact never-registered summary leaves, atomically changes begin-index and outcome-directory entries from Detailed to Compacted while preserving their same replay ref, and creates compact `NeverRegisteredFloorRecord {assigned_global_logical_command_seq,source_command_digest:WeakDigest,rewritten_lookup_key_commitment,summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,applied_global_prefix}` with no retaining edge to its command/spec/evidence. It creates, burns, or rewrites no attempt/family/conflict entry and can never change the previously returned reason or response bytes.

`GlobalOutcomeExpirySpec` is a separate ordered control `{capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,leaf_key_digest,expected_begin_index_entry:BoundCompacted|NeverRegisteredCompacted,expected_outcome_directory_entry:CompactedTerminal|NeverRegisteredCompacted,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,expected_verifier_key_and_profile_floors,expected_begin_retry_and_lookup_floors,expected_retention_cut_ref:StrongRef<ActivatedHistoryCut>,permanent_begin_tombstone_body,permanent_outcome_tombstone_body,terminal_audit_freeze:AuditFreezeField::Required<MetaControl>,terminal_audit_gate}`. The evidence must classify the replay object's exact window `Expired` for this action, the two compact leaves and replay identity must agree, and every promised retry/lookup floor must be closed. Apply atomically changes both leaves to `ExpiredTombstone` values containing the same scoped-key/token/capability commitments and expiry-evidence digest, drops the replay edge only in that transition, and permanently prevents key/token reuse. A valid old capability thereafter returns `TokenExpired`; an invalid or unauthorized input remains `NotVisibleOrUnknown`. Expiry does not remove the internal terminal summary leaf or fabricate an attempt identity.

`AttemptDeltaCompletionEvidence` is `Committed{global_delta_batch_ref:StrongRef<GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch>,global_delta_publish_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,published_frontier_at_or_beyond_commit_proof_ref:StrongRef<GlobalDeltaFrontierProof>}|NoDeltaApplicable{terminal_kind:Aborted|ReadClosed,proof_digest}`. `AttemptCompactionEvidenceRef` is the exact closed union `MetaDecision{record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>}|MetaConstraintRelease{record_ref:StrongRef<ConstraintReleaseRecord>}|MetaAuditTerminal{visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,audit_outcome_identity_and_commitment:WeakDigest}|MetaResultDisposition{disposition:ResultDispositionRef}|MetaWorkspaceRelease{evidence:WorkspaceReleaseEvidenceRef}|MetaBackupRelease{record_ref:StrongRef<BackupReleaseRecord>}|MetaReplayRetention{record_ref:StrongRef<ReplayRetentionRecord>}|MetaRemoteRelease{record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionReleaseTombstone>}|ShardPostVisibility{ack_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardAppliedAck>}|ShardConstraintRelease{ack_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardConstraintReleaseAck>}|ShardWorkspaceRelease{ack_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardWorkspaceReleaseAck>}`. `IndependentAttemptObligationRef` is `Result{retention_ref:StrongRef<ResultIndependentRetentionRecord>}|Backup{retention_ref:StrongRef<BackupIndependentRetentionRecord>}|Replay{retention_ref:StrongRef<ReplayRetentionRecord>}|RemoteGrant{grant_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>}|CapabilityFloor{floor_ref:StrongRef<CapabilityLookupFloor>}`. `ClosedAttemptEvidenceBundle` is the heavy pre-order closure `{sorted_terminal_ready_outcome_refs[StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord>],begin_idempotency_and_public_status_proof_refs[StrongRef<BeginPublicStatusProof>],sorted_evidence_refs[AttemptCompactionEvidenceRef],sorted_delta_completion_evidence[AttemptDeltaCompletionEvidence],statement_terminal_summary_proof_refs[StrongRef<StatementTerminalSummaryProof>],result_delivery_disposition_proof_refs[StrongRef<ResultDeliveryDispositionProof>],workspace_generation_and_local_overlay_release_proof_refs[StrongRef<WorkspaceReleaseProof>],workspace_remote_grant_release_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardWorkspaceReleaseAck>],overlap_close_proof_refs[StrongRef<ConflictOverlapCloseProof>],complete_selected_attempt_and_public_outcome_commitment}`. `ClosedAttemptCompactionSpec` is `{compaction_id,basis_global_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_global_prefix,basis_attempt_index_digest,basis_begin_index_digest,basis_outcome_directory_digest,basis_conflict_index_digest,basis_authoritative_floor_digest,basis_pending_root_digest,selected_terminal_attempt_keys[],evidence_bundle_ref:StrongRef<ClosedAttemptEvidenceBundle>,expected_prior_summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,candidate_summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,extension_proof_ref:StrongRef<PersistentMapExtensionProof>,retained_active_attempt_commitment,still_live_independent_obligation_refs[IndependentAttemptObligationRef],terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply verifies every selected attempt is `TerminalReady` and audit-visible and that its candidate leaf exactly reproduces the detailed `TerminalPublicOutcome`; decision/certificate publication, every admitted shard post-visibility acknowledgement, committed Global-delta publication/frontier, and applicable abort/read-close no-delta proof must be complete. Every statement is `Published|Failed|Abandoned`; every statement output is NoResult/NotApplicableTerminal or, together with every terminal/autocommit result, Acknowledged, ReleasedUnacked, or independently retained; every workspace overlay and remote grant has exact release evidence or a listed independent owner; overlap is closed; and no untransferred backup/replay/retention/capability obligation remains. Apply creates unsigned compact `ClosedAttemptCompactionFloorRecord {compaction_id,assigned_global_logical_command_seq,source_command_digest:WeakDigest,through_global_prefix,terminal_summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,selected_attempt_commitment,selected_public_outcome_commitment,retained_active_attempt_commitment,still_live_independent_obligation_commitment,meta_checkpoint_identity:WeakDigest}` with no retaining edge to the command/spec/evidence and inserts it into `PendingAttemptCompactionRoot {generation,entries[compaction_id->StrongRef<ClosedAttemptCompactionFloorRecord>],nonretaining_predecessor_digest}`. Build does **not** advance the authoritative floor, rewrite detailed indexes, or release heavy evidence. Generated plane-negative fixtures reject both former `StrongRef<AuditOutcomeRecord>` back-edges.

Only after the compact record and its audit visibility advance are durable does the Meta quorum create `AttemptCompactionAttestation {SameGroupCertificateHeader,compaction_id,floor_record_identity:WeakDigest,build_control_identity:WeakGlobalCommandIdentity,build_resulting_root_identity:WeakDigest,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,selected_attempt_commitment,selected_public_outcome_commitment,through_global_prefix,retained_active_attempt_commitment,still_live_independent_obligation_commitment,one_digest_signer_lock_ref:StrongRef<CertificateSignerLock>,quorum_signatures}`. It has no strong edge to the compact record, build command/root, prior floor, spec, or heavy evidence; the certificate is the exact portable release proof, not a semantic command identity. The certificate ledger temporarily retains the build closure until publication. `GlobalClosedAttemptFloorPublishSpec` is `{floor_record_ref:StrongRef<ClosedAttemptCompactionFloorRecord>,attestation_ref:StrongRef<AttemptCompactionAttestation>,expected_pending_entry_digest,expected_prior_authoritative_floor:WeakDigest,expected_summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,exact_selected_attempt_index_rewrite_commitment,exact_selected_outcome_directory_rewrite_commitment,exact_overlap_closed_conflict_rewrite_commitment,exact_begin_status_result_workspace_remote_grant_and_statement_closure_commitment,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply revalidates monotonic extension, removes exactly that pending entry, replaces selected detailed family/attempt/outcome entries with `CompactedTerminal{capability_replay_ref,summary_root_ref,leaf_key_digest,public_outcome,lookup_floor}`, removes only proved overlap-closed conflict detail, and leaves every independently retained result/workspace grant/backup/replay/capability object rooted by its own typed lease or floor. The installed public outcome must byte-match the authenticated leaf and the pre-compaction detailed response. It installs `GlobalAttemptCompactionFloor {summary_root_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot>,closed_through_global_prefix,retained_active_attempt_commitment,still_live_independent_obligation_commitment,last_update:Genesis|NeverRegistered{applied_prefix,record_identity:WeakDigest}|Closed{attestation_ref:StrongRef<AttemptCompactionAttestation>}}`. This floor has no strong/CRSR path to the previous floor, the build record, spec, or heavy evidence. Fresh Meta Genesis and Local activation both create the canonical empty `TerminalAttemptSummaryRoot`, empty `PendingAttemptCompactionRoot`, and `GlobalAttemptCompactionFloor{closed_through=0,last_update=Genesis}`; therefore the first extension has a real prior root. `GlobalClosedAttemptFloorInstallSpec` is the closed tag `InstallNeverRegistered{spec_ref:StrongRef<NeverRegisteredFloorSpec>}|BuildClosed{spec_ref:StrongRef<ClosedAttemptCompactionSpec>}|PublishClosed{spec_ref:StrongRef<GlobalClosedAttemptFloorPublishSpec>}`. Heavy evidence becomes reclaimable only after `PublishClosed`, its audit visibility advance, and an activated checkpoint cut covering the publish/build command and certificate-ledger transfer, subject to every independent live floor. Neither phase decides, aborts, or times out prepared work. Status tests race build, audit visibility advance, attestation, publish, expiry, and post-cut detail reclamation and require identical validated-capability response bytes, service epoch, and privacy shape before expiry and the exact privacy-collapsed expiry response afterward.

`MetaConfigurationTransitionSpec` is `{phase:ProposeJoint|CommitJoint|CommitNew|CommitRetirementFloor,basis_global_state:WeakStateIdentity,current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,prospective_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,candidate_closure_proof_ref:StrongRef,expected_global_prefix,expected_topology_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,resulting_topology_body_digest,paired_checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<GlobalRecoveryCheckpoint>?,paired_floor_ref:StrongRef<MetaConfigPayloadFloor>,required_certificate_floor_ref:StrongRef<MetaCertificateFloor>,required_remote_obligation_floor_ref:StrongRef<RemoteObligationFloor>,prepared_ownership_transfer_refs[StrongRef<MetaPreparedOwnershipTransferRecord>],terminal_audit_gate}`. Deterministic apply constructs a `TopologyState` successor with identical epoch, form, shard assignments, and routing but the phase-appropriate current/joint/new `meta_configuration_ref` plus this applied control; no topology object may retain the retired Meta configuration as current. Every phase is separate, and `CommitRetirementFloor` proves the new set owns all Meta prepared, certificate, and certified-remote obligations. The sole transfer format is the role-specialized `PreparedOwnershipTransferRecord<Meta>` defined above; no later generic or cross-role encoding exists. A current Meta prepared root may carry a prior-configuration entry only in that exact transfer state, and old ownership releases only after new ownership plus Raft/snapshot/apply/GC acknowledgement.

Shard membership is Meta-authorized and later Meta-adopted. `ShardReconfigurationAuthorizationSpec` is `{shard_id,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,old_configuration_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,expected_meta_remote_trust_generation,expected_meta_selected_old_evidence_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,joint_and_final_canonical_configuration_bytes_and_digests,transition_nonce,payload_predicates,required_checkpoint_floor_remote_prepared_certificate_obligations,successor_evidence_recipe,terminal_audit_gate}`; its applied `ShardReconfigurationAuthorizationRecord` fixes the one permitted transition and current trust selector. Each `ShardConfigurationTransitionSpec` consumes the certified record and transfers every old-configuration owner. After `CommitRetirementFloor`, old and new quorums create `ShardConfigurationAdoptionEvidence {SameGroupCertificateHeader,authorization_identity,old/joint/new configuration identities,final_floor,through_shard_index/global_prefix,remote_obligation_commitment,predecessor_remote_configuration_evidence_identity,old_and_new_quorum_signatures,one_digest_signer_lock_ref}`. The successor `RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence::Successor` is authenticated from the currently trusted predecessor and both quorums; new members alone cannot bootstrap it.

`RemoteGrantRetirementPlan` is the Meta-local pre-order object `{plan_id,basis_topology_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,basis_remote_trust_generation,retired_authority_domain,retired_configuration_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,successor_configuration_ref:StrongRef<RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence>,sorted_entries[{old_grant_id,old_grant_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionGrantEvidence>,target_identity,consumer_domain,target_closure_commitment,disposition:ReplaceWithSuccessorGrant{successor_grant_body_recipe,required_consumer_adoption_predicate}|Release{release_request_body_recipe},required_six_stage_transition:RequestConsumerRelease->PublishConsumerReleaseEvidence->ApplyAuthorityRelease->PublishAuthorityReleaseAck->ConsumeReleaseAck->Acknowledged,completion_predicate}],complete_old_grant_set_and_target_coverage_digest,expected_consumer_root_digest,completion_floor_body_recipe}`. It names no future request, tombstone, ack, applied record, or result; adoption verifies the exhaustive old-grant set before selecting it. `ShardConfigurationAdoptionSpec` is Meta input `{authorization_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,adoption_evidence_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardConfigurationAdoptionEvidence>,expected_topology_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,expected_old_shard_configuration_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,expected_remote_trust_generation,expected_selected_old_evidence_ref:RemoteConfigurationRef,successor_configuration_evidence_ref:StrongRef<RemoteAuthorityConfigurationEvidence>,expected_successor_evidence_digest,old_grant_retirement_plan_ref:StrongRef<RemoteGrantRetirementPlan>,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply verifies the direct trust transition and atomically CASes both the topology shard entry and `RemoteConfigurationTrustRoot` selector/generation; neither may advance alone. Only then may the six-stage handshake release old-configuration grants and membership. Stale trust generation, anchor, topology, old/new signature domain, or successor bytes reject with no partial adoption.

Distributed GC separates preflight, semantic authorization/quarantine, physical completion, and aggregate completion. A stage-4 certificate is not release completion. `PortableRemoteAuthorityConfigurationProjection` is the reference-free `{authority_domain,configuration_oid,configuration_digest,canonical_configuration_bytes,payload_predicate_digest,trust_transition_proof_bytes,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. `PortableRemoteReleaseAckEvidence` is `{authority_domain,consumer_domain,grant_id,target_identity,release_nonce,ack_leaf_bytes_and_identity,authority_configuration_evidence:PortableRemoteAuthorityConfigurationProjection,summary_entry_key,summary_inclusion_proof,permanent_release_proof_floor,authority_signatures}` and contains no CRSR, new retention grant, or cross-group strong edge. `GcRemoteReleaseCompletionRef` is the closed role-checked union `AuthorityPublished{publish_record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionAckPublishRecord>,summary_entry_ref:StrongRef<RemoteReleaseSummaryEntry>,ack_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>>}|ConsumerAcknowledged{consume_record_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionConsumeAckRecord>,ack_leaf_ref:StrongRef<ExportLeaf<RemoteRetentionReleaseAckCertificate>>,current_no_old_grant_ref_proof_ref:StrongRef<RemoteRetentionNoOldGrantProof>}|ImportedPermanent{evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableRemoteReleaseAckEvidence>}`. The candidate's ownership/consumer role selects the one legal arm; every arm proves stage 5 or stage 6 plus the permanent summary floor. A request, stage-4 certificate, tombstone, plan, unsigned identity, or a newly granted CRSR cannot inhabit it. A shard first orders `ShardGcPreflightSpec {basis_shard_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_shard_raft_index,basis_global_prefix,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,topology_epoch,complete_typed_root_inventory_ref:StrongRef<GcRootInventory>,candidate_derivation_profile,excluded_obligation_commitment}`. Apply creates `ShardGcPreflightRecord {source_spec_ref:StrongRef<ShardGcPreflightSpec>,basis identities,generated current/cut/prepared/certificate/lease/key/derived/backup/replay/restore/remote inventories,exact_candidate_local_reclaim_set,excluded_obligations,candidate_set_digest}`; only afterward does the quorum create `ShardGcPreflightEvidence {SameGroupCertificateHeader,preflight_record_ref:StrongRef<ShardGcPreflightRecord>,candidate_set_digest,root_inventory_digest,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`. Meta likewise builds `MetaGcPreflightRecord` for its local physical universe before ordering `GlobalGcAuthorizationSpec {basis_global_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_global_prefix,meta_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,meta_preflight_record_ref:StrongRef<MetaGcPreflightRecord>,exact_meta_candidate_set,sorted_shard_preflight_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardGcPreflightEvidence>],exact_per_shard_candidate_commitments,excluded_global_obligation_commitment,completed_remote_release_proof_refs[GcRemoteReleaseCompletionRef],terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply creates `GlobalGcAuthorizationRecord` authorizing only those bounded sets; a planned remote release is not completion, and an active/ReleaseRequested grant target is never a reclaim candidate.

Each participant next orders a role-specific **Semantic quarantine** command. `GcMemberOperationPlan` is the exact `{generation,operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,storage_member_id,storage_profile_oid,target_inventory_digest,canonical_action_body_recipe_and_digest}`. `GcOperationFamily` is `{family_id,base_target_set_commitment,base_generation_zero_plan_set_commitment,successor_operation_id_recipe,max_generations,body_digest}`. `ShardGcApplySpec` is `{authorization_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalGcAuthorizationRecord>,authorization_visibility_projection_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,preflight_record_ref:StrongRef<ShardGcPreflightRecord>,expected_current_shard_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,expected_topology_epoch,expected_root_inventory_digest,exact_authorized_local_reclaim_set,completed_remote_release_ack_refs[GcRemoteReleaseCompletionRef],current_reachability_recomputation_ref:StrongRef<GcReachabilityProof>,newly_reachable_disjoint_proof_ref:StrongRef<GcDisjointnessProof>,operation_family:GcOperationFamily,sorted_generation_zero_plans[GcMemberOperationPlan],complete_member_target_plan_and_family_bijection_digest}`. `MetaGcApplyQuarantineSpec` is the exact Meta-local `{authorization_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalGcAuthorizationRecord>,authorization_visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,meta_preflight_record_ref:StrongRef<MetaGcPreflightRecord>,expected_current_global_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_meta_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,expected_topology_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,expected_root_inventory_digest,exact_authorized_meta_reclaim_set,completed_remote_release_ack_refs[GcRemoteReleaseCompletionRef],current_reachability_recomputation_ref:StrongRef<GcReachabilityProof>,newly_reachable_disjoint_proof_ref:StrongRef<GcDisjointnessProof>,operation_family:GcOperationFamily,sorted_generation_zero_plans[GcMemberOperationPlan],complete_member_target_plan_and_family_bijection_digest,terminal_audit_gate}`. Followers recompute from the **current** Semantic state, both RootSlots, Protocol/Prepared/Consensus state, and the candidate result. They require `reclaim_set ⊆ authorized_set ∩ preflight_set`, disjointness from the current reachable set, and exact family/base-plan coverage; “unchanged or stronger” is not a relation. Apply creates the role-specific reclaim-authorization record and `GcSemanticState::Quarantined` only. Every object encoder, dedupe lookup, prepared receipt, command validator, and root publisher rejects a new retaining edge or realization selection to a quarantined identity. All floors retain the family, every generation, and all cancellation/abandonment/status receipts through aggregate terminality.

After each quarantine is visibly certified, that group's Protocol plane uses the common physical machine defined for Local GC. Neither Shard nor Meta quarantine apply creates a Protocol Requested record, and no maintenance transition mutates Semantic quarantine. `PortableGcPhysicalTerminalEvidence` binds role/group, authorization/quarantine/family/all-generation commitments, complete receipt bijection, pre/post physical-root set, configuration/fence/slot ancestry, and terminal kind. A role-specific `GcPhysicalDispositionImportSpec` then imports that evidence into Semantic state. Only after the resulting Semantic disposition is applied, role-validly published, and audit-visible may a signer-locked certificate attempt create one of four noninterchangeable acks: `MetaGcCompletedAck`, `ShardGcCompletedAck`, `MetaGcCancelledAck`, or `ShardGcCancelledAck`. Completed acks bind reclaimed inventory/post-physical roots; cancelled acks bind global cancellation authority, exact participant no-start evidence, unchanged/no-reclaim physical-root proof, and cancelled-disposition visibility. No apply handler emits an ack or certificate directly.

Distributed cancellation is a fenced protocol, never an independent per-group choice. Global coordination is monotone `Executing -> CancellationPreparing -> CancellationAuthorized|CompletionRequired -> CancelledBeforeAnyParticipantStart|Completed`. `GlobalGcCancellationPrepareSpec {authorization_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalGcAuthorizationRecord>,expected_global_gc_state:Executing,cancellation_nonce,exact_required_participant_commitment,expected_latest_progress_generation,terminal_audit_gate}` creates `GlobalGcCancellationPrepareRecord`; only after that record is applied and audit-visible may the quorum create `GlobalGcCancellationPrepareCertificate`.

Each participant then orders Protocol `GcParticipantCancellationFenceInstallSpec<Role> {authorization_identity,cancellation_prepare_certificate:RoleValidMetaEvidence,expected_quarantine_generation,expected_operation_family,expected_protocol_generation}`. It does one thing atomically: installs `CancellationFenceInstalled`, increments a fence generation, and blocks every new Armed->SentUnknown, send, and successor creation. Status/cancel I/O occurs only afterward under the published fence through the universal dispatch protocol. `GcParticipantCancellationStatusImportSpec` imports authenticated provider status, and `GcParticipantCancelBeforeStartSpec` may request the provider's permanent cancellation tombstone for a NotRegistered or Requested operation. Ambiguous/unavailable status, provider Requested without a terminal cancellation receipt, or unresolved SentUnknown remains blocking. `GcParticipantCancellationEvidenceFinalizeSpec<Role>` then creates exactly one local record: `NoStartFenced{complete base/current-generation target bijection over Armed-AbandonedBeforeSend or provider-CancelledBeforeStart,dispatch-never-sent_and_cancellation_receipt_refs,fence_generation,unchanged_physical_root_commitment}` or `StartedObserved{exact first Started|Completed receipt/status observation,operation,target,member_counter,remaining_family_commitment}`. Only after that record's role-valid publication may Meta create a local certificate or a shard create `CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GcParticipantCancellationEvidence>`. `NoStartFenced` remains reversibly fenced and owns every plan; it is not yet a Semantic cancellation.

Global mode selection is asymmetric. `GlobalGcCancellationAuthorizeSpec` requires current `NoStartFenced` evidence from Meta and every and only required shard plus a complete participant/family/target bijection; it creates `GlobalGcCancellationAuthorizationRecord`, and only after publication may the quorum create `GlobalGcCancellationAuthorizationCertificate`. Conversely, `GlobalGcCompletionRequiredSpec` may be ordered immediately from the first certified StartedObserved/Completed evidence anywhere—or from an explicit authority-bound audited cancellation withdrawal—and permanently selects `CompletionRequired`; it never waits for other participant fences. No later cancellation authorization is legal. Under CancellationAuthorized, `GcPhysicalDispositionImportSpec::Cancelled` consumes role-indexed authority and the still-current no-start evidence; after publication it may yield the typed cancelled ack. `GlobalGcCancellationCompletionSpec {authorization_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalGcCancellationAuthorizationRecord>,authorization_certificate_ref:StrongRef<GlobalGcCancellationAuthorizationCertificate>,meta_cancelled_ack_ref:StrongRef<MetaGcCancelledAck>,exact_sorted_shard_cancelled_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardGcCancelledAck>],complete_participant_family_target_and_no-start_bijection_proof_ref,expected_latest_progress_generation,terminal_audit_gate}` alone selects `CancelledBeforeAnyParticipantStart`.

Under CompletionRequired, `GcParticipantRollForwardSpec<Role>` consumes the role-valid completion-required certificate, preserves every Started/Completed target and original operation ID, and creates family-derived successor operations only for targets previously AbandonedBeforeSend or provider-CancelledBeforeStart; it then selects Protocol `CompletionRequired` and unblocks dispatch. Ordinary retry never consumes a successor generation. `GlobalGcProgressSpec` represents each participant exactly as `Pending|FenceInstalled|NoStartFenced|StartedObserved|RollForwardPending|Completed{typed_completed_ack}|Cancelled{typed_cancelled_ack}|FailedRetryable`, retains every partial evidence/ack set, and cannot mix a global mode with incompatible participant terminals. `GlobalGcCompletionSpec` requires either `NoCancellationPreparePublishedProof` over the exact current coordination generation or the published CompletionRequired record/certificate, plus one `MetaGcCompletedAck`, one `ShardGcCompletedAck` from every and only required shard, and a complete participant/reclaim/family bijection. Completed and Cancelled ack types are uninhabitable in the opposite aggregate. Checkpoints, configuration transitions, backup/restore, key destruction, and topology retirement root every in-flight family generation, dispatch/status lineage, fence, participant evidence, global mode record/certificate, successor plan, and partial ack set. Tests cover every start position, all-no-start, ambiguous status, prepare/fence/start races, resolution loss/replay, successor exhaustion, missing/duplicate/stale evidence, mixed ack substitution, configuration/topology change, and every quarantine/dispatch/deletion/Protocol/Semantic/publication boundary.

Effects use a two-stage acyclic construction. A shard first builds `ShardEffectBasis {global_txn_id,registration_generation,shard_id,registered_snapshot,topology_epoch,active_meta_projection_root_ref:StrongRef<ActiveMetaProjectionRoot>,authority_projection:ShardAuthorityBindingProjection,operation_audit_admission_commitment,sorted_coordinate_bindings,write_key_material_projection_refs[StrongRef<ShardKeyMaterialProjection>],final_effect_refs[ConditionalCoordinateRef],logical_delta_fragment_template_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaTemplate>,read_witness_refs[ShardReadWitnessRef],final_write_summary,constraint_requests_and_deltas,resource_charge_basis,intent_semantics_oid,basis_effect_digest}`. Prepared traversal follows the conditional effect refs strongly; after the global marker assigns the commit coordinate, only matching activated/provisional effect cuts may stop them, while partition versions, delta leases, backup, and replay roots independently retain what remains promised. The complete authority projection binds principal/ceiling/purpose and exact clipped effect/read/disclosure/resource scopes; the basis cannot be recombined with another principal under the same policy root. `basis_effect_digest` hashes the effect-only projection through `intent_semantics_oid`; it excludes every authorization decision, audit object, outcome preparation, resource transition object, fragment wrapper, prepare record, and certificate but includes the authority projection commitment required to interpret the permitted coordinates. `ParticipantRoutingDerivationProof` is `{topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,initial_footprint_commitment,ordered_fixed_point_rounds[{round,prior_participant_set,discovered_cross_partition_effects,discovered_cascades,discovered_constraint_owners,resulting_participant_set}],final_read_write_cascade_constraint_footprint,partition_function_oid,proof_checker_version}`. Meta reaches that deterministic fixed point and builds `ParticipantRoutingCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,sorted_basis_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardEffectBasis>],sorted_participants,sorted_constraint_owners,complete_read_write_cascade_constraint_footprint,partition_derivation_proof_ref:StrongRef<ParticipantRoutingDerivationProof>,complete_participant_commitment,operation_audit_admission_commitment,aggregate_authority_projection_commitment,aggregate_template_digest,global_final_effect_digest,quorum_signatures}`, where `global_final_effect_digest = H("fgdb:global-effect:v1", sorted(shard_id,basis_effect_digest))`. Missing or extra bases/owners, mixed authority projections, a non-fixed-point cascade, or an unsupported routing proof fails closed.

The operation ticket, when required, has already passed external issuance, Meta `AuditTicketAdmissionSpec` import, and exclusive registration claim. After the participant fixed point exists, Meta builds exactly one standalone `GlobalAuthorizationDecisionRecord {authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,operation_audit_admission:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,mutation_permit_ref:StrongRef<MutationPermitRecord<Meta>>,authority_ceiling_digest,routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ParticipantRoutingCertificate>,sorted_basis_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardEffectBasis>],global_final_effect_digest,expected_branch_heads,expected_branch_key_epochs,constraint_and_resource_basis_commitment,policy_epoch,revocation_index,decision,authorization_audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}`. The header, permit, registration, routing certificate, basis projections, and repeated epochs must identify the same complete Meta binding and non-widenable ceiling. A Required authorization gate names that already-claimed operation ticket; this object never points to a future ticket. The global validator replays the permit across the union, including hidden cascades and disclosure contracts; per-shard approvals cannot substitute. Meta next creates resource transitions, `GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord`, and the immutable transaction audit-basis commitment—but no outcome event, resolution, or terminal gate yet. Admission orders and publishes that exact still-pending bundle.

Only then may a participant build `ShardEffectFragment {authority_projection:ShardAuthorityBindingProjection,basis_ref:StrongRef<ShardEffectBasis>,prepare_admission_record_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,admission_generation,routing_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ParticipantRoutingCertificate>,global_authorization_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalAuthorizationDecisionRecord>,global_resource_transition_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Meta>>],global_outcome_preparation_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord>,operation_audit_claim_commitment,transaction_audit_basis_commitment,fragment_envelope_digest}`. The projection must byte-match the basis, imported admission, routing certificate, and clipped Global decision. Every Meta object is an exact certified-remote wrapper bound to the Meta authority domain/configuration, this shard consumer domain, payload availability, and two-sided grant; only `basis_ref` is shard-local. The admission ExportLeaf closure owns the ticket claim without exposing a raw cross-group ticket ref. Wrong role/group/incarnation/configuration/grant or a locally encoded Meta `StrongRef` is unrepresentable and covered by substitution tests. The admission identifies all exact objects/commitments and includes this shard; the envelope digest is excluded from `global_final_effect_digest`. Prepare certificates bind the claimed-pending operation-audit and transaction-basis commitments.

Every protected terminal—not only an admitted W12 write—first freezes exactly the state that its signed event will terminalize. `TerminalAuditOwner` is the closed union `Local{owner:LocalAuditTicketOwner}|LocalPendingBegin{reservation_identity,txn_token,request_basis_commitment}|Meta{owner:AuditTicketOwner}|MetaPendingBegin{reservation_identity,scoped_begin_key_digest,request_basis_commitment}`; pending-begin owner arms are legal only before registration for their exact role's BeginTerminal scope and resolve `AuditResolutionOrigin::Unclaimed`, while Local/Meta owner arms require the registered claim domain. Every tag must match group, ticket admission, request basis, and freeze scope. `ControlTerminalBasisHoldRoot {domain,freeze_scope,typed_held_predicates[ControlTerminalHeldPredicate],strong_dependency_refs[RegisteredStrongRef],coordinate_index_config_key_policy_resource_continuity_coverage_bitmap,conflict_matrix_version,body_digest}` is generated from the exact control spec/expected conditions. Every possibly conflicting mutation, checkpoint cut, GC, key/config/policy retirement, and restore/backup transition consults the active hold root and must wait, transfer it exactly, or reject.

`AuditTerminalFreezeRecord` is an immutable predecessor-linked Local-or-Meta object `{domain:{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role:Local|Meta,group_id},predecessor_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>?,state:Armed{freeze_scope,owner:TerminalAuditOwner,ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,expected_state_conditions_digest,terminal_semantic_basis_commitment,control_terminal_basis_hold_root_ref:StrongRef<ControlTerminalBasisHoldRoot>,scaffolding_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef}|Consumed{terminal_input_identity,assigned_role_order,hold_disposition:ConsumedByTerminal}|ReleasePending{release_admission_closed_at_role_order,control_terminal_basis_hold_root_ref:StrongRef<ControlTerminalBasisHoldRoot>,closed_terminal_attempt_index_generation,closed_signer_release_fence_vector[{signer_id,event_journal_generation,resolution_journal_generation,release_fence_receipt_ref}],blocking_set_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuditSignerBlockingSetProof>,begin_release_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef}|Released{typed_no_terminal_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoTerminalSignatureOrOrderProof>,hold_release_commitment,release_applied_ref:AppliedControlRef}}`; `AuditTerminalFreezeIndex` maps `(domain,owner,ticket)` to the unique latest version and forbids two successors. Prepared/configuration floors, backup/restore, and GC retain each live hold. The terminal apply or FinalizeRelease is the only operation that releases it.

`AuditTerminalFreezeSpec` is the closed lifecycle-scaffolding transition `Arm{group_role,freeze_scope,owner:TerminalAuditOwner,ticket_ref,expected_state_conditions_digest,terminal_semantic_basis_commitment,control_terminal_basis_hold_root_ref:StrongRef<ControlTerminalBasisHoldRoot>,expected_index_absence_or_matching_retry,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}|BeginRelease{group_role,armed_freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,expected_index_value:Armed,expected_terminal_attempt_index_generation,sorted_signer_release_fence_receipts,blocking_set_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuditSignerBlockingSetProof>,expected_state_conditions_digest,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}|FinalizeRelease{group_role,release_pending_freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,no_terminal_signature_or_order_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoTerminalSignatureOrOrderProof>,expected_index_value:ReleasePending,expected_closed_attempt_and_signer_journal_vector,exact_hold_release_recipe,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. Arm scopes are `LocalControl`, `LocalBeginTerminal`, `MetaControl{registered_global_control_tag,spec_body_digest,expected_state_conditions_digest}`, `MetaBeginTerminal`, `MetaStatementPublishOrAbort`, `MetaActiveCancel`, and `MetaReadClose`; Arm apply atomically validates the complete basis, installs its hold, and freezes it before any terminal signature. BeginRelease first makes every signer in the complete eligible universe—or a registered blocking set proved to intersect every legal event and resolution threshold subset—durably reject new attempts/locks/shares for this freeze at the named journal generations, then selects ReleasePending without dropping the hold. FinalizeRelease byte-matches proof/freeze/ticket/domain/basis, unchanged generations, and exact hold release and alone produces Released; `Consumed` remains an atomic terminal output. Admitted Local mutation and W12 write use their ordered final-certification reservation holds instead.

`AuditTerminalFreezeRef` is the closed union `LocalMutation{fence_ref:StrongRef<LocalTerminalAdmissionFence>,required_fence_state:Armed,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReservation>,required_reservation_state:Active}|LocalControl{freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,required_scope:LocalControl}|LocalBeginTerminal{freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,reservation_identity,txn_token,request_basis_commitment,required_scope:LocalBeginTerminal}|MetaControl{freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,required_scope:MetaControl{registered_global_control_tag,spec_body_digest,expected_state_conditions_digest}}|MetaBeginTerminal{freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,reservation_identity,scoped_begin_key_digest,request_basis_commitment,required_scope:MetaBeginTerminal}|MetaStatementPublishOrAbort{freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,required_scope:MetaStatementPublishOrAbort}|MetaActiveCancel{freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,required_scope:MetaActiveCancel}|MetaReadClose{freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,required_scope:MetaReadClose}|MetaAdmittedWrite{fence_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalAdmissionFence>,required_fence_state:Armed,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalFinalCertificationReservation>,required_reservation_state:Active}`. The exact SequenceNeutralSpec envelope field matrix makes this ref required on every final protected terminal tag and forbidden elsewhere; it is never silently injected into a body. An admitted-write plan byte-matches the reservation's one selected terminal. Final protected apply atomically consumes its unique reservation/freeze or constructs the terminal successor while Armed; release is legal only after ReleasePending with the typed proof that no signature share, quorum object, final command, or candidate root exists.

`NoTerminalSignatureOrOrderProof` is an authority-local quorum certificate `{SameGroupCertificateHeader,freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,required_freeze_state:ReleasePending,ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,owner:TerminalAuditOwner,terminal_semantic_basis_commitment,plan_disposition:NeverCreated{terminal_attempt_index_absence_path,signer_release_fence_and_attempt_absence_proofs_by_signer}|SemanticPlanAbandoned{terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,abandonment_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalPlanAbandonment>,all_event_attempt_disposition_refs[],no_event_or_resolution_object_proof,retired_attempt_epoch_floor},audit_terminal_attempt_index_root_digest,sorted_event_signer_journal_closures,sorted_resolution_signer_journal_closures,certificate_ledger_root_digest,complete_signer_set_and_key_epoch,raft_nonorder_evidence:{term,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,read_barrier_index,commit_index,applied_index,closed_role_order,terminal_command_index_absence_path},candidate_root_and_audit_visibility_pipeline_absence_proof,external_audit_continuity_digest_and_cas_version,quiescence_barrier_id,checker_version,certificate_attempt_plan_ref,certificate_signer_lock_refs,quorum_signatures}`. All paths, release fences, attempt dispositions, signer-journal closures, and authority roots are observed at one linearizable quiescence barrier after BeginRelease selected ReleasePending; generations equal the closed vector in that state. A release fence covers every current eligible event/resolution signer or a proved blocking set intersecting every legal subset. NeverCreated is invalid if any matching semantic plan/signing attempt/lock/share existed. SemanticPlanAbandoned requires every concrete event attempt terminally superseded/abandoned with no event; a partial-share attempt needs quorum-impossibility and signer-closure evidence. The certificate is built after BeginRelease and before FinalizeRelease, names no future release command/result/root, and remains through freeze/audit retirement floors. Any missing signer/blocking proof, later-orderable command, candidate, stale Raft/configuration basis, or latent completable attempt makes release ineligible.

`AuditTerminalPlanAbandonSpec` is the exact freeze-backed Local-or-Meta maintenance input `{group_role,release_pending_freeze_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalFreezeRecord>,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,expected_attempt_index_entry:PlanSelected|EventAttemptSelected|AttemptSuperseded,expected_per_signer_release_fence_vector,event_attempt_disposition_refs[],no_event_or_resolution_object_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuditStageNonpublicationProof>,complete_eligible_signer_universe,attempt_epoch_retirement_recipe,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. It is legal only while the freeze is ReleasePending, every concrete event attempt is either share-free or quorum-impossible and durably closed, no event/resolution/terminal order/candidate exists, and every otherwise eligible signer is release-fenced. Apply creates `AuditTerminalPlanAbandonment {freeze_identity,terminal_plan_identity,closed_attempt_index_generation,closed_signer_journal_vector,event_attempt_disposition_commitment,retired_attempt_epoch_floor,maintenance_applied_index}` and marks the semantic plan AbandonedForFreezeRelease. It erases nothing and authorizes no replacement terminal. Any event, resolution attempt, prepared terminal, order, or candidate makes true plan abandonment permanently illegal. Admitted-write reservations cannot use this spec; they keep the selected terminal and use signing-attempt supersession until completion. All abandoned plan/attempt/lock/share evidence remains rooted through FinalizeRelease and the audit-retirement floor.

After the freeze/reservation, matching mutation/read/control/no-effect audit bytes use one acyclic, crash-recoverable signing pipeline; only the completed ticket/event/resolution triple becomes the final `TerminalAuditGate`. `SequenceNeutralAuditEventBody` is the pre-order immutable `{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,owner:TerminalAuditOwner,chosen_terminal_kind,semantic_input_commitment,authorization_decision_digest,outcome:TransactionMutation|ControlMutation|ReadResult|Denied|Failed|Abandoned,event_attested_at,stable_ticket_domain_profile_epoch_and_unresolved_membership_commitment,body_digest}`. Its registered digest excludes only `body_digest` and contains no plan/attempt/lock/share/aggregate, mutable audit-continuity head/CAS version/append position, future role order/HLC/marker/candidate, or result root. `SequenceNeutralAuditResolutionBody` is `{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,owner,terminal_class,expected_event_body_digest,stable_ticket_domain_profile_epoch_and_unresolved_membership_commitment,resolution_attested_at,body_digest}` with the same exclusions. `AuditTerminalSigningPlan` is the immutable semantic plan `{plan_id,plan_generation,predecessor_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>?,terminal_freeze_ref:AuditTerminalFreezeRef,ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,owner:TerminalAuditOwner,chosen_terminal_kind,semantic_input_commitment,event_body_ref:StrongRef<SequenceNeutralAuditEventBody>,event_body_digest,resolution_body_ref:StrongRef<SequenceNeutralAuditResolutionBody>,resolution_body_digest,event_signer_policy_ref,resolution_signer_policy_ref,event_threshold_rule,resolution_threshold_rule,signature_algorithm_and_encoding}`. It selects no concrete signer subset and binds only stable ticket/domain/profile/epoch/unresolved-membership facts; unrelated ticket issue/resolve and the mutable external audit head may advance without invalidating it.

Concrete subsets are replaceable attempts. `AuditEventSigningAttemptPlan {attempt_id,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,event_body_digest,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,canonical_signer_subset[{signer_id,signing_key_id,signer_set_epoch,key_epoch}],threshold,signature_algorithm_and_encoding}` is installed through nonsemantic maintenance before any event lock. For each selected signer, `AuditEventSignerLock {attempt_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventSigningAttemptPlan>,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,event_body_digest,signer_id,signing_key_id,signer_set_epoch,key_epoch,canonical_signing_transcript_digest,one_digest_only=true}` is signer-journal-published before `AuditEventSignatureShare`. `AuditEventRecord` deterministically adds `{terminal_plan_ref,event_attempt_plan_ref,event_body_ref,event_lock_refs,event_signature_share_refs,signer_subset_bitmap,threshold,canonical_aggregate_or_signature_set}` to the exact pre-order body; its signing transcript is `H(domain,terminal_plan_oid,event_attempt_oid,event_body_digest,signer identity/key/epochs)` and thereby binds the plan without a plan↔event cycle.

Only after the event exists may maintenance install `AuditResolutionSigningAttemptPlan {attempt_id,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>,resolution_body_digest,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,canonical_signer_subset[{signer_id,signing_key_id,signer_set_epoch,key_epoch}],threshold,signature_algorithm_and_encoding}`. Each selected signer publishes `AuditResolutionSignerLock {attempt_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditResolutionSigningAttemptPlan>,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>,resolution_body_digest,signer_id,signing_key_id,signer_set_epoch,key_epoch,canonical_signing_transcript_digest,one_digest_only=true}` before its share. `AuditTicketResolution` deterministically adds the terminal plan, resolution attempt, event ref, exact resolution lock/share bijection, signer bitmap, threshold, and canonical aggregate/signature set to the pre-order resolution body. Signer identity/key/epoch are part of every lock/share transcript, so selected signers cannot share a lock ObjectId. Missing, duplicate, unplanned, wrong-key, wrong-epoch, reordered, or aggregate-mismatched shares fail closed.

`AuditSigningAttemptSupersedeSpec` is the nonsemantic maintenance input `{stage:Event|Resolution,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,old_attempt_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventSigningAttemptPlan|AuditResolutionSigningAttemptPlan>,new_attempt_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventSigningAttemptPlan|AuditResolutionSigningAttemptPlan>,required_identical_body_and_event_commitment,old_signer_release_fence_vector,old_share_state:ZeroShares|PartialShares{complete_share_bitmap,quorum_impossibility_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuditSignerQuorumImpossibilityProof>},no_completed_stage_object_or_terminal_order_candidate_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuditStageNonpublicationProof>,terminal_freeze_or_final_certification_reservation_ref,terminal_audit_gate:LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired}`. Every old signer capable of completing the subset must close that attempt; the new attempt uses a higher ID, the identical terminal/event/resolution body, and a currently legal subset. Event supersession is illegal after an event exists; resolution supersession preserves the exact event. It never changes/release the terminal freeze, final-certification selection, ticket, semantic body, or result. Old attempts/locks/shares/closures remain rooted. Thus signer loss before or after partial shares cannot strand an admitted terminal, while two attempts can never both complete. True freeze release/terminal-plan abandonment remains the separate preterminal path below.

`AuditVisibilityAdvanceInputRef` is the closed same-group union `Local{spec_ref:StrongRef<AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Local>>,maintenance_command_ref:StrongRef<LocalMaintenanceCommand>}|Meta{spec_ref:StrongRef<AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Meta>>,maintenance_command_ref:StrongRef<MetaMaintenanceCommand>}`; each command must be the registered envelope whose sole body is the named spec. `AuditTerminalAttemptRecord` is a predecessor-linked durable stage chain `{predecessor_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalAttemptRecord>?,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,state:PlanSelected|EventAttemptSelected{attempt_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventSigningAttemptPlan>}|EventLocksPublished{attempt_plan_ref,event_lock_refs[StrongRef<AuditEventSignerLock>],complete_planned_lock_bijection}|EventSharesCollected{attempt_plan_ref,event_lock_refs,event_share_refs[StrongRef<AuditEventSignatureShare>],event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>}|ResolutionAttemptSelected{event_ref,resolution_attempt_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditResolutionSigningAttemptPlan>}|ResolutionLocksPublished{event_ref,resolution_attempt_plan_ref,resolution_lock_refs[StrongRef<AuditResolutionSignerLock>],complete_planned_lock_bijection}|Prepared{event_ref,resolution_attempt_plan_ref,resolution_lock_refs,resolution_share_refs[StrongRef<AuditResolutionSignatureShare>],resolution_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketResolution>,terminal_gate_digest}|Ordered{terminal_input_identity,role_order}|CandidateApplied{candidate_root_identity}|ExternalBatchCasCommitted{batch_ref:StrongRef<AuditExternalResolutionBatch>,receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuditExternalBatchCasReceipt>,batch_member_ordinal}|VisibilityReleased{advance_input_ref:AuditVisibilityAdvanceInputRef,assigned_maintenance_index,pipeline_order,released_candidate_identity}|AttemptSuperseded{stage,old_attempt_identity,new_attempt_identity,supersede_evidence_ref:StrongRef<AuditSigningAttemptSupersession>}}`. Each successor retains its predecessor and needed artifacts. `VisibilityReleased` names only the already-built spec/maintenance command and the scalar index assigned while applying it; it never points to the future maintenance-applied record or resulting Protocol root. The matching prepared root plus generated `AuditTerminalAttemptIndex` maps `(domain,freeze_or_reservation_identity,terminal_plan_identity)` to the latest record and separately maps each concrete signing-attempt ID to Active|Superseded|Completed; exact absence/membership proofs are therefore available even at PlanSelected.

Before event locks, validation checks the Armed freeze/Active final-certification reservation rather than reserving semantic order. Immediately before ordering, the final transaction/control input consumes that hold, takes the then-next role order/HLC through the order supplement, and records Ordered. Candidate apply appends the RequiredPending pipeline entry, selects Semantic `AuditTicketIndexValue::TerminalPrepared`, and records CandidateApplied without exposing it. The external resolver selects only a gap-free prefix from the pipeline, builds `AuditExternalResolutionBatch`, CASes from the then-current continuity head to one direct-successor `TicketsResolvedBatch` record, and returns `AuditExternalBatchCasReceipt`; each included attempt records `ExternalBatchCasCommitted` with its exact member ordinal. `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec` verifies that batch and releases exactly the same pipeline prefix, creates `VisibilityReleased` and `AuditOutcomeRecord` successors using its already-built maintenance command identity plus assigned maintenance index, and advances public visibility through `VisibleAuditOutcomeIndex`. Signed bodies never bind a stale expected continuity head. Crash resumes the selected or legally superseded signing attempt, locked digests, collected shares, durable stage, exact external batch/CAS, or pending visibility advance. A definitive external conflict triggers fail-closed recovery/permanent-gap handling and retains the attempt; it never exposes a candidate. Decision/shard apply and public-result guards verify the visibility-released audit outcome against the original TerminalPrepared claim and hold. Crash/DPOR suites race unrelated ticket issue/resolve operations, every scope/lock/share/supersede stage, alternate terminal, leader loss, reproposal, batch selection, external CAS, partial response loss, and visibility-advance recovery.

`ShardPrepareCommand` is fence-free canonical input `{command_version,authority_projection:ShardAuthorityBindingProjection,registration_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,prepare_admission_record_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,admission_generation,admission_global_logical_command_seq,admission_global_commit_seq,expected_prior_global_applied_prefix,routing_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ParticipantRoutingCertificate>,basis_ref:StrongRef<ShardEffectBasis>,fragment_ref:StrongRef<ShardEffectFragment>,active_meta_projection_root_ref:StrongRef<ActiveMetaProjectionRoot>,shard_pre_command_base_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ShardPreCommandBaseAvailabilityCertificate>,expected_shard_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,expected_topology_epoch,expected_policy_epoch,expected_revocation_index,expected_branch_heads,expected_branch_key_epochs,prepare_key:{global_txn_id,registration_generation,admission_generation},local_prepare_generation}`. Before ordering, the shard first advances through every global command up to and including the unique admission—typed projection for every relevant control and exhaustive irrelevance proof otherwise—then verifies the complete authority projection, active Meta projection, topology/policy/revocation/key/audit state, configuration, admission, basis, fragment, and exact D1 base certificate byte-for-byte. The command projection must equal the basis projection and certified admission source; Prepare does not advance that prefix a second time. A stale/mixed projection or matching abort tombstone rejects. Apply builds `ShardPrepareRecord {command_ref:StrongRef<ShardPrepareCommand|ShardDecisionApplyCommand>,prepare_key,shard_id,shard_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,topology_epoch,admission_global_prefix,local_prepare_generation,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<ShardPrepareRecord>?,basis_ref:StrongRef<ShardEffectBasis>,fragment_ref:StrongRef<ShardEffectFragment>,reservation_refs[StrongRef<ConstraintReservationRecord>],base/current_closure_inventory,state:Prepared|GloballyCommitted{global_logical_command_seq,global_commit_seq,decision_identity}|GloballyAborted{global_logical_command_seq,decision_identity}|Applied{global_logical_command_seq,global_commit_seq?,decision_identity,fragment_batch_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaBatch>?}|GcEligible{terminal_floor_ref:RegisteredStrongRef}}` and atomically installs the canonical constraint reservations plus a `ShardCommandRecord`; no local fence appears in either. Successors preserve the base inventory and strengthen the current closure. The shard-group `ShardPrepareCertificate` then quorum-signs its `SameGroupCertificateHeader` plus `{prepare_record_ref,admission/registration/routing remote identities,authority_projection_commitment,prepare_key,admission_generation/admission global prefix,shard/topology/active-projection identities,registered snapshot,expected heads/key epochs,basis/fragment/global-effect/witness/write/constraint/resource/authorization/audit commitments,pre_command_base_availability_certificate_ref,verifier_version,quorum_signatures}`. Followers recompute all fields. A late prepare after an abort tombstone, a second admission generation under one registration, or any mismatched/stale admission/routing/fragment/authority projection is rejected; the later decision adds its assigned global sequence.

`ConstraintReservationRecord` is an owner-shard object `{reservation_id,constraint_id,canonical_domain_key_or_range,owner_shard,topology_epoch,prepare_key,registration_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,prepare_admission_record_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,routing_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ParticipantRoutingCertificate>,exact_global_basis_digest,before_contribution,after_contribution,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<ConstraintReservationRecord>?,state:Held|Consumed{global_decision_identity}|Released{global_decision_identity}}`. `GlobalConstraintReservationCertificate` is produced by that owner shard after prepare and is `{SameGroupCertificateHeader,reservation_record_ref:StrongRef<ConstraintReservationRecord>,prepare_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ShardPrepareCertificate>,admission_record_remote_identity,constraint/domain/owner/topology identities,prepare_key,exact_global_basis_digest,before/after contribution,registered_snapshot,expiration:None,quorum_signatures}`. Meta receives only its generated `ExportLeaf` through a certified-remote ref.

For a constraint whose topology declares meta ownership, admission apply creates `MetaConstraintReservationRecord {reservation_id,constraint_id,canonical_domain_key_or_range,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,admission_spec_ref:StrongRef<GlobalPrepareAdmissionSpec>,admission_generation,exact_global_basis_digest,before_aggregate,after_aggregate,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<MetaConstraintReservationRecord>?,state:Held|Consumed{global_txn_command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnCommand>,global_logical_command_seq}|Released{applied_abort_ref:AppliedAbortRef}}` from the spec's pre-order request. `ConstraintReservationRef` is the closed tag `LocalMeta{reservation_ref:StrongRef<MetaConstraintReservationRecord>} | RemoteShard{reservation_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalConstraintReservationCertificate>}`. `GlobalTxnCommand::Commit` names the exact complete sorted set; meta apply consumes local reservations atomically and each shard decision consumes its remote reservations. Abort names whatever remote certificates already exist, releases all meta reservations, and sends the published abort to every admitted shard so a present or late reservation cannot survive. Reservations are acquired in canonical order; clock timeout, suspicion, or coordinator loss has no effect.

`DistributedSerializationCertificate` is meta-owned and sequence-neutral and counter-free `{SameGroupCertificateHeader,attempt_key:{global_txn_id,registration_generation},lifecycle_basis:ReadOnly{read_routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ReadParticipantRoutingCertificate>}|ReadWrite{prepare_admission_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,admission_generation,routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ParticipantRoutingCertificate>},held_final_certification_basis_commitment,conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity,conflict_index_basis_digest:WeakDigest,overlapping_attempt_key_set_commitment,candidate_read_witness_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef],candidate_write_summary_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef],candidate_edges[{kind:wr|ww|rw,from_attempt_key,to_attempt_key,witness_or_version_proof_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef}],retained_graph_projection_digest,scc_verifier_version,acyclic_result,conservative_fallbacks[],quorum_signatures}`. `GlobalConflictIndex` is `{basis_global_logical_command_seq,entries[(global_txn_id,registration_generation) -> {snapshot/start/terminal prefixes,state:Active|PrepareAdmitted|Committed|Aborted|ReadClosed,admission_generation?,participants,read_witness_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef],write_summary_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef],incoming/outgoing_edges,overlap_close_floor}],safe_snapshot_frontier}`. Every meta follower reconstructs both directions of `may_affect`, verifies every routed shard/constraint contribution, and reruns the registered SCC algorithm; stale/missing support rejects. `GlobalAttemptIndex`, `GlobalTxnOutcomeIndex`, and the matching conflict entry must make the same lifecycle transition atomically. A new registration generation never overwrites an older overlapping generation; each entry remains rooted until its own overlap-close rule proves no future registered transaction can add an edge. The certificate names the conflict/head/constraint basis later held by `GlobalFinalCertificationReservation`; assigned next logical/commit counters occur only in `MetaOrderAttemptInput`.

`GlobalTxnCommand` is the closed union with common `{command_version,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,prepare_admission_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,terminal_admission_fence_ref:StrongRef<MetaTerminalAdmissionFence>,required_terminal_admission_fence_state:Armed,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalFinalCertificationReservation>,required_final_certification_state:Active,admission_generation,meta_pre_command_base_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<MetaPreCommandBaseAvailabilityCertificate>,order_attempt_input_ref:StrongRef<MetaOrderAttemptInput>,order_attempt_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<MetaOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate>,topology_state_ref:StrongRef<TopologyState>,policy_epoch,revocation_index}` and exactly one intent. The header, repeated epochs, authorization decision or terminal-abort authority, admission fence, and final-certification reservation must byte-match one complete non-widened authority binding. The supplement alone supplies both expected semantic counters when applicable and HLC evidence; neither is signed into the audit event/resolution. A harmless intervening Meta command retires/replaces only the provably unordered supplement against the same still-Active reservation and Armed fence. Dynamic basis fields are integrity projections of the reservation, not fresh CAS gates. `Commit{routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ParticipantRoutingCertificate>,participant_prepare_certificate_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardPrepareCertificate>],distributed_serialization_certificate_ref:StrongRef<DistributedSerializationCertificate>,constraint_reservation_refs[ConstraintReservationRef],global_authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAuthorizationDecisionRecord>,resource_transition_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Meta>>],global_outcome_preparation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord>,terminal_audit_gate:TerminalAuditGate,expected_branch_heads[WeakMarkerIdentity],expected_branch_key_epochs,global_final_effect_digest}` requires exact admission/fence/reservation/order-subject equality, one valid prepare for every admitted participant, the complete reservation set, and byte-equality across every serialization/auth/resource/outcome/audit field;
