apply assigns the exact next GlobalLogicalCommandSeq and, only for Commit, the exact next GlobalCommitSeq from the supplement, atomically marks it Ordered, consumes the final-certification reservation, and constructs/selects the unique TerminalOrdered fence successor. `AbortAfterAdmission{terminal_abort_authority_ref:StrongRef<TerminalAbortAuthority<Meta>>,complete_no_effect_and_release_proof_ref:StrongRef<FinalAbortNoEffectAndReleaseProof<Meta>>,stable_public_reason_class,known_prepare_certificate_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardPrepareCertificate>],known_remote_constraint_reservation_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalConstraintReservationCertificate>],meta_constraint_reservation_refs[StrongRef<MetaConstraintReservationRecord>],resource_release_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Meta>>],global_outcome_preparation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord>,terminal_audit_gate:TerminalAuditGate,expected_conflict_index_basis:WeakStateIdentity}` is constructible only from the byte-identical `FinalCertificationSelection::MetaAbort`, matching Active MetaAbort reservation, matching MetaOrderSubject::Terminal supplement, published admission, and typed no-effect/release/audit evidence even if no participant prepared; known certificates are retained only for deterministic release. It adopts no effects, creates no marker/delta, releases every reserved Meta resource named by that selection, consumes the certification reservation, constructs/selects the unique terminal fence successor, and terminalizes conflict/attempt/outcome state. Shard abort acknowledgements necessarily arise only after decision -> certificate publication -> shard apply; they gate client completion, attempt compaction, remote-retention release, and GC, never construction/admission of this abort command. Active-but-unadmitted cancellation is exclusively `GlobalAttemptCancelSpec`, so the two abort paths cannot race.

Meta transaction apply assigns the next global logical sequence/HLC; commit also assigns commit sequence. Commit builds marker/heads/terminal outcome/constraints/payload; abort builds terminal no-effect state; then apply constructs `GlobalTxnRecord` and candidate `GlobalStateRoot`, never shard batches. A required terminal audit gate keeps that candidate fenced until the external batch CAS and exact gap-free Protocol visibility advance publish it. Only after the releasing slot is durable does the group build `PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>` for that candidate. `GlobalDecisionCertificate` then begins with `SameGroupCertificateHeader` and quorum-signs `{global_txn_record_ref,resulting_global_state_root_ref,terminal_visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,prepare_admission_record_ref,admission_generation,exact participants,meta term/index,assigned sequences/HLC,decision,marker/effect digest,topology_epoch,retention_floor}`. Deterministic signer selection/bytes are ledgered before release. `GlobalDecisionCertificatePublishSpec {decision_certificate_ref,expected_transaction_identity,expected_candidate_root_identity,expected_terminal_visibility_certificate_identity,terminal_audit_gate}` then installs it in the global registry; only this published certificate is exportable. Recovery uses exact ledgered bytes under the retained decision configuration and never substitutes another signer transcript or a semantic “mirror” record.

`ShardAbortTombstone` is `{prepare_key,prepare_admission_record_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,decision_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalDecisionCertificate>,decision_publish_record_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,shard_id,topology_epoch,applied_shard_raft_index,global_abort_logical_command_seq,state:TerminalAbort}`. The consumer-local wrappers are rooted in the shard's applied-decision index/configuration floor until compaction; no bare remote identity or local `StrongRef` can replace them. `ShardDecisionApplyCommand` is `{authority_projection:ShardAuthorityBindingProjection,decision_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalDecisionCertificate>,decision_publish_record_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,prepare_key,expected_prepare_record_ref?,expected_absent_or_matching_abort_tombstone,expected_prior_global_applied_logical_command_seq,exact_decision_global_logical_command_seq,expected_topology_epoch,shard_pre_command_base_availability_certificate_ref}`. The authority projection's certified Meta source, local security component, clipped scopes, principal/ceiling/purpose, decision, and current active Meta projection must all byte-match; a scalar epoch, bare `ShardSecurityStateProjection`, or generic projection digest is invalid. Its D1 certificate uses the exact current domain/config/predicate and complete decision/publish plus prior-state closure. Commit requires Prepared; abort accepts Prepared or proven absence and installs the terminal tombstone when absent. At global decision `N`, deterministic apply consumes/releases reservations, materializes hidden fragment effects/batch for commit, and creates `ShardDecisionInstallRecord {command_ref:StrongRef<ShardDecisionApplyCommand>,prepare_key,decision_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalDecisionCertificate>,decision_publish_record_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,resulting_terminal_prepare_or_tombstone_ref:ShardDecisionTerminalRef,fragment_batch_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaBatch>?,decision_global_prefix,precomputable_transition_commitment}`. The transition commitment hashes only prior shard-state identity, command identity, assigned shard/global order, and already constructed result identities; it excludes the future payload, command record, and root. Apply advances only `N-1 -> N`; after all results exist, `ShardCommandRecord.resulting_shard_payload_digest` cross-checks the constructed payload. It never jumps through the later Meta audit visibility advance or certificate-publication control. That record/root is durable but not yet a visibility acknowledgement.

The shard must next apply the exact audit-visibility `MetaControlProjectionCertificate` at `N+1` (and any preceding required control in order). Only that projection can make `audit_visible_global_prefix >= N`. From the post-visibility root, the quorum creates `ShardAppliedAck {SameGroupCertificateHeader,decision_certificate_identity,decision_publish_record_identity,audit_visibility_certificate_identity,prepare_key,terminal_result:Prepared{terminal_prepare_record_ref}|NoPrepare{abort_tombstone_ref},resulting_post_visibility_shard_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,fragment_batch_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaBatch>?,global_applied_logical_command_seq>=N+1,decision_global_logical_command_seq:N,global_applied_commit_seq,local_raft_index,audit_visible_prefix>=N,shard_payload_floor_ref,quorum_signatures}`. No pre-visibility ack is exportable. Meta publishes this typed ack in its directory and requires one from every admitted participant before global delta construction, client completion, attempt compaction, remote release, or GC. Retry reproduces the same terminal commitment; decision install never bypasses the audit visibility advance.

After every committed participant has produced its batch and acknowledgement, meta constructs `GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch {global_txn_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnRecord>,marker_identity:WeakMarkerIdentity,global_commit_seq,sorted_fragment_batches[{shard_id,fragment_batch_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<LogicalDeltaBatch>,fragment_digest}],sorted_apply_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardAppliedAck>],complete_participant_commitment,resulting_global_frontier,batch_digest}`. The already-known transaction record points to no batch, so the object graph is acyclic. `GlobalDeltaPublishSpec {global_batch_ref:StrongRef<GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch>,expected_prior_global_frontier,expected_transaction_and_marker:WeakDigest,terminal_audit_gate}` is a `GlobalControlCommand`; apply verifies exact prepare-set equality and contiguous frontier, then atomically installs the batch/index/frontier through payload→control-record→root. Ripple and client commit acknowledgement wait for this publication, every touched shard durability/apply quorum, terminal audit visibility, and durable outcome; a crash before them yields capability-scoped `Unknown`, never partial delta visibility.

`ShardCheckpointInstallSpec` is `{basis_shard_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_shard_raft_index,basis_global_prefix,basis_payload_digest:WeakDigest,basis_projection_digest,checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<ShardRecoveryCheckpoint>,checkpoint_state_vector_digest,paired_shard_config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ShardConfigPayloadFloor>?,retention_cut_body_ref:StrongRef<RetentionCutBody>?,reset_field_tags[]}`. Genesis requires no body/floor and produces `CheckpointInstalled`; operational replacement requires both, atomically installs the matching provisional cut, applies only registered reset/equivalence transitions, and preserves `Operational`. `ShardInitialFloorInstallSpec` is `{checkpoint_installed_shard_state:WeakStateIdentity,checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<ShardRecoveryCheckpoint>,checkpoint_state_vector_digest,initial_shard_config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ShardConfigPayloadFloor>,initial_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,expected_shard_raft_index}` and is the sole fresh `CheckpointInstalled -> Operational` transition. Local-to-W12 migration uses only the canonical `ShardProjectionInstallSpec` above with consumer-local certified transition/projection refs and the exact role-transition Genesis authorization; its barrier must byte-match the plan predicate and canonical strong-closure schema. It is invalid for an ordinary fresh shard. `ShardHistoryCutActivationSpec` is `{provisional_cut_ref:StrongRef<ProvisionalRetentionCutSet>,checkpoint_install_record_ref:StrongShardCommandRef,expected_retention_basis:WeakStateIdentity,verified_contiguous_suffix_start}` and only strengthens the exact provisional set.

`ShardConfigurationTransitionSpec` is `{phase:ProposeJoint|CommitJoint|CommitNew|CommitRetirementFloor,basis_shard_state:WeakStateIdentity,current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,prospective_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,candidate_closure_proof_ref:StrongRef,expected_shard_raft_index,expected_global_prefix,paired_checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<ShardRecoveryCheckpoint>?,paired_floor_ref:StrongRef<ShardConfigPayloadFloor>,meta_authorization_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardReconfigurationAuthorizationRecord>,prepared_ownership_transfer_refs[StrongRef<ShardPreparedOwnershipTransferRecord>]}`; every field byte-matches the Meta authorization, and final apply enables adoption evidence but does not mutate Meta topology. `ShardTopologyCopyApplySpec` is `{copy_record_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<TopologyTransitionRecord>,transfer_plan_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<TopologyTransferPlan>,source_checkpoint_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardCheckpointCertificate>],received_inventory_digest,expected_destination_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,through_global_prefix}`. `ShardDerivedBuildSpec` and `ShardDerivedAdvanceSpec` bind the exact `DerivedIdentity`, input `GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch` CRSR interval, active Meta projection, topology/config, prior component, result component root, answer contract, and availability floor; apply roots the component before a certificate is exported. Key lifecycle uses only `ShardKeyMaterialStageSpec`, `ShardKeyMaterialActivateSpec`, `ShardKeyZeroReferenceSpec`, `ShardKeyDestroyApplySpec`, and `ShardKeyPhysicalDestructionCompletionSpec` with the exact plan/manifest/authorization, current topology/configuration, prior registry entry, full root-set basis, and envelope/wrap/opener or physical-receipt closure described above. These typed controls, not a generic payload import, are the only derived/key installation, activation, proof, or destruction paths.

The same generated registry drives classification, certification, import, and apply. Each `GlobalControlKind` has exactly one `MetaControlEffectRow`: `NoShardEffect{exhaustive_checker_id,proof_rule}` or `Targeted{destination_selector,ordered_slot_tags[MetaProjectionSlot],typed_payload_schema_id,per_slot_cas_rule}`. `TypedCertifiedMetaProjectionPayload` is the closed object `{source_global_control_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,source_global_command_kind,target_shard_id,effect_row_id,before_global_prefix,after_global_prefix,ordered_slot_deltas[{slot_tag,before_value_digest,after_value:InlineRegisteredValue|StrongRef,import_projection[RemoteObjectIdentity],retention_grant_identity,slot_specific_transition_commitment}],exhaustive_slot_bitmap,topology_and_configuration_selector,projection_checker_version}`. The bitmap must equal the row's generated slot set; a missing, duplicate, extra, reordered, or wrong-schema delta is invalid.

`MetaControlProjectionCertificate` is the Meta-quorum-signed `{SameGroupCertificateHeader,source_global_control_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,source_global_command_kind,target_shard_id,before_global_prefix,after_global_prefix,effect_row_id,typed_payload_ref:StrongRef<TypedCertifiedMetaProjectionPayload>,exhaustive_slot_bitmap,payload_closure_inventory_digest,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`. It is valid only for one published relevant control and one target; it cannot summarize transaction effects or a range. `ShardMetaControlApplySpec` is `{projection_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<MetaControlProjectionCertificate>,typed_payload_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<TypedCertifiedMetaProjectionPayload>,expected_shard_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_active_meta_projection_root_ref:StrongRef<ActiveMetaProjectionRoot>,expected_prior_global_prefix,ordered_slot_cas_expectations[{slot_tag,expected_before_value_digest}],expected_topology_epoch,expected_remote_trust_selectors[],expected_audit_visibility_prefix}`. Its body contains no authority value; the outer tag-specific `ShardControlAuthority<ApplyMetaControl>` must name the same projection CRSR and effect commitment. Apply verifies the exact next Global command, certificate/payload equality, generated destination and slot bitmap, imports only the typed payload's remote identities under their two-sided grants, CAS-updates those slots, and advances exactly one contiguous durable prefix. A `NoShardEffect` row is admissible only inside the contiguous `MetaRelevanceRangeProof` path and cannot be wrapped as an empty projection. Audit visibility may remain fixed behind a fence but never decrease; only the generated audit-visibility row advances it. G0 and negative tests delete, duplicate, swap, or substitute every slot/payload/destination and require rejection.

`ShardControlAuthority<Tag>` is the closed tag `MetaAuthorized{authorization_ref:MetaAuthorizationEvidence<Tag>,authorized_effect_commitment}|ProtocolEvidenceAuthorized{evidence_ref:ProtocolAuthorizationEvidence<Tag>,protocol_kind,effect_commitment}|StructurallyInapplicable{system_authority_class,no_global_semantic_effect_proof}`. The generated associated types `MetaAuthorizationEvidence<Tag>` and `ProtocolAuthorizationEvidence<Tag>` are each either one exact typed `StrongRef<T>` or `CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<T>` schema or `Uninhabited`; no erased reference union exists. Every body tag has exactly one allowed authority arm/evidence target, and the outer evidence must byte-match the body input. Ambient shard-maintainer privilege cannot change trust, retention, keys, GC, backup, restore, format, topology, security, allocation, escrow, or visibility.

`ShardControlCommand` is the fence-free generated envelope `{command_version,kind,body,control_authority:ShardControlAuthority<kind>,expected_shard_raft_index,expected_state_conditions[],shard_pre_command_base_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ShardPreCommandBaseAvailabilityCertificate>}`. `command_contracts.toml` expands its closed v1 body union to one exact arm for: guarded recovery; irrelevant-range and typed Meta projection apply; remote-retention/trust release; checkpoint, initial floor, history cut, configuration and format projection; migration projection and service/reopen completion; certified allocation range install/disposition; constraint-owner and escrow grant stage/activate/freeze/disposition; derived-build progress/validation; key stage/activate/zero-reference/destruction; GC preflight, Semantic quarantine, Meta coordination projection, and Semantic physical-disposition import; backup pin/closure/release/abort; restore activation/visibility/prepare/open/reopen/abandon/pin-release/registry-retirement; topology copy/cutover/retirement; capability migration; and every other `MetaProjectionSlot` update whose generated effect row is not `NoShardEffect`. GC initialize, dispatch, provider status/receipt imports, cancellation fencing/evidence, roll-forward, successor generation, and physical evidence retirement are ShardMaintenanceCommand rows only. Each body is exactly `{spec_ref:StrongRef<ExactRegisteredInput>}` unless its contract row names a certified remote input directly. `RecoveryBridge` alone accepts `StructurallyInapplicable{RecoverySystemAuthority}` under `PendingRestoreCreationGuard`; every other authority arm and evidence target is tag-associated. Shards execute clipped DP fragments only under a Meta-certified `DpExecutionGrant` through the shard prepare/decision protocol; they never own a privacy-ledger or DP-charge control arm. They also never own semantic SchemaOps, branch heads, global allocation/escrow authority, audit visibility, or a Global result-delivery decision. There is no generic payload, authority, projection, or cleanup arm.

The D1 certificate, owner, authority, body, current domain/configuration, and expected conditions must match before proposal. The command cannot change global semantic values. `AdvanceIrrelevantRange` is legal only for an exhaustive `MetaRelevanceRangeProof`; topology, policy, revocation, key, audit-visibility, admission/decision, remote-retention, derived activation, backup/restore, GC, or target-data effects require their typed path and can never be skipped. `audit_visible_global_prefix` never decreases: unresolved pipeline candidates may hold it unchanged while durable apply advances, and only an exact generated visibility projection advances it. Never-registered and closed-attempt compaction powers remain Meta-only; neither a floor nor timeout may terminalize a prepared attempt.

Topology evidence is typed. `TopologyTransferPlan` is `{old_topology_ref,new_joint_topology_ref,new_final_topology_ref,affected_ranges,copy_basis_global_prefix,sorted_source_checkpoint_identities,object_and_suffix_inventory,destination_assignments,copy_crypto_profile,completion_predicate}`. A destination shard produces `TopologyTransferAck {SameGroupCertificateHeader,plan_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<TopologyTransferPlan>,destination_shard,received_inventory_digest,applied_through_global_prefix,destination_checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<ShardCheckpointCertificate>,signatures}`. Meta builds `TopologyAttemptClosureProof {SameGroupCertificateHeader,old_topology_epoch,cutover_global_prefix,registration_generation_range,sorted_attempt_evidence[(global_txn_id,registration_generation) -> Terminal{outcome_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord>,published_decision_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>?,old_owner_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardAppliedAck>]}|AbortForPostCutoverRetry{abort_outcome_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord>,published_abort_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,old_owner_release_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardAppliedAck>],retry_request_nonce,intent_commitment,old_to_future_attempt_mapping_commitment}],exact_old_epoch_active_and_admitted_set_commitment,conflict_index_basis,signatures}`. The sorted evidence is exhaustive against old-epoch attempt indexes. No new-epoch registration can preexist cutover: only a later meta command after ownership switches may create a new generation and link it to the recorded retry intent. A registration racing the cutover basis is either included/terminalized under the old epoch or rejected for later retry. `TopologyRetirementFloor` is `{cutover_record_ref:StrongRef<TopologyTransitionRecord>,through_global_prefix,old_owner_inventory,remaining_certified_remote_obligations,backup_floor,key_floor,retention_floor,prepare_floor,external_backup_and_key_receipt_refs[StrongRef],required_meta_ack_set,required_shard_ack_set}`. `TopologyRetirementAck` carries `SameGroupCertificateHeader` and `{floor_ref:LocalMeta{StrongRef<TopologyRetirementFloor>}|RemoteMeta{CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<TopologyRetirementFloor>},satisfied_floor_digest,signature}`; only meta encodes `LocalMeta`, and every shard encodes `RemoteMeta`.

Movement has three distinct pre-order global-control specs so apply never consumes ambient evidence. `TopologyCopyBeginSpec` is `{move_id,old_topology_ref,new_joint_topology_ref,new_final_topology_ref,affected_ranges,copy_basis_global_prefix,old/new_shard_configuration_refs[RemoteConfigurationRef],source_checkpoint_certificate_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardCheckpointCertificate>],transfer_plan_ref:StrongRef<TopologyTransferPlan>,expected_branch/key/allocation_epochs,terminal_audit_gate}`. `TopologyCutoverSpec` is `{move_id,copy_record_ref:StrongRef<TopologyTransitionRecord>,cutover_global_prefix,sorted_transfer_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<TopologyTransferAck>],new_owner_catchup_certificate_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardCheckpointCertificate>],active_attempt_closure_proof_ref:StrongRef<TopologyAttemptClosureProof>,old/new_shard_configuration_refs[RemoteConfigurationRef],expected_branch/key/allocation_epochs,terminal_audit_gate}` and proves every old-epoch registration has exhaustive Terminal or `AbortForPostCutoverRetry` evidence before ownership switches at the next prefix. `TopologyRetireOldSpec` is `{move_id,cutover_record_ref:StrongRef<TopologyTransitionRecord>,topology_retirement_floor_ref:StrongRef<TopologyRetirementFloor>,sorted_meta_ack_refs[StrongRef<TopologyRetirementAck>],sorted_shard_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<TopologyRetirementAck>],terminal_audit_gate}`. Each apply constructs one `TopologyTransitionRecord {move_id,phase:CopyInProgress|CutoverCommitted|OldOwnersRetired,command_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlCommand>,phase_predecessor:NoneForCopy|Copy{ref:StrongRef<TopologyTransitionRecord>}|Cutover{ref:StrongRef<TopologyTransitionRecord>},copy_basis_global_prefix,cutover_global_prefix?,affected_ranges,evidence_commitment,resulting_topology_ref,assigned_global_logical_command_seq}`. The predecessor is the immediately prior same-move phase; completed moves do not form an unbounded strong chain. Source bytes/keys remain live through final retirement and its two-sided remote floors.

**AdjRunSegment**: `{ graph, branch_projection, source_partition, direction, edge_type, source_ordinal_map_id, destination_map_table[{partition,ordinal_map_id}], source_ordinal_range, edge_count, seq_range, codec_table, offsets_ef, encoded_neighbor_lists, stable_eids, origin_birth_orders, branch_admission_orders?, marker_aware_retire_or_hole_data, property_locators, canonical_stable_id_digest, checksum }`. Each destination carries either stable VId or a destination-map-table slot plus ordinal. Mutable labels do not identify a primary run; per-list codec governs legal rank/select/skip operations.

**IdentityContinuityRecord / IdAllocatorState / IdRangeLease**: `IdentityContinuityRecord` binds `{database_security_namespace_id,allocation_epoch,predecessor_digest,external_registry_id,cas_version,status,issued_at,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`; its external registry/witness copy is the anti-rollback authority and its Chronicle copy is checked against it. Allocator state binds `{graph_id,element_kind,allocation_epoch,identity_continuity_record_ref,topology_epoch,per_partition_high_water,spent_range_commitments,exhausted_partitions}`. Pre-order `IdRangeGrantSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` binds the requested range, allocator basis, `authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>`, and issuance profile/window. `IdRangeLease<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,lease_id,graph,element_kind,allocation_epoch,partition,half_open_range,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,time_authority_profile_oid,issuance_not_before,issuance_not_after,permanent_spent_commitment,body_digest}`; its authenticated transcript covers the complete authority binding and profiled window. `ShardIdRangeLease` is the distinct shard-owned `{authority_projection:ShardAuthorityBindingProjection,meta_grant_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<MetaIdRangeGrantCertificate>,shard_id,lease_id,graph,element_kind,allocation_epoch,partition,half_open_range,shard_install_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef::Shard,time_authority_profile_oid,issuance_not_before,issuance_not_after,permanent_spent_commitment,body_digest}`. The applied tag must match the owning Local/Meta/Shard state machine and assigned position; role substitution or a digest-only authority projection is unencodable. Use consumes fresh `TimeValidationEvidence::Usable`; expiry ends issuance authority only through matching `Expired` evidence and every epoch/range pair remains permanently spent and can never overlap or recycle.

An identity becomes externally stable at its **first escape**, not at transaction commit. `AllocationValueKind` is the closed union `VertexId{graph,partition}|EdgeId{graph,partition}|GeneratedKey{graph,generator_id,key_type}`. `TxnAllocationSlotKey` is `{posture:Local|Sharded,attempt_identity,statement_seq_or_terminal,tmp_ref_ordinal,value_kind}` and is never a client value. `EscapingAllocationCause` is `PublishedStatementResult|LaterStatementWorkspace|ProcedureOrUdfBoundary|DurableProvenanceOrAudit|TerminalResultPreparation`; the `TerminalResultPreparation` arm is optional because a terminal-only value may instead be assigned in finalization before publication. `EscapingAllocationBinding` is `{slot_key,permanent_value,lease_ref:Local{lease_ref:StrongRef<IdRangeLease>}|Shard{lease_ref:StrongRef<ShardIdRangeLease>},lease_offset,escape_cause,first_escaping_statement_seq,canonical_binding_digest}`. The permanent value must equal the role/kind/epoch/partition/offset encoded by its lease; generated-key encoders additionally obey the registered generator profile. A binding is immutable, namespace-scoped, and cannot be repurposed for a different temporary reference, attempt, kind, or create.

Local orders `LocalEscapingAllocationReservationSpec {registration_ref:StrongRef<LocalAttemptRegistration>,expected_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<LocalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,expected_allocator_root:WeakStateIdentity,sorted_binding_recipes[],sorted_lease_refs[StrongRef<IdRangeLease>],fresh_lease_use_evidence_refs[StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>],expected_unconsumed_offsets_and_spent_commitment,resulting_allocation_binding_root_ref:StrongRef<TxnAllocationBindingRoot>,resulting_workspace_generation_recipe,terminal_audit_gate}` before the first escaping manifest/workspace/procedure object is constructed. Apply checks a canonical bijection between recipes and fresh offsets, marks every offset consumed in `IdAllocatorState`, installs `EscapingAllocationReservationRecord {source_spec_ref,applied_control_ref,registration_ref,sorted_bindings,resulting_binding_root_ref,permanent_spent_commitment}`, and advances the workspace generation atomically. The statement publication or later terminal preparation names that record and can encode only its permanent values. A lost response retries the same record and result bytes.

In Sharded posture, the shard owning each disjoint range first orders `ShardEscapingAllocationReserveSpec {registration_projection_ref,workspace_generation,lease_ref:StrongRef<ShardIdRangeLease>,sorted_binding_recipes_for_this_shard,fresh_lease_use_evidence_ref,expected_shard_allocator_root,resulting_shard_binding_root_ref}` and installs both the consumed-offset state and `ShardEscapingAllocationReservationRecord` in one shard command. Its quorum exports `ShardEscapingAllocationEvidence {SameGroupCertificateHeader,reservation_record_ref:StrongRef<ShardEscapingAllocationReservationRecord>,workspace_generation,sorted_bindings,permanent_spent_commitment,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`. Meta then orders `GlobalEscapingAllocationReservationSpec {registration_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAttemptRegistration>,expected_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,sorted_shard_evidence_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardEscapingAllocationEvidence>],complete_tmp_ref_to_permanent_value_bijection,resulting_global_binding_root_ref:StrongRef<TxnAllocationBindingRoot>,successor_workspace_generation_ref:StrongRef<GlobalTxnWorkspaceGeneration>,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply rejects a missing, duplicate, overlapping, foreign-range, wrong-workspace, or nonparticipant binding and makes the global workspace's mapping authoritative. Shard holds remain independently rooted until Meta publication and the ordinary two-sided retention release; a Meta failure cannot make a certified slot reusable.

`TxnAllocationBindingRoot` maps every escaped `TxnAllocationSlotKey` to exactly one binding and carries `{generation,attempt_identity,through_statement_seq,sorted_spent_commitments,root_digest}`. Rebase and finalization must preserve all existing leaves and may append only new escapes; if the logical create disappears, conflicts with a constraint, or changes kind/partition, the attempt aborts or records `AbandonedAllocationTombstone {binding_digest,attempt_terminal_identity,reason_class,permanent_spent_commitment}`. Abort, no-op, statement failure after reservation, lease expiry, compaction, branch fork/merge, topology movement, backup/restore, and same-identity recovery retain the spend through the allocator's cumulative spent commitment even after detailed binding evidence is cut. `AllocationBindingCompactionSpec` may replace closed-attempt detail only with a checked cumulative spent-range extension and exact tombstone commitment; it never returns capacity. Tests force crash/lost response before and after every Local, shard, and Meta publication; expose an ID to a later statement and force rebase; abort after exposure; retry under a new process; expire a lease; restore an old backup; move a partition; and prove that the visible value is stable and no offset is ever issued twice.

Confined temporary references also require durable allocation **before** their permanent values enter a capsule or shard effect basis; “assign in commit order” is not an ambient counter operation. `FinalizationAllocationPlan<Role>` is the sequence-neutral pre-order object `{role,registration_identity,admission_generation,finalization_generation,expected_allocation_binding_root_ref:StrongRef<TxnAllocationBindingRoot>,allocator_basis:WeakStateIdentity,topology_and_owner_basis,sorted_mappings[{slot_key,value_kind,permanent_value,lease_ref,lease_offset,source_intent_ordinal}],sorted_lease_use_evidence_refs[StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>],exact_before_lease_cursor_and_high_water_values,exact_after_lease_cursor_and_high_water_values,permanent_spent_extension_commitment,complete_confined_tmp_ref_bijection,exhaustion_and_checked_overflow_proof,body_digest}`. It excludes every previously escaped slot, has no future command/result/root, and must map every and only remaining create/generated-key reference in the normalized effect set.

Local `LocalFinalCertificationReserveSpec` names that plan, and its apply atomically updates `IdAllocatorState`, installs `FinalizationAllocationReservationRecord<Local> {plan_ref,registration_identity,finalization_generation,sorted_mappings,permanent_spent_extension_commitment,applied_control_ref,state:Active}`, and installs the Active final-certification/constraint/resource/escrow holds. Only afterward are final effects, terminal result preparation, capsule, and terminal audit bytes constructed, and each strongly names the reservation record. Commit atomically replaces it with `Consumed{terminal_input_identity,marker_identity}`. Cancel or abort replaces it with `AbandonedSpent{terminal_or_cancel_identity,tombstone_summary_ref}`; because durable plan/effect candidates may already name the values, capacity is never returned.

In Sharded posture, each allocation-owner shard orders `ShardFinalizationAllocationReserveSpec` before constructing `ShardEffectBasis`, consumes exact offsets in its authoritative leased-range ledger, and exports `ShardFinalizationAllocationEvidence {SameGroupCertificateHeader,reservation_record_ref:StrongRef<FinalizationAllocationReservationRecord<Shard>>,sorted_mappings,permanent_spent_extension_commitment,topology_and_configuration_basis,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`. The effect basis and routing certificate bind the exact certified evidence. `GlobalFinalCertificationReserveSpec` requires a canonical bijection over all such evidence and every remaining temporary reference, holds those reservations, and cannot remap them. Global commit/abort disposition is projected back to every shard; participant loss retains the spent reservation until recovery applies that disposition. Meta never guesses a shard's next slot and a shard never uses an uncertified Meta mapping.

`FinalizationAllocationTombstoneSummary` is the compact cumulative `{role_and_owner,allocation_epoch,sorted_spent_ranges_or_offsets,attempt_and_finalization_generation,terminal_disposition_commitment,predecessor_summary_digest:WeakDigest,body_digest}`. Checkpoint, configuration, backup/restore, role transition, topology movement, prepared/final-certification, and GC inventories retain every Active reservation and cumulative consumed/abandoned spend. Tests race two candidates on the same allocator basis, crash before/after reservation/capsule/audit/order, cancel and abort, force rebase/partition movement, expire leases, restore an old allocator root, and exhaust every high-water counter; exactly one mapping wins, overflow fail-closes, and no permanent value is ever reused.

**CommittedEffectCapsule**: `{ authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>, read_snapshot_basis, validated_apply_basis, intent_semantics_oid, read_graph_reference_bindings[], validated_write_coordinate_bindings[], validated_write_key_epoch_bindings[{graph,branch,envelope_epoch,write_key_epoch}], coordinate_schema_transitions[], topology_epoch, policy_epoch, revocation_index, effect_set_ref:StrongRef<CommittedEffectSet<Local>>, effect_directory_root, committed_effect_refs[ConditionalCoordinateRef], constraint_evidence, authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Local>>, authorization_decision_digest:WeakDigest, authorized_permit_scope_digest, resource_transition_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Local>>], resource_charge_effects, txn_outcome_preparation_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomePreparationRecord>, final_read_witnesses, final_write_summary, birth_ordinals, merge_record_ref:StrongRef<MergeRecord>?, logical_delta_template_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaTemplate>, logical_delta_template_digest:WeakDigest, optional_physical_accelerators[{apply_basis,digest,payload_ref:ConditionalCoordinateRef,answer_equivalence_proof_ref:StrongRef}], txn_token, final_effect_digest }`. Prepared traversal follows every conditional effect/accelerator strongly; after commit the marker supplies the coordinate and a matching effect cut may stop old optional accelerators/effects, while active derived generations independently retain bytes still used. Repeated digests cross-check typed refs. The header, effect set, decision, resources, template, bindings, key epochs, and final digest must agree. The capsule contains no future command/result or writer fence; the applied marker becomes resulting coordinate heads without entering this prebuilt object.

**CommitCommand / ControlCommand / LogicalCommandRecord / PreparedCommitRecord.** A transaction order unit is `{command_version,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,txn_token,final_certification_reservation_ref:StrongRef<LocalFinalCertificationReservation>,required_reservation_state:Active,order_attempt_input_ref:StrongRef<LocalOrderAttemptInput>,order_attempt_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate>,capsule_ref:StrongRef<CommittedEffectCapsule>,logical_delta_template_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaTemplate>,authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Local>>,resource_transition_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>],txn_outcome_preparation_ref:StrongRef<TxnOutcomePreparationRecord>,payload_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PayloadAvailabilityCertificate>,expected_branch_heads[WeakMarkerIdentity],expected_branch_key_epochs[{graph,branch,envelope_epoch,write_key_epoch}],topology_epoch,policy_epoch,revocation_index,final_effect_digest}`. A `ControlCommand` is `{command_version,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Local>,kind,schema_version,typed_payload_ref:StrongRef<SequenceNeutralSpec>,payload_availability_certificate_ref:None|Some{certificate_ref:StrongRef<PayloadAvailabilityCertificate>},order_attempt_input_ref:StrongRef<LocalOrderAttemptInput>,order_attempt_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalOrderAttemptAvailabilityCertificate>,expected_state_conditions[WeakStateIdentity|WeakMarkerIdentity|ExpectedEpoch|ExpectedIndex],authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Local>>}`; CAS expectations are comparison-only and never retain a prior root. The header, authorization decision, spec, reservation/order subject, and repeated namespace/policy/revocation values must byte-match one complete Local binding. The command's `LocalOrderSubject` must exactly match transaction reservation or control spec/conditions/audit freeze. The spec wrapper named by `typed_payload_ref` contains the one terminal audit gate. The generated `PayloadBearingControlKind` table requires `Some` for branch fork/grant bundles, remote-retention/config-anchor evidence publication, backup/restore, and any other spec whose stable closure adds payload bytes; that certificate excludes the future command and order supplement. Non-payload kinds require `None`; every semantic Local command separately carries order/HLC evidence through its replaceable order-attempt input/certificate.

Every typed sequence-neutral spec is canonically encoded once as `SequenceNeutralSpec<Tag> {wire_tag:u16,schema_version,body:Body<Tag>,terminal_audit_freeze:AuditFreezeField<Tag>,terminal_audit_gate:TerminalAuditGate}`; `GlobalSequenceNeutralSpec<Tag>` uses the identical wrapper with the separate Global tag registry. `AuditFreezeField<Tag>` is the generated closed associated type `Required{freeze_ref:the exact registered AuditTerminalFreezeRef arm}|Forbidden{compatibility_row_id}`. Every final protected terminal tag has exactly one Required arm; admitted Local/Meta writes require their admission-fence arm, protected Local/Meta controls require their scope-specific freeze arm, and nonterminal/scaffolding/audit-recovery tags can encode only Forbidden. Appendix prose flattens `body` fields into the surrounding braces for readability: shown audit fields are wrapper fields, not fields inside `Body<Tag>`; when a compact rendering omits them, `wire_types.toml` and `durable_fields.toml` still supply exactly one tag/version/freeze/gate row. No `ControlCommand` or `GlobalControlCommand` contains either audit field. `spec_body_digest` hashes exactly canonical `Body<Tag>` bytes; the domain-separated wrapper transcript and ObjectId include tag, version, body, freeze, and gate, while D1 and the command retain that complete wrapper. Apply revalidates and consumes the required armed freeze atomically with the protected terminal output. Golden encodings and negative fixtures prove that changing, removing, or substituting the freeze/gate leaves `spec_body_digest` unchanged but changes the wrapper ObjectId and is rejected by the tag-associated arm matrix.

The local `SequenceNeutralSpec` semantic union is generated from `command_contracts.toml`; it is exhaustive and has no hand-maintained allowlist. Its closed v1 family projection contains: guarded recovery/bootstrap; begin reservation and terminalization; attempt registration, ownership reattachment/renewal/expiry, statement publication/abort, prepare/read-close/commit/rollback/cancel, outcome compaction, capability-lineage transitions, and initial result detachment; first-escape and finalization allocation reserve/disposition; checkpoint/floor/history-cut/configuration/format transitions; remote retention/trust; branches and merge; ID allocation, valid-time, constraint, resource, and escrow state; policy, revocation, security binding, time authority, privacy continuity and DP; audit ticket admission, freeze, signing-plan/attempt, terminal, recovery, and completeness; bulk-load staging; derived-build/schema activation; keys; semantic GC authorization; semantic backup/restore lifecycle; and sharding freeze/unfreeze/role-transition initiation. Result activation/ACK/release/expiry/resume, audit visibility advancement, cursor/artifact transport disposition, prepared recovery, consensus hard-state, and purely physical progress live only in `LocalMaintenanceCommand`. Each concrete inner tag is a separate registry row with its exact body, result, authority, audit, availability, construction, plane, and state-slot contract.

Required Local semantic rows include `TxnOwnershipTransitionSpec<Local>`, `TxnOwnershipExpiryAbortSpec<Local>`, every `AllocationReservationTransitionSpec<Local>` and `FinalizationAllocationDispositionSpec<Local>`, `FormatTransitionSpec<Local>`, `EscrowRightsTransitionSpec`, `PrivacyContinuityImportSpec<Local>`, the exact §12.7 DP semantic transitions, `AuditTerminalPlanAbandonSpec<Local>`, `BulkLoadTransitionSpec<Local>`, `DerivedBuildTransitionSpec<Local>`, and every exact semantic backup/restore/GC/key transition. Required Local maintenance rows separately include `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Local>`, `ResultResumeTransitionSpec<Local>`, result activation/ACK/release/expiry, protocol-only artifact/cursor/replay-delivery tags, `GcPhysicalExecutionInitializeSpec<Local>`, `GcPhysicalDispatchMarkSentUnknownSpec<Local>`, every typed GC start/progress/completion/cancellation-receipt import, successor-generation and evidence-retirement spec, and the physical/protocol phases of backup/restore. W12 adds the exact Meta/Shard quarantine, participant cancellation-fence/roll-forward, Meta cancellation prepare/resolve/completion, participant cancelled-ack, progress, and aggregate-completion rows in their role-valid Semantic or Protocol registries. `AuditTicketResolutionBatchSpec` is absent. `TxnSubmissionSpec` is not an ordered input: transaction submission is the pre-order construction pipeline that yields `CommitCommand`. Ordinary DDL is a transaction-carried `SchemaOp`; the synthetic catalog/schema/index control types are forbidden. Audit visibility has no semantic mirror/fence command, DP has no `RecordCharge` or `RecordDelivery`, and result cleanup has no `ResetOrExpire` catchall. Missing, extra, or wrong-plane rows fail G0.

Exact local `ConfigurationTransitionSpec` is `{phase:ProposeJoint|CommitJoint|CommitNew|CommitRetirementFloor,basis_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_logical_command_seq,basis_commit_seq,basis_raft_index,current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,prospective_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,candidate_closure_proof_ref:StrongRef,paired_checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>?,paired_config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>,required_prepared/certificate/remote/key/audit/backup_obligation_floor,terminal_audit_gate}`; every phase is a separate command and final retirement proves the new configuration owns every obligation. The sole exact Local `GcProposal` and all GC state/transition schemas are defined under **PayloadReceipt / PayloadAvailabilityCertificate / ConfigPayloadFloor / GcEpoch** below; this registry projection intentionally does not repeat an abbreviated second body. Follower recomputation must derive the identical bounded set, and only its applied `GcDecisionRecord` may authorize the later Semantic quarantine command.

A replica-local `PreparedCommitRecord` is `{prepared_ownership_id,prepare_id,record_generation,predecessor_ref:StrongRef<PreparedCommitRecord>?,database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role:Local,group_id,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,payload_predicate_digest,local_writer_fence_epoch,txn_token,capsule_ref:StrongRef,command_ref:StrongRef?,base_closure_inventory_digest,current_closure_inventory_digest,strong_logical_refs[StrongRef],exact_realizations[{object_or_subobject_id,encoding_id,placement_id,key_opener_ref:StrongRef}],status:Preparing|Receipted|Ordered{raft_index}|Applied{logical_command_seq,commit_seq}|Abandoned{fence_proof_digest},receipt_nonce}`. Recomputing its ownership ID and every receipt transcript includes the exact database/incarnation/Local group/configuration OID and predicate digest. `LocalPreparedPayloadRoot` maps the stable ownership ID to the latest immutable successor through the role-specific entry type above. Every successor repeats the same domain/config/predicate/base inventory and has an identical or superset current closure; a receipt commitment is retained. `Receipted`/`Ordered` ownership cannot be removed by timeout or crash; only the fenced terminal rules in §5.2 and a committed GC decision may release it. On apply, ownership transfers to the command record/marker/logical state/outcome index before the prepared entry becomes terminal.

**LogicalDeltaTemplate / LogicalDeltaBatch.** A template is `{format,intent_semantics_ref:StrongRef<IntentSemantics>,final_effect_digest,source_intent_root_digest,coordinate_entries[]}`, where entries are canonically sorted and each is `{graph,branch,relation_id,validated_schema_binding,schema_transition:None|Some{transition_ref:StrongRef<SchemaTransition>},delta_payload_ref:ConditionalCoordinateRef,delta_payload_digest}`. The payload is a canonical exact Z-set over `CanonicalRow`; duplicate coordinates/relations, zero weights, noncanonical order, a transition inconsistent with its binding, or a payload not equal to the capsule's before/after effects are invalid. Prepared traversal follows every conditional edge as strong; committed traversal supplies the enclosing marker sequence and may apply only a matching `EffectCut`.

`LogicalDeltaBatch<Role>` has a generated common body `{format,role,coordinate_entries[{source_binding,resulting_binding,relation_id,delta_payload_ref:RegisteredStrongRef<DeltaPayload>}],batch_digest}` and exactly two role schemas. `LogicalDeltaBatch<LocalCommitted>` adds `{source_template_digest:WeakDigest,commit_marker_identity:WeakMarkerIdentity,commit_seq,frontier:Local{commit_seq}}`. `LogicalDeltaBatch<ShardFragment>` adds `{source_fragment_basis_digest:WeakDigest,decision_identity:WeakGlobalCommandIdentity,shard_id,global_logical_command_seq,global_commit_seq,frontier:ShardFragment{global_commit_seq}}`. `batch_digest` covers every preceding canonical field and is the batch idempotency key; the object's full ObjectId is `LogicalDeltaBatchId<Role>`. Local apply/rematerialization and shard decision apply must reproduce the same role-specific object. A retained batch owns payloads directly but does not retain template/fragment, marker/decision, capsule, or predecessor history. Only `LocalCommitted` may inhabit `CommittedDeltaSourceRef::Local`; shard fragments are internal inputs to a `GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch` and are never a public CDC source.

Delta indexes are bounded authenticated windows, not append-only strong maps. `LocalDeltaBatchIndex {format,retained_after_commit_seq,frontier,entries[every commit_seq in (retained_after_commit_seq,frontier] -> StrongRef<LogicalDeltaBatch<LocalCommitted>>],retired_prefix_commitment,active_cut_ref:StrongRef<ActivatedDeltaRetentionCut<Local>>?}` and `GlobalDeltaBatchIndex {format,retained_after_global_commit_seq,frontier,entries[every global_commit_seq in (retained_after_global_commit_seq,frontier] -> StrongRef<GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch>],retired_prefix_commitment,active_cut_ref:StrongRef<ActivatedDeltaRetentionCut<Meta>>?}` require a gap-free exact map in their retained interval. `ShardFragmentDeltaBatchIndex {format,shard_id,retained_after_global_commit_seq,observed_through_global_prefix,entries[participating committed global_txn_id/global_commit_seq -> StrongRef<LogicalDeltaBatch<ShardFragment>>],nonparticipant_or_noncommit_coverage_commitment,retired_prefix_commitment,active_cut_ref:StrongRef<ActivatedDeltaRetentionCut<Shard>>?}` requires an exact bijection with every locally applied committed participant decision in its retained interval; it does not invent fragments for unrelated global commits.

`DeltaRetentionObligationRef<Role>` is the generated closed union of role-valid cursor/delivery, derived-generation, replay, backup/export, CDC, shard-ack/global-publication, and explicit administrative history promises. `DeltaRetentionCutEvidence<Role> {role_and_group,basis_index_digest,cut_through_position,exact_sorted_obligation_refs[],transferred_batch_owner_refs[],released_or_gap_transition_refs[],authoritative_rematerialization_availability_or_retirement_proof,complete_obligation_bijection,checker_version}` proves that every promise at or below the cut has either moved to an independent strong owner, completed/released, or taken an explicit `GAP`/new-baseline transition. `ProvisionalDeltaRetentionCut<Role>` may authenticate inherited absence but authorizes no new edge removal. The role-specific ordered `LocalDeltaBatchRetentionCutSpec`, `GlobalDeltaBatchRetentionCutSpec`, or `ShardFragmentDeltaRetentionCutSpec` may install `ActivatedDeltaRetentionCut<Role>` and advance `retained_after_*` only after the paired checkpoint/floor and ordinary activated retention cut cover the same position. Apply replaces old strong entries with the prefix commitment in the same state transition; a missing retained entry is corruption, while a query below a proved cut returns `HistoryUnavailable` or the already-recorded gap contract. Checkpoint/configuration floors and backup/restore inventories retain the current index, active cut, and every still-live independent owner. Crash, lease-transfer, concurrent cursor, cut replacement, and rematerialization tests cover every boundary; no checkpoint cut silently invalidates a promised resume range.

**DeliveredBaselinePayload / DeliveredDeltaPayload / DeltaDeliveryEnvelope / resume cursor and lease registry.** `DeliveredBaselinePayload` is `{format,baseline_id:OpaquePublicNonce,derived_identity_digest,source_coverage,baseline_frontier,authorized_cursor_position,output_schema,result_answer_contract,canonical_encoding_version,canonical_row_segment_refs[StrongRef],canonical_payload_len,internal_baseline_digest,public_baseline_digest}`. `InternalBaselineDigest` is exactly `BLAKE3("fgdb:internal-baseline:v1" || canonical(format,baseline_id,derived_identity_digest,source_coverage,baseline_frontier,authorized_cursor_position,output_schema,result_answer_contract,canonical_encoding_version,canonical_payload_len,exact_concatenated_canonical_result_bytes))`; it is never a default wire value. `PublicBaselineDigest` is exactly `BLAKE3("fgdb:public-baseline:v1" || canonical(format,baseline_id,authorized_cursor_position,output_schema,result_answer_contract,canonical_encoding_version,canonical_payload_len,exact_concatenated_canonical_result_bytes))`. Both transcripts exclude storage-only keyed segment refs and their digest fields; only the public transcript excludes the derived identity, source coverage, and raw frontier. `DeliveredDeltaPayload` is `{format,output_schema,result_answer_contract,canonical_encoding_version,canonical_zset_segment_refs[StrongRef],canonical_payload_len,output_payload_digest}` with exact transcript `BLAKE3("fgdb:delivered-zset:v1" || canonical(format,output_schema,result_answer_contract,canonical_encoding_version,canonical_payload_len,exact_canonical_zset_bytes))`, likewise excluding segment refs and itself. Mutation/golden tests independently change every included and excluded field and include low-entropy coverage/frontier dictionary attacks against the public digest.

`CommittedDeltaSourceRef` is exactly `Local{batch_ref:StrongRef<LogicalDeltaBatch>,commit_seq}|Global{batch_ref:StrongRef<GlobalLogicalDeltaBatch>,global_commit_seq}`. `DeltaDeliveryEnvelope<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,format,derived_identity_digest,baseline_id,start_frontier,resulting_frontier,delivery_id:OpaquePublicNonce,delivery_sequence,authorized_cursor_position,output_payload_ref:StrongRef<DeliveredDeltaPayload>,output_payload_digest,provenance:Atomic{source:CommittedDeltaSourceRef,source_batch_digest}|Coalesced{source_role:Local|Global,first_batch_id,last_batch_id,ordered_source_commitment,source_count},internal_delivery_digest}`. Local and Global frontiers/source IDs have distinct internal tags; W12 emits only Meta-published Global sources, never shard `LogicalDeltaBatch` fragments, while migrated outstanding leases preserve Local but cannot be delivered after a service-epoch change without the explicit posture-transition baseline. The full header byte-matches the cursor, derived identity, payload contract, policy, and release authorization. `InternalDeliveryDigest` hashes every canonical envelope field except itself, so it commits authority, service epoch, role, output, and full provenance. `AuthorizedDeltaPublicProvenance` and `PublicDeliveryDigest` are separately constructed exactly as §9.5 and Appendix D specify; neither can acquire a hidden envelope field except through the Declassified arm's explicit authorization and audit release. `DeliveryId` is the pre-minted opaque public nonce, not an ObjectId or digest of the internal envelope. `delivery_id`, `baseline_id`, `delivery_lease_id`, `resume_cursor_id`, and every successor capability nonce are pre-minted in the input transition spec.

`DeliveryFrontier = Local{commit_seq,batch_frontier}|Global{global_commit_seq,batch_frontier}` is internal-only. The exact `SubscriptionCursorDeliveryRecord`, `PendingCursorOutputRecord`, and `SubscriptionDeliveryLeaseRecord` schemas are those in §9.5; no Appendix rendering may add a raw applied Local/Global command sequence to a public projection. `SubscriptionDeliveryTransitionSpec<Role>` is generated across the Protocol maintenance tags `ReleaseInitialVisibility|ActivateInitialBaseline|AckBaselineComplete|PublishDeltaPending|ReleaseDeltaVisibility|ActivateDelta|AckDeltaComplete|PublishResetPending|ReleaseResetVisibility|ActivateResetNotice|AcknowledgeReset|ActivateResetBaseline|RotateCapability|PauseForDisconnect|ResumeAfterDisconnect|PostActivationCursorTransition|CloseCursor|ExpireCursor`; only `CreateSubscriptionCursorSpec<Role>` is semantic and uses `AtomicProtocolDetach`. ACK transitions require exact durable END-send completion plus authenticated receipts, remove exact leases, and retain current/immediate-predecessor replay refs through successor acknowledgement or subject terminality. Reset owns a successor baseline payload/policy before selection but creates its delivery lease only after reset ACK. The posture transition creates a Sharded PendingInitialVisibility baseline under the promoted epoch; no Global delta exists before its ACK, no old-epoch token resumes it, and no range crosses roles. Capability bytes authorize lookup only through rooted cursor lineage.

**CommitMarker and physical batching.** A canonical marker is independently hashed:

```text
CommitMarker {
  logical_command_seq:u64, commit_seq:u64,
  effect_source:
    Local { capsule_ref:StrongRef<CommittedEffectCapsule>,
            logical_delta_template_digest:WeakDigest }
    | Global { global_txn_input_ref:ConditionalGlobalTxnInputRef,
               routing_and_participant_commitment:WeakDigest,
               final_effect_digest:WeakDigest,
               aggregate_template_digest:WeakDigest },
  prev_global:ConditionalMarkerRef{axis=Global}?,
  head_updates[{graph,branch,
                expected_previous:ConditionalMarkerRef{axis=Branch(graph,branch)}?}],
  merge_record_oid?, coordinate_schema_transition_digest,
  topology_epoch, policy_epoch, revocation_index,
  txn_token, commit_hlc,
  final_effect_digest,
  authorization_decision_digest, resource_effect_digest,
  payload_availability_certificate_oid?, flags, chain_hash
}
```

The marker itself is the implicit new head for each update. Its effect-source tag is fixed by posture: local apply uses the already-built capsule/template pair; W12 meta apply uses `ConditionalGlobalTxnInputRef {command_oid,assigned_global_logical_command_seq}` to the already-built transaction input plus routing/participant/effect/template commitments. The later global record at that exact sequence must name the same command. Before a matching global command cut, traversal follows the conditional input into the full prepared closure; afterward the head marker remains valid without permanently resurrecting remote prepare grants or effect bytes. A reader that does not know the required W12 tag rejects it. The marker deliberately does not name the terminal outcome or any future record/fragment/global batch; apply constructs the marker first and later objects may name it, avoiding outcome/distributed-batch cycles. `chain_hash` hashes the prior chain value plus marker bytes excluding `chain_hash`. `MarkerBatch {first_seq,last_seq,previous_batch_digest,entry_directory[{marker_oid,offset,length}],canonical_marker_bytes,mmr_update,checksum}` is only a physical pack; object locations resolve each marker independently.

**RecoveryCheckpoint / CheckpointStateVector / RetentionMap.** A checkpoint is `{basis_logical_command_seq,basis_commit_seq,command_basis:Genesis{command_chain_genesis_seed}|Ordered{covered_basis_logical_command:WeakCommandIdentity,covered_command_chain_hash},marker_basis:NoCommittedTransactionAtBasis{marker_chain_genesis_seed,proof_ref:StrongRef<NoCommittedTransactionAtBasis>}|Committed{covered_global_marker:StrongMarkerRef,covered_marker_chain_hash},basis_payload_digest:WeakDigest,basis_projection_digest,logical_hlc_state,checkpoint_state_vector_ref:StrongRef<CheckpointStateVector>,nonretaining_predecessor_checkpoint_digest}`. `command_basis=Genesis` is legal iff `basis_logical_command_seq=0`; otherwise Ordered is required. `marker_basis=NoCommittedTransactionAtBasis` is legal iff `basis_commit_seq=0`; otherwise Committed is required. Thus a control-only operational database can checkpoint without inventing a marker, while any positive transaction prefix must name the exact marker. The weak command identity and chain/payload/projection commitments authenticate the cut without retaining prior command/spec/checkpoint chains. The vector is generated from the exact versioned payload schema and has one typed entry per field: `CopiedStrong`, `ProjectedSnapshot`, `ResetByCheckpointInstall`, `SelfExcluded`, or the single constrained `InstallProvisionalCut{field_tag=retention_map_root,retention_cut_body_ref,checkpoint_binding=InstallThisCheckpoint,install_input_kind,assigned_order_literal}`. That fifth tag is required exactly once for an operational basis and forbidden for Genesis; it commits the already-built checkpoint-free body and deterministic applied-result recipe without a future checkpoint/result OID. `recovery_checkpoint_ref` always uses `InstallThisCheckpoint`; operational `config_payload_floor_ref` uses `InstallPairedConfigFloor`, while Genesis copies absence. `basis_projection_digest = BLAKE3("fgdb:checkpoint-projection:v1" ‖ canonical(vector reconstruction with exact literals))`; it never hashes a future checkpoint/floor/cut OID. A missing, duplicate, illegally self-excluded, or otherwise unclassified field is invalid. Control-only checkpoint/cut/recovery fixtures cover every `(logical_seq>0,commit_seq=0)` crash point.

`CheckpointInstallSpec` is `{basis:WeakStateIdentity,basis_logical_command_seq,basis_commit_seq,basis_payload_digest:WeakDigest,basis_projection_digest,checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>,checkpoint_state_vector_digest,paired_config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>?,retention_cut_body_ref:StrongRef<RetentionCutBody>?,reset_field_tags[]}`. It must be the first retained record at `basis_logical_command_seq+1`; apply verifies the exact current basis/vector, installs its own already-known checkpoint ref, and, for every operational basis, atomically installs the required paired floor and deterministic provisional cut whose checkpoint/vector/prefix and weak observations match. `retention_cut_body_ref=None` is legal only for Genesis, where no prior absence needs re-attestation. Genesis advances to `CheckpointInstalled`; later installs preserve `Operational`, apply only declared resets, and otherwise preserve every field. `InitialConfigFloorInstallSpec {checkpoint_installed_state:WeakStateIdentity,checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>,checkpoint_state_vector_digest,initial_config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>,initial_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,expected_logical_command_seq,terminal_audit_gate}` is the sole local `CheckpointInstalled -> Operational` transition.

The checkpoint-free pre-order cut-recipe union is `CommandCutRecipe {axis:Logical,stop_at:WeakCommandIdentity} | GlobalCommandCutRecipe {axis:GlobalLogical,stop_at:WeakGlobalCommandIdentity,exact_covered_global_prefix} | ShardCommandCutRecipe {axis:ShardRaft,stop_at:WeakShardCommandIdentity,exact_covered_shard_raft_index,exact_covered_global_prefix} | MarkerCutRecipe {axis,stop_at:WeakMarkerIdentity} | EffectCutRecipe {graph,branch,through_seq,effect_payload_digest_range}`. `RetentionCutBody` is `{role:Local|Meta|Shard{shard_id},basis_state_identity:WeakStateIdentity,command_cut_recipe,sorted_marker_cut_recipes[],sorted_effect_cut_recipes[],covered_policy_epoch,covered_coordinate_set_commitment,verified_first_retained_order,completeness_digest}` and must contain the role's one command recipe plus every marker/effect recipe to be superseded. It contains **no checkpoint identity or reference** and therefore exists before the vector/checkpoint that will consume it. Construction is strictly `RetentionCutBody -> CheckpointStateVector -> RecoveryCheckpoint -> checkpoint-install input`. The vector's `InstallProvisionalCut` entry means “attach this body to `InstallThisCheckpoint` at the assigned install order”; it never points backward from the body to that checkpoint.

Apply deterministically constructs `ProvisionalRetentionCutSet {body_ref:StrongRef<RetentionCutBody>,checkpoint_ref:Local{StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>}|Meta{StrongRef<GlobalRecoveryCheckpoint>}|Shard{StrongRef<ShardRecoveryCheckpoint>},install_input_ref:Local{StrongRef<ControlCommand>,assigned_logical_command_seq}|Meta{StrongRef<GlobalControlCommand>,assigned_global_logical_command_seq}|Shard{StrongRef<ShardControlCommand>,assigned_shard_raft_index},basis_projection_digest:WeakDigest}` before constructing the payload. Role, basis, recipe, checkpoint/vector, assigned order, and install-input fields must match exactly. The checkpoint and input are already known at apply time; the future result root/record is not named. The outer state root and command head then prove the corresponding applied record.

`HistoryCutActivationSpec {provisional_cut_ref:StrongRef<ProvisionalRetentionCutSet>,checkpoint_install_record_ref:StrongCommandRef,expected_retention_map_basis:WeakStateIdentity,verified_contiguous_suffix_start,terminal_audit_gate}` is the sole local strengthening path; Global/Shard use the matching global/shard record tags. Apply creates `ActivatedRetentionCutSet {provisional_cut_ref:StrongRef<ProvisionalRetentionCutSet>,checkpoint_install_record_ref:StrongCommandRef|StrongGlobalCommandRef|StrongShardCommandRef,activation_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}` and atomically replaces that provisional map entry. `RetentionMap` contains policy epochs and exactly one `Provisional|Activated` entry for each retained cut body. Operational checkpoint reset atomically drops superseded entries and installs the new provisional set; at every crash point, either the old activated authority or the new provisional authority authenticates already-missing bytes. Traversal may stop only when axis/group/checkpoint/body completeness and the provisional input+assigned-order or activated record proof all validate. Strong roots always win. A cut transfers authority to checkpoint+install+suffix; it does not assert that pruned capsules or old delta ranges remain individually recoverable. Recovery/crash tests begin with bytes already reclaimed under the prior cut and interrupt every install/activation step for all three roles.

**Constraint state.** Constraint identity is sequence-neutral. `ConstraintCoordinateKey` is `{graph,branch,schema_root_oid,schema_epoch}` and contains no selected head or future marker; the enclosing branch-head/payload association supplies system time. `ConstraintCanonicalizationProfile` fixes scalar equality/order/hash encoding, Unicode normalization/collation, Decimal coercion/scale, signed-zero and NaN key behavior, null policy `NullForbidden|NullsDistinct|NullsEqual`, tuple encoding, valid-time endpoint domain/precision, and all profile ObjectIds. No session or ambient schema default participates.

`ConstraintDefinition` has common `{constraint_id,domain_id,coordinate_schema_key,scope_predicate_ref:StrongRef<BoundedConstraintPredicate>,canonicalization_profile_ref:StrongRef<ConstraintCanonicalizationProfile>,disclosure_contract,ownership_profile,evaluator_semantics_oid}` plus exactly one body: `PropertyExistence|PropertyType|Check{restricted_expression_ref}|Unique{composite_property_roles}|Key{composite_property_roles}|Range{lower,upper,bound_flags}|Regex{pattern_ref,regex_profile_oid}|EdgeEndpoint{source_label_set,target_label_set,edge_type}|Cardinality{anchor_role,direction,edge_type,endpoint_predicate,min,max}|Referential{source_key_roles,target_constraint_id}|ValidTimeNonOverlap{partition_key_roles,interval_contract_id}`. Check/regex predicates are deterministic fuel/memory-bounded IR and cannot call native code. A generated table assigns every body one state-map kind, witness/evidence kind, owner function, merge law, and disclosure behavior.

`CanonicalConstraintDomainKey` is `Equality{canonical_tuple}|Cardinality{anchor_element,direction,edge_type,endpoint_partition}|Reference{target_constraint_id,canonical_tuple}|Temporal{canonical_partition_tuple}|Element{element_id}`. `ConstraintStateValue` is `EqualityOwner{element_id}|CardinalityAggregate{count:u128}|ReferenceAggregate{target_element,source_count:u128}|TemporalIntervalSet{interval_tree_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalIntervalTree>}`. `ConstraintStateRoot` is `{format_version,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,coordinate:ConstraintCoordinateKey,state_generation,definition_set_ref:StrongRef<ConstraintDefinitionSet>,equality_map_root,cardinality_map_root,reference_map_root,temporal_interval_map_root,body_digest}`. Row-local existence/type/check/range/regex/endpoint constraints have validation evidence but no fabricated map entry. `ConstraintStateDirectoryRoot` maps every coordinate key to one root; fork creates a new outer coordinate entry with immutable structural sharing, and no state root retains its predecessor.

`ConstraintStateMutation` is the closed union `SetEquality{constraint_id,key,before:None|Owner,after:None|Owner}|SetCardinality{constraint_id,key,before:u128,after:u128}|SetReference{constraint_id,key,before:None|ReferenceAggregate,after:None|ReferenceAggregate}|SetTemporalInterval{constraint_id,partition_key,interval_identity,before:None|CanonicalInterval,after:None|CanonicalInterval}`. Mutations are unique and sorted by `(constraint_id,domain_key,subkey)`, contain no no-op, use checked arithmetic, and express the net change after labels, properties, cascades, endpoints, slice-set changes, deletes, imports, and rebase. Construction is candidate after maps/root; then `ConstraintMutationBatch {coordinate,apply_basis:WeakMarkerIdentity,before_root_ref:StrongRef<ConstraintStateRoot>,after_root_ref:StrongRef<ConstraintStateRoot>,sorted_mutations,removed_and_added_contribution_commitment,body_digest}`; then `ConstraintValidationEvidence {mutation_batch_ref,complete_affected_definition_bitmap,sorted_row_local_evidence,sorted_positive_negative_gap_range_interval_witness_refs,owner_reservation_refs,authorization_and_disclosure_binding,canonicalization_profile_commitment,reference_evaluator_version,body_digest}`; then `ConstraintStateTransition {transition_id,mutation_batch_ref,validation_evidence_ref,body_digest}`; then the effect. None names a future marker/command/record/payload. Apply requires the exact current before root and basis and atomically installs graph effects plus the after root.

Local exclusion uses `LocalConstraintReservationRecord {reservation_id,owner:{registration_ref,prepare_admission_ref,admission_generation},sorted_domain_keys_and_ranges,before_root_commitment,reservation_generation,state:Held|Consumed{terminal_input_identity}|Released{abort_or_cancel_identity}}` and `LocalConstraintReservationRoot`, which maps every held key/range to one latest reservation. Acquisition uses canonical coordinate/constraint/key order; overlaps have one deterministic winner and no clock expiry. Final certification holds the complete set; commit consumes and abort/cancel releases exactly it. W12 derives `ConstraintOwnerAssignment=MetaOwned|ShardOwned{shard_id}` solely from topology, ownership profile, constraint ID, and canonical key. Shard roots store owned maps, contribution state, and prepare reservations; Global root stores Meta maps plus `(constraint,domain,shard)->contribution` and exact aggregates. Participant certificates commit before/after contributions; Meta verifies a complete owner/reservation/contribution bijection and updates the aggregate, and shard decision applies the certified local transition. Constraint DDL is an ordinary SchemaEffect with pinned shadow validation, contiguous delta catch-up, zero-violation evidence, and atomic new-epoch/root activation. Bulk import, branch merge, and role activation use this same path.

Constraint state is synchronous canonical truth, not a Beacon index. Unrepairable bytes make affected writes fail closed; a verified rebuild must reproduce the expected ObjectId. Tests cover every scalar/composite/null/collation/Decimal/signed-zero/NaN key law; labels/properties entering/leaving domains; parallel edges/endpoints/cardinality/cascades/references; valid-time boundaries/slices; Local reservation races/recovery/final holds; fork/merge/history; DDL catch-up/bulk/rebase; corrupt-root rebuild; every W12 owner/topology/prepare/abort/reconfiguration/contribution omission; and hidden-domain noninterference.

**Resource accounting.** `ResourceRequest` is `{ephemeral:EphemeralResourceBudget,durable:DurableChargeVector}`. Ephemeral budget is `{cpu_ns,resident_bytes,temp_bytes,read_iops:WindowedRateBudget,write_iops:WindowedRateBudget,egress_bytes,output_bytes,output_rows,max_snapshot_age,maximum_subscription_backlog}` and is enforced by cancel-correct runtime obligations, never canonical state. Durable vector is the fixed versioned checked nonnegative `{canonical_durable_bytes,retained_history_bytes,branch_count,index_count,view_count,subscription_count}`. Replica/FEC/cache/placement bytes are operational capacity, not semantic tenant charges. Only `ResourceAccountingRole::Local|Meta` authors canonical durable quota; shards enforce ephemeral local capacity.

`QuotaPath` is a canonical nonempty root-to-leaf path of `Database|Tenant|Graph|Branch|Feature`. `ResourceLimitPolicy` fixes the path tree, per-bucket hard vectors, protected maintenance reserve, class rules, and `ResourceLimitPolicyEpoch`. Every transition updates the exact leaf and each unique ancestor once. `ResourceOwnerKey` is `Attempt{posture,registration_identity}|Statement{posture,registration_identity,statement_seq}|ResultDelivery{posture,result_delivery_id}|Subscription{cursor_id}|DurableObject{object_class,stable_logical_owner_key}|Replay{replay_manifest_key}|BackupPin{backup_id,scope}|PreparedClosure{prepared_ownership_id}|Maintenance{job_id,registered_class}` and never a future ObjectId.

`ResourceLedgerState` is `{format_version,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,role,ledger_generation,limit_policy_ref:StrongRef<ResourceLimitPolicy>,limit_policy_epoch,bucket_state_root,reservation_entry_root,charge_entry_root,applied_transition_index_root,body_digest}`. Each bucket is `{hard_limit,protected_maintenance_reserve,ordinary_reserved,maintenance_reserved,committed}`. All arithmetic is checked; ordinary use satisfies `committed+ordinary_reserved <= hard_limit-protected_reserve`, and registered maintenance consumes only its named reserve. The transition index maps a transition ID to immutable body digest and applied outcome, making exact retry idempotent and body drift invalid.

`ResourceLedgerBasis` is the nonretaining `{role,state_identity:WeakStateIdentity,ledger_generation,ledger_root_digest,limit_policy_oid,limit_policy_epoch}`. `ResourceLedgerTransition<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the sequence-neutral pre-order object `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,transition_id,idempotency_key_digest,basis:ResourceLedgerBasis,owner:ResourceOwnerKey,resource_class,quota_path,operation,exact_sorted_before_buckets,exact_sorted_after_buckets,checked_arithmetic_commitment,authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>>,release_or_measurement_evidence_ref?,body_digest}`. `operation` is exactly `Reserve{reservation_id,reservation_generation:0,vector,expiry:None|ProfiledDeadline}|Charge{reservation_id,expected_reservation_generation,charge_id,vector,release_unused_reservation:true,stable_subject_key}|Release{subject:Reservation|Charge,expected_generation,exact_vector,release_authority_ref}|Expire{reservation_id,expected_generation,exact_vector,owner_quiescence_proof_ref,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>}|Transfer{subject:Reservation|Charge,expected_generation,source_owner,source_path,target_owner,target_path,exact_conserved_vector,transfer_authority_ref}|Adjust{charge_id,expected_generation,before_vector,after_vector,stable_subject_key,measurement_or_logical_change_proof_ref}`. The header, basis role, owner, decision, and containing command must byte-match. Expire evidence must be `Expired` for the exact subject/action.

Reserve creates one active reservation. Charge consumes it completely, creates one charge, and releases the deterministic unused remainder. Release/Expire/Transfer operate on a whole entry; partial changes use Adjust. Transfer conserves the vector globally and checks destination ancestors after netting shared ancestors. No timeout, disconnect, process death, logical delete, or stale cache releases an entry, and expiry fails while prepared ownership, final certification, result delivery, backup, or remote grants retain the subject. `ResourceLedgerTransitionSpec {expected_basis,sorted_transition_refs,complete_transition_id_set_commitment,semantic_subject_basis,terminal_audit_gate}` is the standalone-control wrapper; transactions and exact lifecycle specs embed transition refs directly. A generated enclosing-command matrix fixes legal operation tags.

Apply verifies one common basis, IDs, exact before buckets, canonical net deltas, policy/authorization/evidence and constructs the new state, then `ResourceLedgerAppliedRecord {command_ref,assigned_role_logical_position,transition_refs,before_root_ref,after_root_ref}`, then payload/command record. Pre-order transitions never name the applied record or after payload. `ResourceChargeEffect` is exactly `{transition_ref:StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition>,stable_subject_key,before_vector,after_vector}` and the effect set requires a generated bijection with transaction-owned transition refs. Graph/object and ledger state apply atomically. Tests exhaust arithmetic boundaries, ancestor races, idempotency drift, remainder release, shared-ancestor transfer, destination insufficiency, every hold/release/expiry race, every crash/root/audit publication cut, Local/Meta differential replay, and checkpoint/backup/restore/activation/GC coverage.

**Local transaction cross-reference.** The sole Local schemas for `TxnOutcomePreparationRecord`, `TxnAbortSpec`, `TxnOutcomeRecord`, and `TxnOutcomeIndex` are the exact BeginStaging/Registered/PrepareAdmitted/TerminalPending definitions in **Local 1.0 durable transaction formats** above. There is no `TxnSubmissionSpec`, `Submitted`, or `Absent -> terminal` alternate schema. W12 uses separately named `GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord`, `GlobalTxnOutcomeRecord`, and global indexes. G0 rejects duplicate logical-schema names or a second body hash for any ObjectKind. A `TxnOutcomeCapability` authenticates lookup/retry of the posture-tagged record and never grants mutation authority.

**BranchManifest / branch key-envelope DAG.** A branch manifest is `{graph,branch_id,origin:Genesis{creation_checkpoint_or_genesis_basis:GenesisBranchBasis}|Fork{parent_branch,parent_head:StrongMarkerRef,fork_boundary_logical_command_seq,boundary_reservation_identity},base_checkpoint_ref?,key_envelope_root_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,current_envelope_epoch,current_write_key_epoch,policy}`. The Genesis tag forbids parent/head/boundary fields; Fork requires all four and verifies parent ancestry/head/epochs at the exact already assigned `BranchEpochBoundaryReservation` boundary. The later install command sequence is deliberately absent. Both wire tags are registered and cross-tag bytes are noncanonical.

Expensive key closures never depend on a future install sequence. `BranchEpochBoundaryReserveSpec {reservation_id,operation:Fork|SnapshotGrant|StartOrContinueAdvancingGrant|RevokeAdvancingGrant|Rotate,graph,branch,parent_or_target_scope,expected_head:WeakMarkerIdentity,expected_envelope_epoch,expected_write_epoch,transition_nonce,complete_affected_coordinate_set,expected_boundary_index_absence,terminal_audit_gate}` is ordered first. Apply validates current state and creates immutable `BranchEpochBoundaryReservation {source_spec_ref:StrongRef<BranchEpochBoundaryReserveSpec>,reservation_id,operation,graph_branch_scope,frozen_head_and_epoch_commitment,boundary_logical_command_seq,transition_nonce,exclusive_branch_head_and_epoch_hold_root_ref:StrongRef<BranchEpochBoundaryHoldRoot>,state:Active}`. Every branch mutation/key transition consults the hold root; conflicting work waits or rejects, while disjoint commands may proceed. `ObjectCreationBoundary` is the exact tag `CommittedExisting{creation_marker_identity,creation_commit_seq}|TransactionReserved{final_certification_reservation_identity,permanent_object_ordinal}|BranchEpochReserved{boundary_reservation_ref:StrongRef<BranchEpochBoundaryReservation>,permanent_object_ordinal}`. It supplies stable AAD identity before ordering; actual applied sequence remains in the command/result and is not part of pre-order object identity.
