`WeakEpochIdentity` is `{graph,branch,kind,epoch,record_oid,rotation_chain_digest}` and is nonretaining. `BranchKeyEpochRecord` is pre-order-buildable only after the boundary reservation and is `{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,graph,branch,kind:Envelope|Write,epoch,key_id,predecessor:Genesis|SameEpochLifecycle{ref:StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>}|PriorEpoch{identity:WeakEpochIdentity},transition_nonce,boundary_reservation_ref:StrongRef<BranchEpochBoundaryReservation>,state:Active{opened_at_boundary}|Closed{opened_at_boundary,closed_through_boundary,close_reason}}`; both boundary fields are the already-assigned reservation identity/sequence, never the later install order. Closing an epoch uses the strong same-epoch predecessor; opening a successor uses only the weak prior-epoch identity/rotation-chain commitment. Command history, transparency, and the epoch registry authenticate rotation without making the current epoch retain every old key forever. The record points to no future install command. Apply installs it only while the exact reservation/hold is Active and byte-matches the frozen head/epochs. Old envelope roots/nodes/grants/backups—not a permanent epoch chain—are the strong owners that keep an old key decryptable; when they disappear, two-stage destruction can prove zero references. Genesis/no-fake-parent, cross-branch/head, boundary-substitution, unrelated-command interleaving, and reproposal tests are mandatory.

`KeyEnvelopeNode` is `{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,owning_graph,owning_branch,envelope_epoch,node_kind,coverage_cut:{through_logical_command_seq,through_marker:WeakMarkerIdentity?},children[{range,child_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeNode>,child_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<KeyEnvelopeNode>,wrapped_child_dek,wrap_profile,aad_bytes,aad_digest}],inherited_roots[{source_graph,source_branch,source_envelope_epoch,source_root_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,source_root_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,maximum_logical_command_seq,maximum_marker:WeakMarkerIdentity?,wrapped_source_envelope_key,wrapped_source_root_dek,wrap_profile,aad_bytes,aad_digest}],write_epochs[{write_epoch_record_ref:StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>,allowed_creation_range,wrapped_write_epoch_key,wrap_profile,aad_bytes,aad_digest}]}`. `KeyEnvelopeRoot` is `{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,graph,branch,envelope_epoch,envelope_epoch_record_ref:StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>,current_write_epoch_record_ref:StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>,root_node_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeNode>,root_node_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<KeyEnvelopeNode>,coverage_cut,profile_oid}`. Nodes are canonically sorted persistent-tree objects; inherited/source roots, their selected ciphertext realizations, and epoch records are constructed first. The four exact AAD domains are:

```text
H("fgdb:key-envelope-child:v1", namespace, incarnation,
  graph, branch, envelope_epoch, parent_node_kind, child_range,
  child_oid, canonical(child_ciphertext_ref), wrap_profile, envelope_key_id)
H("fgdb:key-envelope-inherit:v1", namespace, incarnation,
  target_graph, target_branch, target_envelope_epoch,
  source_graph, source_branch, source_envelope_epoch, source_root_oid,
  canonical(source_root_ciphertext_ref),
  maximum_logical_command_seq, maximum_marker, wrap_profile, target_envelope_key_id)
H("fgdb:key-envelope-write:v1", namespace, incarnation,
  graph, branch, envelope_epoch, write_epoch_record_oid,
  allowed_creation_range, wrap_profile, envelope_key_id)
H("fgdb:branch-object-dek-wrap:v1", namespace, incarnation,
  logical_oid, ciphertext_id, dek_id, graph, branch, write_epoch,
  canonical(object_creation_boundary), wrap_profile, write_key_id)
```

The stored AAD bytes must equal the canonical transcript; a digest alone is not usable AAD. `KeyEnvelopeGrantBytes` is the portable signed `{format_version,grant_id,generation,predecessor_grant_digest:WeakDigest?,authority_binding_public_commitment,security_state_binding_public_commitment,tenant_principal_ceiling_purpose_commitment,graph,branch,envelope_root_identity_and_digest,root_ciphertext_identity_and_digest,envelope_epoch,write_epoch?,coverage_cut,mode:SnapshotAtHead|AdvancingBranch,recipient_id,recipient_public_key_id,wrapped_root_object_dek,wrapped_envelope_key,wrap_profile,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before:AuthorityInstant,expires_at:AuthorityInstant,issuer_id,grant_aad_bytes,grant_aad_digest,signer_set_epoch,signature}` with no local `StrongRef`. Its AAD is exactly `canonical("fgdb:key-envelope-recipient:v1", every unsigned field except wrapped_root_object_dek, wrapped_envelope_key, grant_aad_bytes, grant_aad_digest, and signature)`; excluding the ciphertexts avoids a circular encryption input. Stored AAD bytes must equal that transcript and the digest must match. The outer signature transcript explicitly covers the entire portable body—including both wrapped ciphertexts and the exact AAD bytes/digest—and excludes only the signature.

`KeyEnvelopeGrantRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is built after its root, selected root ciphertext, and portable bytes and is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,grant_id,generation,predecessor_record_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantRecord<Role>>?,portable_grant_bytes_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantBytes>,portable_grant_digest,graph,branch,envelope_root_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,root_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,envelope_epoch,write_epoch?,coverage_cut,mode,recipient_id,recipient_public_key_id,time_basis_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantWindow<Role>>,issuance_time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,state:Current|Revoked{revocation_evidence_ref}|Expired{time_validation_evidence_ref},body_digest}`. Generation one has no predecessor; renewal is the exact prior+1 direct successor, preserves authority/subject/recipient/mode/coverage and wrapped-key identities, advances only the profiled window and issuance fields, and uses checked arithmetic. `KeyGrantRegistry<Role>` is the sole CAS-linear owner `{generation,entries[grant_id -> current_record_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantRecord<Role>>|Terminal{terminal_authority_commitment,grant_identity,terminal_portable_digest,status:Revoked|Expired}],recipient_and_subject_indexes,nonreuse_floor,body_digest}`; exact retry joins one generation and competing renewal/revoke/expire transitions race one current leaf. A snapshot grant can name only a frozen closed root. An advancing grant intentionally covers new wraps under the named active epoch until rotation; a later epoch requires a new ordered grant. The profiled window is finite; expiry alone never claims recipient erasure, but the ordered Expired successor ends new use and releases retention only after every independent promise floor closes. A live local record pins its exact named root/ciphertext even if compaction creates another realization; an equal-scalar portable grant under another authority binding is noninterchangeable and cannot be imported without its matching local record.

After the ordered boundary reservation, the entire physical/logical key closure is finalized before the later install order. `BranchForkBundle<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{boundary_reservation_ref:StrongRef<BranchEpochBoundaryReservation>,transition_nonce,parent,parent_expected_head:WeakMarkerIdentity,parent_expected_envelope_epoch,parent_expected_write_epoch,child_branch_id,child_policy,closed_parent_envelope_epoch_record_ref:StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>,closed_parent_write_epoch_record_ref:StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>,frozen_parent_root_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,frozen_parent_root_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,parent_successor_epoch_record_refs[StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>],parent_successor_root_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,parent_successor_manifest_ref:StrongRef<BranchManifest>,child_epoch_record_refs[StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>],child_root_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,child_manifest_ref:StrongRef<BranchManifest>,grant_record_refs[StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantRecord<Role>>],portable_grant_bytes_refs[StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantBytes>],resulting_key_grant_registry_ref:StrongRef<KeyGrantRegistry<Role>>,complete_grant_record_bytes_bijection_proof_ref,complete_object_wrap_ciphertext_realization_inventory,closure_digest}`. `BranchGrantTransitionRef<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the generated exact union `IssueOrRenew{record_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantRecord<Role>>,portable_bytes_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantBytes>}|Revoke{current_record_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeGrantRecord<Role>>,revocation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<KeyGrantRevocationEvidence>,terminal_registry_body_recipe}`. `BranchGrantBundle<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{boundary_reservation_ref:StrongRef<BranchEpochBoundaryReservation>,transition_nonce,operation:SnapshotAtHead|StartOrContinueAdvancing|RevokeAdvancing,branch,expected_head:WeakMarkerIdentity,expected_envelope_epoch,expected_write_epoch,closed_epoch_record_refs[StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>],frozen_root_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>?,frozen_root_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>?,successor_epoch_record_refs[StrongRef<BranchKeyEpochRecord>],successor_root_ref:StrongRef<KeyEnvelopeRoot>,successor_manifest_ref:StrongRef<BranchManifest>,grant_transitions[BranchGrantTransitionRef<Role>],expected_key_grant_registry_ref:StrongRef<KeyGrantRegistry<Role>>,resulting_key_grant_registry_ref:StrongRef<KeyGrantRegistry<Role>>,complete_object_wrap_ciphertext_realization_inventory,closure_digest}`. Every epoch/wrap/grant/manifest/registry field byte-matches the same Active reservation and uses `ObjectCreationBoundary::BranchEpochReserved`. The bundle is built bottom-up under a stable prepared owner: epoch records → ciphertexts/encodings/placements/openers → typed `StrongCiphertextRef<T>` availability certificates → envelope nodes/roots → portable grant bytes → local grant records/registry successor → manifests → bundle. Its payload-availability certificate covers that exact closed inventory and excludes only the future install control/order supplement.

`BranchForkSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{boundary_reservation_ref:StrongRef<BranchEpochBoundaryReservation>,required_reservation_state:Active,bundle_ref:StrongRef<BranchForkBundle<Role>>,bundle_payload_availability_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PayloadAvailabilityCertificate>,expected_parent_head:WeakMarkerIdentity,expected_parent_envelope_epoch,expected_parent_write_epoch,transition_nonce,terminal_audit_gate}`. `BranchGrantSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the analogous `{boundary_reservation_ref,required_reservation_state:Active,bundle_ref:StrongRef<BranchGrantBundle<Role>>,certificate_ref,expected_head,expected_envelope_epoch,expected_write_epoch,transition_nonce,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply creates no key material/ciphertext/placement/envelope/epoch/grant/manifest and performs no ambient physical selection: it validates the complete bundle/certificate/reservation/held basis, closure disjointness, and grant subset laws, installs the known manifests/registries, consumes the reservation, and releases its holds atomically. The actual install sequence is assigned by the ordinary order supplement and recorded only in applied lifecycle/provenance. Snapshot issue and revocation encode close→frozen root→double rotation; advancing issue may bind the exact active root/epoch without rotation. `BranchEpochBoundaryCancelSpec` is legal before any bundle/prepared owner/certificate/wrap exists and requires `NoBoundaryClosureProof`; `BranchEpochBoundaryAbandonSpec` is legal after construction only with complete signer/prepared closure, no install order/candidate/external grant publication, and typed cleanup/zeroization evidence. Once a grant or revocation is externally published, recovery must complete the matching install; timeout cannot release the hold. Expiry without ordered revocation/rotation does not claim cryptographic cutoff. Envelope reachability is part of GC/key-destruction/backup inventory. Fork/handoff/rotation tests cover sibling, post-fork-parent, post-snapshot, post-revocation, expired-grant, stale-prepared-wrap, unrelated command interleaving, reproposal, cancel/abandon, merge overbreadth, restore, every reserve/build/order/apply crash boundary, and collected-key decryption.

**MergeRecord / BranchMergeLedger.** A squash `MergeRecord` retains `{merge_token,merge_request_commitment,target,target_basis:StrongMarkerRef,source_heads[StrongMarkerRef],merge_bases[StrongMarkerRef],imported_range_tokens[{branch,first:WeakMarkerIdentity,last:WeakMarkerIdentity,base:WeakMarkerIdentity,chain_digest}],imported_range_keys[],resolution_policy_oid,resolution_digest,final_effect_digest}` and is linked from capsule/marker. Range tokens are weak provenance, not target ancestry or implicit retention. Pre-order `MergePrepareSpec` is `{client_request_nonce,request_commitment,candidate_merge_token,capability_replay_ref:StrongRef<MergeOutcomeCapabilityReplayRecord>,expected_merge_index_absence_or_matching_retry,terminal_audit_gate}`; the coordinator mints token and every capability byte before ordering, and apply uses no entropy. The replay record's body is the sole exact `MergeOutcomeCapability` body and the common header supplies the complete namespace/incarnation/service/tenant/principal/authority/policy/revocation/key/profile/window domain. The ledger first maps `{tenant,principal,target,client_request_nonce}` to the chosen token plus replay ref before evaluation; a same-request retry returns identical canonical bytes, a different proposed candidate is ignored after success, and conflicting commitment reuse is rejected. `ImportedRangeKey` hashes `{database namespace,target graph/branch,source branch/range/chain digest,merge semantics,resolution policy}` and excludes the mutable target basis. `BranchMergeLedgerRoot` contains `outcomes[merge_token -> {original_request,capability_replay_ref,basis:WeakMarkerIdentity,state:InFlight|Committed{target_marker:WeakMarkerIdentity,final_effect_digest}|Rejected{stable_reason_digest}|Compacted{summary_ref}|ExpiredTombstone{token_digest,capability_commitment,expiry_evidence_digest}}]` plus `coverage_domains[H(namespace,target,source_branch,source_chain_domain,merge_semantics,resolution_policy) -> normalized_nonoverlapping_ranges[{first:WeakMarkerIdentity,last:WeakMarkerIdentity,chain_digest,imported_range_key}]]`. Transaction apply validates the prepared token/request/range-key set and installs the terminal outcome, normalized coverage, and merge marker atomically. Token retries return the original outcome; interval lookup detects exact and partial overlap, which rejects `AlreadyImported` or applies only a declared canonical uncovered remainder. Both maps are non-forgetting idempotency state preserved across checkpoint/history cuts, backup/restore, reconfiguration, and GC; replay ref retirement requires the separate profiled expiry transition.

**PayloadReceipt / PayloadAvailabilityCertificate / ConfigPayloadFloor / GcEpoch.** `RemotePreparedIdentity { storage_member,prepared_ownership_id,base_closure_inventory_digest,latest_record_observation:NonRetainingRemoteObservation{record_digest:WeakDigest,observed_prepared_payload_root_generation} ? }` names the stable signed ownership promise; the optional diagnostic is neither a local ObjectId nor a reachability edge. A receipt binds `{database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,configuration_state_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,payload_predicate_digest,storage_member,failure_domain,local_writer_fence_epoch,prepared_owner:RemotePreparedIdentity,encoding_and_placement_requirements[],symbol/source-block coverage,fsync_generation,proposal_freshness_basis_ref:StrongRef<PayloadReceiptProposalFreshnessBasis>,proposal_freshness_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,nonce}` and a domain-separated member signature/MAC. The freshness basis is built from the complete stable receipt body excluding itself, its evidence ref, nonce, and signature; the evidence is then built, and the final receipt signs both typed identities. The signer must still hold the matching `WriterLeaseGuard/FenceToken`; a receiver verifies the epoch against the member's current fenced identity/configuration and requires `TimeValidationEvidence::Usable` for `FormNewProposal` immediately before using the receipt. Successor records preserve that ownership/base closure and may only strengthen current closure. Proposal freshness is never a storage lease, retention horizon, or deletion permission; an expired receipt may remain valid evidence of durable ownership but cannot form a new proposal. A certificate repeats namespace/incarnation, the exact configuration ref/predicate digest, canonically lists receipt refs, exact base logical-object plus encoding/placement requirements, FEC recoverability calculation, verifier version, and threshold signature.

Installed local `ConfigPayloadFloor` is the exact schema `{schema_version,payload_schema_oid,protocol_registry_epoch,basis_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_logical_command_seq,basis_commit_seq,basis_raft_index,closed_through_raft_index,closed_through_logical_command_seq,current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,prospective_joint_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>?,prospective_final_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,failure_domain_policy_ref:StrongRef,current_checkpoint:WeakCheckpointIdentity,checkpoint_state_vector_digest:WeakDigest,retained_command_suffix_endpoints,retained_marker_suffix_endpoints,mandatory_inventory_root:StrongRef<MandatoryInventory>,prepared_owner_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalPreparedOwnerFloor>,receipt_certificate_signer_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalReceiptCertificateSignerFloor>,audit_ticket_fence_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalAuditTicketFenceFloor>,retention_cut_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalRetentionCutFloor>,branch_key_allocator_merge_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalBranchKeyAllocatorMergeFloor>,outcome_conflict_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalOutcomeConflictFloor>,delivery_replay_derived_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalDeliveryReplayDerivedFloor>,continuity_service_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalContinuityServiceFloor>,remote_trust_release_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalRemoteTrustReleaseFloor>,gc_key_destruction_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalGcKeyDestructionFloor>,backup_restore_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalBackupRestoreFloor>,migration_transition_floor_ref:StrongRef<LocalMigrationTransitionFloor>,coverage_bitmap,nonretaining_predecessor_floor_digest:WeakDigest}`; its ObjectId is its floor identity. It has no strong edge to its basis root, checkpoint/vector, predecessor floor, transition spec, or applied command.

`MandatoryInventory` is `{schema_version,payload_schema_oid,protocol_registry_epoch,role:Local|Meta|Shard{shard_id},ordered_entries[MandatoryInventoryEntry],coverage_bitmap,body_digest:BodyDigest<MandatoryInventory>}`. `MandatoryInventoryEntry` is `{class_tag,source_slot_tag,activation_predicate_id,value:RetainedStrong{typed_ref:RegisteredStrongRef}|CurrentCheckpointObservation{checkpoint:WeakCheckpointIdentity,checkpoint_state_vector_digest:WeakDigest,logical_inventory_digest,availability_receipt_refs[StrongRef]}}`. `RegisteredStrongRef` is the closed generated union of role-legal typed strong-reference encodings, never a raw ObjectId. `MandatoryInventoryClassRegistry` maps each stable class tag to one exact target kind, reference encoding, payload field or protocol-state slot, live predicate, cut/release predicate, and owning floor field. Entries are canonical by class tag and the bitmap equals every currently activated class exactly once. Unknown, duplicate, missing, wrong-role, or dormant-feature entries fail G0; deleting each class in turn is a generated negative fixture.

The Local inventory and named floors cover current/joint/final configurations; command and marker suffixes; prepared owners, receipts, certificates, signer locks, and the complete audit-visibility pipeline with both endpoints and every candidate closure; provisional and activated cuts plus the retention map; branch/head/key-envelope/grant/merge and allocation state; transaction outcome and conflict state; cursor, delivery, replay, archive and derived state; identity, privacy, audit, and cluster-incarnation/service-catalog continuities; remote trust and every stage of the six-transition release; local GC and key destruction; every nonterminal Local backup pin, closure, publication and release; `RestoreSourceLease`, reconciliation, hidden activation/visibility, service promotion and catalog receipt; and sharding freeze, transition, migration authority transfer and retirement obligations until adoption/finalize. `RetiredLocal` has no `ConfigPayloadFloor`: its manifest owns only the compact certified transition/retirement/audit proof closure.

For every role, `CurrentCheckpointObservation` is the sole weak checkpoint exception and must byte-match the separately strong current payload checkpoint. An operational install creates a `ProvisionalRetentionCutSet`: it may authenticate bytes already absent under inherited activated coverage, but it cannot satisfy any new-reclamation, key-destruction, backup-pin-release, remote-release, topology-retirement, or ownership-discharge predicate. A replacement provisional must dominate every absorbed activated absence until its own activation; only an `ActivatedRetentionCutSet` can authorize a new physical release. Prefix, checkpoint, payload-schema, registry-epoch, or obligation drift requires recomputation before installation. Initial installation is the fresh `CheckpointInstalled -> Operational` command; configuration retirement requires a final floor committed after and covering through `CommitNew`.

`GcProposal` is the Local pre-order bounded-set authorization `{gc_id,basis_state:WeakStateIdentity,basis_logical_command_seq,basis_commit_seq,basis_raft_index,configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>,config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>,activated_retention_cut_ref:StrongRef<ActivatedRetentionCutSet>,retention_map_digest,complete_typed_root_inventory_ref:StrongRef<GcRootInventory>,candidate_derivation_profile_oid,sorted_reclaim_objects_and_realizations,exact_reclaim_set_digest,required_storage_profile_and_member_set,completed_remote_release_refs[GcRemoteReleaseCompletionRef],excluded_live_prepared_certificate_remote_key_backup_legal_hold_replay_audit_capability_restore_result_workspace_and_delivery_obligation_commitment,terminal_audit_gate}`. It uses only weak basis identities and strong current floors/inventories. Every follower recomputes the candidate and either rejects the command or applies `GcDecisionRecord {proposal_ref:StrongRef<GcProposal>,applied_control_ref:AppliedControlRef,stable_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>,retention_map_digest,typed_root_validation_digest,decision:Rejected{stable_reason}|Accepted{gc_id,authorized_reclaim_set_digest,required_storage_member_set_digest,quarantine_generation}}`; the input never names this result.

An accepted decision does **not** authorize physical work by itself. GC has two non-overlapping state machines. Semantic `GcSemanticState<Role>` is `ProposedOrRejected|Quarantined{gc_id,authorization_commitment,quarantine_generation,reclaim_set_digest,operation_family_commitment,no_new_reference_rule_id,physical_state:AwaitingPhysicalDisposition}|PhysicalCompletedPendingCut{terminal_evidence_commitment}|CancelledBeforePhysicalStart{terminal_evidence_commitment}|Retired`. `LocalGcApplyQuarantineSpec {decision_record_ref:StrongRef<GcDecisionRecord>,required_decision:Accepted,expected_current_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,expected_root_slot_generations,current_complete_typed_root_inventory_ref:StrongRef<GcRootInventory>,current_reachability_recomputation_ref:StrongRef<GcReachabilityProof>,newly_reachable_disjoint_proof_ref:StrongRef<GcDisjointnessProof>,exact_authorized_reclaim_set,operation_family:GcOperationFamily,sorted_member_operation_plans[{generation=0,operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,storage_member_id,storage_profile_oid,target_inventory_digest,canonical_request_transcript_digest}],complete_member_target_and_family_bijection_digest,terminal_audit_gate}` requires `reclaim_set ⊆ authorized_set`, disjointness from every current RootSlot, candidate result, Protocol/Prepared/Consensus owner, and independently retained obligation, and exact conformance of every generation-zero plan to the bounded family. Semantic apply installs `LocalGcReclaimAuthorizationRecord` and `GcSemanticState::Quarantined` only; it does not create or mutate a Protocol operation record. The quarantine and every checkpoint/configuration/backup/restore floor retain the complete family, all generation-zero plans, and the deterministic successor recipe through terminal disposition. Every encoder, dedupe lookup, prepared receipt, command validator, compactor, and root publisher enforces the registered no-new-reference rule against that audit-visible quarantine.

After the quarantine's role-valid `CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>` exists, Protocol maintenance initializes `GcPhysicalExecutionRecord<Role>` with common `{gc_id,authorization_commitment,quarantine_generation,operation_family:GcOperationFamily,all_generation_plan_and_disposition_lineage,current_generation,dispatch_mode:Open|CancellationFenceInstalled{fence_generation}|CancellationReconciling{fence_generation}|NoStartFenced{fence_generation,evidence_record_ref}|CompletionRequired{authority_commitment},physical_state:Requested|Started|Progress|CompletingAfterCancellationRace|Completed{complete_receipt_bijection,terminal_evidence}|CancelledBeforeStart{complete_never_started_bijection,terminal_evidence},body_digest}` in `GcPhysicalExecutionIndex<Role>`. `NoStartFenced` blocks every reclaim send; `CompletionRequired` permanently forbids the cancelled terminal. The family is the exact Semantic family including base plan set. A successor generation may cover only a target proved locally AbandonedBeforeSend or provider-CancelledBeforeStart in the prior generation, and exhaustion fails closed. Started/completed targets retain their original operation IDs and are never repeated.

`GcPhysicalExecutionInitializeSpec` byte-matches the publication evidence and operation-family/base-plan commitment and creates exactly one `IrreversibleDispatchAttempt<GcMemberReclaim>::Armed` for every active plan. `GcPhysicalDispatchMarkSentUnknownSpec` advances exactly one Armed attempt to SentUnknown before transport eligibility. A generated `GcIrreversibleDispatchGuard` is the GC specialization of the universal guard and revalidates the current all-plane root inventory, exact quarantine generation and reclaim-set disjointness, dispatch mode, configuration, writer/member fences, continuity, authority, service epoch, and time immediately before sending the byte-identical member request. `GcPhysicalStartReceiptImportSpec`, `GcPhysicalProgressReceiptImportSpec`, `GcPhysicalCompletionReceiptImportSpec`, `GcPhysicalCancellationReceiptImportSpec`, `GcParticipantCancellationFenceInstallSpec`, `GcParticipantCancellationStatusImportSpec`, `GcParticipantCancellationEvidenceFinalizeSpec`, `GcParticipantRollForwardSpec`, `GcPhysicalSuccessorGenerationSpec`, and `GcPhysicalEvidenceRetireSpec` mutate only this Protocol index and registered dispatch slots. Maintenance may read current Semantic commitments but cannot write `GcSemanticState`, `GcReclaimQuarantineRoot`, a logical locator, or a semantic sequence. Cross-plane matching uses signed commitments/certificates and the same RootManifest publication, never a strong ObjectId edge from Semantic to Protocol or back.

Each member service has one linear idempotent operation state keyed by `(operation_id,idempotency_token_digest)`: `NotRegistered -> Requested{canonical_request_digest,request_registration_counter} -> Started{member_monotonic_counter,pre_operation_physical_root_digest} -> Completed{member_monotonic_counter,post_operation_physical_root_digest,receipt_digest}`; cancellation allows either `Requested -> CancelledBeforeStart{canonical_request_digest,counter,cancellation_receipt_digest}` or the explicit idempotent tombstone `NotRegistered -> CancelledBeforeStart{canonical_request_digest,cancellation_tombstone_counter,cancellation_receipt_digest}`. The latter permanently rejects later registration/start for that operation/request; a roll-forward must use the family-derived successor ID. Its authenticated status API returns exactly one state and signed monotonic counter. The machine cannot move from Started/Completed to Cancelled, regress a counter, or bind an existing operation ID to different bytes, and timeout changes no state. `GcPhysicalStartReceipt`, `GcPhysicalCancellationReceipt`, and `GcPhysicalMemberReceipt` begin with `FenceBoundIrreversibleReceiptHeader` and bind `{gc_id,authorization_commitment,quarantine_generation,operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,storage_member_id,storage_profile_oid,target_inventory_digest,canonical_request_transcript_digest,pre_operation_physical_root_digest,member_monotonic_counter}`; cancellation adds the exact never-started tombstone counter/fence, and completion adds the exact deleted-or-crypto-erased inventory, post-operation physical-root and probe digests, durability evidence, completion counter/time profile, and signature. A member durably persists and fences Started before its first irreversible byte/key action. A lost response is recovered by status lookup under the same operation ID; signed NotRegistered alone permits only the same reclaim request or the separately registered cancel-before-start request under a fresh guard, never inferred success or local abandonment.

Standalone Local cancellation has its own ordered `LocalGcCancellationAuthorizeSpec {quarantine_record_ref,protocol_no_start_fenced_evidence_ref,complete_member_target_family_and_no-start_bijection,current_unchanged_physical_root_proof,terminal_audit_gate}` and applied `LocalGcCancellationAuthorizationRecord`; it cannot be constructed from a timeout or partial member set. `GcCancellationAuthorityFor<Local>` is `LocalStandalone{authorization_ref:StrongRef<LocalGcCancellationAuthorizationRecord>}`. `GcCancellationAuthorityFor<Meta>` is `GlobalMeta{authorization_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalGcCancellationAuthorizationRecord>,certificate_ref:StrongRef<GlobalGcCancellationAuthorizationCertificate>}`. `GcCancellationAuthorityFor<Shard>` is `GlobalShard{certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalGcCancellationAuthorizationCertificate>}`. Once Protocol reaches a complete terminal disposition, a separate Semantic `GcPhysicalDispositionImportSpec<Role> = Completed{quarantine_publication_evidence:CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>,protocol_terminal_evidence:PortableGcPhysicalTerminalEvidence,current_reachability_recomputation_ref,no_resurrection_proof_ref}|Cancelled{quarantine_publication_evidence:CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>,protocol_terminal_evidence:PortableGcPhysicalTerminalEvidence,complete_never_started_bijection_proof,cancellation_authority:GcCancellationAuthorityFor<Role>}` imports the portable signed terminal evidence and alone moves `GcSemanticState` to `PhysicalCompletedPendingCut` or `CancelledBeforePhysicalStart`. A per-group no-start result alone cannot cancel W12 Semantic state. The Cancelled arm requires an exact target bijection over locally AbandonedBeforeSend/DispatchNeverSentProof or provider-CancelledBeforeStart receipts and `dispatch_mode=NoStartFenced`; SentUnknown, Requested, Started, CompletionRequired, or missing evidence blocks it. Any authenticated Started/Completed observation permanently rules out cancellation and forces CompletionRequired. A later activated retention cut moves the Semantic state to Retired and permits Protocol evidence compaction. Missing, duplicate, wrong-family/generation/member/inventory, stale-root/fence, partial, wrong role authority, mixed aggregate mode, or cross-plane-substituted evidence rejects import. Crash/lab tests cut every quarantine, initialize, dispatch, provider cancel tombstone, start/destructive action, receipt import, fence/status/evidence, global mode, successor generation, terminal state, Semantic import/publication/ack, aggregate terminal, cut activation, and evidence-retirement boundary.

**KeyWrap / KeyDestroyRecord / BackupManifest.** `KeyWrap` binds `{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,logical_oid,ciphertext_id,dek_id,scope:Recipient|Database|BranchEpoch{graph,branch,write_epoch,object_creation_boundary:ObjectCreationBoundary}|Replica|Backup,wrap_algorithm,wrap_key_id,wrap_key_epoch,wrapped_dek,aad_bytes,aad_digest,issuer_signature}`; BranchEpoch AAD is exactly the branch-object transcript, while every other scope has its own registered transcript. Namespace-key escrow uses separately typed `ObjectIdKeyWrap` for `K_oid`. Rewrap creates only a new wrap; re-encryption creates a new CiphertextId and EncodingId realizations. `KeyDestroyFloorRef` is `Checkpoint{checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>}|Configuration{floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>}` and `KeyDestroyExternalAckRef` is `Backup{ack_ref:StrongRef<BackupKeyReleaseAck>}|LegalHold{ack_ref:StrongRef<LegalHoldReleaseAck>}|RemoteConsumer{ack_ref:StrongRef<RemoteKeyConsumerReleaseAck>}`. `KeyDestructionTarget` is the closed union `KmsKeyVersion{kms_authority_id,key_uri,key_version}|HsmObject{hsm_cluster_id,slot_id,object_handle_digest}|StorageMemberReplica{consensus_domain,member_id,material_class,material_identity_digest}`; canonical target identity is `H(domain,canonical(target))`. `KeyDestructionOperationPlan` is `{operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,target:KeyDestructionTarget,canonical_request_transcript_digest,required_receipt_profile_oid}` and plans are sorted by target identity with no duplicate target or operation ID.

`KeyDestroyProposal` is the pre-order body `{key_identity,expected_key_state:RetainedDecryptOnly,basis_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,expected_prospective_configuration_set_digest,exact_root_slot_generations,checkpoint_and_configuration_floor_refs[KeyDestroyFloorRef],generated_scanned_root_inventory_ref:StrongRef<KeyReferenceInventory>,zero_reference_proof_ref:StrongRef<ZeroReferenceProof>,backup_legal_hold_and_external_consumer_ack_refs[KeyDestroyExternalAckRef],threshold_authorization_ref:StrongRef<KeyDestructionAuthorization>,sorted_destruction_operation_plans[KeyDestructionOperationPlan],complete_target_set_digest,expected_state_conditions,terminal_audit_gate}`. The generated inventory includes every branch epoch, envelope node/root/grant, advancing-grant obligation, reachable ciphertext/wrap/opener, prepared/certificate/audit/outcome/result/workspace/replay/DP/backup, current or prospective configuration, and signing/verifier dependency. Apply atomically installs `KeyReferenceQuarantine {key_identity,quarantine_generation,proposal_identity:WeakDigest,installed_at_logical_command_seq,target_set_digest,no_new_reference_rule_id}` and one immutable Semantic `ExternalKeyDestructionOperationRecord {operation_id,key_identity,quarantine_ref:StrongRef<KeyReferenceQuarantine>,target:KeyDestructionTarget,idempotency_token_digest,canonical_request_transcript_digest,required_receipt_profile_oid,state:AuthorizedUndispatchedRecipe,source_command_ref:StrongRef<ControlCommand>,assigned_logical_command_seq}` for every and only plan, then moves the key to `DestroyPending`. This record is an authorization recipe, never a mutable physical-status record. The canonical operation-root map is keyed by target identity and proves a plan/record bijection.

Only after the quarantine's role-valid `CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>` exists may `KeyDestructionDispatchInitializeSpec` create one Protocol `IrreversibleDispatchAttempt<KeyDestruction>::Armed` per authorization recipe. `KeyDestructionDispatchMarkSentUnknownSpec` durably advances an attempt before transport eligibility. The generated `KeyDestructionDispatchGuard` then rereads both RootSlots, the complete current Semantic/Protocol/Prepared/Consensus inventory, the exact key quarantine and no-new-reference proof, current/prospective configurations, writer fence, continuity head, service/security authority, target provider profile, and time evidence, and acquires the registered destruction hold immediately before the byte-identical request is submitted. KMS, HSM, and storage-member provider profiles must expose authenticated idempotent registration/status and monotonic terminal evidence; an unsupported provider cannot be configured as a destruction target. Recovery queries the same operation ID first, and a signed NotRegistered response permits only the same request under a fresh guard. `KeyDestructionReceiptImportSpec` moves the Protocol attempt to TerminalReceiptImported. Timeout, local expiry, provider ambiguity, or process death neither retries with a new ID nor implies destruction.

Key-destruction terminal evidence is target-typed. `ExternalKeyDestructionProviderReceipt` is the KMS/HSM-issued portable `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=KeyDestruction},target:KmsKeyVersion|HsmObject,destruction_result:Destroyed{target_terminal_counter,provider_evidence_digest}|AlreadyDestroyed{matching_prior_operation_id,target_terminal_counter,provider_evidence_digest},completed_at:AuthorityInstant,time_authority_profile_oid,provider_key_id,provider_key_epoch,signature}`. `AlreadyDestroyed` is accepted only when provider evidence cryptographically binds the same target and canonical request lineage. A current member imports it through `ExternalKeyDestructionReceiptImportRecord {operation_record_ref,dispatch_attempt_identity,portable_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ExternalKeyDestructionProviderReceipt>,current_configuration_writer_fence_and_authority_commitment,imported_at_maintenance_index,body_digest}` before any completion transition.

`StorageMemberKeyDestructionReceipt` is the same-group member-issued `{header:FenceBoundIrreversibleReceiptHeader,operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,target:StorageMemberReplica,canonical_request_transcript_digest,key_material_identity_and_inventory_digest,pre_operation_physical_root_digest,post_operation_physical_root_digest,destroyed_or_crypto_erased_probe_digest,member_monotonic_counter,completion_counter,completed_at:AuthorityInstant,time_authority_profile_oid,member_signature}`. Its header proves the exact current configuration/member/writer/quarantine fence and that Started was durable before the first irreversible byte/key action; it is imported through `StorageMemberKeyDestructionReceiptImportRecord` under that same group. An external-provider receipt cannot stand for a storage member, and a member fence receipt cannot stand for KMS/HSM authority. `KeyDestructionTerminalReceiptRef` is the closed union `ExternalProvider{receipt_ref,import_record_ref}|StorageMember{receipt_ref,import_record_ref}` with target-discriminant equality. `TargetCompletionBijectionProof` binds the proposal target-set digest, canonical operation-root digest, sorted typed terminal receipt/import identities, and proves exactly one valid matching arm per target/operation with no missing, extra, duplicate, substituted, or conflicting result.

`PortableKeyDestructionDispatchTerminalEvidence` is the independently verifiable, Protocol-object-identity-free `{dispatch_contract_id,key_identity,quarantine_generation,source_publication_evidence_commitment,operation_plan_and_authorization_recipe_commitment,sorted_terminal_attempt_and_receipt_commitments,complete_target_bijection_commitment,provider_status_lineage_commitment,configuration_fence_continuity_and_authority_commitment,signer_set_epoch,canonical_signatures}`. Its source commitment is over the exact role-valid `CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>`; it is not hard-coded to a Local/Meta visibility certificate. `KeyDestroyFinalizeSpec` is `{proposal_ref:StrongRef<KeyDestroyProposal>,expected_quarantine_ref:StrongRef<KeyReferenceQuarantine>,quarantine_publication_evidence:CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>,sorted_operation_record_refs[StrongRef<ExternalKeyDestructionOperationRecord>],dispatch_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableKeyDestructionDispatchTerminalEvidence>,typed_terminal_receipt_refs[KeyDestructionTerminalReceiptRef],target_completion_bijection_proof_ref:StrongRef<TargetCompletionBijectionProof>,exact_current_role_payload_and_root_slot_identities,current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,current_prospective_configuration_set_digest,current_complete_generated_root_inventory_ref:StrongRef<KeyReferenceInventory>,current_zero_reference_recomputation_ref:StrongRef<ZeroReferenceProof>,no_new_reference_since_quarantine_proof_ref:StrongRef<NoNewKeyReferenceProof>,expected_state_conditions,terminal_audit_gate}`. It requires current-root/configuration/slot equality or a registered quarantine-preserving successor proof, zero retaining references, byte-matching role-valid quarantine publication evidence, the exact proposal/recipe/Protocol-terminal/typed-receipt bijection, and no raw Protocol reference. Apply selects `DestroyedPendingCertificate` and creates compact `KeyDestroyRecord {key_identity,quarantine_generation,proposal_identity:WeakDigest,finalize_spec_identity:WeakDigest,complete_target_set_digest,operation_and_receipt_commitment,verified_current_inventory_digest,applied_finalize_identity:WeakAuthorityAppliedIdentity,terminal_audit_commitment}`; the record has no strong path to proposal, finalize, receipts, scanned inventories, old floors, Protocol attempts, or a future certificate.

Only after that record, its resulting durable root, and its required audit visibility advance exist may the current quorum create `KeyDestructionCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,key_identity,quarantine_generation,key_destroy_record_ref:StrongRef<KeyDestroyRecord>,post_finalize_state_root_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStateRoot>,operation_and_receipt_commitment,verified_current_inventory_digest,finalize_visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local>>,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`. The Meta analogue uses `PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>` through the generated role arm. `KeyDestroyCertificatePublishSpec {key_destroy_record_ref:StrongRef<KeyDestroyRecord>,certificate_ref:StrongRef<KeyDestructionCertificate>,expected_key_state:DestroyedPendingCertificate,expected_quarantine_generation,expected_current_state:WeakStateIdentity,terminal_audit_gate}` is the sole transition to final `Destroyed` and installs a compact certificate-index edge. Heavy inputs remain owned by the key lifecycle and certificate ledgers until publication's audit visibility advance plus an activated cut; compact result/receipt commitment/certificate remain through the crypto-erasure evidence floor. No state reaches final `Destroyed` if any reachable or independently obligated object can still require the key, any target lacks a receipt, or the certificate is absent.

Backup content and publication-envelope references are distinct identity classes. `BackupSourceIdentity<T> {source_authority_domain:ConsensusDomain,source_cluster_incarnation,object_kind,object_id,canonical_digest}` is nonretaining evidence and is never accepted by local or remote mark traversal. `BackupContentSubject<T>` is the closed union `SourceObject{expected_source:BackupSourceIdentity<T>}|RecoveryProjection{backup_id,projection_digest}`; generated type/kind rows make `RecoveryProjection` legal only for `T=BackupRecoveryProjection`. A manifest body, signature set, manifest, publication receipt, or transparency proof is never smuggled into this union: those objects are constructed only after the content-inventory root is fixed.

The root-free `BackupInventoryEntryRef<T>` is `{backup_id,archive_authority_id,inventory_key,subject:BackupContentSubject<T>,archived_byte_digest,archived_length,protection_profile,required_recovery_proof_class}`. It can be embedded while the final inventory root is not yet known. The externally resolvable content reference `BackupArchiveRef<T>` is `{entry:BackupInventoryEntryRef<T>,content_archive_inventory_root_digest,inventory_leaf_index,inventory_inclusion_proof}` and is constructed only after the final content inventory exists. Its proof must authenticate the exact entry under that root and a current matching archive-retention grant. Live source entries use `SourceObject`; the recovery projection uses its dedicated arm.

Publication-envelope artifacts use the disjoint portable `BackupPublicationArtifactRef<T> {backup_id,publication_bundle_id,archive_authority_id,artifact_kind:ManifestBody|ManifestSignatureSet|Manifest,expected_canonical_digest,expected_length,authenticated_locator,publication_receipt_digest,receipt_artifact_index}`. Generated type/kind rows make each artifact tag legal only for its exact `T`. Resolution requires the separately supplied authentic `BackupPublicationReceipt`, byte-equality with the indexed locator/digest/length row in that receipt, and a current matching `ArchiveRetentionGrant`; the reference is not a content-inventory leaf and contains no content-inventory inclusion proof. The receipt itself and any later transparency inclusion are carried by their own portable evidence types and cannot self-locate through a future receipt.

Portable recovery is specified by one plane-complete object rather than inferred from inventory names. `RecoverySlotDisposition` is the closed union `TransformCurrent|CarryPriorEvidence|RecreateCurrentAuthority|ReconcileExternal|SuspendProtocolOwner|DiscardReplicaLocal{registered_law_id}|Reject`. `PortableRoleRecoveryCut<Role>` is `{role_and_group,source_manifest_identity,source_slot_generation_and_digest,semantic_cut:{applied_state:BackupInventoryRoleStateRef<Role>,visible_state:BackupInventoryRoleStateRef<Role>,applied_and_visible_positions},protocol_cut:{protocol_state:BackupInventoryRoleProtocolStateRef<Role>,maintenance_generation_and_index,audit_pipeline_and_pending_queue_inventory:StructurallyInapplicable|Present{inventory_entry_ref},result_cursor_artifact_error_escrow_inventory,security_barrier_state},prepared_cut:{prepared_root:BackupInventoryRolePreparedRef<Role>,live_owner_inventory},consensus_cut:{applied_cut_snapshot,retained_suffix,configuration,certificate_and_attempt_ledgers,remote_obligation_floors},checkpoint_and_floor_cut,external_continuity_reconciliation_inputs,bootstrap_reconstruction_inputs,sorted_slot_dispositions[{slot:DurableStateSlotRef,disposition:RecoverySlotDisposition}],complete_durable_state_slot_coverage_proof,body_digest}`. The `BackupInventoryRole*Ref` families are generated closed role-specific unions over exact root-free `BackupInventoryEntryRef<T>` targets; no erased archive ref and no final inventory-root digest is legal inside the projection.

`BackupRecoveryProjection` is `Local{common,local_cut:PortableRoleRecoveryCut<Local>}|Sharded{common,meta_cut:PortableRoleRecoveryCut<Meta>,sorted_shard_cuts[PortableRoleRecoveryCut<Shard>],exact_global_barrier_and_partition_bijection_proof}`. Every activated `durable_state_slots.toml` row and mandatory inventory class has exactly one disposition. Same-identity recovery preserves applied/visible divergence, the exact pending audit queue, independent output owners, prepared owners, and maintenance suffix. Clone requires either a certified `CloneExportReady` cut with no applied-hidden barrier or an explicit exclude/transfer/reissue disposition for every hidden entry; it may never make source hidden state target-visible. Old transport leases/capabilities restore as suspended/no-send until fresh target authorization and time evidence, and source Raft hard state is historical evidence rather than current target consensus state.

`BackupManifestBody` is `{backup_id,database_id,database_security_namespace_id,source_cluster_incarnation,source_posture:Local|Sharded,barrier,backup_state:BackupState,exact_logical_commit_base_and_anti_rollback_ranges,content_archive_inventory_root_digest,content_archive_inventory_count,recovery_projection_ref:BackupArchiveRef<BackupRecoveryProjection>,recovery_projection_digest,branch_checkpoint_history_prepared_certificate_key_derived_trust_continuity_cursor_delivery_replay_inventories,object_id_key_escrow_policy,recipient_policy,format_matrix,canonical_semantic_digest,trust_policy,transparency_policy:NotRequired{policy_digest}|Required{log_id,minimum_checkpoint_policy_oid},prior_transparency_checkpoint?}`. Construction order is exact and acyclic: build/archive every source object and its root-free entry -> build/archive `BackupRecoveryProjection` from only those entries -> add its root-free RecoveryProjection entry -> compute the final content-inventory root and inclusion proofs -> construct all rooted `BackupArchiveRef` values -> construct `BackupManifestBody`. Publication-envelope objects are excluded from this content inventory and are built later. Body construction verifies the projection's slot bitmap/digest and every rooted ref proof against the fixed inventory. `BackupManifestSignatureSet` is `{body_ref:StrongRef<BackupManifestBody>,body_digest,signature_policy_oid,signer_set_epoch,configuration_chain_digest,canonical_sorted_signers[{signer_id,signing_key_id,key_epoch}],threshold,canonical_signing_transcript_digest,canonical_signature_set}`; the transcript binds the complete body bytes/digest and signer identities/keys/epochs. `BackupManifest` is exactly `{body_ref:StrongRef<BackupManifestBody>,signature_set_ref:StrongRef<BackupManifestSignatureSet>,body_digest,signature_set_digest,manifest_format_major,manifest_format_minor}` and contains no self digest, receipt, inclusion, grant, or reopen proof. Its canonical digest is computed only after encoding and becomes a later object's field.

`PortableRecoveryLocator` is the bounded reference-free signed closed union `ArchiveObject{provider_authority_id,locator_profile_oid,opaque_locator_bytes:BoundedBytes<4096>,target_artifact_kind,target_artifact_canonical_digest,expected_archived_byte_digest,expected_length,access_scope_commitment,locator_digest,provider_locator_signature}|KmsWrap{provider_authority_id,locator_profile_oid,opaque_locator_bytes:BoundedBytes<4096>,key_or_wrap_identity_commitment,recipient_and_ceremony_commitment,locator_digest,provider_locator_signature}|HsmObject{...same exact common fields plus nonexportable_object_commitment...}|ThresholdEscrow{...same exact common fields plus participant_set_and_threshold_commitment...}`. Opaque bytes are actionable provider input, not a digest masquerading as a locator; their digest, provider, kind, profile, target, scope, and signature are always present and cross-action substitution is invalid.

`BackupRecoveryBootstrapDescriptor` is constructed before publication and is the bounded portable public `{backup_id,publication_bundle_id,leakage_class:RestoreBootstrapMetadata,manifest_artifacts[{artifact_kind:ManifestBody|ManifestSignatureSet|Manifest,canonical_digest,length,ciphertext_id,encoding_id,archive_locator:PortableRecoveryLocator::ArchiveObject,key_opener_locators[PortableRecoveryLocator::KmsWrap|HsmObject|ThresholdEscrow],decoder_profile_oid}],recovery_projection_bootstrap:{content_inventory_entry_identity_and_digest,archived_byte_digest,length,ciphertext_id,encoding_id,archive_locator:PortableRecoveryLocator::ArchiveObject,key_opener_locators[PortableRecoveryLocator::KmsWrap|HsmObject|ThresholdEscrow],decoder_profile_oid},content_archive_inventory_root_digest,descriptor_digest}`. Locator bytes are deterministic/preallocated under the publication bundle and provider contract before the request is Armed; a provider that cannot honor them refuses without effect rather than substituting a location. The descriptor exposes only identities, sizes, cipher/encoding IDs, bounded opaque provider locators, wrapped-key/escrow locators, and decoder profiles—not plaintext, keys, graph contents, or the full object inventory. Restore must later byte-match it against the publication receipt and decrypted signed manifest/inventory; mismatch is a permanent acquisition failure.

`BackupPublicationRequestBinding` is the canonical external request `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=BackupPublishEnvelope},backup_id,publication_bundle_id,archive_authority_id,manifest_canonical_digest,manifest_body_digest,manifest_signature_set_digest,content_archive_inventory_root_digest,content_archive_inventory_count,recovery_bootstrap_descriptor_digest,required_durability_profile,authority_binding_public_commitment,service_visibility_epoch}`. `BackupPublicationReceipt` is the portable externally signed `{request_binding:BackupPublicationRequestBinding,publication_envelope_locations_and_byte_digests[{artifact_kind:ManifestBody|ManifestSignatureSet|Manifest,archive_locator:PortableRecoveryLocator::ArchiveObject,canonical_digest,length}],recovery_bootstrap_descriptor:BackupRecoveryBootstrapDescriptor,content_inventory_durability_commitment,publication_generation,archive_monotonic_counter,published_at,time_authority_profile_oid,signer_set_epoch,signature}`. The returned descriptor and every locator byte/digest must equal the request-bound prebuilt descriptor; provider substitution is impossible. It contains no local `StrongRef`; its transcript covers every field except `signature`, every status/result byte from the archive authority binds the same request, and exact retry returns the same generation/bytes. A different request under the same idempotency token is a permanent conflict. `BackupTransparencyInclusion` is the later portable `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=BackupAppendTransparency},backup_id,publication_bundle_id,publication_receipt_digest,manifest_canonical_digest,log_id,leaf_index,tree_size,root_hash,inclusion_path,checkpoint_bytes,checkpoint_signatures,cross_log_consistency_evidence?,included_at,time_authority_profile_oid}` and verifies the body-declared policy and prior checkpoint. A NotRequired body makes this object illegal; a Required body makes a valid inclusion mandatory before the grant below.

Every external backup effect is rooted before transport. `BackupExternalOperationKind` is the closed union `PublishEnvelope|AppendTransparency|IssueArchiveGrant|RenewArchiveGrant|PrepareSourceReleaseHold|ActivateSourceReleaseHold`; hold completion remains the separately typed `ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionOperationRecord` below. `BackupExternalOperationSpecRef<Role>` is the generated exact union `Publish{spec_ref:StrongRef<BackupPublicationAuthorizeSpec<Role>>}|Transparency{spec_ref:StrongRef<BackupTransparencyAppendAuthorizeSpec<Role>>}|GrantIssue{spec_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveGrantIssueAuthorizeSpec<Role>>}|GrantRenew{spec_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveGrantRenewAuthorizeSpec<Role>>}|HoldPrepare{spec_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveSourceReleaseHoldPrepareAuthorizeSpec<Role>>}|HoldActivate{spec_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveSourceReleaseHoldActivateAuthorizeSpec<Role>>}`; action tags and reference targets are noninterchangeable. `BackupExternalOperationRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the immutable Semantic authorization `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,backup_id,operation_kind,operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,target_authority_id,canonical_action_body_recipe_and_digest,exact_subject_and_predecessor_commitment,expected_backup_registry_generation,source_spec:BackupExternalOperationSpecRef<Role>,state:AuthorizedUndispatchedRecipe,body_digest}`. Each exact authorize spec owns the current manifest/receipt/inclusion/grant/head inputs required by its action, expected predecessor CAS/head, immutable action-body recipe, resource reservation, and terminal audit gate; it contains no future publication evidence, full request binding, dispatch attempt, or receipt.

Only the authorizing candidate's role-valid `PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Role>` may initialize Protocol dispatch. `BackupExternalDispatchInitializeSpec<Role>` byte-matches that certificate and deterministically constructs the full request as `ExternalActionRequestBinding{source_publication_evidence_commitment,...} + canonical_action_body`; the complete request digest then lives in `IrreversibleDispatchAttempt<ExactBackupAction>::Armed|SentUnknown`, never in the earlier Semantic record. The universal mark-SentUnknown, current authority/time/continuity/registry guard, byte-identical send, authenticated status-first recovery, receipt import, and action-typed `PortableBackupExternalOperationTerminalEvidence` rules apply. A Semantic completion/import transition must byte-match the authorization record, publication evidence, full request, portable terminal evidence, provider result, and direct external predecessor/successor relation. No direct archive/transparency/grant/hold call exists, and a timeout, unavailable status, or signed NotRegistered after SentUnknown permits only exact retry—not abort or request substitution.

The six authorize bodies are exact. `BackupPublicationAuthorizeSpec<Role>` binds the sealed manifest/body/signature/content-inventory identities, publication bundle/authority, durability profile, operation/idempotency identities, expected registry state `Sealed`, and action-body recipe. `BackupTransparencyAppendAuthorizeSpec<Role>` binds the imported publication receipt, Required policy/prior checkpoint, log authority/head, leaf body, operation/idempotency identities, and expected `PublicationReceiptedPendingTransparency`. `ArchiveGrantIssueAuthorizeSpec<Role>` binds the receipt plus NotRequired policy or imported inclusion, exact immutable grant body/subject set, expected grant-authority predecessor/head, operation/idempotency identities, and the appropriate pending-grant state. `ArchiveGrantRenewAuthorizeSpec<Role>` binds the selected Active generation/head, identical subject-set proof, successor window/body, and expected Published-or-later nonterminal state. `ArchiveSourceReleaseHoldPrepareAuthorizeSpec<Role>` binds the verified artifact, exact participant set, release generation, current grant/head, Prepared window, and expected `ArtifactVerified`; `ArchiveSourceReleaseHoldActivateAuthorizeSpec<Role>` binds the imported Prepared hold/head, same participant/release generation, no-pin-release-yet proof, and expected `HoldPrepared`. Every body includes the complete authority header, resource reservation, and terminal audit gate and has a distinct command-contract row, operation-result type, portable receipt type, and negative cross-action fixture.

The publication sequence is therefore `Sealed -> PublicationAuthorizedUndispatched -> PublicationReceiptedPendingTransparency|PublicationReceiptedPendingGrant -> TransparencyAuthorizedUndispatched -> TransparencyIncludedPendingGrant -> GrantIssueAuthorizedUndispatched -> Published`. The NotRequired policy skips only the transparency operation, never publication or grant issuance. Grant renewal creates `GrantRenewalAuthorizedUndispatched` while the prior Active generation remains selected; exact imported renewal atomically selects the direct successor and preserves the identical subject set. Hold preparation/activation/completion use the same operation family and their exact external head predecessors. Local and Meta use distinct role tags but identical generated state/dispatch contracts; Shard never originates an archive-authority operation.

`ArchiveRetentionGrant` is the immutable successor record `{backup_id,publication_bundle_id,archive_authority_id,grant_id,generation,predecessor_grant_digest:WeakDigest?,subject_set:{content_archive_inventory_root_digest,content_archive_inventory_count,manifest_body_digest,manifest_signature_set_digest,manifest_canonical_digest,publication_receipt_digest,recovery_bootstrap_descriptor_digest,transparency:NotRequired{policy_digest}|Included{inclusion_digest,checkpoint_digest}},permitted_recovery_and_reader_authority_digest,minimum_retain_through,maximum_lease_through,renewal_policy_oid,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,archive_monotonic_counter,status:Active|Revoked{authorized_reason,revocation_evidence_digest}|Expired{time_evidence_digest},external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=IssueArchiveGrant|RenewArchiveGrant},signer_set_epoch,signature}`. The subject set excludes the grant itself. Generation one has no predecessor; renewal preserves every subject and increments generation/counter with checked arithmetic. Early revoke before `minimum_retain_through` requires the manifest-declared exceptional authority and cannot claim deletion; expiry/revocation only makes resolution fail closed. Source-pin release requires an `Active` grant whose usable window and minimum-retention promise cover the publication contract. Construction is exactly `content inventory -> BackupManifestBody -> BackupManifestSignatureSet -> BackupManifest -> rooted publication operation/receipt including the bounded bootstrap descriptor -> rooted required transparency operation/inclusion -> rooted grant-issuance operation/Active ArchiveRetentionGrant -> BackupArtifactReopenProof`; no object contains its own digest or a future receipt/signature/inclusion/grant/proof. A released manifest contains only source identities and archive refs, never a live source `StrongRef` or CRSR.

Grant freshness is externally linearized. `ArchiveGrantAuthorityHead` is the archive authority's CAS chain `{archive_authority_id,grant_id,cas_version,current_generation,current_grant_digest,current_status:Active|Revoked|Expired,current_subject_set_digest,archive_monotonic_counter,active_source_release_hold:None|Prepared{hold_id,backup_id,release_generation,participant_set_digest,expires_at}|Activated{hold_id,backup_id,release_generation,participant_set_digest,activated_counter}|Completed{hold_id,backup_id,release_generation,participant_set_digest,source_release_evidence_digest,completion_counter},predecessor_head_digest,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. The Completed head contains no future receipt ref/digest. A successor generation, revoke, expire, hold prepare/activate, or hold complete must name the direct predecessor; no skip/fork is valid. While an Activated hold exists, the authority rejects revoke/expire/subject removal and retains the complete subject set. `PortableArchiveGrantAuthorityObservation` is the challenged, reference-free signed `{archive_authority_id,grant_id,current_head_digest,current_cas_version,current_generation,current_grant_digest,current_status,current_subject_set_digest,archive_monotonic_counter,active_source_release_hold_projection,intended_action:ArtifactReopen|ArtifactVerify|RestoreSourceLeaseRenew{lease_identity,expected_lease_generation}|LocalSourcePinRelease|GlobalSourceReleaseAuthorize|ShardSourcePinRelease{shard_id}|GlobalSourceReleaseFinalize|SourceReleaseCompletionImport{posture:Local|Sharded,release_generation},action_subject_digest,challenge_nonce,authority_tick,time_authority_observation_digest,time_authority_profile_oid,time_observation_interval:AuthorityInterval,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. `ArchiveGrantAuthorityObservationImport<Role>` is the exact role-local `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,role_and_group,current_configuration_writer_fence_and_publication_commitment,portable_observation_ref:StrongRef<PortableArchiveGrantAuthorityObservation>,time_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityObservationImport>,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,exact_authority_projection_grant_time_and_action_transcript_match_digest,imported_at_maintenance_identity}`. Local and Meta arms carry their complete authority header; the Shard arm carries only the exact certified authority projection and action-clipped grant projection. Presentation creates a linear `ArchiveGrantUseGuard<Role>` only from this exact role-bound import; a stale generation/head, different action/subject, role/group/configuration/fence mismatch, non-Active status where Active is required, absent/mismatched activated or completed hold for the selected action, or profile/window failure rejects. Portable backup, restore, or archive evidence contains only the portable observation or its digest, never the import or either local time reference.

`ArchiveSourceReleaseHold` is the portable `{archive_authority_id,grant_id,backup_id,hold_id,release_generation,participant_set_digest,state:Prepared{time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at}|Activated{activation_head_digest,activation_cas_version,activation_counter,no_time_based_release:true}|Completed{completion_receipt_identity,completion_receipt_digest,completion_head_digest,completion_counter},subject_set_digest,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}` projection of the matching head and release contract. Local release prepares and activates a hold for participant set `{Local}` before ordering its pin release. Meta does the same for the exact Meta-plus-shard set before `GlobalBackupReleaseAuthorizeSpec`; activation is externally durable before any shard can see authorization. Prepared holds may expire only before activation and before any pin release. Activated holds have no time-based escape hatch: they complete only after the exact Local source-release record or Global source-release record plus every shard acknowledgement is presented to the archive authority. Crash, timeout, or partial release therefore retains the artifact while recovery completes the same release generation.

`ArchiveSourceReleaseEvidence` is the closed portable union `Local{backup_id,release_generation,local_release_applied_identity,post_release_root_identity,no_source_pin_proof_digest,audit_release_evidence,role_signatures}|Sharded{backup_id,release_generation,meta_release_applied_identity,post_release_global_root_identity,meta_no_source_pin_proof_digest,exact_sorted_shard_release_acks[PortableShardBackupReleaseAck],complete_participant_bijection_digest,audit_release_evidence,meta_signatures}`. It contains no Local/Meta/Shard `StrongRef` or CRSR. The releasing Semantic command also creates immutable `ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionOperationRecord {operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,archive_authority_id,grant_id,backup_id,hold_id,release_generation,activated_head_digest,source_release_evidence_digest,participant_set_digest,canonical_request_transcript_digest,state:AuthorizedUndispatchedRecipe}`.

Only after that release candidate's `PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local|Meta>` exists may `ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionDispatchInitializeSpec` create `IrreversibleDispatchAttempt<ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletion>::Armed`; the universal mark-SentUnknown, status-first recovery, fresh release-hold/continuity/authority/time guard, and external receipt-import rules apply. The archive authority durably registers the exact operation/request lineage, verifies the Activated hold, subject, participant set, role/configuration signatures, audit release, and source-pin absence, CASes the direct Activated head to the root-known Completed arm, and only then emits `ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionReceipt {external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletion},archive_authority_id,grant_id,backup_id,hold_id,release_generation,activated_head_digest,completion_head_digest,completion_cas_version,completion_counter,source_release_evidence_digest,participant_set_digest,subject_set_digest,completed_at,time_authority_profile_oid,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. Exact retry/status returns identical terminal bytes; a different evidence set, partial participants, stale head, or competing generation fails. Protocol receipt import produces `PortableArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionTerminalEvidence` with no Protocol ObjectId.

`ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionImportSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole> {backup_id,expected_registry_state:ReleasedPendingArchiveHoldCompletion,operation_record_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionOperationRecord>,activated_hold_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveSourceReleaseHold>,dispatch_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionTerminalEvidence>,completion_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveSourceReleaseCompletionReceipt>,completed_hold_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveSourceReleaseHold>,expected_prior_grant_projection_digest,current_completed_head_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveGrantAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,required_observation_action:SourceReleaseCompletionImport{posture,release_generation},terminal_audit_gate}` is the only ordered transition to compact `Released`. The completed hold is built after the existing receipt and carries only its portable identity/digest; the head contains no receipt edge. The spec byte-matches the dispatch lineage and direct CAS successor and creates the completed `ArchiveGrantAuthorityProjection<Role>`. Until this command and its audit visibility advance exist, checkpoint/backup/GC floors retain the release evidence, all shard acknowledgements, operation/dispatch attempt, Activated hold, observation import, and artifact. Only a later activated cut may replace that heavy closure with the compact completed receipt/hold/grant identities.

`ArchiveGrantAuthorityProjection<Role>` is the immutable role-local `{role_and_group,archive_authority_id,grant_id,selected_head_digest,selected_cas_version,selected_generation,selected_grant_digest,selected_status,selected_subject_set_digest,selected_archive_counter,selected_release_hold_projection?,source_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveGrantAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,predecessor_projection_digest:WeakDigest?,continuity_status:DirectSuccessor|PermanentGap}`. `ArchiveGrantAuthorityImportSpec<Role> {backup_id,expected_backup_registry_generation,expected_prior_projection_digest:WeakDigest?,authority_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveGrantAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,expected_direct_successor_or_genesis,current_grant_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveRetentionGrant>,hold_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveSourceReleaseHold>?,terminal_audit_gate}` orders each relied-on head/observation/hold transition into the role-valid backup registry. Apply accepts genesis or the exact direct successor, rejects rollback/fork/skip unless the archive authority supplies a separately audited permanent-gap reconciliation, and creates the projection; exact retry is idempotent. Artifact verification retains its observation import, while every pin-releasing order obtains a new current observation under the activated hold.

Backup proof and pin vocabularies are closed. `BackupPinnedClosureRef<Role>` is the generated tagged union of `RegisteredStrongRef` arms legal for the exact Local, Meta, or Shard source-payload schema; every value carries its `source_slot_tag`, and the associated coverage bitmap must equal every live mandatory-inventory class at the barrier exactly once. A role-invalid, untyped, duplicate, missing, or ambient strong edge is unencodable. `BackupInventoryBijectionProof` is `{backup_id,scope:Local|Meta|Shard{shard_id},source_pin_identity:WeakDigest,source_inventory_digest,archive_inventory_root_digest,canonical_sorted_source_to_archive_key_commitment,no_missing_source,no_extra_archive,no_duplicate_target,per_entry_length_and_digest_commitment,checker_version}`. `BackupRecoverabilityProof` is `{backup_id,scope,archive_inventory_root_digest,protection_profile_oid,required_failure_domain_predicate,observed_failure_domain_inventory,deterministic_decode_campaign_commitment,key_escrow_recovery_commitment,all_required_profiles_satisfied,checker_version}`.

Reopen proof is deliberately two-stage. `BackupContentReopenProof`, created before sealing by an independent fresh reader, is `{backup_id,scope,source_pin_identity:WeakDigest,archive_authority_id,archive_inventory_root_digest,recovery_projection_ref:BackupArchiveRef<BackupRecoveryProjection>,recovery_projection_digest,complete_slot_and_plane_coverage_verification_digest,authenticated_archive_access_receipt,precommitted_challenge_digest,opened_entry_commitment,decoded_root_and_sample_commitment,recomputed_semantic_digest,reader_software_and_profile_oid,observed_at,signer_epoch,signature}`; it independently decodes the projection and verifies every role cut, disposition, and inventory edge, but names no future manifest, signature set, publication receipt, transparency inclusion, or retention grant. Copy verification owns the inventory, recoverability, and content-reopen proofs, and closure certificates/manifest body commit their digests.

`BackupArtifactReopenProof<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is necessarily post-publication and is `{role_and_group,backup_id,scope,manifest_ref:BackupPublicationArtifactRef<BackupManifest>,manifest_body_digest,manifest_signature_set_digest,recovery_projection_ref:BackupArchiveRef<BackupRecoveryProjection>,recovery_projection_digest,complete_slot_and_plane_coverage_verification_digest,publication_operation_identity_and_request_digest,publication_dispatch_and_provider_status_lineage_commitment,publication_receipt_ref:StrongRef<BackupPublicationReceipt>,transparency_state:NotRequired{policy_digest}|Included{operation_identity_and_request_digest,dispatch_and_provider_status_lineage_commitment,inclusion_ref:StrongRef<BackupTransparencyInclusion>,checkpoint_digest},grant_issue_operation_identity_and_request_digest,grant_dispatch_and_provider_status_lineage_commitment,archive_retention_grant_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveRetentionGrant>,grant_authority_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveGrantAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,required_observation_action:ArtifactReopen,archive_inventory_root_digest,authenticated_fresh_archive_access_receipt,precommitted_artifact_challenge_digest,opened_manifest_signature_receipt_grant_head_and_inventory_commitment,verified_key_escrow_and_decoder_profile_commitment,decoded_root_and_required_sample_commitment,recomputed_semantic_digest,reader_software_and_profile_oid,observed_at,time_authority_profile_oid,signer_epoch,signature}`. A different independent fresh reader opens the actually published portable artifact, verifies the manifest artifact reference against the exact rooted operation/request/status/receipt locator lineage, exact manifest bytes/signatures, projection identity and completeness, required transparency operation/inclusion, grant operation/current head, inventory, key recovery material, and every Semantic/Protocol/Prepared/Consensus/bootstrap recovery recipe while consuming an action-bound current-grant use guard. The object cannot be committed by the manifest it validates. Only this artifact proof—not the earlier content proof—may establish content/publication equivalence; pin release additionally requires a new current observation import under an Activated source-release hold at the release command. Required transparency remains an explicit nonterminal state until inclusion; optional/not-required policy is signed in the manifest and cannot be inferred from absence. Lost-response retry returns the same proof identity, while any operation, idempotency key, provider status lineage, manifest, projection, signature, receipt, grant/head generation, policy, inventory, key, or semantic drift fails closed.

Local backup starts with `LocalBackupBarrierSpec {backup_id,authorized_request_digest,expected_source_root_manifest_ref:StrongRef<RootManifest>,expected_logical_state_root_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStateRoot>,expected_logical_command_seq,expected_commit_seq,expected_raft_index,expected_checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>,expected_checkpoint_vector_digest,expected_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,expected_config_payload_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>,expected_prepared_root_generation,expected_certificate_ledger_digest,expected_retained_history_interval,archive_profile_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveProfile>,recipient_and_key_recovery_profile_ref:StrongRef<KeyRecoveryProfile>,format_matrix_ref:StrongRef<FormatMatrix>,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply requires `Operational` and exact equality, then creates `LocalBackupPinRecord {backup_id,source_domain:ConsensusDomain,barrier_logical_command_seq,barrier_commit_seq,barrier_raft_index,source_root_manifest_ref:StrongRef<RootManifest>,source_logical_state_root_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStateRoot>,pinned_closure_refs[BackupPinnedClosureRef<Local>],pinned_closure_coverage_bitmap,complete_logical_and_physical_inventory_root_ref:StrongRef<BackupSourceInventory>,pin_generation}`. The generated Local union includes the exact checkpoint/vector, current/joint/prospective configuration and floor, prepared/certificate/Raft, retained stream/history, key/envelope/grant/wrap, identity/privacy/audit/continuity, replay/cursor/delivery/derived, and remote-trust/retention classes previously grouped informally. This ordered record, not the manifest, is the source retention lease.

After copy and an independent content reopen, `LocalBackupCopyVerification {backup_id,pin_identity:WeakDigest,sorted_source_to_archive_entries[{source:BackupSourceIdentity,archive:BackupArchiveRef}],complete_inventory_bijection_proof_ref:StrongRef<BackupInventoryBijectionProof>,per_object_decode_and_digest_results,recoverability_and_failure_domain_proof_ref:StrongRef<BackupRecoverabilityProof>,content_reopen_proof_ref:StrongRef<BackupContentReopenProof>,canonical_semantic_digest}` and `LocalBackupClosureCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,backup_id,barrier_coordinates,source_state:BackupSourceIdentity<LogicalStateRoot>,copy_verification_ref:StrongRef<LocalBackupCopyVerification>,archived_state_ref:BackupArchiveRef<LogicalStateRoot>,complete_archive_inventory_digest,inventory_bijection_proof_digest,recoverability_proof_digest,content_reopen_proof_digest,semantic_digest,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}` contain no retaining source edge.

The Local registry is exactly `Absent -> Pinned{pin_ref} -> ClosureCertified{certificate_ref} -> Sealed{manifest_ref} -> PublicationAuthorizedUndispatched{operation_record_ref} -> PublicationReceiptedPendingTransparency{receipt_ref,required_policy_digest}|PublicationReceiptedPendingGrant{receipt_ref}`; the Required arm continues `-> TransparencyAuthorizedUndispatched{operation_record_ref} -> TransparencyIncludedPendingGrant{inclusion_ref} -> GrantIssueAuthorizedUndispatched{operation_record_ref}`, while the NotRequired arm continues directly `-> GrantIssueAuthorizedUndispatched{operation_record_ref}`. Both converge at `Published{receipt_ref,transparency:NotRequired|Included,grant_ref,selected_grant_projection_ref} -> ArtifactVerified{artifact_proof_ref,verification_observation_import_ref} -> HoldPrepareAuthorizedUndispatched{operation_record_ref} -> HoldPrepared{hold_ref} -> HoldActivateAuthorizedUndispatched{operation_record_ref} -> HoldActivated{hold_ref} -> ReleasedPendingArchiveHoldCompletion{source_release_evidence_ref,activated_hold_ref} -> Released{manifest_identity,archive_grant_identity,artifact_proof_identity,completed_hold_identity,completion_receipt_identity}`. `LocalBackupClosurePublishSpec` and `LocalBackupSealSpec` retain their exact pin/copy/certificate/manifest fields above. `LocalBackupPublicationAuthorizeSpec` is the sole transition out of Sealed and creates the exact publication operation record; it performs no I/O. `LocalBackupPublicationReceiptImportSpec` consumes the portable dispatch terminal evidence and receipt, then selects the policy-matched pending state. Required manifests next authorize/import transparency and then grant issuance; NotRequired manifests authorize grant issuance directly.
