The mandatory order is every-group admission freeze -> ordered and audit-visible Local/Meta rotation intent -> universal-dispatch external issuance close -> ordered Local/Meta close plus certified Shard close applies -> every-group reservation drain and quiescence closure -> every offline issuer fence -> universal-dispatch final issuance fence -> post-fence Local or Meta-plus-all-shards inventory closure -> drain hold -> dual-signed handoff plan -> universal-dispatch external registry CAS/receipt -> ordered Local/Meta transition -> successor issuance. Before any close send, typed abort may reopen the exact unchanged freezes. From `SentUnknown` close onward, and especially after the final fence, only structural roll-forward authority exists. Every old-profile subject has a finite checked expiry no later than the derived drain bound. Renewable leases/grants move to the successor before their old window ends; immutable capabilities and attenuated offline tokens remain verifiable under the old profile until expiry.

Exact activation is `TimeAuthorityProfileTransitionSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole> {completion_authority:TimeAuthorityTransitionCompletionAuthority<Role>,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,original_admin_authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>>,rotation_intent_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityRotationIntentRecord<Role>>,expected_time_authority_registry_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityRegistry<Role>>,expected_registry_generation,expected_selected_profile_state:IssuanceQuiescent,retiring_profile_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityProfile>,successor_profile_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityProfile>,issuance_closing_receipt_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityIssuanceClosingReceipt>,issuance_fence_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityIssuanceFence>,epoch_transition_plan_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityEpochTransitionPlan>,external_registry_transition_receipt_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityRegistryTransitionReceipt>,epoch_transition_record_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityEpochTransitionRecord<Role>>,old_profile_subject_inventory_closure_ref:StrongRef<RoleTimeBoundSubjectInventoryClosure<Role>>,old_observation_and_verifier_retention_hold_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityDrainHold<Role>>,configuration_retention_basis:RoleConfigurationRetentionBasis<Role>,drain_floor_set:RoleTimeAuthorityDrainFloorSet<Role>,terminal_audit_gate}`. It verifies the complete intent/dispatch/closing/quiescence/fence/inventory bijection and atomically selects the successor for new issuance while retaining the predecessor as Draining. Completion cannot widen the original authorization, change the successor, sets, operation IDs, or resources, or solicit a fresh client choice after the irreversible fence.

Exact retirement is `TimeAuthorityProfileRetirementSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole> {cleanup_authority:TimeAuthorityRetirementCleanupAuthority<Role>,authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,expected_time_authority_registry_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityRegistry<Role>>,expected_registry_generation,epoch_transition_record_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityEpochTransitionRecord<Role>>,retiring_profile_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityProfile>,successor_profile_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityProfile>,complete_current_root_subject_scan_ref:StrongRef<TimeBoundSubjectInventory<Role>>,sorted_subject_dispositions[TimeSubjectDisposition<Role>],complete_subject_bijection_proof_ref:StrongRef<TimeBoundSubjectRetirementProof<Role>>,old_observation_service_shutdown_receipt_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityShutdownReceipt>,old_verifier_key_retirement_floor_ref:StrongRef<VerifierKeyRetirementFloor>,configuration_retention_basis:RoleConfigurationRetentionBasis<Role>,retirement_floor_set:RoleTimeAuthorityRetirementFloorSet<Role>,terminal_summary_recipe,terminal_audit_gate}`. Every inventory row appears exactly once. Meta's retirement floor set contains one matching ack from every configured shard. Apply first verifies the heavy closure, deterministically creates `TimeAuthorityTransitionTerminalSummary<Role>`, selects `Retired{terminal_summary_ref}`, and releases the drain hold only after shutdown, zero-dependency verifier/offline-issuer floors, shard floors, and paired all-plane/configuration floors are durable. The detailed proof/transition/admin-authority closure survives until the retirement candidate is audit-visible and its cut activates, then becomes collectible; no terminal state indefinitely retains completion or cleanup authority. Activation/retirement, issuance closing/reservations/quiescence, compaction, and every failure boundary are permanent registry/model/DPOR gates.

`PortableContinuityAuthorityObservation` is the continuity authority's challenged, reference-free signed `{database_security_namespace_id,continuity_profile_id,current_continuity_record_digest,current_cas_version,cluster_incarnation,member_writer_id,lease_generation,scope,lease_status:Active,authority_tick,time_authority_observation_digest,time_authority_profile_oid,time_observation_interval:AuthorityInterval,intended_irreversible_action_digest,challenge_nonce,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. `ShardContinuityAuthorityProjection` is the exact Shard-local `{source_meta_prefix,source_meta_configuration_identity,portable_continuity_head_and_lease_scope,source_head_digest_and_cas_version,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,typed_projection_payload_identity_and_digest,projection_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<MetaControlProjectionCertificate>,retention_grant_and_payload_availability_evidence,body_digest}` whose certificate uses the `TypedCertifiedMetaProjectionPayload::ContinuityAuthority` arm. `ContinuityAuthorityCurrentBasis<Role>` is the generated closed role union `Local{current_root_manifest_and_slot_identity,current_logical_state_root_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStateRoot>,current_continuity_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>,current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,writer_fence_epoch}|Meta{current_root_manifest_and_slot_identity,current_global_state_root_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStateRoot>,current_continuity_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>,current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,writer_fence_epoch}|Shard{current_root_manifest_and_slot_identity,current_active_meta_projection_root_ref:StrongRef<ActiveMetaProjectionRoot>,continuity_projection_slot_ref:StrongRef<ShardContinuityAuthorityProjection>,source_meta_prefix_and_configuration,writer_fence_epoch}`. The Shard projection contains the complete portable continuity head/lease scope plus its certified Meta source, configuration, retention, and trust evidence; it is never a digest-only head.

`ContinuityAuthorityObservationImport<Role>` is the role-local `{role_and_group,current_basis:ContinuityAuthorityCurrentBasis<Role>,portable_observation_ref:StrongRef<PortableContinuityAuthorityObservation>,time_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityObservationImport>,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,exact_current_basis_observation_time_and_action_match_digest,imported_at_maintenance_identity}`. The imported head/CAS/incarnation/member/generation/scope must equal the current rooted basis, not merely a valid authority signature. `IncarnationLeaseGuard` exists only from the exact current role-bound import for the same member/generation/scope/window/action and same current process-incarnation time budget. Every segment/object/root seal, prepared-root publication, receipt, Raft proposal/ack, certificate, and RootSlot publication consumes a fresh action-bound guard. Cached validity, canonical HLC, OS wall time, or a signature over an older/uninstalled continuity head never authorizes a write. Portable continuity receipts and certificates carry only the portable observation or its digest.

`ClusterIncarnationLease` is `{continuity_record_digest,cas_version,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,restore_id?,member_writer_id,scope:RecoveryOnly|OrdinaryWrite|RoleTransitionOnly,lease_generation,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,nonce,authority_signature}`. `CommitCx` contains a nonconstructible `IncarnationLeaseGuard`; recovery gets `RecoveryCommitCx` with only RecoveryOnly and role migration gets a guard whose encoders accept only transition objects. Immediately before every irreversible action, code validates the inode lease/fence, exact external continuity head/CAS version, action-bound authority observation/evidence, live member lease, and operation scope. Authority outage, ambiguity, expiry, or rollback poisons publication; cached validity is insufficient. `RootSlot` and every role payload carry the continuity digest/CAS version, service visibility epoch, and time-authority binding.

Prior-incarnation/archive edges use exact nonlocal types. `PriorIncarnationObjectRef<T> {restore_id,source_backup_manifest_digest,source_cluster_incarnation,target_cluster_incarnation,source:BackupArchiveRef<T>}` is legal only for content-inventory objects in restore plans/results/bridges/evidence/provenance and never enters the current mark set; `T=BackupManifest|BackupManifestBody|BackupManifestSignatureSet` is a generated negative. `RestoreSourceLeaseIdentity` is `{restore_id,source_backup_identity:{source_database_id,source_database_security_namespace_id,source_cluster_incarnation,backup_id,backup_manifest_digest,publication_bundle_id,archive_retention_grant_id,archive_grant_subject_set_digest,archive_inventory_root_digest,permitted_inventory_digest},target_holder_identity:{target_database_id,target_database_security_namespace_id,target_incarnation,target_service_visibility_epoch,recovery_authority_digest},archive_authority_id,lease_lineage_id,holder_authority_digest}`. Stable identity deliberately excludes grant and lease generations. `PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest` is `BLAKE3("fgdb.restore-source-lease-state.v1" || canonical(lease_identity,generation,selected_grant_generation_and_digest,window_profile_domain_epoch_and_bounds,archive_counter,holder_and_subject_set_commitment))`; it is derived entirely from portable archive-authority state and is never a keyed Local/Meta ObjectId or body digest. `PortableArchiveGrantLineageProof` is the reference-free signed `{archive_authority_id,grant_id,selected_generation,selected_grant_digest,ordered_predecessor_generation_and_digest_chain,current_head_digest_and_cas_version,unchanged_subject_set_digest,window_coverage,archive_monotonic_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. `RestoreSourceLeaseAcquireReceipt` and `RestoreSourceLeaseRenewReceipt` are distinct portable external receipts with `ExternalActionRequestBinding`, the stable lease identity, exact predecessor generation and portable lease-state digest for renewal, selected grant generation/digest, complete signed `PortableRestoreSourceLeaseLineageProof` bytes and digest, resulting lease generation/window/counter, resulting `PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest`, and signature. The lineage proof names request/result-head commitments but never its enclosing receipt digest, so receipt construction remains acyclic.

Before a target database exists, `PreBootstrapRestoreSourceLeaseOwner` is a registered prebootstrap artifact schema, not a logical object: `{identity:RestoreSourceLeaseIdentity,generation,portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,predecessor_owner_digest?,portable_plan_digest,source_gate_digest,record_kind:Acquire{receipt_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<RestoreSourceLeaseAcquireReceipt>}|Renew{receipt_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRenewReceipt>},selected_archive_grant_generation_and_digest,grant_lineage_proof_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PortableArchiveGrantLineageProof>,lease_window_successor_proof_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<LeaseWindowSuccessorProof>?,lease_lineage_proof_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PortableRestoreSourceLeaseLineageProof>,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,archive_monotonic_counter,recovery_authority_public_commitment,journal_id,owner_publication_journal_head_generation_digest_and_cas_version,owner_publication_dispatch_state_generation_and_digest,relocation_chain_head_commitment,body_digest}`. It deliberately contains no inode, path, local mirror identity, or mutable dispatch-root locator. The acquire/renew terminal payload must journal the complete signed lineage proof before this owner can become Current; a digest-only result is invalid after restart or relocation. `PreBootstrapRestoreSourceLeaseCurrent` stores `owner_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PreBootstrapRestoreSourceLeaseOwner>` plus checked generation/portable-state/lineage commitment and resolves it only through the authenticated current journal head and relocation chain. Acquire creates generation one; registered prebootstrap renewal creates `g+1`, advances Current, and makes the prior artifact reclaimable only after the successor/head/capsule publication is durable. Relocating the cache inode changes neither owner identity nor authority; a stale mirror that cannot prove the current head/relocation chain is unusable. This owner is never a current-database authority record.

`LeaseWindowSuccessorProof` is an independently addressable registered logical schema with the closed body `SameEpochNonshrinking{lease_identity,prior_and_successor_generations,same_profile_domain_epoch,prior_and_successor_bounds,checked_nonshrinking_bounds_and_counter,archive_authority_signature}|CrossEpochHandoff{lease_identity,prior_and_successor_generations,old_and_new_profile_identities,dual_signed_transition_proof,no_gap_coverage_proof,checked_domain_mapping,archive_authority_signature}`. `PortableRestoreSourceLeaseLineageCheckpoint` is the reference-free signed contiguous-range summary `{lease_identity,start_generation_and_predecessor_head,end_generation_and_complete_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,ordered_operation_id_idempotency_request_result_head_and_outcome_accumulator,complete_generation_range_and_no_fork_proof,unchanged_holder_subject_archive_authority_and_permitted_inventory_commitment,start_and_end_archive_counters,predecessor_checkpoint:Genesis|Prior{complete_checkpoint_bytes_and_digest},provider_status_and_operation_nonreuse_floor,checkpoint_availability_and_retention_certificate,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. It cannot omit a generation, summarize an unresolved operation, change subject/holder/inventory, or refer only to unavailable bytes. `PortableRestoreSourceLeaseLineageProof` is the reference-free signed `{lease_identity,selected_generation,selected_portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,lineage_basis:Genesis|Checkpointed{complete_prior_checkpoint_bytes_and_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseLineageCheckpoint},ordered_generation_operation_request_and_result_head_commitments_after_basis,unchanged_holder_subject_and_archive_authority_commitment,current_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,archive_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`; it never names a keyed Local/Meta record or body digest. Prebootstrap owner generations retain the complete checkpoint artifact; canonical `RestoreSourceLeaseLineageCheckpointImport<Role>` carries a full `AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>`, the complete portable checkpoint, current lease-lineage proof, and exact byte-match proof. The current canonical lease record and then its release operation summary retain the latest import through the provider status/nonreuse/retention floor; checked lease-history compaction may replace earlier details only when the checkpoint covers them and remains independently available.

Canonical `RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,identity:RestoreSourceLeaseIdentity,generation,portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,predecessor:Genesis|Prior{record_digest:WeakDigest},record_kind:AcquireImported{prebootstrap_owner_digest,receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseAcquireReceipt>}|RenewImported{receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRenewReceipt>,lease_window_successor_proof_ref:StrongRef<LeaseWindowSuccessorProof>},selected_archive_grant_generation,selected_archive_grant_digest,grant_lineage_proof_ref:StrongRef<PortableArchiveGrantLineageProof>,lease_lineage_proof_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreSourceLeaseLineageProof>,lineage_basis:Genesis|Checkpointed{checkpoint_import_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseLineageCheckpointImport<Role>>},time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,archive_monotonic_counter,body_digest}`. The portable state digest byte-matches the receipt, authority head, observations, proofs, checkpoint basis, and all portable projections; the keyed role-local `body_digest` and ObjectId remain separate. The first Local/Meta bridge imports the latest prebootstrap generation—not necessarily generation one—with the full current authority header. Later renewal constructs `prior+1`, preserves all stable identities/subject sets, advances the counter, and carries exact successor and lineage proofs; raw tick comparison across domains or authority epochs is invalid. The prior visible registry strongly owns the old record until renewal finalize becomes visible, after which the weak predecessor digest plus signed lineage proof/checkpoint avoids an unbounded strong chain without making historical status evidence disappear.

`ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjectionSource` is the closed union `Bootstrap{bootstrap_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardBootstrapProjectionCertificate>,bootstrap_projection_identity_and_digest,source_lease_projection_payload_identity_and_digest}|Refresh{meta_control_projection_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<MetaControlProjectionCertificate>,typed_meta_projection_payload_identity_and_digest}`. `ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjection` is `{source:ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjectionSource,source_meta_prefix,source_meta_configuration_identity,lease_identity,generation,portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,selected_grant_generation_and_digest,window_profile_domain_epoch_and_bounds,archive_counter,holder_and_subject_set_commitment,retention_grant_and_payload_availability_evidence,body_digest}`. Bootstrap validates the exact `RestoreShardBootstrapProjectionCertificate` and its certificate-free payload; Refresh uses only `TypedCertifiedMetaProjectionPayload::RestoreSourceLease`, must name the same restore/plan/shard/lease lineage and unchanged holder/subject/inventory/recipient commitments, and may advance only `g -> g+1` with the exact successor proof. A Shard never owns a canonical lease record and cannot renew or release it.

`RestoreArchiveRef<T> {source:PriorIncarnationObjectRef<T>,lease_identity:RestoreSourceLeaseIdentity,permitted_inventory_leaf,leaf_proof}` contains no strong lease-generation edge. `RestorePublicationArtifactRef<T> {source:BackupPublicationArtifactRef<T>,lease_identity:RestoreSourceLeaseIdentity,permitted_grant_subject_leaf,grant_subject_leaf_proof,publication_receipt_binding}` is legal only for exact publication-envelope artifact kinds and proves the locator row against the lease-bound publication receipt/grant subject set. Every reference and registry common field must match both source and target identities. Before the bridge, resolution uses the current prebootstrap owner; afterward Local/Meta resolution requires the latest canonical live lease record and Shard resolution requires the exact current certified projection, plus subject-preserving grant lineage, `TimeValidationEvidence::Usable`, and the exact inventory/grant proof.

Source acquisition precedes canonical target creation and is externally recoverable. Pre-send `RestoreIdentityKeyPlan` is the closed union `PreserveSameIdentity{identical_namespace_basis,source_k_oid_identity_and_commitment,recover_and_rewrap_operation_recipe,target_rewrap_recipient_commitment,required_key_equality_proof_profile,required_zero_plaintext_persistence_proof_profile}|CloneFreshIdentity{distinct_namespace_basis,source_k_oid_source_open_only_commitment,recover_source_k_oid_for_decode_operation_recipe,create_target_k_oid_operation_recipe,target_k_oid_recipient_commitment,required_source_key_nontransplant_proof_profile}`. It contains only recipes, expected identities, and proof profiles. Post-provider `RestoreIdentityKeyDispositionEvidence` is the distinct closed union `PreservedSameIdentity{plan_digest,identical_namespace_proof,recover_and_rewrap_receipt_ref:RestoreKeyProviderReceiptRef,source_k_oid_commitment,target_rewrapped_k_oid_commitment,key_equality_proof,zero_plaintext_persistence_proof}|ClonedFreshIdentity{plan_digest,distinct_namespace_proof,recover_source_k_oid_receipt_ref:RestoreKeyProviderReceiptRef,create_target_k_oid_receipt_ref:RestoreKeyProviderReceiptRef,source_k_oid_source_open_only_commitment,target_k_oid_commitment,source_key_nontransplant_proof}`. Clone source `K_oid` and DEKs authorize source decoding only; every target ciphertext receives a fresh target DEK under the newly created target identity.

`RestoreClaimedTargetAuthorityRecipe` is the pre-claim closed union `RecoverSameIdentity{expected_operational_or_fence_predecessor_digest_and_cas_version,restore_id,source_backup_identity,planned_target_incarnation,recovery_writer_authority_digest,service_visibility_epoch,prior_lease_fence_plan_commitment}|CloneNewIdentity{identity_reservation_id,expected_reserved_head_digest_and_cas_version,claim_id,claimant_identity,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,recovery_writer_authority_digest,service_visibility_epoch,continuity_profile:DirectoryBound{filesystem_profile_id,mount_device_directory_and_target_manifest_inode_identity,target_manifest_staging_inode_creation_nonce_digest,whole_inode_fence_identity,parent_directory_durability_recipe,fixed_local_continuity_recipe_and_digest,zero_existing_root_slot_proof_recipe,directory_bound_creation_evidence_recipe_digest}|ExternalCas{expected_continuity_predecessor_digest_and_cas_version,enter_clone_reserved_operation_recipe,recovery_only_lease_recipe}}`. It contains no resulting claimed/reserved head and no plan digest field that would make the plan self-referential. DirectoryBound carries no external continuity head or CAS recipe; ExternalCas requires both.

`RestoreSourceAcquisitionActionKey` is a plan key, not a second dispatch-action registry. Its generated total mapping selects exactly one live **RestorePreBootstrap contract row** in `irreversible_dispatch_contracts.toml`, whose action kind is one of `EnterRestoreFencePending|PriorIncarnationLeaseIssuerFence{issuer_key}|PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortRevoke{issuer_key,cohort_key}|EnterRestoreReserved|ReservationClaim{reservation_kind,reservation_id}|EnterCloneReserved|RestoreArchiveAcquire|CommitAcquisitionRefinementBootstrapOpeners|CommitAcquisitionRefinementVerifiedObjectInventory|RecoverAndRewrapPreservedObjectIdKey|RecoverSourceObjectIdKeyForDecode|CreateTargetObjectIdKey|RecoverSourceBootstrapDek{artifact_key}|RecoverSourceObjectDekForReprotect{inventory_ordinal}|RestoreArchiveLeaseRenew{family_id,ordinal}|RecoveryIncarnationLeaseAcquireOrRefresh{family_id,ordinal}|RestoreArchiveLeaseRelease|ReservationBurnBeforeUse{reservation_kind,reservation_id,claim_id?}|CatalogTombstoneRestoreTarget{catalog_profile}|RestoreContinuityAbandon|RevokeTransientRecoveryWrap{artifact_key}|DestroyFreshUnpublishedTargetObjectIdKey{artifact_key}|RestoreUnpublishedTargetPhysicalDelete{target_ordinal}`. Every key maps bijectively to one exact `dispatch_contract_id`, operation/body recipe, source-gate recipe, prebootstrap attempt/import type, resource relation, and cleanup disposition; a canonical-plane row for the same provider action is a different contract and cannot satisfy the mapping. No alias may rename or merge dispatch kinds. `EnterCloneReserved`, both refinement-commit actions, `RecoveryIncarnationLeaseAcquireOrRefresh`, and `RestoreContinuityAbandon` are ExternalCas-only. `CatalogTombstoneRestoreTarget` is inhabitable only for `ExternalCasCataloged|DirectoryBoundCataloged` and is forbidden for `DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog`. DirectoryBound refinement and target-authority transitions are local authenticated journal/RootSlot publications, not transport keys. Source `K_oid` and source DEK destruction are uninhabited; cleanup may revoke only transient recovery wraps/grants. Destruction is legal solely for a clone-created target `K_oid` with key-origin plus zero-canonical-publication proofs. `RetainedTombstonedNoUse` is a local resource disposition, never an externally dispatched action.

Repeated prebootstrap operations use a bounded family fixed before the first member can be authorized. `PreBootstrapActionFamilyRecipe` is `{family_id,action_kind:RestoreArchiveLeaseRenew|RecoveryIncarnationLeaseAcquireOrRefresh,max_members,member_key_and_operation_id_recipe,member_idempotency_recipe,stable_subject_and_action_body_invariants,per_member_predecessor_and_window_recipe,resource_budget,terminal_history_and_nonreuse_floor,body_digest}`. A member key is exactly `{family_id,ordinal}` with `0 <= ordinal < max_members`; the operation/idempotency identities are deterministic from the sealed recipe and ordinal, and no member may change the lease/continuity subject, holder/writer, scope, target incarnation, first-root action digest, or other stable action bytes. The source-renew recipe derives member `n` only from the journal-durable terminal result of `n-1`, binding the then-current Active head/CAS, generation, portable lease-state digest, grant lineage/counter, and requested successor window. The recovery-lease recipe likewise binds the current lease generation/status and requested successor window produced or observed after `n-1`. The next ordinal may be authorized only after every prior ordinal is terminal and the resulting current head is journal-authority durable. Family exhaustion permanently abandons this restore attempt; recovery may start only a new `restore_id` under the profile's required fresh claim and, for same-identity recovery, a strictly newer incarnation—never mutate or extend the committed family. Source-lease renewal and ExternalCas recovery-incarnation-lease acquire/refresh are the only v1 families; fixed issuer/cohort/reservation/key/refinement/release/cleanup actions remain singleton keys. `RestoreBridgeFreezeSpec` closes the source-lease-renew family at an exact final ordinal, proves every created renew member terminal and no later renew member authorized, consumes all source/projection guards, and leaves only the separately bounded recovery-incarnation refresh family open. The sealed import, terminal accumulator, availability certificate, action/resource bijection, status API, and nonreuse floors retain every member—not merely the family name.

Availability construction is acyclic and does not assume that one local staging inode survives the declared failure model. `PortableRestorePlanRecipe` contains all Phase-A plan fields with one typed `AvailabilityCertificateDigest` hole and action recipes with their later `PlanDigest` holes. Before any target claim or dispatch-capable local root, recovery creates stable random `journal_id`, nonexportable `K_restore_journal`, and exact `PreBootstrapRestoreRecoveryCapsule {format_version,restore_id,journal_id,plan_recipe_bytes_and_digest,recovery_or_admin_authority_bytes_and_digest,journal_key_commitment,wrapped_journal_key_bytes_and_digest,wrap_recipient_and_profile,journal_mac_profile_oid,initial_generation_capsule_recipe_digest,availability_service_identity_and_locators,required_failure_domain_policy,recovery_and_relocation_policy,resource_ceiling,body_digest}`. The capsule contains no plaintext journal key and is itself encrypted/authenticated to the recovery authority.

`PreBootstrapJournalGenerationCapsule` is the exact complete encrypted state `{journal_id,restore_id,head_generation,dispatch_state_generation,predecessor_journal_head_digest_and_cas_version,plan_recipe_or_final_plan_digest,availability_certificate_digest?,current_staging_incarnation,complete_dispatch_root_body,dispatch_state_digest,complete_action_attempt_artifact_lease_and_resource_map,local_slot_generation_and_predecessor_digest,journal_mac_profile_oid,canonical_capsule_digest}`. Every journal-authority successor increments `head_generation` exactly once. `dispatch_state_generation` increments only when the plan/action/attempt/artifact/lease/resource state changes; a pure relocation preserves it and `dispatch_state_digest` while changing the staging incarnation and capsule bytes. The capsule contains only values known before the journal-head CAS—never a future authority head, receipt, status, or provider-result hole. In particular a generation that changes an ordinary target action to SentUnknown contains that complete SentUnknown state.

`prebootstrap_journal_publication_contracts.toml` has exactly two contracts, Bootstrap and SuccessorOrRelocation. Their fixed protocol is: (1) construct the complete immutable encrypted capsule; (2) replicate and independently reopen it in every required failure domain; (3) CAS reference-free `PreBootstrapJournalAuthorityHead {journal_id,restore_id,head_generation,dispatch_state_generation,dispatch_state_digest,encrypted_generation_capsule_digest,plan_recipe_or_final_plan_digest,availability_certificate_digest?,current_staging_incarnation,predecessor_head_digest,current_cas_version,publication_operation_id,publication_idempotency_digest,state:Active|TerminalCanonicalized{certificate_digest,target_root_publication_commitment,retention_floor}|TerminalAbandoned{certificate_digest,zero_canonical_publication_commitment,retention_floor},authority_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`; (4) mirror the exact returned head and capsule locally; and only then (5) allow any ordinary target request whose SentUnknown state is contained in that head/dispatch generation to leave. Receipt/status knowledge from that target action is known data in a later complete capsule/head generation. The authority permanently indexes `(journal_id,head_generation,publication_operation_id)` and supports authenticated status/idempotent exact retry; changed capsule bytes at the same identity are rejected. Only Active accepts SuccessorOrRelocation. Either terminal arm permanently rejects relocation, application-state publication, or another terminalization. Because the head CAS publishes a complete predecessor-known generation rather than its own attempt state, the protocol is nonrecursive.

Initial head publication uses signed bootstrap recovery/admin authority stored in `PreBootstrapRestoreRecoveryCapsule`; every successor CAS requires the exact current head and current staging incarnation. `PreBootstrapRestorePlanAvailabilityCertificate` is the portable threshold-signed `{restore_id,plan_recipe_digest,journal_id,initial_journal_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,journal_key_commitment,journal_key_wrap_recipient_profile_and_digest,journal_mac_profile_oid,initial_capsule_generation_and_root_digest,recovery_capsule_locations_and_digests,recovery_and_relocation_policy,canonical_action_recipe_set_digest,required_failure_domain_policy,verified_reopen_quorum,retain_until:Canonicalized{root_slot_durable_sealed_journal_import_and_covering_cut}|Abandoned{terminal_certificate_and_all_external_status_nonreuse_resource_floors},signer_set_epoch,signatures}` and does not name the final plan. The final portable plan fills only the availability-certificate hole; its actions later fill only their plan-digest holes. A claim, fence, archive receipt, or refinement commit alone never releases a plan/refinement/journal capsule.

If the current local journal inode is lost or becomes unusable, recovery opens the latest authenticated authority head/capsule and performs the SuccessorOrRelocation publication contract with `result_head_generation = prior_head_generation + 1`, unchanged dispatch state generation/digest, and a strictly newer `current_staging_incarnation`. `PreBootstrapJournalRelocationReceipt` is the head CAS's portable signed `{journal_id,prior_head_generation,result_head_generation,unchanged_dispatch_state_generation_and_digest,publication_operation_id,publication_idempotency_digest,prior_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,old_staging_incarnation,new_staging_incarnation,new_filesystem_profile_mount_device_inode_and_creation_nonce_commitment,old_staging_fenced_by_head_successor:true,unchanged_plan_action_attempt_lease_and_resource_lineage_commitment,result_capsule_digest,result_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,authority_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. It is journal-publication evidence, not an `ExternalActionRequestBinding`/application receipt. Because it names the result head, it is never embedded in the result capsule/head it proves; recovery retains it as authenticated side evidence and may copy it only into a later successor capsule whose own head it does not name. The new inode is materialized from the result capsule, acquires a fresh whole-inode fence, and becomes dispatch-capable only after its local mirror byte-matches the result head. Every old inode's guard fails the current-head/staging-incarnation check. Equal-head-generation disagreement remains fatal because no relocation reuses a head generation. Thus the local two-slot file is a fenced publication cache/mirror, while the loss-tolerant journal authority and encrypted capsules are durable recovery authority; no same-inode recreation claim is required.

`PortableRestoreSourceAcquisitionPlan` is the immutable Phase-A prebootstrap-artifact schema `{restore_id,mode,claimed_target_authority_recipe:RestoreClaimedTargetAuthorityRecipe,plan_availability_certificate_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PreBootstrapRestorePlanAvailabilityCertificate>,source_publication_envelope_identity,selected_grant_head,requested_lease_identity_and_window,phase_a_actions,identity_key_plan:RestoreIdentityKeyPlan,recipient_and_reprotection_profile,decoder_bootstrap_profiles,global_resource_ceiling,prebootstrap_abandonment_resource_disposition_recipe,phase_a_action_authority_and_cleanup_bijection}`. It intentionally does not pretend to know the encrypted manifest/projection's complete per-object inventory.

After ArchiveAcquire, `RestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanRefinement` is the prebootstrap-artifact closed phase union `BootstrapOpeners{phase_a_plan_digest,archive_receipt_digest,authenticated_publication_envelope_and_recovery_projection_cipher_inventory,source_k_oid_and_exact_manifest_projection_bootstrap_dek_actions,resource_suballocation,refinement_commit_recipe:DirectoryBoundLocalJournal|ExternalCasContinuity,complete_bootstrap_artifact_action_bijection}|VerifiedObjectInventory{phase_a_plan_digest,bootstrap_refinement_digest,verified_manifest_and_recovery_projection_digests,exact_object_key_cipher_encoding_and_format_inventory,per_object_dek_actions,decoder_and_format_inventory,resource_suballocation,refinement_commit_recipe:DirectoryBoundLocalJournal|ExternalCasContinuity,complete_verified_object_action_bijection}`. `PreBootstrapRefinementAvailabilityCertificate` is the portable signed `{restore_id,phase,phase_a_plan_digest,predecessor_refinement_digest?,refinement_recipe_and_digest,journal_id,current_journal_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,current_capsule_generation_and_root_digest,current_staging_incarnation,recovery_capsule_locations_and_digests,recovery_and_relocation_policy,required_failure_domain_policy,verified_reopen_quorum,retain_until:Canonicalized{root_slot_durable_sealed_journal_import_and_covering_cut}|Abandoned{portable_terminal_abandonment_certificate_and_all_external_status_nonreuse_resource_floors},signer_set_epoch,signatures}`.

DirectoryBound refinement commitment uses two journal generations to avoid a receipt/head cycle. The commit generation contains only `LocalRefinementCommitMarker {restore_id,phase,phase_a_plan_digest,predecessor_refinement_digest?,candidate_refinement_digest,availability_certificate_digest,journal_staging_incarnation,journal_staging_inode_identity,journal_whole_inode_fence_identity,prior_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation,candidate_result_dispatch_root_body_digest,committed_refinement_artifact_locator_and_digest,exact_candidate_journal_membership_proof,marker_digest}` and CASes the journal authority to the committed head. After that head is known, recovery constructs MAC-authenticated `LocalRefinementCommitReceipt {restore_id,phase,phase_a_plan_digest,predecessor_refinement_digest?,candidate_refinement_digest,availability_certificate_digest,journal_id,predecessor_journal_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,committed_journal_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,journal_staging_incarnation,journal_staging_inode_identity,journal_whole_inode_fence_identity,prior_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation,committed_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation,commit_marker_digest,committed_refinement_artifact_locator_and_digest,exact_candidate_journal_membership_proof,mac_under_k_restore_journal}`. A later receipt-import generation stores that already-complete receipt and advances the journal head again; the receipt never names its own containing receipt-import head. Only the receipt-import generation constructs `RefinementCommittedGate` and may authorize actions introduced by the refinement. The target-manifest inode is plan context only and can never inhabit the marker, receipt, or gate. ExternalCas uses the registered continuity-CAS external action and its ordinary later terminal import. Each refinement is made loss-tolerantly available and profile-validly committed before any introduced action can be Armed. BootstrapOpeners derives only from the signed archive receipt/publication envelope; after those openers recover, recovery independently opens/verifies the manifest and projection and only then creates VerifiedObjectInventory. Thus encrypted inventory is never magically known, while every per-object provider action is fixed before send.

A late-bound recipe contains one typed `PlanDigest` or `RefinementDigest` hole; the containing artifact hashes recipe identity/parameters-with-hole, then derivation fills exactly that hole after the digest exists. Full request bytes never feed the artifact that they themselves name, and external heads commit portable canonical digests rather than future target-keyed ObjectIds.

RecoverSameIdentity Phase A requires EnterRestoreFencePending, one issuer-fence row per issuer, one revoke action for every cohort selected Revoked, EnterRestoreReserved, AllocationEpoch claim, ArchiveAcquire, the complete `RestoreIdentityKeyPlan`, and complete phase-A cleanup recipes. CloneDirectoryBound Phase A requires NewDatabaseIdentity and AllocationEpoch claims, the exact target-manifest filesystem/directory/inode recipe, whole-inode fence and zero-existing-RootSlot proof, ArchiveAcquire, the fresh-target-key creation recipe, and no EnterCloneReserved action or external continuity head. CloneExternalCas adds EnterCloneReserved and a live RecoveryOnly continuity lease. No key-provider operation runs in Phase A. BootstrapOpeners, after its refinement is committed, performs the mode-valid same-identity source-`K_oid` recovery/rewrap or clone source-`K_oid` source-open-only recovery plus fresh target-`K_oid` creation, together with the exact publication/projection DEK recovery set. VerifiedObjectInventory requires one source-DEK recovery per verified inventory member. The hierarchical bijection proves every issuer/cohort/reservation/key/object/lease/continuity resource and every transient/fresh key cleanup appears exactly once across Phase A and both refinements; continuity abandonment is ExternalCas-only, while DirectoryBound abandonment proves zero RootSlot under the target-manifest inode fence and burns/destroys only its claimed or freshly created resources.

`PreBootstrapRestoreDispatchGateHeader` is `{journal_id,current_journal_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,current_journal_generation,current_staging_incarnation,current_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation,current_journal_staging_inode_and_whole_inode_fence_commitment}`. `PreBootstrapRestoreDispatchSourceGate<Action>` is a generated finite family whose every arm begins with that header: `TargetClaimPlanGate{recovery_or_admin_authorization,profile_specific_expected_current_authority_or_target_manifest_inode_fence,plan_digest,plan_availability_certificate_digest}|RestoreFencePendingGate{returned_fence_head_bytes_digest_and_cas,plan_and_journal_commitment}|PriorLeaseBarrierCompleteGate{fence_head_digest,unsigned_barrier_body_and_row_commitment,complete_issuer_cohort_bijection}|ClaimedReservationGate{reservation_kind,id,claimed_head_digest_and_cas,claim_receipt_and_terminal_evidence_commitment}|RestoreSourceAcquisitionGate{source_gate:RestoreSourceAcquisitionSourceGate}|RefinementCommitCandidateGate{phase,phase_a_plan_digest,predecessor:ArchiveAcquired{archive_receipt_identity_and_digest}|PriorRefinementCommitted{prior_refinement_digest,prior_commit_evidence_identity_and_digest},commit_profile:DirectoryBoundLocal{journal_id,current_journal_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,journal_staging_incarnation,journal_staging_inode_identity,journal_whole_inode_fence_identity,expected_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation}|ExternalCas{current_target_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,continuity_operation_recipe},candidate_refinement_digest,availability_certificate_artifact_identity_and_digest,exact_candidate_artifact_and_journal_membership_proof}|RefinementCommittedGate{phase,phase_a_plan_digest,required_predecessor_refinement_digest?,refinement_digest,availability_certificate_digest,commit_result:DirectoryBoundLocal{local_refinement_commit_receipt_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<LocalRefinementCommitReceipt>,committed_journal_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,receipt_import_journal_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,receipt_import_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation}|ExternalCas{returned_target_authority_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,commit_receipt_identity_and_digest,portable_terminal_evidence_identity_and_digest,prebootstrap_terminal_import_commitment}}|PreBootstrapAbandonmentCleanupGate{sealed_abandonment_spec_artifact_identity_and_digest,frozen_projection_and_creation_guard_commitment,exact_zero_canonical_publication_proof,complete_action_and_resource_disposition_map_commitment,current_action_specific_external_head_and_cleanup_eligibility}`. Each external action row permits exactly one transport-capable arm and byte-matches the full stable journal/head plus selected gate commitment in `ExternalActionRequestBinding`; local publication evidence additionally binds the current journal inode/fence. The target-manifest creation evidence remains separately bound by the plan/claim/creation guard and cannot substitute for journal authority. The DirectoryBound local refinement arm is validated by the dispatch-root transition registry and never creates an external request/status/receipt. Its receipt-import head must be the direct journal successor that contains the already-complete receipt, and the receipt's named committed head must be its predecessor; a receipt naming its own containing head is rejected. BootstrapOpeners consumes only the ArchiveAcquired candidate; VerifiedObjectInventory consumes only the prior-refinement candidate. Their profile-valid commit result constructs the corresponding `RefinementCommittedGate`, which alone can authorize actions introduced by that refinement. Cleanup actions select only an exact projection of `PreBootstrapAbandonmentCleanupGate`; no live acquisition/claim/refinement gate can authorize destructive cleanup. Construction order is recovery capsule/journal bootstrap head -> local journal mirror -> plan availability certificate -> Phase-A plan -> target claim/fence and issuer/cohort actions -> complete barrier/reservation claims -> RestoreReserved or profile-specific clone authority -> archive acquisition -> BootstrapOpeners refinement availability/candidate commit/receipt-import/committed gate/actions -> verified manifest/projection -> VerifiedObjectInventory refinement availability/candidate commit/receipt-import/committed gate/actions. Every authority-changing/provider send and every dispatch-capable local generation is therefore recoverable after local inode loss.

`PortableClaimedReservationGateMember` is the reference-free `{reservation_kind,reservation_id,claim_id,intended_use_commitment,claimed_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,claim_receipt_identity_and_digest,claim_terminal_evidence_identity_and_digest,prebootstrap_terminal_import_commitment}`. `RestoreSourceAcquisitionSourceGate` is the closed portable proof `RecoverSameIdentity{restore_source_acquisition_plan_digest,prebootstrap_dispatch_root_identity,current_restore_reserved_head_bytes_and_digest_and_cas_version,target_incarnation,prior_lease_barrier_digest,sorted_claimed_reservations[AllocationEpoch],exact_claimed_reservation_plan_and_journal_bijection,exact_plan_and_journal_commitment_membership_proof}|CloneDirectoryBound{restore_source_acquisition_plan_digest,prebootstrap_dispatch_root_identity,filesystem_profile_and_mount_device_commitment,target_manifest_staging_inode_identity_and_creation_nonce_digest,whole_inode_fence_identity,parent_directory_durability_commitment,zero_existing_root_slot_proof_digest,fixed_local_continuity_recipe_and_digest,directory_bound_creation_evidence_recipe_digest,sorted_claimed_reservations[NewDatabaseIdentity,AllocationEpoch],exact_claimed_reservation_plan_and_journal_bijection}|CloneExternalCas{restore_source_acquisition_plan_digest,prebootstrap_dispatch_root_identity,clone_reserved_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,recovery_only_lease_commitment,sorted_claimed_reservations[NewDatabaseIdentity,AllocationEpoch],exact_claimed_reservation_plan_and_journal_bijection,exact_plan_and_journal_commitment_equality_proof}`. `RestoreSourceAcquisitionRequest` is the portable canonical `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=RestoreArchiveAcquire},source_gate:RestoreSourceAcquisitionSourceGate,restore_source_acquisition_plan_digest,restore_id,mode,source_manifest_artifact_ref:BackupPublicationArtifactRef<BackupManifest>,source_publication_receipt_digest,content_archive_inventory_root_digest_and_count,archive_retention_grant_id,expected_current_grant_head_digest_and_cas_version,requested_publication_envelope_and_bootstrap_subject_set,target_holder_identity,requested_lease_lineage_id,requested_lease_window,key_recovery_ceremony_profile_oid,recipient_and_reprotection_profile_oid,authority_binding_public_commitment}`. The encrypted recovery-projection ref/digest is deliberately absent from Phase A.

The archive authority keeps a reference-free CAS-linear `RestoreSourceLeaseAuthorityHead {archive_authority_id,lease_lineage_id,cas_version,predecessor_head_digest,authority_counter,state:Registered{source_gate_digest,plan_digest,request_digest,operation_id,idempotency_token_digest,lease_identity,requested_generation=1}|Active{lease_identity,generation,portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,selected_grant_generation_and_digest,window_profile_domain_epoch_and_bounds,archive_counter,holder_and_subject_set_commitment,last_operation_id,last_idempotency_token_digest,last_request_digest}|Released{lease_identity,released_through_generation,released_portable_lease_state_digest,zero_successor_generation_commitment,last_operation_id,last_idempotency_token_digest,last_request_digest},signer_set_epoch,signatures}` keyed by `(archive_authority_id,lease_lineage_id)`, with absence represented by an authenticated empty-map proof rather than an encoded head. Acquisition is Absent -> Registered -> Active, renewal is Active(g) -> Active(g+1), and release is Active(g) -> Released. Heads contain only pre-CAS facts/result state, never a future receipt digest.

Provider no-effect evidence is a closed, action-tagged portable family. `RestoreSourceLeaseAcquireOperationTombstone` is `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=RestoreArchiveAcquire},archive_authority_id,lease_lineage_id,terminal_kind:PermanentlyRefused|ConflictNoEffect|ExpiredNotRegisteredNoEffect,stable_reason,authenticated_absent_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,permanent_provider_operation_index_tombstone_digest,zero_registered_or_active_operation_and_lease_state_commitment,provider_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}` and cannot carry a Registered or Active-state digest. An unchanged Registered head is nonterminal and must be status-resolved/rolled forward; it can never be relabeled zero-effect. `RestoreSourceLeaseRenewOperationTombstone` and `RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationTombstone` each begin with their exact action binding and additionally require `{unchanged_active_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,unchanged_portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,zero_effect_commitment}`. A tombstone for one action cannot decode as another or substitute an Acquire absence for an unchanged Active lease. A failed renewal/release never replaces or weakens the still-active lease head. Every status, receipt, observation, and no-successor proof byte-matches complete authenticated head bytes, portable lease-state digest, predecessor digest, CAS version, authority counter, and signatures.

`PortableRestoreArchiveAcquisitionReceipt` is the archive authority's terminal `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=RestoreArchiveAcquire},source_gate_digest,restore_source_acquisition_plan_digest,prior_registered_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,result_active_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,source_manifest_artifact_ref,publication_receipt_bytes_and_digest,current_archive_grant_bytes_and_observation:PortableArchiveGrantAuthorityObservation,restore_source_lease_acquire_receipt:RestoreSourceLeaseAcquireReceipt,authenticated_publication_envelope_and_recovery_projection_bootstrap_locators[PortableRecoveryLocator],exact_descriptor_locator_request_receipt_match_digest,exact_permitted_publication_and_bootstrap_subject_proofs,bootstrap_key_and_ciphertext_inventory,bootstrap_decoder_profile_inventory,provider_full_inventory_advisory_digest?,provider_status_lineage,acquisition_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. The returned actionable locator bytes must equal the signed publication descriptor and receipt; commitments alone are insufficient. Only the bootstrap inventory is authoritative at this stage; any provider full-inventory claim is advisory and never drives per-object dispatch. The nested lease-acquire receipt byte-matches the same outer `ExternalActionRequestBinding`; it is a typed terminal payload member of the one atomic RestoreArchiveAcquire action, not a second dispatch effect.

Key recovery/creation is a separate multi-provider ceremony with one exact common transcript. `PortableRestoreKeyProviderReceiptCommon` is `{external_request_binding,action_kind:RecoverAndRewrapPreservedObjectIdKey|RecoverSourceObjectIdKeyForDecode|CreateTargetObjectIdKey|RecoverSourceBootstrapDek|RecoverSourceObjectDekForReprotect,phase:BootstrapOpeners|VerifiedObjectInventory,restore_id,phase_a_plan_digest,refinement_digest,source_gate_digest,provider_authority_id,prior_provider_head_bytes_digest_and_version,result_provider_head_bytes_digest_and_version,provider_status_lineage,key_or_ciphertext_identity,inventory_ordinal?,target_recipient_commitment,outcome:RecoveredWrapped|CreatedFresh|AlreadyAppliedEquivalent,provider_counter,required_proof_profile_oid,signer_key_epoch,signatures}`. `RestoreKeyProviderReceiptRef` is the closed portable union `Kms{common,unwrap_rewrap_or_create_attestation}|Hsm{common,nonexportability_and_key-origin_attestation}|Threshold{common,participant_share_transcript_and_threshold_certificate}|ArchiveEscrow{common,grant_and_share-release_proof}`. Action/phase/refinement/common/arm equality is mandatory; no provider arm can stand for another. Refusal/conflict uses the action's `ExternalTerminalArtifactRef` and belongs to the abandonment/replan disposition; a successful acquisition bundle contains success/equivalent receipts only.

Once the target `K_oid` disposition and both required refinements succeed, canonical import is explicit and self-contained. A prebootstrap artifact is never made canonical merely by copying its `PreBootstrapArtifactRef`: import decodes and verifies the exact artifact, creates a target-keyed logical copy whose structured fields contain only canonical refs/values, and retains the original authenticated wrapper bytes only as bounded opaque historical evidence. A staging locator inside those opaque bytes is never dereferenced after import and is removed with the detailed import closure at its retention floor.

`CanonicalRestorePlanAvailabilityCopy<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,plan_recipe_digest,journal_id,prebootstrap_journal_authority_head_digest_and_cas_version,journal_key_commitment_and_wrap_profile_digest,journal_mac_profile_oid,capsule_generation_and_root_digest,recovery_and_relocation_policy,canonical_action_recipe_set_digest,loss_tolerant_capsule_content_commitments,required_failure_domain_policy,verified_reopen_quorum,retain_until_condition,signer_set_epoch,signatures,original_artifact_id_and_inner_digest,verified_original_wrapper_bytes_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceBytes>,portable_to_canonical_projection_digest,body_digest}`. `CanonicalRestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanCopy<Role>` is the exact canonical semantic projection of `PortableRestoreSourceAcquisitionPlan`: `{authority_bound_header,restore_id,mode,claimed_target_authority_recipe,plan_availability_copy_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalRestorePlanAvailabilityCopy<Role>>,source_publication_envelope_identity,selected_grant_head,requested_lease_identity_and_window,phase_a_actions,identity_key_plan,recipient_and_reprotection_profile,decoder_bootstrap_profiles,global_resource_ceiling,prebootstrap_abandonment_resource_disposition_recipe,phase_a_action_authority_and_cleanup_bijection,original_artifact_id_and_inner_digest,verified_original_wrapper_bytes_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceBytes>,portable_to_canonical_projection_digest,body_digest}`. The generated projection replaces every prebootstrap artifact reference with its exact canonical copy or a nonauthoritative historical artifact identity; no staging locator is a structured field.

`CanonicalBootstrapOpenersRef<Role>` and `CanonicalVerifiedObjectInventoryRef<Role>` are distinct generated strong-reference types and cannot cross-decode. `CanonicalBootstrapOpenersCopy<Role>` is `{authority_bound_header,phase_a_plan_copy_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalRestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanCopy<Role>>,archive_receipt_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreArchiveAcquisitionReceipt>,authenticated_publication_envelope_and_recovery_projection_cipher_inventory,source_k_oid_and_exact_manifest_projection_bootstrap_dek_actions,resource_suballocation,commit_evidence:DirectoryBoundLocal{local_refinement_commit_receipt_copy_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalLocalRefinementCommitReceiptCopy<Role>>}|ExternalCas{typed_terminal_import_ref:StrongRef<ExternalTerminalImportRecord<CommitAcquisitionRefinementBootstrapOpeners,Role>>,typed_receipt_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_ref},complete_bootstrap_artifact_action_bijection,original_artifact_and_availability_certificate_evidence,body_digest}`. `CanonicalVerifiedObjectInventoryCopy<Role>` is `{authority_bound_header,phase_a_plan_copy_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalRestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanCopy<Role>>,bootstrap_refinement_copy_ref:CanonicalBootstrapOpenersRef<Role>,verified_manifest_and_recovery_projection_refs_and_digests,exact_object_key_cipher_encoding_and_format_inventory,per_object_dek_actions,decoder_and_format_inventory,resource_suballocation,commit_evidence:DirectoryBoundLocal{local_refinement_commit_receipt_copy_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalLocalRefinementCommitReceiptCopy<Role>>}|ExternalCas{typed_terminal_import_ref:StrongRef<ExternalTerminalImportRecord<CommitAcquisitionRefinementVerifiedObjectInventory,Role>>,typed_receipt_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_ref},complete_verified_object_action_bijection,original_artifact_and_availability_certificate_evidence,body_digest}`. `CanonicalLocalRefinementCommitReceiptCopy<Role>` verifies the original journal MAC during import, then retains its semantic transcript and opaque authenticated source bytes without a live `K_restore_journal` dependency.

`CanonicalPreBootstrapContractImportRef<Role>` is a generated finite role/contract union with one arm for every exact `origin_plane=RestorePreBootstrap` contract in the selected Phase-A/refinement/abandonment plan. Each arm names the immutable `dispatch_contract_id`, its action kind, and one state: `NotAuthorized{zero_authorization_membership_proof}|AuthorizedNeverArmed{canonical_operation_and_authorization_copy_ref,operation_id,idempotency_digest,canonical_action_body_digest,source_gate_projection,zero_armed_membership_proof}|AbandonedBeforeSend{canonical_operation_and_authorization_copy_ref,source_gate_projection,armed_publication_evidence_copy_ref,canonical_never_sent_proof_ref}|Terminal{canonical_operation_and_authorization_copy_ref,source_gate_projection,complete_ordered_authenticated_status_lineage,terminal_artifact_ref:ExternalTerminalArtifactRef<Contract>,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<Contract>>,canonical_terminal_import_ref,final_external_head_or_no_effect_commitment}`. Thus absence of authorization is distinguishable from a permanently nonreusable authorized operation that never armed, and a canonical-plane contract for the same action kind cannot be imported as pre-bootstrap evidence.

`SealedPreBootstrapDispatchJournalImport<Role>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,journal_id,plan_copy_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalRestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanCopy<Role>>,bootstrap_refinement_copy_ref:CanonicalBootstrapOpenersRef<Role>,verified_object_refinement_copy_ref:CanonicalVerifiedObjectInventoryRef<Role>,source_journal_authority_head_digest_and_cas_version,source_dispatch_root_identity_and_final_generation,current_staging_incarnation_and_whole_inode_fence_commitment,sorted_contract_imports[CanonicalPreBootstrapContractImportRef<Role>],canonical_lease_owner_and_renewal_lineage,sorted_resource_dispositions,complete_plan_refinement_contract_attempt_status_terminal_artifact_resource_and_cleanup_bijection,original_dispatch_root_slot_and_mac_transcript_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceBytes>,journal_import_digest,body_digest}`. Every authorized contract has exactly one canonical attempt lineage; every created/claimed/acquired resource has exactly one live owner or terminal disposition; no canonical contract import points to an artifact locator, mutable dispatch root, future bridge/root, or destroyed journal key.

`RestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanImportRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,plan_copy_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalRestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanCopy<Role>>,bootstrap_refinement_copy_ref:CanonicalBootstrapOpenersRef<Role>,verified_object_refinement_copy_ref:CanonicalVerifiedObjectInventoryRef<Role>,sealed_dispatch_journal_import_ref:StrongRef<SealedPreBootstrapDispatchJournalImport<Role>>,target_k_oid_disposition_evidence,verified_manifest_projection_inventory_commitment,artifact_set_to_canonical_import_or_terminal_disposition_bijection,canonical_import_closure_digest,body_digest}`. `CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceBytes` has an exact bounded plaintext schema `{evidence_kind,source_artifact_or_dispatch_root_identity,canonical_bytes,length,canonical_digest,authentication_profile_and_verification_digest,body_digest}`, is always authenticated and re-encrypted under the target database protection domain before journal-key destruction, is never interpreted as current authority, and has generated size limits, golden/mutation/fuzz tests, redacted diagnostics, and the same no-logging rule as other protected recovery evidence.

Detailed plan/refinement/journal imports remain strongly retained through creation, restore, replay, status, and unresolved-operation floors. `PreBootstrapDispatchEvidenceCompactSpec<Role>` is an explicit **preterminal** canonical maintenance input run promptly after the first RootSlot, sealed journal import, and canonical import closure are role-validly published. At that boundary every prebootstrap action/family member is terminal and every prebootstrap-created resource is either transferred to one exact canonical owner—such as the canonical source-lease record, key-access resource inventory, reservation-use record, or target object owner—or has a proof-capable terminal disposition. No source-access cleanup or overall restore terminal is required. The spec constructs `PreBootstrapDispatchTerminalAccumulator<Role>`, which compactly authenticates the sorted `(action_key_or_family_member_key,operation_id,request_digest,source_gate_commitment,terminal_disposition,terminal_artifact_digest,final_head_commitment)` map, exact prebootstrap-resource-to-canonical-owner-or-terminal-disposition map, family-final-ordinal proofs, and canonical import-closure digest. It has no edge to a future release, cleanup result, terminal common, tombstone, or overall restore result. The canonicalization handshake immediately consumes this accumulator and terminalizes the external journal head; after canonicalization is pending or complete, no successor/relocation is legal. Later source-access cleanup, source-lease release, and terminal tombstones retain the already-existing proof-capable accumulator; they never manufacture or revise it.

`CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceReencryptionOwner<Role>` is the exact target-keyed `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,journal_id,sorted_source_evidence_members[{source_artifact_or_dispatch_identity,source_canonical_digest,source_authentication_profile,target_ciphertext_ref:StrongCiphertextRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceBytes>,target_dek_wrap_and_recipient_ref,target_ciphertext_encoding_placement_and_opener_commitment}],complete_source_wrapper_to_target_ciphertext_and_key_bijection,zero_plaintext_persistence_policy,detailed_replay_and_status_floor,body_digest}`. `CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceReencryptionProof<Role>` is the later checked `{owner_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceReencryptionOwner<Role>>,sorted_verified_member_results[{source_identity_and_digest,target_ciphertext_identity,reopened_plaintext_canonical_digest,authentication_and_reencryption_transcript_digest,target_recoverability_proof}],complete_member_bijection,zero_dependency_on_k_restore_journal_or_staging_locator_proof,verification_root_and_checker_version,body_digest}`. Neither name is an untyped placeholder.

Canonical restore uses exact `RestoreJournalKeyDisposition<Role>` with two noninterchangeable states: `CanonicalizedDetailedThenDestroyed{sealed_import_ref:StrongRef<SealedPreBootstrapDispatchJournalImport<Role>>,terminal_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapDispatchTerminalAccumulator<Role>>,canonicalization_certificate_and_terminal_head_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapJournalCanonicalizationImportRecord<Role>>,canonical_evidence_reencryption_owner_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceReencryptionOwner<Role>>,canonical_evidence_reencryption_proof_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceReencryptionProof<Role>>,destruction:LocalZeroizationEvidence|ProviderDestructionReceipt}` and `CanonicalizedCompactedThenDestroyed{compacted_prebootstrap_evidence_ref:StrongRef<CompactedPreBootstrapEvidence<Role>>,compact_key_destruction_summary_ref:StrongRef<RestoreJournalKeyDestructionSummary<Role>>}`. `RestoreJournalKeyDestructionSummary<Role>` is the compact `{role,restore_id,journal_id,journal_key_commitment,destruction_kind,destruction_evidence_digest,canonicalization_certificate_terminal_head_and_accumulator_commitment,zero_live_journal_key_or_reencryption_dependency_proof,retention_floor,body_digest}` with no edge to the sealed import, detailed canonicalization import, reencryption owner/proof, wrapper ciphertexts, or destruction receipt.

`CompactedPreBootstrapEvidence<Role>` is the proof-capable nonauthorizing `{terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,restore_id,journal_id,target_database_security_incarnation_and_service,canonicalization_certificate_and_terminal_head_public_commitment,first_root_publication_and_canonical_import_closure_commitment,terminal_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapDispatchTerminalAccumulator<Role>>,closed_action_family_and_resource_transfer_disposition_accumulator_roots,reencryption_inventory_accumulator_root,journal_key_destruction_summary_commitment,provider_status_nonreuse_and_reservation_floor_commitment,closed_detailed_replay_status_and_capsule_floors,checker_version,body_digest}`. It contains no live authority or detailed plan/refinement/import, source acquisition bundle, `CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceBytes`, `SealedPreBootstrapDispatchJournalImport`, canonicalization import, reencryption owner/proof, or live journal-key edge.

The no-RootManifest path instead uses noncanonical `PreBootstrapJournalKeyDisposition = AbandonedThenDestroyed{portable_terminal_certificate:PortablePreBootstrapAbandonmentTerminalCertificate,terminalization_receipt:PreBootstrapJournalAbandonmentTerminalizationReceipt,authenticated_result_terminal_head_bytes_and_digest,external_status_nonreuse_and_resource_floors,destruction:LocalZeroizationEvidence|ProviderDestructionReceipt}`. It has no role parameter, Local/Meta strong reference, target `K_oid`, or dependency on the destroyed journal MAC. Status/nonreuse proofs remain derivable from the canonical accumulator/compacted evidence or, for prebootstrap abandonment, the portable certificate plus terminalization receipt/head and provider heads.

Successful creation terminalizes the journal only after the first canonical root exists. Once that RootSlot is durable and the sealed import plus preterminal accumulator are available, recovery constructs reference-free unsigned `PortablePreBootstrapJournalCanonicalizationCertificateBody {restore_id,journal_id,final_active_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,final_head_and_dispatch_state_generations,final_staging_incarnation,root_manifest_and_slot_publication_evidence_projection,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,sealed_journal_import_digest,canonical_import_closure_digest,closed_action_family_member_accumulator_root,resource_transfer_and_disposition_accumulator_root,availability_certificate_and_capsule_release_floor,body_digest}`. The journal authority verifies the named root publication and imported closure, threshold-signs that body, and atomically CASes the exact Active head to `TerminalCanonicalized{certificate_digest,target_root_publication_commitment,retention_floor}` with `head_generation + 1`; it returns signed `PortablePreBootstrapJournalCanonicalizationCertificate` plus the complete terminal head bytes. This is the SuccessorOrRelocation publication substrate, not an application dispatch action, and exact retry/status uses its publication operation identity.

Ordered `PreBootstrapJournalCanonicalizationImportSpec<Role>` consumes the already-created certificate/head, final Active predecessor, sealed import, preterminal accumulator, first-root publication evidence, and exact availability/capsule floor relation. Apply creates `PreBootstrapJournalCanonicalizationImportRecord<Role> {authority_bound_header,restore_id,journal_id,certificate_bytes_and_digest,terminal_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,predecessor_active_head_digest_and_cas_version,sealed_import_ref,terminal_accumulator_ref,root_manifest_slot_and_canonical_import_closure_commitment,availability_capsule_release_floor,assigned_role_maintenance_identity,body_digest}`. Only after this import is durable may recovery release the canonicalized capsule floor or destroy `K_restore_journal`; `CanonicalizedThenDestroyed` binds that import and the destruction evidence. The availability certificates' Canonicalized retention arm is therefore satisfied by exact certificate/head/import/covering-cut evidence, never by the mere existence of a first root or sealed import.

The canonical payload retains that result through state, not command ancestry. `RestorePreBootstrapEvidenceState<Role>` is the exact closed union `DetailedPreCanonical{plan_import_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanImportRecord<Role>>,sealed_journal_import_ref:StrongRef<SealedPreBootstrapDispatchJournalImport<Role>>,canonical_evidence_reencryption_owner_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceReencryptionOwner<Role>>,detailed_replay_floor}|CanonicalizedDetailed{canonicalization_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapJournalCanonicalizationImportRecord<Role>>,terminal_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapDispatchTerminalAccumulator<Role>>,journal_key_disposition_ref:StrongRef<RestoreJournalKeyDisposition<Role>>,canonical_evidence_reencryption_owner_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceReencryptionOwner<Role>>,canonical_evidence_reencryption_proof_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalPreBootstrapEvidenceReencryptionProof<Role>>,detailed_replay_floor}|CanonicalizedCompacted{compacted_evidence_ref:StrongRef<CompactedPreBootstrapEvidence<Role>>,journal_key_disposition_ref:StrongRef<RestoreJournalKeyDisposition<Role>>,compact_status_replay_nonreuse_floor}`. The state checker requires the detailed disposition arm in CanonicalizedDetailed and the compact disposition arm in CanonicalizedCompacted. `CanonicalizedRestorePreBootstrapEvidenceRef<Role>` is the generated tag-refined strong-reference union accepting exactly either canonicalized state and preserving which substate it selected.

`RestorePreBootstrapEvidenceCanonicalizeSpec<Role>` consumes DetailedPreCanonical, the canonicalization import, complete re-encryption proof, and already-created local-zeroization/provider-destruction evidence, then atomically installs CanonicalizedDetailed; it cannot drop the detailed replay floor or any status/nonreuse proof. Later `RestorePreBootstrapDetailedEvidenceCutSpec<Role>` is the sole detailed cut. It requires an activated retention cut proving every detailed replay/status/capsule/provider-verification floor closed, verifies the terminal accumulator and complete reencryption/destruction inventories, constructs `CompactedPreBootstrapEvidence<Role>` plus `RestoreJournalKeyDestructionSummary<Role>`, and atomically installs CanonicalizedCompacted. That successor has no strong path back to the sealed import, canonicalization import, detailed plan/refinement copies, wrapper ciphertexts, reencryption owner/proof, acquisition bundle, or destruction receipt, so ordinary GC can actually reclaim them. Every nonterminal restore state retains one canonicalized substate after canonicalization, and terminal common/tombstones retain the tag-refined union until their compact status/nonreuse floor. No implication from a command chain substitutes for this owner.

`RestoreCanonicalAcquisitionWorkingSet<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the pre-freeze target-keyed closure `{authority_bound_header,plan_copy_ref,bootstrap_refinement_copy_ref:CanonicalBootstrapOpenersRef<Role>,verified_object_refinement_copy_ref:CanonicalVerifiedObjectInventoryRef<Role>,source_gate_copy_ref,archive_receipt_ref,sorted_key_provider_receipt_refs,identity_key_disposition_evidence,source_k_oid_wrap_and_key_commitment,target_k_oid_wrap_and_key_commitment,sorted_object_dek_opener_recipes,decoder_and_format_profile_inventory,complete_plan_refinement_manifest_projection_object_key_provider_receipt_and_opener_bijection,live_prebootstrap_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation,body_digest}`. It is usable only with a live `RestoreProjectionBuildGuard` that revalidates the current dispatch root and source-lease owner; by itself it authorizes no source read or provider action.

`RestoreSourceAcquisitionBundle<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is created only after bridge freeze and is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,plan_import_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanImportRecord<Role>>,working_set_ref:StrongRef<RestoreCanonicalAcquisitionWorkingSet<Role>>,source_gate_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceAcquisitionSourceGate>,archive_receipt_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreArchiveAcquisitionReceipt>,canonical_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role>>,frozen_latest_lease_generation_and_portable_state_digest,sorted_key_provider_receipt_refs[RestoreKeyProviderReceiptRef],identity_key_disposition_evidence:RestoreIdentityKeyDispositionEvidence,source_k_oid_wrap_and_key_commitment,target_k_oid_wrap_and_key_commitment,sorted_object_dek_opener_recipes[{source_object_identity,source_ciphertext_and_encoding_identity,portable_receipt_member_commitment,opener_policy_oid,target_recipient_commitment,proof_of_inventory_membership}],decoder_and_format_profile_inventory,complete_prebootstrap_artifact_plan_gate_manifest_projection_object_key_provider_receipt_opener_and_cleanup_bijection,bridge_freeze_commitment,body_digest}`. It contains only wrapped material/recipient commitments and canonical copies of typed portable receipts; no prebootstrap artifact locator remains load-bearing after import. Missing source `K_oid`, missing target `K_oid` creation/rewrap, a missing DEK opener, uncovered subject, unclassified format, or created artifact without cleanup disposition fails before the first authoritative target-format escape.

The acquisition uses the registered pre-bootstrap dispatch machine because the target has no canonical RootManifest yet. Before the plan, recovery creates the stable journal/capsule authority above. `RestoreAcquisitionDispatchRoot` is a two-slot, tear-checked and MAC-authenticated, whole-inode-fenced **local mirror/cache** in the exclusive `journal_staging_inode`—outside the target database directory and registered as the sole pre-bootstrap local mutable-root exception. Each slot MAC covers `{restore_id,journal_id,current_journal_authority_head_digest_and_cas_version,portable_plan_digest_or_recipe_digest,plan_availability_certificate_identity,current_staging_incarnation,journal_staging_inode_identity,slot_index,generation,predecessor_slot_digest,complete action map and typed artifact locators,current prebootstrap time basis,current lease owner,resource dispositions,state}` under `K_restore_journal`; recovery rejects equal-generation disagreement, invalid MAC, wrap/claim/head mismatch, rollback, or artifact-locator substitution. No local generation is dispatch-capable until the matching encrypted generation-capsule recipe is available in the required failure domains, the journal authority head has advanced to it, and the local mirror byte-matches that result.

Its payload state is `Building|AcquiredProjectionAllowed|BridgeFrozenForCanonicalCreation{bridge_freeze_spec_artifact_ref,working_set_projection_and_bridge_recipe_commitment,frozen_latest_source_lease_generation_and_portable_state_digest,recovery_incarnation_refresh_family_state?,complete_terminal_attempt_and_resource_owner_bijection}|FirstRootPublicationFrozen{first_root_freeze_spec_artifact_ref,closed_recovery_incarnation_refresh_family_final_ordinal?,final_recovery_incarnation_lease_generation_and_window?,final_sealed_import_bundle_and_creation_guard_commitment,complete_terminal_attempt_family_member_and_resource_owner_bijection}|AbandonmentDrainPending{sealed_abandon_intent_artifact_ref,frozen_new_action_projection_and_creation_guards,outstanding_preexisting_attempt_bitmap,zero_canonical_publication_proof}|AbandonmentAuthorized{sealed_abandonment_spec_artifact_ref,complete_terminal_preexisting_attempt_inventory,complete_action_and_resource_disposition_plan,zero_canonical_publication_proof}|CleanupInProgress{sealed_abandonment_spec_artifact_ref,sorted_action_dispositions,sorted_resource_dispositions,outstanding_cleanup_bitmap}|TerminallyAbandoned`. `AcquiredProjectionAllowed` permits source opens, transform/projection construction, status resolution, and exact source-lease renewal through the live journal. `RestoreBridgeFreezeSpec` is a sealed prebootstrap artifact whose fields commit the completed working set, transform/projection identities and digests, exact first-bridge recipes, latest source-lease owner generation/state, current ExternalCas RecoveryOnly incarnation-lease generation if applicable, zero unresolved source/provider attempt proof, the closed source-renew family ordinal, and the complete action/resource owner/disposition bijection. Its one journal-authority-backed root transition consumes every projection/source-use guard, forbids new source/provider operations and source-lease renewal, and enters `BridgeFrozenForCanonicalCreation` while leaving only the bounded recovery-incarnation refresh family open.

The sole operation after bridge freeze but before the second freeze is exact `RecoveryIncarnationLeaseAcquireOrRefresh` for an ExternalCas target. Its action body preserves database/security identity, target incarnation, writer identity, `RecoveryOnly` scope, current continuity head/CAS, and first-root action digest; only the current predecessor lease generation/status, requested validity window, and pre-minted member identity advance. It grants no archive/key/source access. A successful refresh advances the journal head/dispatch state, invalidates every unrooted sealed-import/bundle/creation-guard candidate derived from the prior lease, and deterministically rebuilds candidates against the new lease. DirectoryBound has no such action.

`RestoreFirstRootPublicationFreezeSpec` is the second sealed prebootstrap artifact. It consumes the current bridge-frozen state, exact latest terminal recovery-lease member/head, final sealed-import/bundle/creation-guard recipes, and a proof that no refresh member is Armed, SentUnknown, unresolved, or authorizable beyond the selected final ordinal. Its journal-authority-backed transition closes the recovery-incarnation refresh family and enters `FirstRootPublicationFrozen`; only then are the final `SealedPreBootstrapDispatchJournalImport<Role>`, `RestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanImportRecord<Role>`, `RestoreSourceAcquisitionBundle<Role>`, and role-valid first-root creation guard constructible. After this second freeze the only legal outcomes are byte-exact first-root publication under that guard or transition to abandonment drain. If the closed lease/window becomes unusable before publication, the restore fails closed to abandonment and a new restore attempt; the family can never reopen.

Abandonment begins with `PreBootstrapRestoreAbandonIntent`, not a cleanup gate. Its journal-authority-backed transition enters `AbandonmentDrainPending`, revokes every **new** action/projection/creation authorization, and freezes the exact set of already Authorized/Armed/SentUnknown attempts. `PreBootstrapAbandonmentDrainCarryForwardGuard<Action>` is inhabitable only for one frozen preexisting attempt: it preserves the attempt's original request and original source-gate commitment, permits status-first recovery, permits byte-identical retry only when the original row/status/eligibility allows it, and permits an Armed-unsent attempt to use its original never-sent proof. It cannot create a new operation, change bytes, open source data, build projection, or publish service. Registered ArchiveAcquire and every other provider-started attempt therefore roll forward under their original identity until Applied/AlreadyApplied/refused/conflict or a definitive never-sent terminal; an unchanged Registered head is never zero-effect.
