Only after the outstanding bitmap is empty may `PreBootstrapRestoreAbandonAuthorizeSpec` construct the exact total cleanup plan and enter `AbandonmentAuthorized`. That state permanently revokes all old carry-forward guards; only new action-specific release/burn/revoke/destroy cleanup operations, their status recovery, and disposition publication are legal. CleanupInProgress monotonically records one typed action/resource disposition at a time under `PreBootstrapAbandonmentCleanupGate`. Every attempt uses the universal generated state machine and owns action-typed `PreBootstrapArtifactRef` values for status results, receipts/refusals/conflicts, portable terminal evidence, and import records; a digest alone is not recoverable authority. `PreBootstrapDispatchPublicationEvidence<Action>` and `PreBootstrapNeverSentProof<Action>` are specific to the journal authority plus local mirror and cannot be decoded as ordinary RootManifest evidence.

While `BridgeFrozenForCanonicalCreation`, recovery may construct and independently verify only unrooted candidate copies for the sealed import, canonical lease, plan import, bundle, and creation guard; every refresh invalidates them. Only `FirstRootPublicationFrozen` may construct the final canonical artifacts, whose immutable history covers every pre-first-root renewal/refresh/status transition and whose creation guard consumes that exact final journal head/mirror generation. Journal-key destruction/zeroization waits until the detailed-import floor is replaced by the explicitly preterminal `PreBootstrapDispatchTerminalAccumulator<Role>`. Abandonment instead seals the complete disposition transcript and retains its loss-tolerant recovery capsule through the same external nonreuse/status floor.

Prebootstrap time validation is explicit. `PreBootstrapTimeAuthorityBasis` is the portable certified `{restore_id,claimed_continuity_or_identity_reservation_authority,plan_digest,plan_availability_certificate_digest,permitted_time_profile_identities_and_digests,authority_domains_and_epochs,challenge_service_contracts,maximum_round_trip_and_uncertainty,valid_through,signer_set_epoch,signatures}` committed by every target claim/head. `PreBootstrapTimeAuthorityObservationImport` pairs that basis with a challenged portable observation and same-process `LocalChallengeRoundTripWitness`; it creates a linear nonserializable guard for only the named prebootstrap action. The first Local/Meta bridge proves exact profile/epoch/service handoff or a dual-signed transition into the canonical `TimeAuthorityRegistry`; no prebootstrap observation survives restart or authorizes post-bootstrap work.

`PendingRestoreAcquisitionGuard` is the linear/nonexportable `{restore_id,portable_plan_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PortableRestoreSourceAcquisitionPlan>,plan_availability_certificate_artifact_ref,exclusive_journal_staging_inode_identity_and_whole_inode_lease,current_dispatch_root_generation,current_prebootstrap_time_basis,exact_current_target_claim_continuity_reservation_and_grant_observation_imports,resource_bounds,remaining_action_bitmap,allowed_effects:DispatchOrAbandon,unconsumed}`. It may publish only registered prebootstrap attempts, immutable unreachable prebootstrap artifacts, lease-owner generations, or the abandonment transition. It becomes `RestoreProjectionBuildGuard<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` only after target `K_oid` exists and the complete required-success/equivalent action bijection/current lease owner are terminally imported. That linear guard is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,canonical_working_set_ref:StrongRef<RestoreCanonicalAcquisitionWorkingSet<Role>>,live_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation,current_prebootstrap_lease_owner_and_time_guard,authorized_archive_ref_set,decoder_opener_key_and_resource_inventory,allowed_effects:OpenTransformProjectRenewFreezeOrAbandon,unconsumed}`. Every archive/key open re-reads `PreBootstrapRestoreSourceLeaseCurrent`, the live grant/time evidence, and the unchanged action/resource inventory; the working set or a transform-plan digest alone never authorizes a read. The guard may build the exact transform plan/projection, renew through the same journal, or convert to abandonment. After projection and bridge recipes are complete it may consume itself only into `RestoreBridgeFreezeSpec`; the bridge-frozen guard can create/rebuild unrooted canonical candidates, refresh only the recovery-incarnation lease, enter the second freeze, or abandon, but can perform no source open or renewal. The second frozen guard can only publish the one first root or abandon.

Pre-bootstrap abandonment has separate total action and resource disposition protocols because no RestoreRegistry exists yet. `PreBootstrapActionDisposition` is the closed one-per-action union `NotAuthorized{action_key,zero_authorization_membership_proof}|AuthorizedNeverArmed{action_key,authorization_artifact_ref,zero_armed_membership_proof}|AbandonedBeforeSend{action_key,armed_publication_evidence_artifact_ref,typed_prebootstrap_never_sent_proof_artifact_ref}|Succeeded{action_key,typed_receipt_artifact_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_artifact_ref,terminal_import_artifact_ref,final_external_head_commitment}|NoEffect{action_key,disposition:PermanentlyRefused|ConflictNoEffect|ExpiredNotRegisteredNoEffect,typed_provider_tombstone_artifact_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_artifact_ref,terminal_import_artifact_ref,unchanged_external_head_commitment}`.

`UnpublishedTargetObjectSetQuarantine` is the exact prebootstrap artifact `{restore_id,journal_id,projection_result_identity_and_digest,zero_root_slot_and_zero_canonical_reference_proof,sorted_logical_ciphertext_encoding_placement_and_segment_inventory,sorted_local_staging_members[{filesystem_profile,mount_device,target_manifest_staging_inode_or_segment_identity,name_generation,whole_inode_or_segment_fence,expected_extent_inventory}],sorted_remote_members[{provider,target_identity,operation_family_and_pre_minted_cleanup_ids}],target_k_oid_disposition_basis,complete_projected_object_to_physical_owner_bijection,cleanup_floor,body_digest}`. `UnpublishedTargetObjectSetDisposition` is `CryptoErasedAwaitingPhysicalGc{quarantine_ref,target_k_oid_destroy_receipt_and_terminal_evidence,proof_every_ciphertext_depends_on_destroyed_fresh_target_identity,registered_physical_gc_family_and_retention_floor}|PhysicallyDeleted{quarantine_ref,sorted_local_member_deletion_receipts[FenceBoundIrreversibleReceiptHeader+exact_inode_name_generation_extent_proof],sorted_remote_deletion_terminal_imports[ExternalTerminalImportRecord<RestoreUnpublishedTargetPhysicalDelete,PreBootstrap>],complete_target_to_receipt_bijection,zero_remaining_owned_placement_proof}`. Crypto erasure is legal only for a clone-created fresh target identity; same-identity recovery must physically delete or retain quarantine until ordinary GC can prove deletion. Remote/object-store deletion uses registered dispatch; local deletion uses a member-issued fenced physical receipt. Neither a directory scan nor dropping a filename is terminal evidence.

`PreBootstrapResourceDisposition` is the closed one-per-resource union `ReleasedArchiveLease{resource_key,lease_identity,released_through_generation,disposition_action_key,typed_receipt_and_full_no_successor_proof_ref}|RegisteredArchiveAcquisitionRolledForwardThenReleased{resource_key,original_archive_acquire_action_key,registered_head_and_status_lineage_commitment,active_receipt_and_lease_identity,release_action_key,release_receipt_and_full_no_successor_proof_ref}|BurnedReservation{resource_key,reservation_kind,reservation_id,claim_id?,disposition_action_key,typed_burn_receipt_and_nonreuse_commitment}|CatalogTargetCleanup{resource_key,target_catalog_row_key,profile:ExternalCasCataloged|DirectoryBoundCataloged,terminal_disposition:PreBootstrapCatalogRestoreTargetTerminalDisposition::Cataloged}|ContinuityCleanup{resource_key,restore_id,target_incarnation,terminal_disposition:PreBootstrapRestoreContinuityCleanupDisposition}|DirectoryBoundZeroPublicationClosed{resource_key,target_manifest_staging_inode_and_fence_identity,zero_root_slot_proof,claim_burn_set_commitment,catalog_disposition:Cataloged{catalog_resource_key_and_terminal_commitment}|EmbeddedNoCatalog{zero_catalog_applicability_proof}}|RevokedTransientRecoveryWrap{resource_key,wrap_or_grant_identity,disposition_action_key,typed_terminal_evidence_commitment}|DestroyedFreshUnpublishedTargetObjectIdKey{resource_key,key_origin_proof,zero_canonical_publication_proof,disposition_action_key,typed_terminal_evidence_commitment}|UnpublishedTargetObjects{resource_key,quarantine_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<UnpublishedTargetObjectSetQuarantine>,terminal_disposition:UnpublishedTargetObjectSetDisposition}|RetainedTombstonedNoUse{resource_key,permanent_nonuse_commitment}`. Source-key destruction has no arm. ExternalCas cleanup has exactly one ContinuityCleanup row and, unless its authenticated profile proves no target catalog applies, one prior CatalogTargetCleanup row; a no-mutation continuity disposition is just as explicit as a CAS abandonment. DirectoryBound uses DirectoryBoundZeroPublicationClosed, requires its catalog sub-arm to match the profile, and cannot encode a continuity action. `TerminallyAbandoned` proves every projected logical object, ciphertext, encoding, placement, segment/extent, transient wrap, fresh key, claim, lease, and continuity/catalog resource appears exactly once in this disposition inventory.

`PreBootstrapRestoreAbandonIntent` is first sealed with the exact zero-canonical-publication proof, frozen current action/resource inventory, and cleanup-policy recipe, then its authenticated journal transition enters `AbandonmentDrainPending` as above. After every preexisting attempt terminalizes, `PreBootstrapRestoreAbandonAuthorizeSpec` seals the now-exact total action/resource disposition plan and enters `AbandonmentAuthorized`. Only that durable state can construct `PreBootstrapAbandonmentCleanupGate` and enter CleanupInProgress. It schedules exactly the lease release, reservation burns, profile-valid continuity abandonment or DirectoryBound zero-publication closure, transient-wrap revocations, clone-target-key destruction/tombstoning, and unpublished target-object disposition required by the resource inventory. If ArchiveAcquire was already RegisteredPending, drain recovery preserves its original request/source gate until it reaches Active, then the later cleanup plan immediately releases the returned lease and records `RegisteredArchiveAcquisitionRolledForwardThenReleased`. Release/status remains legal after lease expiry under its exact cleanup contract; claim/ordinary acquisition does not. No cleanup send is legal while the root remains Building, AcquiredProjectionAllowed, BridgeFrozenForCanonicalCreation, or AbandonmentDrainPending.

Terminal journal state is `TerminallyAbandoned{sorted_action_dispositions[PreBootstrapActionDisposition],complete_planned_action_and_family_member_bijection,sorted_resource_dispositions[PreBootstrapResourceDisposition],complete_claimed_acquired_created_resource_bijection,action_to_resource_disposition_relation,full_operation_idempotency_request_source_gate_status_and_typed_terminal_lineage_commitment,final_external_head_set_commitment,zero_canonical_publication_proof,terminal_body_digest}`. No two-receipt shortcut or duplicate release/burn row exists. Crash before Armed may use the typed never-sent proof; crash after SentUnknown must status-resolve the same bytes; crash after acquisition can only resume the same projection/creation until AbandonmentAuthorized is durable, after which it can only complete the exact disposition set. The plan availability capsule, claimed authorities, external heads, typed portable artifacts, and sealed journal—not an unrooted local file—are durable recovery authority.

Before `K_restore_journal` can be destroyed on the no-RootManifest path, recovery constructs unsigned reference-free `PortablePreBootstrapAbandonmentTerminalCertificateBody {restore_id,journal_id,predecessor_active_journal_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,predecessor_head_and_dispatch_state_generations,final_active_capsule_digest,plan_recipe_and_final_plan_digests,plan_and_refinement_availability_certificate_digests,terminal_journal_body_digest,complete_action_and_family_member_terminal_accumulator_root,complete_resource_disposition_accumulator_root,final_external_head_and_provider_operation_tombstone_set,permanent_operation_id_and_reservation_nonreuse_floors,zero_canonical_RootManifest_RootSlot_and_target_authority_publication_proof,profile_terminal_disposition:ExternalCasCataloged{catalog_tombstone_terminal_commitment,continuity_cleanup_disposition:PreBootstrapRestoreContinuityCleanupDisposition}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{catalog_tombstone_terminal_commitment,zero_publication_closure_commitment}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{zero_catalog_applicability_proof,zero_publication_closure_commitment},unpublished_target_object_terminal_disposition_commitment,journal_key_commitment,journal_key_destruction_authorization_digest,retention_floor,body_digest}`. It names only the already-existing Active predecessor, never the future terminal head. The profile arm, action accumulator, and resource accumulator must agree exactly on whether catalog and continuity actions existed; no generic “abandoned” bit can hide a missing cleanup stage.

The journal authority verifies and threshold-signs that body as `PortablePreBootstrapAbandonmentTerminalCertificate`, computes its digest, and atomically CASes the exact Active predecessor to `TerminalAbandoned{certificate_digest,retention_floor}` with `head_generation + 1`. It returns separate `PreBootstrapJournalAbandonmentTerminalizationReceipt {journal_id,publication_operation_id,publication_idempotency_digest,predecessor_active_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,certificate_digest,result_terminal_head_bytes_and_digest_and_cas_version,authority_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. The certificate never contains the result-head digest, and neither certificate nor terminal head embeds the later receipt. The receipt is retained outside the result capsule and may enter only a later immutable recovery package that does not name its own publication result. `PreBootstrapJournalKeyDisposition::AbandonedThenDestroyed` retains the certificate, receipt, and authenticated terminal head. None contains a local inode, artifact locator, journal MAC, or plaintext/key-dependent proof. Local zeroization or provider destruction evidence byte-matches the certificate and its key-destruction authorization. Thereafter recovery/status/nonreuse checks use the certificate, terminalization receipt/head, and authenticated provider heads/floors, never the destroyed MAC key. Successful creation instead imports and seals the complete journal lineage into the first canonical RootManifest.

**Restore registry, source-lease operations, terminal compaction, and abandonment.** `RestoreRegistry` is the closed role union `Local{registry_generation,activity:Idle|OneLive{restore_id},entries[restore_id->LocalRestoreRegistryValue]}|Meta{registry_generation,activity:Idle|OneLive{restore_id},entries[restore_id->MetaRestoreRegistryValue]}|Shard{shard_id,registry_generation,activity:Idle|OneLive{restore_id},entries[restore_id->ShardRestoreRegistryValue]}`; role/group substitution or more than one live restore is unencodable. `RestoreLeaseOperationKind` is the closed type-level tag `Renew|Release`; `RestoreLeaseDispatchContract<Role,Kind>` is the generated exact family `Renew -> CanonicalRestoreArchiveLeaseRenewContract<Role>` and `Release -> CanonicalRestoreArchiveLeaseReleaseContract<Role>`. Those canonical contracts are distinct from the restore-prebootstrap renew/release contracts even when operation bodies are byte-equal. `RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole,Kind:RestoreLeaseOperationKind>` is the exact `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,lease_identity,operation_kind:Kind,dispatch_contract_id:RestoreLeaseDispatchContract<Role,Kind>,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_action_body_digest,terminal:Applied{typed_receipt_ref:RestoreLeaseAppliedReceiptRef<Role,Kind>,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<RestoreLeaseDispatchContract<Role,Kind>>>,terminal_import_ref:StrongRef<ExternalTerminalImportRecord<RestoreLeaseDispatchContract<Role,Kind>,Role>>,result_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,result_portable_lease_state_digest:RestoreLeaseAppliedResultState<Kind>}|NeverSent{dispatch_never_sent_proof_ref:StrongRef<DispatchNeverSentProof<RestoreLeaseDispatchContract<Role,Kind>>>,abandonment_publication_evidence_commitment,current_head_observation_ref:RestoreLeaseCurrentHeadObservationRef<Role,Kind>}|NoEffect{typed_provider_operation_tombstone_ref:RestoreLeaseNoEffectArtifactRef<Role,Kind>,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<RestoreLeaseDispatchContract<Role,Kind>>>,terminal_import_ref:StrongRef<ExternalTerminalImportRecord<RestoreLeaseDispatchContract<Role,Kind>,Role>>,unchanged_head_observation_ref:RestoreLeaseCurrentHeadObservationRef<Role,Kind>,disposition:RestoreLeaseNoEffectDisposition<Kind>},body_digest}`. Detailed records own protected payload/evidence bytes and therefore retain the full nonwidenable authority binding through their status/replay floor; only the later payload-free proof accumulator may use a terminal authority commitment. `RestoreLeaseAppliedResultState<Renew>` requires the returned successor portable lease state and `RestoreLeaseAppliedResultState<Release>` requires the terminal released-head/no-successor commitment; `RestoreLeaseNoEffectDisposition<Kind>` and every receipt/artifact/observation ref are generated closed, kind-specific unions with no nullable or cross-kind arm. `RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalRecordRef<Role>` is exactly `Renew{record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalRecord<Role,Renew>>}|Release{record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalRecord<Role,Release>>}`. `RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalIndex<Role>` is the immutable map `(lease_identity,operation_kind,operation_id) -> RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalRecordRef<Role>`; every renewal/release terminal—success, never-sent abandonment, or no-effect—appends exactly one entry, so an operation ID can never disappear or be reused after later generations. `RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalAccumulator<Role>` is the checked compact authenticated map/MMR over the same sorted keys and terminal record canonical digests, with proof generation sufficient for status/nonreuse after the detailed replay floor closes. `RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalHistory<Role>` is `Detailed{index_ref:StrongRef<RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalIndex<Role>>}|Compacted{accumulator_ref:StrongRef<RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalAccumulator<Role>>,compacted_through_floor}`. `RestoreRegistryCommon<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,restore_epoch,mode:RecoverSameIdentity|CloneNewIdentity,source_posture,target_posture,source_manifest_identity,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,source_lease_identity:RestoreSourceLeaseIdentity,transform_plan_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationTransformPlan<Role>>,projection_result_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationProjectionResult<Role>>,source_key_access_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessInventory<Role>>,lease_bound_source_access_set_ref:StrongRef<RestoreLeaseBoundSourceAccessSet<Role>>,prebootstrap_evidence_state:RestorePreBootstrapEvidenceState<Role>,lease_operation_terminal_history:RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalHistory<Role>,state_generation}`. Both source-access edges exist from the first bridge until cleanup generation zero atomically assumes ownership; detailed prebootstrap-evidence compaction cannot collect them.

Restore phase and lease operation are orthogonal. `RestoreLeaseState<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is exactly `Current{latest_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role>>}|RenewAuthorizedUndispatched{latest_lease_record_ref,operation_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRenewOperationRecord<Role>>,resume_phase_commitment}|RenewalClosed{latest_lease_record_ref,renewal_close_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRenewalCloseRecord<Role>>}|ReleaseAuthorizedUndispatched{latest_lease_record_ref,renewal_close_record_ref,operation_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationRecord<Role>>,completion_evidence_ref:RestoreCompletionEvidenceRef<Role>}`. Only one renewal or release can exist. Renewal is legal in every hidden/recovery phase that still needs the source, including RecoveryAbandoned, but `RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupFinalizeSpec<Role>` may enter AwaitingSourceRelease only by proving no unresolved renewal and atomically creating `RestoreSourceLeaseRenewalCloseRecord<Role> {authority_bound_header,restore_id,lease_identity,latest_generation_and_lineage_checkpoint,complete_renew_operation_terminal_history_commitment,zero_authorized_armed_sent_unknown_or_import-pending_renewal_proof,closed_at_applied_identity,body_digest}` plus `RenewalClosed`. No renew authorization or retry is inhabitable afterward. Release is legal only from AwaitingSourceRelease + RenewalClosed. A lease operation freezes the exact restore phase and finalization returns to that phase or advances its one registered terminal path.

If a renew authorization becomes audit-visible but eligibility expires before an Armed attempt is published, `RestoreSourceLeaseRenewAuthorizedNeverArmedFinalizeSpec<Role>` consumes the operation record, a current zero-attempt-membership proof over the exact Protocol attempt slot, current ineligibility evidence, and a permanent operation-ID/request nonreuse tombstone; it appends a distinct `AuthorizedNeverArmed` terminal-history member and returns the unchanged lease to Current/frozen phase. It does not require `DispatchNeverSentProof`, which exists only after Armed publication. Release has the symmetric `RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseAuthorizedNeverArmedFinalizeSpec<Role>` for administrative cancellation or invalidated authority before Arm; lease expiry alone does not force that path because the release cleanup contract remains exactly retryable after expiry. Once Armed exists, only the registered never-sent, status, no-effect, or applied paths are legal.

`RestoreSourceKeyAccessResource` is the generated exact inventory member for every source-`K_oid`, bootstrap-DEK, object-DEK, transient unwrap/rewrap grant, threshold share, HSM session, archive-escrow release, ephemeral plaintext opener, and one-shot provider handle created by the acquisition bundle. `RestoreSourceKeyAccessInventory<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is constructed before the first bridge and installed by it as `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,source_lease_identity,source_acquisition_bundle_identity_and_digest,sorted_resources[RestoreSourceKeyAccessResource],each_resource_origin_provider_contract_recipient_subject_lease_binding_and_canonical_owner,complete_bundle_artifact_receipt_opener_grant_share_session_handle_to_resource_bijection,zero_unclassified_source_access,inventory_digest,body_digest}`. It owns the exact canonical resource records/copies, not the acquisition bundle or a prebootstrap locator. `RestoreLeaseBoundSourceAccessSet<Role>` is the separate pre-order `{authority_bound_header,restore_id,source_lease_identity,source_access_inventory_identity_and_digest,sorted_members[{resource_identity,resource_kind,provider_contract_identity_and_digest,provider_binding_proves_no_use_after_matching_release:true}],complete_lease_bound_subset_proof,set_digest,body_digest}`. It contains no release body, disposition, operation, or receipt. The first bridge makes the registry own both objects; cleanup generation zero atomically assumes those edges, so evidence compaction cannot collect them.

`RestoreSourceKeyAccessDisposition` is the closed union `PromotedCurrentTargetKey{resource_identity,required_kind:PreservedSameIdentityObjectIdKey,key_equality_and_target_authority_proof}|LeaseBoundAutoRevoked{resource_identity,lease_bound_set_membership_proof,provider_contract_identity_and_digest,source_lease_identity,canonical_release_action_body_digest,provider_binding_proves_no_use_after_matching_release:true}|ExplicitlyRevoked{resource_identity,revocation_dispatch_contract_id,revocation_operation_id,typed_receipt_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_ref,terminal_import_ref,provider_head_and_counter_commitment}|ProviderAlreadyClosedNoUse{resource_identity,typed_provider_closed_tombstone_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_ref,terminal_import_ref,zero_successor_or_reopen_proof}|EphemeralUseConsumedAndClosed{resource_identity,required_kind:OneShotThresholdShare|HsmSession|PlaintextOpener|OneShotArchiveEscrowRelease,one_shot_provider_contract_identity_and_digest,consumption_or_session_close_terminal_evidence,local_zeroization_evidence,no_replay_or_successor_proof}`. The canonical release action body names only `RestoreLeaseBoundSourceAccessSet<Role>.set_digest`; dispositions may later name that already-computable body digest, so no identity cycle exists. `PromotedCurrentTargetKey` is uninhabited for clone source keys or any DEK/transient grant. `EphemeralUseConsumedAndClosed` is uninhabited for any renewable grant, reusable key, retained unwrap authority, or resource with a provider revocation endpoint.

`RestoreSourceAccessRevocationOperationRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole,Kind>` is the exact `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,source_lease_identity,source_resource_identity,resource_kind:Kind,expected_provider_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,dispatch_contract_id:RestoreSourceAccessRevocationContract<Role,Kind>,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_action_body_recipe_and_digest,authorized_from_progress_generation,resume_progress_recipe,terminal_audit_gate_commitment,body_digest}`; it contains no future attempt, publication evidence, receipt, provider result, or successor progress. `RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupProgress<Role>` is the generationed exact `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,source_lease_identity,source_access_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessInventory<Role>>,lease_bound_source_access_set_ref:StrongRef<RestoreLeaseBoundSourceAccessSet<Role>>,progress_generation,sorted_resource_states[(resource_identity)->PromotedTargetKeyTerminal{proof_commitment}|EphemeralClosedTerminal{close_and_zeroization_evidence_commitment}|LeaseBoundPendingRelease{set_membership_proof,provider_contract_identity_and_digest}|ExplicitRevocationPending{resource_kind,current_provider_head_and_contract_body_recipe,prior_terminal_operation_history}|ExplicitRevocationAuthorized{operation_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceAccessRevocationOperationRecord<Role,ExactResourceKind>>,dispatch_contract_id,operation_id}|ExplicitRevocationPendingAfterAuthorizedNeverArmed{retired_operation_record_ref,zero_attempt_membership_and_operation_nonreuse_tombstone_ref,next_operation_recipe}|ExplicitRevocationTerminal{operation_record_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<RestoreSourceAccessRevocationContract<Role,ExactResourceKind>>>,typed_receipt_ref,terminal_import_ref,provider_head_and_counter_commitment}|ProviderAlreadyClosedTerminal{typed_closed_tombstone_evidence_and_import}],outstanding_explicit_revocation_bitmap,complete_source_resource_to_state_bijection,current_terminal_pin_basis_commitment,required_shard_source_access_closure_set_commitment,body_digest}`. Each explicit revocation has its own canonical-plane contract instance; response loss is status-resolved under the same contract/operation bytes. Authorization moves only Pending -> Authorized. If eligibility disappears before Armed, an exact zero-attempt/nonreuse finalize appends history and returns the resource to PendingAfterAuthorizedNeverArmed; it does not pretend the resource is closed. No digest-only “done” state or untyped progress owner is legal.

`RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Role>` is the checked compact `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,source_lease_identity,source_access_inventory_and_lease_bound_set_commitment,sorted_terminal_resource_projections[RestoreSourceKeyAccessTerminalProjection<Role>],complete_source_inventory_and_required_shard_closure_to_disposition_bijection,zero_unclassified_or_live_source_access_proof,canonical_release_action_body_and_lease_bound_set_binding_commitment,detailed_terminal_evidence_retention_floor,checker_version,accumulator_root,body_digest}`. `RestoreSourceKeyAccessTerminalProjection<Role>` is a generated closed per-disposition union; each arm carries exactly its legal operation/request/status/receipt/import/provider-head/nonreuse or promoted/ephemeral proof commitment, with no nullable fields. Because the accumulator owns protected proof/status bytes, it retains the full authority header until a later payload-free digest tombstone after its floor. `RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupRecord<Role>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,source_lease_identity,source_access_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessInventory<Role>>,lease_bound_source_access_set_ref:StrongRef<RestoreLeaseBoundSourceAccessSet<Role>>,final_progress_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupProgress<Role>>,sorted_resource_dispositions[RestoreSourceKeyAccessDisposition],required_shard_source_access_closure_refs:RestoreRequiredShardSourceAccessClosureSet<Role>,complete_provider_resource_and_shard_closure_to_disposition_bijection,independent_reopen_or_abandonment_basis,zero_unclassified_source_access_proof,canonical_release_action_body_recipe_and_digest,accumulator_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Role>>,body_digest}`. `RestoreRequiredShardSourceAccessClosureSet<Local>` is the exact empty proof; `<Meta>` is every-and-only configured certified `ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure`. Cleanup finalize requires zero Pending, Authorized, SentUnknown, import-pending, or unresolved renewal; constructs the stable release body over the pre-order set digest; creates the accumulator, record, renewal-close record, and generation; and atomically enters AwaitingSourceRelease + RenewalClosed. The completion/pin-install transition that first enters AwaitingSourceAccessCleanup must simultaneously construct progress generation zero from every inventory member and required Shard closure—there is no phase with a missing progress owner. Later checked compaction may drop detailed records only after the accumulator's declared status/replay floor.

Physical pin ownership is terminal-disposition-shaped rather than promotion-shaped. `RestoreTerminalDisposition` is the closed tag `Operational|Abandoned`. `RestoreTerminalPhysicalInventory<Role,Disposition>` is the exact role/disposition-refined `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,restore_id,restore_epoch,role_and_group,terminal_disposition:Disposition,source_root_manifest_slot_and_publication_commitment,sorted_logical_members[{typed_object_ref,logical_kind,schema_and_format_epoch,key_and_wrap_member,encoding_member,placement_member,segment_or_extent_membership,keep_through_terminal_floor_or_later_gc_disposition}],sorted_physical_generation_lease_and_fence_members,complete_object_ciphertext_encoding_key_wrap_placement_segment_extent_and_lease_bijection,availability_requirement:IndependentReopen|OperationalAck|AbandonmentForensicsAndSafeGc,reopen_or_forensics_checker_profile,complete_closure_proof_ref,inventory_digest,body_digest}`.

`RestorePromotionRootSealRecipe<Role>` is the pre-order `{restore_id,role_and_group,promotion_or_open_applied_identity,canonical_publication_evidence,sealed_role_state_root_identity,sealed_root_manifest_identity,winning_root_slot_generation_and_digest,promotion_authority_profile_and_composite_receipt_digest,service_visibility_epoch,operational_inventory_identity_and_digest,planned_pin_id_and_generation,allowed_pending_reopen_successor_classes,recipe_digest}`. It excludes the pin durability receipt, seal identity/digest, seal-install result, reopen proof, and every later acknowledgement. `RestoreTerminalPinOwnerSubject<Role,Disposition>` is exact: `Operational{promotion_seal_recipe:RestorePromotionRootSealRecipe<Role>}` or `Abandoned{abandonment_publication_evidence,abandonment_tombstone_ref:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneRef<Role>,zero_service_commitment}`.

`RestoreTerminalPinOperationFamily<Role,Disposition>` pre-mints one activation and one release operation/contract/idempotency/body recipe for every exact storage-domain member range in the inventory. `RestoreTerminalPinIndex<Role>` is the DB-owned immutable map `pin_id -> RestoreTerminalPinLedgerEntry<Role,Disposition>`, rooted by the role's RetentionMap/restore anchor. The entry is the closed generationed state `ActivationPending{authority_binding,inventory_ref,owner_subject,operation_family_ref,outstanding_activation_bitmap}|Active{authority_binding,inventory_ref,owner_subject,operation_family_ref,activation_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinDurabilityReceipt<Role,Disposition>>}|ReleaseAuthorized{authority_binding,inventory_ref,owner_subject,operation_family_ref,activation_receipt_ref,release_authorization_ref,outstanding_release_bitmap}|Released{terminal_authority_commitment,inventory_digest,owner_subject_public_commitment,activation_and_release_accumulator_root,release_completion_identity,provider_status_and_nonreuse_floor}`. Each storage-domain activation/release uses its exact canonical universal-dispatch contract; timeout is never an acknowledgement, and the ledger records every Armed/SentUnknown/status/terminal lineage.

`RestoreTerminalPinDurabilityReceipt<Role,Disposition>` is constructed only after all activation members are terminal Applied/AlreadyApplied and is `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,pin_id,pin_generation,restore_id,restore_epoch,role_and_group,terminal_disposition:Disposition,inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPhysicalInventory<Role,Disposition>>,owner_subject:RestoreTerminalPinOwnerSubject<Role,Disposition>,activation_pending_applied_identity,active_install_assigned_identity,sorted_storage_pin_terminal_imports[{storage_domain,member_range,dispatch_contract_operation_request_status_receipt_and_provider_head_commitment,acknowledged_inventory_digest,durable_pin_generation,fence_or_lease_commitment}],complete_inventory_member_to_storage_ack_bijection,no_partial_pin_proof,release_authority_class_and_minimum_floor,body_digest}`. The operational receipt binds the seal **recipe**, never a seal identity; the later seal may safely reference this receipt. The abandoned receipt binds the already-published tombstone/publication. Cross-role/disposition decoding fails.

`RestoreTerminalPinReleaseAuthorizationRecord<Role,Disposition>` freezes the Active ledger entry, exact member set, release operation family, cleanup authority, and expected terminal owner disposition without dropping any pin. `RestoreTerminalPinReleaseCompletion<Role,Disposition>` is created only after every member release import proves Released/AlreadyReleased for the exact pin generation and is `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,pin_id,pin_generation,inventory_digest,release_authorization_ref,sorted_release_terminal_import_commitments,complete_member_bijection,post_release_zero_provider_pin_proof,provider_status_and_nonreuse_floor,body_digest}`. `RestoreTerminalPinReleaseFinalizeSpec` consumes that completion, current Active/ReleaseAuthorized entry, and a current no-other-owner proof; only its durable apply changes the ledger to Released and permits the payload/registry to drop the basis. Local, Meta, and Shard release flows all use this sequence rather than an atomic local “pin drop.”

`RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Role>` is the generated exact union `Operational{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,restore_id,terminal_disposition:Operational,promotion_seal_ref:StrongRef<RestorePromotionRootSeal<Role>>,physical_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPhysicalInventory<Role,Operational>>,pin_id,pin_durability_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinDurabilityReceipt<Role,Operational>>,independent_reopen_or_operational_ack_commitment,ack_subject:AuthorityOwning{not_applicable_proof}|Shard{expected_operational_ack_subject_recipe},body_digest}|Abandoned{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,restore_id,terminal_disposition:Abandoned,abandonment_publication_evidence:CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Role>,abandonment_tombstone_ref:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneRef<Role>,retained_recovery_physical_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPhysicalInventory<Role,Abandoned>>,pin_id,pin_durability_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinDurabilityReceipt<Role,Abandoned>>,zero_service_observation_and_profile_terminal_authority_commitment,body_digest}`. The generated role validator exposes the first ack-subject arm only to Local/Meta and the second only to Shard; neither encodes a nullable field or future ack identity. `OperationalRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Role>` and `AbandonedRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Role>` are tag-refined refs. Both arms own protected bytes and retain full authority. The abandoned arm is constructed only after the abandonment root is durable and activation ledger is Active; its install atomically creates cleanup progress generation zero for Local/Meta or enters Shard AwaitingSourceAccessClosure. Each role keeps exactly one Active basis until its storage-domain release completion is imported and finalized.

The payload owns this closure through one phase-shaped field. `RestoreAbandonmentApplySubjectRecipe<Role>` is the normalized reference-free `{restore_id,restore_epoch,role_and_group,abandonment_authority_seed_digest,expected_predecessor_phase_and_generation,assigned_command_sequence_index_and_hlc_inputs,command_kind:LocalApply|MetaFinalize|ShardApply,expected_no_service_and_source_cleanup_policy,subject_recipe_digest}`. It explicitly excludes the abandonment spec/operation record, pending owner, tombstone/skeleton identity, command record, result payload/root/manifest/slot, publication evidence, pin, receipt, and acknowledgement.

`RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRecipe<Role>` is the generated reference-free role union `AuthorityOwning{restore_id,restore_epoch,role:Local|Meta,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,abandonment_authority_seed_digest,authority_profile_projection_recipe,fixed_no_service_no_same_incarnation_successor_and_higher_incarnation_only_rules,source_access_cleanup_and_release_latest_lease_obligation_recipe,typed_holes:[AbandonOperationRecordIdentity,AbandonAppliedIdentity,ProfileTerminalImportsOrDirectoryBoundReceiptImport,PendingPinOwnerIdentity],hole_fill_rules_and_expected_schema_tags,skeleton_recipe_digest}|Shard{restore_id,restore_epoch,shard_id,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,abandonment_authority_seed_digest,source_meta_operation_public_commitment,participant_apply_authorization_public_commitment,own_no_target_observation_public_commitment,source_access_cleanup_projection_recipe,fixed_no_service_no_same_incarnation_successor_and_higher_incarnation_only_rules,typed_holes:[ParticipantCertificateIdentity,ShardApplyIdentity,ShardProfileAppliedBasis,PendingPinOwnerIdentity],hole_fill_rules_and_expected_schema_tags,skeleton_recipe_digest}`. DirectoryBound authority-owning recipes contain only assigned slot generation and expected result/receipt **recipes**; actual result root/slot/receipt values are later fill evidence and never enter the skeleton digest. `RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeleton<Role>` is `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,recipe:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRecipe<Role>,skeleton_digest:BodyDigest<RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeleton<Role>>}`. Its digest covers the recipe bytes, ordered hole tags, and fill rules but excludes every future hole value, tombstone body digest, result root, publication evidence, pin, receipt, acknowledgement, and terminal common. `RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRef<Role>` is the generated exact strong-ref family for Local, Meta, or Shard. `RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneMaterializationProof<Role>` is the inline checked `{skeleton_ref:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRef<Role>,sorted_filled_hole_values_and_source_evidence,complete_unique_fill_proof,profile_and_role_checker_version,materialization_transcript_digest}`; its transcript excludes the containing tombstone's `body_digest`.

`RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwnerRecipe<Role>` is the reference-free role-tagged `{restore_id,restore_epoch,role_and_group,apply_subject_recipe:RestoreAbandonmentApplySubjectRecipe<Role>,tombstone_skeleton_recipe:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRecipe<Role>,retained_recovery_physical_inventory_recipe_digest,pin_acquisition_recipe_digest,zero_service_observation_and_profile_terminal_authority_commitment,source_root_and_configuration_commitment,recipe_digest}`; it is portable enough for Meta to certify a Shard's exact construction but cannot own bytes or authorize a pin by itself. `RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Role>` is the target-keyed pre-order `{authority_binding_or_shard_projection:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,recipe:RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwnerRecipe<Role>,abandonment_tombstone_skeleton_ref:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRef<Role>,retained_recovery_physical_inventory_recipe,pin_acquisition_recipe,complete_recipe_expansion_and_role_match_digest,body_digest}`; it contains no future root, publication evidence, tombstone ref, pin, or receipt. A Shard certificate entry carries the complete two recipes plus availability evidence, not a digest-only promise. `RestoreRetentionAnchor<Role>` is the generated exact union `None|PromotionSealPendingReopen{seal_ref:StrongRef<RestorePromotionRootSeal<Role>>}|AbandonmentPinPending{pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Role>>}|TerminalPinBasis{basis_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Role>>}`. Pending reopen owns the seal; operational completion/participant-complete owns the operational basis; abandonment finalize/apply owns the prebuilt pending owner; abandonment-pin install replaces it with the abandoned basis; terminal release selects None. Exactly one arm is legal for each registered restore phase, and the generated payload/registry cross-check rejects an unrooted seal, pending owner, basis, inventory, or pin.

`LocalRestoreStateRef` and `MetaRestoreStateRef` are generated role-refined strong references to the corresponding `RestoreState` arm. `LocalRestorePhase` is `ActiveHidden{restore_state_ref:LocalRestoreStateRef}|AbandonAuthorizedHidden{restore_state_ref:LocalRestoreStateRef,abandon_operation_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonOperationRecord<Local>>}|PromotedAwaitingReopen{promotion_applied_ref,manifest_ref:StrongRef<RestoreServicePromotionManifest>,promotion_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreServicePromotionReceipt>,retention_anchor_arm:PromotionSealPendingReopen}|AbandonedAuthorityAppliedAwaitingReceipt{directory_bound_abandon_apply_identity,skeleton_ref:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRef<Local>,pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Local>>,receipt_body_and_expected_result_publication_recipes,expected_abandonment_publication_subject_recipe,source_lease_current_ref}|AbandonedAwaitingTerminalPin{abandon_finalize_or_receipt_import_applied_ref,abandonment_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<AuthorityOwningRestoreAbandonmentTombstone<Local>>,abandonment_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentReceipt>,pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Local>>,expected_abandonment_publication_subject_recipe}|AwaitingSourceAccessCleanup{terminal:Operational{completion_applied_ref,promotion_receipt_ref,independent_reopen_proof_ref:StrongRef<LocalRestoreIndependentReopenProof>,terminal_pin_basis_ref:OperationalRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Local>}|Abandoned{abandon_finalize_applied_ref,abandonment_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<AuthorityOwningRestoreAbandonmentTombstone<Local>>,abandonment_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentReceipt>,terminal_pin_basis_ref:AbandonedRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Local>},cleanup_progress_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupProgress<Local>>}|AwaitingSourceRelease{terminal,terminal_pin_basis_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Local>>,source_access_cleanup_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupRecord<Local>>,source_access_cleanup_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Local>>,prebootstrap_dispatch_terminal_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapDispatchTerminalAccumulator<Local>>}`. `MetaRestorePhase` is the generated corresponding exact union using `MetaRestoreStateRef`, `RestoreAbandonOperationRecord<Meta>`, `AuthorityOwningRestoreAbandonmentTombstone<Meta>`, the participant-apply certificate, exact required-shard commitment, complete operational/abandon ack plus Shard source-access-closure commitment, pending owner or exact matching `RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Meta>` arm, `RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupProgress<Meta>`, `RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupRecord<Meta>`, `RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Meta>`, and the preterminal accumulator in AwaitingSourceRelease; `AbandonedAuthorityAppliedAwaitingReceipt` is Local-only. Renewal remains legal while receipt recovery, cleanup, or abandoned pin installation is pending. After each pending root is durable, publication evidence is constructed outside that root. `LocalRestoreAbandonmentPinInstallSpec` and `MetaRestoreAbandonmentPinInstallSpec` consume that evidence, the already-published abandonment root/tombstone, exact `RestoreTerminalPhysicalInventory<Role,Abandoned>`, new `RestoreTerminalPinDurabilityReceipt<Role,Abandoned>`, and no-other-owner proof, install `RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Role>::Abandoned`, and atomically create cleanup progress generation zero before entering AwaitingSourceAccessCleanup. No source-access cleanup, source release, or abandon acknowledgement is legal before that install. `RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAuthorize/Import/FinalizeSpec<Role>` use universal dispatch for every explicit revocation and may finalize only when the complete resource and required-Shard-closure bijection is terminal or bound to the exact forthcoming lease-release body. Cleanup finalize requires the already-existing `PreBootstrapDispatchTerminalAccumulator<Role>`, closes renewal, enters AwaitingSourceRelease, and preserves the same terminal pin basis.

`LocalRestoreRegistryValue` is `Nonterminal{common:RestoreRegistryCommon<Local>,phase:LocalRestorePhase,lease_state:RestoreLeaseState<Local>}|ReleasedTerminal{terminal_common:RestoreRegistryTerminalCommon<Local>,release_operation_summary_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationSummary<Local>>,release_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveLeaseReleaseReceipt>,release_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreSourceLeaseReleaseTerminalEvidence>,terminal_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<LocalRestoreTerminalTombstone>,release_finalize_applied_identity:WeakAuthorityAppliedIdentity}`.

`MetaRestoreRegistryValue` is `Nonterminal{common:RestoreRegistryCommon<Meta>,phase:MetaRestorePhase,lease_state:RestoreLeaseState<Meta>}|SourceReleasedAwaitingPinReleaseAuthorizationPublication{terminalizing_common:RestoreRegistryTerminalCommon<Meta>,release_operation_summary_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationSummary<Meta>>,release_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveLeaseReleaseReceipt>,release_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreSourceLeaseReleaseTerminalEvidence>,terminal_pin_basis_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Meta>>,pin_release_authorization_body_recipe,required_shard_set_commitment}|SourceReleasedAwaitingParticipantPinRelease{terminalizing_common:RestoreRegistryTerminalCommon<Meta>,release_operation_summary_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationSummary<Meta>>,release_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveLeaseReleaseReceipt>,release_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreSourceLeaseReleaseTerminalEvidence>,terminal_pin_basis_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Meta>>,pin_release_authorization_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinReleaseAuthorization>,required_shard_set_commitment,outstanding_shard_ack_bitmap}|ReleasedTerminal{terminal_common:RestoreRegistryTerminalCommon<Meta>,release_operation_summary_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationSummary<Meta>>,release_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveLeaseReleaseReceipt>,release_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreSourceLeaseReleaseTerminalEvidence>,terminal_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<MetaRestoreTerminalTombstone>,complete_shard_pin_release_ack_commitment,release_finalize_applied_identity:WeakAuthorityAppliedIdentity}`. Source lease release, authorization-certificate publication, participant pin release, and Meta pin release are distinct. Meta cannot expose an authorization before the release-finalize root exists or compact to terminal while any configured Shard acknowledgement is missing. Operational and abandoned terminals use the same protocol but retain different tag-refined pin bases. Both terminal roles retain the action-typed portable receipt/evidence needed for independent status verification; only their live source authority, lease, transform, projection, terminal pin basis, and operation-record edges are cut.

`ShardRestoreRegistryCommonProjection` is `{restore_id,restore_epoch,mode,source_and_target_posture,source_manifest_identity,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,shard_id,source_meta_prefix_and_configuration_identity,bootstrap_certificate_identity,local_transform_plan_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationTransformPlan<Shard>>,local_projection_result_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationProjectionResult<Shard>>,source_lease_identity,source_lease_projection_generation_and_digest,state_generation,body_digest}`. It contains no `AuthorityBoundHeader`, raw Meta object, canonical source-lease record, reservation, continuity, or provider authority. `ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure` is the exact `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Shard>,restore_id,restore_epoch,shard_id,source_meta_prefix_and_configuration_identity,source_lease_projection_identity_generation_and_digest,sorted_source_access_members[{resource_identity,resource_kind,origin_provider_and_recipient_commitment,terminal_disposition:EphemeralClosed{close_and_zeroization_evidence}|LeaseBoundRetained{meta_release_set_member_projection}|PromotedTargetKey{key_equality_and_target_authority_proof}|ExplicitlyRevoked{operation_receipt_terminal_evidence_and_provider_head_commitment}}],zero_live_archive_open_key_unwrap_threshold_share_hsm_session_plaintext_opener_or_source_read_guard_proof,source_access_close_fence_generation,complete_shard_bootstrap_projection_resource_to_disposition_bijection,terminalizing_projection_recipe,body_digest}`. It contains no future acknowledgement/root/certificate and cannot treat a lease-bound resource as locally closed. `ShardRestoreRegistryTerminalizingProjection` is the compact `{restore_id,restore_epoch,mode,target_identity_incarnation_service_and_shard_commitment,source_meta_bootstrap_and_configuration_commitment,transform_projection_and_final_restore_commitment,source_lease_lineage_commitment,source_access_closure_identity_and_digest,terminal_disposition:Operational|Abandoned,pin_release_generation,replay_floor,body_digest}` with no live transform/projection/lease/seal edge. `ShardRestoreTerminalTombstone` is `{restore_id,restore_epoch,shard_id,target_identity_incarnation_and_service_commitment,terminal_disposition,meta_pin_release_authorization_identity,local_seal_and_pin_release_apply_commitment,source_lease_continuity_catalog_and_source_access_closure_commitments,replay_and_status_floor,body_digest}` and contains no future acknowledgement identity, live source projection, lease authority, promotion seal, pin, recovery proof, or remote strong reference.

`ShardRestoreRegistryValue` is `ActiveHidden{common_projection:ShardRestoreRegistryCommonProjection,restore_state_ref:StrongRef<RestoreState>,source_lease_projection_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjection>}|OpenedAwaitingIndependentReopen{common_projection:ShardRestoreRegistryCommonProjection,open_applied_ref,promotion_receipt_ref,source_lease_projection_ref}|OperationalAwaitingSourceAccessClosure{common_projection:ShardRestoreRegistryCommonProjection,reopen_confirm_applied_ref,independent_reopen_proof_ref,promotion_receipt_ref,terminal_pin_basis_ref:OperationalRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Shard>,source_lease_projection_ref}|OperationalParticipantComplete{common_projection:ShardRestoreRegistryCommonProjection,reopen_confirm_applied_ref,independent_reopen_proof_ref,promotion_receipt_ref,terminal_pin_basis_ref:OperationalRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Shard>,source_access_closure_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure>,source_lease_projection_ref}|AbandonedAwaitingTerminalPin{common_projection:ShardRestoreRegistryCommonProjection,abandon_apply_ref,abandonment_tombstone_ref,abandonment_receipt_ref,expected_abandonment_publication_subject_commitment,pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Shard>>,source_lease_projection_ref}|AbandonedAwaitingSourceAccessClosure{common_projection:ShardRestoreRegistryCommonProjection,abandon_apply_ref,abandonment_tombstone_ref,abandonment_receipt_ref,terminal_pin_basis_ref:AbandonedRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Shard>,source_lease_projection_ref}|AbandonedParticipantComplete{common_projection:ShardRestoreRegistryCommonProjection,abandon_apply_ref,abandonment_tombstone_ref,abandonment_receipt_ref,terminal_pin_basis_ref:AbandonedRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Shard>,source_access_closure_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure>,source_lease_projection_ref}|ParticipantTerminalPendingAck{terminal_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreTerminalTombstone>,pin_release_applied_identity,authorization_identity}|ParticipantTerminal{terminal_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreTerminalTombstone>,global_completion_certificate_commitment}`. Shards never own raw source-lease or external-authority records and cannot renew/release the lease. Reopen confirmation enters OperationalAwaitingSourceAccessClosure. After the AbandonedAwaitingTerminalPin root is durable, recovery constructs its `CanonicalPublicationEvidence<Shard>` externally; `ShardRestoreAbandonmentPinInstallSpec` consumes it and the exact pending owner, installs the abandoned basis, and enters AbandonedAwaitingSourceAccessClosure. Exact `ShardRestoreSourceAccessCloseSpec` consumes either awaiting-closure arm, current certified lease/grant projection, the complete per-shard source-access inventory, and a zero-live-guard quiescence proof; it creates `ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure`, advances the close fence, and alone enters the matching ParticipantComplete arm. Only then may the Shard create operational/abandon acknowledgement. `ShardRestoreTerminalPinReleaseSpec` begins the later registered pin-ledger release from a participant-complete arm using its exact matching basis; it cannot bypass storage-domain release acknowledgements. The pin-release ack is created only after the PendingAck root is durable and is never embedded in that root.

Initial construction is `claimed target and reservations -> prebootstrap Phase-A/refinement dispatch journal -> canonical working-set copies -> RestoreProjectionBuildGuard<Role> -> transform plan -> projection result -> exact bridge recipes -> RestoreBridgeFreezeSpec -> BridgeFrozenForCanonicalCreation -> zero or more bounded recovery-incarnation refresh members with candidate rebuilds -> RestoreFirstRootPublicationFreezeSpec -> FirstRootPublicationFrozen -> final sealed journal import/latest canonical lease/RestoreSourceAcquisitionPlanImportRecord<Role>/RestoreSourceAcquisitionBundle<Role> -> role-valid creation guard -> reconciliation root -> initial hidden RestoreState -> initial role-valid RestoreRegistry -> RecoveryBridgeSpec<Role>`. Neither initial object names the bridge, command record, payload, state root, manifest, or RootSlot. No source read or source/provider operation is possible after bridge freeze; no external action at all is possible after the first-root publication freeze.

Source-lease renewal uses the universal four-stage protocol. `RestoreSourceLeaseRenewAuthorizeSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,expected_registry_basis_and_phase,current_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role>>,live_renew_eligibility:{current_lease_authority_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,current_archive_grant_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveGrantAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,required_grant_observation_action:RestoreSourceLeaseRenew{lease_identity,expected_lease_generation},lease_usable_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,grant_usable_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>},current_descendant_grant_lineage_proof_ref:StrongRef<PortableArchiveGrantLineageProof>,requested_successor_window_and_profile,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_renew_action_body_recipe,terminal_audit_gate}` and names no successor. Both challenged observations must select the exact current lease/grant heads, subject set, holder, action, and requested successor basis; a lineage proof without a fresh current grant observation cannot authorize renewal. Apply creates `RestoreSourceLeaseRenewOperationRecord<Role> {authority_bound_header,restore_id,lease_identity,expected_generation_and_portable_lease_state_digest,expected_grant_generation_and_digest,requested_successor_window_and_profile,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_action_body_recipe_and_digest,resume_phase_commitment,authorization_and_live_eligibility_commitment,state:AuthorizedUndispatchedRecipe,body_digest}` and selects RenewAuthorizedUndispatched.

After that candidate is audit-visible, dispatch initializes `RestoreArchiveLeaseRenew`. Initial send/exact retry requires current Live evidence. Once SentUnknown, an Applied/AlreadyApplied receipt may import after local expiry; signed NotRegistered after expiry creates a permanent no-effect operation tombstone and forbids resend. `RestoreSourceLeaseRenewFinalizeSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` consumes the operation record, typed renew receipt, portable terminal evidence, action-typed terminal import, fresh grant observation/import lineage, `LeaseWindowSuccessorProof`, and signed lease-lineage proof, then constructs generation `g+1` with the returned portable lease-state digest, appends an Applied terminal record, and returns to the frozen phase with Current. `RestoreSourceLeaseRenewNoEffectFinalizeSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` consumes the typed refusal/conflict/expired-NotRegistered tombstone and action-typed terminal evidence/import, returns to the same phase with the old Current record, and appends the exact NoEffect terminal record. A registered never-sent finalize similarly restores the frozen phase and appends NeverSent. Another renewal or release is illegal while resolution is unknown.

Role exactness is structural. `RestoreTerminalCleanupAuthority<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the nonwidenable `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,restore_epoch,target_identity_incarnation_and_service_epoch,terminal_disposition:Operational|Abandoned,originating_completion_or_abandon_authority_commitment,current_configuration_writer_fence_and_publication_equality_recipe,allowed_effects:SourceAccessCleanup|CloseSourceLeaseRenewal|ReleaseSourceLease|ReleaseTerminalPins|CompactRestoreEvidence,no_ordinary_query_write_service_configuration_or_authority_effects:true,authority_generation,body_digest}`. Every cleanup command rereads the current role root/configuration/fence and consumes only the exact allowed effect; certificates, proofs, or acknowledgements without this current authority cannot authorize mutation.

`LocalRestoreCompletionEvidence` is the closed union `Operational{cleanup_authority_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalCleanupAuthority<Local>>,restore_id,completion_applied_ref,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local>>,promotion_receipt_ref,independent_reopen_proof_ref,terminal_pin_basis_ref:OperationalRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Local>}|Abandoned{cleanup_authority_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalCleanupAuthority<Local>>,restore_id,abandon_finalize_applied_ref,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local>>,abandonment_receipt_ref,tombstone_ref,no_target_observation_proof_ref,terminal_pin_basis_ref:AbandonedRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Local>}`. `MetaRestoreCompletionEvidence` is `Operational{cleanup_authority_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalCleanupAuthority<Meta>>,restore_id,completion_applied_ref,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,promotion_receipt_ref,meta_independent_reopen_proof_ref,terminal_pin_basis_ref:OperationalRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Meta>,exact_sorted_operational_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardOperationalAck>],exact_sorted_shard_source_access_closure_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure>],complete_required_group_commitment}|Abandoned{cleanup_authority_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalCleanupAuthority<Meta>>,restore_id,abandon_finalize_applied_ref,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,abandonment_receipt_ref,meta_tombstone_ref,terminal_pin_basis_ref:AbandonedRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Meta>,exact_sorted_shard_abandon_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardAbandonAck>],exact_sorted_shard_source_access_closure_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure>],complete_required_group_commitment}`. `RestoreCompletionEvidenceRef<Local> = StrongRef<LocalRestoreCompletionEvidence>` and `RestoreCompletionEvidenceRef<Meta> = StrongRef<MetaRestoreCompletionEvidence>`; the Shard specialization is uninhabited. Each is built only after the named completion/abandon candidate's audit visibility advance and contains no source-lease strong edge. Cross-role or cross-disposition evidence cannot decode or satisfy a release transition. Release additionally requires current cleanup authority, the matching role-exact source-access cleanup record, and terminal pin basis.

`PortableRestoreSourceLeaseAuthorityObservation` is the reference-free signed `{lease_identity,current_generation,current_portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,head_state:Active|Released,window_class:Usable|Expired,current_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,zero_successor_generation_commitment,archive_counter,action_binding:Renew{canonical_action_body_digest}|Release{canonical_action_body_digest}|Status{canonical_status_query_digest},challenge_nonce,time_authority_observation_digest,time_authority_profile_oid,time_observation_interval:AuthorityInterval,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. Expired is a window classification, never a fabricated external head state; action binding is mandatory and nonoptional. `RestoreSourceLeaseAuthorityObservationImport<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,role_and_group,current_configuration_writer_fence_and_publication_commitment,current_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role>>,portable_observation_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreSourceLeaseAuthorityObservation>,time_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityObservationImport>,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,exact_current_local_record_portable_state_head_time_and_action_transcript_match_digest,imported_at_maintenance_identity}`. `RestoreSourceLeaseNoSuccessorProof` is the portable signed `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=RestoreArchiveLeaseRelease},canonical_full_request_digest,status_query_lineage_digest,archive_authority_id,lease_identity,current_head_bytes_and_digest_and_cas_version,latest_generation,latest_portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,zero_successor_through_authority_counter,holder_and_subject_set_commitment,intended_action:Release,challenge_nonce,time_authority_observation_digest,time_authority_profile_oid,time_observation_interval,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`.

`RestoreLeaseReleaseEligibility<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `Live{latest_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role>>,current_lease_authority_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,usable_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>}|Expired{latest_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role>>,expired_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,portable_expiry_attestation_ref:StrongRef<PortableTimeExpiryAttestation>,current_lease_authority_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,observation_signed_zero_successor_generation_projection,exact_lease_record_observation_action_body_recipe_expiry_and_zero_successor_match_digest}`. The Expired arm is preauthorization evidence: its observation is bound to the stable release action-body recipe/digest but contains no `ExternalActionRequestBinding`, canonical full request digest, dispatch publication commitment, or later operation status. The full `RestoreSourceLeaseNoSuccessorProof` is constructible only after a release request exists and is reserved for status/terminal evidence and successful release finalize. Expired is legal only for release/status of the exact lineage and grants no archive read, reopen, renewal, or other use.

`RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,expected_registry_basis_and_phase:AwaitingSourceRelease,completion_evidence_ref:RestoreCompletionEvidenceRef<Role>,source_access_cleanup_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupRecord<Role>>,source_access_cleanup_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Role>>,canonicalized_prebootstrap_evidence_ref:CanonicalizedRestorePreBootstrapEvidenceRef<Role>,lease_identity:RestoreSourceLeaseIdentity,expected_latest_generation,expected_portable_lease_state_digest,expected_lineage_digest,release_eligibility:RestoreLeaseReleaseEligibility<Role>,operation_recipe:{operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,archive_authority_id,canonical_release_action_body:{lease_identity,release_through_generation,expected_portable_lease_state_digest,expected_lineage_digest,holder_identity,archive_authority_id,lease_bound_source_access_resource_set_commitment}},terminal_audit_gate}`. Every `LeaseBoundAutoRevoked` disposition and accumulator member must byte-match the action body and provider contract; the release receipt/terminal evidence must confirm that those access resources became unusable atomically with the lease head. The prebootstrap evidence must already be canonicalized and its journal key disposition terminal before source release can arm. Eligibility is excluded from the stable action body, so an authorized request retries byte-identically after expiry. Apply constructs immutable `RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationRecord<Role> {authority_bound_header,restore_id,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,lease_identity,expected_latest_generation,expected_portable_lease_state_digest,expected_lineage_digest,archive_authority_id,canonical_release_action_body_recipe_and_digest,completion_evidence_digest,source_access_cleanup_record_and_accumulator_digest,canonicalized_prebootstrap_evidence_commitment,authorization_and_eligibility_commitment,resume_phase_commitment,state:AuthorizedUndispatchedRecipe,body_digest}` and selects ReleaseAuthorizedUndispatched. It contains no source-spec ref, live eligibility refs, future publication evidence, full request, or receipt.

Only after that candidate's `PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local|Meta>` exists may `RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseDispatchInitializeSpec` create `IrreversibleDispatchAttempt<RestoreArchiveLeaseRelease>::Armed` and derive the full binding/request. Its specialized cleanup row accepts current Live or exact Expired eligibility. Provider status/idempotency tombstones outlive lease expiry; signed NotRegistered after expiry permits only the same release request under a fresh cleanup guard because the row selects `ExactCleanupRetry`, never a different action. The specialized `PortableRestoreSourceLeaseReleaseTerminalEvidence` is the generated action member plus `{release_receipt_bytes_and_digest,full_signed_no_successor_proof:RestoreSourceLeaseNoSuccessorProof,portable_terminal_time_evidence:PortableArchiveLeaseTerminalTimeEvidence}`. It therefore retains revalidatable signed evidence—not a digest—for every Released/AlreadyReleased/AlreadyExpired terminal and contains no Protocol ObjectId.

`PortableArchiveLeaseTerminalTimeEvidence` is the provider-signed reference-free `{archive_authority_id,lease_identity,release_operation_id,intended_action:Release,provider_time_observation:PortableTimeAuthorityObservation,lease_window_or_expiry_attestation,observation_and_attestation_match_digest,provider_time_key_epoch,signature}`; it contains no local `TimeValidationEvidence` digest. `ArchiveLeaseReleaseReceipt` is portable `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=RestoreArchiveLeaseRelease},archive_authority_id,lease_identity,prior_active_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,prior_portable_lease_state_digest,result_released_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,released_through_generation,proved_latest_generation,zero_successor_generation_commitment,prior_and_result_archive_counters,outcome:Released|AlreadyReleased|AlreadyExpired,released_at,portable_terminal_time_evidence:PortableArchiveLeaseTerminalTimeEvidence,signer_epoch,signature}`. AlreadyExpired is terminal only with the exact no-successor proof and portable provider expiry evidence; timeout, OS time, or local evidence digest is never a substitute.

`RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationSummary<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{role,terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,restore_id,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,lease_identity,released_through_generation,canonical_request_digest,status_lineage_and_terminal_evidence_commitment,receipt_and_result_head_commitment,completion_evidence_commitment,source_access_cleanup_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Role>>,lease_operation_terminal_history:RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalHistory<Role>,body_digest}`. `RestoreRegistryTerminalCommon<Role>` is `{role,restore_id,restore_epoch,mode,source_and_target_identity_commitment,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,terminal_disposition:Operational|Abandoned,terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,transform_and_projection_commitment,source_lease_lineage_commitment,lease_operation_terminal_history:RestoreLeaseOperationTerminalHistory<Role>,canonicalized_prebootstrap_evidence_ref:CanonicalizedRestorePreBootstrapEvidenceRef<Role>,source_key_access_cleanup_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Role>>,final_restore_state_audit_and_promotion_or_abandonment_commitment,body_digest}`. Detailed terminal history and the detailed prebootstrap/source-access import closures remain strongly retained through the advertised retry/status/replay floor; only a checked compaction command may replace them with their proof-capable accumulators/state. `LocalRestoreTerminalTombstone` is `{terminal_common_digest,release_operation_summary_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationSummary<Local>>,canonicalized_prebootstrap_evidence_ref:CanonicalizedRestorePreBootstrapEvidenceRef<Local>,source_key_access_cleanup_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Local>>,release_receipt_and_terminal_evidence_commitment,final_external_head_cas_no_successor_source_access_revocation_and_portable_time_commitment,local_terminal_pin_release_apply_commitment,promotion_or_abandonment_authority_terminal_commitment,replay_and_status_floor,body_digest}`. `MetaRestoreTerminalTombstone` is `{terminal_common_digest,release_operation_summary_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseOperationSummary<Meta>>,canonicalized_prebootstrap_evidence_ref:CanonicalizedRestorePreBootstrapEvidenceRef<Meta>,source_key_access_cleanup_accumulator_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceKeyAccessCleanupAccumulator<Meta>>,release_receipt_and_terminal_evidence_commitment,final_external_head_cas_no_successor_source_access_revocation_and_portable_time_commitment,meta_terminal_pin_release_apply_commitment,required_shard_set_commitment,complete_sorted_shard_pin_release_ack_commitment,promotion_or_abandonment_authority_terminal_commitment,replay_and_status_floor,body_digest}`. Neither contains live authority, lease, transform/projection, completion evidence, operation spec/record, seal/proof, Protocol attempt, or optional cross-role fields; the canonicalized evidence ref is compact proof authority, not a live journal.

`RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseFinalizeSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` consumes the operation record, portable terminal evidence, typed release receipt, action-typed terminal import, exact no-successor proof, current release-authorized registry, and exact completion commitment. Apply verifies the direct external successor, constructs the compact operation summary/terminal common/role-specific tombstone recipe, and removes the canonical lease edge exactly when the candidate becomes audit-visible. `RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseNoEffectFinalizeSpec<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the distinct refusal/conflict path: it consumes the action-typed zero-effect terminal artifact/evidence/import, proves the archive head remains the exact Active predecessor, returns to `AwaitingSourceRelease + Current`, and inserts a permanent release-operation tombstone; it never releases a pin or pretends the source lease ended. A fresh release requires a new operation ID and current eligibility.

Successful Local finalize releases the exact matching `RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Local>` pin and enters ReleasedTerminal; an Operational terminal consumes its promotion-seal basis, while an Abandoned terminal consumes its abandonment-publication/inventory basis. Successful Meta finalize instead enters `SourceReleasedAwaitingPinReleaseAuthorizationPublication`: it retains its exact terminal pin basis, freezes the exact required Shard set, and commits only `RestoreTerminalPinReleaseAuthorizationBody {restore_id,restore_epoch,terminal_disposition:Operational|Abandoned,target_identity_incarnation_and_service,release_finalize_applied_identity,release_operation_request_receipt_terminal_evidence_and_no_successor_commitment,required_shard_set_and_each_expected_terminalizing_projection_commitment,meta_terminal_pin_basis_commitment,authorization_generation}`. After that Meta root and audit visibility are durable, a certificate attempt creates `RestoreTerminalPinReleaseAuthorization {SameGroupCertificateHeader,body:RestoreTerminalPinReleaseAuthorizationBody,post_release_meta_state_root_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStateRoot>,root_slot_durability_digest,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`. `GlobalRestorePinReleaseAuthorizationInstallSpec` consumes that already-created certificate, byte-matches the frozen body recipe, and alone enters `SourceReleasedAwaitingParticipantPinRelease`; no future certificate identity is embedded in the release-finalize predecessor.

`ShardRestoreTerminalPinReleaseSpec` is the exact `{authorization_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinReleaseAuthorization>,expected_registry_state:OperationalParticipantComplete|AbandonedParticipantComplete,expected_terminalizing_projection,own_terminal_pin_basis_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Shard>>,source_lease_projection_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjection>,current_root_no_other_pin_owner_proof_ref,post_release_tombstone_body_recipe}`. The authorization disposition, registry arm, terminal projection, and pin-basis arm must all match. Apply verifies its Shard is exactly one authorization member, drops only its own seal-or-abandonment-basis/pin/projection edges, creates `ShardRestoreTerminalTombstone`, and enters `ParticipantTerminalPendingAck`; it cannot name the future acknowledgement. Only after that root is RootSlot-durable may a certificate attempt create `ShardRestoreTerminalPinReleaseAck {SameGroupCertificateHeader,authorization_identity,restore_id,restore_epoch,terminal_disposition,shard_id,terminal_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreTerminalTombstone>,pin_release_applied_identity,post_release_shard_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,root_slot_durability_digest,current_root_no_pin_proof_ref,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`.

`GlobalRestoreParticipantPinReleaseFinalizeSpec` consumes the installed authorization and every-and-only required certified `ShardRestoreTerminalPinReleaseAck`, verifies their disposition/tombstone/root/no-pin subjects and the frozen bijection, releases the matching Meta terminal pin basis, builds `MetaRestoreTerminalTombstone`, and alone selects Meta ReleasedTerminal. Only after that finalize root and its audit visibility are durable may a certificate attempt create `GlobalRestoreParticipantPinReleaseCompletionCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,restore_epoch,terminal_disposition,pin_release_authorization_identity,participant_pin_release_finalize_applied_identity,exact_sorted_ack_identities,meta_terminal_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<MetaRestoreTerminalTombstone>,post_finalize_global_state_root_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStateRoot>,meta_pin_no_owner_proof_ref,root_slot_durability_digest,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`. Each Shard orders exact `ShardRestoreParticipantTerminalConfirmSpec {completion_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalRestoreParticipantPinReleaseCompletionCertificate>,expected_registry_state:ParticipantTerminalPendingAck,own_terminal_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreTerminalTombstone>,own_pin_release_applied_identity,expected_authorization_identity,expected_terminal_disposition}` and changes only to compact ParticipantTerminal carrying the certificate's portable commitment; it adds no acknowledgement back-edge and cannot substitute another restore, authorization, disposition, shard set, or Meta terminal. Heavy prior inputs remain rooted until their respective visibility/pin-ack/final-projection floors close, then compact terminal state cuts them. Conflicting operation, inventory, holder, authority, generation, transcript, disposition, or shard set fails closed.

`RestoreNoTargetObservationProof<Role>` is generated from the exact role schema and binds `{restore_id,restore_epoch,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,role_and_group,current_hidden_state_root_identity,current_root_slot_generations,zero_ordinary_writer_lease_proof,zero_READY_handshake_session_query_transaction_result_delivery_and_capability_issuance_proof,no_service_promotion_receipt_proof,profile_specific_catalog_or_zero_catalog_observation,complete_endpoint_set,service_admission_log_commitment,audit_commitment,checker_version,signatures}`. Sharded abandonment requires a Meta proof and one certified proof from every configured shard.
