`CatalogAbandonPredecessor` is the exact portable closed union `AuthenticatedAbsent{target_row_key,authenticated_empty_map_proof,expected_catalog_map_root_digest,row_nonreuse_floor}|ReservedHidden{target_row_head_bytes_digest_and_version,reservation_id_and_digest,admission_hidden_proof}|ActivatedHidden{target_row_head_bytes_digest_and_version,reservation_id_and_activation_digest,composite_receipt_required_proof}`. The catalog tombstone action may install or idempotently join a `CatalogAuthorityHead::TombstonedNoEndpoint` successor from any exact arm and permanently advances the row nonreuse floor; it may not infer absence from a lookup miss. Thus abandonment remains legal before `CatalogReserveHidden`, after reservation, or after hidden activation.

The cleanup-only action kind `CatalogTombstoneRestoreTarget` has two distinct pre-order bodies. `PreBootstrapCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetActionBody` is `{abandonment_authority_seed:RestoreAbandonmentAuthoritySeed,catalog_profile:ExternalCasCataloged|DirectoryBoundCataloged,predecessor:CatalogAbandonPredecessor,prebootstrap_abandonment_spec_and_zero_publication_commitment,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest}`. `CanonicalCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetActionBody<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` has the same seed/profile/predecessor/operation fields but requires the role's authority-binding public commitment and originating abandonment-operation public commitment. Neither body contains cleanup recipe digests, an abandonment-manifest digest, future skeleton/tombstone body, result head, receipt, or publication evidence.

The universal registry emits `PreBootstrapCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Profile>` with `origin_plane=RestorePreBootstrap` and source gate `PreBootstrapAbandonmentCleanupGate`, plus `CanonicalCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Role,Profile>` with `origin_plane=CanonicalProtocol` and the audit-visible `RestoreAbandonOperationRecord<Role>` candidate source gate. Their operation namespaces, request bindings, attempts, evidence, and imports are disjoint. `CatalogAuthorityHead` is the reference-free CAS-linear `{target_row_key,predecessor:Absent{authenticated_catalog_map_root_digest_and_version,row_nonreuse_floor}|Prior{head_digest,cas_version},cas_version,row_nonreuse_floor,state:ReservedHidden|ActivatedHidden|TombstonedNoEndpoint{restore_id,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,catalog_profile,origin_plane,abandonment_authority_seed_digest,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,admission_visible:false,endpoint_set_empty:true,no_reactivation_or_same_incarnation_reservation:true},authority_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`; no state arm contains its own head digest or result CAS version, and absent creation never fabricates predecessor-head bytes. The authority performs one linearizable direct transition and returns exact `CatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetReceipt<Contract> {external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{dispatch_contract_id=Contract},predecessor:CatalogAbandonPredecessor,result_head_complete_bytes,result_head_digest_and_cas_version,action_body_digest,authority_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. Already-applied is equivalent only when every field, plane, seed, contract, complete returned head byte, and result version match; refusal/conflict proves zero effect and cannot authorize the next abandonment stage.

`PreBootstrapCatalogRestoreTargetTerminalDisposition` is `Cataloged{contract_id:PreBootstrapCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Profile>,action_body_digest,terminal_import_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PreBootstrapExternalTerminalImportArtifact<PreBootstrapCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Profile>>>,portable_terminal_evidence_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<PreBootstrapCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Profile>>>,receipt_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<CatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetReceipt<PreBootstrapCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Profile>>>,result_catalog_authority_head_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<CatalogAuthorityHead>,status_and_nonreuse_accumulator_commitment}|EmbeddedNoCatalog{zero_catalog_applicability_proof}`. `CanonicalCatalogRestoreTargetTerminalDisposition<Role,Profile>` is `Cataloged{contract_id:CanonicalCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Role,Profile>,action_body_digest,terminal_import_ref:StrongRef<ExternalTerminalImportRecord<CanonicalCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Role,Profile>,Role>>,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<CanonicalCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Role,Profile>>>,receipt_ref:StrongRef<CatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetReceipt<CanonicalCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Role,Profile>>>,result_catalog_authority_head_ref:StrongRef<CatalogAuthorityHead>,status_and_nonreuse_commitment}|EmbeddedNoCatalog{zero_catalog_applicability_proof}`. The referenced head must decode the exact `TombstonedNoEndpoint` arm and byte-match the receipt. No-root continuity/DirectoryBound closure, journal-key destruction authorization, and the no-root terminal certificate require the prebootstrap type; rooted finalize requires the canonical type. EmbeddedNoCatalog cannot encode a placeholder catalog result. Catalog evidence and its result head remain rooted through their declared status/nonreuse floor, and no plane can consume the other's disposition.

`CanonicalCatalogedTargetCleanupRecipe<Role:AuthorityOwningRole,Profile>` is the pre-order `{catalog_predecessor:CatalogAbandonPredecessor,contract_id:CanonicalCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetContract<Role,Profile>,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_action_body:CanonicalCatalogTombstoneRestoreTargetActionBody<Role>,required_terminal_head_arm:TombstonedNoEndpoint,required_receipt_evidence_and_import_schema_ids,recipe_digest}` and contains no future result. `RestoreAbandonAuthorityProfile<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the generated exact union `ExternalCasCataloged{catalog_cleanup_recipe:CanonicalCatalogedTargetCleanupRecipe<Role,ExternalCasCataloged>,expected_continuity_head_commitment,continuity_cleanup_recipe:CanonicalAbandonByCas{predecessor:RestoreAbandonableContinuityPredecessor,contract_id:CanonicalRestoreContinuityAbandonContract<Role>,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_action_body_recipe}}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{creation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DirectoryBoundCreationEvidence<Local>>,fixed_local_continuity_digest,catalog_cleanup_recipe:CanonicalCatalogedTargetCleanupRecipe<Local,DirectoryBoundCataloged>,expected_local_state_root_and_slot,local_abandon_publication_recipe}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{creation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DirectoryBoundCreationEvidence<Local>>,fixed_local_continuity_digest,zero_catalog_applicability_proof,expected_local_state_root_and_slot,local_abandon_publication_recipe}`. The generated family exposes the two DirectoryBound arms only for `Role=Local`; `Role=Meta` is statically ExternalCasCataloged. Because a canonical restore root already proves a reserved/refined continuity state, no canonical profile has a no-mutation/absent-continuity arm.

`RestoreAbandonSpec` is the closed `Local{restore_id,expected_hidden_state,no_target_observation_proof_ref:StrongRef<RestoreNoTargetObservationProof<Local>>,authority_profile:RestoreAbandonAuthorityProfile<Local>,abandonment_pin_pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Local>>,abandonment_manifest_body_recipe,source_lease_disposition:ReleaseRequired,terminal_audit_gate}|ShardedMeta{restore_id,expected_hidden_meta_state,meta_no_target_observation_proof_ref:StrongRef<RestoreNoTargetObservationProof<Meta>>,exact_sorted_shard_no_observation_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreNoTargetObservationProof<Shard>>],complete_group_commitment,authority_profile:RestoreAbandonAuthorityProfile<Meta>,meta_abandonment_pin_pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Meta>>,sorted_shard_abandonment_pin_pending_owner_recipes[RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwnerRecipe<Shard>],complete_shard_recipe_group_and_no_observation_bijection,abandonment_manifest_body_recipe,source_lease_disposition:ReleaseRequired,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply remains hidden and enters AbandonAuthorizedHidden.

`RestoreAbandonOperationRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the generated role/profile-tagged immutable record `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,restore_epoch,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,originating_abandon_spec_ref:RestoreAbandonSpecRef<Role>,expected_hidden_restore_state_and_registry_generation,exact_no_target_observation_set_and_group_commitment,selected_authority_profile:RestoreAbandonAuthorityProfile<Role>,abandonment_authority_seed:RestoreAbandonmentAuthoritySeed,profile_operation_plan:ExternalCasCataloged{canonical_catalog_contract_operation_and_action_body_recipe,canonical_continuity_contract_operation_and_action_body_recipe,required_direct_predecessor_relation}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{canonical_catalog_contract_operation_and_action_body_recipe,creation_evidence_and_inode_lineage_commitment,local_apply_identity_recipe_and_expected_slot}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{zero_catalog_applicability_proof,creation_evidence_and_inode_lineage_commitment,local_apply_identity_recipe_and_expected_slot},source_lease_identity_and_minimum_observed_lineage_floor,source_lease_disposition:ReleaseLatestLineageRequired,pin_owner_plan:Local{pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Local>>}|Meta{meta_pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Meta>>,sorted_shard_pending_owner_recipes[RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwnerRecipe<Shard>],complete_shard_recipe_group_and_no_observation_bijection},authorized_hidden_generation,expected_abandonment_publication_subject_recipe,terminal_audit_gate_commitment,body_digest}`. `RestoreAbandonSpecRef<Local>` and `<Meta>` are generated tag-refined references to only the matching outer spec arm. The `Local` pin-plan arm is inhabitable only for `Role=Local`, the `Meta` arm only for `Role=Meta`, and the generated validator rejects every role/profile mismatch. Legal renewal during receipt/pin/cleanup recovery may advance the lease beyond the minimum floor; terminal cleanup closes renewal and releases the then-latest lineage. The record is constructed in the AbandonAuthorizedHidden candidate before any catalog/continuity send or DirectoryBound apply. It contains no future request binding, receipt, terminal evidence, returned authority head, publication evidence, result root, pin, acknowledgement, or certificate.

`AuthorityOwningRestoreAbandonmentTombstone<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the generated role/profile-tagged immutable no-resurrection body `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,skeleton_ref:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRef<Role>,materialization_proof:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneMaterializationProof<Role>,restore_id,restore_epoch,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,role_and_group,abandonment_authority_seed,abandon_operation_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonOperationRecord<Role>>,abandon_applied_identity,expected_role_valid_publication_subject_recipe,profile_applied_basis:ExternalCasCataloged{canonical_catalog_tombstone_terminal_import_ref,canonical_continuity_abandon_terminal_import_ref,returned_terminal_authority_head_commitment}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{canonical_catalog_tombstone_terminal_import_ref,creation_evidence_and_exact_target_manifest_inode_lineage,directory_bound_abandon_apply_identity,actual_result_root_slot_and_receipt_import_commitment}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{zero_catalog_applicability_proof,creation_evidence_and_exact_target_manifest_inode_lineage,directory_bound_abandon_apply_identity,actual_result_root_slot_and_receipt_import_commitment},permanent_no_service_and_no_same_incarnation_operational_successor_proof,higher_incarnation_only_rule,pending_pin_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Role>>,source_access_cleanup_and_release_latest_lease_obligation_commitment,replay_status_and_nonreuse_floor,body_digest}`. The DirectoryBound arms are materialized only by the later receipt-import command, after the apply root/slot and member receipt exist; the earlier apply result contains only skeleton/pending owner/recipes.

`ShardRestoreAbandonmentTombstone` is the distinct `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Shard>,skeleton_ref:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRef<Shard>,materialization_proof:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneMaterializationProof<Shard>,restore_id,restore_epoch,shard_id,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,abandonment_authority_seed,participant_apply_authorization_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalRestoreAbandonParticipantApplyCertificate>,own_entry_ordinal,source_meta_abandon_operation_public_commitment,own_no_target_observation_proof_ref:StrongRef<RestoreNoTargetObservationProof<Shard>>,shard_abandon_apply_identity,certified_manifest_receipt_and_profile_terminal_commitments,pending_pin_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Shard>>,permanent_no_service_and_no_same_incarnation_successor_proof,higher_incarnation_only_rule,source_access_cleanup_projection_and_replay_floor,body_digest}`. It has no `RestoreAbandonOperationRecord<Shard>`, raw Meta strong ref, or canonical external import owned by Meta. `RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneRef<Role>` is the generated exact family `Local{ref:StrongRef<AuthorityOwningRestoreAbandonmentTombstone<Local>>}|Meta{ref:StrongRef<AuthorityOwningRestoreAbandonmentTombstone<Meta>>}|Shard{ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreAbandonmentTombstone>}`.

Every repeated field and filled hole must byte-match its skeleton/materialization proof; a tombstone identity is computed only after those inputs exist and is never part of a predecessor recipe. Both tombstone forms own protected evidence and therefore retain a full authority binding until checked compaction creates a separate payload-free digest tombstone. Later publication evidence, installed terminal pin basis, acknowledgements, and source-release terminal live in their own later objects and byte-match the precommitted seed/recipes. This split prevents tombstone/root/receipt/pin cycles while preserving permanent nonresurrection evidence.

`RestoreAbandonAuthorityProfileProjection<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the generated reference-free closed union `ExternalCasCataloged{abandonment_authority_seed,canonical_catalog_cleanup_projection,expected_continuity_head_commitment,canonical_continuity_abandon_predecessor_and_action_body_projection}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{abandonment_authority_seed,creation_evidence_public_commitment,fixed_local_continuity_digest,canonical_catalog_cleanup_projection,expected_local_state_root_and_assigned_slot_recipe_public_commitment,local_abandon_publication_recipe_digest}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{abandonment_authority_seed,creation_evidence_public_commitment,fixed_local_continuity_digest,zero_catalog_applicability_proof_digest,expected_local_state_root_and_assigned_slot_recipe_public_commitment,local_abandon_publication_recipe_digest}`; DirectoryBound arms exist only for Local. After its audit visibility advance, `RestoreAbandonmentManifest` is the reference-free signed closed union with common `{restore_id,restore_epoch,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,abandonment_authority_seed,abandon_operation_record_public_commitment,authority_profile_projection,source_lease_identity_and_disposition:ReleaseLatestLineageRequired,profile_specific_predecessor_and_operation_action_body_commitments,permanent_no_service_and_higher_incarnation_only_rule,signer_set_epoch,signatures,body_digest}` and exactly one posture body: `Local{target_tombstone_skeleton_recipe,no_target_observation_proof_public_commitment,local_pending_owner_public_commitment}|Sharded{meta_tombstone_skeleton_recipe,meta_no_target_observation_proof_public_commitment,required_group_and_topology_commitment,meta_pending_owner_public_commitment,sorted_shard_entries[{shard_id,no_target_observation_proof_public_commitment,tombstone_skeleton_recipe:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRecipe<Shard>,pending_owner_recipe:RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwnerRecipe<Shard>,recipe_availability_and_retention_certificate}],complete_entry_bijection}`. It contains complete portable recipes/projections rather than local refs, so every Shard can materialize its skeleton without a raw Meta object. The manifest commits action bodies that bind only the earlier seed; neither action body points back to the manifest. `ExternalCasRestoreAbandonmentReceiptRef` is the generated tag-refined strong reference to only `RestoreAbandonmentReceipt::ExternalCasCataloged`. The sequences are noninterchangeable:

- `ExternalCasCataloged`: canonical cleanup first installs or joins the exact target-epoch `CatalogAuthorityHead::TombstonedNoEndpoint` successor without touching any older operational endpoint, then dispatches only `CanonicalRestoreContinuityAbandonContract<Role>` and changes the exact registered `RestoreAbandonableContinuityPredecessor` to `RestoreAbandoned{restore_id,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,abandonment_basis:CanonicalRooted{abandonment_authority_seed_digest,target_tombstone_skeleton_digest,prior_lease_closure_disposition},retired_through_incarnation,no_operational_successor_at_this_incarnation}`. The no-mutation dispositions exist only in the no-root prebootstrap cleanup path.
- `DirectoryBoundCataloged`: the canonical catalog tombstone is installed first. Ordered `DirectoryBoundAbandonApplySpec {authority_bound_header,restore_id,abandon_operation_record_ref,skeleton_ref,pending_owner_ref,catalog_terminal_disposition,current_creation_evidence_ref,live_target_manifest_inode_lease_and_publication_sequencer,fixed_local_continuity_digest,exact_predecessor_root_and_slot,assigned_result_slot_generation,expected_result_payload_manifest_and_receipt_body_recipes_excluding_all_result_and_tombstone_digests,terminal_audit_gate}` then clears `restore_state_ref`, selects `RecoveryAbandoned`, and enters `AbandonedAuthorityAppliedAwaitingReceipt` under the same inode fence. That result contains only its assigned apply identity, skeleton, pending owner, current source-lease owner, and the acyclic recipes—**no tombstone, actual result-root/slot digest, or receipt**. Only after that RootSlot is durable may a member issue `DirectoryBoundAbandonmentReceipt {FenceBoundIrreversibleReceiptHeader,restore_id,abandon_apply_identity,prior_root_and_slot,result_root_complete_bytes_digest_and_slot_generation,catalog_tombstone_terminal_commitment,creation_evidence_and_fixed_continuity_commitment,no_service_and_permanent_no_operational_successor_proof,receipt_body_recipe_match_digest}`. Ordered `DirectoryBoundAbandonReceiptImportSpec` consumes that receipt, the awaiting-receipt root, skeleton, pending owner, and exact result publication evidence; it fills the DirectoryBound holes, materializes `AuthorityOwningRestoreAbandonmentTombstone<Local>`, and enters `AbandonedAwaitingTerminalPin`. Its tombstone names the earlier apply result/receipt, never the receipt-import result that contains it.
- `DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog`: the same local apply/receipt sequence runs with the zero-catalog-applicability proof and no catalog operation or placeholder version.

Sharded abandonment has an explicit Meta-to-Shard authority bridge; a Shard never acts from prose, a raw external receipt, or a digest-only projection. After the ExternalCas catalog/continuity terminal imports and `RestoreAbandonmentReceipt::ExternalCasCataloged` are durable, Meta orders `GlobalRestoreAbandonParticipantApplyAuthorizeSpec {restore_id,restore_epoch,abandon_operation_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonOperationRecord<Meta>>,abandonment_manifest_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentManifest>,external_abandonment_receipt_ref:ExternalCasRestoreAbandonmentReceiptRef,meta_no_target_observation_proof_ref,exact_sorted_shard_no_observation_refs,expected_source_meta_prefix_and_configuration,required_shard_set_and_topology_commitment,meta_pending_owner_ref,sorted_shard_recipe_entries[{shard_id,tombstone_skeleton_recipe:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRecipe<Shard>,pending_owner_recipe:RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwnerRecipe<Shard>,recipe_availability_and_retention_certificate}],complete_proof_recipe_shard_and_group_bijection,terminal_audit_gate}`. Apply remains hidden, freezes that exact participant set and complete recipe map, and contains no future certificate. Only after its audit visibility advance may a certificate attempt create `GlobalRestoreAbandonParticipantApplyCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,restore_epoch,authorization_applied_identity,abandon_operation_record_identity,abandonment_manifest_identity_and_digest,external_receipt_identity_and_digest,source_meta_prefix_and_configuration,required_shard_set_and_topology_commitment,sorted_shard_entries[{shard_id,no_target_observation_proof_identity,tombstone_skeleton_recipe,pending_owner_recipe,recipe_availability_and_retention_certificate}],complete_entry_bijection,post_authorization_global_state_root_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStateRoot>,root_slot_durability_digest,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`.

Each Shard locally expands and verifies only its certified recipe bytes, then orders `ShardRestoreAbandonApplySpec {authorization_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalRestoreAbandonParticipantApplyCertificate>,own_entry_ordinal,own_no_target_observation_proof_ref:StrongRef<RestoreNoTargetObservationProof<Shard>>,current_restore_state_ref:StrongRef<RestoreState>,current_registry_ref:StrongRef<RestoreRegistry>,current_source_lease_projection_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjection>,constructed_tombstone_skeleton_ref:RestoreAbandonmentTombstoneSkeletonRef<Shard>,constructed_pending_owner_ref:StrongRef<RestoreAbandonmentPinPendingOwner<Shard>>,complete_recipe_expansion_availability_and_entry_match_proof,expected_source_meta_prefix_configuration_and_topology}` under the matching `ShardControlAuthority::MetaAuthorized` arm. Apply clears `restore_state_ref`, selects RecoveryAbandoned, materializes the role-correct Shard tombstone from the certified basis, installs `RestoreRetentionAnchor::AbandonmentPinPending`, and enters `AbandonedAwaitingTerminalPin`; it contains no future publication evidence, pin, or acknowledgement. After the role-valid abandonment-pin install and the separate Shard source-access close transition, the Shard may create exact `RestoreShardAbandonAck {SameGroupCertificateHeader,authorization_certificate_identity,restore_id,restore_epoch,shard_id,abandon_apply_applied_identity,abandonment_pin_install_applied_identity,source_access_close_applied_identity,abandonment_tombstone_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreAbandonmentTombstone>,abandoned_terminal_pin_basis_ref:AbandonedRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Shard>,source_access_closure_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure>,terminalizing_projection_commitment,post_close_shard_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,root_slot_durability_digest,source_lease_projection_identity_generation_and_digest,no_service_and_no_same_incarnation_successor_commitment,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`. `GlobalRestoreAbandonFinalizeSpec` consumes the same authorization, manifest, external receipt, Meta pending owner, and every-and-only required certified Shard ack; missing, duplicate, wrong-configuration, wrong-recipe, wrong-disposition, missing source-access closure, or wrong-restore acknowledgements remain blocking. It then creates the Meta abandonment tombstone and enters Meta `AbandonedAwaitingTerminalPin`. This order ensures no Meta terminal can discard the participant recipe/proof/closure before all Shards have durably accepted it.

`RestoreAbandonmentReceipt` is the portable closed union `ExternalCasCataloged{catalog_tombstone_lineage,continuity_abandon_lineage,complete_profile_stage_bijection}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{catalog_tombstone_lineage,directory_bound_abandonment_receipt_bytes_and_digest,complete_profile_stage_bijection}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{directory_bound_abandonment_receipt_bytes_and_digest,zero_catalog_applicability_proof_digest,complete_profile_stage_bijection}`. A DirectoryBound local apply contains only skeleton/pending-owner/result/receipt recipes, never its future tombstone, receipt, or result digest. Reversing a cataloged profile's order is illegal because it could leave an unowned endpoint reservation. For ExternalCas, `LocalRestoreAbandonFinalizeSpec` consumes the exact canonical profile evidence, clears `restore_state_ref`, selects `RecoveryAbandoned`, materializes the Local tombstone, and enters `AbandonedAwaitingTerminalPin`; for DirectoryBound, `DirectoryBoundAbandonReceiptImportSpec` is the sole tombstone materializer and transition from `AbandonedAuthorityAppliedAwaitingReceipt`. `ShardRestoreAbandonApplySpec` and `GlobalRestoreAbandonFinalizeSpec` likewise end in their role-specific `AbandonedAwaitingTerminalPin`, never directly in participant-complete or cleanup. After each such root is durable, the role constructs publication evidence, inventory, pin-ledger activation evidence, and basis; the role-specific abandonment-pin install enters AwaitingSourceAccessCleanup or the Shard awaiting-source-closure state. Only after exact Shard source-access closure may it create an abandon ack; only after Local/Meta progress generation zero may cleanup begin. RecoveryAbandoned also admits exact prebootstrap-evidence canonicalization, re-encryption/journal-key destruction, detailed-evidence cut, receipt recovery, abandonment-pin installation, source-access cleanup, lease renewal/close/release/finalize, participant pin release, and registered tombstone compaction under RecoveryCommitCx. It rejects ordinary open, service, sessions, capabilities, user work, unrelated maintenance, reconfiguration, and resurrection. These evidence-maintenance successors are included in the seal/allowed-successor registry so a delayed journal-key cut cannot deadlock release. A later same-identity restore must reserve a strictly higher incarnation.

`RecoveryTransformSourceBasis<Role>` is the generated role family `Local{working_set_ref:StrongRef<RestoreCanonicalAcquisitionWorkingSet<Local>>,stable_lease_identity,construction_observed_lease_generation_and_portable_state_digest,live_prebootstrap_dispatch_root_commitment}|Meta{working_set_ref:StrongRef<RestoreCanonicalAcquisitionWorkingSet<Meta>>,stable_lease_identity,construction_observed_lease_generation_and_portable_state_digest,live_prebootstrap_dispatch_root_commitment}|Shard{certified_bootstrap_projection_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardBootstrapProjectionCertificate>,stable_lease_identity,construction_observed_projection_generation_and_portable_state_digest,exact_archive_inventory_and_key_recipient_subset}`. The observed generation is provenance for plan construction, not a permanent read capability: Local/Meta source opens always resolve the current `PreBootstrapRestoreSourceLeaseCurrent` under `RestoreProjectionBuildGuard`, while Shard opens always resolve the current certified projection. A later renewal does not invalidate the immutable plan if identity/holder/subject/inventory commitments are unchanged; it changes the current use guard and is captured by bridge freeze. `PendingRestoreCreationGuard` alone carries the exact final canonical lease generation/state imported from that freeze.

`RecoveryIncarnationTransformPlan<Role>` is the pre-order object `{restore_id,mode:RecoverSameIdentity|CloneNewIdentity,source_basis:RecoveryTransformSourceBasis<Role>,working_acquisition_or_certified_bootstrap_lineage_commitment,identity_key_disposition_commitment,source_manifest_identity_and_digest,source_recovery_projection_identity_and_digest,restore_source_lease_identity,source_posture:Local|Sharded,source_database_and_security_identity,source_backup_incarnation,externally_retired_incarnation_floor,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,target_service_visibility_epoch,registry_versions:{durable_fields,command_contracts,durable_state_slots,protocol_state_fields,prepared_state_fields,consensus_state_fields,bootstrap_frames,prebootstrap_artifact_kinds},canonical_topological_source_inventory,sorted_transform_recipes[],expected_posture,expected_semantic_digest,complete_working_set_or_projection_manifest_key_opener_schema_field_slot_and_plane_coverage_digest}`. It is post-acquisition/certified-bootstrap: every source key/opener/decoder is the unique source-basis member selected by the coverage proof, but each actual use must pass the current lease/projection guard above. Each recipe is exactly `ReaddressAndReprotect{source:RestoreArchiveRef<T>,target_kind_and_schema,canonical_body_recipe,references_to_earlier_mapping_entries[],target_physical_profile,target_key_recipient_profile}|ReuseIncarnationNeutral{source,neutrality_registry_row,proof_of_no_incarnation_authority_capability_or_reference_fields,target_availability_recipe}|CarryPriorIncarnationEvidence{source,evidence_class,original_canonical_bytes_and_signature_digest,original_signer_domain_and_verification_chain,target_prior_evidence_registry_slot}|RecreateCurrentAuthority{source_evidence_inputs[],target_configuration_key_or_certificate_recipe,target_signing_ceremony_ref}|InvalidateOrSuspendCapability{source_capability_or_protocol_owner_domain,invalidation_or_suspension_recipe,reauthentication_policy}|Reject{stable_reason}`. Invalidation creates no replacement capability; a live Protocol owner may instead restore suspended and reactivate only under fresh target-epoch authority.

Execution is leaves-first. `ReaddressAndReprotect` opens/verifies source bytes, substitutes only earlier target mappings, canonical-encodes, computes the new ObjectId, encrypts under new AEAD AAD containing that ObjectId, computes CiphertextId, builds complete encoding/placement/opener/wrap records, and verifies recoverability. Changed bodies cannot reuse old ciphertext. Historical external signatures are carried byte-identically and never presented as target authority; current configurations/certificates/keys/trust are recreated. The completed `RecoveryIncarnationProjectionResult<Role> {plan_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationTransformPlan<Role>>,source_recovery_projection_digest,sorted_source_to_target_entries[],target_semantic_root_recipes,target_protocol_reconstruction_recipes,target_prepared_root_recipes,target_consensus_bootstrap_recipes,target_bootstrap_and_physical_inventory,target_availability_certificates,carried_prior_evidence_registry_root,invalidated_or_suspended_capability_and_protocol_owner_root,recreated_current_authority_root,no_missing_duplicate_or_unclassified_entry_proof,posture_preservation_proof,applied_visible_nonwidening_proof,semantic_equivalence_digest,plane_complete_slot_coverage_proof,complete_field_and_object_coverage_digest}` exists before any bridge and names no future root. RecoverSameIdentity preserves the applied/visible gap and pending queue. Clone accepts only CloneExportReady or explicit hidden-entry dispositions, and no recipe transplants source Raft hard state as current target consensus state.

The prior-incarnation barrier is per issuer and per homogeneous lease cohort, with future receipt values represented only as typed holes. `PriorLeaseCohortInventoryRecipe` is the pre-fence reference-free `{cohort_recipe_id,continuity_domain_id,database_security_namespace_id,prior_incarnation,issuer_id,issuer_configuration_identity_and_digest,issuer_authority_epoch,time_authority_profile_oid,time_domain_id,time_authority_epoch,permitted_scope_set,permitted_generation_and_counter_ranges,maximum_issued_generation,source_issuance_ledger_inventory_root_and_configuration_proof_digest,issuance_fence_receipt_digest_hole,cohort_fence_closure_row_digest_hole,final_window_digest_hole}`. A cohort may contain only leases whose issuer, authority/profile/domain epochs, scope class, and counter/generation range interpretation are identical; mixed profile/domain/epoch leases are split before planning. `CohortDispositionDecisionRecipe` is `{cohort_recipe_id,decision_policy:ExpiredOnlyWithDefinitivePreBootstrapAttestationOtherwiseRevoke,revoke_operation_id,revoke_idempotency_digest,revoke_action_body_template_with_fence_receipt_and_window_digest_holes,expiry_attestation_subject_template,cleanup_recipe}`. RestoreFencePending and the Phase-A plan commit the exact sorted recipe/decision sets and pre-minted revoke operation identities—not a future cohort-window or receipt digest. No maximum-lifetime arithmetic or counter-to-time inference is accepted as authority.

`PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortFenceClosureRow` is the portable signed `{cohort_recipe_id,issuer_id,issuer_authority_epoch,time_authority_profile_oid,time_domain_id,time_authority_epoch,covered_generation_and_counter_ranges,last_legal_issuance_or_renewal_counter,maximum_issued_or_renewed_expires_at:AuthorityInstant,issuance_ledger_accumulator_root,range_completeness_proof,source_inventory_configuration_and_recipe_match_digest,no_future_issue_or_renew_for_covered_range:true,row_digest}`. Its `AuthorityInstant` must use the cohort's exact profile/domain/epoch and is the maximum actual expiry found by an authenticated exhaustive issuance-ledger range proof—not a derived duration.

`PriorIncarnationLeaseIssuerFenceReceipt` is the portable `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=PriorIncarnationLeaseIssuerFence},issuer_id,issuer_configuration_identity_and_digest,issuer_authority_epoch,covered_generation_and_counter_ranges,prior_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,result_fenced_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,sorted_cohort_closure_rows[PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortFenceClosureRow],complete_recipe_range_issuance_ledger_and_row_bijection,last_legal_issuance_or_renewal_counter,global_no_future_prior_incarnation_issue_or_renew:true,issuer_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. After that receipt is terminally imported, the journal fills each matching typed hole and constructs exact `PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortWindow {cohort_recipe_id,issuer_fence_receipt_digest,cohort_closure_row_bytes_and_digest,time_authority_profile_oid,time_domain_id,time_authority_epoch,covered_generation_and_counter_ranges,maximum_issued_or_renewed_expires_at,issuance_ledger_accumulator_and_range_completeness_commitment,window_digest}`. For each exact window it durably selects `CohortDispositionDecision = Expired{prebootstrap_expiry_attestation_artifact_ref}` only when a definitive `PreBootstrapPortableTimeExpiryAttestation` for that same profile/domain/epoch proves `observation_interval.earliest >= maximum_issued_or_renewed_expires_at`; otherwise it selects `Revoke{precommitted_operation_id_and_filled_action_body}`. An expiry crossing after planning is therefore resolved from current evidence, never from a stale guessed decision.

`PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortRevocationReceipt` is the distinct portable `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortRevoke},issuer_id,cohort_key,cohort_window_digest,covered_generation_and_counter_subrange,prior_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,result_revoked_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,no_future_use_proof,issuer_counter,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. Both actions use the restore-prebootstrap dispatch plane and action-specific source gate. The revoke request must be the filled version of the precommitted template and retain the same operation/idempotency identities; a different window or post-fence operation is not equivalent.

`PriorIncarnationLeaseIssuerFenceRow` is the reference-free portable projection `{issuer_id,issuer_kind:OnlineContinuity|OfflineIssuer,issuer_configuration_identity_and_digest,issuer_authority_epoch,covered_generation_and_counter_ranges,predecessor_head_digest_and_cas_version,resulting_fenced_head_digest_and_cas_version,fence_receipt_identity_and_digest,sorted_cohort_closure_row_digests,complete_recipe_range_and_row_bijection,terminal_evidence_identity_and_digest,no_future_prior_incarnation_issue_or_renew:true,issuer_counter}`. `PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortDisposition` is exactly `Revoked{cohort_recipe_id,cohort_window_digest,cohort_closure_row_digest,decision_record_digest,issuer_fence_row_key,covered_generation_and_counter_subrange,revocation_operation_id,revocation_receipt_identity_and_digest,terminal_evidence_identity_and_digest}|Expired{cohort_recipe_id,cohort_window_digest,cohort_closure_row_digest,maximum_issued_or_renewed_expires_at,decision_record_digest,prebootstrap_portable_expiry_attestation_identity_and_digest}`. An Expired arm is legal only when the separately imported prebootstrap attestation's subject, profile, time domain, authority epoch, window, maximum expiry, basis, and intended barrier action equal that cohort; a Revoked arm is legal only when the issuer's fenced ranges cover the cohort completely and the action is the filled precommitted template. No receipt, expiry, or time-domain mapping is inferred from a successor head, counter, nominal lifetime, or unaddressable inline pseudo-object.

`LeasesRevokedOrExpiredThrough` is the external reference-free certificate `{database_security_namespace_id,continuity_profile_id,restore_id,prior_incarnation,planned_target_incarnation,current_restore_fence_pending_digest,current_cas_version,cohort_inventory_recipe_set_digest,cohort_disposition_decision_recipe_set_digest,exact_issuer_configuration_set_commitment,sorted_issuer_fence_rows[PriorIncarnationLeaseIssuerFenceRow],exact_cohort_window_set_commitment,sorted_cohort_dispositions[PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortDisposition],complete_recipe_to_window_to_decision_to_action_or_attestation_bijection,complete_issuer_and_cohort_bijection_commitment,no_future_prior_incarnation_lease_issuance_or_renewal_proof,authority_tick,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. It intentionally names the already-existing RestoreFencePending head and planned target, never the later RestoreReserved result. Mixed Revoked and Expired cohorts are normal; there is no all-revoked/all-expired union, cross-issuer maximum generation, cross-epoch scalar expiry, or unsigned range shortcut.

Barrier signing is not a hidden external side effect. The `EnterRestoreReserved` request carries the complete unsigned barrier body/row commitment through `PriorLeaseBarrierCompleteGate`. The continuity authority verifies every issuer/cohort receipt, expiry attestation, no-future-issuance proof, and bijection; in one registered CAS operation it signs the portable barrier naming the existing RestoreFencePending head, computes its digest, and CASes the direct RestoreReserved successor that commits that digest. `EnterRestoreReservedReceipt` begins with `ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=EnterRestoreReserved}` and contains `{prior_fence_pending_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,signed_barrier_bytes_and_digest,result_restore_reserved_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,target_incarnation,plan_and_journal_commitment,continuity_counter,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. Thus the signed barrier and result head are returned together, with no future-reference cycle or separate signing call.

Before target `K_oid`, RestoreReserved consumes only the portable barrier digest plus the sealed prebootstrap action/artifact lineage committed by its source gate; a local barrier-import ObjectId cannot yet exist. `PortableRestoreContinuityAncestryProof` is the reference-free authority-signed `{restore_id,target_incarnation,ordered_heads[{transition_kind:EnterRestoreFencePending|EnterRestoreReserved|CommitAcquisitionRefinementBootstrapOpeners|CommitAcquisitionRefinementVerifiedObjectInventory,head_bytes_digest,cas_version,predecessor_head_digest,operation_id,canonical_request_digest}],enter_restore_reserved_receipt_digest,barrier_digest,current_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,allowed_chain_complete:true,continuity_counter,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. The chain must begin at the exact FencePending head, include the exact receipt-returned RestoreReserved successor, then contain zero or the two ordered refinement successors and no other transition.

After target `K_oid` exists but still before the first RootManifest, creation constructs `PriorIncarnationLeaseBarrierBootstrapImport<Role:AuthorityOwningRole> {authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,role_and_group,restore_fence_pending_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>,cohort_inventory_and_decision_recipe_set_commitment,sorted_cohort_window_refs[StrongRef<PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortWindow>],portable_barrier_ref:StrongRef<LeasesRevokedOrExpiredThrough>,enter_restore_reserved_receipt_ref:StrongRef<EnterRestoreReservedReceipt>,enter_restore_reserved_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<EnterRestoreReserved>>,enter_restore_reserved_prebootstrap_terminal_import_artifact_identity_bytes_and_digest,continuity_ancestry_proof_ref:StrongRef<PortableRestoreContinuityAncestryProof>,sorted_issuer_fence_imports[{issuer_row_key,fence_receipt_ref:StrongRef<PriorIncarnationLeaseIssuerFenceReceipt>,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<PriorIncarnationLeaseIssuerFence>>,prebootstrap_terminal_import_artifact_identity_bytes_and_digest}],sorted_expired_cohort_imports[{cohort_window_ref:StrongRef<PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortWindow>,prebootstrap_time_validation_evidence_artifact_identity_and_digest,prebootstrap_portable_expiry_attestation_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapPortableTimeExpiryAttestation>,historical_time_profile_and_verifier_evidence_commitment}],sorted_revoked_cohort_imports[{cohort_window_ref:StrongRef<PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortWindow>,issuer_fence_row_key,decision_record_digest,revocation_receipt_ref:StrongRef<PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortRevocationReceipt>,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<PriorIncarnationLeaseCohortRevoke>>,prebootstrap_terminal_import_artifact_identity_bytes_and_digest}],sealed_prebootstrap_journal_lineage_commitment,complete_recipe_window_decision_issuer_cohort_disposition_action_and_artifact_bijection_commitment,complete_transcript_match_digest,body_digest}`. It deliberately contains no current RootSlot/manifest publication commitment, ordinary `ContinuityAuthorityObservationImport`, `TimeAuthorityObservationImport`, or canonical `TimeValidationEvidence`; those would create a pre-first-root cycle. Each issuer row has exactly one typed fence artifact import, each Expired cohort has exactly one prebootstrap validation/portable attestation import, and each Revoked cohort has exactly one action-typed receipt/evidence/artifact import.

After the bridge RootSlot is durable, the recovery-only ordered `PriorIncarnationLeaseBarrierCanonicalValidateSpec<Role>` consumes that bootstrap import plus the exact current role root/configuration/writer-fence publication, a current `ContinuityAuthorityObservationImport<Role>` whose head equals the ancestry proof's allowed current descendant, and retained historical time-profile/verifier/registry-ancestry evidence for every Expired cohort. It does **not** require a fresh challenge to a retired old time epoch: expiry is monotone once a preserved signed attestation proves `observation_interval.earliest >= expires_at`, while fresh current continuity ancestry still proves no later prior-incarnation issuance path reopened. It creates `PriorIncarnationLeaseBarrierCanonicalValidationRecord<Role> {authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,bootstrap_import_ref:StrongRef<PriorIncarnationLeaseBarrierBootstrapImport<Role>>,current_configuration_writer_fence_and_publication_commitment,continuity_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ContinuityAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,sorted_expired_cohort_validations[{cohort_window_ref,prebootstrap_portable_expiry_attestation_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapPortableTimeExpiryAttestation>,historical_time_profile_ref,historical_verifier_key_and_retirement_floor_ref,historical_registry_ancestry_proof_ref,attestation_signature_window_and_subject_validation_digest}],exact_current_head_ancestry_recipe_window_decision_and_all_evidence_match_digest,assigned_role_maintenance_identity,body_digest}`. Restore reconciliation retains the bootstrap import until this record and its audit visibility are durable; readiness, Shard bootstrap projection, promotion, and ordinary service require the canonical validation record. Thus initial creation is acyclic, old valid backups do not depend on a long-retired clock service, and post-root operation still proves current continuity authority.

`ShardPriorIncarnationLeaseBarrierProjection` is the certified Meta projection `{source_meta_prefix,source_meta_configuration_identity,restore_id,prior_and_target_incarnations,barrier_identity_and_digest,meta_canonical_validation_record_identity_and_digest,complete_issuer_cohort_bijection_commitment,typed_projection_payload_identity_and_digest,projection_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<MetaControlProjectionCertificate>,retention_grant_and_payload_availability_evidence,body_digest}` using the exact `TypedCertifiedMetaProjectionPayload::PriorIncarnationLeaseBarrier` arm after `PriorIncarnationLeaseBarrierCanonicalValidationRecord<Meta>` is durable and audit-visible. A Shard never imports raw continuity authority, reservation heads, fence receipts, or local time evidence. Omitted/duplicate issuer or cohort, a scalar expiry, unsigned range, future RestoreReserved reference, portable local reference, pre-validation projection, or ordinary-Protocol dispatch for these prebootstrap actions is invalid.

The three allocation-style reservations share one nonreuse protocol but have distinct bodies. `NewDatabaseIdentityTargetCreationCommitment` is the closed union `DirectoryBoundLocal{filesystem_domain_id,target_directory_claim,filesystem_profile_id,target_manifest_staging_recipe_digest,expected_mount_device_class,zero_existing_root_slot_proof_recipe_digest,fixed_local_continuity_recipe_digest}|ExternalCas{continuity_authority_domain,expected_absent_target_identity_basis,continuity_reservation_recipe_digest,target_posture:Local|Sharded}`. DirectoryBound is inhabitable only for a Local target; Meta/W12 and every Sharded target require ExternalCas. `NewDatabaseIdentityReservation` is `{reservation_id,restore_id,mode:CloneNewIdentity,new_database_id,new_database_security_namespace_id,target_creation_commitment:NewDatabaseIdentityTargetCreationCommitment,expected_target_incarnation,precommitted_claimant_ceiling_and_intended_use_template_digest,authority_epoch,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,issuance_nonce,signature}`. It never binds the prebootstrap journal inode, and an ExternalCas/Sharded reservation never pretends that one local target inode covers multiple groups.

`ServiceVisibilityEpochReservation` is `{reservation_id,database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,migration_nonce,source_posture:Local,target_posture:Sharded,source_service_visibility_epoch,target_service_visibility_epoch,external_service_epoch_floor,complete_target_group_topology_and_endpoint_commitment,expected_continuity_digest_and_cas_version,expected_catalog_version,authority_epoch,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,issuance_nonce,signature}`, with target strictly greater than both source and external floor. `AllocationEpochReservation` is `{reservation_id,restore_id,mode:RecoverSameIdentity|CloneNewIdentity,target_database_id,target_database_security_namespace_id,target_incarnation,source_manifest_digest,sorted_allocator_families[{allocator_kind,source_max_epoch,external_retired_floor,target_epoch,possibly_issued_source_ranges_digest,burn_through_range,successor_start}],complete_source_allocator_root_and_range_coverage_digest,external_allocator_floor_digest,precommitted_claimant_ceiling_and_intended_use_template_digest,authority_epoch,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expires_at,issuance_nonce,signature}`; every target epoch/range begins strictly beyond all source, external, restored, and possibly issued values with checked arithmetic.

Throughout restore, `journal_staging_inode_identity` means only the relocatable prebootstrap dispatch-cache inode, while `target_manifest_staging_inode_identity` means the eventual database `manifest.root` creation inode. They have distinct wire fields, proof types, fences, and negative substitution tests; no prose or encoder may abbreviate either as unqualified `staging_inode_identity`.

`ReservationBodyRef` is the closed noninterchangeable canonical union `NewDatabaseIdentity{body_ref:StrongRef<NewDatabaseIdentityReservation>}|ServiceVisibilityEpoch{body_ref:StrongRef<ServiceVisibilityEpochReservation>}|AllocationEpoch{body_ref:StrongRef<AllocationEpochReservation>}` and may appear only after target `K_oid` exists in Local/Meta authority-owning state. `PreBootstrapReservationBodyRef` is the separate closed union `NewDatabaseIdentity{artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<NewDatabaseIdentityReservation>}|AllocationEpoch{artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<AllocationEpochReservation>}`; ServiceVisibilityEpoch is never a restore-prebootstrap reservation. The first bridge copies exact bytes into target-keyed canonical reservation objects and proves a total artifact/body/head equality relation. Shards consume certified Meta reservation projections. Each selected arm's kind, reservation ID, authority epoch, profile/window, issuance nonce, and canonical body digest must byte-match the authority head; neither identity class accepts a raw body digest or an arm from the other class.

`ReservationAuthorityHead` is the external CAS-linear, reference-free `{authority_domain,reservation_kind,reservation_id,cas_version,predecessor_head_digest,monotonic_nonreuse_floor,issuance_body_digest,state:Reserved|ClaimedForUse{claim_id,intended_use_kind,intended_use_commitment,claimant_identity,claim_operation_id,claim_idempotency_key_digest,canonical_claim_request_digest,no_reassignment:true}|Consumed{claim_id,applied_use_identity,consume_operation_id,consume_idempotency_key_digest,canonical_consume_request_digest}|BurnedBeforeUse{claim_id?,stable_reason,no_matching_use_proof_digest,burn_operation_id,burn_idempotency_key_digest,canonical_burn_request_digest},authority_tick,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. A successor commits only pre-CAS request/operation facts and never its later receipt digest. Legal direct successors are exactly `Reserved -> ClaimedForUse -> Consumed`, `Reserved -> BurnedBeforeUse`, and `ClaimedForUse -> BurnedBeforeUse` with authority-verified no-use evidence. Expiry can authorize burn but never reuse; the nonreuse floor only advances. A claim has no timeout/reassignment path: exact retry joins it, a conflicting commitment fails, and recovery must consume or prove the fixed use absent before burn.

`PortableReservationAuthorityObservation` is the challenged/action-bound signed `{authority_domain,reservation_kind,reservation_id,current_head_bytes_and_digest,current_cas_version,current_state_projection,current_claim_and_intended_use_projection,monotonic_nonreuse_floor,intended_action:Claim|Consume|BurnBeforeUse|Status,canonical_action_body_digest,challenge_nonce,time_authority_observation_digest,time_authority_profile_oid,time_observation_interval:AuthorityInterval,authority_tick,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. `ReservationAuthorityObservationImport<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the role-local `{role_and_group,current_configuration_writer_fence_and_publication_commitment,reservation_body_ref:ReservationBodyRef,current_head_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,portable_observation_ref:StrongRef<PortableReservationAuthorityObservation>,time_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<TimeAuthorityObservationImport>,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,exact_body_head_observation_time_and_action_match_digest,imported_at_maintenance_identity}`. Claim authorization requires a current Reserved observation and `TimeValidationEvidence::Usable`. Consume/burn authorization requires its exact current predecessor and the action-specific eligibility below; an observation never substitutes for dispatch publication.

Before target `K_oid`, `PreBootstrapReservationAuthorityObservationImport` is the distinct artifact schema `{reservation_body_ref:PreBootstrapReservationBodyRef,current_head_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,portable_observation_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PortableReservationAuthorityObservation>,prebootstrap_time_observation_import_artifact_ref:PreBootstrapArtifactRef<PreBootstrapTimeAuthorityObservationImport>,exact_body_head_observation_time_plan_and_action_match_digest,dispatch_root_generation}`. `PreBootstrapReservationBurnSource` is `Reserved{body_ref:PreBootstrapReservationBodyRef,current_head_and_observation_import_artifacts}|ClaimedUnused{body_ref,claimed_head_claim_receipt_terminal_evidence_and_import_artifacts,complete_no_use_proof}`. `PreBootstrapReservationBurnEligibility` is `ExpiredUnused{prebootstrap_expiry_observation_and_evidence_artifacts,portable_expiry_attestation_artifact_ref}|ExplicitAbandonment{sealed_zero_canonical_publication_proof,resource_disposition_authority}`. These are the only prebootstrap claim/burn inputs; ordinary role-local imports and Semantic visibility certificates are uninhabited before the first root.

The three typed portable terminal receipts all begin with the exact full request binding. `ReservationClaimReceipt` is `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=ReservationClaim},authority_domain,reservation_kind,reservation_id,reserved_head_digest_and_cas_version,claimed_head_bytes_and_digest_and_cas_version,claim_id,intended_use_kind,intended_use_commitment,claimant_identity,monotonic_nonreuse_floor,authority_tick,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. `ReservationConsumptionReceipt` is `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=ReservationConsume},authority_domain,reservation_kind,reservation_id,claim_id,intended_use_kind,intended_use_commitment,claimed_head_digest_and_cas_version,consumed_head_bytes_and_digest_and_cas_version,applied_use_identity,monotonic_nonreuse_floor,authority_tick,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. `ReservationBurnReceipt` is `{external_request_binding:ExternalActionRequestBinding{action_kind=ReservationBurnBeforeUse},authority_domain,reservation_kind,reservation_id,claim_id?,prior_head_digest_and_cas_version,burned_head_bytes_and_digest_and_cas_version,stable_reason,no_matching_use_proof_digest,monotonic_nonreuse_floor,authority_tick,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. Authority domain byte-matches the head and request binding. None contains a local reference. Each has its distinct generated `PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<Action>` member and role-local receipt import; rollback, fork, skipped CAS version, receipt/action substitution, or scalar-only equality is a permanent gap.

Reservation ownership is durable, state-shaped, and authority-bound. `ReservationOperationTerminalRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the generated exact action family `{role,reservation_kind,reservation_id,action:Claim|Consume|BurnBeforeUse,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_action_body_digest,terminal:Applied{typed_receipt_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_ref,terminal_import_commitment,result_head_digest_and_cas_version}|NeverSent{dispatch_never_sent_proof_ref,abandonment_publication_evidence_commitment}|NoEffect{typed_provider_tombstone_ref,portable_terminal_evidence_ref,terminal_import_commitment,unchanged_head_digest_and_cas_version,reason:PermanentlyRefused|ConflictNoEffect|ExpiredNotRegisteredNoEffect},terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,body_digest}` with concrete noninterchangeable refs per action. `ReservationOperationTerminalHistory<Role>` is an immutable map `(action,operation_id) -> StrongRef<ReservationOperationTerminalRecord<Role>>`; every terminal attempt appends exactly once and operation IDs are never reused.

`ReservationClaimOperationRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,reservation_body_ref:ReservationBodyRef,reserved_head_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,reserved_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,claim_id,intended_use_kind,intended_use_commitment,claimant_identity,operation_id,idempotency_key_digest,canonical_claim_action_body_recipe_and_digest,state:AuthorizedUndispatchedRecipe,body_digest}`. Claim identity and operation identity are deterministically derived from the reservation ID, precommitted claimant ceiling/intended-use template, final intended-use commitment, and selected restore/transition identity; legitimate recovery contenders therefore converge on identical bytes rather than racing arbitrary claimant IDs.

`ReservationAppliedUseRecipeCommitment<Kind>` is the pre-claim reference-free `{reservation_kind:Kind,reservation_id,expected_reserved_predecessor_and_authority_domain,claimant_identity_and_ceiling,intended_use_kind,intended_use_commitment,target_identity_incarnation_and_service,kind_specific_subject_derivation_rule,claim_operation_and_action_body_recipe_commitment,recipe_digest}`. It contains typed holes for the later claim head/receipt/import and, for ServiceVisibilityEpoch, the later RoleTransitionReserved continuity successor. The claim request/head bind this recipe digest, not those unavailable future values. `ClaimToAppliedUseIdentityDerivationProof<Role,Kind>` is the checked later `{recipe_commitment,realized_claim_head_receipt_and_import_or_role_transition_reserved_head,realized_applied_use_identity,exact_unique_derivation_and_no_alternate_subject_proof,checker_version,proof_digest}`.

`BootstrapReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Role,Kind>` is the post-claim, pre-first-root reference-free `{role_and_group,restore_id,restore_epoch,reservation_kind:Kind,reservation_id,reservation_body_digest,claim_id,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,intended_use_kind,intended_use_commitment,claimant_identity,claim_import_public_commitment,claim_to_applied_use_derivation_proof_digest,kind_specific_use_subject:NewDatabaseIdentity{target_creation_commitment_and_direct_creation_authority_recipe_public_commitment}|AllocationEpoch{exact_allocator_family_epoch_range_burn_and_external_floor_commitment},authority_binding_public_commitment,identity_digest}` with concrete generated kinds only for `NewDatabaseIdentity|AllocationEpoch`. Its digest transcript explicitly excludes every recovery-bridge body, command record, applied result, state/registry/root/manifest/slot identity, publication action/evidence, filled first-root recipe, certificate recipe, and receipt beyond the already-created claim import. It is therefore a pure materialized subject identity rather than a claim about durable first-root application.

`ReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the closed noninterchangeable union `BootstrapNewDatabaseIdentity{identity:BootstrapReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Role,NewDatabaseIdentity>}|BootstrapAllocationEpoch{identity:BootstrapReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Role,AllocationEpoch>}|RoleTransitionServiceVisibilityEpoch{role_and_group,reservation_id,claim_id,reservation_body_digest,migration_nonce,source_and_target_service_visibility_epochs,intended_use_commitment,claimant_identity,claim_import_public_commitment,already_returned_role_transition_reserved_continuity_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,claim_to_applied_use_derivation_proof_digest,role_transition_plan_and_target_group_commitment,authority_binding_public_commitment,identity_digest}`. The ServiceVisibility arm is legal only after the external RoleTransitionReserved successor already exists; it never names the later target bridge/root. There is no generic “ordinary semantic result” arm: if another reservation class is introduced, it must register an already-known derivation recipe and its own exact union arm. The claim commits `ReservationAppliedUseRecipeCommitment`; the later authority record, creation guard, applying input, resulting reservation-use record, and consume request carry the unique realized identity plus `ClaimToAppliedUseIdentityDerivationProof`.

`ReservationUseRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the closed union `ReservedAvailable{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,reservation_body_ref:ReservationBodyRef,current_reserved_head_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,operation_terminal_history_ref:StrongRef<ReservationOperationTerminalHistory<Role>>}|ClaimAuthorizedUndispatched{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,base_reserved_ref:StrongRef<ReservationUseRecord<Role>>,operation_record_ref:StrongRef<ReservationClaimOperationRecord<Role>>}|ClaimedAvailable{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,reservation_body_ref:ReservationBodyRef,claimed_head_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,claim_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ReservationClaimReceipt>,claim_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<CanonicalReservationClaimContract<Role>>>,intended_use_kind,intended_use_commitment,claim_id,claimant_identity,operation_terminal_history_ref:StrongRef<ReservationOperationTerminalHistory<Role>>}|AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,reservation_kind,reservation_id,reservation_body_digest,claim_id,claimed_head_digest_and_cas_version,intended_use_kind,intended_use_commitment,claimant_identity,applied_use_identity:ReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Role>,operation_terminal_history_ref:StrongRef<ReservationOperationTerminalHistory<Role>>}|ConsumeAuthorizedUndispatched{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,applied_record_ref:StrongRef<ReservationUseRecord<Role>>,consume_operation_id,consume_idempotency_key_digest,canonical_consume_action_body_recipe_and_digest,authorization_and_eligibility_commitment}|BurnAuthorizedUndispatched{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,source:ReservationBurnSource<Role>,burn_operation_id,burn_idempotency_key_digest,canonical_burn_action_body_recipe_and_digest,eligibility:ReservationBurnEligibility<Role>}|Consumed{reservation_kind,reservation_id,claim_id,applied_use_identity:ReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Role>,consumption_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ReservationConsumptionReceipt>,operation_terminal_history_ref:StrongRef<ReservationOperationTerminalHistory<Role>>,terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,terminal_nonreuse_commitment}|BurnedBeforeUse{reservation_kind,reservation_id,claim_id?,burn_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ReservationBurnReceipt>,operation_terminal_history_ref:StrongRef<ReservationOperationTerminalHistory<Role>>,terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,terminal_nonreuse_commitment}|ReservationConflictQuarantined{reservation_kind,reservation_id,expected_predecessor_and_operation_commitment,authenticated_winning_successor_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,winning_operation_request_status_receipt_lineage_commitment,conflict_class:NonEquivalentClaimantOrUse|BurnedAfterDurableAppliedUse|DivergentAuthorityHistory,terminal_authority_commitment:TerminalAuthorityCommitment<Role>,abort_enclosing_restore_or_transition_required:true,terminal_nonreuse_commitment}`. `CanonicalReservationClaimContract<Role>` is the generated contract-ID type for `(ReservationClaim,CanonicalProtocol,Role)`; the prebootstrap contract is distinct. Terminal arms do not retain reusable body/observation/dispatch authority. `ReservationUseRegistry<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is keyed by `(reservation_kind,reservation_id)` and has one checked-generation successor.

The bootstrap identity becomes externally consumable only after an after-the-fact proof of its durable use. `BootstrapReservationUsePublicationCertificateRecipe<Role,Kind>` is the pre-order `{role_and_group,reservation_kind:Kind,bootstrap_applied_use_identity_digest,claim_import_public_commitment,claim_to_applied_use_derivation_proof_digest,expected_initial_bridge_applied_identity_recipe,expected_post_bridge_reservation_record_digest_recipe,expected_first_root_manifest_slot_and_publication_subject_recipe,signer_policy_epoch,recipe_digest}` and contains no future identity. `BootstrapReservationUsePublicationCertificate<Role,Kind>` is the exact `{role_and_group,reservation_kind:Kind,bootstrap_applied_use_identity:BootstrapReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Role,Kind>,reservation_claim_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapReservationClaimCanonicalImportRecord<Role,Kind>>,claim_to_applied_use_derivation_proof_ref:StrongRef<ClaimToAppliedUseIdentityDerivationProof<Role,Kind>>,initial_recovery_bridge_applied_identity,post_bridge_reservation_use_record_ref:BootstrapAppliedAwaitingConsumeRecordRef<Role,Kind>,first_root_state_manifest_and_slot_public_commitment,root_publication_evidence:RootPublicationEvidence,exact_recipe_identity_claim_bridge_payload_record_and_publication_match_digest,authority_binding_public_commitment,signer_set_epoch,signatures,body_digest}` with generated concrete types for `Kind=NewDatabaseIdentity|AllocationEpoch`. `BootstrapAppliedAwaitingConsumeRecordRef<Role,Kind>` is a generated tag-refined strong reference to `ReservationUseRecord<Role>::AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization` with the exact Bootstrap origin/kind/identity. The certificate is constructible only after the first RootSlot is durable and that record contains the byte-identical realized identity. It does not enter that first root and cannot create a reference cycle. A bootstrap consume authorization must carry this certificate; the ServiceVisibilityEpoch arm instead carries the already-returned RoleTransitionReserved head, its derivation proof, and the role-transition candidate's publication evidence. Cross-kind, cross-role, cross-restore, cross-slot, or equal-scalar/different-root substitution fails.

`ReservationDispositionEligibility<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is exactly `Live{current_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,usable_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>}|ExpiredFixedClaim{applied_record_ref:StrongRef<ReservationUseRecord<Role>>,required_record_tag:AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization|ConsumeAuthorizedUndispatched,current_claimed_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,expired_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,portable_expiry_attestation_ref:StrongRef<PortableTimeExpiryAttestation>,claim_identity_and_intended_use_commitment,exact_applied_use_identity,consume_operation_and_action_body_commitment?}`. `ExpiredFixedClaim` authorizes only status, a fresh Consume authorization, or exact retry/finalization of that already-durable use. `ReservedReservationUseRef<Role>` and `ClaimedReservationUseRef<Role>` are generated tag-refined references to only `ReservedAvailable` and `ClaimedAvailable`. `ReservationBurnSource<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `UnclaimedReserved{reserved_record_ref:ReservedReservationUseRef<Role>,current_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>}|ClaimedUnused{claimed_record_ref:ClaimedReservationUseRef<Role>,current_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>}`. `ReservationBurnEligibility<Role>` is `ExpiredUnused{expired_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,portable_expiry_attestation_ref:StrongRef<PortableTimeExpiryAttestation>,complete_no_use_proof_ref}|ExplicitAbandonment{published_abandonment_authority:SemanticVisibilityCertificateRef<Role>,typed_no_use_proof_ref,authorized_reason}`. The prebootstrap counterpart uses `PreBootstrapReservationBodyRef` and the sealed zero-canonical-publication proof. Burn is structurally uninhabited from AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization or ConsumeAuthorizedUndispatched.

For an operational Local/Meta database, `ReservationClaimAuthorizeSpec<Role>` consumes `ReservedAvailable` and atomically installs `ClaimAuthorizedUndispatched`; only its audit-visible `PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Role>` initializes `IrreversibleDispatchAttempt<ReservationClaim>`. `ReservationClaimImportSpec<Role>` consumes the operation record, exact typed terminal evidence/receipt, returned direct-successor head, and current registry basis, appends Applied terminal history, and installs `ClaimedAvailable`. `ReservationClaimNoEffectFinalizeSpec<Role>` consumes a never-sent proof or typed refusal/expired-NotRegistered terminal artifact/evidence/import, proves the authority head is still the exact Reserved predecessor, appends the terminal record, and returns to `ReservedAvailable`; only then may an independently authorized burn or fresh claim proceed. A CAS conflict whose authority head advanced is never handled by this no-effect path. Ambiguous status leaves ClaimAuthorizedUndispatched and blocks both. Timeout alone never reassigns or burns.

After the canonical use is durable, the same apply installs `AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization`; no external operation ID is invented by deterministic use apply. `ReservationConsumeAuthorizationBasis<Role,Kind>` is the closed union `RestoreBootstrap{applied_record_ref:BootstrapAppliedAwaitingConsumeRecordRef<Role,Kind>,publication_certificate_ref:StrongRef<BootstrapReservationUsePublicationCertificate<Role,Kind>>}|RoleTransitionServiceVisibility{applied_record_ref:RoleTransitionServiceVisibilityAppliedAwaitingConsumeRecordRef<Role>,claim_to_applied_use_derivation_proof_ref:StrongRef<ClaimToAppliedUseIdentityDerivationProof<Role,ServiceVisibilityEpoch>>,role_transition_reserved_head_and_claim_match_proof,applying_candidate_publication_evidence:PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificateRef<Role>}`. The Bootstrap arm is generated only for `Kind=NewDatabaseIdentity|AllocationEpoch`; the role-transition arm only for `Kind=ServiceVisibilityEpoch`. Both record refs are generated tag-refined strong references to `ReservationUseRecord<Role>::AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization`, never refs to an embedded identity value. `ReservationConsumeAuthorizeSpec<Role,Kind>` consumes the matching basis, chooses a fresh operation/idempotency identity for the exact same applied use, and installs `ConsumeAuthorizedUndispatched`; only that candidate's role-valid visibility certificate initializes the canonical-plane `ReservationConsume` dispatch instance, and `ReservationDispositionEligibility::ExpiredFixedClaim` preserves cleanup after expiry. `ReservationDispositionImportSpec<Role>::Consumed` consumes the authorized record, returned direct-successor head, typed receipt/import/terminal evidence, and transcript proof, appends Applied history, and installs compact Consumed. `ReservationConsumeNoEffectFinalizeSpec<Role>` consumes a never-sent or typed refusal/expired-NotRegistered terminal, proves the head remains Claimed with the same fixed use, appends history, and returns to `AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization`; it can only authorize a fresh Consume operation and can never burn or alter the use.

`ReservationBurnAuthorizeSpec<Role>` consumes one exact `ReservationBurnSource<Role>` plus eligibility and installs `BurnAuthorizedUndispatched`; its visibility initializes `ReservationBurnBeforeUse`. `ReservationDispositionImportSpec<Role>::BurnedBeforeUse` consumes that record plus returned head, typed burn receipt/import/terminal evidence, and exact no-use proof, appends Applied history, and installs compact BurnedBeforeUse. `ReservationBurnNoEffectFinalizeSpec<Role>` consumes never-sent or typed refusal/expired-NotRegistered evidence, proves the external head and no-use state are unchanged, appends history, and returns to the exact prior `ReservedAvailable` or `ClaimedAvailable` arm. Ambiguous status remains blocking. No no-effect path changes reservation ownership or creates a reusable identity.

An authenticated advanced head uses exact `ReservationExternalSuccessorReconcileSpec<Role> {expected_registry_record_ref,current_authority_observation_import_ref,winning_head_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,winning_operation_request_status_and_typed_receipt_lineage,local_claim_and_applied_use_commitment?,complete_equivalence_or_conflict_proof,terminal_audit_gate}`. It constructs `EquivalentReservationSuccessorAdoptionRecord<Role>` only for one of: `ClaimedEquivalent{same_claimant,intended_use,claim_id,operation_and_request_bytes}`, `ConsumedEquivalent{same_claim_and_exact_applied_use}`, or `BurnedLegitimatelyBeforeUse{complete_zero_local_use_proof}`. Those arms adopt the winning successor into `ClaimedAvailable`, `Consumed`, or `BurnedBeforeUse` and append the winning operation's terminal history. Any non-equivalent claimant/use, or a Burned successor after durable local application, selects `ReservationConflictQuarantined` and orders abort/abandonment of the enclosing restore or transition; it never returns to Reserved or changes the fixed use.

`PreBootstrapReservationExternalSuccessorReconcileArtifact` is the exact journal-backed analogue. It retains the authenticated winning head, request/status/receipt lineage, plan/action/resource relation, and equivalence/conflict proof. Equivalent adoption updates the prebootstrap claimed/consumed/burned authority projection under the same journal generation protocol; conflict freezes all creation/projection authority and requires `AbandonmentDrainPending`. Provider `ConflictNoEffect` is terminal no-effect only when its signed evidence proves the predecessor head is unchanged and the operation can never later appear. Reservation head changes, CAS races, and legitimate equivalent successors are never collapsed into that class.

Clone/restore bootstrap is the sole pre-canonical exception: the authenticated pre-bootstrap root owns the exact claim/burn authorization and uses the registered `PreBootstrapDispatchAttempt<ExactPreBootstrapContract>` rows. `PendingRestoreCreationGuard` owns already exclusive claims; the first hidden bridge installs every matching `AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization`, and after-publication certificates plus canonical-plane consume contracts finish them. Checkpoint, backup/restore, GC, role-transition, and prebootstrap-journal floors retain every nonterminal use and its original contract ID/plane. Generated command/state/dispatch tests cover claim-response loss, NotRegistered-before/after expiry, competing claim, apply-before-consume crash, consume after expiry, consume-response loss, claimed/unclaimed burn races, stale observations, action/receipt/contract-plane substitution, and reservation nonreuse.

The first restore bridge consumes already exclusive claims, never bare Reserved objects. `PreBootstrapReservationClaimCanonicalImportRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole,Kind>` is the exact canonical `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,reservation_kind=Kind,canonical_reservation_body_ref:ReservationBodyRef,claimed_head_ref:StrongRef<ReservationAuthorityHead>,claim_receipt_ref:StrongRef<ReservationClaimReceipt>,portable_terminal_evidence_ref:StrongRef<PortableExternalActionTerminalEvidence<PreBootstrapReservationClaimContract<Kind>>>,prebootstrap_terminal_import_artifact_identity_bytes_and_digest,sealed_dispatch_root_lineage_commitment,body_head_receipt_action_contract_plan_and_plane_match_digest,body_digest}`. `PreBootstrapReservationClaimContract<Kind>` is the generated exact contract-ID type for the selected prebootstrap reservation kind; it cannot decode as `CanonicalReservationClaimContract<Role>`.

`RestoreFirstRootSourceLeaseUseGuard<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the linear/nonserializable runtime `{restore_id,role_and_group,latest_source_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role>>,current_lease_authority_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,current_archive_grant_observation_import_ref:StrongRef<ArchiveGrantAuthorityObservationImport<Role>>,lease_usable_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,grant_usable_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,frozen_journal_head_generation_and_digest,plan_and_first_root_action_digest,stable_holder_subject_inventory_and_recipient_commitment,same_process_observation_budget,unconsumed}`. Both observations must select the exact current lease/grant heads, generation, holder, subject/inventory set, and intended first-root action. After `FirstRootPublicationFrozen`, status/challenged-observation refresh may reconstruct this runtime guard without changing provider state; no renewal, refresh family member, new action, source open, or plan change is legal. Immediately before every irreversible target seal, physical-generation publication, and RootSlot write the guard rechecks both heads and Usable evidence. Failure, expiry, ambiguity, or drift consumes it and permits only abandonment/new restore.

`DirectoryBoundCreationEvidence<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the exact canonical `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,restore_id,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,filesystem_profile_id,verified_mount_device_and_directory_identity,manifest_root_inode_identity_and_creation_nonce_digest,exclusive_whole_inode_lease_acquisition_evidence,root_publication_sequencer_instance_commitment,parent_directory_create_and_sync_durability_evidence,zero_structurally_credible_root_slot_proof_over_both_slots,fixed_local_continuity_recipe_and_digest,new_database_identity_claim_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapReservationClaimCanonicalImportRecord<Role,NewDatabaseIdentity>>,allocation_claim_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapReservationClaimCanonicalImportRecord<Role,AllocationEpoch>>,bridge_frozen_dispatch_root_identity_and_generation,first_root_manifest_and_slot_recipe_commitment,complete_profile_inode_claim_continuity_and_recipe_match_digest,body_digest}`. It is constructed only while the exact inode lease and sequencer are live. Runtime `DirectoryBoundFirstRootPublicationGuard<Role>` is linear/nonserializable and contains that evidence plus `source_lease_use_guard:RestoreFirstRootSourceLeaseUseGuard<Role>`, the open inode lease, sequencer, current path/mount/inode revalidation, and frozen creation guard; immediately before every immutable target seal and the first slot write it reopens and byte-matches every field and consumes the source-use guard at the final submission boundary. It can publish only the one committed RecoveryReconciling first-root recipe. Any source lease/grant/time, path, mount, inode, creation nonce, lease, fence, profile, claim, continuity digest, slot, or recipe drift consumes it without writing. The resulting RootManifest/RootSlot byte-match the evidence and become the sole durable DirectoryBound continuity basis; no external continuity record is fabricated.
