Independently addressable `RestoreDirectCreationAuthorityRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,mode:RecoverSameIdentity{restore_reserved_continuity_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>,live_recovery_incarnation_lease:ClusterIncarnationLease,prior_lease_barrier_bootstrap_import_ref:StrongRef<PriorIncarnationLeaseBarrierBootstrapImport<Role>>}|CloneNewIdentity{new_database_and_security_namespace_claim_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapReservationClaimCanonicalImportRecord<Role,NewDatabaseIdentity>>,creation_authority:DirectoryBound{creation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DirectoryBoundCreationEvidence<Role>>}|ExternalCas{clone_reserved_continuity_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>,live_recovery_incarnation_lease:ClusterIncarnationLease},audit_clone_boundary_ref:StrongRef<AuditCloneBoundary>},allocation_claim_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapReservationClaimCanonicalImportRecord<Role,AllocationEpoch>>,complete_prebootstrap_to_canonical_authority_import_bijection,body_digest}`. Both clone profiles therefore consume the same nonreassignable new-identity claim; only their incarnation-continuity authority differs. The post-root canonical barrier validation is a required reconciliation successor, never a precondition of this first publication.

`ExternalCasFirstRootPublicationGuard<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the linear/nonserializable `{role_and_group,restore_id,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,source_lease_use_guard:RestoreFirstRootSourceLeaseUseGuard<Role>,current_restore_reserved_or_clone_reserved_refinement_head_bytes_digest_and_cas_version,current_prebootstrap_continuity_observation_and_time_validation_artifacts,current_recovery_only_member_lease,bridge_frozen_journal_head_digest_and_cas_version,target_manifest_staging_inode_identity_and_filesystem_profile,local_whole_inode_lease,root_publication_sequencer,exact_first_root_action_digest,exact_first_root_manifest_slot_and_physical_lease_recipes,observation_budget,unconsumed}`. It is constructed only from the newest bridge-frozen journal generation, current source-lease/grant observations, and `RecoveryIncarnationLeaseAcquireOrRefresh` result. Immediately before every immutable seal, plane-root construction boundary, physical-generation-lease publication, and RootSlot write it revalidates the source lease/grant/time guard, exact external continuity head/CAS, challenged observation/time budget, live RecoveryOnly lease, journal head, target inode/path/mount/fence/sequencer, and unchanged action digest. Any drift consumes it without publication. A stored source-lease record, `ClusterIncarnationLease`, or continuity record alone never authorizes the first write.

`RestoreFirstRootPublicationAuthority<Role>` is the generated exact family `LocalDirectoryBound{guard:DirectoryBoundFirstRootPublicationGuard<Local>}|LocalExternalCas{guard:ExternalCasFirstRootPublicationGuard<Local>}|MetaExternalCas{guard:ExternalCasFirstRootPublicationGuard<Meta>}`. `MetaDirectoryBound` is uninhabited, as is any role/profile mismatch. `PendingRestoreCreationGuard<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is linear/nonexportable `{restore_id,target_role_and_group,first_root_publication_authority:RestoreFirstRootPublicationAuthority<Role>,direct_creation_authority_ref:StrongRef<RestoreDirectCreationAuthorityRecord<Role>>,source_acquisition_bundle_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceAcquisitionBundle<Role>>,latest_source_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Role>>,frozen_journal_head_generation_and_digest,projection_result_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationProjectionResult<Role>>,initial_protocol_state_recipe_ref:StrongRef<InitialProtocolStateRecipe<Role>>,sorted_bootstrap_reservation_applied_use_identities[BootstrapReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Role,NewDatabaseIdentity|AllocationEpoch>],sorted_after_publication_certificate_recipes[BootstrapReservationUsePublicationCertificateRecipe<Role,NewDatabaseIdentity|AllocationEpoch>],exact_claim_import_identity_bridge_record_and_certificate_recipe_bijection,exact_semantic_protocol_prepared_consensus_bootstrap_recipe_commitments,complete_durable_slot_bitmap,exact_allowed_object_inventory,exact_recovery_bridge_body_recipe,exact_first_recovery_root_manifest_and_slot_recipes,service_visibility_epoch,unconsumed}`. The bridge atomically installs canonical reservation `AppliedAwaitingConsumeAuthorization` records with those precomputed identities, sealed prebootstrap action lineage, allocator epochs/burns, and the initial hidden all-plane state; it does not claim the first root is durable. After that RootSlot is durable, recovery constructs the exact `BootstrapReservationUsePublicationCertificate<Role,Kind>` set and only then may a canonical-plane consume authorization arm. External Consumed heads are finalized through those later dispatches. A crash before publication may burn only under the sealed zero-publication proof or refresh the exact ExternalCas RecoveryOnly lease and rebuild from the new frozen generation; afterward it can only finish the exact certificate-and-consumption set.

Sharded restore bootstraps Meta first. `ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjectionPayload` is the certificate-free portable `{lease_identity,generation,portable_lease_state_digest:PortableRestoreSourceLeaseStateDigest,selected_grant_generation_and_digest,window_profile_domain_epoch_and_bounds,archive_counter,holder_and_subject_set_commitment,shard_inventory_and_recipient_subset_commitment}`. `RestoreAllocationEpochShardSlicePayload` is the certificate-free portable `{restore_id,restore_epoch,reservation_id,claim_id,bootstrap_allocation_applied_use_identity_commitment,target_database_and_security_identity,target_incarnation,shard_id,target_topology_and_meta_configuration,sorted_allocator_families[{allocator_kind,target_epoch,parent_reserved_range,assigned_disjoint_subranges,burn_floor,successor_start}],parent_allocation_reservation_body_and_external_floor_commitment,per_shard_subset_proof,complete_cross_shard_partition_commitment,source_meta_prefix,body_digest}`. For every allocator family, the sorted Shard subranges are pairwise disjoint, their declared union exactly equals the Meta parent assignment after the explicit burn floor, and no gap may be silently reassigned.

From the durable hidden Meta recovery root, Meta issues `RestoreShardBootstrapProjection {restore_id,restore_epoch,shard_id,target_topology_and_configuration,service_visibility_epoch,continuity_recovery_only_projection_payload,allocation_epoch_shard_slice_payload:RestoreAllocationEpochShardSlicePayload,prior_lease_barrier_projection_payload,source_lease_projection_payload:ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjectionPayload,archive_inventory_and_key_recipient_subset,shard_transform_recipe_commitment,expected_initial_semantic_protocol_prepared_consensus_bootstrap_slot_recipes,source_meta_prefix_and_configuration,retention_grant_and_payload_availability_evidence,projection_digest}` and `RestoreShardBootstrapProjectionCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,projection_ref:StrongRef<RestoreShardBootstrapProjection>,projection_identity_and_digest,source_meta_hidden_root_and_slot_commitment,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`. After verifying that CRSR and the slice, the Shard constructs consumer-local `ShardAllocationEpochAuthorityProjection {certified_bootstrap_projection_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardBootstrapProjectionCertificate>,slice_payload:RestoreAllocationEpochShardSlicePayload,source_meta_prefix_configuration_and_parent_allocation_commitment,local_allocator_registry_recipe_commitment,exact_certificate_slice_subset_partition_and_local_recipe_match_digest,body_digest}` and its `ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjection`. Duplicate, overlapping, gapped, wrong-parent, wrong-prefix, wrong-topology, or wrong-configuration slices fail before bridge construction; no future-certificate cycle exists.

`ShardRecoveryPublicationGuard` is a nonserializable action-bound guard derived from the certified RecoveryOnly continuity payload plus a current member-bound lease, challenged continuity/time observation, local inode lease/fence, and current source Meta prefix/configuration. It is revalidated immediately before every immutable seal, plane-root, manifest, and RootSlot write and cannot authorize ordinary service. `PendingShardRestoreCreationGuard` is the separate linear `{certified_bootstrap_projection_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardBootstrapProjectionCertificate>,current_source_lease_projection_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjection>,shard_recovery_publication_guard:ShardRecoveryPublicationGuard,target_manifest_staging_inode_identity_and_filesystem_profile,local_whole_inode_lease,root_publication_sequencer,shard_projection_result_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationProjectionResult<Shard>>,initial_shard_protocol_prepared_consensus_recipes,complete_slot_bitmap,exact_allowed_shard_object_inventory,exact_shard_bridge_and_first_root_recipes,unconsumed}`. It contains no raw continuity record/lease, reservation body/head/observation, barrier import, Meta acquisition bundle, or canonical Meta source-lease record. A long build may accept only a certified same-restore/plan/shard lease-projection refresh for generation `g+1` with `LeaseWindowSuccessorProof`, unchanged authority/inventory/recipient subset, and current Meta prefix; old projections cease authorizing reads at expiry. Otherwise Meta must reissue the complete bootstrap projection.

Direct Local/Meta bridge ordering is canonical imports -> transform/projection -> initial objects -> bridge -> complete all-plane manifest/slot. Sharded ordering is Meta bridge/root -> certified shard bootstrap projections -> shard creation guards/bridges -> ready barriers. Each encoder permits only its role-valid acquisition/projection/current-authority/configuration/checkpoint/floor/initial-state/bridge closure and rejects Genesis, Operational, user work, ordinary receipts/Raft, service advertisement, or a second general command. It uses exclusive staging, full sync, no-replace rename, directory sync, inode revalidation, and exact publication permit/reread, then yields only `RecoveryWriterLeaseGuard`. Double-use, competing restore, lease expiry, missing opener, cross-role authority, omission, crash at every plane/root/external-finalize boundary, and checked-range exhaustion are permanent model/property/crash tests.

`RestoreReconciliationEvidenceRef<Role>` and `RestoreExplicitRetentionOwnerRef<Role>` are generated closed tagged unions over the role-valid `RegisteredStrongRef` targets for identity/allocation, privacy, audit, prepared/decision, cursor/delivery, resource, key, remote retention/trust, history, replay, backup, DP, derived state, and capability invalidation. `RestoreReconciliationRoot<Role>` has exactly one generated slot per activated class; each value is `Pending{reason_code}|Resolved{evidence:RestoreReconciliationEvidenceRef<Role>}|ExplicitlyRetained{owner:RestoreExplicitRetentionOwnerRef<Role>,floor,expiry_or_terminal_rule}|Disabled{exact_fail_closed_policy}`. `RestoreReconciliationCompletionProof<Role>` is `{restore_id,restore_epoch,role_and_group,reconciliation_root_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReconciliationRoot<Role>>,activated_slot_bitmap,resolved_slot_bitmap,explicitly_retained_slot_bitmap,disabled_slot_bitmap,zero_pending_proof,all_explicit_retention_rules_live_proof,complete_transform_projection_coverage_digest,checker_version}`. The four bitmaps are disjoint and exhaustive. `RestoreReconciliationStatus<Role>` is `Pending{unresolved_slot_bitmap}|Complete{proof_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReconciliationCompletionProof<Role>>}`; no nullable proof or inferred readiness exists.

`RestoreContinuityBasis<Role>` is the generated exact role family `Local{basis:DirectoryBound{creation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DirectoryBoundCreationEvidence<Local>>,fixed_local_continuity_digest,target_manifest_inode_identity_and_fence_commitment}|ExternalCas{continuity_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>,head_digest_and_cas_version,recovery_lease_generation_and_lineage_commitment}}|Meta{continuity_record_ref:StrongRef<ClusterIncarnationContinuityRecord>,head_digest_and_cas_version,recovery_lease_generation_and_lineage_commitment}|Shard{continuity_projection_ref:StrongRef<ShardContinuityAuthorityProjection>,source_meta_prefix_and_configuration,projected_head_digest_and_cas_version,recovery_only_member_lease_projection_commitment}`. Meta has no DirectoryBound arm; Shard has no raw continuity record/CAS authority. `RecoveryBridgeSourceLeaseBasis<Role>` is the generated exact `Local{lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Local>>,generation,portable_lease_state_digest}|Meta{lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Meta>>,generation,portable_lease_state_digest}|Shard{lease_projection_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjection>,generation,portable_lease_state_digest,certified_source_meta_prefix_and_configuration}`. Every field byte-matches `RecoveryBridgeAuthority<Role>`.

`RestoreStateCommon<Role>` is `{restore_id,restore_epoch,source_posture,source_manifest_identity,source_backup_incarnation,externally_retired_incarnation_floor,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,target_role_and_group,continuity_basis:RestoreContinuityBasis<Role>,transform_plan_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationTransformPlan<Role>>,projection_result_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationProjectionResult<Role>>,source_lease_basis:RecoveryBridgeSourceLeaseBasis<Role>,target_configuration_and_topology_identity,reconciliation_root_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReconciliationRoot<Role>>,reconciliation_status:RestoreReconciliationStatus<Role>,service_gate:Hidden,state_generation}`.

`RestoreState` is the closed role union `Local{common:RestoreStateCommon<Local>,phase:Reconciling|VisibilityCommitted{activation_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}|ServicePreparedHidden{prepare_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}}|Meta{common:RestoreStateCommon<Meta>,phase:Reconciling|ActivationOrdered{activation_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}|VisibilityCommitted{visibility_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}|ServicePreparedHidden{prepare_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}}|Shard{common:RestoreStateCommon<Shard>,shard_id,phase:Reconciling|ActivationAppliedHidden{activation_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}|VisibilityApplied{visibility_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}|ServicePreparedHidden{prepare_applied_ref:AuthorityAppliedRef}}`. The generated role validator accepts only the matching Local, Meta, or Shard `AuthorityAppliedRef` arm. A ready certificate/barrier may be signed only over `Reconciling + Complete`; it is owned outside `RestoreState` by the certificate ledger after the source root exists. Each applied phase stores only its already identified input command plus assigned order—never the future command record, resulting root, post-apply certificate, or acknowledgement. `ServicePreparedHidden` additionally requires exact visibility evidence but does not clear the state or service gate. No ordinary work, session admission, endpoint advertisement, capability issuance, or result delivery is legal while `restore_state_ref` is present.

`RecoveryBridgeDependencyRef<Role>` is the generated closed union of role-valid recreated signing/wrap/verifier authority, identity/privacy/audit reconciliation-plan, and prepared/cursor/delivery/remote-obligation plan objects. `RecoveryBridgeAuthority<Role>` is the generated role family `Local{direct_creation_authority_ref:StrongRef<RestoreDirectCreationAuthorityRecord<Local>>,source_acquisition_bundle_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceAcquisitionBundle<Local>>,latest_source_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Local>>}|Meta{direct_creation_authority_ref:StrongRef<RestoreDirectCreationAuthorityRecord<Meta>>,source_acquisition_bundle_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceAcquisitionBundle<Meta>>,latest_source_lease_record_ref:StrongRef<RestoreSourceLeaseRecord<Meta>>}|Shard{certified_meta_bootstrap_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardBootstrapProjectionCertificate>,source_meta_prefix_configuration_and_trust_commitment,source_lease_projection_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceLeaseProjection>}`. Only the matching arm is inhabitable.

`InitialRestoreStateRef<Role>` is the generated tag-refined strong reference whose Local, Meta, and Shard concrete validators accept only the matching `RestoreState` role arm in `Reconciling` with `service_gate=Hidden`, complete role-valid common identity, and no future applied phase. `InitialRestoreRegistryRef<Role>` is the generated tag-refined strong reference whose Local/Meta validators accept only `Nonterminal{phase=ActiveHidden{restore_state_ref=the exact InitialRestoreStateRef<Role>},lease_state=Current{latest matching source lease record}}` and whose Shard validator accepts only `ActiveHidden` with the exact initial state, certified source-lease projection, transform/projection, Meta prefix/configuration, and no terminal/pin state. A generic `StrongRef<RestoreState|RestoreRegistry>` cannot inhabit a recovery bridge.

`RecoveryAllocationEpochAuthority<Role>` is the generated closed role family `Local{allocation_claim_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapReservationClaimCanonicalImportRecord<Local,AllocationEpoch>>,bootstrap_applied_use_identity:BootstrapReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Local,AllocationEpoch>,expected_initial_allocator_state_and_spent_range_recipe_commitment}|Meta{allocation_claim_import_ref:StrongRef<PreBootstrapReservationClaimCanonicalImportRecord<Meta,AllocationEpoch>>,bootstrap_applied_use_identity:BootstrapReservationAppliedUseIdentity<Meta,AllocationEpoch>,expected_initial_allocator_state_and_spent_range_recipe_commitment}|Shard{authority_projection_ref:StrongRef<ShardAllocationEpochAuthorityProjection>,expected_initial_allocator_state_recipe_commitment}`. Local/Meta byte-match the exact claim import and the corresponding entry in the pending creation guard; Shard byte-matches only its certified consumer-local slice projection and never encodes a reservation head, receipt, or Local/Meta import.

`RecoveryBridgeSpec<Role>` is the exact role-specialized first-control body `{restore_id,restore_epoch,authority:RecoveryBridgeAuthority<Role>,source_manifest_identity_and_digest,transform_plan_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationTransformPlan<Role>>,projection_result_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationProjectionResult<Role>>,source_lease_basis:RecoveryBridgeSourceLeaseBasis<Role>,target_role_payload:RoleGeneratedRecoveryPayload<Role>,initial_restore_state_ref:InitialRestoreStateRef<Role>,initial_restore_registry_ref:InitialRestoreRegistryRef<Role>,initial_protocol_state_recipe_commitment,target_checkpoint_floor_and_prepared_consensus_body_recipe_commitments,allocation_epoch_authority:RecoveryAllocationEpochAuthority<Role>,dependency_refs[RecoveryBridgeDependencyRef<Role>],dependency_coverage_bitmap,complete_posture_field_slot_plane_and_object_coverage_digest,terminal_audit_gate:StructurallyInapplicable{RecoverySystemAuthority}}`. `RoleGeneratedRecoveryPayload<Local>` is `{target_configuration_ref,assigned_logical_command_origin}`; Meta adds topology and assigned global origin; Shard is `{shard_id,target_configuration_ref,target_topology_epoch,assigned_shard_raft_origin,certified_meta_bootstrap_identity}`. A Shard spec cannot encode Local/Meta external-authority refs.

The spec carries already-authenticated recipe commitments and earlier objects only, never future Protocol/Prepared/Consensus/manifest/slot identities. Its mode/role must match the guard, transform/projection, initial state/registry, and protocol recipe. Initial `RestoreState` and registry are built before the spec, hidden `Reconciling`/`ActiveHidden`, and name neither the spec nor future records. `RecoveryBridgeSpec<Local|Meta>` is consumed only by `PendingRestoreCreationGuard<Role>`; `RecoveryBridgeSpec<Shard>` only by `PendingShardRestoreCreationGuard` and outer `ShardControlAuthority::MetaAuthorized` naming the same certified bootstrap projection. Apply constructs the role payload and real role-valid command record, then the guard builds Protocol, Prepared, Consensus, manifest, and slot roots in universal order. Fresh Genesis alone has no head; restore never validates an old root as current and never creates a headless non-Genesis state.

`RestoreReadyClosureRef<Role>` is a generated closed union over the role-valid checkpoint/vector/floor/history/prepared/certificate/key/derived/remote-obligation/configuration roots, and `RestoreReadyClosureInventory<Role>` is `{role_and_group,sorted_refs[RestoreReadyClosureRef<Role>],exhaustive_coverage_bitmap,inventory_digest}`. No raw `[StrongRef]` bag is legal. For sharded restore, `RestoreShardReadyBarrier {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,restore_epoch,service_visibility_epoch,shard_id,target_domain,exact_global_barrier,target_topology_and_configuration,local_projection_result_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryIncarnationProjectionResult<Shard>>,state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,ready_closure_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReadyClosureInventory<Shard>>,active_meta_projection_root_ref:StrongRef<ActiveMetaProjectionRoot>,reconciliation_completion_proof_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReconciliationCompletionProof<Shard>>,local_root_slot_durability_digest,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}` strongly owns the exact local closure. Its completion proof allows both Resolved and ExplicitlyRetained slots and rejects any Pending slot.

`GlobalRestoreActivationSpec` is `{restore_id,expected_meta_state:WeakStateIdentity,exact_global_barrier,expected_topology_and_meta_configuration,meta_reconciliation_proof_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReconciliationCompletionProof<Meta>>,meta_ready_closure_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReadyClosureInventory<Meta>>,exact_sorted_ready_barrier_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardReadyBarrier>],complete_group_partition_and_authority_commitment,continuity_reservation_digest,recovery_lease_generation,service_visibility_epoch,terminal_audit_gate}`. After apply and the exact audit visibility advance, Meta creates `GlobalRestoreActivationCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,activation_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,exact_global_barrier,topology_and_meta_configuration_identity,sorted_ready_barrier_identities,complete_group_partition_and_authority_commitment,post_apply_global_state_root_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStateRoot>,service_visibility_epoch,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`. `ShardRestoreActivationApplySpec` is `{activation_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalRestoreActivationCertificate>,own_ready_barrier_ref:StrongRef<RestoreShardReadyBarrier>,expected_local_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_topology_configuration_and_remote_trust_selector,exact_typed_restore_projection_payload}` and leaves the shard hidden. Its `ShardControlAuthority` compatibility row requires `MetaAuthorized` with that exact activation CRSR; there is no duplicate authority field in the body. It then emits `RestoreShardActivationAck {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,activation_identity,ready_barrier_identity,post_apply_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,applied_shard_raft_index,exact_global_barrier,service_visibility_epoch,service_gate:Hidden,root_slot_durability_digest,audit_visibility_prefix,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`.

`RestoreVisibilityCommitSpec` is `{restore_id,activation_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,activation_certificate_ref:StrongRef<GlobalRestoreActivationCertificate>,exact_sorted_activation_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardActivationAck>],expected_meta_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_topology_and_configurations,complete_required_shard_commitment,service_visibility_epoch,terminal_audit_gate}`. It makes Meta `VisibilityCommitted` but still service-hidden and, after its audit visibility advance, yields `RestoreVisibilityCommitCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,visibility_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,activation_identity,sorted_activation_ack_identities,topology_and_configuration_commitment,post_apply_global_state_root_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStateRoot>,service_visibility_epoch,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`. Each shard orders `ShardRestoreVisibilityApplySpec {visibility_commit_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreVisibilityCommitCertificate>,own_activation_ack_ref:StrongRef<RestoreShardActivationAck>,expected_local_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,service_visibility_epoch}`; its outer authority row requires `MetaAuthorized` with that visibility CRSR. It becomes `VisibilityApplied` but remains hidden and emits `RestoreShardVisibilityAck {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,visibility_commit_identity,own_activation_ack_identity,post_apply_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,applied_shard_raft_index,service_visibility_epoch,service_gate:Hidden,root_slot_durability_digest,audit_visibility_prefix,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`.

Service opening is acyclic and receipt-first. `GlobalRestoreServicePrepareSpec` is `{restore_id,visibility_commit_ref:StrongRef<RestoreVisibilityCommitCertificate>,exact_sorted_visibility_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardVisibilityAck>],expected_meta_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,exact_topology_configuration_and_endpoint_set,promotion_authority_basis:ExternalCasCataloged{expected_restore_reserved_or_clone_reserved_continuity_digest_and_cas_version,expected_catalog_predecessor_version},service_visibility_epoch,terminal_audit_gate}`. W12/Meta requires `ExternalCasCataloged`; neither DirectoryBound arm is inhabitable. It remains `RecoveryReconciling`, installs Meta `ServicePreparedHidden`, and after its audit visibility advance creates `GlobalRestoreServicePrepareCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,prepare_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,visibility_commit_identity,sorted_visibility_ack_identities,topology_configuration_endpoint_commitment,promotion_authority_basis,service_visibility_epoch,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`.

Each shard orders `ShardRestoreServicePrepareSpec {prepare_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalRestoreServicePrepareCertificate>,own_visibility_ack_ref:StrongRef<RestoreShardVisibilityAck>,expected_local_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_topology_configuration_and_remote_trust_selector,service_visibility_epoch}` under the outer exact `MetaAuthorized` row, remains hidden, and emits `RestoreShardServiceReadyAck {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,prepare_identity,own_visibility_ack_identity,post_prepare_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,applied_shard_raft_index,service_visibility_epoch,service_gate:Hidden,root_slot_durability_digest,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`.

`RestorePromotionAuthorityProfile` is the portable closed union `ExternalCasCataloged{expected_reserved_continuity_head_digest_and_cas_version,expected_catalog_predecessor_digest_and_version,continuity_transition_body_digest,catalog_endpoint_set_commitment}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{directory_bound_creation_evidence_public_commitment,fixed_local_continuity_digest,expected_catalog_predecessor_digest_and_version,catalog_endpoint_set_commitment}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{directory_bound_creation_evidence_public_commitment,fixed_local_continuity_digest,zero_external_catalog_applicability_proof,embedded_endpoint_or_inprocess_admission_commitment}`. The two DirectoryBound arms are Local-only; Sharded/Meta is always ExternalCasCataloged. The signed `RestoreServicePromotionManifest` is the portable closed posture union with common `{restore_id,database_id,database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,target_posture:Local|Sharded,service_visibility_epoch,authority_profile:RestorePromotionAuthorityProfile,signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}` and exactly one body: `Local{local_prepare_evidence:PortableCertificateProjection<LocalRestoreServicePrepareCertificate>,local_configuration_and_endpoint_commitment}` or `Sharded{meta_prepare_evidence:PortableCertificateProjection<GlobalRestoreServicePrepareCertificate>,sorted_shard_ready_evidence[PortableCertificateProjection<RestoreShardServiceReadyAck>],complete_group_topology_configuration_and_endpoint_commitment}`. `PortableCertificateProjection<T>` embeds the target `RemoteObjectIdentity`, complete export projection bytes, canonical configuration/trust evidence bytes, and signatures; it contains no `StrongRef`, `CertifiedRemoteStrongRef`, or consumer-local locator. Nullable cross-posture/profile fields are forbidden.

`RestorePromotionRootSeal<Role>` closes the proof-of-reopen race. After the role's promotion/finalize/open candidate is durably public under the role-valid proof—`PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local|Meta>` for Local/Meta, `ShardStatePublicationCertificate` for Shard—and RootSlot-visible, recovery first builds `RestorePromotionRootSealRecipe<Role>` and completes the exact pin-ledger activation family. Only after `RestoreTerminalPinDurabilityReceipt<Role,Operational>` exists does it construct the seal `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,recipe:RestorePromotionRootSealRecipe<Role>,sealed_role_state_root_ref:StrongRef<RoleStateRoot>,sealed_root_manifest_ref:StrongRef<RootManifest>,typed_physical_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPhysicalInventory<Role,Operational>>,pin_durability_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreTerminalPinDurabilityReceipt<Role,Operational>>,seal_digest}`. The receipt byte-matches the recipe digest and inventory but never contains the seal identity; the recipe excludes the receipt, so the construction graph is publication -> recipe/inventory -> pin activation receipt -> seal. The publication evidence, applied identity, state root, manifest, and winning slot must describe one publication; a Shard seal never claims an audit-visibility advance it does not have. The role-specific ordered seal-install spec installs the seal into still-hidden `OperationalPendingIndependentReopen::Sealed`. Before seal installation the only legal work is exact promotion recovery, source-lease renewal where owned, Local/Meta audit visibility or Shard publication recovery, pin activation/status recovery, evidence canonicalization/key cut, shutdown, or seal installation. Afterward only those operations plus reopen confirmation are legal. Every allowed successor preserves the same Active pin ledger/basis; ordinary admission, unrelated maintenance, checkpoint, GC, reconfiguration, key/policy/schema/data mutation, and a second promotion are rejected.

`RestoreIndependentReopenProof<Role>` opens the seal from an independent fresh process/context, not merely the current mutable head. It binds the seal, reopens every typed pinned object/encoding/key, reconstructs the sealed canonical/prepared/Raft closure, revalidates the exact profile-specific promotion authority receipt and service epoch, and supplies an authenticated proof that every successor from the sealed root to the current pending root is one of the allowed nonsemantic/recovery classes. An ExternalCas proof revalidates catalog and continuity heads; DirectoryBoundCataloged revalidates the catalog row plus inode-bound local promotion receipts; DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog revalidates the inode/fence/slot lineage and zero-catalog-applicability proof. Slot churn or a lease renewal therefore cannot erase the proof basis or legitimize an intervening mutation. The role-specific reopen-confirm command consumes that exact proof and is the only transition to `Operational`; its published successor installs `RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Role>::Operational`. An abandonment finalize/apply instead installs `RestoreRetentionAnchor<Role>::AbandonmentPinPending` from the prebuilt pending owner; only the later role-specific pin-install command, after the abandonment root is durably published and its physical inventory is pinned, replaces that anchor with `RestoreTerminalPinBasis<Role>::Abandoned`. Local/Meta retain the matching terminal pin basis until `RestoreSourceLeaseReleaseFinalizeSpec`; that finalize atomically removes the Local pin owner or starts the Meta participant-release protocol and emits no promotion-shaped authority for an abandoned restore. Each shard retains its matching basis through its operational or abandonment acknowledgement and removes it only through `ShardRestoreTerminalPinReleaseSpec` consuming the matching certified terminal authorization. Release/tombstone floors retain every basis, seal or abandonment evidence, inventory, proof where applicable, and pin receipt until all configured shard release acknowledgements and the source-release terminal are complete. Crash tests cut every seal-build or abandonment-basis build, pin, slot publish, proof open, confirm, source-access cleanup, source-release, and shard-pin-release step, including arbitrary slot convergence and allowed lease renewals.

Promotion is profile-discriminated and service remains hidden throughout. For cataloged profiles the catalog exposes a CAS-linear per-database row head, so unrelated rows never force request rewriting. `ServiceCatalogPromotionReservationReceipt` is the portable result of `CatalogReserveHidden` and binds `{external_request_binding,restore_or_transition_id,target_database_and_security_identity,target_posture,target_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,manifest_digest,expected_target_row_predecessor_digest_and_version,reservation_id,reservation_digest,reserved_target_row_digest_and_version,endpoint_set_commitment,state:ReservedHiddenNonexpiring,authority_signatures}`. The row CAS accepts only the exact predecessor, `AlreadyReservedEquivalent` for the same operation/request, or a semantically equivalent target-row descendant whose target remains absent/hidden with identical reservation semantics. It never accepts an arbitrary rebased row. The reservation is nonexpiring after the profile's pending boundary and blocks every competing endpoint install.

`ServicePromotionExternalOperationKind` is `CatalogReserveHidden|ContinuityEnterPromotionPending|CatalogActivateReserved|ContinuityFinalizeOperational`, specialized by `operation_subject:Restore{restore_id}|RoleTransition{migration_nonce}`. The continuity arms are inhabitable only for ExternalCas. Each external action has a distinct `ServicePromotionExternalOperationRecord<Role>` created by an ordered hidden Semantic authorize spec with exact action-body recipe, operation/idempotency identity, expected predecessor, original promotion authorization/header, resource hold, and no future publication evidence. Only its role-valid publication initializes the universal Armed/SentUnknown/status/receipt-import machine.

The exact profile sequences are:

- `ExternalCasCataloged`: (1) external `CatalogReserveHidden`; (2) external continuity CAS `RestoreReserved|CloneReserved|RoleTransitionReserved -> PromotionCatalogPending`; (3) external `CatalogActivateReserved` to `CatalogActivatedHidden{admission=CompositePromotionReceiptRequired}`; (4) external continuity CAS `PromotionCatalogPending -> Operational{PromotionReceiptRequired}`. The four noninterchangeable results are `CatalogPromotionReservationReceipt`, `PromotionContinuityPendingReceipt`, `CatalogPromotionActivationReceipt`, and `PromotionContinuityFinalizeReceipt`, each with its exact request binding/status/import lineage.
- `DirectoryBoundCataloged`: (1) external `CatalogReserveHidden`; (2) ordered Local `DirectoryBoundEnterPromotionPendingSpec` consumes the current `DirectoryBoundCreationEvidence<Local>`, hidden prepare certificate, reservation receipt, live inode lease/sequencer, and exact current slot, then RootSlot-publishes `DirectoryBoundPromotionPending`; only afterward may `DirectoryBoundPromotionPendingReceipt` be issued with `FenceBoundIrreversibleReceiptHeader`, prior/result state-root and slot evidence, fixed local continuity digest, and catalog-reservation binding; (3) external `CatalogActivateReserved` binds that local pending receipt and remains admission-hidden; (4) ordered Local `DirectoryBoundFinalizeOperationalAuthoritySpec` consumes the activation receipt and current pending state, RootSlot-publishes `DirectoryBoundOperationalReceiptRequired`, then emits distinct `DirectoryBoundOperationalAuthorityReceipt` with exact inode/fence/slot/state lineage. No external continuity action, record, CAS version, status, or receipt is created.
- `DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog`: ordered Local pending and operational-authority transitions exactly as above but bind the manifest's zero-catalog-applicability proof and in-process admission commitment; no catalog action/row/version/receipt and no external continuity action exist.

The pending transition is the irreversible no-downgrade boundary for both DirectoryBound profiles. Before it, cancellation requires exact no-send/no-publication evidence and, for DirectoryBoundCataloged, a durable catalog reservation cancellation/tombstone. After it, recovery has only profile-specific roll-forward authority. Every local transition revalidates the same creation evidence, live inode lease, publication sequencer, fixed continuity digest, hidden service gate, and exact predecessor RootSlot immediately before publication; a member-issued receipt from unreachable staging or a different inode is invalid.

`RestoreServicePromotionReceipt` is the portable closed union `ExternalCasCataloged{restore_id,manifest_digest,service_visibility_epoch,catalog_reservation_lineage,continuity_pending_lineage,catalog_activation_lineage,continuity_finalize_lineage,prior_pending_and_operational_continuity_heads_and_cas_versions,catalog_predecessor_reserved_and_activated_versions,endpoint_set_commitment,complete_four_stage_bijection_digest,authority_signatures}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{restore_id,manifest_digest,service_visibility_epoch,catalog_reservation_lineage,directory_bound_pending_receipt_bytes_and_digest,catalog_activation_lineage,directory_bound_operational_authority_receipt_bytes_and_digest,creation_evidence_public_commitment,fixed_local_continuity_digest,catalog_predecessor_reserved_and_activated_versions,endpoint_set_commitment,complete_profile_stage_bijection_digest,authority_signatures}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{restore_id,manifest_digest,service_visibility_epoch,directory_bound_pending_receipt_bytes_and_digest,directory_bound_operational_authority_receipt_bytes_and_digest,creation_evidence_public_commitment,fixed_local_continuity_digest,zero_catalog_applicability_proof_digest,embedded_admission_commitment,complete_profile_stage_bijection_digest,authority_signatures}`. `ExternalCasRestoreServicePromotionManifestRef` and `ExternalCasRestoreServicePromotionReceiptRef` are generated tag-refined strong references whose validators require respectively the Sharded/ExternalCas manifest arm and the ExternalCasCataloged receipt arm; variant syntax is never used as a reference target. The receipt exists only after every stage of its one profile succeeds, and decoders reject cross-profile optionality or fabricated placeholder versions.

`RestorePromotionConflictAbandonSpec` is profile-specific. ExternalCasCataloged and DirectoryBoundCataloged first install a rooted no-endpoint catalog tombstone/cancel the reservation. ExternalCas then CASes the matching reserved or PromotionCatalogPending continuity head to RestoreAbandoned; DirectoryBound instead orders the inode-fenced local abandonment state and never emits a continuity CAS. Embedded DirectoryBound orders only that local abandonment. No profile can restore the same incarnation to service after its permanent conflict/abandon terminal.

`GlobalRestoreServiceFinalizeSpec {restore_id,prepare_certificate_ref:StrongRef<GlobalRestoreServicePrepareCertificate>,manifest_ref:ExternalCasRestoreServicePromotionManifestRef,promotion_receipt_ref:ExternalCasRestoreServicePromotionReceiptRef,exact_sorted_ready_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardServiceReadyAck>],expected_meta_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_profile_terminal_authority_state,service_visibility_epoch,terminal_audit_gate}` verifies the complete rooted ExternalCas four-stage lineage and clears Meta restore state only into `OperationalPendingIndependentReopen::Unsealed`; request admission and routing remain disabled. After its audit visibility advance it yields `GlobalRestoreServiceFinalCertificate {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,finalize_record_ref:StrongRef<GlobalControlRecord>,manifest_profile_and_receipt_digests,ready_ack_commitment,post_apply_global_state_root_ref:StrongRef<GlobalStateRoot>,service_visibility_epoch,visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Meta>>,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`.

A shard leaves `RecoveryReconciling` only through `ShardRestoreServiceOpenSpec {final_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalRestoreServiceFinalCertificate>,own_ready_ack_ref:StrongRef<RestoreShardServiceReadyAck>,locally_verified_promotion_receipt_ref:ExternalCasRestoreServicePromotionReceiptRef,expected_local_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,service_visibility_epoch}` under its exact outer Meta authority, and that command selects `OperationalPendingIndependentReopen::Unsealed`, not Operational. After the open root has a durable `ShardStatePublicationCertificate`, it builds/installs the role seal above. A fresh process then creates `ShardRestoreIndependentReopenProof`, and `ShardRestoreReopenConfirmSpec {restore_id,final_certificate_ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<GlobalRestoreServiceFinalCertificate>,seal_ref:StrongRef<RestorePromotionRootSeal<Shard>>,independent_reopen_proof_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreIndependentReopenProof>,expected_pending_state:OperationalPendingIndependentReopen::Sealed,service_visibility_epoch}` selects OperationalAwaitingSourceAccessClosure. Only after `ShardRestoreSourceAccessCloseSpec` installs the exact closure may the applied-and-durable ParticipantComplete root produce `RestoreShardOperationalAck {SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,final_certificate_identity,own_ready_ack_identity,reopen_confirm_applied_identity,source_access_close_applied_identity,post_close_state_root_ref:StrongRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>,reopen_publication_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ShardStatePublicationCertificate>,seal_ref:StrongRef<RestorePromotionRootSeal<Shard>>,operational_terminal_pin_basis_ref:OperationalRestoreTerminalPinBasisRef<Shard>,independent_reopen_proof_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreIndependentReopenProof>,source_access_closure_ref:StrongRef<ShardRestoreSourceAccessClosure>,terminalizing_projection_commitment,applied_shard_raft_index,service_visibility_epoch,root_slot_durability_digest,certificate_attempt_ref:StrongRef<CertificateAttemptRecord>,canonical_signature_set}`. The seal supplies the earlier open-root publication evidence; the reopen certificate byte-matches `reopen_confirm_applied_identity`, and the closure/basis/projection byte-match the post-close root. Meta routing waits for the target shard ack; direct shard endpoints reject every pending-reopen or pending-source-closure phase and require the current ExternalCas receipt, final Meta certificate, Operational participant-complete root, and reopen proof.

Meta follows the same pattern after finalize: it installs `RestorePromotionRootSeal<Meta>`, a fresh context produces `MetaRestoreIndependentReopenProof`, and `GlobalRestoreServiceCompletionSpec {restore_id,final_certificate_ref:StrongRef<GlobalRestoreServiceFinalCertificate>,meta_seal_ref:StrongRef<RestorePromotionRootSeal<Meta>>,meta_independent_reopen_proof_ref:StrongRef<MetaRestoreIndependentReopenProof>,exact_sorted_operational_ack_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<RestoreShardOperationalAck>],promotion_receipt_ref:ExternalCasRestoreServicePromotionReceiptRef,expected_restore_registry_state:PromotedAwaitingReopen,expected_meta_pending_state:OperationalPendingIndependentReopen::Sealed,expected_source_lease_generation,terminal_audit_gate}` verifies one ack from every required shard and alone selects Meta Operational. Its audit visibility advance installs `RestoreRetentionAnchor::TerminalPinBasis` with the operational basis and moves the registry to `AwaitingSourceAccessCleanup::Operational`; only the later complete source-access cleanup finalize may enter AwaitingSourceRelease. It neither hides an unready group nor releases the source lease/pins directly.

The exact Local path is independent of sharded evidence. `LocalRestoreReadyCertificate` is `{SameGroupCertificateHeader,restore_id,restore_epoch,service_visibility_epoch,current_hidden_state_root_ref:StrongRef<LogicalStateRoot>,reconciliation_completion_proof_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReconciliationCompletionProof<Local>>,ready_closure_inventory_ref:StrongRef<RestoreReadyClosureInventory<Local>>,current_configuration_ref:StrongRef<ConfigurationState>,current_checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<RecoveryCheckpoint>,current_config_floor_ref:StrongRef<ConfigPayloadFloor>,root_slot_durability_digest,one_digest_signer_lock_ref,quorum_signatures}`. `LocalRestoreActivationSpec` is `{restore_id,ready_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalRestoreReadyCertificate>,expected_local_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_configuration_checkpoint_and_floor,promotion_authority_basis:ExternalCasCataloged{expected_reserved_continuity_digest_and_cas_version}|DirectoryBound{creation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<DirectoryBoundCreationEvidence<Local>>,fixed_local_continuity_digest},service_visibility_epoch,terminal_audit_gate}` and moves only Reconciling -> VisibilityCommitted. After its gap-free release, `LocalRestoreActivationCertificate` owns the hidden root, exact ready evidence, profile basis, and `visibility_certificate_ref:StrongRef<PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local>>`. `LocalRestoreServicePrepareSpec` is `{restore_id,activation_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalRestoreActivationCertificate>,expected_local_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,exact_configuration_and_endpoint_set,promotion_authority_profile_recipe:ExternalCasCataloged{expected_catalog_predecessor_version}|DirectoryBoundCataloged{expected_catalog_predecessor_version}|DirectoryBoundEmbeddedNoCatalog{zero_catalog_applicability_proof},service_visibility_epoch,terminal_audit_gate}` and moves only to ServicePreparedHidden; its gap-free release yields `LocalRestoreServicePrepareCertificate` over the prepare record, hidden root, visibility certificate, endpoint/configuration commitment, exact promotion-authority profile basis, epoch, and signer transcript.

The Local arm of `RestoreServicePromotionManifest` executes exactly its selected profile and produces that profile's composite receipt first. `LocalRestoreServicePromotionSpec {restore_id,prepare_certificate_ref:StrongRef<LocalRestoreServicePrepareCertificate>,manifest_ref:StrongRef<RestoreServicePromotionManifest{target_posture=Local}>,promotion_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreServicePromotionReceipt>,exact_profile_stage_operation_dispatch_or_local_publication_lineage_refs,expected_local_restore_state:WeakStateIdentity,expected_profile_terminal_authority_state,service_visibility_epoch,terminal_audit_gate}` requires manifest/prepare/receipt/profile equality and clears restore state only into `OperationalPendingIndependentReopen::Unsealed` after its exact `PortableSemanticVisibilityCertificate<Local>` is durable; its registry becomes PromotedAwaitingReopen and still owns the source lease. It then installs `RestorePromotionRootSeal<Local>`. The exact generated `RestoreIndependentReopenProof<Role>` schema is the seal-bound proof above, including the allowed-successor-chain proof rather than an unpinned observation of whichever slot happens to be current. A fresh process produces `LocalRestoreIndependentReopenProof`; `LocalRestoreServiceCompletionSpec {restore_id,promotion_record_ref:StrongRef<LogicalCommandRecord>,promotion_receipt_ref:StrongRef<RestoreServicePromotionReceipt>,seal_ref:StrongRef<RestorePromotionRootSeal<Local>>,independent_reopen_proof_ref:StrongRef<LocalRestoreIndependentReopenProof>,expected_restore_registry_state:PromotedAwaitingReopen,expected_pending_state:OperationalPendingIndependentReopen::Sealed,expected_source_lease_generation,terminal_audit_gate}` alone selects Operational; its gap-free release installs `RestoreRetentionAnchor::TerminalPinBasis` with the operational basis, moves the registry to `AwaitingSourceAccessCleanup::Operational`, and yields the completion visibility certificate. Complete source-access cleanup then enters AwaitingSourceRelease; source release uses the ordered operation/dispatch/receipt/finalize protocol above. Completion never directly drops the lease or pin.

Every handshake/session/request/forwarded shard request/transaction/query child/delivery cursor/cross-session capability carries `{cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch}`. Mixed or stale epochs fail before authorization or observation. `RestoreSourceLease` remains live through promotion/finalize/open, independent target reopen, operational acknowledgements, and the role-valid post-open completion audit visibility advance. `CloneNewIdentity` uses the same transform/result/guard/reconciliation/promotion machinery but assigns a new database/security namespace, `K_oid`, continuity domain, current groups/authority, and Audit clone boundary; historical evidence remains carried, old capabilities remain invalid, and source authority is never current target authority. `source_posture == expected_posture` is mandatory for both modes; Local-to-W12 is a later separate role transition.

**ReplayManifest / executable and archive closure.** `ReplayCompleteness` is `Replayable|StructuralReplay|VerifiableIfArtifactsSupplied|AuditOnly` with explicit class lists. `ArchiveObjectIdentity` is nonlocal and never a local reachability edge. `ReplayObjectRef<T>` is exactly `LocalStrong{ref:StrongRef<T>}|CertifiedRemote{ref:CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<T>}|Archived{identity,expected_logical_object_id,expected_canonical_digest}`. CertifiedRemote requires a live matching authority configuration/trust selector/grant and participates in two-sided retention; Archived resolves only through the active registry and never marks local data. Every slot uses `ReplayEvidenceRef<T> = Present{object:ReplayObjectRef<T>}|Unavailable{class,expected identity/digest?,reason}|Redacted{class,commitment?,policy}`. `ReplayLogicalStateEvidence = Local{state:ReplayEvidenceRef<LogicalStateRoot>}|Sharded{global_state:ReplayEvidenceRef<GlobalStateRoot>,topology:ReplayEvidenceRef<TopologyState>,exact_global_prefix,sorted_shard_states[ReplayEvidenceRef<ShardLogicalStateRoot>],complete_partition_commitment}`; a Sharded grade is no stronger than its weakest required group/evidence lease.

`ReplayManifest` binds `{format major/minor,slot schema,database/security namespace/incarnation,logical/commit seq,logical_state:ReplayLogicalStateEvidence,graph bindings,topology/policy/revocation epochs,language/semantic/scalar/numeric profiles,answer requirement/result contract,logical/physical plan IDs,bound/normalized query,typed parameters,replay authority,module/UDF set,external inputs,derived generation snapshots,decision cards/policies/evidence,executable artifacts,structural projection,result encoding/digest,structural completeness,archive requirements,created HLC}`. The generated slot schema derives the maximum grade: Replayable requires every execution-semantic Local or Sharded slot live; structural replay requires its typed projection; supply-later carries all identities/digests; audit/redaction is explicit. Live effective grade may only weaken as any local, certified-remote, or archive trust/lease/authority fails. Legacy Local manifests survive activation unchanged.

`ExecutableArtifactSet` binds `{ exact_binary:ReplayObjectRef | reproducible_build_closure[ReplayObjectRef], source_tree:ReplayObjectRef?, rust_toolchain:ReplayObjectRef,target,cpu_feature_contract,runtime_allocator_config:ReplayObjectRef,vm_jit_compiler_artifacts[ReplayObjectRef],kernel_profile_registry:ReplayObjectRef,platform_abi:ReplayObjectRef,build_recipe:ReplayObjectRef,artifact_digest }`; exactly one executable route must be complete whenever this set is present. `StructuralReplayProjection` binds its reproduced event/data-shape classes, canonical projection schema, required evidence-slot classes, omitted/redacted classes, expected commitments, and output verifier. `ReplayArchiveRecord` is immutable `{ archive_id,generation,predecessor_record_digest:WeakDigest?,security_domain,resolver_profile_oid,authenticated_external_locator,archive_closure_digest:WeakDigest,sorted_object_inventory[{identity:ArchiveObjectIdentity,expected_logical_object_id,expected_canonical_digest,length}],availability_commitment,time_authority_profile_oid,lease_not_before,lease_not_after,status:Active|Revoked|Expired,signer_set_epoch,signatures }`; its signature transcript covers the profile and both bounds. Its inventory/closure explicitly excludes the record itself, `ReplayRegistry`, and the locally rooted manifest, so archive and manifest identities can be constructed in the order `external evidence inventory -> archive record -> manifest -> registry` without a digest cycle. `ReplayRegistry` maps `manifest_id -> {manifest:StrongRef<ReplayManifest>,lease/status}` and `archive_id -> latest_record:StrongRef<ReplayArchiveRecord>`. Renewal creates a logical-command-sequenced successor after re-verifying the entire inventory and validating fresh Usable evidence. Revocation/expiry never asserts deletion; the ordered status successor retains the exact subject-bound time evidence when expiry is its cause, then consensus removes the registry availability edge, after which ordinary GC may release otherwise-unrooted local manifest objects/records. A manifest is advertised `Replayable` only while every required local object opens and every archive requirement resolves through an active, time-validated, trusted record with a matching inventory/closure digest. Resolver credentials and replay contents require scoped `Replay` authority; capability-safe lookup does not disclose whether another authority's manifest/archive exists.

**Authorization, durable capabilities, DP, resources, and audit records.** `MutationPermitRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,issuer,policy_semantics_ref:StrongRef<PolicySemantics>,exact_role_bearing_read_bindings,role_free_write_coordinates,allowed_snapshot_scope,allowed_query_operator_disclosure_scope,result_answer_contract_ceiling,operation_scope,presentation_binding:ServerInternal|SessionBound{session_binding,auth_generation}|ChannelBound{transport_exporter_digest},nonce,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expiry,signer_epoch,signature_or_mac_transcript,body_digest}`. `ReadPermitRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the distinct no-write format `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,issuer,policy_semantics_ref:StrongRef<PolicySemantics>,exact_role_bearing_read_bindings,allowed_snapshot_scope,allowed_query_operator_disclosure_scope,result_answer_contract_ceiling,presentation_binding,nonce,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,expiry,signer_epoch,signature_or_mac_transcript,body_digest}`; it has no write coordinate, mutation capability, or empty-effect convention. The header supplies namespace/incarnation/service/tenant/principal/ceiling/policy/revocation exactly once, and every permit transcript covers the complete referenced binding, scope, presentation binding, time profile, and closed-open window.

`AuthorityPermitRef<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is exactly `ReadOnly{permit_ref:StrongRef<ReadPermitRecord<Role>>}|ReadWrite{permit_ref:StrongRef<MutationPermitRecord<Role>>}`. `MutationPermitReadProjection<Role>` is `{permit_ref:StrongRef<MutationPermitRecord<Role>>,permit_body_digest,authority_binding_body_digest,exact_role_bearing_read_bindings,allowed_snapshot_scope,allowed_query_operator_disclosure_scope,result_answer_contract_ceiling,presentation_binding,projection_digest}` and may only narrow the referenced mutation permit. `ReadCapablePermitRef<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `DedicatedRead{permit_ref:StrongRef<ReadPermitRecord<Role>>}|MutationReadProjection{projection_ref:StrongRef<MutationPermitReadProjection<Role>>}`. `ImportedReadCapablePermitEvidence` is the generated shard-portable exact union carrying the source permit/projection public identity, complete read projection, exact `ShardAuthorityBindingProjection`, source configuration/prefix/retention evidence, and signatures; a permit identity plus digest or bare security projection is insufficient. Clients never serialize or choose local `PermitRecord` refs: FGP/embedded calls present credentials, token bytes, and their required session/channel binding, the trusted verifier persists the role-local record, and only a nonconstructible verified typed ref crosses into database code. Use requires matching fresh `TimeValidationEvidence::Usable`, the exact current-or-held authority binding, and a linear guard. `AuthorizationLease` is the exact profiled signed consensus-domain/service-epoch/snapshot-range/read-binding/query-disclosure/answer-ceiling lease defined in §14.3, not an inferred extension. Rust exposes only verified nonconstructible wrappers.

`AuditCompletenessProfile` is `{profile_oid,schema_version,audit_domain_id,effective_audit_epoch,exact_operation_rules[AuditOperationTag -> Required|NotRequired],redaction_contract_oid,signer_set_epoch,signatures}`. `AuditRequirementDecisionRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is the same-group immutable `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,profile_ref:StrongRef<AuditCompletenessProfile>,audit_domain_id,effective_audit_epoch,operation_tag,request_basis_commitment,requirement:Required|NotRequired,evaluator_version}`. Shards consume only the exact imported decision/claim projection certified from Meta; they cannot originate this object. `AuditCoverageClaimRef` is the role-aware closed union `Local{claim_ref:StrongRef<LocalAuditTicketClaimRecord>}|Meta{claim_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketClaimRecord>}` whose arm must match the containing domain. `NonRequiredAuditEvidence` is `ProfileExempt{decision_ref:StrongRef<AuditRequirementDecisionRecord>}|CoveredByParentClaim{claim_ref:AuditCoverageClaimRef,coverage_rule_id,covered_body_digest}|LifecycleScaffolding{decision_ref:StrongRef<AuditRequirementDecisionRecord>,registered_scaffolding_spec_tag,scaffolding_body_digest,parent_operation_claim_commitment?,compatibility_row_id}`. The LifecycleScaffolding arm is constructible only when the profile's exact operation rule says NotRequired for that internal registration/admission/freeze step and the generated row proves it cannot terminalize or expose the protected semantic operation; it cannot excuse a later commit, abort, read release, control mutation, or result publication. `LifecycleScaffoldingNotRequired` is the exact inline value `{evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence::LifecycleScaffolding}` rather than a magic enum literal. Every evidence form's authority binding, decision/claim, operation tag, request/body basis, audit epoch, policy, and revocation state must match the containing object. `StructuralAuditExemption` is a generated inline singleton `{spec_tag,system_authority_class,compatibility_row_id}` constructible only for the exact registered recovery/audit/bootstrap tag; the associated type is uninhabited for every ordinary/user tag.

`AuthorizationAuditGate` is the same-group-only union `StructurallyInapplicable{exemption:StructuralAuditExemption}|NotRequired{evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence}|Required{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>}`; an encoder may place Required only in an object whose consensus domain owns that ticket. `AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>` is `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,decision_body}` where `decision_body` is the closed tag `Mutation{permit_ref:StrongRef<MutationPermitRecord<Local>>,permit_scope_digest,final_effect_digest,write_and_hidden_cascade_commitment,decision,authorization_audit_gate}` | `ReadAccess{access_admission_ref:StrongRef<ReadAccessAdmissionRecord<Role>>}` | `ReadRelease{release_decision_ref:StrongRef<ReadResultReleaseDecisionRecord<Role>>}` | `Lifecycle{lifecycle_decision_ref:StrongRef<LifecycleAuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>>}` | `GlobalMutation{global_decision_ref:StrongRef<GlobalAuthorizationDecisionRecord>}`. The outer role arm must byte-match the selected child and its permit/admission/release record; tenant/principal/policy/revocation are never separately forgeable projections. The generated role matrix permits Mutation only in Local, GlobalMutation only in Meta, and ReadAccess/ReadRelease/Lifecycle only in the exact role/scope variant defined below. A Shard decision necessarily carries `AuthorityBindingFor<Shard>::Shard`; it cannot encode an `AuthorityBoundHeader<Shard>`.

`ImportedOperationAuditAdmissionEvidence` is the closed union `NotRequired{source_registration_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,registration_field_tag,evidence_projection:ImportedNonRequiredAuditEvidence}|Claimed{source_registration_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,registration_field_tag,ticket_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,claim_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,owner:AuditTicketOwner::Operation,request_basis_commitment}`. `ImportedStatementAuditAdmissionEvidence` is the distinct union `NotRequired{source_statement_registration_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,statement_field_tag,evidence_projection:ImportedNonRequiredAuditEvidence}|Claimed{source_statement_registration_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,statement_field_tag,ticket_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,claim_identity:RemoteObjectIdentity,owner:AuditTicketOwner::Statement,request_basis_commitment}`. `ImportedNonRequiredAuditEvidence` embeds the exact profile/decision or parent-claim projection, source field tag, audit epoch, policy/revocation, coverage rule, and signatures; it is not an opaque digest. `ImportedReadAuditEvidence` is exactly `TerminalAttempt{operation:ImportedOperationAuditAdmissionEvidence}|Statement{operation:ImportedOperationAuditAdmissionEvidence,statement:ImportedStatementAuditAdmissionEvidence}`. No generic Owner parameter or independent gate commitment exists.

Read authorization is split at the observation boundary. `ReadAccessCommon<Role>` is `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,permit_scope_digest,registered_snapshot_and_workspace_scope,exact_read_graph_bindings,descriptor_visibility_projection_ref:StrongRef<DescriptorVisibilityProjection>,allowed_query_operator_disclosure_scope,result_answer_contract_ceiling,decision}`; the generated Role matrix fixes exactly one authority arm and never permits a nullable or mismatched variant. `ReadAccessAdmissionRecord<Role>` is the closed posture/scope union `LocalTerminal{common:ReadAccessCommon<Local>,execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::LocalTerminal,permit_ref:ReadCapablePermitRef<Local>,operation_audit:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}|LocalStatement{common,execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::LocalStatement,permit_ref:ReadCapablePermitRef<Local>,operation_audit:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,statement_audit:LocalStatementAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}|MetaTerminal{common:ReadAccessCommon<Meta>,execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::MetaTerminal,permit_ref:ReadCapablePermitRef<Meta>,operation_audit:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}|MetaStatement{common,execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::MetaStatement,permit_ref:ReadCapablePermitRef<Meta>,operation_audit:OperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,statement_audit:StatementAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}|ShardTerminal{common:ReadAccessCommon<Shard>,execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::ShardTerminal,permit:ImportedReadCapablePermitEvidence,operation_audit:ImportedOperationAuditAdmissionEvidence}|ShardStatement{common,execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::ShardStatement,permit:ImportedReadCapablePermitEvidence,operation_audit:ImportedOperationAuditAdmissionEvidence,statement_audit:ImportedStatementAuditAdmissionEvidence}`. Scope refs, complete authority binding/projection, and audit owners must byte-match; Local terminal and Local statement are not substitutable. The Shard gate is derived exactly from tagged imported admission and is not locally reissued. Only this admission can create `AuthorizedSnapshot`/secure-view cursors, and it contains no read witness, realized result, or result digest.

`ReadResultReleaseCommon<Role>` is `{release_authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,access_admission_ref:StrongRef<ReadAccessAdmissionRecord<Role>>,nonwidening_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuthorityNonwideningProof>,read_routing_or_local_view_commitment,workspace_generation_and_overlay_commitment,read_witness_commitment,realized_result_answer_contract,disclosure_contract_digest,canonical_result_digest,release_decision}`. The release binding may equal the admission binding or be a demonstrably narrower current binding, never wider; the generated Role matrix fixes the authority arm. `ReadResultReleaseDecisionRecord<Role>` is the closed union `LocalTerminal{execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::LocalTerminal,common:ReadResultReleaseCommon<Local>,operation_audit:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,release_audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}|LocalStatement{execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::LocalStatement,common,operation_audit:LocalOperationAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,statement_audit:LocalStatementAuditAdmission::Claimed|NotRequired,release_audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}|ShardTerminal{common:ReadResultReleaseCommon<Shard>,imported_audit:ImportedReadAuditEvidence::TerminalAttempt}|ShardStatement{common,imported_audit:ImportedReadAuditEvidence::Statement}`. `GlobalReadAuthorizationDecisionRecord` is the Meta release object `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Meta>,execution_scope:ReadExecutionScope::MetaTerminal|MetaStatement,access_admission_ref:StrongRef<ReadAccessAdmissionRecord<Meta>>,audit_evidence:MetaReadAuditEvidence,read_routing_certificate_ref:StrongRef<ReadParticipantRoutingCertificate>,sorted_shard_release_decision_refs[CertifiedRemoteStrongRef<ReadResultReleaseDecisionRecord<Shard>>],complete_shard_release_decision_commitment,workspace_and_witness_commitment,result_answer_contract,canonical_result_digest,decision,release_audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}`. The exact order is permit/imported projection -> access admission -> authorized traversal into buffered bytes -> result-release decision -> event/resolution/terminal gate -> publication/read close -> external audit release -> Protocol visibility/activation -> guarded output. A wrong Local Terminal/Statement owner, raw Meta permit/ticket/claim/registration/workspace ref in a shard object, wrong binding/domain/configuration/grant/field tag/owner, swapped claim, access-after-result construction, widening release, or illegal scope cross-product is unencodable and has a negative golden fixture.

`LifecyclePresentedPermitRef<Role>` is a generated associated type: Local contains only `AuthorityPermitRef<Local>`, Meta only `AuthorityPermitRef<Meta>`, and Shard is uninhabited. `LifecycleAuthoritySource<Role>` is the generated closed union `PresentedPermit{permit_ref:LifecyclePresentedPermitRef<Role>}|RegisteredAuthority{registration_ref:RoleExactRegistrationRef,current_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,nonwidening_proof_ref:StrongRef<AuthorityNonwideningProof>}|PresentedCapability{validation_ref:StrongRef<DurableCapabilityValidationEvidence>,capability_kind}|AuthenticatedSession{session_authorization_ref:StrongRef<SessionAuthorizationRecord<Role>>,request_digest}`; only role-valid arms inhabit each operation row. `LifecycleOperation` is exactly `RegisterAttempt|PrepareAdmissionScaffolding|RequestTerminalAbort{stage:Active|PrepareAdmitted}|StatusLookup{registered_status_kind}|CapabilityManagement{registered_kind}`. The `GlobalControlCommand(kind=CancelActiveAttempt)` and Local `TxnAbortSpec` both require `RequestTerminalAbort{stage:Active}`; admitted Local/Meta abort requires `stage:PrepareAdmitted`. `LifecycleEffectClass<Operation>` is a generated associated closed tag: registration and preparation use their exact scaffolding classes; terminal abort uses `TerminalAbortNoEffectReleaseOnly{stage,registered_result_workspace_conflict_and_hold_disposition_schema}`; status is read-only projection; and capability management is its exact rotate/attenuate/revoke class. There is no nullable permit or catch-all maintenance class. `LifecycleAuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>` is the pre-result form `{authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,operation:LifecycleOperation,authority_source:LifecycleAuthoritySource<Role>,sequence_neutral_spec_body_digest,requested_snapshot_or_scope_commitment,effect_class:LifecycleEffectClass<operation>,operation_audit_admission_commitment?,decision,authorization_audit_gate:AuthorizationAuditGate}`. The generated Role/operation matrix fixes every authority/source/effect arm, and the source, requested scope, audit evidence, lifecycle stage, and containing command must byte-match. RegisterAttempt uses a PresentedPermit; PrepareAdmission uses `PrepareAdmissionScaffolding` with exact NotRequired proof while its spec embeds the one GlobalMutation decision for complete effects; RequestTerminalAbort uses RegisteredAuthority plus the exact authenticated client request or the distinct administrative record; status and capability management require their exact presented capability/session or registered-authority source. Protocol/system maintenance uses its registered structural system-authority command arm, never Lifecycle. ReadClose uses GlobalRead; each shard basis uses its own Read decision. A mutation/read/status decision, scalar epoch pair, fabricated zero effect, wrong-stage abort, or `StatusOrMaintenance` alias cannot substitute.

`TerminalAuditGate` is the same-group-only union `StructurallyInapplicable{exemption:StructuralAuditExemption}|NotRequired{evidence:NonRequiredAuditEvidence}|Required{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>,terminal_resolution_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketResolution>}`. A required gate is encodable only in the ticket-owning consensus domain and only when all three objects and the earlier authorization gate agree on namespace/domain/profile/principal/request; the resolution owns the event and its typed outcome matches the command/spec. The sole Local `TxnOutcomePreparationRecord` body is the PrepareAdmitted CAS schema in the Local transaction section; `Absent` and `Submitted` tags are unregistered. W12 exclusively uses the separate gate-free `GlobalTxnOutcomePreparationRecord`; no object is reinterpreted or wrapped as the other kind, and only the later `GlobalTxnCommand` carries W12's terminal gate. `DurableCapabilityVerifierKey {issuer_id,key_id,key_epoch,algorithm,public_or_protected_verifier_material,time_authority_profile_oid,not_before,verify_through,format_versions[],rotation_predecessor?,status}` is Chronicle-sequenced and strongly retained until no live capability can require it; retirement requires exact Expired evidence for its registered subject/action. Each capability uses the exact common header/body/MAC format, names this key, and is never reinterpreted across versions or visibility epochs.

The sole DP schemas and transition tags are those in §12.7. Their construction DAG is normative: `DpRequestKey -> DpSeedMaterial/ciphertext/wraps -> DpSeedEnvelope -> DpTransitionSpec::Prepare -> command -> Prepared record -> payload/record/root`; then local typed owners -> `PortablePrivacyChargeRequest -> PrivacyChargeOperationRecord -> DpTransitionSpec::ArmCharge -> command -> ChargeRequested record -> payload/record/root -> ExternalPrivacyChargeSendGuard -> external tombstone/successor roots -> returned reference-free PrivacyContinuityRecord -> ExternalPrivacyChargeReceipt`; then `PrivacyContinuityImportSpec -> command -> DpChargeBinding(s) -> successor request records -> PrivacyLedgerState/DpRequestIndex -> payload/record/root`; then compute-lease basis -> Start/Reclaim -> command -> applied lease/Computing record -> payload/record/root; and finally `DpOutputCandidate`/manifest -> CommitResult -> command -> ordinary result lease/resource transfer -> independent retention -> DpCommittedOutput -> ResultCommitted record -> both indexes -> payload/record/root. G0 rejects every inversion, portable object containing a local ref, or future edge.

`PrivacyLedgerState<Role>`, `DpRequestIndex<Role>`, and every state-shaped `DpRequestRecord<Role>` use exact typed refs from §12.7; `weak_predecessor_identity` is nonretaining. `DpRequestRecord` has no Delivered/Acknowledged/Released state and no standalone encrypted-result arm. Local and Meta payloads carry exactly `privacy_ledger_root:StrongRef<PrivacyLedgerState<Role>>`, `privacy_continuity_record_ref:StrongRef<PrivacyContinuityRecord>`, `dp_request_index_root:StrongRef<DpRequestIndex<Role>>`, and their ordinary role-valid result-delivery index. The result owner unions add exactly one noninterchangeable DpResult arm. Shard payloads carry only certified DP execution grants/fragments and their retention floor—never a privacy ledger, seed, or noise generator. Checkpoint vectors, configuration floors, backup/restore, key scans, GC, role transition, and service-epoch transition inventory every live seed, receipt, request, fragment, and independently retained result.

`AuditTicket` is `{database_security_namespace_id,audit_domain_id,cluster_incarnation_provenance,tenant,principal_scope,ticket_sequence,event_class,request_digest,completeness_profile_oid,issued_at,sequencer_epoch,signature}`. The sole `SequenceNeutralAuditEventBody`, `SequenceNeutralAuditResolutionBody`, semantic plan, and concrete attempt schemas are those in the audit construction contract above; Appendix A registers those exact body hashes and no duplicate inline body. Final `AuditEventRecord` is `{event_body_ref:StrongRef<SequenceNeutralAuditEventBody>,ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,event_attempt_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventSigningAttemptPlan>,canonical_signing_transcript_digest,event_lock_refs[StrongRef<AuditEventSignerLock>],event_signature_share_refs[StrongRef<AuditEventSignatureShare>],signer_subset_bitmap,threshold,canonical_aggregate_or_signature_set}`. The body owns the authorization/outcome/attested-time/stable-ticket facts; every repeated ticket/plan/digest field byte-matches it. Lock/share arrays are sorted by the concrete attempt's `(signer_id,signing_key_id,signer_set_epoch,key_epoch)` order and are an exact bijection. The signing transcript binds terminal plan ID, concrete attempt ID, event body digest, and signer identity/key/epochs. Transaction/control semantic basis excludes capsule/command/certificate identity, terminal gate, assigned sequence/HLC/marker/audit-chain position, and mutable external continuity head while still committing every stable effect/condition/security input. Mutation fixtures change each included field and each forbidden exclusion independently.

`AuditTicketResolution` is `{resolution_body_ref:StrongRef<SequenceNeutralAuditResolutionBody>,terminal_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalSigningPlan>,resolution_attempt_plan_ref:StrongRef<AuditResolutionSigningAttemptPlan>,ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,terminal:Completed{event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>}|Denied{event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>}|Failed{event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>}|Abandoned{event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>,typed_no_effect_reason},canonical_signing_transcript_digest,resolution_lock_refs[StrongRef<AuditResolutionSignerLock>],resolution_signature_share_refs[StrongRef<AuditResolutionSignatureShare>],signer_subset_bitmap,threshold,canonical_aggregate_or_signature_set}`. The body/terminal/event/plan/attempt must agree exactly; lock/share arrays form the concrete resolution attempt's exact bijection. Attestation time comes only from the pre-order body and is not canonical logical HLC. Apply verifies the later command/result as the deterministic extension of stable committed bases. No final event/resolution body points back into a plan that already embeds it, so the durable strong graph remains acyclic.

`AuditOutcomeRecord` is constructed only by exact `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Local|Meta>` maintenance apply and is `{domain:{database_security_namespace_id,audit_domain_id,cluster_incarnation,group_role:Local|Meta},terminal_attempt_visible_ref:StrongRef<AuditTerminalAttemptRecord::VisibilityReleased>,ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>,resolution_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketResolution>,external_resolution_batch_ref:StrongRef<AuditExternalResolutionBatch>,external_batch_cas_receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuditExternalBatchCasReceipt>,batch_member_ordinal,pipeline_order,advance_input_ref:AuditVisibilityAdvanceInputRef,assigned_maintenance_index,candidate_state_identity:WeakStateIdentity,outcome_commitment}`. The terminal attempt passes through `ExternalBatchCasCommitted` for the same batch/receipt/member before its `VisibilityReleased` successor, and both objects name the same already-built advance spec/maintenance command plus assigned scalar index—not a future applied record. Deterministic construction is maintenance input/command -> per-entry VisibilityReleased attempt successors -> AuditOutcomeRecords/protected-output release successors -> visible outcome/delivery indexes -> successor pipeline/protocol state -> maintenance applied record -> RootManifest. Activation objects are absent from this DAG and are constructed only by later independent maintenance; no object points forward and no semantic payload/result owns the protocol root that owns it. Local/Global semantic outcome state retains the terminal gate/claim commitment; status and compaction select the exact protocol-plane outcome through a generated release proof, never a fictitious strong edge inserted into the earlier candidate payload.

`AuditContinuityRecord` is the external reference-free `{database_security_namespace_id,audit_domain_id,audit_epoch,highest_ticket_sequence,ticket_index_commitment,unresolved_ticket_commitment,audit_chain_head_digest,predecessor_digest,external_registry_id,cas_version,status:Complete{profile_oid}|PermanentGap{latest_transition_bytes_and_digest,current_profile_oid},last_transition:TicketIssued{ticket_digest}|TicketsResolvedBatch{role_and_group,first_pipeline_order,last_pipeline_order,batch_digest,ordered_resolution_commitment,last_candidate_root_digest:WeakDigest}|CompletenessChanged{transition_bytes_and_digest},signer_set_epoch,threshold_signatures}`. A single ticket is the canonical one-member TicketsResolvedBatch form. The registry retains authenticated ticket/event/resolution/transition bytes or authenticated resolver refs. `AuditExternalCasReceipt` is built **after** a TicketIssued or CompletenessChanged returned continuity record and binds `{external_registry_id,prior_head_digest,returned_head_bytes_and_digest,prior_cas_version,returned_cas_version,transition_kind,subject_digest,registry_signature_set}`; `AuditExternalBatchCasReceipt` is the noninterchangeable nonempty contiguous-resolution-batch counterpart with the exact batch identity. Neither receipt nor continuity record contains a local reference, and a continuity record never points forward to either receipt.
