Exact `AuditTicketAdmissionSpec` is `{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,expected_prior_continuity_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>,expected_prior_cas_version,returned_continuity_ref:StrongRef<AuditContinuityRecord>,external_cas_receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuditExternalCasReceipt>}`. Its validator requires the direct TicketIssued successor, exact sequence increment, exact ticket/unresolved addition, matching receipt/CAS versions, and no unrelated change; recovery uses the same validator. The only visibility owner is the separately addressable Local/Meta protocol state and pipeline; shard groups never originate one. Exact `AuditTerminalSpec` is `{ticket_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicket>,expected_pending_ticket_digest:WeakDigest,event_ref:StrongRef<AuditEventRecord>,terminal_resolution_ref:StrongRef<AuditTicketResolution>}`; it is the non-mutating/pre-effect terminal path and rejects every scope/outcome mismatch. Transaction preparation and each state-changing semantic control carry the same triple through `TerminalAuditGate::Required`. External resolution uses `AuditExternalResolutionBatch`/receipt, and the sole ordered visibility input is the **maintenance** `AuditVisibilityAdvanceSpec<Local|Meta>`; there is no `AuditContinuityMirrorSpec`, semantic visibility command, batch-spec command, active-fence digest, singular candidate-import command, or fence-removal transition. Its validator enforces the gap-free/direct-successor/bijection relation, updates protocol continuity/outcomes and protected outputs to ReleasedAwaitingActivation, and changes public visibility only through the manifest-owned prefix. `ProtectedOutputActivationSpec` is a later, visibility-neutral maintenance input. Portable `AuditCompletenessTransitionRequest`, local `AuditCompletenessTransitionOperationRecord<Role>`, reference-free returned `AuditContinuityRecord`/receipt, and semantic `AuditCompletenessTransitionSpec<Role>` retain the exact pre-CAS/direct-successor/import schemas above. Admission, recovery, and completeness semantic transitions use StructurallyInapplicable gates; visibility and activation maintenance use distinct system-authority arms; terminal consumes the existing ticket.

`AuditCloneBoundary` is `{source_database_security_namespace_id,source_audit_domain_id,source_continuity_head_digest,target_database_security_namespace_id,target_audit_domain_id,clone_manifest_ref,retained_source_evidence_contract,source_signatures,target_signatures}` and never rewrites source tickets into the target domain. `AuditSegment` is a contiguous signed range with previous-chain digest, ticket/resolution ranges, event records, redaction profile, signing epoch, and external-anchor ref. `AuditTicketIndex` makes every issued ticket pending or uniquely resolved and retains its event, external receipt, and local import status across recovery. `AuditCheckpoint` commits retained ticket/event floors and heads, unresolved-ticket set, continuity/gap heads, signer-rotation chain, and anchor. Missing external evidence disables completeness-gated operations; it never silently weakens the advertised profile.

**WitnessPolicy / TransparencyCheckpoint.** `WitnessPolicy` is `{format_version,log_id,security_domain_id,policy_epoch,claim_class:EquivocationDetection|NonEquivocation,ordered_witnesses[{witness_id,key_id,verification_key,endpoint_identity,optional_cross_log_authority}],n,threshold_t,byzantine_bound_f,signature_profile,consistency_proof_profile,durable_head_state_profile,anti_rollback_profile,authenticated_gossip_profile:None|Required{transport_auth_profile,peer_identity_registry,checkpoint_message_schema,replay_window,evidence_retention_floor,maximum_convergence_delay},activation_log_size,maximum_checkpoint_age,cross_log_policy:None|Required{ordered_authorities,threshold},rotation:Genesis|JointSuccessor{predecessor_policy_ref:StrongRef<WitnessPolicy>}}`. Canonical validation requires exactly `n` unique ordered witnesses, `0 <= f < t <= n`, supported signature/consistency/persistence profiles, and—before `claim_class=NonEquivocation` is usable—`2t > n + f`. A detection claim that relies on gossip requires the complete authenticated profile; an unnamed/best-effort channel proves nothing. Each honest witness durably stores `{log_id,security_domain_id,policy_oid,last_size,last_root,anti_rollback_counter}` before acknowledging a signature; it signs only a verified append-only consistency extension, never two roots at one size or non-extending histories. Rollback or loss of this state disables that witness until an authenticated recovery/rotation procedure; it cannot silently start fresh.

`TransparencyCrossLogReceipt` is the reference-free signed `{authority_id,authority_key_id,source_log_id,source_security_domain_id,source_policy_identity_and_digest,source_checkpoint_digest,source_log_size,source_root,target_log_id,target_policy_identity_and_digest,target_log_size,target_inclusion_commitment,target_inclusion_proof,target_checkpoint_digest,target_checkpoint_consistency_proof,receipt_time_authority_profile_oid,receipt_window:{not_before:AuthorityInstant,expires_at:AuthorityInstant},canonical_receipt_digest,signature}`. The target inclusion leaf is exactly the domain-separated source checkpoint digest plus source log/domain/policy/size/root; it provides causality without comparing clocks from different authorities.

`TransparencyCheckpoint` is `{format_version,mode:LocalRollbackDetection|WitnessedEquivocationDetection{witness_policy_ref:StrongRef<WitnessPolicy>}|WitnessedNonEquivocation{witness_policy_ref:StrongRef<WitnessPolicy>},log_size,global_mmr_root,branch_head_commitment_root,security_domain_commitment,previous_checkpoint_digest:WeakDigest,consistency_proof_from_previous,issuance_time_authority_profile_oid,issuance_window:{not_before:AuthorityInstant,expires_at:AuthorityInstant},operator_key_id,operator_signature,sorted_witness_signatures[{witness_id,key_id,signature}],cross_log_receipt_refs[StrongRef<TransparencyCrossLogReceipt>],rotation_proof:None|JointActivation{old_policy_ref:StrongRef<WitnessPolicy>,new_policy_ref:StrongRef<WitnessPolicy>,old_sorted_signatures[],new_sorted_signatures[]}}`. Every signature covers the domain-separated canonical checkpoint body excluding all signature arrays. A witnessed mode requires its policy identity, threshold of distinct configured keys, and any required cross-log receipts; freshness is never inferred from an unprofiled `issued_at` scalar.

Local `TransparencyCheckpointValidationRecord<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,checkpoint_ref:StrongRef<TransparencyCheckpoint>,checkpoint_freshness_basis_ref:StrongRef<TransparencyCheckpointFreshnessBasis>,checkpoint_time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,sorted_cross_log_validations[{receipt_ref:StrongRef<TransparencyCrossLogReceipt>,freshness_basis_ref:StrongRef<CrossLogTransparencyFreshness>,time_validation_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>,target_checkpoint_verification_evidence_ref}],policy_threshold_and_consistency_verification_digest,validated_claim_class,body_digest}`. The two generated time-basis schemas from §16 bind the exact checkpoint/receipt bodies and their windows; they cannot be replaced by wall time, logical HLC, or a raw age calculation.

Canonical cross-log validation requires `1 <= cross_log_policy.threshold <= count(unique ordered authorities)`; at least that many receipts come from distinct configured authority/key pairs, and every accepted receipt's source policy/log/security domain/checkpoint digest/size/root equals the current checkpoint exactly. Its target inclusion proof and target checkpoint consistency proof must verify under the named target policy/log/size, the inclusion leaf must causally contain the exact source checkpoint digest, and both the source checkpoint and each receipt/target checkpoint must have independently usable profiled freshness evidence in their own time domains. No validator orders or subtracts instants across domains/epochs. Duplicates, foreign source roots, stale evidence, missing target-checkpoint verification, or self-asserted target sizes fail. `JointActivation` additionally requires: the old policy equals the currently active policy; `new.rotation == JointSuccessor{predecessor=old}`; old/new log and security-domain identities are equal; `new.policy_epoch > old.policy_epoch`; checkpoint mode names the new policy; `log_size >= new.activation_log_size`; and the checkpoint body has valid distinct-key thresholds under both old and new policies. Only after that exact activation checkpoint may successors use the new policy alone.

The branch commitment is domain-separated and reveals no plain branch identity/head; authorized clients receive scoped inclusion/consistency proofs under the metadata-declassification policy, and mutually hidden branch sets use distinct security-domain logs. `LocalRollbackDetection` proves only consistency relative to a client's cached head. `WitnessedEquivocationDetection` promises durable evidence once conflicting heads reach the declared gossip/cross-log path. Only `WitnessedNonEquivocation` with a valid intersection-safe policy, honest durable-head assumption, consistency proofs, joint rotation, and freshness may claim split-view prevention. If any premise fails, verification fails closed or downgrades the advertised claim to detection/local scope; later gossip is never relabeled as prevention. **DecisionPolicyEpoch** is `{policy_id,version,scope,logical_effect_class,pinned_table_oid,fallback_oid,evidence_refs[],previous_epoch_oid}`; statistical evidence may justify promotion but is never encoded as an invariant. It is not `SecurityPolicyEpoch`.

## Appendix B — Graph Intent Log (the semantic vocabulary)

Intents are deterministic commands captured at statement execution; they are **not** committed effects or Ripple deltas. `IntentElementRef` is the closed union `Existing{database_security_namespace_id,graph,vid_or_eid}|EscapedAllocation{binding_ref:StrongRef<EscapingAllocationReservationRecord>,slot_key,permanent_value}|StatementLocal{tmp_ref_ordinal}`. `StatementLocal` can appear only within one `GraphIntentBatch` and cannot enter result bytes, a later statement, procedure/UDF output, provenance/audit, a durable witness, or a committed effect. First escape uses §4.5's ordered reservation and all subsequent intents use the EscapedAllocation arm. `IntentCoordinateRef` is exactly `{binding_role_id,coordinate:CoordinateSchemaBinding,write_basis:WeakMarkerIdentity}`; no raw graph/branch name, run locator, or untyped reference is legal.

`CapturedIntentObservation` is the generated closed union of exact value/CAS, element-version, key absence/range gap, adjacency/degree, valid-time range, constraint, escrow, and procedure-output observations. Every arm names its coordinate role, schema/contract epoch, witness ref, disclosure class, and deterministic recomputation rule. `GraphIntent<Role>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,intent_ordinal,intent_semantics_oid,read_coordinate_roles[],write_coordinate_refs[],captured_observation_refs[CapturedIntentObservationRef],structural_effect_class,operation:GraphIntentOperation}`. `GraphIntentBatch<Role>` is the independently addressable per-statement object `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,registration_identity,statement_seq,canonical_statement_classification_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalStatementClassification>,intent_semantics_ref:StrongRef<IntentSemantics>,prior_workspace_generation_identity,sorted_intents,escaped_allocation_binding_root_ref:StrongRef<TxnAllocationBindingRoot>,statement_observation_commitment,resource_bounds,body_digest:BodyDigest<GraphIntentBatch<Role>>}`. Ordinals are contiguous, arrays and maps have registered size/depth/string/value limits, and the batch's binding/semantics/coordinate values must match every child. A child intent cannot carry another root, service epoch, purpose, principal, or ceiling even when policy/revocation scalars happen to match. Registration owns no future batch. `LocalStatementPublicationSpec`/`GlobalStatementPublicationSpec` must name the already-built exact batch, prior/successor workspace and cumulative roots, and the Published successor plus successor workspace/cumulative root strongly own it. A statement abort proves no batch exists or retains one exact failed diagnostic batch under its registered retention policy. Every shard workspace delta/certificate binds its routed batch projection, exact Shard security projection, and complete batch digest. Prepare proves a gap-free bijection between published statements, batches, workspace effects, observations, and cumulative roots; DAG and substitution tests reject a registration-to-future-batch edge, missing/extra batch, authority substitution, or reused certificate from another batch.

```
CreateVertex   { new_ref, label_set, props, valid_time_assignment }
DeleteVertex   { target, expected_version, cascade_policy }
AddLabel/RemoveLabel { vid, label }
AddEdge        { new_ref, src, etype, dst, requested_key?, props, valid_time_assignment }
RemoveEdge     { target, expected_version }
SetProp        { elem, name, value, merge_policy_id }
RemoveProp     { elem, name }
EnsureEdge     { src, etype, dst, constraint_id, props }
CompareAndSet  { elem, name, expected, value, mismatch: NoOp|StatementError|TxnAbort }
SetInsert      { elem, name, element, set_epoch }
SetRemove      { elem, name, element, observed_tags_or_remove_policy }
SetValidTime   { elem, contract_id, expected:Any|Exact(ValidTimeAssignment),
                 replacement:ValidTimeAssignment,
                 mismatch:NoOp|StatementError|TxnAbort }
AdjustCounter  { elem, property, delta,
                 algebra:ExactAdd|Escrow{constraint_id,domain_epoch},
                 operation_key, overflow:Reject }
SketchUpdate   { elem_or_relation, sketch_profile_oid, operation_key,
                 canonical_input, exact_delete_token? }
AssertSameAs   { a, b, confidence, source } // canonical sugar for AddEdge(SAME_AS)
BulkLoad       { logical_batch_ref:StrongRef<CanonicalBulkLogicalBatch>,
                 source_digest, row_count,
                 optional_staged_run_refs[ConditionalCoordinateRef] }
SchemaOp       { canonical_schema_operation } // ordinary transactional intent/effect
```

`CanonicalBulkLogicalBatch` contains bounded canonical logical rows with explicit operation tag, coordinate role, element/key/value/valid-time/provenance fields, row ordinal, source-row digest, and batch digest. Staged runs are optional accelerators checked against that batch; no effect is inferred from physical run bytes. `AssertSameAs` creates an ordinary protected `SAME_AS` edge with stable EId, endpoints, properties, valid time, and provenance; there is no second entity-resolution mutation mechanism. Catalog graph/branch aliases and all branchable schema/constraint/index definitions use ordinary `SchemaOp` transaction effects and the same marker/head/authorization/SSI path. Database-global topology, security policy, key, configuration, physical index-build progress, and derived-generation activation remain typed control commands, not `SchemaOp`.

LWW policies use canonical `EffectOrder { commit_seq, intent_ordinal, merge_ordinal, effect_ordinal }`, never element birth or an ambient wall clock. `ExactAdd` is checked integer/fixed-decimal addition identified by a registered algebra profile; raw addition is not idempotent, so ACI merge operates on the set-union of unique operation keys followed by deterministic checked summation. Escrow uses §4.7's custody ledger. Every sketch profile fixes its merge/delete algebra, precision/error contract, canonical state, duplicate-operation behavior, and fallback; a sketch without exact deletion either rejects delete/update workloads or rebuilds, and never becomes authoritative constraint state. Generic SetProp/RemoveProp against counter/escrow/sketch-governed state is invalid unless the binder rewrites it into the registered typed operation with captured-before/CAS semantics. `ConsistencyClass` is derived only after composing intent semantics, captured observations, active constraints, rights/authority, and branch policy. Commutativity tables are caches of proved contextual results.

`StatementObservationCommitment` covers the exact canonical published result bytes or NoResult proof, escaped identities/generated values, error class, workspace successor, and every read/constraint/rights observation visible to later statements. Rebase may freely regenerate an unpublished autocommit terminal result, but it may not alter any prior published statement commitment. If re-evaluation would change one byte, identity, row order, error/no-op choice, valid period, or workspace-visible effect, the explicit transaction fails with a serialization/retry outcome rather than silently committing a history the client did not observe.

Finalization evaluates all commands and emits only canonical effects:

```text
CreateVertexEffect { permanent_vid, allocation_binding_or_finalization_proof,
                     birth_ordinal, labels, canonical_props, valid_time_assignment }
DeleteVertexEffect { vid, complete_before_version, retirement_basis,
                     sorted_retired_incident_edge_before_images[],
                     sorted_constraint/temporal/escrow_contribution_retractions[] }
LabelMembershipEffect { vid, label, before, after }
CreateEdgeEffect { permanent_eid, allocation_binding_or_finalization_proof,
                   birth_ordinal, src, type, dst, canonical_key?, props,
                   valid_time_assignment }
DeleteEdgeEffect { eid, complete_before_version, retirement_basis,
                   sorted_constraint/temporal/escrow_contribution_retractions[] }
PropertyEffect { elem, property, before, after }
ValidTimeEffect { elem, contract_id, before, after, before_version_identity,
                  temporal_constraint_transition_refs[], witness_basis }
CounterEffect { operation_key, elem, property, algebra_profile,
                delta, before, after, overflow:Reject }
EscrowAdjustmentEffect { domain_id, epoch, operation_key, contribution_key?,
                         elem_property_or_cardinality_subject, delta,
                         before_value, after_value, consumed_reservation_refs[],
                         produced_right_kind_and_amount, target_owner,
                         conservation_basis_digest }
SketchEffect { operation_key, sketch_profile_oid, before_state_digest,
               after_state_ref, realized_answer_contract }
SchemaEffect { transition:CoordinateSchemaTransition,
               canonical_catalog_schema_constraint_index_operations[] }
ConstraintStateEffect { apply_basis, before_schema_root, before_schema_epoch,
                        after_schema_root, after_schema_epoch,
                        before_constraint_root, after_constraint_root,
                        exact_removed_and_added_contributions[] }
ResourceChargeEffect { reservation_id, scope, before_charge, after_charge, reason }
```

Every effect carries `EffectProvenance {source_batch_ref:StrongRef<GraphIntentBatch>,source_intent_ordinal,source_statement_seq,source_coordinate_role,source_observation_commitment,merge_or_import_origin?,authorization_decision_digest,canonicalization_profile_oid}`. `CoordinateEffectEnvelope {coordinate:WriteCoordinateBinding,effect_ordinal,effect,provenance}` is the sole coordinate source, so nested effects cannot disagree. `GlobalEffectEnvelope` is the closed union `Resource{transition_ref,effect}|Audit{ticket_and_event_commitment}|AuthorityGlobalEscrow{domain_id,operation_summary_ref,effect}`; a coordinate effect cannot smuggle a global charge and a global effect cannot mutate graph state. Resource reservation/charge/release ownership and audit ticket/event ownership are typed strong refs, never bare IDs or an English “handled elsewhere” promise.

`NetEffectNormalForm` groups by coordinate and logical identity/key, cancels only inverse effects proved unobserved and algebraically cancellable, preserves create/delete before-images and all unique operation keys, sorts cascades/contributions canonically, and rejects two incompatible terminal values. It includes a proof mapping every source intent to its surviving effects or an explicit `NoOp{captured_basis,policy}`; no intent disappears implicitly. `CommittedEffectSet<Role:AuthorityOwningRole>` is `{authority_bound_header:AuthorityBoundHeader<Role>,intent_semantics_ref,ordered_source_batch_refs[],statement_observation_commitments[],net_effect_normal_form_ref,coordinate_effect_envelopes[],global_effect_envelopes[],complete_allocation_binding_commitment,constraint_temporal_escrow_evidence,authorization_decision_ref:StrongRef<AuthorizationDecisionRecord<Role>>,resource_transition_refs[StrongRef<ResourceLedgerTransition<Role>>],audit_ownership_commitment,body_digest}`. It has explicit before/after semantics, permanent IDs/BirthOrdinals, branch-aware retirement bases, full cascade before-images, and no conditional operation. Every child effect/provenance/resource reference must agree with the same complete authority binding; equal policy/revocation scalars under a different principal, purpose, ceiling, or service epoch are noninterchangeable.

The sequence-neutral graph/branch/relation/schema/intent-semantics-keyed `LogicalDeltaTemplate` is derived from that normal form and differential-tested against applying the gap-free ordered batches once under the pinned `IntentSemanticsOid` to the recorded basis. Create/delete/property/valid-time/counter/escrow/sketch/schema/constraint deltas are exact typed rows; only the committed `MarkerRef` turns the template into an ordered `LogicalDeltaBatch`. Fuzz/model tests delete or duplicate intents/effects/provenance/observations, reorder bulk rows/cascades, force no-op/CAS drift, replay operation keys, expose IDs then rebase/abort, and compare batch application, workspace state, reference finalization, normal form, committed effects, and integrated deltas byte-for-byte.

## Appendix C — GLA Operator Inventory (executor contracts)

Every physical implementation registers input/output factorization, strict/relaxed determinism, minimum resident set and spill algorithm, maximum work between cancellation checkpoints, incremental derivative or `none`, witness domain, authorization boundary, path preservation, input/output `AnswerContract` transfer or rejection, and legal `ReplanClass`. “Propagate” below means retain the complete upstream approximation provenance and apply a registered sound transformation; it never means relabel approximate data as exact. The initial normative matrix is:

| Family | Memory / spill | Incremental contract | Replan / witness contract | Answer-contract rule |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `ScanVertices/ScanEdges/Values` | bounded cursor; external batch buffers | linear derivative | partition boundary; label/type/range/gap witness | exact base cursor/value input emits `Exact` |
| `IndexProbe{btree,hash,fts,vector,path}` | bounded cursor plus candidate heap; exact tail path may spill | registered per index | before output unless continuation proved; generation/range/gap/watermark witness | emit the pinned generation contract; `Exact` requires exact index+tail completion |
| `HybridSearch` | spillable per-source candidate runs, fusion heap, rerank and expansion frontier | registered only for a complete fusion/expansion derivative; otherwise recompute | before output or a fully materialized candidate/fusion boundary; all source-generation, tail, candidate, fusion, authorization, and expansion witnesses | compose every source/stage; `Exact` only through complete exact sources or pinned exact fallback, otherwise emit fully qualified `Approximate` |
| `Expand/VarLengthExpand` | bounded descriptor cursor; factorized/path history spill | bounded-hop derivative only where registered | descriptor or checkpointed path frontier; adjacency ranges and path cuts | exact adjacency preserves `Exact`; approximate seed/input coverage propagates under a registered law |
| `PathFind` | spillable frontier, history sets, path DAG | registered mode/selector subsets only | no switch after emission without state transfer; full mode-aware witness | `Exact` requires exact graph/input and complete algorithm; approximation needs a selector-specific bound/recall law |
| `FreeJoin/HashJoin` | radix/grace partitions; partitioned COLT | bilinear/custom derivative | build/materialization barrier or verified state transfer; all input predicate witnesses | all-exact inputs yield `Exact`; any approximate input requires a registered join error/completeness transform |
| `LeftOuter/Semi/Anti/Apply/NullExtend` | spillable correlation/match state | custom or recompute | materialization boundary; absence and correlated-domain witnesses mandatory | absence-sensitive forms require exact negative domains for `Exact`; otherwise a registered approximation law or rejection |
| `Select/Project/Unnest/Flatten` | vector-bounded; flatten spillable | linear where expression is registered | batch boundary; preserve or explicitly transform factorization/path/witness laws | deterministic total transforms preserve `Exact`; approximate provenance is transformed, never erased |
| `Aggregate/Distinct` | radix-partitioned retained state | registered algebra with deletion law | completed partition or state transfer; group-domain and multiplicity witness | `Exact` only from exact complete inputs; approximation requires a registered estimator/error algebra |
| `OrderBy/Limit{offset,count}` | top-k heap or external stable merge sort | top-k custom; full sort normally recompute | no switch after emitted prefix; order/tie and early-stop gap witnesses | exact ordered prefix requires exact gap/completeness proof; approximate top-k carries recall/error and population |
| `SetOp{Union,Intersect,Except}` | partition-spillable multiplicity state | registered per ALL/DISTINCT form | materialization boundary; negative/right-side domains where applicable | all-exact inputs yield `Exact`; approximate difference/intersection requires a sound registered law |
| `Fixpoint` | LSM-backed arrangements and iteration spill | validated SCC only | iteration barrier; termination and recursive-domain witness | `Exact` requires exact seed/relations and convergence; approximate recursion is unavailable unless separately registered |
| `TemporalSlice/TemporalJoin` | interval-partition spill | registered interval algebra | interval boundary; system/valid-time ranges and gaps | preserves `Exact` only with complete temporal coverage; otherwise propagates qualified interval approximation |
| `SemiringMxV` | sparse/dense frontier spill | registered annotation algebra | iteration/frontier barrier; projection/capability/path semantics pinned | exact algebra+inputs yield `Exact`; numeric/coverage approximation requires its registered bound |
| `ProcedureCall` | declared per procedure; otherwise reject | none unless explicitly registered | none by default; authorized view only; external nondeterminism badge as required | signature independently declares answer-contract transfer and replay-input capture; external/nondeterministic execution may still be semantically exact but downgrades replay completeness, while an approximate procedure remains approximate even if perfectly replayable |
| `GraphConstruct` | spillable canonical key/value and path/provenance runs; generation-local dense ordinals allowed | registered pure construction derivative with deletion/multiplicity law | batch/materialization boundary; complete source/provenance witnesses and canonical `ViewElementRef` identity | pure deterministic construction preserves/propagates its inputs; it has no proposal or commit side effect |
| `Mutate` | proposal-local buffered intents/effects | emits canonical logical delta only after successful commit | never replanned after side-effect ordering; complete read/write/constraint/authorization witness | consumes `Exact` only; approximate data cannot authorize a mutation target/effect |
| `ViewDelta/CertEmit` | bounded envelope plus spillable forensic detail | stream tap | no semantic replan; watermark/evidence closure must remain exact | preserves and serializes the full input/output contract without upgrading it |

Any operator without a complete row, a total answer-contract rule, and conformance tests is unavailable, not implicitly “bounded.” Strict flatten/factorization, spill/no-spill, compaction, and legal replan variants are metamorphically required to produce identical canonical results **within the same answer contract and pinned approximate generation closure**. A transition that changes approximate candidates/topology is a new semantic generation, not an equivalent physical variant.

## Appendix D — FGP Protocol Contract
Connection state is exactly `TransportEstablished -> VersionNegotiated -> Authenticated{AuthContext} -> Ready{ReadyBinding} -> Draining -> Closed`. `DatabasePosture` is exactly `Local|Sharded`. `HELLO/HELLO_ACK` negotiate protocol mechanics only; they contain no database selector, namespace, incarnation, service epoch, posture, promotion receipt, topology, existence signal, or database-specific limit. `AUTH/AUTH_OK` establish a transport-authenticated principal and immutable session binding. `SELECT_DATABASE/READY` then select one authorized database and independently reread its current Operational role root, continuity state, service-promotion receipt when required, and catalog entry. Nonexistent and unauthorized selectors have the same authenticated failure surface. Changing database, posture, incarnation, service epoch, or promotion receipt drains the Ready binding and requires a fresh selection; it is never an in-band refresh.

The three headers are distinct. `TransportHeader={frame_len,protocol_version,frame_type,flags,request_id,stream_id}` is used through AUTH. `SessionHeader=TransportHeader+{session_binding,auth_context_generation}` is legal after AUTH_OK. `ReadyHeader=SessionHeader+{database_security_namespace_id,cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,database_posture,promotion_receipt_digest_or_direct_authority_commitment}` is mandatory for database work. A stale, mixed, or missing binding fails before allocation, routing, authorization, schema/status/error disclosure, or timing-distinct lookup. `session_binding` is derived from the completed HELLO/HELLO_ACK/AUTH transcript and a server secret before AUTH_OK is encoded; no frame hashes itself.

`AUTH_REFRESH/AUTH_REFRESHED` is a self-transition in Authenticated or Ready. It cannot change the selected database or ReadyBinding and cannot widen beyond the original non-widenable ceiling. New authority applies only to subsequently created children; narrowing or revocation fences every unsent protected byte of existing children while an already admitted semantic decision still completes under its durable rules. A posture or service-epoch change forces Draining and fresh selection/authentication.

Ready owns typed children. A transaction child is `Idle -> BeginStaging -> Begun{OwnershipGeneration,StatementIdle|StatementOpen} -> Closing{ReadClose|WritePrepare|Rollback} -> Terminal -> Idle`; ordered reattach/renew rotates OwnershipGeneration and fences the old session. A query result is `Accepted -> Bound -> Registered -> Executing -> Buffered -> PublicationSubmitted -> PendingVisibility|Deliverable -> SenderAttached|SenderPaused -> IndependentlyRetained -> Acknowledged|ReleasedUnacked`; sender state is not durable disposition. Subscription and durable artifact-output children use their own exact registries; replay execution uses the query-result machine. `Draining{cause,cutoff_child_generation}` admits no later child. Each existing child must become exactly `EphemeralCancelledBeforeRegistration|TransactionOwnershipDetached|SemanticTerminalDurable|AdmittedRecoveryRooted|ResultDurablyDetached|ArtifactOutputDurablyDetached|SubscriptionDurablyDetached|ProtectedErrorDurablyOwned`, and every transport obligation must become Sent, CancelledBeforeWrite, or Failed. Drain never fabricates ACK/release/expiry and never waits for an offline client; independent result/output/cursor owners remain rooted after connection/process close. GOODBYE is emitted only after durable handoff; transport failure closes without another protected frame.

Every role-sensitive public identity is a closed opaque posture union: begin reservation, attempt, statement registration/publication, result owner, snapshot, marker outcome, logical position, delivery position, cancellation lineage, and audit release. A client never receives a Local ObjectId, Meta/shard reference, raw Raft index, or untagged integer from which posture must be inferred.

`AuditReleaseEvidence` is exactly `NotRequired{release_subject_commitment,nonrequired_evidence_projection,visibility:Opaque{visibility_commitment}|Declassified{authorized_position_projection,declassification_decision_digest}}|RequiredVisible{release_subject_commitment,audit_outcome_and_visibility_commitment,visibility:Opaque{visibility_commitment}|Declassified{authorized_position_projection,declassification_decision_digest}}`. It contains no nullable fields, raw ticket/event/root references, or default logical/commit/audit positions; a raw position is legal only in the explicit authorized declassification arm. `ReadReleaseAuthorizationEvidence` is `{posture,release_subject_commitment,public_result_or_output_digest:PublicResultDigestProjection|PublicOutputDigestProjection,release_decision_commitment,authority_binding_public_commitment,security_state_binding_public_commitment,disclosure_contract_digest,portable_disclosure_receipt?}` and is the portable projection of the exact Local/Global release decision. It never serializes an internal policy epoch, revocation index, authority digest, root identity, or decision-record reference.

The socket-write boundary uses two nested linear guards without holding an ordinary lock across `await`. `AuthGenerationUseGuard<Role:AuthorityOwningRole> {ready_binding,session_binding,auth_context_generation,current_authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,release_subject_commitment,intended_frame_digest_or_range}` is acquired while linearizing against the session authority generation and proves that the complete current policy/revocation/credential/service/principal/purpose/ceiling binding still covers the release subject. Those internal roots/counters never enter the frame; only their registered public commitments may appear in authorized projection evidence.

Time legality is a generated set, never a singular ambient guard. `FrameTimeRequirement` is the closed union `RequiredUsableNow{subject:TimeBoundSubject,intended_use_digest,subject_body_digest}|RequiredDurableExpired{subject:TimeBoundSubject,intended_terminal_use_digest,expired_evidence_ref:StrongRef<TimeValidationEvidence>}|NoTimeNeeded{registered_public_mechanics_class}`. `NoTimeNeeded` is inhabitable only for a finite registry of payload-free protocol-mechanics frames whose truth does not depend on a capability, lease, freshness, expiry, or time-based disclosure. `FrameTimeRequirementSet {frame_class,frame_subject_version,canonical_requirements[],requirement_subject_bijection_commitment,minimum_required_remaining_guard_ticks,body_digest}` is generated from `frame_time_requirements.toml`; it lists every and only time-bearing subject needed to authorize that exact frame/range. `FrameTimeUseGuardSet` is the nonserializable bijection from each `RequiredUsableNow` row to one fresh `TimeUseGuard` and each `RequiredDurableExpired` row to its exact durable evidence; it records the minimum remaining monotonic use budget. Missing, duplicate, extra, cross-subject, or NoTimeNeeded substitution fails before queue admission.

`FrameSubjectVersion` is the closed type-specific union `PreparedStatement{statement_handle:PublicHandle<PreparedStatement>,state_token:PublicStateToken<PreparedStatement>}|ResultDelivery{delivery_handle:PublicResultDeliveryHandle,state_token:PublicStateToken<ResultDelivery>}|ArtifactDelivery{delivery_handle:PublicArtifactOutputHandle,state_token:PublicStateToken<ArtifactDelivery>}|CursorOutput{cursor_handle:PublicCursorHandle,output_handle:PublicHandle<CursorOutput>,state_token:PublicStateToken<CursorOutput>}|ProtectedError{delivery_handle:PublicErrorDeliveryHandle,state_token:PublicStateToken<ProtectedErrorDelivery>}|TxnStatus{txn_handle:PublicTxnHandle,state_token:PublicStateToken<TxnProgress>}|MergeStatus{merge_handle:PublicMergeHandle,state_token:PublicStateToken<MergeProgress>}|AuditStatus{subject_handle:PublicAuditSubjectHandle,state_token:PublicStateToken<AuditStatus>}`; a naked numeric version or cross-kind token cannot decode. `PublicFrameSendGuard` is the closed queue-admission union `PreparedMetadata{prepare_authorization_digest,catalog_compatibility_digest}|DataOutput{read_release_authorization_digest,audit_release_digest,active_delivery_authorization_digest}|CapabilityTerminalOutcome{validated_capability_commitment,terminal_projection_authorization_digest,audit_release_digest}|PendingOutcomeStatus{validated_outcome_capability_commitment,opaque_status_projection_digest}|ProtectedOutputManagementStatus{validated_management_handle_commitment,output_kind:Result|SubscriptionInitial|SubscriptionDelta|SubscriptionReset|Artifact|ProtectedError,released_state_token,release_authorization_digest}|AuditReceiptStatus{audit_subject_handle_commitment,current_scoped_audit_authority_digest,receipt_status_projection_digest}|ProtectedError{error_delivery_authorization_digest,audit_release_digest}` plus common `{auth_generation_use_guard,ready_binding,session_binding,stream_id,frame_subject_version:FrameSubjectVersion,exact_frame_class_and_digest_or_byte_range,frame_time_requirement_set_ref:StrongRef<FrameTimeRequirementSet>,queue_frame_time_guard_set:FrameTimeUseGuardSet}`. `PreparedMetadata` queues only the frozen PREPARED parameter/schema contract after current catalog and authority revalidation. `DataOutput` queues bytes only from the exact ActiveDeliverable cursor/output arm. `CapabilityTerminalOutcome` alone queues `NeverRegistered`, `TerminalVisiblePendingPostconditions`, `TerminalReady`, or `MERGE_STATUS_RESULT::{Committed,Rejected}` after exact audit-visible terminal authorization. `PendingOutcomeStatus` alone queues `BeginStaging`, `InProgress`, `FinalizingOpaque`, `MERGE_STATUS_RESULT::InFlight`, `TokenNotYetValid`, `TokenExpired`, `TimeAuthorityUnavailable`, or `NotVisibleOrUnknown`; none may encode a semantic terminal kind, and TokenExpired requires the exact durable-expired requirement arm. `ProtectedOutputManagementStatus` queues only a frozen ACCEPTED/AwaitingActivation projection for a PendingVisibility or ReleasedAwaitingActivation owner and can never queue payload bytes, a lease, or a Resume capability. `AuditReceiptStatus` queues only its authorized receipt projection, and `ProtectedError` uses its bounded owner lifecycle. The generated public-frame-class and time-requirement matrices assign every protected frame or byte range to exactly one arm/set and reject aliases or wildcard classes. `OutputSendGuard` is the legacy alias for `DataOutput`, not a generic escape hatch.

Queue-admission guard consumption atomically copies the exact authenticated frame bytes into a bounded `TransportSendObligation {session_binding,queued_auth_context_generation,frame_subject_version,frame_digest,encoded_len,logical_row_charge,frame_time_requirement_set_commitment,required_release_and_audit_commitments,flow_reservation:FlowReservation{stream_key,internal_debit_generation,reserved_encoded_bytes,reserved_logical_rows,frame_cost_rule_id},state:Queued|Writing{write_start_generation,write_start_authority_commitment,write_start_frame_time_guard_set_commitment,minimum_remaining_guard_ticks_at_start,bytes_written}|Sent{committed_encoded_bytes,committed_logical_rows}|CancelledBeforeWrite{refunded_reservation_digest}|Failed{bytes_written,consumed_reservation_digest,transport_poisoned:true}}` under the authority and FlowState CAS, then releases both queue-admission guard sets. The obligation retains the canonical requirement-set commitment, not an ephemeral guard.

Actual socket I/O has a second mandatory linearization point. `SocketWriteStartGuard<Role:AuthorityOwningRole> {obligation_identity,ready_binding,session_binding,current_auth_context_generation,current_authority_binding:AuthorityBindingFor<Role>,frame_subject_version,exact_frame_digest,current_release_audit_and_active_owner_commitment,frame_time_requirement_set_ref:StrongRef<FrameTimeRequirementSet>,fresh_frame_time_guard_set:FrameTimeUseGuardSet,current_flow_generation,transport_write_generation}` is acquired only while CASing the exact obligation `Queued -> Writing`. It reruns the complete arm-specific authority, subject-state, audit-release, every lease/capability/freshness/expiry requirement, service epoch, and flow check against current rooted state; queue admission never carries validity across backpressure. Failure before the CAS selects `CancelledBeforeWrite` and refunds the reservation without emitting bytes. The CAS commits the exact write-start authority and requirement-set commitments and their minimum remaining monotonic budget. Once Writing begins, the guard set is consumed into the one transport writer; the complete authenticated frame must reach Sent before that minimum budget expires. Authority refresh/revocation waits for that finite frame to reach Sent or Failed and cannot begin another old-generation frame. `Sent` means the complete authenticated frame was accepted by the transport within the committed budget and atomically moves that exact reservation to committed; `CancelledBeforeWrite` alone refunds it. Any write failure or budget overrun after the Writing CAS—including zero bytes or a partial prefix—selects `Failed`, consumes the reservation, permanently poisons that connection, and forces close without an `AUTH_REFRESHED` or later application frame. A peer can accept only a complete frame, so a partial prefix is never application-visible; the independently retained delivery/result/proof owner remains resumable on a fresh connection from its canonical boundary. Every durable END-send completion byte-matches the write-start authority and full requirement-set commitments.

A narrowing/revoking refresh cancels every still-Queued old-generation obligation and waits for every Writing obligation to become Sent or Failed; it emits `AUTH_REFRESHED(new_generation)` only if all such obligations reached Sent and the transport remains usable, otherwise it closes. Drain/close performs the same join. Backpressure cannot retain unbounded obligations because both queue memory and flow cost were reserved and capped before queue admission. Refresh/revocation, epoch/receipt drift, subject-version change, ACK/release, audit mismatch, lease/capability expiry, flow-generation drift, or authority-generation change before write start cancels the queued frame. No guard is serialized or retained as durable evidence; durable END-send records name the terminal Sent obligation and reconciled reservation. DPOR tests block and fail the socket while interleaving refresh/revocation/drain/credit/expiry at queue guard acquisition, byte-copy, reservation, queue admission, write-start guard acquisition, Queued-to-Writing CAS, every partial-write boundary, completion, and reconnect/resume.

`ResumeCapabilityBody` is the closed union `SubscriptionCursor{cursor_handle:PublicCursorHandle,issuance_cursor_state_token:PublicStateToken<Cursor>,capability_lineage_token:PublicStateToken<ResumeLineage>,public_derived_identity_commitment,baseline_handle:PublicBaselineHandle,public_state_commitment}|BufferedResult{posture,owner_commitment,delivery_handle:PublicResultDeliveryHandle,issuance_lease_token:PublicStateToken<ResultLease>,issuance_delivery_state_token:PublicStateToken<ResultDelivery>,capability_lineage_token:PublicStateToken<ResumeLineage>,canonical_result_digest}|DurableArtifactOutput{posture,output_kind,request_key_commitment,delivery_handle:PublicArtifactOutputHandle,issuance_lease_token:PublicStateToken<ArtifactLease>,issuance_delivery_state_token:PublicStateToken<ArtifactDelivery>,capability_lineage_token:PublicStateToken<ResumeLineage>,canonical_output_digest}`. It uses the common profiled `DurableCapabilityHeader`. No mutable state hint or numeric internal generation is encoded; the rooted Protocol index alone determines current/terminal state, and PendingVisibility/ReleasedAwaitingActivation cannot have a Resume capability at all. Current or immediate-predecessor retry returns the same prebuilt successor or compact consumption proof; older generations fail capability-safely. No arm exposes internal commit provenance, raw DerivedIdentity, or retention authority by itself; the corresponding active durable owner strongly retains the exact replay record bytes.

`ExecutionScope` is `Autocommit{autocommit_idempotency_key,txn_operation_ceiling:ReadOnlyOnly|MayMutate,isolation,transaction_error_policy,default_snapshot_selector}|Transaction{txn_handle,ownership_generation,statement_seq,statement_idempotency_key,expected_prior_statement_frontier,expected_workspace_generation}`. Clients declare only the ceiling needed for admission; after binding parameters and selectors, the server constructs the exact `CanonicalStatementClassification` and rejects any class above the ceiling. No client-supplied `statement_class` or mutation/read claim is authoritative. Before autocommit work, Local or Meta orders the exact reservation/registration mapping from scoped client key and canonical request digest/classification to one `TxnOutcomeCapabilityFields`, attempt, and eventual result capability. `EXECUTE_ACCEPTED` is emitted before result/outcome frames. Exact retry joins; any query/template, parameter, classification, authority, posture, epoch, snapshot, or option drift fails.

For `Autocommit{ReadWrite,WriteReturning}`, the buffered manifest owner is the distinct posture-valid `AutocommitWriteTerminal`. Prepared ownership retains it through prepare/commit/audit. No streamable result lease exists until the semantic commit is TerminalReady, the exact terminal audit outcome is released through the gap-free Protocol visibility advance, the Local/Global delta and durability postconditions are satisfied, and an exact independently retained delivery lease is installed by inline or independent activation. Commit completion never waits for client ACK; ACK/release only discharges that independent result promise.

**Authoritative v1 delivery and control corrections.** `QUERY_CANCEL` controls computation and the current sender only. Before publication it may select the role-valid no-result/abort path; after independent retention it can only durably pause that session's sender after joining queued/writing transport obligations. It never ACKs/releases a result, changes capability lineage, or frees durable charge. `ClientResultReleaseAuthority` is the closed source union `Fgp{presented_resume_capability_commitment,session_binding,auth_context_generation,request_frame_digest}|Embedded{stream_release_token_commitment,host_session_generation,explicit_release_call_digest}|Bolt{bolt_session_binding,qid,request_kind:DISCARD|RESET,request_message_digest}`. `AuthenticatedClientResultReleaseReceipt<Role>` binds the complete role/domain/service/principal/authority/result ID/state version/digest/release nonce/source/authentication attestation. `ResultReleaseEvidence` is exactly `ClientRelease{receipt_ref:StrongRef<AuthenticatedClientResultReleaseReceipt<Role>>}|LeaseExpired{expiry_evidence_ref}`. Rust Drop, transport loss, QUERY_CANCEL, drain, and process/session death cannot construct it.

Result, cursor, and artifact-output capability rotation is ordered, not an in-memory cursor update. Every active owner strongly retains `ResultCapabilityLineage {current_replay_ref,current_commitment,generation,immediate_predecessor:None|LiveUntilSuccessorAcknowledgedOrSubjectTerminal{replay_ref,consuming_request_digest}|Compacted{commitment,request_digest,tombstone_ref}}`. There is no independent recovery deadline. `RotateForResume`, ACK, Release, cursor ACK/reset/close, and expiry CAS the same type-specific active state token; exact immediate-predecessor retry reproduces the successor while the successor/subject remains live, anything older fails. The acyclic order is manifest -> released owner/policy -> fresh time basis/evidence -> capability replay object -> protocol activation/transition -> applied lease/retention -> protocol index/root.

Subscription transition tags are generated from the exact semantic/maintenance split in §9.5: semantic `CreateSubscriptionCursorSpec`, then Protocol `ReleaseInitialVisibility|ActivateInitialBaseline|AckBaselineComplete|PublishDeltaPending|ReleaseDeltaVisibility|ActivateDelta|AckDeltaComplete|PublishResetPending|ReleaseResetVisibility|ActivateResetNotice|AcknowledgeReset|ActivateResetBaseline|RotateCapability|PauseForDisconnect|ResumeAfterDisconnect|PostActivationCursorTransition|CloseCursor|ExpireCursor`; `CreateCursorAndBaselineLease`, `CreateLease`, `AckComplete`, `BeginReset`, and `ResetOrExpire` are invalid ambiguous aliases. Reset ACK precedes construction of the successor baseline lease; baseline ACK precedes successor deltas. Explicit pause preserves the exact outstanding lease/frontier, rotates capability, and creates a replayable notice—it is neither ACK, release, gap, nor reset. Ordinary transport disconnect merely detaches the runtime sender.

Flow control separates client-known grants from server debits. `FlowState` is `{key:{session_binding,stream_id,stream_generation},grant_generation,next_credit_sequence,cumulative_granted_bytes,cumulative_granted_rows,reserved_queued_bytes,reserved_queued_rows,committed_sent_bytes,committed_sent_rows,internal_debit_generation,state:Open|Closing|Closed}` with checked `available=granted-reserved-committed`. `FLOW_CREDIT` carries expected grant generation, next credit sequence, and cumulative nondecreasing byte/row totals; server debits never invalidate the client's expected grant generation. A generated frame-cost matrix charges final encoded bytes and logical rows exactly at each row's final fragment (factorized multiplicity included; FEC symbols charge zero rows). A linear `FlowReservation` moves cost to reserved before queueing; CancelledBeforeWrite refunds; Sent moves reserved to committed; Failed poisons the connection and never reuses credit. Credits never move between streams; only bounded mechanics frames use a separate control reserve.

The following connection-control rows are additional to the one normative frame inventory below; `wire_types.toml` joins them into that inventory exactly once and rejects any duplicate tag or alternative spelling:

```text
TXN_REATTACH{txn_outcome_capability,expected_ownership_state_token,
             reattach_request_idempotency_key,requested_control_mode}
TXN_REATTACHED{posture,registration_handle:PublicRegistrationHandle,
               txn_handle:PublicTxnHandle,ownership_state_token:PublicStateToken<TxnOwnership>,
               rotated_txn_outcome_capability,statement_recovery_state,
               control_mode}
TXN_RENEW{txn_handle,ownership_state_token,txn_outcome_capability,
          renewal_request_idempotency_key,requested_window}
TXN_RENEWED{txn_handle,ownership_state_token,rotated_txn_outcome_capability,
            not_before,expiry}

AUTHORITY_RESET{cursor_handle:PublicCursorHandle,cursor_state_token:PublicStateToken<CursorOutput>,
  reset_notice_handle:PublicHandle<CursorOutput>,
  public_reset_commitment,prior_public_authority_commitment,
  successor_public_authority_commitment,successor_authorized_cursor_position,
  successor_baseline_handle:PublicBaselineHandle,
  successor_public_baseline_digest,resume_capability,
  read_release_authorization,audit_release}
SUBSCRIBE_PAUSE{resume_capability,expected_cursor_state_token,
                pause_request_idempotency_key,pause_reason_projection}
SUBSCRIBE_PAUSED{rotated_resume_capability,cursor_state_token,
                 outstanding_public_projection}
SUBSCRIBE_RESUME_AFTER_PAUSE{resume_capability,expected_cursor_state_token,
                             resume_request_idempotency_key}
```

Artifact/replay requests use opaque handles and the durable output registry: `EXPORT_ACCEPTED`/`REPLAY_ACCEPTED` name the canonical request and output-request handle; BEGIN/CHUNK/END frames carry a typed public delivery handle/state token, Resume capability only in ActiveDeliverable, and public projection digest, never internal refs. `REPLAY_GET{request_key,replay_handle,requested_projection}` retrieves `PublicReplayManifestProjection`; `REPLAY_EXECUTE{request_key,replay_handle,requested_projection,fetch,resource_request}` returns `REPLAY_EXECUTE_ACCEPTED` then uses ordinary result frames. `PROOF` carries only a closed `ProofBody`. `FEC_BEGIN` carries `PublicFecTransferDescriptor`; `FEC_SYMBOL` carries `WireFecSymbolRecord` MACed with channel-exporter-derived `K_wire_symbol`; reconnect creates a new transfer descriptor/key over the same public output digest.

Normative frame families:

```text
HELLO{versions,features,client_nonce,transport_limits,concurrency_profiles}
HELLO_ACK{version,features,server_nonce,transport_limits,concurrency_profile,transcript_binding}
AUTH{token,channel_binding,transcript_binding,requested_authority_ceiling}
AUTH_OK{session_binding,auth_context_generation,principal_binding,
        credential_issuer_and_epoch,transport_non_widenable_authority_ceiling_digest,
        authentication_time_profile_oid,auth_not_before,auth_expiry}
SELECT_DATABASE{database_selector,expected_posture?}
READY{auth_context_generation,database_posture,operational_role_identity,
      cluster_incarnation,service_visibility_epoch,service_authority_commitment,
      security_state_binding_public_commitment,ready_binding_digest,
      database_limits,concurrency_profile}
AUTH_REFRESH{token,channel_binding,expected_auth_state_token}
AUTH_REFRESHED{auth_state_token,non_widenable_authority_commitment,
               security_state_binding_public_commitment,time_authority_profile_oid,
               auth_not_before,auth_expiry,
               existing_child_effect:NewWorkOnly|NarrowedUnsentOutputFenced}

BEGIN{begin_idempotency_key,txn_operation_ceiling,isolation,transaction_error_policy,
      write_coordinates,default_snapshot_selector,read_your_writes_capability?,resource_request}
BEGIN_ACCEPTED{posture,reservation_handle:PublicHandle<TxnStaging>,
               staging_state_token:PublicStateToken<TxnStaging>,txn_outcome_capability,
               canonical_begin_request_commitment,service_visibility_epoch}
BEGUN{posture,txn_handle:PublicTxnHandle,
      ownership_state_token:PublicStateToken<TxnOwnership>,
      registration_handle:PublicRegistrationHandle,txn_outcome_capability,
      registered_snapshots:PublicEffectiveSnapshotSet,
      workspace_state_token:PublicStateToken<TxnWorkspace>,next_statement_request_ordinal,
      transaction_error_policy}
BEGIN_OUTCOME{txn_outcome_capability,status:PublicTxnStatusProjection}

PREPARE{prepare_request_idempotency_key,text,language_contract_oid,requested_parameter_contract?,
        requested_authority_ceiling_commitment}
PREPARED{prepared_statement_handle:PublicHandle<PreparedStatement>,
         prepared_state_token:PublicStateToken<PreparedStatement>,param_schema,
         public_template_commitment,compatibility_contracts,
         prepare_authorization_projection}
EXECUTE{scope:ExecutionScope,prepared_statement_handle,prepared_state_token,params,
        consistency,read_your_writes_capability?,
        determinism,answer_requirement,fetch,resource_request}
EXECUTE_TEXT{scope:ExecutionScope,text,language_contract_oid,params,consistency,
             read_your_writes_capability?,determinism,answer_requirement,fetch,resource_request}
EXECUTE_ACCEPTED{scope:
    Autocommit{posture,registration_handle:PublicRegistrationHandle,
               txn_outcome_capability,canonical_request_commitment}
  | Transaction{posture,statement_handle:PublicHandle<Statement>,
                statement_state_token:PublicStateToken<Statement>,
                canonical_request_commitment}}

RESULT_BEGIN{result_delivery_handle:PublicResultDeliveryHandle,
             delivery_state_token:PublicStateToken<ResultDelivery>,
             owner:PublicResultOwnerProjection,
             result_resume_capability,read_release_authorization:ReadReleaseAuthorizationEvidence,
             audit_release:AuditReleaseEvidence,schema,result_answer_contract,
             canonical_encoding_version,result_digest:PublicResultDigestProjection,
             total_chunks,total_len,
             replay_manifest_projection?}
RESULT_CHUNK{result_delivery_handle,delivery_state_token,result_digest,
             chunk_ordinal,chunk_count,canonical_offset,chunk_digest,payload}
RESULT_END{result_delivery_handle,delivery_state_token,result_digest,
           effective_snapshots:PublicEffectiveSnapshotSet}
RESULT_RESUME{resume_capability,replay_from:Beginning|AfterCompleteChunk{chunk_ordinal,chunk_digest}}
RESULT_RESUMED{rotated_resume_capability,result_delivery_handle,delivery_state_token,
               accepted_replay_from_chunk,result_digest,total_chunks}
RESULT_ACK{resume_capability,result_delivery_handle,expected_delivery_state_token,
           result_digest,complete_result_end_transcript_digest,client_ack_nonce}
RESULT_RELEASE{management_handle_or_resume_capability,result_delivery_handle,
               expected_delivery_state_token,request_idempotency_key,client_release_nonce}
RESULT_ACKED{result_delivery_handle,delivery_state_token,
             capability_disposition:Rotated{resume_capability}|Consumed{consumption_proof}}
RESULT_RELEASED{result_delivery_handle,terminal_state_token,disposition_proof}

ROLLBACK{txn_handle,ownership_generation,expected_statement_frontier,
         expected_delivery_detachment_frontier,expected_workspace_generation}
COMMIT{txn_handle,ownership_generation,expected_statement_frontier,
       expected_delivery_detachment_frontier,expected_workspace_generation}
TXN_OUTCOME{txn_outcome_capability,status:PublicTxnStatusProjection::TerminalReady}
TXN_STATUS{txn_outcome_capability}
TXN_STATUS_RESULT{status:PublicTxnStatusProjection}

READ_YOUR_WRITES_ISSUED{txn_outcome_capability,read_your_writes_capability,
                        posture,floors:PublicRywFloorSet}
READ_YOUR_WRITES_WAIT{read_your_writes_capability,
                      requested_subset_selectors:[{graph:WireGraphReferenceSpec,
                                                  minimum_floor:PublicMarkerSelector}]}
READ_YOUR_WRITES_READY{rotated_or_same_capability,posture,
                       effective_snapshots:PublicEffectiveSnapshotSet,
                       satisfied_floors:PublicRywFloorSet,
                       read_release_authorization,audit_release}

MERGE_PREPARE{merge_request_idempotency_key,canonical_request_commitment,
              target:WireGraphReferenceSpec,
              source_heads:[PublicMarkerSelector],merge_bases:[PublicMarkerSelector],
              resolution_policy_oid}
MERGE_PREPARED{merge_handle:PublicMergeHandle,merge_state_token:PublicStateToken<MergeProgress>,
               merge_outcome_capability,canonical_request_commitment,
               service_visibility_epoch}
MERGE_EXECUTE{merge_outcome_capability,expected_target_basis:PublicMarkerSelector,
              resource_request}
MERGE_STATUS{merge_outcome_capability}
MERGE_STATUS_RESULT{status:PublicMergeStatusProjection}

QUERY_CANCEL{request_id,expected_child_state_token,cancel_nonce}
QUERY_CANCEL_RESULT{state:CancelledBeforePublication|CancellationRequested|
    SenderPaused{result_delivery_handle:PublicResultDeliveryHandle,
                 delivery_state_token:PublicStateToken<ResultDelivery>}|TooLateSemantic|Unknown}
COMMIT_CANCEL{txn_handle,expected_attempt_state_token,cancel_nonce}
COMMIT_CANCEL_RESULT{CancelledBeforeAdmission|TooLate|Unknown}
CANCEL_EXPLANATION{lineage,exact_drain_state,read_release_authorization,audit_release,
                   statistical_estimate?}

SUBSCRIBE_BEGIN{subscription_idempotency_key,canonical_request_digest,definition,
                snapshot_mode,authority_mode}
SUBSCRIBE_ACCEPTED{subscription_idempotency_key,canonical_request_commitment,
                   cursor_handle:PublicCursorHandle,
                   cursor_state_token:PublicStateToken<Cursor>,
                   management_handle:PublicHandle<PendingOutputManagement>,
                   state:PendingVisibility|ReleasedAwaitingActivation}
SUBSCRIBE_READY{subscription_idempotency_key,canonical_request_commitment,
                cursor_handle:PublicCursorHandle,cursor_state_token:PublicStateToken<CursorOutput>,
                public_derived_identity_commitment,public_graph_binding_projections,
                authorized_cursor_position,resume_capability,
                baseline_lease_handle:PublicDeliveryLeaseHandle,
                baseline_handle:PublicBaselineHandle,baseline_digest:PublicResultDigestProjection,
                read_release_authorization,audit_release}
SNAPSHOT_BEGIN{baseline_lease_handle,baseline_handle,cursor_state_token,authorized_cursor_position,
               read_release_authorization,audit_release,output_schema,result_answer_contract,
               canonical_encoding_version,total_chunks,total_len,digest}
SNAPSHOT_CHUNK{baseline_lease_handle,baseline_handle,cursor_state_token,digest,
               chunk_ordinal,chunk_count,canonical_offset,payload}
SNAPSHOT_END{baseline_lease_handle,baseline_handle,cursor_state_token,digest}
SNAPSHOT_ACK{resume_capability,baseline_lease_handle,baseline_handle,baseline_digest,
             expected_cursor_state_token,complete_snapshot_end_transcript_digest,
             client_ack_nonce}
SNAPSHOT_ACKED{rotated_resume_capability,cursor_state_token,authorized_cursor_position}

AuthorizedDeltaPublicProvenance =
    Opaque{delivery_sequence,authorized_cursor_position}
  | Declassified{delivery_sequence,authorized_cursor_position,
                 source_posture_and_frontiers,atomic_or_coalesced_source_commitment,
                 declassification_authorization,audit_release}
DELTA_BEGIN{delivery_lease_handle,delivery_handle:PublicHandle<CursorOutput>,
            public_delivery_digest,cursor_state_token,
            authorized_cursor_position,output_schema,result_answer_contract,
            canonical_encoding_version,canonical_payload_len,output_payload_digest,
            public_provenance:AuthorizedDeltaPublicProvenance,
            read_release_authorization,audit_release,total_chunks}
DELTA_CHUNK{delivery_handle,public_delivery_digest,output_payload_digest,cursor_state_token,
            chunk_index,chunk_count,canonical_offset,payload}
DELTA_END{delivery_handle,public_delivery_digest,output_payload_digest,cursor_state_token,
          authorized_proof_projection?}
ACK{resume_capability,delivery_lease_handle,delivery_handle,public_delivery_digest,
    expected_cursor_state_token,complete_delta_end_transcript_digest,client_ack_nonce}
ACKED{rotated_resume_capability,cursor_state_token,authorized_cursor_position}
RESUME{resume_capability}
RESUMED{rotated_resume_capability,cursor_state_token,
        outstanding:Baseline{baseline_lease_handle,baseline_handle,baseline_digest}|
                    Delta{delivery_lease_handle,delivery_handle,public_delivery_digest}|
                    ResetNotice{reset_notice_handle,public_reset_commitment}|None,
        authorized_cursor_position}
SUBSCRIBE_CLOSE{resume_capability,expected_cursor_state_token,request_idempotency_key,
                outstanding_disposition:None|BaselineAck{receipt_commitment}|
                    DeltaAck{receipt_commitment}|AuthenticatedRelease{release_commitment}|
                    ResetSupersession{gap_supersession_commitment}}
SUBSCRIPTION_ENDED{terminal_digest,capability_disposition,final_cursor_state_token,
                   read_release_authorization,audit_release}
GAP{cursor_handle,cursor_state_token,reset_notice_handle,public_reset_commitment,
    expected_authorized_cursor_position,available_from_authorized_position,
    successor_baseline_handle,successor_public_baseline_digest,resume_capability,
    read_release_authorization,audit_release}
GAP_ACK{resume_capability,reset_notice_handle,expected_cursor_state_token,
        complete_gap_transcript_digest,client_ack_nonce}
GAP_ACKED_AWAITING_BASELINE{cursor_handle,cursor_state_token,
                            management_handle:PublicHandle<PendingOutputManagement>}
RESET_BASELINE_READY{cursor_handle,cursor_state_token,rotated_resume_capability,
                     baseline_lease_handle,baseline_handle,baseline_digest,
                     read_release_authorization,audit_release}
PROOF{body:ProofBody,read_release_authorization,audit_release}

CAPABILITY_MIGRATE{legacy_capability,transition_identity,expected_legacy_generation,
                   migration_request_idempotency_key}
CAPABILITY_MIGRATED{successor_kind:TxnOutcome|MergeOutcome|ReadYourWrites|
                                   BufferedResult|SubscriptionCursor|DurableArtifactOutput,
                    successor_capability,successor_public_state_projection,
                    migration_record_commitment}

EXPORT_BEGIN{export_idempotency_key,graph_reference_specs:WireGraphReferenceSpec[],export_projection,
             format_contract_oid,snapshot_selector,consistency,resource_request}
EXPORT_ACCEPTED{canonical_request_commitment,output_request_handle:PublicHandle<ArtifactRequest>,
                management_handle:PublicHandle<PendingOutputManagement>,
                artifact_state_token:PublicStateToken<ArtifactDelivery>,
                state:PendingVisibility|ReleasedAwaitingActivation}
EXPORT_READY{export_request_commitment,output_delivery_handle:PublicArtifactOutputHandle,
             artifact_state_token:PublicStateToken<ArtifactDelivery>,
             output_resume_capability,posture,public_projection_digest,
             read_release_authorization,audit_release,
             output_digest:PublicOutputDigestProjection,total_chunks,total_len}
EXPORT_CHUNK{output_delivery_handle,artifact_state_token,public_projection_digest,
             output_digest,chunk_ordinal,
             canonical_offset,payload}
EXPORT_END{output_delivery_handle,artifact_state_token,public_projection_digest,output_digest}

REPLAY_GET{replay_request_idempotency_key,replay_handle,requested_projection}
REPLAY_ACCEPTED{canonical_request_commitment,output_request_handle:PublicHandle<ArtifactRequest>,
                management_handle:PublicHandle<PendingOutputManagement>,
                artifact_state_token:PublicStateToken<ArtifactDelivery>,
                state:PendingVisibility|ReleasedAwaitingActivation}
REPLAY_MANIFEST_BEGIN{replay_handle,public_projection_digest,effective_completeness,
                      output_delivery_handle,artifact_state_token,output_resume_capability,
                      read_release_authorization,audit_release,total_chunks,total_len}
REPLAY_MANIFEST_CHUNK{output_delivery_handle,artifact_state_token,public_projection_digest,
                      chunk_ordinal,canonical_offset,payload}
REPLAY_MANIFEST_END{output_delivery_handle,artifact_state_token,public_projection_digest}
REPLAY_EXECUTE{replay_request_idempotency_key,replay_handle,requested_projection,
               fetch,resource_request}
REPLAY_EXECUTE_ACCEPTED{replay_execution_identity,canonical_request_digest}
REPLAY_STATUS{replay_handle,effective_completeness,authorized_availability_projection,
              read_release_authorization,audit_release}

ARTIFACT_OUTPUT_RESUME{resume_capability,replay_from:Beginning|AfterCompleteChunk{chunk_ordinal,chunk_digest}}
ARTIFACT_OUTPUT_ACK{resume_capability,output_delivery_handle,expected_artifact_state_token,
                    output_digest,complete_output_end_transcript_digest,client_ack_nonce}
ARTIFACT_OUTPUT_ACKED{output_delivery_handle,terminal_state_token,capability_consumption_proof}
ARTIFACT_OUTPUT_RELEASE{management_handle_or_resume_capability,output_delivery_handle,
                        expected_artifact_state_token,request_idempotency_key,client_release_nonce}
ARTIFACT_OUTPUT_RELEASED{output_delivery_handle,terminal_state_token,disposition_proof}

AuditReceiptSubject = Transaction{txn_outcome_capability}
                    | Result{resume_capability}
                    | Subscription{resume_capability}
                    | ArtifactPreactivation{management_handle}
                    | ArtifactActive{resume_capability}
                    | CursorPreactivation{management_handle}
                    | ProtectedError{error_delivery_handle}
AUDIT_RECEIPT_REQUEST{subject:AuditReceiptSubject}
AUDIT_RECEIPT{subject,state:NotRequired{evidence_projection}
                           |RequiredPending{ticket_identity_projection}
                           |RequiredVisible{portable_receipt_projection}
                           |NotReadyOrUnavailable}

FLOW_CREDIT{stream_id,stream_generation,expected_grant_generation,credit_sequence,
            cumulative_byte_credit,cumulative_row_credit}
FLOW_CREDIT_ACCEPTED{stream_id,stream_generation,grant_generation,next_credit_sequence,
                     cumulative_byte_credit,cumulative_row_credit}
DATA_FRAGMENT{envelope_id,chunk_ordinal,row_ordinal,fragment_ordinal,total_fragments,
              canonical_row_len,row_digest,payload}
FEC_BEGIN{descriptor:PublicFecTransferDescriptor,
          read_release_authorization,audit_release}
FEC_SYMBOL{record:WireFecSymbolRecord}
FEC_END{transfer_id,descriptor_digest,emitted_symbol_count,transfer_digest}
ERROR{scope,code,retry_class,detail:
    PublicNonDataDependent{redacted_detail?}
  | Protected{error_delivery_handle:PublicErrorDeliveryHandle,
              error_state_token:PublicStateToken<ProtectedErrorDelivery>,
              public_reason:PublicReasonProjection,read_release_authorization,
              audit_release,padded_public_fields}}
ERROR_ACK{error_delivery_handle,expected_error_state_token,
          complete_error_frame_transcript_digest,client_ack_nonce}
ERROR_ACKED{error_delivery_handle,terminal_state_token,disposition_proof}
GOODBYE{reason}
```

`SubscriptionCreationIndex` durably maps the scoped subscription key and canonical request digest to exactly one semantic cursor creation plus its Protocol pending owner. Exact retry reproduces `SUBSCRIBE_ACCEPTED` while PendingVisibility/ReleasedAwaitingActivation and reproduces the frozen `SUBSCRIBE_READY` only after BaselineDeliverable; drift fails. No creation entry owns an absolute lease or Resume capability before activation. `SUBSCRIBE_CLOSE` presents the exact authenticated outstanding disposition, orders `CloseCursor`, stores a capability-safe tombstone, and makes lost close-response replay deterministic. Generic STREAM_END, socket loss, or drain is not unsubscribe; ordinary disconnect merely detaches the runtime sender, while an explicit durable pause is a separate authenticated policy transition. `FLOW_CREDIT` is a versioned cumulative grant: the next client-owned credit sequence and expected grant generation must match; server debit generation is private. Exact duplicate is idempotent; conflicting duplicate, gap, decrease, overflow, wrong stream generation, or closed stream rejects. `GAP` is emitted only from ResetNoticeDeliverable; `GAP_ACK` durably completes that notice before the separately activated successor baseline receives a fresh lease. `GAP`, explicit `AUTHORITY_RESET`, reset-baseline readiness, and `SUBSCRIPTION_ENDED` are protected data-dependent output and require the matrix-assigned fresh guard immediately before every write; socket disconnect itself emits no durable application frame.

The complete internal delta provenance remains in `DeltaDeliveryEnvelope` and its `InternalDeliveryDigest`; it is never a wire type. Every `baseline_digest` frame field above means `PublicBaselineDigest`, and every baseline ACK validates that public digest; internal baseline identity/coverage/frontier are likewise server-side only. `AuthorizedDeltaPublicProvenance` and `PublicDeliveryDigest` are separately derived after authorization. The default Opaque arm reveals only delivery sequence and authorized cursor position. Atomic/coalesced source ranges, batch IDs/digests, source count, zero-net cadence, and hidden commits appear only in the explicitly authorized and audited Declassified arm. Hidden-commit differential tests require byte-identical public streams, timings within the declared deterministic envelope, and digests under the Opaque policy.

Local dispatch uses `LocalStatementAbortSpec`, `TxnAbortSpec`, `LocalResultDeliveryTransitionSpec`, Local read-close/prepare/terminal-completion, and Local status/index types. Sharded dispatch uses `GlobalStatementAbortSpec`, `GlobalAttemptCancelSpec`, `GlobalResultDeliveryTransitionSpec`, Global read-close/prepare/terminal-completion, and Meta outcome/directory types. After publication, QUERY_CANCEL can only pause the current sender; only RESULT_RELEASE or lease expiry releases independently retained delivery, and neither retracts Published. After prepare admission, semantic cancellation returns TooLate or Unknown and recovery completes the admitted terminal. `CANCEL_EXPLANATION` and data-dependent cancellation errors are protected output.

`CAPABILITY_MIGRATE` is legal only under a fresh Sharded E2 Ready binding and the exact six-arm `PostActivationCapabilityMigrationSpec`, including DurableArtifactOutput. The E capability is checked solely as proof with the same principal, no-wider authority, immediately preceding Local epoch, and certified transition. It grants no E read, delivery, lookup, or existence authority. Exact retry returns the same prebuilt E2 successor; ineligible or invalid proof is a uniform nonrevealing failure. Subscription migration requires no outstanding or pending Local output and creates a new pending baseline; no Local-to-Global delta range is emitted. PendingVisibility and ReleasedAwaitingActivation promises have no legacy capability, so `RoleTransitionProtectedPromiseInventory` and `PostActivationProtectedPromiseTransferSpec` transfer them by exact owner commitment before capability migration can complete.

Frame-order validity never substitutes for value, authorization, decompression, time, or resource validation. Every `*_BEGIN`, chunk, end, proof, replay/status projection, exported byte, protected error, and cancellation explanation consumes the one fresh `PublicFrameSendGuard` arm assigned by the generated frame-class matrix; `DataOutput` is not valid for status, terminal outcomes, audit receipts, or protected errors. Canonical chunking uses deterministic record/row boundaries; oversized legal rows use `DATA_FRAGMENT` after bounded spill reservation, and nothing is exposed until row and envelope verification completes. ACK is legal only after the matching END transcript. Incomplete unacked output replays from its declared canonical boundary or releases through a typed transition; it is never silently acknowledged.

A ReplayManifest ObjectId is internal and never a wire selector; only an opaque public replay handle plus current scoped Replay authority can select a typed public projection. Replay execution results use the ordinary result lifecycle. `WireFecSymbolRecord` is a channel-bound transfer record, never the at-rest SymbolRecord, and is verified against `PublicFecTransferDescriptor` before admission. Compression/FEC is negotiated only after authenticated public metadata and resource reservation. HTTP/2, gRPC, WebSocket, and Bolt adapters preserve the same idempotency, posture, explicit release, output-guard, cancellation, result-resume, and cutover semantics; Bolt exposes only its declared read-only downgrade.

State-model, golden, differential, fuzz, crash, and DPOR suites cover pre-auth database non-disclosure; missing/out-of-order AUTH_OK/READY; Local/Sharded dispatch parity; lost/duplicate/conflicting BEGIN, autocommit, subscription, result resume/ACK, unsubscribe, export, and replay requests; NotRequired versus RequiredVisible release; revocation/refresh/epoch change between every two chunks; protected-error silence; hidden zero-net provenance noninterference; E-to-E2 migration exclusivity; and adapter parity. No frame or state row ships without its exact generated wire tag, authorization/release rule, size bound, retry rule, and negative transition fixture.

## Appendix E — Working Bibliography (anchors reviewed for this plan)

Storage: Sortledton (PVLDB 15(6), 2022) · Teseo (PVLDB 14(6), 2021) · LiveGraph (PVLDB 13(7), 2020) · Aspen (PLDI 2019) · GraphOne (FAST 2019) · GTX / Spruce / RapidStore (2024–25) · RadixGraph (2026) · "Revisiting In-Memory Dynamic Graph Storage" (2025) · Dostoevsky/Fluid LSM (Dayan & Idreos, SIGMOD 2018) · S3-FIFO (Yang et al., SOSP 2023) · SuRF (Zhang et al., SIGMOD 2018). Processing: Kùzu (CIDR 2023) + "Columnar Storage and List-based Processing" (PVLDB 14(11)) + A+ Indexes · NPRR/AGM & Leapfrog Triejoin · EmptyHeaded (SIGMOD 2016) · Free Join (SIGMOD 2023) · ADOPT (2023) · HoneyComb (2025) · Umbra (CIDR 2020) · LeanStore · morsel-driven parallelism (SIGMOD 2014) · SPJM converged optimization (2024). Incremental: DBSP (PVLDB 16 / VLDBJ 2025, Lean-formalized; Feldera) · Differential/Timely Dataflow · streaming-graph SGQ work. Temporal: AeonG (PVLDB 17(6), 2024; VLDBJ 2025) · SQL:2011 system time · T-GQL, Clock-G. Languages: ISO/IEC 39075:2024 (GQL) · ISO/IEC 9075-16:2023 (SQL/PGQ) · GQL/PGQ pattern-matching & expressive-power papers · path-multiset representations (Martens et al.) · openCypher TCK. Vector: HNSW (Malkov & Yashunin) · DiskANN/Vamana · 2025–26 fresh-ANN and disaggregated-HNSW literature. Verification: FoundationDB simulation testing · Elle (Kingsbury & Alvaro) · Cahill/Fekete SSI · Serial Safety Net (Wang et al.) · asupersync formal-semantics & proof-lane artifacts. Distributed alternatives (evaluated, not adopted — §3.4, §14): Weaver refinable timestamps · G-Tran RDMA MV-OCC. Calibration & sequential statistics (§15.0): conformal prediction (Vovk, Gammerman & Shafer) and conformal risk control · game-theoretic probability, e-processes & anytime-valid inference (Shafer & Vovk; Ramdas, Grünwald, Vovk & Shafer; Ville 1939) · Wald's SPRT (1945) · Shiryaev–Roberts change detection · Bayesian online changepoint detection (Adams & MacKay 2007) · empirical Bayes / James–Stein (Robbins; Efron & Morris) · Good–Turing (1953) · robust estimation (Huber; Catoni 2012) · off-policy evaluation (Dudík, Langford & Li 2011) · learning-augmented algorithms (Lykouris & Vassilvitskii 2018). Control & operations (§16.6): CVaR (Rockafellar & Uryasev 2000) · extreme value theory / POT-GPD (Pickands 1975) · network calculus (Le Boudec & Thiran 2001) · max-plus algebra (Baccelli et al.) · Kingman's bound; Little's law · submodular maximization (Nemhauser, Wolsey & Fisher 1978; Krause & Golovin) · critical slowing down / early-warning signals (Scheffer et al. 2009). Consistency & distribution (§7.4, §14): CALM (Hellerstein & Alvaro, CACM 2020) · Red–Blue consistency (Li et al., OSDI 2012) · escrow transactions (O'Neil 1986) · SWIM (Das, Gupta & Motivala 2002) + Lifeguard (Dadgar et al. 2018) · closed timestamps (CockroachDB) · hybrid logical clocks (Kulkarni et al. 2014) · Merkle anti-entropy (DeCandia et al., Dynamo 2007) · consistent hashing with bounded loads (Mirrokni et al. 2016) · optimal transport (Villani; Cuturi's Sinkhorn 2013).
